PT P
PN CN108434982-A; CN208406622-U
TI Cyclohexanone tail gas treating device, has oxidation reactor for converting carbon monoxide and volatile organic compounds in tail gas into carbon dioxide, water and purified gas based on oxidation reaction.
AU HU J
   LI W
AE HANGZHOU PUYU TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (ZJRY-C)
GA 2018683244
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first heater for heating and discharging to-be-processed cyclohexanone tail gas into an expander. An expansion power machine is provided with an expander and a generator. The expander is connected with an outlet end of the first heater. The generator generates electricity by using energy output by the expander. A second heater heats and discharges the to-be-processed cyclohexanone tail gas into an oxidation reactor. The oxidation reactor converts carbon monoxide and volatile organic compounds in the to-be-processed cyclohexanone tail gas into carbon dioxide, water and purified gas based on oxidation reaction.
   USE - Cyclohexanone tail gas treating device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is simple in structure, and has high safe and stable operation, high recovery rate and better economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a cyclohexanone tail gas treating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a cyclohexanone tail gas treating device. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN CN207692525-U
TI Automatic flower greenhouse control system, has deflation valve starting device, temperature sensor, heating device, cooling device, light intensity sensor and LED plant growth lamp device that are electrically connected with fan.
AU GUO L
AE GUO L (GUOL-Individual)
GA 2018632076
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a novel flower greenhouse automatic control system, comprising a growth chamber, the growth chamber is set in the room, the room is fixed with a controller, the controller is composed of a fixedly installed in the controller of the soil moisture detecting control system, an air humidity detection and control system, a carbon dioxide concentration detection control system, the growth chamber is temperature regulating system, adjusting the illumination system and a ventilation system, and the controller is connected with the soil water content detection sensor, an air humidity detecting sensor. rotary nozzle starting device, a carbon dioxide concentration detection device, the deflation valve starting device, a temperature sensor, a heating device, a cooling device, a light intensity sensor, the LED plant growth lamp device is electrically connected with the fan. The beneficial effects of the utility model are simple structure and strong practicability.
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); S02 (Engineering Instrumentation); T06 (Process and Machine Control)

PN JP2018114429-A; JP6667831-B2
TI Manufacturing method for filter in filtering machinery, involves sewing filter cloth of thermoplastic resin fibers using carbon dioxide laser or fiber laser.
AU OSHIKAWA T
AE TAKARA KOGEISHA KK (TAKA-Non-standard)
   TAKARA KOGEISHA KK (TAKA-Non-standard)
GA 201858359T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sewing the filter cloth, particularly a padding cloth (10-8), using carbon dioxide laser or fiber layers. Filling of the seal portion of the filter cloth is carried out. The cloth is manufactured from thermoplastic resin fibers.
   USE - Manufacturing method for filter used in filtering machinery of pressurizing, pressure reduction, centrifugal wet type, or dry type (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Labor and production costs can be reduced while harm on human health can be minimized, by not employing cutting process, needle and thread sewing process, and resin coating process. Since needle and threads are not used, filter cloth fraying and solvent usage can be avoided, ensuring stable filtration function for long time. By using the filter cloth, issues such as process liquid leakage, dust infiltration, etc., are avoided, providing the filtering machinery with excellent durability and long term filtration function.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic views of the woven fabric in plain weave, twill weave, and satin weave forms.
   Weight insertion portions (10-6)
   Sewn portions (10-7)
   Padding cloth (10-8)

PN CN108252697-A
TI Petroleum extracting method, involves injecting carbon dioxide into straight well until top part of steam chamber is formed with carbon heat insulating layer to discharge oil in oil storage layer by using horizontal well.
AU SUN X
   SANG L
   DING C
   WANG L
   YANG G
   LIU M
   LV B
   XIAO W
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201855297C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves forming an oil storage layer in a horizontal well (10). A steam chamber (20) is formed in a reservoir layer. An oil layer is formed in a straight well (30). Carbon dioxide is injected into the straight well until a top part of the steam chamber is formed with a carbon heat insulating layer to discharge oil in the oil storage layer by using the horizontal well. A horizontal production well (11) is connected with a horizontal steam injection well (12). An interlayer region is formed in the steam chamber. The steam chamber is connected with the straight well. An injection hole (31) is formed in an upper part of the horizontal production well.
   USE - Petroleum extracting method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively improving yield of the petroleum.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a petroleum extracting unit.
   Horizontal well (10)
   Horizontal production well (11)
   Horizontal steam injection well (12)
   Steam chamber (20)
   Straight well (30)
   Injection hole (31)

PT P
PN CN108238596-A; CN108238596-B
TI Doped carbon aerogel material useful for as gas adsorbent including at least one of carbon dioxide, methane, hydrogen and nitrogen or useful as supercapacitor electrode, aerogel having a porous structure, and is doped with heteroatoms.
AU SUI Z
   ZENG F
   HAN B
AE NAT CENT NANOSCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CHINA (NANI-C)
   NAT CENT NANOSCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CHINA (NANI-C)
GA 201853955E
AB    NOVELTY - Doped carbon aerogel material, is claimed. The aerogel material having a porous structure, and is doped with heteroatoms, where the heteroatom is nitrogen heteroatom, sulfur heteroatom and/or phosphorus heteroatom, and the doped carbon aerogel material has a specific surface area of 1000-3000 m2/g.
   USE - The aerogel material is useful for as gas adsorbent including at least one of carbon dioxide, methane, hydrogen and nitrogen, or useful as supercapacitor electrode (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel material: has large specific surface area, wide pore size, three-dimensional porous structure, excellent gas adsorption properties, and electrochemical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the aerogel material comprising (i) polymerizing pyrrole and aldehyde compound monomer under high salt zinc chloride conditions for generating zinc chloride-containing polymer gel material, and drying the gel material to obtain zinc chloride-containing polymer bulk material, and (ii) subjecting the resulting polymer bulk material to activation reaction at 600-1000 degrees C to obtain doped carbon aerogel material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pore volume of the porous structure of the doped carbon aerogel material is 0.2-2 cm3/g, and aperture size is 0.0004-50 mu m. The aldehyde compound monomer is formaldehyde, glyoxal, glutaraldehyde, 2- thiophenecarboxaldehyde and/or pyridoxal phosphate. The amount ratio of pyrrole and aldehyde compound monomer in the substance is 0.5-6:1. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the polymerization is specifically carried out by adding pyrrole and aldehyde compound monomer into the salt zinc chloride solution at 50-130 degrees C, polymerizing for 2-24 hours, and then drying the resulting gel material at 30-200 degrees C to obtain zinc chloride-containing polymer bulk material. In the step (ii), the activation reaction time is 0.5-6 hours, preferably 1-3 hours, the activation reaction pre-heating speed rate is 1-20 degrees C/minute, preferably 5-10 degrees C/minute, the rate of temperature drop after the activation reaction is 1-20 degrees C/minute, preferably 5-10 degrees C/minute, and the activation reaction temperature is 700-1000 degrees C, preferably 800-950 degrees C. Preferably, the activation reaction is carried out in a tubular furnace, where the tubular furnace is with gas protection. The protection gas is nitrogen, argon gas, helium gas, neon gas, krypton and/or xenon. The preparation method of the aerogel material specifically comprises (i) adding pyrrole and aldehyde compound monomer into the salt zinc chloride solution, uniformly mixing in ice water bath, where the mass concentration of the salt solution is 40-60%, then polymerizing at 105 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain zinc chloride-containing polymer gel material, and then drying the gel material at 80 degrees C to obtain zinc-containing polymer bulk material, and (ii) placing the chloride-containing polymer bulk material in argon or nitrogen at a rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute, raising the temperature to 800-950 degrees C, activating the reaction at 800-950 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and cooling to room temperature at a rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute to obtain doped carbon aerogel material.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); ; L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); V01 (Resistors and Capacitors)
MC E10-J02D1; E11-Q02C; E12-A10; E12-C02; E31-A03; E31-H03; E31-N05C; E35-C03; J01-D; J01-E02B3; J01-E03C; J04-X; L03-B03G; L03-H05; A02-A07; A05-J08; A10-D; A10-E05B; A12-E07B; A12-E14; A12-W11; A12-W11D; V01-B01A3; V01-B01D5

PN UZ1301-U
TI Laboratory unit for gas-liquid extraction of ingredients from plant material, comprises cylinder with carbon dioxide, extractor heater, and extractor tank and compressor which is connected in series into closed loop.
AU GAFUROV K K
   MUKHAMMADIEV B T
   SHARIPOVA M F
AE BUKHARA ENG & TECHNOLOGY INST (BUKH-Non-standard)
GA 201923050P
AB    NOVELTY - Laboratory unit comprises cylinder with carbon dioxide, extractor heater, extractor tank and compressor which is connected in series into closed loop, high-pressure extractor with cassette for placing plant sample. The separator-evaporator is connected to the bottom of the extractor through throttle valve. The condenser is connected between the compressor and the extractor tank. The extractor heater is located between the extractor tank. The high-pressure extractor is connected to the top of the extractor, compressor, separator-evaporator, throttle valve and condenser form closed loop of the heat pump with a working agent serving as heat agent for the separator-evaporator, and for the condenser-coolant. The condenser of the heat pump is connected with coil separator-evaporator. The evaporator of the heat pump is connected with condenser jacket.
   USE - Laboratory unit for gas-liquid extraction of ingredients from plant material.
   ADVANTAGE - The laboratory unit saves energy costs, and maintains necessary temperature in the evaporator separator and cooling the extractant in the condenser.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN WO2018072989-A1; CA3034580-A1; CN109715764-A; EP3526313-A1; EP3526313-B1; US2021284927-A1; CN109715764-B
TI Converting feedstock comprising solid hydrocarbons to sweet synthesis gas comprises gasifying feedstock, conditioning raw synthesis gas and contacting sour shift feed gas and sulfided material catalytically active in water gas shift process.
AU CHIRON F P
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 201832188W
AB    NOVELTY - Converting feedstock comprising solid hydrocarbons to sweet synthesis gas, comprises: (a) gasifying feedstock in presence of steam, oxygen rich gas and sour process gas to form raw synthesis gas optionally comprising tar; (b) optionally conditioning raw synthesis gas to sour shift feed gas; (c) contacting sour shift feed gas with sulfided material catalytically active in water gas shift process for providing sour hydrogen enriched synthesis gas; and (d) separating hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide synthesis gas to give sour recycle gas and sweet hydrogen enriched synthesis gas.
   USE - The method is useful for converting a feedstock comprising solid hydrocarbons to a sweet synthesis gas, and producing methane, ammonia, methanol or dimethyl-ether and hydrocarbon (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is only a single step for separating hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from the synthesis gas, while maintaining a sufficient amount of sulfur in the synthesis gas for keeping the material catalytically active in the water gas shift process sulfided.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises directing the tar to contact a material catalytically active in converting hydrocarbons to carbon monoxide to hydrogen. The step (b) comprises heat recovery by transfer of thermal energy to a heat exchange medium, removal of tar, removal of particulate matter and compression. The oxygen rich gas is either atmospheric air or atmospheric air having undergone an oxygen enrichment procedure. The sweet hydrogen enriched gas is directed to contact a material having a sulfur absorption capacity prior to contacting the material catalytically active in methanation. The feedstock comprises a sulfur dopant, taken from the group of sulfur rich biological material, sulfur rich waste or sulfur containing chemicals. The method for production of methane involving production of a sweet synthesis gas further comprises directing the sweet hydrogen enriched synthesis gas to contact a material catalytically active in methanation, for providing a gas rich in methane. The material catalytically active in methanation is cooled by thermal contact with a heat exchange medium in step (e), and optionally transferring the heat exchange medium to step (b) if present. The method of production of ammonia involving production of a sweet synthesis gas further comprises directing the sweet hydrogen enriched synthesis gas to contact a material catalytically active in formation of ammonia, for providing a gas rich in ammonia. The method for production of methanol or dimethyl-ether involving production of a sweet synthesis gas involves directing the sweet hydrogen enriched synthesis gas to contact a material catalytically active in formation of methanol or dimethyl-ether, for providing a gas rich in methanol or dimethyl-ether. The method for production of a hydrocarbon involving production of a sweet synthesis gas involves the further step of directing the sweet hydrogen enriched synthesis gas to contact a material catalytically active in the Fischer Tropsch process, for providing a product rich in hydrocarbons. Preferred Components: The sulfided material catalytically active in the water gas shift process comprises 1-5% cobalt, 5-15% molybdenum or tungsten and a support comprising at least one metal oxide, such as alumina, magnesia, titanium or magnesium-alumina spinel. The sour process gas comprises at least 200 ppmv sulfur. The feedstock comprises sulfur resulting in 80-500 ppmv hydrogen sulfide and carbon monosulfide in the synthesis gas. The feedstock comprises material comprising plant material, animal material, biological waste, industrial waste or household waste.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC H09-C

AE GUANGZHOU BAOSHI WIRELESS POWER SUPPLY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201815541N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing cold water to activate a heating program of a water thermal energy generating device. A gas explosion is formed at the open end of a generator. The gas explosion is outputted to obtain high-temperature gaseous molecular thermal energy. The VOCS off-gas is collected and the high-temperature gaseous water molecules are passed into the VOCS off-gas to decompose it. The VOCS off gas is decomposed in a high temperature environment to form water vapor, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide gas to complete the purification of the VOCS off gas.

   ADVANTAGE - The device is very simple in structure, easy to use, low in energy consumption, and suitable is in a wide range of applications.

TI Producing L-glutamic acid involves providing culture medium comprising yeast extract, culturing L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium and increasing amount of produced L-glutamic acid in culture medium by yeast extract.
AU ANJALI S
   LOHMANN D
   YENYING H
AE MITSUI CHEM INC (MITA-C)
GA 2022814120
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing L-glutamic acid involves providing a culture medium comprising 1-6 g/l yeast extract, culturing a L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium and increasing an amount of the produced L-glutamic acid in the culture medium by the yeast extract, where the L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium includes an acetyl-coenzyme A (CoA) production cycle obtained by imparting enzymatic activity of malate thiokinase, and optionally imparting at least one type of enzymatic activity chosen from malyl-CoA lyase, glyoxylate carboligase and 2-hydroxy-3-oxopropionate reductase to the L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium that does not have any of e.g. a carbon dioxide fixation cycle having an enzymatic reaction from malonyl-CoA to malonate semialdehyde or 3-hydroxypropionate, and a carbon dioxide fixation cycle having an enzymatic reaction from acetyl-CoA and carbon dioxide to pyruvate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing L-glutamic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces L-glutamic acid with reduced cost of fermentative production as it consumes surplus reduction power and moderates excessive aeration/stirring.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing L-glutamic acid involves providing a culture medium comprising 1-6 g/l yeast extract, culturing a L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium and increasing an amount of the produced L-glutamic acid in the culture medium by the yeast extract, where the L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium includes an acetyl-coenzyme A (CoA) production cycle obtained by imparting enzymatic activity of malate thiokinase, and optionally imparting at least one type of enzymatic activity chosen from malyl-CoA lyase, glyoxylate carboligase and 2-hydroxy-3-oxopropionate reductase to the L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium that does not have any of (a) a carbon dioxide fixation cycle having an enzymatic reaction from malonyl-CoA to malonate semialdehyde or 3-hydroxypropionate, (b) a carbon dioxide fixation cycle having an enzymatic reaction from acetyl-CoA and carbon dioxide to pyruvate, (c) a carbon dioxide fixation cycle having an enzymatic reaction from crotonyl-CoA and carbon dioxide to ethylmalonyl-CoA or glutaconyl-CoA, (d) a carbon dioxide fixation cycle having an enzymatic reaction from carbon dioxide to formate and (e) at least one of malate thiokinase and malyl-CoA lyase, where none of (a), (b), (c), or (d) is imparted to the bacterium, or the bacterium exhibits none of the functions of (a), (b), (c), and (d) even though at least one of (a), (b), (c), or (d) is imparted.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Bacterium: The L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium is Corynebacterium glutamicum. The L-glutamic acid producing coryneform bacterium is modified by using an yeast golden gate (yGG) B gene so that L-glutamic acid-producing ability of the strain is enhanced as compared to a non-modified strain. The coryneform bacterium is modified by introducing a mutant yggB gene. The coryneform bacterium is further modified so to decrease alpha -ketoglutarate dehydrogenase activity. The coryneform bacterium is further modified so to decrease lactate dehydrogenase activity. Preferred Method: The method involves providing the culture medium comprising 1-4 g/l yeast extract.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of construction of plasmids for expression of malate thiokinase derived from Methylococcus capsulatus (ATCC 33009), construction of plasmid for expression of mtk and mcl derived from M.capsulatus, construction of plasmid pHSG-P1-MMGG and method for constructing bacterial strains that do not support the claims.

   (B) taking 1.5 ml of the treated sample and adding 1.5 ul of trypsin to a sterile centrifuge tube, incubating at 37 degrees C for 1 hours in a carbon dioxide incubator, and inoculating on HCT-8 cells in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 2 hours, shaking gently every 20 minutes;
   (C) adding 10 mL of maintenance solution to the cell bottle, and placing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, and observing every 12 hours; and
   (D) harvesting culture solution after 72 hours, freezing harvested liquid repeatedly and thawing 3 times, and then inoculating into HCT-8 cells, blindly transmitting 4-5 generations, and observing cell lesions every 12 hours, reaching shedding area to 50% 10ng/microliter at -80 degrees C.

TI System useful for coupling new energy and low            energy consumption to achieve carbon dioxide capture            and utilization comprises water electrolysis reactor,            methanation reactor, calcining/reduction reactor, air            reactor, and carbonation reactor.

   BIAN R

AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2019961909
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a water electrolysis reactor                (1), a methanation reactor (2), a                calcining/reduction reactor (4), an air reactor                (8), and a carbonation reactor; the                calcination/reduction reactor, the air reactor and                the carbonation reactor are connected circularly.                The water electrolysis reactor and the methanation                reactor are connected in turn and then connected                with the calcination/reduction reactor after                passing through the condenser A (3) by the                methanation reactor.
   USE - The system is useful for coupling new energy                and low energy consumption to achieve carbon                dioxide capture and utilization (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes new energy electrolyzed                water to prepare hydrogen, carbon dioxide                hydromethanation and zero carbon emissions, and                achieves polygeneration of high concentration gas                e.g. carbon dioxide, methane, oxygen, nitrogen,                hydrogen and other gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                coupling new energy and low energy consumption to                realize carbon dioxide capture and utilization,                comprising entering oxidized oxygen carrier and the                calcium-based absorbent in calcination/reduction                reactor, subjecting oxygen carrier and methane to a                reduction reaction in the calcination/reduction                reactor, generating carbon dioxide and water, and                releasing heat to calcine the calcium-based                absorbent to form calcium oxide and carbon dioxide,                (ii) separating generated carbon dioxide and water                by a condenser B, introducing water to the water                electrolysis reactor as a raw material, passing                carbon dioxide through heat exchanger A after                recovering heat, mixing hydrogen produced by the                electrolysis of water in water electrolysis reactor                in the methanation reactor, subjecting carbon                dioxide for methanation under the action of a                catalyst to form methane and water, separating                methane and water through the condenser A,                introducing water to the water electrolysis reactor                as a raw material, and conveying methane to the                calcination/reduction reactor as oxygen carrier                chemical chain combustion fuel, (iii) entering                calcined calcium-based absorber and reduced oxygen                carrier into the air reactor via a one-way control                valve A, introducing reduced oxygen carrier and air                into the air reactor for oxidation reaction,                obtaining an oxidized oxygen carrier and a high                concentration of nitrogen, using heat exchanger B                to recover the heat of nitrogen, (iv) entering                calcined calcium-based absorbent and the oxidized                oxygen carrier into the carbonation reactor via a                one-way control valve B, adding main component                calcium oxide in calcined calcium-based absorbent                into the carbonation reactor for cabon dioxide                carbonation reaction, generating calcium carbonate,                so as to remove the carbon dioxide in the flue gas,                and (v) introducing carbonated absorbent after the                carbonation and the oxidized oxygen carrier into                the calcination/reduction reactor via the one-way                control valve C, carrying out undergo chemical                chain combustion of oxidized oxygen carrier and                methane to provide the heat required for pyrolysis                of calcium-based absorbents.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the system for coupling new energy and low                energy consumption to achieve carbon dioxide                capture and utilization (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   Water electrolysis reactor (1)
   Methanation reactor (2)
   Condenser A (3)
   Calcining/reduction reactor (4)
   Air reactor (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), oxygen                  carrier preferably comprises a metal-based oxygen                  carrier, non-metal based oxygen carrier or                  complex oxygen carrier; the metal-based oxygen                  carrier comprises a nickel-based, copper-,                  manganese-, iron- or cobalt- oxygen carrier; the                  non-metal based oxygen carrier comprises calcium                  sulfate or barium sulfate, complex oxygen carrier                  comprises copper-nickel based or nickel-cobalt-                  oxygen carrier. The calcium-based absorbent is                  calcium carbonate or calcium oxide/calcium                  hydroxide/ calcium carbonate as main ingredient                  of natural minerals or waste. In step (ii),                  catalyst comprises noble metal catalyst, the                  transition metal catalyst or complex catalyst;                  noble metal catalyst comprises ruthenium,                  rhodium, platinum or palladium-based catalyst;                  the transition metal catalyst comprises                  nickel-based, cobalt- or iron- catalyst; the                  complex catalyst comprises nickel-ruthenium, or                  nickel-cobalt; in the step (ii), temperature of                  the carbon dioxide methanation reaction is                  200-500 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is                  0.1-5 MPa. Preferred Method: In step (i) the                  temperature needed for calcining the                  calcium-based absorbent is 900-950 degrees C, the                  reaction pressure is normal pressure. In the step                  (iii), oxidation reaction temperature in the air                  reactor is 200-750 degrees C, the reaction                  pressure is normal pressure. In step (iv)                  carbonation reaction temperature in the                  carbonation reactor is 600-750 degrees C, the                  reaction pressure is 0.01-2 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The                  calcination/reduction reactor is connected with                  the heat exchanger A by the condenser B, heat                  exchanger A is connected with the methanation                  reactor and condenser B is connected with the                  electrolytic water reactor. The air reactor with                  one end is connected with the heat exchanger B.                  The calcination/reduction reactor and the air                  reactor, the air reactor and the carbonation                  reactor, a carbonation reactor with the                  calcination/reduction reactor respectively pass                  through single-way control valve A, an                  unidirectional control valve B, an unidirectional                  control valve C.

PT P
PN WO2022189343-A1
TI Producing ethylene, comprises e.g. introducing            fuel gas, hydrocarbons, oxidant gas and steam to            annular jet vortex chamber to provide swirling fluid            flow pattern, and cooling portion of cracking product            in first cracking product cooling zone.
AU LIU Z
   GOPALAKRISHNAN M
   KEITH TURNER S
   PANNALA S
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2022B67029
AB    NOVELTY - Production of ethylene involves (a)                introducing a fuel gas, hydrocarbons, an oxidant                gas, and steam to an annular jet vortex chamber                (ANJEVOC) (100) to provide for a swirling fluid                flow pattern, (b) cooling at least a portion of the                cracking product in a cracking product cooling zone                (210) to yield a cooled cracking product (P1) at                temperature (T2), (c) cooling at least a portion of                the cooled cracking product (P1) in a cracking                product heat exchanger to yield a cooled cracking                product (P2) at temperature (T3) while heating a                heat exchange medium in the cracking, (d)                separating at least a portion of the cooled                cracking product (P2) into a cracked gas and                removed water, (e) introducing at least a portion                of the cracked gas to a continuous regeneration                carbon dioxide removal unit to produce a carbon                dioxide lean cracked gas, and (f) introducing at                least a portion of the carbon dioxide lean cracked                gas to a carbon dioxide removal unit to produce a                carbon dioxide depleted cracked gas.
   USE - Production of ethylene used in break-resistant                containers and packaging materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces olefins with                an annular jet vortex chamber reactor, where                specific separation processes have to be employed                to recover the ethylene from the pyrolysis products                of the ANJEVOC. The process for producing ethylene                comprises specific separation steps that are                required for processing the unique efflux of                ANJEVOC in order to obtain final olefin products                (e.g., ethylene) of specific purities; while                recycling and/or utilizing other valuable                components recovered from the separation steps as                feed and/ or fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of ethylene involves (a)                introducing a fuel gas, hydrocarbons, an oxidant                gas, and steam to an annular jet vortex chamber                (ANJEVOC) (100) to provide for a swirling fluid                flow pattern, (b) cooling at least a portion of the                cracking product in a cracking product cooling zone                (210) to yield a cooled cracking product (P1) at                temperature (T2), (c) cooling at least a portion of                the cooled cracking product (P1) in a cracking                product heat exchanger to yield a cooled cracking                product (P2) at temperature (T3) while heating a                heat exchange medium in the cracking, (d)                separating at least a portion of the cooled                cracking product (P2) into a cracked gas and                removed water, (e) introducing at least a portion                of the cracked gas to a continuous regeneration                carbon dioxide removal unit to produce a carbon                dioxide lean cracked gas, (f) introducing at least                a portion of the carbon dioxide lean cracked gas to                a carbon dioxide removal unit to produce a carbon                dioxide depleted cracked gas, and (g) separating at                least a portion of the carbon dioxide depleted                cracked gas into ethylene, ethane, and a tail gas                comprising methane, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen.                The ANJEVOC comprises a combustion zone which is                upstream of a cracking zone. The oxidant gas does                not contact the fuel gas and hydrocarbons outside                of the ANJEVOC. The hydrocarbons comprise ethane                and/or saturated hydrocarbons other than ethane. In                the combustion zone, at least a portion of the fuel                gas and at least a portion of the oxidant gas                contact each other, to produce a combustion                product. The combustion product comprises water and                carbon dioxide, the swirling fluid flow pattern                provides for conveying at least a portion of the                combustion product to the cracking zone. The                combustion product heats the hydrocarbons in the                cracking zone to a temperature effective for a                cracking reaction, and at least a portion of the                hydrocarbons undergoes a cracking reaction in the                cracking zone to produce a cracking product. The                cracking product comprises ethylene, acetylene,                ethane, methane, water, carbon dioxide, carbon                monoxide, hydrogen, and oxygenates. The amount of                water present in the cracking product is 10-60 wt.%                %, based on the total weight of the cracking                product at cracking product temperature (T1). The                temperature (T2) is lower than the temperature                (T1), the difference between the temperature (T1)                and the temperature (T2) is 30&#176; C or more,                and the cracking product cooling zone (Z1) has a                residence time of less than about 2000                milliseconds. The temperature (T3) is lower than                temperature (T2), the difference between the                temperature (T2) and the temperature (T3) is                300&#176; C or more. The removed water                comprises at least a portion of the oxygenates of                the cracking product, and the cracked gas comprises                ethylene, acetylene, ethane, methane, carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen. The weight                ratio of the carbon dioxide in cracked gas to the                carbon dioxide in lean cracked gas is 5 or more.                The carbon dioxide depleted cracked gas comprises                ethylene, acetylene, ethane, methane, carbon                monoxide, and hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                ethylene production system.
   100Annular jet vortex chamber
   210Cracking product cooling zone
   250Quench tower
   530Depropanizer
   1000Ethylene production system
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (b) further                  involves contacting at least a portion of the                  cracking product with quench water to yield the                  cooled cracking product (P1). The step (g)                  further involves separating at least a portion of                  the carbon dioxide depleted cracked gas into the                  tail gas, 2C or less hydrocarbon stream, and 3C                  or more hydrocarbon stream. Preferred Components:                  The cooled cracking product (P1) comprises                  ethylene, acetylene, ethane, methane, carbon                  dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, oxygenates,                  at least a portion of the water of the cracking                  product, and at least a portion of the quench                  water. The heat exchange medium comprises fuel                  gas and/or hydrocarbons, and further comprises                  water. The carbon dioxide depleted cracked gas                  further comprises 3C or more hydrocarbons, at                  least a portion of acetylene is converted to                  ethylene. The 2C or less hydrocarbons stream                  comprises ethylene, acetylene, and ethane, and                  the 3C or more hydrocarbons stream comprises 3C                  or more hydrocarbons. Preferred Composition: The                  cooled cracking product (P1) contains 1-10 wt.%                  quench water, based on the total weight of the                  cooled cracking product (P1). The amount of                  oxygenates present in the cracking product is                  0.07-1 wt.%, based on the total weight of the                  cracking product, the amount of carbon dioxide                  present in the cracking product is 0.5-10 wt.%,                  based on the total weight of the cracking                  product, and the amount of carbon monoxide                  present in the cracking product is 0.5-10 wt.%,                  based on the total weight of the cracking                  product. The carbon dioxide depleted cracked gas                  contains 10 ppmw carbon dioxide, based on the                  total weight of the carbon dioxide depleted                  cracked gas. The amount of acetylene present in                  the cracking product is 1-25 wt.%, based on the                  total weight of the cracking product.

The processing                unit is configured to apply a calibration                procedure. A colorimetric indicator (10) is                arranged to be used in indicator holder (8). The measured colour is stored as a                zero carbon dioxide (C02) colour value when no C02                is present. A C02 concentration relationship is                determined based upon measured zero C02 colour                value. The processing unit is configured to apply                C02 concentration relationship during C02                concentration measurements provided that the                determined calibrated control signal values are                applied when light is emitted.
   USE - Capnoscope assembly for measuring carbon                dioxide concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The simple use and accurate results of the                capnoscope assembly are allowed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for measuring carbon dioxide concentration in                capnoscope assembly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram                illustrating the capnoscope assembly.
   Detection unit (2)
   Camera (6)
   Indicator holder (8)
   Colorimetric indicator (10)
   Processing unit (12)

PT P
PN CN110761081-A; CN110761081-B
TI Modifying aramid fibers in supercritical carbon            dioxide environment by cleaning and drying aramid            fiber, adding reaction reagent, introducing carbon            dioxide gas, performing swelling reaction, releasing            pressure, washing and drying.
AU LIU L
   MA Z
   JIA C
   YUAN C
   DONG J
   DU Y
   HUANG Y
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 202014097B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for modifying aramid fibers in                supercritical carbon dioxide environment involves                (i) cleaning and drying the aramid fiber, (ii)                placing the cleaned and dried aramid fiber and                reaction reagent in a mass ratio of 1:(40-120) in                closed container, maintaining the aramid fiber                tension to 2-20 N without contacting the reaction                reagent, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide gas at                4-25 degrees C, heating and increasing the pressure                to make the closed container into a supercritical                carbon dioxide state, (iv) performing swelling                reaction at 35-100 degrees C under pressure of                7.5-12.5 MPa for 10-120 minutes, and slowly                releasing the pressure to obtain the product and                (v) washing and drying to obtain the finished                product.
   USE - Method for modifying aramid fibers in                supercritical carbon dioxide environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves longitudinal tensile                strength, mechanical, and surface properties,                realizes the activation of aramid fiber surface,                strengthens the binding between fiber skin layer                and core layer through crosslinking reaction                between molecular chains inside fiber, improves the                structure of sheath and core, increases the                transverse strength of the fiber, and greatly                significance the modification of aramid fibers and                improves use efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the                  reaction reagent comprises 1,2-dichloroethane or                  1,4-dichlorobutane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the                  cleaning liquid used is acetone, the cleaning                  temperature is 50-80 degrees C, and cleaning time                  is 24-48 hours. The drying process in step (i) is                  carried out at 40-70 degrees C for 3-5 hours. The                  step (iv) involves slowly and uniformly releasing                  the pressure for pressure 30-90 minutes. In step                  (v), the cleaning solution used is acetone, the                  cleaning temperature is 20-40 degrees C and                  cleaning time is 10-60 minutes.    (2) Use of the catalyst, in the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol.
   (3) A method for preparing methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, which involves reacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the presence of the catalyst to obtain methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc salt is zinc nitrate and zinc sulfate. The salt of M element is cerous nitrate, chromium nitrate and gallium nitrate. Preferred Process: The coprecipitation preferably includes reacting an aqueous solution containing a zinc salt and a salt of an M element with an alkaline solution, where the temperature of coprecipitation is 60-80degrees Celsius, the drying temperature is 50-80degrees Celsius, and the roasting temperature is 450-550degrees Celsius. The alkali solution is ammonium carbonate aqueous solution, and has concentration of 0.30-0.32 mol/L. The molar ratio of the total metal elements and alkali in the aqueous solution containing the zinc salt and the salt of the M element is 1:1.4-1:1.6. The concentration of the total metal element in the aqueous solution of the salt containing the zinc salt and the M element is 0.190-0.204 mol/L. The catalyst is subjected to hydrogenation reduction in the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of hydrogenation reduction is 300-400degrees Celsius, the hydrogenation reduction time is 6-16 hours. The pressure of hydrogenation reduction is 0.01-0.5 MPa. The space velocity of hydrogen for hydrogenation reduction is 2500-3500 ml/g/h. The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1:2-1:7. The reaction temperature is 300-400degrees Celsius, the reaction pressure is 2-7 MPa, and reaction space velocity is 5000-24000 ml/g/h.

PN CN112390318-A
TI Removing oil by freezing and thawing carbon dioxide hydrate by adding low-temperature oily wastewater into reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting while stirring, opening pressure relief valve and exhaust and discharging mixed liquid.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG D
   WANG R
   ZHAO W
   LIU Z
   YANG M
   SONG Z
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 202121075T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing oil by freezing and thawing carbon dioxide hydrate involves adding suitable volume of low-temperature oily wastewater into a high-pressure-resistant closed reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting while performing continuous mechanical stirring, when the liquid phase is completely transformed into a solid phase, opening the pressure relief valve and exhaust to the normal pressure in the reactor and when the solid phase is fully dissolved, discharging the mixed liquid in the reactor into the gravity separation device for oil-water separation.
   USE - The method is useful for removing oil by freezing and thawing carbon dioxide hydrate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need filler, hence saves the filling processing cost and avoids the secondary pollution problem, has lower energy consumption, is rapid even without adding thermodynamic accelerator and has stronger capability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the method, the low-temperature oil-containing wastewater is oil-containing wastewater from petroleum exploitation, which includes suspended oil, dispersed oil and emulsified oil. The method involves pretreating the oil-containing wastewater from petroleum exploitation by large tank sedimentation and air flotation separation, and then feeding into a high-pressure-resistant closed reactor for reaction at 0.5-2 degrees C, and introducing carbon dioxide at 3.5 MPa, where the total volume of the liquid phase in the high-pressure-resistant closed reactor cannot exceed one-fifth of the effective volume. The method involves performing mechanical stirring at 600 revolutions per minute (rpm). The method involves when the oil and water are separated, if the oil content of the effluent does not meet the standard, feeding into the freeze-thaw cycle again. The method involves when the oil and water are separated, if the oil content of the effluent reaches the standard, reusing the effluent as refill water.

AU CHENG X
AE DONGMING GROWTH BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 2019563036
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting resveratrol using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method comprises (i) washing the knotweed, drying to constant weight and crushing to 70-90 mesh; (ii) adding 3-5 volumes of ethanol by volume ratio and sonicating for 25-35 minutes; (iii) taking the ultra-sonicated mixed solution, adding propanol and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; (iv) concentrating the extract and adding 0.01-0.03 times of the enzyme for enzymatic hydrolysis; and (v) subjecting the hydrolyzed product solution to suction filtration and recrystallization by ethanol and freeze-drying.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting resveratrol using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates the raw materials and improves the utilization rate of resources, use of propanol and ethanol as modifier for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of resveratrol and enzymatic hydrolysis with enzymes to improve the yield and purity of resveratrol extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii) the concentration of ethanol is 75-85%. In step (iii) the amount of propanol is 0.6-0.8 times the amount of ethanol. Preferred Method: In step (iv) the enzyme is cellulase or amylase or protease, and the enzymatic hydrolysis is carried out for 3-5 hours and the enzymatic hydrolysis temperature is 35-45 degrees C.

PN CN108217692-A
TI Production of sodium cyanate, involves dissolving sodium chloride in aqueous ammonia solution, precipitating ammonium chloride, adding mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide, and carrying out carbonation.
AU ZHOU Q
   MA C
   YE M
   CHEN J
   WANG S
   ZHANG J
AE JIANGSU RUIDA ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201853395Y
AB    NOVELTY - The production of sodium cyanate, involves dissolving sodium chloride in aqueous ammonia solution at 10-30 degrees C and precipitating ammonium chloride, reacting at 20-55 degrees C and controlling pressure of 0-0.4 MPa, adding mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide as raw material gas to molten salt solution to obtain sodium hydrogencarbonate, carrying out carbonation at 20-60 degrees C under pressure of 0-0.4 MPa, adding gas containing carbon dioxide as raw material gas to precipitate sodium hydrogencarbonate, filtering sodium bicarbonate, calcining at 160-250 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours to obtain sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide gas, mixing urea and sodium carbonate at mass ratio of 1.8:2.4 and heating at 120-240 degrees C to obtain sodium cyanate, water, ammonia and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Production of sodium cyanate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of sodium cyanate by simple, environmentally-friendly and economical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sodium chloride is mixed salt of industrial salt or treated industrial by-product salt or sodium chloride with purity greater than or equal to 90%.Preferred Conditions: The ammonia gas is mixed gas produced by synthesis of pure ammonia or sodium cyanate using jet absorber or ammonia absorption tower at 10-40 degrees C.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of ammonia and carbon dioxide is 2:1.

PN CN107902631-A
TI Synthesizing carbon dioxide and coproducing sulfur            dioxide and potassium sulfate, comprises e.g. mixing            milled potassium-feldspar mineral powder, calcium            sulfate and carbon powder, molding and activating.
AU YUE H
   LIAO R
   GAN Z
   LI C
   LIANG B
   XIE H
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201830096C
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing carbon dioxide and coproducing                sulfur dioxide and potassium sulfate comprises                mixing milled potassium-feldspar mineral powder,                calcium sulfate and carbon powder, molding,                activating by high-temperature reductive coupling                to obtain sulfur dioxide, mineralizing calcined                slag with carbon dioxide to separate and to obtain                mineralized slag containing calcium carbonate,                calcium silicate and aluminosilicate and                water-soluble potassium sulfate fertilizer.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing carbon                dioxide and coproducing sulfur dioxide and                potassium sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces industrial solid waste                phosphogypsum which is applied to the sulfuric acid                industry thus solves the environmental problems,                completely utilizes the carbon dioxide carbonation                characteristics to carry out mineralization and                carbon reduction, processes water-insoluble                potassium feldspar ore into water-soluble potassium                sulfate as fertilizer, applies reactants to form a                complex during the reaction process to reduce                reaction temperature and energy consumption, has                the triple effect of energy saving and carbon                reduction, is environmentally-friendly and produces                useful chemicals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The inclusion bodies are                  formed by using reactants, reducing reaction                  temperature thus reduces energy consumption,                  carrying out carbonation using carbon dioxide,                  simultaneously processing of water insoluble                  potassium feldspar ore, using industrial solid                  waste containing calcium sulfate (phosphogypsum,                  desulfurized gypsum, titanium gypsum, etc.),                  producing sulfur dioxide, curing carbon dioxide                  and producing water-soluble potassium fertilizer.                  Preferred Components: The potassium feldspar is a                  naturally mined potassium feldspar ore having a                  total potassium content of 8-12 wt.% (calculated                  as potassium oxide) prepared by breaking,                  pulverizing, and sieving using 140-160 meshes.                  The industrial solid waste of calcium sulfate is                  e.g. dry powder of phosphogypsum, desulfurized                  gypsum and titanium gypsum, etc. The carbon                  powder is industrially used anthracite dry                  powder. Preferred Method: The method comprises                  collecting pure carbon dioxide, separating during                  the production process, and also producing e.g.                  synthetic ammonia and by-product pure carbon                  dioxide gas after coal gasification, where                  content of carbon dioxide is greater than 80%.                  The method comprises grinding potassium feldspar                  powder, calcium sulfate and the carbon powder,                  stirring, mixing uniformly, pressing and molding                  into pellets, where molded pellets have diameter                  at 1-50 mm pellets, or cylindrical pellets at                  1-50 mm in diameter and 1-30 mm in height. The                  high-temperature reduction and activation of the                  molded material is carried out in a calcination                  furnace e.g. shaft kiln or a rotary kiln filling                  with a nitrogen atmosphere, and simultaneously                  activating potassium feldspar and calcium sulfate                  during the calcination process, reacting and                  reducing calcium sulfate and carbon powder, where                  baking is carried out at 900-1200 degrees C for                  30-600 minutes. The method comprises calcining                  reacted solid, cooling, grinding, according to                  the solid-liquid mass ratio of 1:1-1:100, adding                  process water into a slurry, adding into a                  pressurized three-phase reactor and carrying out                  mineralization reaction by stirring at 1-30MPa at                  20-300 degrees C for 10-600 minutes and feeding                  carbon dioxide to obtain slurry and gas-phase                  carbon dioxide gas. The filtering is carried out                  to obtain slag and drying the mineralized                  reaction product containing calcium carbonate,                  calcium silicate and aluminosilicate, evaporating                  filtrate and crystallizing potassium                  sulfate.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

A solution source                supplies an electrolytic solution containing water                to the anode flow path. A voltage between the                cathode and the anode are supplied to reduce the                first substance and thus produce the first product                on the cathode, and to oxidize the second substance                and thus produce the second product on the anode.                The data selected from the group consisting of a                data indicating a discharge amount per unit time of                a liquid containing water to be discharged is                acquired from flow path selected from the group.                The supply of the carbon dioxide and the                electrolytic solution is stopped, and supply of a                gaseous substance is started to the flow path in                accordance with the at least one data.

PN CN111924804-A
TI Preparing hydrochloric acid involves adding ammonium chloride, calcium hydroxide, sodium carbonate solution, sodium metal, chromium chloride, hydrogen peroxide and ammonium chloride.
AU JING L
AE SICHUAN BAOMAHE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2020B52892
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrochloric acid involves adding 2-6 pts. wt. ammonium chloride, 1-2 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 2-3 pts. wt. sodium carbonate solution, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium metal, 2-6 pts. wt. chromium chloride, 1-2 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide and 3-6 pts. wt. ammonium chloride. The ammonium chloride and calcium hydroxide are introduced into the first reactor, and heated the first reactor, the heating time is 0.5-1 hours, stirred at a uniform speed by the stirring shaft in the first reactor, and the stirring time is 1-2 hours. The calcium chloride, pure water and ammonia gas are produced in the first reactor to lead the ammonia gas into the first gas tank for collection, and introduced calcium chloride and water into the first solution tank for collection to lead the sodium carbonate solution into the second reactor. The carbon dioxide is passed into the second reactor. The sodium carbonate solution in the second reactor reacts with carbon dioxide, after the reaction is over.
   USE - Method for preparing hydrochloric acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare hydrochloric acid which produce a large amount of hydrogen chloride gas, and the concentration is relatively high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hydrochloric acid involves adding 2-6 pts. wt. ammonium chloride, 1-2 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 2-3 pts. wt. sodium carbonate solution, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium metal, 2-6 pts. wt. chromium chloride, 1-2 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide and 3-6 pts. wt. ammonium chloride. The ammonium chloride and calcium hydroxide are introduced into the first reactor, and heated the first reactor, the heating time is 0.5-1 hours, stirred at a uniform speed by the stirring shaft in the first reactor, and the stirring time is 1-2 hours. The calcium chloride, pure water and ammonia gas are produced in the first reactor to lead the ammonia gas into the first gas tank for collection, and introduced calcium chloride and water into the first solution tank for collection to lead the sodium carbonate solution into the second reactor. The carbon dioxide is passed into the second reactor. The sodium carbonate solution in the second reactor reacts with carbon dioxide, after the reaction is over. The second reactor is heated for 30-60 minutes. The sodium bicarbonate is obtained from the second reactor for use. The reaction time of the sodium carbonate solution and carbon dioxide is 10-20 minutes, and the heating temperature of the second reactor is 50-70 degrees Celsius. The metallic sodium is introduced into the combustion tank, and passed chlorine into the combustion tank, and ignited the sodium metal and heat the combustion tank to 90-100 degrees C. The sodium chloride is produced in the combustion tank, and the reaction time is 0.15-0.5 hours. The ammonia, sodium bicarbonate and sodium chloride are introduced into the third reactor. The sodium carbonate and ammonium chloride is produced, after crystallization and precipitation in the third reactor and heated the third reactor. The third reactor is heated until ammonium chloride decomposes hydrogen chloride and ammonia gas to lead hydrogen chloride and ammonia into the second gas tank for collection. The sodium carbonate and ammonium chloride in the third reactor are separated, and the heating temperature of the third reactor is 90-100 degrees Celsius to lead the ammonia and water into the fourth reactor. The ammonia and water are mixed, and after mixing, carbon dioxide is introduced into the fourth reactor. The ammonium bicarbonate is produced in the fourth reactor, and the ammonium bicarbonate is introduced into the second solution tank for collection. The sodium chloride and the ammonium bicarbonate are introduced into the fifth reactor from the work of the fifth reactor. The sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride are produced, and the fifth reactor is heated. The fifth reactor is heated until ammonium chloride produces hydrogen chloride and ammonia gas. The hydrogen chloride and ammonia gas are collected into the third gas tank, cooled the third gas tank to get ammonium chloride in the third gas tank. The chromium chloride, ammonium chloride are introduced, and ammonia gas and hydrogen peroxide into the sixth reactor, and the sixth reactor will work, and the sixth reactor will produce chromium hexaammonium chloride. The water vapor is passed at a temperature of 120-240 degrees C into the sixth reactor. The water in the sixth reactor reacts with hexaammonium chromium trichloride, the sixth reactor produces hexahydrate chromium trichloride and six ammonia gases, and the six ammonia gasses are introduced into the fourth gas tank for collection. The ammonium chloride is introduced into the sixth reactor, and heated the sixth reactor to water at 80-90 degrees Celsius. The sixth reactor produces hexaammonium chromium trichloride and hydrogen chloride gas. The hydrogen chloride gas is introduced into the fifth gas tank, and the hexaammonium chromium trichloride and water are introduced into the filter. The aqueous solution is added to the fifth gas tank. The stirring time in the fifth gas tank is 1-2 hours, and hydrochloric acid is obtained in the fifth gas tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The materials put into the sixth reactor are hydrogen peroxide, ammonium chloride, chromium chloride and ammonia in order. The temperature of the third gas tank is lowered, and the temperature of the third gas tank is rapidly lowered to a temperature of 0-10 degrees Celsius by cooling tower. The sieved hexaammonium chromium trichloride is introduced into the dryer, and the dryer is used for drying.

GA 202273184V
AB    NOVELTY - Doped cadmium sulfide (CdS) photocatalyst is a                CdS photocatalyst doped with iron, indium, cerium,                copper or tellurium elements. The doped CdS                photocatalyst is used for catalytic reduction of                NAD+ to NADH.
   USE - The doped CdS photocatalyst is useful for                catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst prevents difficulty in                regeneration of coenzyme NADH and difficulty in                recycle and utilization of free enzyme in the                pathway of enzyme-catalyzed carbon dioxide                synthesis of important compounds and improves                stability of the enzymatic reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   preparation method of the doped CdS                photocatalyst involves dissolving sodium sulfide                nonahydrate, cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate and                hydrate containing doping elements in deionized                water, respectively to form respective aqueous                solution, stirring, adding cadmium nitrate                tetrahydrate aqueous solution to the sodium sulfide                nonahydrate aqueous solution under an inert gas                atmosphere, after the reaction, obtaining CdS                photocatalyst crude product dispersion, stirring,                adding dropwise an aqueous solution containing the                hydrate of the element into the CdS photocatalyst                crude product dispersion under an inert gas                atmosphere, after the reaction, obtaining doped CdS                photocatalyst crude product dispersion,                centrifuging and washing the crude dispersion of                the doped CdS photocatalyst to remove unsolidified                raw materials, and drying to obtain the doped CdS                photocatalyst, where the hydrate containing doping                elements comprise iron(III) chloride hexahydrate,                indium nitrate, cerous nitrate hexahydrate, copper                (II) nitrate trihydrate and sodium selenite                pentahydrate;
   photoenzyme-coupled bifunctional catalytic                material for catalytic conversion of carbon                dioxide, which is a doped CdS photocatalyst                prepared by the doped CdS photocatalyst, where the                preparation method CdS photocatalyst is coupled                with the carrier-immobilized oxidoreductase;
   method for preparing a photoenzyme-coupled                bifunctional catalytic material involves                immobilizing the oxidoreductase in the carrier to                obtain carrier-immobilized oxidoreductase, coupling                the carrier-immobilized oxidoreductase with the                doped CdS photocatalyst by a hydrothermal synthesis                method;
   ystem for photoenzyme coupled catalytic                conversion of carbon dioxide based on a doped CdS                photocatalyst comprising (a) photoenzyme-coupled                bifunctional catalytic material for catalytic                conversion of carbon dioxide or material for                catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide prepared by                the preparation method, (b) catalytic reduction of                NAD+ to NADH under the action of doped CdS                photocatalyst and (c) enzymatically convert carbon                dioxide into a high value-added compound under the                action of an oxidoreductase using NADH as a                coenzyme, where the high value-added compound                comprises formic acid or formaldehyde; and
   usage method of photoenzyme coupled                bifunctional catalytic material for catalytic                conversion of carbon dioxide, involves converting                carbon dioxide into a high-value-added compound                through enzymatic catalysis under action of the                photoenzyme-coupled bifunctional catalytic                material, where the high-value-added compound                comprises formic acid or formaldehyde.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of                  sulfur in sodium sulfide nonahydrate and cadmium                  in cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate is 1. The molar                  ratio of cadmium element and doped element is                  1:(0.05-1). The                  oxidoreductase uses NADH as a coenzyme comprising                  formate dehydrogenase (FateDH) and/or                  formaldehyde dehydrogenase. The carrier is                  metal-organic framework (MOF) material,                  preferably zeolitic imidazolate framework                  (ZIF)-8.Preferred Method: The method involves adding                  the photoenzyme-coupled bifunctional catalytic                  material into a system containing an electron                  transporter and an electron donor and NAD+ under                  the irradiation of light under uniform stirring                  process, catalytically reducing NAD+ to NADH                  under action of doped CdS photocatalyst under                  action of oxidoreductase, enzymatically                  converting carbon dioxide into high value-added                  compounds, where the electron transfer portion                  comprises Mbpy and/or the electron donor includes                  triethanolamine (TEOA) and/or EDTA.

TI System for power generation, comprises compact hydrogen generator, which contains quantity of sorbent material, where feed material produces generator products including hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and steam, and gas/solids separator.
AU SUBBARAMAN G
   MAYS J A
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 201934566K
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises compact hydrogen generator (212), which contains a quantity of a sorbent material, where a feed material produces generator products including hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and steam (234) and the sorbent material absorbs carbon dioxide and forms a used sorbent, a gas/solids separator (239) connected to the compact hydrogen generator to separate the used sorbent from hydrogen and steam, a calciner (272) connected to the gas/solids separator to heat the used sorbent to desorb carbon dioxide from the used sorbent to produce regenerated sorbent, a recycle line to introduce at least a portion of the regenerated sorbent from the calciner to the compact hydrogen generator, and one power generator to receive and utilize at least a portion of the hydrogen product and steam (260) from the gas/solids separator to produce power.
   USE - System for power generation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system is compact and scalable, enables direct production of hydrogen from natural gas as an alternative to steam methane reformers, lower costs, higher hydrogen yield and a concentrated carbon dioxide product stream suitable for carbon capture and sequestration (CCS) or enhanced oil recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The compact hydrogen generator has a steam to carbon ratio operating range between 2.5:1 to 4:1 and produces generator products having a steam to hydrogen ratio (volume basis) of 1:8 and 1:2. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing power via hydrogen gas, which involves introducing feed material into a sorbent enhanced reformer to produce products including hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and steam, sorbent enhanced reformer containing a quantity of a sorbent material to absorb carbon dioxide and form a used sorbent; introducing the hydrogen, steam and used sorbent from the sorbent enhanced reformer to a gas/solids separator to separate the hydrogen and steam from the used sorbent; introducing at least a portion of the separated hydrogen and steam to at least one power generator to produce power; introducing at least a portion of the separated used sorbent to a calciner to heat the used sorbent to desorb carbon dioxide from the used sorbent to produce regenerated sorbent and carbon dioxide; and recycling at least a portion of the regenerated sorbent to the sorbent enhanced reformer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view for a system for converting natural gas assets to electric power, hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
   Steam (234)
   Gas/solids separator (239)
   Steam (260)
   Calciner (272)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The feed material comprises a methane-containing fuel and steam which comprises natural gas. The system additionally comprises a desulfurizer to remove sulfur from the natural gas prior to passage to the compact hydrogen generator. Preferred Conditions: The compact hydrogen generator operates at a pressure of at least 35 pound-force per square inch to produce the hydrogen, carbon dioxide and stream. The calcines heats the used sorbent to desorb high purity carbon dioxide from the used sorbent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - Preparation of amino-modified organosilicon film for separation of carbon dioxide and nitrogen mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method effectively controls the pore size of film, which is verified by measuring the amount of nitrogen adsorption and carbon dioxide adsorption heat and gas permeability, and produces amino-modified organosilicon film with adjustable microstructure, and excellent carbon dioxide capture performance and affinity to carbon dioxide molecules.

PN KR2254172-B1
TI Producing dry ice using calcium carbonate generated from waste gas comprises discharging calcium carbonate reaction product produced by mixing calcium hydroxide with carbon dioxide contained in combustion gas generated during combustion.
AU JEONG C
AE JEONG C (JEON-Individual)
GA 202153434M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing dry ice using calcium carbonate generated from waste gas comprises discharging calcium carbonate (CaCO3) reaction product produced by mixing calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2) with carbon dioxide (CO2) contained in combustion gas generated during combustion in an industrial boiler, sintering and separating into calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2) and then compressing the separated carbon dioxide (CO2), where calcium carbonate (CaCO3) is a mixture of carbon dioxide (CO2) with calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2), mixing sodium hydroxide as a reaction sustaining agent and magnesium oxide as a reaction retarding agent to create an absorbent, and mixing an amine-based promoter with the absorbent to create and separate calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing dry ice using calcium carbonate generated from waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is eco-friendly, recycles resource, and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium carbonate is limited to light calcium carbonate. The mixture of 95-99.5 wt.% absorbent is used with 0.5-5 wt.% amine-based promoter. The amine-based promoter is one of ethanolamine, triethanolamine, piperazine, and triethylamine. Preferred Method: The composition is obtained by mixing calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2), magnesium oxide (MgO), and sodium hydroxide (NaOH) into carbon dioxide (CO2) contained in the combustion gas generated and discharged during combustion in an industrial boiler is added to produce reaction product of calcium carbonate. The method comprises separating calcium carbonate (CaCO3) into calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2), when size of the calcium carbonate is 50 mm for 14 hours 53 minutes at 925 degrees C or 7 hours 30 minutes at 950 degrees C, 3 hours at 1000 degrees C, baking at 55 minutes or 1 hour 50 minutes at 1100 degrees C or 1 hour and 13 minutes at 1200 degrees C. The method comprises separating calcium carbonate (CaCO3) into calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2), when the size of the calcium carbonate is 30 mm for 7 hours 52 minutes at 925 degrees C or 3 hours 30 minutes at 950 degrees C, 1 hour at 1000 degrees C, baking in either time 55 minutes or 1 hour at 1100 degrees C or 38 minutes at 1200 degrees C. The method comprises separating when calcium carbonate (CaCO3) into calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2), when size of the calcium carbonate is 20 mm, 4 hours 53 minutes at 925 degrees C or 2 hours 05 minutes at 950 degrees C, baking at 1000 degrees C for 1 hour and 10 minutes, or at 1100 degrees C for 40 minutes or at 1200 degrees C for 24 minutes. The method comprises calcining calcium carbonate (CaCO3) and separating into calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2), producing separated carbon dioxide (CO2) by compressing separated carbon dioxide (CO2) at an absolute pressure of 1 atm below -78 degrees C. The amine-based promoter is obtained by combining 1.5-2.3 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. absorbent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).)

   USE - Reaction process for preparing 1,3-butadiene by dehydrogenation of 1-butene by carbon dioxide oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the carbon deposition of catalyst in the reaction process, leads the catalyst to regenerate, removes the reaction heat in high temperature reaction, produces fine-tuning effect on surface appearance of catalyst, and improves the carbon deposition resistance, reaction activity and stability of catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves performing oxidative dehydrogenation reaction at 550-650 degrees C under reaction pressure of 0-0.3 MPa. The mass space velocity based on 1-butene is 1.5-10/hour. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 1-butene raw material and carbon dioxide is 1:(2-13).
