PN KR2020066039-A; KR2232071-B1
TI Manufacturing sintered ore, comprises e.g. reacting mineral containing wollastonite with acetic acid, capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas, and producing calcium carbonate by reacting carbon dioxide with residue.
AU JI Y K
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
GA 202052976D
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing sintered ore, comprises reacting the mineral containing wollastonite with acetic acid to obtain silicon dioxide, capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas, and producing calcium carbonate by reacting the carbon dioxide with a residue produced by the reaction of the mineral and acetic acid; or mixing raw materials with an auxiliary raw material, a fuel material and semi-glossy, preparing pseudo-particles by adding and mixing the silicon dioxide and the calcium carbonate to the mixed raw material, auxiliary material, fuel material, and semi-gloss and sintering the pseudo-particles.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing sintered ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce the emission of carbon dioxide by utilizing silicon dioxide and calcium carbonate; and has the effect of improving the sintering productivity and the quality index (recovery rate, binder material cost), thus improving the strength and sintering reactivity of the finely divided granules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step of obtaining the silicon dioxide, the recovered acetic acid is used for reaction with minerals. The step of obtaining the silicon dioxide comprises a reaction comprising reacting one mole of wollastonite with 2 moles of acetic acid to obtain calcium ion, one mole of acetic acid, one mole of water and one mole of silicon dioxide. The method further comprises collecting the carbon dioxide from flue gas generated from at least one processes in sintering process, blast furnace process and coke process. The step of obtaining the calcium carbonate comprises reaction comprising reacting one mole of calcium ion, one mole of acetic acid, one mole of water and one mole of carbon dioxide to obtain two moles of acetic acid and one mole of calcium carbonate. The method further comprises preparing the pellets by agitating the silicon dioxide and the calcium carbonate with pulverized ore prior to the step of preparing the pseudo-particles. The pulverized ore has an average particle size of 4 mm or less. The pellet is 1-8 mm. The pseudo-particles include 5-20 wt.% pellets. The pseudo-particles are sintered at 1000-1400 degrees C for 10-60 minutes. The method comprises collecting carbon dioxide from the flue gas generated in the process after sintering the pseudo-particles, obtaining the calcium carbonate, the captured carbon dioxide reacting with the residue.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27))

PN CN111036077-A
TI Preparation of high-abundance carbon-13 isotope using ethylene involves feeding ethylene in gas diffusion cascade, fractionating in gas diffusion cascades with carbon dioxide, and combining gas diffusion cascades.
AU ZHOU M
   JIANG D
   JIAN C
   PAN J
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 202034716D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-abundance carbon-13 isotope involves feeding natural abundance of ethylene feedstock in gas diffusion cascade (C1), obtaining ethylene at end of heavy fraction of cascade (C1) with carbon-13 isotope abundance 45% or more, fully burning to obtain carbon dioxide with carbon 13 isotope abundance of 45% or more, feeding with ethylene into gas diffusion cascade (C2), performing fractionation of heavy fraction of gas diffusion cascade (C2) to obtain carbon dioxide with carbon-13 isotope abundance 99% or more, and combining gas diffusion cascade (C1) and (C2).
   USE - Preparation of high-abundance carbon-13 isotope using ethylene and carbon dioxide as medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high efficiency, and uses relatively low molecular weight of ethylene and carbon dioxide, has relatively large gas diffusion separation coefficient, and relatively few required cascade stages. No other impurities are introduced during process of gas diffusion separation, and purity is high.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The total number of gas diffusion cascade (C1) is 650-850. The flow rate of gas diffusion cascade (C1) heavy fraction is 0.001- 0.005 times feed flow rate. The feed grade is located at a position of 600-800 grades from end of heavy fraction. The total flow of gas diffusion cascade (C1) is 8000-12000 times of feed flow. The total number of gas diffusion cascade (C2) is 1100-1300. The feed grade is located at a position of 800-1000 grades from the end of the heavy fraction. The flow rate of gas diffusion cascade (C2) heavy fraction is 0.25-0.3 times of feed flow rate. The total flow of gas diffusion cascade (C2) is 180000-220000 times of feed flow. Preferred Composition: The carbon-13 isotope abundance in light fraction of gas diffusion cascade (C1) is 1% or less. The carbon-13 isotope abundance in light fraction gas diffusion cascade (C2) is 25-35%.

PN WO2022160060-A1
TI Oxy-fuel combustion power generation system for            use in power plant, has gas turbine for oxy-fuel            combustion of fuel, and heat exchanger for transferring            heat from gas turbine to closed supercritical steam            turbine cycle.
AU ESCOBAL L J C
   GEORGAKIS M
   GREEN E J
AE INDUSTRIASYS CORP (INDU-Non-standard)
GA 2022994617
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a gas turbine for                oxy-fuel combustion of a fuel stream (105), an                oxygen stream (102) and a recycle stream (126) to                form an exhaust stream (113). A separator separates                the exhaust stream into a water-rich stream and                carbon dioxide-rich streams, where a portion of the                carbon dioxide rich stream is divided to form the                recycle stream. An air separation unit receives                atmospheric air, and generates the oxygen and                nitrogen streams from the atmospheric air. A boiler                generates supercritical steam, and a steam turbine                is powered with the steam. A condenser condenses                the stream before the separator.The fuel stream                comprises biofuel from waste gasification.
   USE - Oxy-fuel combustion power generation system                for removing carbon dioxide from atmospheric air in                power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The system leads elimination of nitrogen                oxides, capture of carbon dioxide, and high                efficiencies for the power plant. The carbon                dioxide can be separated and removed from                combustion gases due to the high composition and an                almost binary mixture case. The system enables                providing oxygen-based combustion as the                oxygen-based combustion has higher temperatures                than air based combustion, since the nitrogen                content is missing in the oxygen based processes.                The system allows for higher temperatures at the                inlet of gas turbines, and higher temperature at                the exit of gas turbine thus resulting in higher                gas turbines and higher steam turbine efficiency                for generating electricity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a method for removing carbon dioxide from                atmospheric air; and
   (2) an apparatus for removing carbon dioxide                from atmospheric air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a power                generation system including a gas turbine, a closed                supercritical steam turbine cycle and separation                and pipelining of produced carbon dioxide.

   Fuel stream (105)

PN WO2021255422-A1
TI Apparatus used for forming alcohol and/or carboxylic acid, consists of dielectric barrier discharge device comprising water electrode and passageway having catalyst comprising nickel and/or cobalt and/or copper, which extends at least in portion through dielectric barrier discharge device.
AU TU X
   WANG Y
AE UNIV LIVERPOOL (UYLI-C)
GA 2021E8084D
AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus consists of a dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) device arranged to generate a plasma and a passageway including an inlet for the carbon dioxide and the hydrogen and an outlet for 1-5C alcohol, carboxylic acid, and having a catalyst comprising nickel and/or cobalt and/or copper on a support therein. The passageway extends at least in portion, through the dielectric barrier discharge device. In use, carbon dioxide is exposed to the catalyst in the presence of the hydrogen in the generated plasma, thereby forming 1-5C alcohol and/or carboxylic acid from at least some of the carbon dioxide and the hydrogen. The DBD device comprises a water electrode.
   USE - Apparatus used for forming 1-5C alcohol and/or carboxylic acid chosen from methanol, ethanol, propane, methanoic acid and ethanoic acid, from carbon dioxide and hydrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus has high carbon dioxide conversion, high selectivity of 1-5C alcohol and/or carboxylic acid, high yield of 1-5C alcohol and/or carboxylic acid, and energy efficiency. The method of carbon dioxide hydrogenation using the device, does not require additional heating and can be conducted at ambient pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) formation of 1-5C alcohol and/or carboxylic acid from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, which involves generating the plasma using the dielectric barrier discharge device and exposing the carbon dioxide in the presence of hydrogen to the catalyst comprising copper and/or nickel and/or cobalt on support in the generated plasma; and
   (2) catalyst, which comprises nickel and/or cobalt and/or copper on a support.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a structure of the dielectric barrier discharge plasma-catalytic reactor using water electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises 1-20 wt.%, preferably 1-9 wt.% metallic nickel and/or 1-20 wt.%, preferably 1-9 wt.% cobalt and/or 1-20 wt.% copper based on weight of the support. The catalyst comprises 10 wt.% bimetallic nickel-cobalt and/or 10 wt.% copper based on the weight of the support. The support comprises fumed and mesoporous silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide, aluminum oxide, cerium oxide, zirconium oxide, zinc oxide, chromium oxide, carbon nanotubes, gallium oxide, indium oxide and ZSM-5. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1:2-1:4. Preferred Process: The exposing of carbon dioxide to the catalyst in the presence of hydrogen in the generated plasma is performed by exposing the carbon dioxide to the catalyst in the presence of hydrogen in the generated plasma at approximately ambient temperature, preferably 18-40 degrees C. The generation time of the stable plasma using the DBD device is 1-60 minutes, preferably 4-20 minutes. The method involves activating the catalyst using the generated plasma by supplying a specific energy input of 5-100 kJ/L, preferably 15-40 kJ/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The device has selectivity of methanol of at least 30%, preferably at least 60%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN112717914-A
TI Catalyst composite carrier used for preparation of catalyst for methane carbon dioxide reforming, is formed by compounding pyrochlore and magnesia-aluminum spinel, where pyrochlore is zirconium lanthanide oxide.
AU XUE H
   WANG H
   JIANG J
   YU H
   ZHAO Q
   BAI Z
   LIANG W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   QILU BRANCH CO CHINA PETROCHEMICAL CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021473127
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst composite carrier is formed by compounding pyrochlore and magnesia-aluminum spinel, where the pyrochlore is a zirconium lanthanide oxide having general chemical formula of Zr2Ln2O7, and Ln is a lanthanide element.
   USE - The catalyst composite carrier is used for preparation of a catalyst for methane carbon dioxide reforming, and the catalyst is used in the preparation of synthesis gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst composite carrier improves the anti-sintering and anti-carbon deposition performance and strength of the methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst by improving the performance of the catalyst composite carrier.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a preparation method of the catalyst composite carrier, involving (a) adding alkali to the solution containing magnesium salt and aluminum salt to make magnesium ions and aluminum ions to form a hydrotalcite-like semi-finished product to form solution (A), and (b) adding a precipitant to the solution containing zirconium salt and lanthanide salt to make the zirconium ion and the lanthanide ion form a pyrochlore precursor to form solution (B), mixing solution (A) and solution (B), using the hydrotalcite-like restructuring effect to combine the hydrotalcite-like semi-finished product with the pyrochlore precursor to form a carrier semi-finished product, and calcining the carrier semi-finished product to obtain a catalyst composite carrier;
   (2) a catalyst for methane carbon dioxide reforming, comprising the above-mentioned catalyst composite carrier which supports nickel oxide on the surface;
   (3) a preparation method of the catalyst for methane carbon dioxide reforming, involving immersing the above-mentioned catalyst composite carrier in a nickel salt solution, and calcining the impregnated catalyst composite carrier; and
   (4) use of the catalyst for methane carbon dioxide reforming in the preparation of synthesis gas, involving using methane and carbon dioxide as raw materials, and preparing the synthesis gas under the catalysis of the catalyst for methane carbon dioxide reforming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of magnesium to aluminum is 0.5-2. The catalyst comprises 6-20 %mass nickel oxide, and remaining the catalyst composite carrier. Preferred Method: The method includes: dissolving magnesium salt and aluminum salt in water, and adding sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide to obtain solution (A); dissolving zirconium salt and lanthanide salt in water, and adding precipitation agent to obtain solution (B); and mixing the solution (A) and solution (B), adjusting the pH to alkaline, heating to not less than 60 degrees C for aging, calcining the aged precipitate, adding a binder, kneading, granulating, extruding, and forming. Preferred Conditions: The immersion time is 6-24 hours. The calcining of the impregnated catalyst composite carrier is performed at 600-850 degrees C for 2-4 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The volume ratio of carbon dioxide to methane is (1.4-1.6):1, where the reaction pressure is 0.1-10 MPa, and the reaction temperature is 700-850 degrees C.

PT P
PN KR2021033744-A
TI Cathode composition of solid oxide electrolytic cell for decomposing carbon dioxide.
AU BAE K W
   PARK S M
AE POSTECH ACAD-IND FOUND (UYPO-C)
GA 202132926W
AB    NOVELTY - Cathode composition of solid oxide electrolytic cell comprises metal oxide based compound (I) and metal nanoparticles.
   USE - The cathode composition of solid oxide electrolytic cell is useful for decomposing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The cathode composition prevents phenomena such as coking and grain coarsening at a driving temperature, discharges carbon dioxide stably without deterioration in performance, increases the efficiency and durability of an electrolytic cell for decomposing carbon dioxide; cathode composition has a structure capable of having high efficiency and high durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cathode composition of solid oxide electrolytic cell for decomposing carbon dioxide, comprises metal oxide based compound of formula (A12B1O4-a) (I) and metal nanoparticles.
   A1 = lanthanide or alkaline earth metal, preferably La, Pr, Nd, Y, Sc, Gd, Mg, Ca, Sr, or Ba;
   B1 = transition metals or rare earth elements, preferably Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Co, Ni, Cu, Ru, Rh or Pd; and
   a = 0 or 1 which makes the compound (I) electrically neutral.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) solid oxide electrolytic cell for decomposing carbon dioxide, comprising cathode that decomposes carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide, an anode positioned symmetrically with the cathode and generating oxygen, located between the cathode and the anode and includes an electrolyte through which oxygen ions move, and cathode is a cathode composition; and
   (2) producing composition for a cathode of a solid oxide electrolytic cell for decomposing carbon dioxide, comprising dissolving metal precursors in a solvent to obtain a solution, evaporating a solvent from the precursor solution to obtain a solid, heat-treating the solid material in air to obtain a solid material from which organic compounds have been removed, heat-treating the solid material from which the organic compound has been removed in air to obtain a compound, and reducing heat-treated compound to obtain the composition. The metal precursors include A1 and B1 compounds in stoichiometric ratio for producing metal oxide based compound of formula (A12B1O3-a) (II).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram showing a structure of a Ludlessden paper of one of the materials constituting the cathode of the solid oxide electrolytic cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound of (I) has Rudlesden Popper structure. The metal nanoparticles comprises the elements of B in (I). The cathode composition is a composite of metal nanoparticles eluted in a Ludlessden paper structure in which carbon dioxide is electrolyzed to generate carbon monoxide. The anode is made of lanthanide-strontium-cobalt-iron oxide (La0.6Sr0.4Co0.2Fe0.8O3)-gadolinia doped ceria (GDC). The electrolyte is at least one of yttria stabilized zirconia (YSZ), scandia stabilized zirconia (ScSZ), Samaria doped ceria (SDC), gadolinia doped ceria (GDC), strontium and magnesium doped lanthanum gallate (LSGM). The electrolyte is made of pellets having a thickness of 300-500 mu m. The compound of (II) is reduced to form metal nanoparticles eluted with the compound of (I). The compound of (II) is lanthanide-strontium-cobalt-manganese oxide (La0.6Sr0.4Co0.7Mn0.3O3), La0.6Sr0.4Co0.5Ni0.2Mn0.3O3, La0.6Sr0.4Co0.2Fe0.8O3 or La0.6Sr0.4Co0.4Fe0.3Mn0.3O3.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112031734-A; CN212508245-U
TI Smart multi-level partitioned fractured reservoir storage system for carbon-dioxide, has conveying hose that is connected with inner pipe of crack-causing sealing pipe close to L-shaped connecting pipe.
AU JIANG S
   WANG H
   ZHANG T
   YU X
   CHEN L
   WANG B
   XUE S
   TANG R
   WANG W
   SHANG Q
   LI C
   LIU S
   JIANG C
AE SHAANXI YANCHANG PETROLEUM GROUP CO LTD (SXYP-C)
GA 2020C39965
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a multi-way pipeline (3) that is connected to a crack-causing sealing pipe (5) through an L-shaped connecting pipe (4) in a sealed manner. A pump station (16) is connected to one end of the multi-way piping away from the L-shaped connecting pipe. One end of an outer tube of the sealing pipe close to the L-shaped connecting pipe is sealed and provided with a sealing separating device. The pump station provided with a liquid carbon dioxide pump and a circulating liquid nitrogen pump. The liquid carbon dioxide pump and the circulating liquid nitrogen pump are respectively connected with a conveying hose (1) through a control switch (14). The conveying hose extends into the multi-way pipeline and passes through an L-shaped connecting pipe, and is connected with an inner pipe of the crack-causing sealing pipe close to the L-shaped connecting pipe. A flow sand pump is connected with the multi-way pipeline through the control switch.
   USE - Smart multi-level partitioned fractured reservoir storage system for carbon-dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses liquid carbon dioxide to instantaneously gasify and expand to generate high pressure. The compact rock formation with complex permeability difference of the geological condition is loosened. The injection flow sand is used for secondary cracking under the low temperature action of liquid nitrogen, so that the permeability of the rock formation is increased. The flowing carbon dioxide realizes the purpose of oil displacement. The system greatly reduces investment of manpower and material resources, improves extraction rate of crude oil, and realizes underground sealing of carbon dioxide, to achieve the purpose of green oil extraction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating a structure of the smart multi-level partitioned fractured reservoir storage system for carbon-dioxide.

   Control switch (14)
   Pump station (16)

PN US2020284189-A1
TI Method for operation of power generation plant, involves combusting fuel from fuel stream with oxygen from oxygen stream in combustor in presence of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide stream to form combustion exhaust gas.
AU IWAI Y
   ITOH M
   SUZUKI S
   MORISAWA Y
   FETVEDT J E
   ALLAM R J
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 202087941N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a fuel flow rate detecting unit output signal, an oxygen flow rate detecting unit output signal, and a carbon dioxide flow rate detecting unit output signal to a control unit (60). One of a required fuel flow rate, a required oxygen flow rate, and a required carbon dioxide flow rate is calculated in the control unit based upon the output signals received from multiple fuel flow rate detecting unit (50,51,52,53), the oxygen flow rate detecting unit, and the carbon dioxide flow rate detecting unit. The flow of one of a fuel stream, an oxygen stream, and a carbon dioxide stream is regulated. The fuel is combusted from the fuel stream with the oxygen from the oxygen stream in the combustor (20) in the presence of the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide stream to form a combustion exhaust gas, and the combustion exhaust gas is passed through a turbine (21) to generate power.
   USE - Method for operation of power generation plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry combustion gas flowing through the pipe obtains in the heat exchanger a heat quantity from the combustion gas exhausted from the turbine and is heated. The flow rate of dry combustion gas (working fluid) flows into the combustor through the pipe increases. The flame temperature increases and the equilibrium composition value of carbon monoxide in the combustion area increases. By structuring the piping such that the position to mix oxidant and dry combustion gas is on the upstream side of the heat exchanger, the occurrence of excessive stress in the pipe of the mixing portion and the increase in facility costs can be prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system diagram of a gas turbine facility.
   Gas turbine facility (10)
   Combustor (20)
   Turbine (21)
   Fuel flow rate detecting unit (50,51,52,53)
   Control unit (60)

PN CN108753464-A
TI Transferring and extracting natural flavors comprises e.g. soaking fresh orange peel in water, rinsing with flowing water, draining, pulverizing, pressing, adding baking soda and sodium sulfate solution, adjusting pH and extracting.
AU LI X
   FU H
   XU X
   WANG J
   LI Q
   ZHU H
   FU J
AE JIANGXI SPICE CHEM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018885413
AB    NOVELTY - Transferring and extracting natural flavors comprises soaking fresh orange peel in water for 3-5 hours, rinsing soaked fresh orange peel with flowing water, draining, pulverizing into 3-5 mm size, pressing, adding 0.25 wt.% baking soda and 5 wt.% sodium sulfate solution in orange peel, adjusting pH to 7-8 to obtain oil-water mixed solution, adding alum in oil-water mixed solution, filtering to obtain mixture, centrifuging at 6000-8000 revolutions/minute to obtain crude oil, dissolving crude oil in organic solvent to obtain mixed oil, extracting mixed oil in device for transfer and extracting natural flavors, placing refined oil under 50-100 degrees C condition, allowing to stand for 5-7 hours, sucking upper clear oil layer, and performing vacuum filtration through a funnel padded with filter paper or asbestos paper to obtain orange essential oil.
   USE - The method is useful for transferring and extracting natural flavors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device comprising a carbon dioxide injection pump, a first extractor (20), a second extractor (30), a separator (40), a compressor (60), a carbon dioxide storage tank (10), and an exhaust gas storage tank. The compressor provides a high pressure environment for the entire system. The mixed oil is placed in the first extractor and the second extractor. The carbon dioxide injection pump pumps carbon dioxide gas stored in the carbon dioxide storage tank into the first extractor and second extractor which forms a carbon dioxide gas saturated with solute and flows into the separator. The separator rapidly separates carbon dioxide from the dissolved matter by pressure provided by the compressor immediately.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device.
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (10)
   First extractor (20)
   Second extractor (30)
   Separator (40)
   Compressor (60)

The orange essential oil is stored in a dark glass bottle and is far from optical fiber, heat and humidity. The organic solvent is benzene, acetone, dichloromethane, trichloromethane, toluene, ethylbenzene, chlorobenzene, xylene, acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran, dioxane, diethylene glycol, polydiethylene glycol dimethyl ether, polydiethylene glycol diethyl ether and/or N-dimethylformamide. Preferred Method: The working temperature of device is 35-40 degrees C.

PN EP3366670-A1; WO2018153630-A1; CA3050197-A1; AU2018224747-A1; IN201947037951-A; CN110382460-A; US2019375649-A1; EP3585767-A1; BR112019017604-A2; US10927012-B2; EP3585767-B1; RU2019129593-A; IN378285-B; CN110382460-B; RU2773193-C2
TI Removing ammonia from ammonia-containing purge gas of urea plant involves contacting purge gas with carbon dioxide, refrigerating purge gas or carbon dioxide, forming ammonium salts and mixed stream, and removing salts from mixed stream.
AU RUGNONE L
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE CO LTD (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2018674282
AB    NOVELTY - Removing ammonia from ammonia-containing purge gas (5) of urea plant (4) involves contacting ammonia-containing purge gas with a stream of carbon dioxide (26), refrigerating the purge gas or carbon dioxide or mixture of purge gas and carbon dioxide resulting from contacting process, reacting at least some of ammonia contained in the purge gas in the refrigerated mixture to form ammonium salt(s) and multiphase mixed stream containing the ammonium salts, and removing ammonium salts from the mixed stream.
   USE - Removing ammonia from ammonia-containing purge gas of urea plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides an efficient and cost-effective system for removing ammonia from the inert-gas containing purge gas vented from urea plant. The process accepts higher ammonia load. The captured ammonia can be recycled after washing into the urea process without introducing large amount of water or the ammonium salts can be separated and disposed as solid, eliminating the need to recycle to the urea process. Recycling of the aqueous stream to the urea plant reduces the total clean water amount needed for scrubbing ammonia. The solid phase containing ammonia is captured with inexpensive techniques such as filter or scrubber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for ammonia capture system, which comprises mixer (11) into which the purge gas and carbon dioxide are introduced, at least one of refrigerator for the purge gas, refrigerator for adding refrigerant medium to mixing device, and phase-separation device arranged to receive mixed stream from the mixer and adapted to remove ammonium salts from mixed stream. The mixer delivers multiphase mixed stream containing solid ammonium salts.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of urea plant including an ammonia capture system.
   Carbon dioxide feed compressor (2)
   Urea plant (4)
   Ammonia-containing purge gas (5)
   Purge gas streams (5a,5b,5c)
   Secondary stream (10)
   Mixer (11)
   Mixed stream (12)
   Scrubber (13)
   Stream of carbon dioxide (26)
   Inter-refrigeration stage (27)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: Purge gas and/or carbon dioxide is refrigerated to a temperature lower than the temperature of the input ammonia-containing purge gas. The mixed stream has a temperature equal to or less than 10 degrees C, preferably 10 degrees C to -30 degrees C. The ammonia-containing purge gas is contacted with carbon dioxide at a pressure of 1-10 bar abs, preferably 1-2 bar abs. The temperature of refrigerated carbon dioxide (26) is equal to or less than -10 degrees C, preferably -25 degrees C to -65 degrees C. The refrigerated carbon dioxide is obtained by expanding secondary stream (10) of carbon dioxide having a pressure of 65-85 bar and a temperature of 40-80 degrees C after inter-refrigeration (27), and is expanded to atmospheric pressure. The refrigerant medium has a temperature equal to or less than -10 degrees C. Preferred Process: The refrigerated carbon dioxide is obtained by separating a secondary gaseous stream of carbon dioxide from an inter-refrigeration stage (27) of a carbon dioxide feed compressor (2) of urea plant, and expanding the secondary stream of carbon dioxide to a lower pressure in order to lower its temperature. During or after contacting ammonia-containing purge gas and carbon dioxide, the refrigerant medium is added. The process further involves contacting ammonia-containing purge gas and the carbon dioxide in a suitable absorber, and injecting refrigerant medium into the absorber, so that the refrigerant medium comes into direct contact with the purge gas and carbon dioxide. The ammonium salts are removed from mixed stream in a filter or by scrubbing process, preferably with water as scrubbing medium. The purge gas comprises purge streams taken from different sections of a urea plant. Preferred Apparatus: Alternately, the ammonia-containing purge gas and the refrigerated carbon dioxide are contacted in a gas ejector. Preferred Components: The ammonia-containing purge gas comprises purge streams taken from different sections of urea plant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The ammonium salts include ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbamate. The refrigerant medium is a solvent with low solubility of ammonia and carbon dioxide. Preferred Composition: The mixed stream (12) contains less than 10 ppm free gaseous ammonia.

TI Kit is prepared by uniformly mixing acidic            substance, carbon dioxide gas-generating substance that            generates carbon dioxide gas by reacting with acidic            substance, thickeners, and water.
AU AKIMOTO K
   YAMASHITA M
   TAKAGAKI K
AE TOYO SHINYAKU CO LTD (TOYS-C)
GA 202286516T
AB    NOVELTY - Kit is prepared by uniformly mixing acidic                substance, carbon dioxide gas-generating substance                that generates carbon dioxide gas by reacting with                acidic substance, thickeners, and water, where the                thickener comprises combination of tamarind gum and                chosen from carrageenan, hydroxyethyl cellulose and                carbomer, carrageenan and/or sodium                carboxymethylcellulose and/or combinations chosen                from hydroxyethyl cellulose and xanthan gum, sodium                carboxymethylcellulose and carbomer, the kit                further comprise (a) a solid composition (A)                comprising an acidic substance and a composition                (B) containing water, a thickener and a carbon                dioxide gas generating substance, (b) a solid                composition (C) containing an acidic substance and                a carbon dioxide gas generating substance, and a                composition (D) containing water and a thickener,                and (c) a solid composition (E) containing a carbon                dioxide gas generating substance and a composition                (F) containing water, a thickener and an acidic                substance.
   USE - The kit is used for preparing effervescent                external preparation for skin, promoting increase                in skin blood flow due to action of carbon dioxide                gas, improving skin quality, rejuvenating,                tightening, partially thinning and cleansing the                skin, preventing the rough skin, moisturizing,                supplementing and maintaining oil to keep the skin                soft, protecting skin, preventing dry skin,                softening skin, reducing wrinkles, rough skin,                rashes and cracking of the skin and preventing                sunburn, acne and oily skin, used for preparing                quasi-drugs and pharmaceuticals, such as medicines                for preventing hot flashes, used as cosmetics, such                as milky lotions, creams, packs and peeling agents,                and medicated cosmetics, and as facial mask with                excellent feeling of use and high skin condition                improving effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The kit increases the water content of the                skin, reduces the water evaporation amount,                improves the skin elasticity, ensures excellent                usability, safety and stability such as ease of                mixing and application, has feeling of use (easy to                mix, smoothness of foam, fineness of foam, uniform                feeling of foam, ease of application, resistance to                dripping, elasticity of foam and carbonic acid                feeling) to excellent feeling of use after use                (skin moisturizing, elasticity, brightness,                redness, smoothness and tightness), when used on                scalp and hair, the effect of imparting glossiness                and smoothness of hair, elasticity and moisturizing                feeling to the scalp is enhanced, promotes the                proliferation action and hair growth of dermal                papilla cells, and prepares the skin with excellent                texture and healthy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Kit is prepared by uniformly mixing acidic                substance, carbon dioxide gas-generating substance                that generates carbon dioxide gas by reacting with                acidic substance, thickeners, and water, where the                thickener comprises combination of tamarind gum and                chosen from carrageenan, hydroxyethyl cellulose and                carbomer, carrageenan and/or sodium                carboxymethylcellulose, combinations chosen from                hydroxyethyl cellulose and carbomer, and/or                combinations chosen from hydroxyethyl cellulose and                xanthan gum, sodium carboxymethylcellulose and                carbomer, and the kit further comprise (a) a solid                composition (A) comprising an acidic substance and                a composition (B) containing water, a thickener and                a carbon dioxide gas generating substance, (b) a                solid composition (C) containing an acidic                substance and a carbon dioxide gas generating                substance, and a composition (D) containing water                and a thickener, and (c) a solid composition (E)                containing a carbon dioxide gas generating                substance and a composition (F) containing water, a                thickener and an acidic substance, and the                thickeners (B), (D) and (F) excluded from following                combinations of sodium alginate with xanthan gum,                cellulose gum, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose,                carbomer, polyvinyl alcohol or bentonite                combination, xanthan gum with cellulose gum,                hydroxypropyl methylcellulose or bentonite                combination, or cellulose gum with hydroxypropyl                methylcellulose or carbomer combination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The kit further                  comprises a polyhydric alcohol.    MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

TI Nitrogen-free gas glass kiln oxygen and carbon            dioxide circulating combustion system comprises oxygen            preparation device, circulating flue gas carbon dioxide            recycling device, oxygen-enriched mixing device, and            raw material feeding device.
AU WU W
   ZHANG X
   LIU Q
   ZHU H
   ZHANG Y
   YUAN J
   JIANG H
   WANG F
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   SHANGHAI YUANHAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E9082F
AB    (CN215102818-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a nitrogen-free gas                glass kiln oxygen + CO2 circulating combustion                system. using CO2 in the circulating flue gas of                oxygen and glass furnace to obtain rich oxygen as                combustion improver, for oxygen-enriched combustion                of glass kiln, the nitrogen-free gas glass kiln                oxygen + CO2 circulating combustion system has: an                oxygen preparation device for preparing oxygen as                oxygen source; circulating flue gas CO2 recycling                device, recycling the CO2 in the glass kiln tail                gas as the oxygen-enriched gas distribution; an                oxygen-enriched mixing device, the oxygen prepared                by the oxygen preparing device and the CO2 recycled                by the circulating flue gas CO2 recycling device                are conveyed to the oxygen-enriched mixing device                in the needed proportion, the oxygen-enriched                mixing device is mixed to form the oxygen-enriched                gas as the combustion improver of the glass kiln;                raw material feeding device, the raw material used                by the glass furnace is provided to the glass                furnace; glass kiln the oxygen-enriched gas formed                by the oxygen-enriched mixing device is provided to                the glass furnace.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                nitrogen-free gas glass furnace oxygen and carbon                dioxide circulating combustion method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the nitrogen-free gas glass kiln oxygen and                carbon dioxide circulating combustion                system.
   Oxygen preparation device (1)
   Circulating flue gas carbon dioxide recovery                device (2)
   Oxygen-enriched mixing device (4)
   Raw material feeding device (6)
   Glass kiln (7)
EA    NOVELTY - System comprises oxygen and carbon dioxide in                a circulating flue gas of a glass kiln are used to                prepare oxygen-enriched combustion agent for                oxygen-enriched combustion of glass kilns. An                oxygen preparation device (1) is used for preparing                oxygen as an oxygen source. A circulating flue gas                carbon dioxide recovery device (2) is used for                recovering the carbon dioxide in the tail gas of a                glass kiln (7) as an oxygen-enriched gas                distribution. In an oxygen-enriched mixing device                (4), the oxygen produced by the oxygen preparation                device and the carbon dioxide recovered by the                circulating flue gas carbon dioxide recovery device                are sent to the oxygen-enriched mixing device and                mixing in an oxygen-enriched mixing device to form                an oxygen-enriched gas for the glass kiln. A raw                material feeding device (6) supplies the raw                materials to the glass kiln and the oxygen-enriched                gas is used as a combustion accelerant to carry out                a combustion reaction with nitrogen-free fuel in                the glass kiln.
   USE - Used as nitrogen-free gas glass kiln oxygen                and carbon dioxide circulating combustion                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures that the optimization of                the combustion environment makes the temperature                distribution in the furnace more reasonable;                prolongs the service life of kilns and boilers;                shortens the heating time of the kiln; reduces the                defective rate and improves the yield; reduces the                requirement of the fuel quality; enables the use of                inferior fuel; ensures that the low-quality fuels                are inexpensive and easy to source and reduces the                overall energy cost of the product.

The                  light to dark ratio is 12:12. The micro algae are                  marine micro algae or freshwater micro algae. The                  micro algae comprise Botrytis, Dunaliella,                  Chlorella, Scenedesmus, Chlamydomonas                  reinhardtii,Crypthecodinium, Pavlova,                  Cylindrotheca, Nannochloris, Isochrysis,                  Phaeodactylum tricornutum, Spirulina,                  Botryococcus braunii and Nannochloropsis. The                  micro algae culture medium can be BG-11 culture                  medium, Tris-acetate-phosphate medium (TAP)                  culture medium or selenium culture medium.Preferred Method: The method comprises                  adding diethanolamine in the micro-algae culture                  medium and introducing carbon dioxide gas into                  the culture. The gas supply rate is 10-50                  ml/minute. The aeration time is 2-12 hours. The                  culture period of the micro algae is 14 days. The                  culture temperature is 25-35&#8451;.

The low-carbon olefin is ethylene and                  propylene.Preferred Method: The preparation of carbon                  coated component comprises treating component (1)                  with a gas mixture containing a gaseous organic                  compound under non-oxygen atmosphere to obtain                  the catalyst. The method comprises treating                  conditions of the mixed gas GHSV of 800-5000 h/1,                  temperature of 350-450&#176;C, pressure of                  0.1-4.0 MPa, and processing time of 1-5 hours.                  The method comprises adopting fixed bed reactor,                  performing hydrogenation reaction at reaction                  temperature to 460&#176;C380-420&#176;C,                  preferably reaction pressure of 0.5-7.0 MPa,                  where the mol ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen                  is 1:(0.4-3.0), GHSV is 1000-8000 h/1.

TI Preparing dry fracturing and acidification            integrated double thickening fracturing fluid,            comprises e.g. heating acid solution, adding thickening            agent, mixing with carbon dioxide dry fracturing fluid            thickening agent and gel breaking agent, mixing with            proppant e.g. ceramsite and pressurizing.
AU BAI X
   ZHENG Y
AE BEIJING AIPU POLYMERS TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022895185
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dry fracturing and acidification                integrated double thickening fracturing fluid,                comprises (1) performing heat preservation of acid                solution at 10-40&#8451;, adding thickening                agent, carbon dioxide dry fracturing fluid                thickening agent and gel breaking agent to obtain                thickening acid fracturing fluid, (2) adding carbon                dioxide dry fracturing fluid thickening agent into                the thickening acid fracturing fluid and mixing                uniformly, (3) adding proppant having designed sand                ratio, mixing the sand evenly, and pressurizing,                and (4) pressurizing liquid carbon dioxide of the                final integrated double-thickening fracturing fluid                to design pressure, combining with thickened acid                fracturing fluid obtained in step (3) and further                pressurizing, quickly mixing and heat exchanging to                obtain mixed liquid phase, and placing the carbon                dioxide dry fracturing fluid thickener into the                liquid carbon dioxide to dissolve and thicken                rapidly.
   USE - The integration double thickening fracturing                fluid is useful as drag reducing fluid or                sand-carrying fluid in oil and gas fields                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not need special closed sand                mixing equipment; can mix sand at normal pressure;                is convenient to prepare; ensures dry fracturing                and acidizing integrated fracturing for integrated                application of drag reduction and sand carrying;                effectively protects and fully transform                reservoirs; fully relieves formation damage and                restore formation permeability; has excellent                stimulation effect and high cost performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dry fracturing and acidification                integrated double thickening fracturing fluid,                comprises (1) performing heat preservation of acid                solution at 10-40&#8451;, adding certain amount                of thickening agent for thickening acid, carbon                dioxide dry fracturing fluid thickening agent and                gel breaking agent to obtain thickening acid                fracturing fluid, the final integrated double                thickening fracturing fluid contains 10-50 wt.%                thickening acid fracturing fluid, (2) adding                certain amount of carbon dioxide dry fracturing                fluid thickening agent into the thickening acid                fracturing fluid and mixing uniformly to obtain                reaction mixture, (3) adding proppant having                designed sand ratio into the reaction mixture,                mixing the sand evenly, pressurizing it to design                pressure, and (4) pressurizing 90-50 wt.% liquid                carbon dioxide of the final integrated                double-thickening fracturing fluid to design                pressure, combining with thickened acid fracturing                fluid obtained in step (3) and further pressurizing                to the design pressure, quickly mixing and heat                exchanging to obtain mixed liquid phase, the                temperature of the system reaches 0-30&#8451;,                and placing the carbon dioxide dry fracturing fluid                thickener into the liquid carbon dioxide to                dissolve and thicken rapidly to obtain final                integrated double-thickening fracturing                fluid.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   integrated double thickening fracturing fluid                prepared by above method.
   dry fracturing and acidifcation integrated                double-thickening fracturing process comprising                transporting the integrated double-thickening                fracturing fluid into a reservoir and shutting in a                well and closed for a certain period of time, and                breaking the gel and flowing back to complete the                fracturing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The integrated                  double-thickening fracturing fluid has                  temperature of 0-30&#8451;, density of                  0.8-1.1 g/cm3and pH of 1-4. In the dry fracturing                  and acidification method, the well shut-in and                  pressure-closing time is 1 hour to 7 days,                  preferably 2-24 hours.Preferred Components: The ratio of                  thickening acid fracturing fluid to liquid carbon                  dioxide in the final integrated double thickening                  fracturing fluid is 10-50:90-50 wt.%; the                  addition amount of thickening agent for                  thickening acid is 0.1-0.5% of the mass fraction                  of the thickening acid fracturing fluid, the mass                  fraction of the carbon dioxide dry fracturing                  fluid thickener in the liquid carbon dioxide                  phase is 0.1-0.5% when the final integrated                  double thickening fracturing fluid is used as a                  drag reducing fluid; the addition amount of                  thickening agent for thickening acid is 0.5-3% of                  the mass fraction of the thickening acid                  fracturing fluid, and the mass fraction of the                  carbon dioxide dry fracturing fluid thickener in                  the liquid carbon dioxide phase is 0.5-3% when                  the final integrated double thickening fracturing                  fluid is used as a sand-carrying fluid. The acid                  solution is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  phosphoric acid, formic acid, acetic acid and/or                  hydrofluoric acid having mass fraction of 5-35%                  as a single acid, or at least one acid form a                  mixed acid in any mass ratio as a multi-component                  acid. The liquid carbon dioxide is liquid carbon                  dioxide that is pressurized to 1.5-2.5 MPa by a                  pressure pump truck, cooled to -25 to                  -15&#8451;, and stored in a special tanker or                  storage tank, the source of the gaseous carbon                  dioxide includes fossils carbon dioxide captured                  after fuel combustion and/or carbon dioxide                  produced by carbon dioxide gas wells. The                  thickening acid thickener includes powder and/or                  emulsion thickening acid thickener. The carbon                  dioxide dry fracturing fluid thickener includes                  fluorine-containing carbon dioxide thickener,                  silicon-containing carbon dioxide thickener                  and/or hydrocarbon polymer thickener. The design                  ratio of thickening agent for thickening acid                  0.1-0.5 wt.% when used as drag reducing fluid and                  0.5-3 wt.% when used as sand-carrying fluid. The                  design ratio of the carbon dioxide dry fracturing                  fluid thickener is 0.1-0.5 wt.% and when used as                  drag reducing fluid 0.5-3 wt.% when used as a                  sand-carrying liquid. The gel breaker includes                  ammonium persulfate and/or capsule gel breaker,                  the design ratio of the gel breaker accounts for                  0.005-0.3 wt.% of the mass fraction of the final                  integrated double-thickening fracturing fluid.                  The proppant includes acid-resistant quartz sand,                  ceramsite and/or resin-coated sand. The designed                  sand ratio of the proppant is 0-10 wt.% when used                  as drag reducing fluid, and 5-45% when used as                  sand-carrying fluid. In step (3), the design                  pressure of the thickened acid fracturing fluid                  after pressurization is 10-40 MPa, preferably                  20-30 MPa. In step (4), the design pressure after                  the pressurization of the liquid carbon dioxide                  is 10-40 MPa, preferably 20-30 MPa, it is                  consistent with the design pressure after the                  viscosified acid fracturing fluid is pressurized.                  In step (4), the final pressure increase is as                  high as 40-120 MPa, preferably 50-70 MPa; the                  integrated double thickening fracturing fluid has                  viscosity of 1-20 mPa.s, drag reduction rate of                  &#8805; 50% and sand ratio of 0-10% when used                  as drag reducing fluid, the integrated                  double-thickening fracturing fluid has a                  viscosity of 20-200 mPa.s, sand ratio of 5-45%                  and flowback rate of &#8805; 50% when used as                  a sand-carrying fluid.

TI Device useful for producing sodium oxalate by electrolysis of carbon dioxide and water, comprises anode circulation loop, cathode circulation loop, crystallization evaporation system, and anode circulation loop.
AU LIU Z
   KANG P
   XING M
   ZHOU W
   QIAN J
   JIA J
   XIAO L
   WANG J
AE EAST CHINA ENG SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCE-C)
   CARBON ENERGY TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0465L
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises anode circulation loop, cathode circulation loop, and crystallization evaporation system. The anode circulation loop and the cathode circulation loop constitute a complete electrolysis system. The anode circulation ring comprises a sodium hydroxide feed pipe (11), an anode circulation tank (12), an anode circulation pump (13) and an anode electrolysis tank (14) arranged in series. The upper end of the anode circulation tank is provided with a first liquid inlet, the top of the anode circulation tank is provided with an air outlet, and the lower end of the anode circulation tank is respectively provided with a second liquid inlet mouth and outlet. The one end of the sodium hydroxide feed pipe is an inlet port, and the other end of the sodium hydroxide feed pipe is connected in parallel with the outlet end of the anode electrolysis cell to the anode cycle.
   USE - The device is useful for producing sodium oxalate by electrolysis of carbon dioxide and water (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises anode circulation loop, cathode circulation loop, and crystallization evaporation system. The anode circulation loop and the cathode circulation loop constitute a complete electrolysis system. The anode circulation ring comprises a sodium hydroxide feed pipe (11), an anode circulation tank (12), an anode circulation pump (13) and an anode electrolysis tank (14) arranged in series. The upper end of the anode circulation tank is provided with a first liquid inlet, the top of the anode circulation tank is provided with an air outlet, and the lower end of the anode circulation tank is respectively provided with a second liquid inlet mouth and outlet. The one end of the sodium hydroxide feed pipe is an inlet port, and the other end of the sodium hydroxide feed pipe is connected in parallel with the outlet end of the anode electrolysis cell to the anode cycle and first inlet of the canister. The second inlet port of the anode circulation tank is connected to the deionized water interface through a pipe. The outlet port of the anode circulation tank is connected to the inlet of the anode circulation pump, and the outlet conduit of the anode circulation pump is connected to the inlet of the anode electrolysis tank. The cathode circulation ring includes a carbon dioxide intake pipe, a gas-liquid mixer, a cathode electrolysis cell, a cathode circulation tank, and a cathode circulation pump disposed in series; the cathode circulation tank a lower end of the is provided with a first liquid outlet, and an upper end of the cathode circulation tank is respectively provided with a liquid inlet and a second liquid outlet. The one end of the carbon dioxide intake pipe is an intake port, and the other end of the pipe is connected to an intake valve of the gas-liquid mixer, and the outlet valve of the gas-liquid mixer is connected to the pipe. An inlet of the cathode electrolysis cell, the outlet pipe of the cathode electrolysis cell is connected to the liquid inlet of the cathode circulation tank, and the first liquid outlet port of the cathode circulation tank is connected. An inlet of a cathode circulation pump, an outlet conduit of the cathode circulation pump is connected to an inner cylinder of the gas-liquid mixer, and a second outlet port of the cathode circulation tank is connected to outside the cathode loop. A diaphragm is disposed between the anode electrolytic cell and the cathode electrolytic cell, and the anode electrolytic cell, the cathode electrolytic cell and the separator constitute an electrolytic cell. The membrane is a cation exchange membrane that allows free passage of cations and water molecules. The evaporative crystallization system comprises an acidification tank, degassing tank, transfer pump I, preheater, an effect evaporator, and a transfer pump II arranged in series. A second effect evaporator, a centrifuge and a dryer, and the two-effect evaporator and the one-effect evaporator are both forced circulation type evaporators. The centrifuge is a constant speed centrifuge. The dryer is a drum dryer; the feed port of the acidification tank is connected to the second outlet of the cathode circulation tank, a liquid port, an acid inlet port of the acidification tank is connected to an oxalic acid inlet, and an outlet pipe of the acidification tank is connected to an inlet of the degassing tank, the degassing tank and an outlet pipe connected to the inlet of the transfer pump I. The outlet port of the pre-heater is connected to the inlet of the evaporator, and the outlet of the evaporator is connected to the inlet of the pump II. The outlet pipe of the transfer pump II is connected to the inlet of the two-effect evaporator, and the outlet port of the two-effect evaporator is connected to the inlet of the centrifuge. The solid phase outlet pipe of the centrifuge is connected to the inlet of the dryer. A mother liquor circulation pump is further disposed between the centrifuge and the pre-heater, and an inlet conduit of the mother liquor circulation pump is connected to a liquid phase outlet of the centrifuge. The outlet end of the mother liquor circulation pump is connected in parallel with the outlet end of the delivery pump I to the inlet of the preheater during operation, the electrolysis system uses carbon dioxide gas as a raw material to catalyze the electrolysis of the sodium salt solution to produce a sodium oxalate solution, when the concentration of the sodium oxalate solution reaches 5-10%, the sodium oxalate solution is passed to a crystallization evaporation system for evaporation crystallization, when the sodium oxalate solution is introduced into the crystallization evaporation system, the acidification and degassing tank of the acidification tank are sequentially removed to remove the carbon dioxide gas, the first effect evaporator and the second effect evaporator are used to carry out the sodium oxalate solution, and crystallization evaporation, centrifuged to dry to obtain sodium oxalate crystal particles, where the sodium oxalate crystal particles contained 99.5% by mass of sodium oxalate. preparing sodium oxalate by electrolysis of carbon dioxide and water, comprising (i) introducing a sodium hydroxide solution into the anode circulation ring through the sodium hydroxide feed pipe and introducing deionized water into the anode circulation tank through the ion water interface, where the concentration of the sodium oxide solution is 0.1-10 mol/l to obtain circulating liquid; and (ii) turning on sequentially anode circulation pump and the cathode circulation pump, circulating the circulating liquid and flowing in the anode circulation loop and the cathode circulation loop.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device for producing sodium oxalate by electrolysis of carbon dioxide and water.
   Sodium hydroxide feed pipe (11)
   Anode circulation tank (12)
   Anode circulation pump (13)
   Anode electrolysis tank (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (iii) introducing a mixed gas into the gas-liquid mixer, where the volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 80-100%, and the carbon dioxide gas forms multiple of small bubbles uniformly distributed in the gas-liquid mixer in the circulating fluid, and with the flow of the circulating fluid, and distributing uniformly small bubbles formed by the carbon dioxide gas in the cathode circulating fluid; (iv) turning on the direct current power supply, and electrolyzing separately the electrolyte in the cathode electrolysis cell and the electrolyte in the anode electrolysis cell, where the electrolysis voltage is 1-30 V, the current is 0.1-5 A, and the pressure is 0.1-1 MPaG, respectively, an anode circulating electrolyte and a cathode circulating electrolyte, the electrolytic reaction formula in the anode electrolytic cell, where the main reaction formula: 4-hydroxyl radical -2 water + oxygen + 4 electron, side reaction formula: sodium hydroxide +carbon-di-oxide-disodium carbonate + water, transferring a certain amount of oxygen in the anode electrolysis tank to the outside from the gas outlet at the top of the anode circulation tank; where the obtained anode circulating electrolyte comprises sodium carbonate and sodium hydrogen carbonate, and the concentration of the sodium carbonate and sodium hydrogen-carbonate is 3-8%, and the pH of the anode circulating electrolyte is 8-12, the electrolytic reaction formula in the cathode electrolysis cell is: main reaction formula: 2 carbon-di-oxide+ 2 electron-oxalate-side reaction formula: 2 water + 2 electron-2 hydroxy radical+ hydrogen, the cathode circulating electrolyte comprises sodium oxalate and sodium hydroxide, and the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 2-5% and when the concentration of the sodium oxalate reaches 5-10%, the cathode circulating electrolyte is passed from the second outlet port of the cathode circulation tank to the crystallization evaporation system; (v) transferring the cathode circulating electrolyte flows out from the second liquid outlet of the cathode circulation tank to the acidification tank in the crystallization evaporation step, and adding pure oxalic acid to the acidification tank to obtain an acidified solution, and the acidification solution has a pH of 1-5 and the reaction formula is: sodium carbonate + oxalic acid + sodium oxalate + carbon dioxide + water; (vi) injecting the acidified solution into the degassing tank and stirring well, when the bubbles in the acidification solution disappear completely, discharging completely the carbon dioxide gas to obtain degassed sodium oxalate solution; (vii) starting the transfer pump I, passing the degassed sodium oxalate solution to the first effect evaporator, and introducing the low efficiency steam into the first effect evaporator, and evaporating the low pressure steam the crystallized sodium oxalate solution, where the first effect evaporator has a temperature of 100-140 degrees C and a pressure of 0.02 MPaG to obtain a one-effect supersaturated solution, the solid content of the first-effect supersaturated solution is 10-20%, the one-effect supersaturated solution comprises a sodium oxalate saturated solution and sodium oxalate crystal, and the concentration of the sodium oxalate saturated solution is 6%, generating the steam condensate by the first-effect evaporator passed through a preheater to preheat the sodium oxalate solution, and the condensate is sent to the external treatment after heat exchange, starting the transfer pump II, and passing the first-effect super-saturated solution to the second-effect evaporator, introducing simultaneously a secondary steam generated by the first effect evaporator to the two-effect evaporator, utilizing the secondary steam as a heating source of the two-effect evaporator, evaporating and crystallization of a one-effect supersaturated solution to produce a condensate for external treatment, where the temperature of the two-effect evaporator is 60-80 degrees C, and the pressure is -0.08 MPaG to obtain a two-way supersaturated solution, where the solid solution of the two-way supersaturated solution is 30-40%, the two-effect supersaturated solution comprises a saturated solution of sodium oxalate and sodium oxalate crystal, and the concentration of the saturated solution of sodium oxalate is 6%; (viii) passing the second-effect supersaturated solution into a centrifuge, where the speed of the centrifuge is 2000 revolutions/minute to obtain a saturated sodium oxalate solution and sodium oxalate crystals, turning on the mother liquor circulation pump, pumping the saturated sodium oxalate solution into the mother liquor circulation pump, passing through the outlet of the mother liquor circulation pump into the preheater, and preheating the saturated solution of sodium oxalate; and (ix) drying the sodium oxalate crystal by a dryer, and speed of the dryer is 1-2 revolutions/minute to obtain dried sodium oxalate crystal particles, where the sodium oxalate crystal particles comprises sodium oxalate and trace impurities, the mass fraction of sodium oxalate is 99.5%, the mass fraction of the trace impurities is 0.5%, and the trace impurities include sodium carbonate and sodium hydrogencarbonate.

TI Analyzing trace oxygen, argon, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and carbon dioxide in industrial hydrogen involves using gas chromatography column gas detection system, where the system includes a first A PQ pretreatment column.
AU CHENG Y
   MA J
   LI S
   ZHANG Q
   GENG L
   FENG X
   LIU X
AE XINJIANG XINYE ENERGY CHEM CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B7264A
AB    NOVELTY - Analyzing trace oxygen, argon, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and carbon dioxide in industrial hydrogen involves using gas chromatography column gas detection system, where the gas chromatography column gas detection system includes a first A PQ pretreatment column, the first molecular sieve analysis column, the second molecular sieve analysis column, the second PQ pretreatment column, the ten-way valve, the first six-way valve, second six-way valve, the first carrier gas pipe, the second carrier gas pipe, the third carrier gas pipe, and the thermal conductivity detector. The air ports of the ten-way valve, the first six-way valve and the second six-way valve are all ordered in a counterclockwise direction, where the one end of the first PQ pretreatment column is connected to the No. 5 port of the ten-way valve and the other end is connected to the No. 2 port of the ten-way valve.
   USE - Method for analyzing trace oxygen, argon, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and carbon dioxide in industrial hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to analyze trace oxygen, argon, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and carbon dioxide in industrial hydrogen solves the problems of relatively high oxygen content in the process, large deoxygenation load, high aging frequency of the deoxygenation column, and heavy maintenance workload.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Analyzing trace oxygen, argon, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and carbon dioxide in industrial hydrogen involves using gas chromatography column gas detection system, where the gas chromatography column gas detection system includes a first A PQ pretreatment column, the first molecular sieve analysis column, the second molecular sieve analysis column, the second PQ pretreatment column, the ten-way valve, the first six-way valve, second six-way valve, the first carrier gas pipe, the second carrier gas pipe, the third carrier gas pipe, and the thermal conductivity detector. The air ports of the ten-way valve, the first six-way valve and the second six-way valve are all ordered in a counterclockwise direction, where the one end of the first PQ pretreatment column is connected to the No. 5 port of the ten-way valve and the other end is connected to the No. 2 port of the ten-way valve. The one end of the first molecular sieve analysis column is connected to No. 6 port of the ten-port valve is connected, the other end is connected to the back TCD of the thermal conductivity detector, the first carrier gas pipe is connected to the No. 7 port of the ten-port valve, the second carrier gas pipe is connected to the No. 4 port of the ten-way valve. The one end of the second PQ pretreatment column is connected to the No. 4 port of the first six-way valve, and the other end is connected to the No. 2 port of the second six-way valve. The second molecular sieve analysis column one end is connected to the No. 3 port of the second six-way valve, and the other end is connected to the No. 4 port of the second six-way valve. The No. 5 port of the second six-way valve is connected to front detector (Front TCD) of the thermal conductivity detector and the third carrier gas pipe is connected to the No. 5 port of the first six-way valve. The sample gas enters from port in the initial state of the ten-port valve, flows out from port and enters the quantitative loop, and non-sample gas components enter port and pass through port. The gas port is discharged and vented to complete the sample gas replacement. The main component of the sample gas is hydrogen, and the impurity gasses includes oxygen, argon, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methane. The spool of the ten-way valve rotates 36 degrees clockwise, and the carrier gas enters the No. 7 port of the ten-way valve through the first carrier gas pipe. The number port flows out and enters the quantitative ring. The sample gas is carried by the carrier gas flows out of the quantitative loop to the No. 1 port, and entered the first PQ pretreatment column from the No. 2 port. The sample gas is separated into oxygen, nitrogen, and nitrogen in the first PQ pretreatment column, mixed component of carbon monoxide, single component of methane, single component of carbon dioxide. The mixed combination of oxygen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide and a single component of methane enter the ten-way valve from the No. 5 port is connected to the first PQ pretreatment column and entered the first port from the No. 6 port. The above two components all enter the first molecular sieve analysis column, the spool of the ten-way valve is rotated at 36 degrees counterclockwise to return to the initial state position, and the carbon dioxide component is remained in the first PQ pretreatment column. The carrier gas in the second carrier gas pipe is entered the ten-way valve through the No. 4 port, and entered the first PQ pretreatment column from the No. 5 port for back flushing. The carbon dioxide component is passed through and the first No. 2 port connected to the PQ pretreatment column entered the ten-way valve, and flow out from the No. 3 port to the atmosphere. The mixed combination of oxygen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide and the single component of methane are entered the first molecular sieve analysis column and separated into four single components of oxygen, nitrogen, methane, and carbon monoxide, and passes through a thermal conductivity detector. The back detector (Back TCD) is performed chromatographic peak quantification of each single component to determine the content of each component of oxygen, nitrogen, methane, and carbon monoxide. The sample gas enters from port in the initial state of the first six-way valve is passed out from port and entered the quantitative loop, where the sample gas replaces the non-sample gas components in the quantitative loop. The mixed combination of oxygen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide and a single component of methane successively flow out from the second PQ pretreatment column and enter the second six-way valve through the second six-way valve. No. 3 port enters the second molecular sieve analysis column. The spool of the second six-way valve is rotated to 60 degrees clockwise, and the above two components temporarily stay in the second molecular sieve analysis column. The second six-way valve is rotated 60 degrees counterclockwise to the initial position, and the mixed components staying in the second molecular sieve analysis column are separated mixed components of oxygen and argon and the three single components of nitrogen, methane, and carbon monoxide. The each component is driven by the carrier gas to flow out of the second six-way valve from the No. 4 port, and from the No. 4 port. The communicating port No. 5 flows out and enters the front detector (Front TCD) of the detector to measure the mixed components of oxygen and argon. The difference between the oxygen and argon mixed component content are measured and the oxygen content measured to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier gas in the first carrier gas pipe is high Pure argon. The carrier gas in the second carrier gas pipe is high Pure argon. The carrier gas in the third carrier gas pipe is high purity hydrogen. The first molecular sieve analysis column is 5A molecular sieve or 13X Molecular sieve and the second molecular sieve analysis column is 5A molecular sieve or 13X Molecular sieve.

TI Method for reducing carbon dioxide footprint associated with production process by cutting tool, involves determining cutting tool for production from determined set of cutting tools based on carbon dioxide emission information data.
AU JOHANSSON D
   HAEGGLUND S
   KREMER G
   HAe;GGLUND S
AE SECO TOOLS AB (SECO-C)
   SECO TOOLS AB (SECO-C)
GA 2021C4820F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a parameter indicative of a selected cutting feature and a selected work-piece material (70) for production by a cutting tool. The set of cutting tools is determined for production based on the obtained parameters. The cutting tool for production is determined from the determined cutting tools based on carbon dioxide emission information data associated with each cutting tool in the determined set of the cutting tools. Cutting data parameters are determined for the production process based on information data. The information data is stored in a memory. The machine (50) readable code is read at the cutting tool by a reader device (10a to 10c).
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide footprint associated with a production process by a cutting tool.
   ADVANTAGE - The amount of carbon dioxide emitted before and during the production process by the cutting tool is reduced, in order to reduce a carbon dioxide footprint associated with a production process. The selection of cutting tools can be manually or supported by a software application that selects a cutting tool dependent on e.g. the desired cutting feature and/or dependent on the work-piece material to be processed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an electronic device for reducing a carbon dioxide footprint associated with a production process; and
   (2) a computer program product with a non-transitory computer readable medium, having a computer program with program instructions, where the computer program being loadable into a processing circuitry and configured to cause execution of the method for reducing carbon dioxide footprint associated with a production process by a cutting tool.

Several pumps are arranged in series or in parallel in order to realize larger gas delivery volume with a maximum proportion of nano-gas bubbles. The gas losses are avoided and the harmful atmospheric pollution is prevented, which in turn reduces the operating costs, increases biomass production through shorter turnover rates and higher efficiency. The environmentally friendly increase in yield of biomass is achieved.

The outlet of the compressor, outdoor heat exchanger, regenerator, ejector, first indoor heat exchanger, the regenerator, the four-way reversing valve and the inlet of the compressor form a refrigeration cycle path. The second indoor heat exchanger, the ejector, the first indoor heat exchanger, and the second port of the gas-liquid separator form a refrigeration cycle path. The high temperature exhaust of the compressor in the heating mode enters the second indoor heat exchanger and the first indoor heat exchanger in turn, thus realizing the countercurrent heat exchange with the indoor air. The heat exchange is sufficient, and the heating capability of the system is improved. The purpose of efficient heating is achieved, so that the system can realize double-efficient cooling and heating.
