PN CN111548251-A; CN111548251-B
TI Preparing methanol by biogas full-component low-temperature plasma catalysis comprises e.g. mixing desulfurized biogas with water vapor in presence of catalyst in low-temperature plasma reactor, and performing reaction.
AU XIE J
   ZHANG Z
   GUO Y
   BI G
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA AGRIC (USCG-C)
GA 202081896G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methanol by biogas full-component low-temperature plasma catalysis comprises mixing desulfurized biogas with water vapor to obtain a mixed gas, and in the presence of a catalyst, in a low-temperature plasma reactor at 200-300 degrees C and normal pressure, and performing reaction to obtain synthesis gas to obtain methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methanol by biogas full-component low-temperature plasma catalysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves stability of catalyst, has mild reaction conditions, reduces formation of carbon deposits, and realizes utilization of methane and carbon dioxide resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for preparing methanol by biogas full-component low-temperature plasma catalysis comprising biogas digester 1, biogas preheating furnace 2, low-temperature plasma double reactor 3, waste heat recovery heat exchanger 4, first water cooling heat exchanger 5, first gas-liquid separation device 6, first gas pressurizing device 7, carbon dioxide separation device 8, second gas pressurizing device 9, syngas preheating furnace 10, methanol synthesis reactor 11, heat recovery heat converter 12, second water cooling heat exchanger 13, second gas-liquid separation device 14. The biogas in the biogas digester 1 enters the biogas preheating furnace 2, and the biogas is preheated to the required temperature. The preheated biogas enters the wet and dry dual reactor 3 to produce synthesis gas. The synthesis gas enters the waste heat recovery heat exchanger 4, which is connected to the first water cooling heat exchanger 5. The gas channel of the water cooling heat exchanger 5 is connected to the first gas-liquid separation device 6, the separated gas enters the first gas pressurization device 7, the pressurized gas enters the carbon dioxide separation device 8, and the synthesis gas after the separation of carbon dioxide enters the second gas pressurization device 9, pressurized synthesis gas enters the synthesis gas preheating furnace 10, after preheating, enters the methanol synthesis reactor 11. The synthesis gas is reacted in the reactor to synthesize methanol, and the obtained mixed gas containing methanol enters heat converter 12 realizes heat recovery. The gas flowing out of the heat converter 12 enters the second water-cooled heat exchanger 13 and then enters the second gas-liquid separation device 14, and the liquid obtained by separation is methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mass ratio of water vapor to methane in the mixed gas is 0.55-5.55, preferably 1.67. The reaction temperature of the low-temperature plasma reactor is 300 degrees C, and the space velocity is 300 minutes-1. The methane content in the biogas is 50-70%, and the carbon dioxide content is 30-50%. When the volume ratio of carbon dioxide to methane in the biogas is /(1/3, adding carbon dioxide to mixed gas until volume ratio of carbon dioxide to methane is /~1/3. The methanol is prepared from synthesis gas under conditions of 0.1-5 Mpa and 220-270 degrees C. The second gas-liquid separation device 14 is connected with a gas-conveying pipeline, which is connected to the second gas-pressurizing device 9 to transport the un-reacted carbon monoxide and hydrogen separated in the second gas-liquid separation device 14 to the second gas booster. The compressor 9 re-enters the subsequent synthesis gas preheating furnace 10, and then enters the methanol synthesis reactor 11 to participate in the reaction again to synthesize methanol. Preferred Components: The catalyst is nickel-based catalysts, copper-based catalysts, or copper-zinc catalysts.

PT P
PN CN111481603-A
TI Essential oil composition used for preparing medicines or health products for inhibiting alpha-glycosidase activity, lowering blood sugar and preventing or treating diabetes, comprises tangerine peel essential oil and tea essential oil.
AU LI Y
   YANG Y
   YE X
AE GUANGDONG IND TECH COLLEGE (GDIT-C)
GA 202075422K
AB    NOVELTY - Essential oil composition comprises tangerine peel essential oil and tea essential oil. The tangerine peel essential oil and tea essential oil are prepared by putting the dried tangerine peels and tea leaves in piles, and crushing them into coarse powder of dried tangerine peels and tea leaves. The coarse particle powder is soaked in a hydrochloric acid solution for extraction to obtain an extraction mixture. The cellulase and pectinase are added to the extraction mixture, and enzymatically hydrolyzed at 35-37 degrees C to obtain an enzymatic hydrolysis mixture. The enzymatic hydrolysis mixture is put into a carbon dioxide supercritical extractor to perform the extraction at 35-45 degrees C and 15-25MPa for 75-120 minutes to collect the enzymatic hydrolysate extract, where and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 20-30 liters per hour.
   USE - Essential oil composition used for preparing medicines or health products for inhibiting alpha -glycosidase activity, lowering blood sugar and preventing or treating diabetes.
   ADVANTAGE - The essential oil composition is safe and effective, effectively lowers blood sugar, effectively treats or prevents diabetes, and avoids the pollution of chemical reagents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Essential oil composition comprises tangerine peel essential oil and tea essential oil. The tangerine peel essential oil and tea essential oil are prepared by putting the dried tangerine peels and tea leaves in piles, and crushing them into coarse powder of dried tangerine peels and tea leaves. The coarse particle powder is soaked in a hydrochloric acid solution for extraction to obtain an extraction mixture. The cellulase and pectinase are added to the extraction mixture, and enzymatically hydrolyzed at 35-37 degrees C to obtain an enzymatic hydrolysis mixture. The enzymatic hydrolysis mixture is put into a carbon dioxide supercritical extractor to perform the extraction at 35-45 degrees C and 15-25MPa for 75-120 minutes to collect the enzymatic hydrolysate extract, where and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 20-30 liters per hour. The enzymatic hydrolysate extract is put in a molecular distillation apparatus, where the feed temperature is 38-48 degrees C, the sampling speed is 450-550ml per hour, the evaporation temperature is 100-110 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Hydrochloric acid solution is also used in the preparation of essential oil composition.
   ACTIVITY - Hyperglycemic; Antidiabetic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. PN CN111302372-A
TI Separating and extracting magnesium and calcium from phosphate tailings by secondary ammonium salt treatment, comprises calcining phosphorus tailings, digesting the calcined phosphorus tailings with an ammonium salt solution.
AU ZHOU J
   SU L
   WANG F
   GUO Z
   ZHAO Z
   SHI Q
   GAO Z
   PENG Z
   WU S
   LIN L
AE UNIV QIANNAN NORMAL NATIONALITIES (UYQI-Non-standard)
GA 202059302D
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and extracting magnesium and calcium from phosphate tailings by secondary ammonium salt treatment, comprises calcining phosphorus tailings at 850-1050 degrees C; digesting the calcined phosphorus tailings with an ammonium salt solution to obtain a digestive solution; according to the content of calcium and magnesium in the digestive solution, adding ammonium salt, heating to boiling, distilling off ammonia gas; filtering and separating, where the filter residue is phosphate concentrate and magnesium hydroxide, and the filtrate is calcium salt solution; adding calcium carbonate solution with carbon dioxide and ammonia gas or carbon dioxide and ammonia water to adjust the pH and precipitating calcium carbonate precipitate; filtering to obtain calcium carbonate solid, where the filtrate is a solution containing ammonium salt, which can be recycled.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and extracting magnesium and calcium from phosphate tailings by secondary ammonium salt treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and extracting magnesium and calcium from phosphate tailings by secondary ammonium salt treatment, comprises (i) calcining phosphorus tailings at 850-1050 degrees C; digesting the calcined phosphorus tailings with an ammonium salt solution to obtain a digestive solution; according to the content of calcium and magnesium in the digestive solution, adding ammonium salt, heating to boiling, distilling off ammonia gas; filtering and separating, where the filter residue is phosphate concentrate and magnesium hydroxide, and the filtrate is calcium salt solution; adding calcium carbonate solution with carbon dioxide and ammonia gas or carbon dioxide and ammonia water to adjust the pH and precipitating calcium carbonate precipitate; filtering to obtain calcium carbonate solid, where the filtrate is a solution containing ammonium salt, which can be recycled; (ii) taking the filter residue obtained after the first step of ammonium hydrolysis and filtering, according to the content of calcium and magnesium, adding ammonium salt again, heating to boiling, steaming with ammonia gas, recovering steam ammonia gas, and converting magnesium hydroxide to magnesium salt; filtering and separating, where the filter residue is phosphate concentrate, and the filtrate is a magnesium salt solution; separating phosphate concentrate, adding ammonia water or ammonia gas, heating to steam out ammonia gas, recovering the distilled ammonia gas, and precipitating magnesium hydroxide; filtering and separating to obtain magnesium hydroxide solid, where the filtrate is a solution containing ammonium salt, and continuing to be recycled; drying the obtained calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The phosphorous tailings are calcined at 950 degrees C. Preferred Components: The ammonium salt is ammonium chloride or ammonium nitrate.

PN CN109824472-A
TI Synthesis of gem-difluoroalkane and alpha-fluorocarboxylic acid involves reacting malonic acid derivative, base, and monovalent silver, oxidizing silver carboxylate to obtain fluorocarboxylic acid, and decarboxylating and fluorinating.
AU CHEN J
   WANG Z
   GUO C
   YANG C
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201951029Q
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of gem-difluoroalkane and ( alpha )-fluorocarboxylic acid involves subjecting a carboxylic acid in malonic acid derivative (I) to a salt formation reaction with monovalent silver to form a silver carboxylate (VII), oxidizing compound (VII) under fluorination reagent to obtain divalent silver salt (X), or oxidizing compound (VII) with nitrogen-containing cation radical to obtain compound (X), oxidizing compound (X) and removing one carbon dioxide molecule to obtain ( alpha )-carboxylic acid radical, reacting with fluorination reagent to obtain ( alpha )-fluorocarboxylic acid (III) as product, subjecting compound (III) to decarboxylation and fluorination, obtaining ( alpha )-fluoro monovalent silver carboxylate (XII), ( alpha )-fluoro divalent silver carboxylate (XIII), removing one carbon dioxide molecule from compound (XIII) to obtain fluorine-containing radical, and reacting with fluorination reagent to obtain difluoroalkane product (II).
   USE - Synthesis of gem-difluoroalkane and ( alpha )-fluorocarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and carried out under mild reaction conditions, uses inexpensive and easily available raw materials, and has excellent chemical selectivity and functional group compatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of gem-difluoroalkane and ( alpha )-fluorocarboxylic acid involves subjecting a carboxylic acid in malonic acid derivative of formula (I) to a salt formation reaction with monovalent silver in the presence of base to form a silver carboxylate of formula (VII), oxidizing compound (VII) with fluorination reagent to obtain divalent silver salt of formula (X), or oxidizing compound (VII) with nitrogen-containing cation radical to obtain compound (X), oxidizing compound (X) and removing one carbon dioxide molecule to obtain ( alpha )-carboxylic acid radical, reacting with fluorination reagent to obtain ( alpha )-fluorocarboxylic acid of formula (III) as product, subjecting compound (III) to decarboxylation and fluorination, obtaining ( alpha )-fluoro monovalent silver carboxylate of formula (XII), ( alpha )-fluoro divalent silver carboxylate of formula (XIII), removing one carbon dioxide molecule from compound (XIII) to obtain fluorine-containing radical, and reacting with fluorination reagent to obtain difluoroalkane product of formula (II)
   R1 = aryl or hydrocarbyl;and
   R2 = hydrocarbyl, preferably 1-20C hydrocarbyl optionally substituted with phenyl, carbonyl, cyano, 2-10C ester, sulfonyl, amido, ether, hydroxy, alkynyl, halo, phthalimido, 1-10C alkoxy, phenyl-substituted 1-4C alkyl, 3-7C cycloalkyl, phenylcarbonyl, benzoyl, four-membered, five-membered, or six-membered N-containing heterocyclyl or benzo-1-6C hydrocarbyl, halo, ester, or N-containing four-membered, five-membered, or six-membered alkoxyheterocyclyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silver catalyst is silver nitrate, silver fluoride, silver oxide, silver acetate, silver sulfate, silver trifluoromethanesulfonate, or silver tetrafluoroborate, preferably silver nitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is a mixed solvent comprising an organic solvent and water. The organic solvent comprises acetonitrile, acetone, halogenated hydrocarbons such as dichloromethane, ethers such as diethyl ether, tert-butyl methyl ether, diisopropyl ether, cyclopentyl methyl ether, tetrahydrofuran, or ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, alkane such as n-hexane, cyclohexane, n-pentane, n-heptane, or cyclopentane, and benzene such as benzene or toluene. The base comprises an inorganic base and an organic base. The inorganic base comprises phosphoric acid such as potassium phosphate, sodium phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium monohydrogen phosphate, or sodium monohydrogen phosphate, carbonic acids such as potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium hydrogen carbonate, or sodium hydrogen carbonate, and hydrofluoric acids such as potassium fluoride, sodium fluoride, or lithium fluoride. The organic base comprises benzoic acid such as sodium benzoate, potassium benzoate, sodium p-chlorobenzoate, or potassium p-chlorobenzoate, amines such as triethylamine or pyridine. During preparing compound (II), base is sodium benzoate and dipotassium hydrogen phosphate. During preparing compound (III), base is dipotassium hydrogen phosphate. The fluorination reagent is Selectfluor (RTM: 1-chloromethyl-4-fluoro-1,4-diazoniabicyclo(2.2.2)octane bis(tetrafluoroborate)), Selectfluor-II (RTM: 1-fluoro-4-methyl-1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane-1,4-diium tetrafluoroborate), or their corresponding hexafluorophosphate. Preferred Composition: The ratio of malonic acid, base, silver salt, and fluorination reagent is 1:1-10:0.1-0.5:2-8. During preparing compound (II), ratio of malonic acid, base, silver salt, and fluorination reagent is 1:2-4:0.1-0.3:4-6, and ratio of acetonitrile, water, and n-hexane is 1:0.5-5:2-5. During preparing compound (III), ratio of malonic acid, base, silver salt, and fluorination reagent is 1:2-4:0.1-0.3:2-4, and ratio of cyclopentyl methyl ether and water is 1:0.5-5.

TI Preparing anti-dirt coating on inner wall surface of large water ring vacuum pump body comprises e.g. surface blasting of large water ring vacuum pump impeller, removing rust and oxide skin, and improving bonding strength between impeller.
AU ZHAO Y
   MENG J
   ZHAO G
   CHEN W
   TANG Z
   LI X
   MENG F
   SUN H
   YAN H
   JING Y
   PU X
   ZHANG R
AE ZIBO WATER RING VACUUM PUMP FACTORY CO L (ZIBO-Non-standard)
   UNIV SHANDONG TECHNOLOGY (SDUT-C)
GA 201922632Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anti-dirt coating on inner wall surface of large water ring vacuum pump body comprises (i) surface blasting of large water ring vacuum pump impeller for removing rust, and oxide skin, and improving bonding strength between impeller and coating, (ii) performing conventional ultrasonic cleaning on surface of large water ring vacuum pump impeller for removing surface pollutants e.g. grease and particles, (iii) using anhydrous ethanol as non-polar solvent, adding titanium dioxide powder, and stirring to obtain titanium oxide ethanol solution, (iv) adding titanium dioxide ethanol solution to supercritical carbon dioxide reaction kettle, increasing the temperature, and pressure, stirring with magnetic stirrer for dispersing titanium dioxide ethanol solution in supercritical carbon dioxide, and (v) carefully opening nozzle valve, spraying titanium dioxide ethanol solution into high temperature zone of DC plasma torch, and evaporating solvent in tiny droplets under high temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing anti-dirt coating on inner wall surface of large water ring vacuum pump body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing anti-dirt coating on inner wall surface of large water ring vacuum pump body comprises (i) surface blasting of large water ring vacuum pump impeller for removing rust, and oxide skin, and improving the bonding strength between the impeller and coating, (ii) performing conventional ultrasonic cleaning on the surface of large water ring vacuum pump impeller for removing surface pollutants e.g. grease and particles, (iii) using anhydrous ethanol as non-polar solvent, adding titanium dioxide powder having a particle diameter of 20 nm, and stirring for 10 minutes to obtain titanium oxide ethanol solution, (iv) adding titanium dioxide ethanol solution to supercritical carbon dioxide reaction kettle, increasing the temperature to 80 degrees C, and pressure to 10 MPa, respectively, stirring with magnetic stirrer for 20 minutes for uniformly dispersing the titanium dioxide ethanol solution in supercritical carbon dioxide, (v) carefully opening the nozzle valve, spraying titanium dioxide ethanol solution into high temperature zone of DC plasma torch, evaporating the solvent in tiny droplets rapidly under high temperature, and high speed environment, and then precipitating titanium dioxide nanoparticles, and rapidly melting into titanium dioxide droplets, and (vi) heating titanium oxide droplets after accelerated depositing on the surface of large water ring vacuum pump impeller, and obtaining layered structure of titanium dioxide anti-dirt coating.

PN CN108439915-A
TI Preparation of solid carbon permeable concrete by collecting industrial waste gas after carbonization reaction with room temperature air, reinjecting into water, discharging after concrete curing, and taking out.
AU WANG S
   JIANG H
   LU L
   YIN H
   ZHANG Z
AE XUZHOU INST TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 201868214Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of solid carbon permeable concrete comprises subjecting industrial waste gas to dust removal treatment; mixing dedusted industrial waste gas with air; mixing reinforcing agent, cement, coarse aggregate and water in mixing container to form permeable concrete mix, injecting industrial waste gas into mixing vessel to carbonized permeable concrete mixture and carbon dioxide in industrial waste gas, and collecting industrial waste gas; placing permeable concrete mix in prefabricated mold, injecting industrial waste gas into permeable concrete carbon fixation chamber, carrying out carbonation reaction, collecting industrial waste gas, and curing; and collecting industrial waste gas after the carbonization reaction in third step and fourth step with room temperature air and dedusted industrial waste gas at 30-40 degrees C, reinjecting into water, discharging after concrete curing time is reached, and taking out prepared permeable concrete from prefabricated mold.
   USE - Method for preparing solid carbon permeable concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can not only reduce the carbon dioxide content in industrial waste gas, but also ensure the applicable performance of permeable concrete.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of solid carbon permeable concrete comprises:
   (A) subjecting industrial waste gas to dust removal treatment through dust filter;
   (B) mixing dedusted industrial waste gas with room temperature air at 30-40 degrees C;
   (C) determining ratio of reinforcing agent, cement, coarse aggregate and water according to the performance parameters required for permeable concrete, mixing reinforcing agent, cement, coarse aggregate and water in mixing container to form permeable concrete mix for 10-15 minutes, injecting industrial waste gas after treatment continuously into mixing vessel to carbonized permeable concrete mixture and carbon dioxide in industrial waste gas, and collecting industrial waste gas after carbonization reaction;
   (D) placing permeable concrete mix in prefabricated mold, injecting industrial waste gas into permeable concrete carbon fixation chamber, carrying out carbonation reaction between concrete mixture and carbon dioxide, collecting industrial waste gas after carbonization reaction, and curing for not less than 15 days; and
   (E) collecting industrial waste gas after the carbonization reaction in third step and fourth step with room temperature air and dedusted industrial waste gas at 30-40 degrees C, reinjecting into water, discharging after concrete curing time is reached, and taking out prepared permeable concrete from prefabricated mold.

PN CN107950336-A
TI Antifreezing method for potato in greenhouse involves filling vegetable fields with water, supplementing lighting, adjusting air content and supplementing concentration of carbon dioxide in shed and performing post-snow management.
AU BAO Y
AE BAO Y (BAOY-Individual)
GA 201833121E
AB    NOVELTY - Antifreezing method for potato in greenhouse involves filling vegetable fields with water, before the cooling down of winds and snows, digging cold-proof ditch around the shed, covering and compacting soil, strengthening and supplementing the lighting in sheds in accordance to the following methods by hanging a light curtain on the back wall of shed, adjusting air content and supplementing the concentration of carbon dioxide in shed, performing post-snow management, where the potato plants has slow adaptation to the external environment, and then recovering normal growth.
   USE - The antifreezing method is useful for potato in greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective, has long and effective antifreezing time, reduces labors and prevents freezing of potato.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Antifreezing method for potato in greenhouse involves (a) filling vegetable fields with water in a timely manner, before the cooling down of winds and snows, the irrigation water is poured thoroughly and poured, ensure that no water accumulates on the surface, after irrigating, it is necessary to seize the opportunity to perform planning, (b) digging cold-proof ditch around the shed, where depth is based on the local frozen ground and width is 50-70 cm, filling ditch with horse dung, sawdust or rice straw, covering and compacting soil, (c) strengthening and supplementing the lighting in the sheds in accordance to the following methods by hanging a light curtain on the back wall of the shed to increase the light intensity on the cultivating bed or install fluorescent lights in the shed, turning lights on sooner or later in case of weak light on cloudy snow days, (d) adjusting air content in the shed, supplementing the concentration of carbon dioxide in the shed in accordance with the following method by using smoke, sulfuric acid and ammonium bicarbonate reaction method, adjusting the air content of the shed and increasing the content of carbon dioxide and (e) performing post-snow management by when the weather is fine, the air is repeatedly released, thus the temperature difference between the interior and exterior of the shed is similar to that of the previous days and then the temperature is slowly increased, where the potato plants has slow adaptation to the external environment, and then recovering normal growth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the cold-proof ditch is above the ground. In the step (b), turning lights on sooner or later for 3 hours.

PN CN107778875-A
TI Polysilazane-zirconium dioxide composite aerogel material useful for removing synthetic dyes in aqueous solution e.g. methylene blue, and in waterproof or thermal insulation field.
AU LIU H
   DENG Q
   LI H
   LI J
   LI Y
   LIU Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 2018209304
AB    NOVELTY - Polysilazane-zirconium dioxide composite aerogel material is claimed. The composite aerogel material is prepared by e.g. (i) dispersing polysilazane precursor in toluene, adding tetrabutyl zirconate and divinylbenzene, transferring dilute mixed solution to hydrothermal reactor, placing reactor containing mixed dilute solution in drying oven, reacting at 160-200 degrees C, cooling to obtain wet gel, then placing wet gel in beaker containing toluene, and (ii) transferring obtained product to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, immersing in liquid carbon dioxide and supercritical drying.
   USE - The material is useful for removing synthetic dyes in aqueous solution e.g. methylene blue; and in waterproof or thermal insulation field (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high porosity, specific surface area, low density, simple preparation method, and mild reaction condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polysilazane-zirconium dioxide composite aerogel material is claimed. The composite aerogel material is prepared by (i) dispersing polysilazane precursor in toluene, adding tetrabutyl zirconate and divinylbenzene, and mixing to obtain dilute solution, transferring dilute mixed solution to hydrothermal reactor, sealing, placing reactor containing mixed dilute solution in drying oven, reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 1-6 hours, cooling to room temperature 20-25 degrees C to obtain wet gel, then placing wet gel in beaker containing toluene, and allowing to stand for 24-36 hours, and (ii) transferring product obtained in step (i) to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, immersing in liquid carbon dioxide for 0.5-2 hours, and supercritical drying to obtain polysilazane-zirconia composite aerogel material, where density of polysilazane-zirconium dioxide composite aerogel material is 0.04-0.10 g/cm3, porosity is 90-98%, specific surface area is 400-600 m2g1, water contact angle is 160-170 degrees , thermal conductivity is 0.015-0.025 W/mK, pore size distribution is 2-100 nm uniform mesoporous material; in step (i), composition of raw materials are 4-10 pts. wt. polysilazane precursor, 4-10 pts. wt. toluene dispersion of polysilazane precursor, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt. tetrabutyl zirconate and 4-8 pts. wt. divinylbenzene; polysilazane is linear structure, main chain consists of silicon and nitrogen atoms, structure has silicon-hydrogen bonds and vinyl side chains, molar mass is 800-1500 g/mol; and in step (ii), supercritical drying conditions are: drying temperature is 40-50 degrees C, drying pressure is 5-10 MPa, and drying time is 5-9 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), composition of raw materials are 5-8 pts. wt. polysilazane precursor, 5-8 pts. wt. toluene dispersion of polysilazane precursor, 1-2 pts. wt. tetrabutyl zirconate and 5-6 pts. wt. divinylbenzene; and molar mass of polysilazane is 1000-1200 g/mol. Preferred Method: In step (i), reacting reaction vessel containing dilute solution for 2-5 hours in dry box at 180-200 degrees C, adding wet gel into beaker, immersing wet gel in toluene and allowing to stand for 25-30 hours. In step (ii), immersing product obtained step (i) in liquid carbon dioxide for 1-1.5 hours, and then supercritical drying conditions are: drying temperature is 45-48 degrees C, drying pressure is 8-10 MPa, and drying time is 6-8 hours. The method further comprises (i) dispersing polysilazane precursor in toluene, adding tetrabutyl zirconate and divinylbenzene, and mixing to obtain dilute solution, transferring dilute mixed solution to hydrothermal reactor, sealing, placing reactor containing mixed dilute solution in drying oven, reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 1-6 hours, cooling to room temperature 20-25 degrees C to obtain wet gel, then placing wet gel in beaker containing toluene, and allowing to stand for 24-36 hours, and (ii) transferring product obtained in step (i) to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, immersing in liquid carbon dioxide for 0.5-2 hours, and supercritical drying to obtain polysilazane-zirconia composite aerogel material, where in step (i), composition of raw materials are 4-10 pts. wt. polysilazane precursor, 4-10 pts. wt. toluene dispersion of polysilazane precursor, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt. tetrabutyl zirconate and 4-8 pts. wt. divinylbenzene; polysilazane is linear structure, main chain consists of silicon and nitrogen atoms, structure has silicon-hydrogen bonds and vinyl side chains, molar mass is 800-1500 g/mol; and in step (ii), supercritical drying conditions are: drying temperature is 40-50 degrees C, drying pressure is 5-10 MPa, and drying time is 5-9 hours.

PN PL420373-A1
TI Processing fly ash from energy using fuel product having controlled content of free calcium oxide and waste heat, comprises e.g. introducing fly ash, water and carbon dioxide into reaction space of rotary reactor and inert gas, and mixing.
AU JANOSZEK T
   BAJERSKI A
   LACZNY J M
   GOGOLA K
   KABIESZ J
   ROG L
   IWASZENKO S
   STANCZYK K

   ZOGALA A

AE GLOWNY INSTYTUT GORNICTWA (GLOW-Non-standard)
GA 201833133P
AB    NOVELTY - Processing fly ash from the energy using fuel product having a controlled content of free calcium oxide with the use of waste heat, comprises e.g. introducing 5-99.9 wt.% of fly ash of the total volume of the reactor space with humidity, 0.1-10% water and 1-99.99% carbon dioxide into the reaction space of the rotary reactor and an inert gas, preferably nitrogen, as the residue, at 0.1-200 (preferably 4) l/minutes, and mixing at a rotational motion for 10-70 minutes at a constant speed of 0.5-60 (preferably 28) rpm.
   USE - The method is useful for processing fly ash from the energy using fuel product having a controlled content of free calcium oxide and waste heat.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing fly ash from the energy using fuel product having a controlled content of free calcium oxide with the use of waste heat, comprises introducing 5-99.9 wt.% of fly ash of the total volume of the reactor space with humidity, 0.1-10% of water and 1-99.99% carbon dioxide into the reaction space of the rotary reactor and an inert gas, preferably nitrogen, as the residue, at 0.1-200 (preferably 4) l/minutes, mixing at a rotational motion for 10-70 minutes at a constant speed of 0.5-60 (preferably 28) rpm, where the step of mixing carbon dioxide and inert gas as the generated waste heat is transferred by convection current with a stream of gas mixture at 0.1-50 (preferably 1) l/minutes to from the reaction space of the reactor to the non-contact shell and tube heat exchanger in which the heat is recovered between the two liquids with a mixture of gas from the reactor and atmospheric air supplied with the outside in countercurrent of 0.1-50 (preferably 0.5) l/minutes, cooling the gas mixture from the heat exchanger and recycling into the reaction space of the reactor for the re-carbonation process, and guiding the heated air stream from the exchanger to the drying chamber where the carbonization products are dried.

   ODONNELL J S
AE RONDO ENERGY INC (ROND-Non-standard)
GA 2022D3496S
AB    NOVELTY - Thermal energy storage system (200) comprises                a storage medium (209) configured to store thermal                energy, a heating element (207) configured to heat                the storage medium using electricity from a                renewable energy source (201), and a fluid movement                system configured to move a fluid through storage                medium to heat fluid to a temperature in a                specified electrolyzer temperature range and to                provide fluid to solid oxide unit configured to                operate in an electrolysis mode to produce hydrogen                and/or carbon monoxide. The fluid comprises water                or carbon dioxide, or a combination of water and                carbon dioxide, where fluid comprises water as a                steam (219). The system is configured to extract                heat from fluid.
   USE - Thermal energy storage system for continuous                supply of hot air, carbon dioxide (CO2) and steam                or heated fluids for various applications including                supply of heat to industrial processes and/or                electrical power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The provision of heat to serve these capture                processes from variable renewable electricity (VRE)                is beneficial in further reducing the emissions and                costs such of carbon capture processes. The thermal                energy storage systems and technologies that                utilize VRE to provide industrial process energy,                which may expand VRE and reduce fossil fuel                combustion. The efficiently constructed,                long-service-life thermal energy storage systems                having materials, fabrication, physical shape, and                other properties that mitigate damage and                deterioration from repeated temperature cycling.                The system provides a continuous (or                near-continuous) flow of output energy based on an                intermittently available source. The system has the                potential to reduce the reliance of industrial                applications on fossil fuels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for maintaining sufficient outlet temperature while                using lower-cost solid media, which involves: (a)                converting electricity from a renewable energy                source into heat; supplying the heat to a thermal                storage medium; (b) circulating a fluid through the                thermal storage medium to heat the fluid to a                temperature in a specified electrolyzer temperature                range; and (c) providing the fluid to a solid oxide                unit configured to operate in an electrolysis mode                to produce hydrogen from water and/or carbon                monoxide from carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                thermal energy storage system.
   200Thermal energy storage system
   201Renewable energy source
   207Heating element
   209Storage medium
   219Steam

The solid oxide unit                  includes a solid oxide electrolysis cell having                  an electrolysis anode and an electrolysis                  cathode, the fluid movement system is configured                  to split the fluid into an electrolysis                  anode-side flow conduit and an electrolysis                  cathode-side flow conduit. The electrolysis                  anode-side flow conduit is configured to permit                  oxygen to be swept from the electrolysis anode,                  and the electrolysis cathode-side flow conduit is                  configured to permit the electrolysis                  cathode-side flow to exit the solid oxide unit                  with output products from the electrolysis                  cathode mixed. The fuel cell cathode-side flow                  conduit is configured to permit oxygen to be                  delivered to the fuel cell cathode to remove heat                  from the solid oxide unit and provide the heat to                  the one or more secondary processes. The fuel                  cell anode-side flow conduit is configured to                  permit the fuel cell anode-side flow to exit the                  solid oxide unit and provide fuel cell anode-side                  reaction products to the one or more secondary                  processes.

TI Upgrading and enriching gas through algae            photobioreactor, by introducing natural gas stream            comprising carbon dioxide into algae media within            contactor, allowing media to consume carbon dioxide,            and discharging natural gas from contactor.
AU CHOUDHARY V
   ONEAL E J
   FEICHT S E
   HANKS P L
   REUTER S J
   MUNSON R G
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202127936K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for upgrading and enriching gas through                algae photobioreactor involves (a) (i) containing                an algae media within contactor, (ii) introducing a                stream of natural gas comprising 80 wt.% carbon                dioxide (CO2) into the contactor, (iii) contacting                the natural gas on the algae media and allowing the                algae media to consume CO2 from the natural gas,                and (iv) discharging a stream of natural gas                comprising 2 wt.% or less CO2 from the contactor,                or (b) (i) introducing a stream of natural gas into                algae photobioreactor containing an algae media,                (ii) generating oxygen (O2) with the algae media,                (iii) stripping at least a portion of the O2 with                the natural gas within the photobioreactor, (iv)                discharging a stream of natural gas enriched with                O2 from the photobioreactor, and (v) using the                stream of natural gas enriched with O2 in an                oxygen-requiring process.
   USE - The method is useful for upgrading and                enriching gas through algae photobioreactor, and                extracting biofuel from microalgae.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for upgrading and enriching gas through                algae photobioreactor involves (a) (i) containing                an algae media within contactor, (ii) introducing a                stream of natural gas comprising 80 wt.% carbon                dioxide (CO2) into the contactor, (iii) contacting                the natural gas on the algae media and allowing the                algae media to consume CO2 from the natural gas,                and (iv) discharging a stream of natural gas                comprising 2 wt.% or less CO2 from the contactor,                (b) (i) introducing a stream of natural gas into                algae photobioreactor containing an algae media,                (ii) generating oxygen (O2) with the algae media,                (iii) stripping at least a portion of the O2 with                the natural gas within the photobioreactor, (iv)                discharging a stream of natural gas enriched with                O2 from the photobioreactor, and (v) using the                stream of natural gas enriched with O2 in an                oxygen-requiring process, or (c) (i) introducing a                stream of a high-CO2 concentration gas into a                contactor containing an algae media, where high-CO2                concentration gas comprising at one of natural gas                having 80 wt.% CO2 and a biogas having 20-40 wt.%                CO2, (ii) contacting the high-CO2 concentration gas                on the algae media and allowing the algae media to                consume CO2 from the high-CO2 concentration gas,                and (iii) discharging a stream of gas comprising 2                wt.% or less CO2 from the contactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Apparatus: The contactor comprises                  algae photobioreactor. The algae photobioreactor                  comprises an enclosed reactor.Preferred Method: The step (a)-(i) involves                  circulating the algae media into the contactor                  from algae photobioreactor, and returning the                  algae media to the algae photobioreactor after                  contacting the natural gas on the algae media                  within the contactor. The step (a) further                  involves conveying the stream of natural gas                  discharged from the contactor to a power plant                  for combustion, pressurizing the contactor to a                  fluid pressure of 500-2000 psig, discharging the                  stream of natural gas from the contactor into a                  pressurized pipeline, generating O2 with the                  algae media, stripping at least a portion of the                  O2 with the natural gas within the contactor and                  discharging a stream of natural gas enriched with                  O2 from the contactor, and using the stream of                  natural gas enriched with O2 in an                  oxygen-requiring process. The oxygen-requiring                  process includes at least one of synthetic gas                  (syngas) production and oxy-combustion for power                  production. The O2 with the algae media is                  generated by contacting the natural gas on the                  algae media, and consuming at least a portion of                  the CO2 from the natural gas with the algae                  media. The oxygen-requiring process is performed                  using the stream of natural gas enriched with O2                  by (i) conveying the stream of natural gas                  enriched with O2 to a combustion power plant, and                  combusting the stream of natural gas enriched                  with O2 in the combustion power plant to generate                  power, or (ii) conveying the stream of natural                  gas enriched with O2 to a partial oxidation                  system, and generating a syngas product in the                  partial oxidation system with the stream of                  natural gas enriched with O2. The                  oxygen-requiring process is preceded by                  dehydrating the stream of natural gas enriched                  with O2. The step (c) further involves (i)                  conveying the stream of gas discharged from the                  contactor to a power plant for combustion, (ii)                  pressurizing the contactor to a fluid pressure,                  and discharging the stream of gas from the                  contactor into a pressurized pipeline, and (iii)                  generating O2 with the algae media, stripping at                  least a portion of the O2 with the high-CO2                  concentration gas within the contactor and                  discharging a stream of gas enriched with O2 from                  the contactor, and using the stream of gas                  enriched with O2 in an oxygen-requiring                  process.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112117020-A
TI Photothermal synergistic catalytic treatment            method of tritiated water used e.g. to fusion devices,            by mixing catalyst and adsorbent, heating, changing            tritiated water into water vapor, mixing with carbon            monoxide and providing light source.
AU YUE L

   XIAO C
   GONG Y
   HOU J
   FU X
   RAN G
   CHEN C
   WANG H
AE NUCLEAR PHYSICS & CHEM RES INST CHINA AC (CAEP-C)
GA 2020C9952P
AB    NOVELTY - Photothermal synergistic catalytic treatment                method of tritiated water involves uniformly mixing                catalyst and adsorbent, adding in reactor, opening                heater, heating, changing tritiated water into                water vapor, uniformly mixing with carbon monoxide,                introducing into reactor, arranging xenon lamp                above transparent window at central position of                upper end surface of reactor, opening xenon lamp,                providing light source in reactor, forming defect                structure on catalyst, promoting water vapor                activation, reacting carbon monoxide molecule                activated on surface of catalyst and water                molecule, and producing product carbon dioxide and                HT. Producing involves irradiating xenon lamp on                surface of catalyst, catalyst electrons                transitioning from valence band to conduction band                position, forming photo-generated electron e- on                conduction band and photo-generated hole h+ at                valence band, inducing, promoting carbon monoxide                activation, and adsorbing tritiated water on oxygen                vacancy to be activated.
   USE - The method is used for photothermal                synergistic catalytic treating tritiated water to                places for generating tritium water fusion device                including fusion devices and nuclear power                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses semiconductor material with                response to UV light as catalyst carrier,                introduces light source auxiliary heat catalysis,                reduces reaction temperature, and realizes fast                treatment of tritiated water at lower                temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Photothermal synergistic catalytic treatment                method of tritiated water involves uniformly mixing                catalyst and adsorbent, adding in reactor, opening                heater, heating reactor, changing tritiated water                into water vapor by gasifier, uniformly mixing with                carbon monoxide, introducing into reactor,                arranging xenon lamp above transparent window at                central position of upper end surface of reactor,                opening xenon lamp, providing light source in                reactor, forming defect structure on catalyst when                light source is irradiated on surface of catalyst,                promoting water vapor activation, reacting carbon                monoxide molecule activated on surface of catalyst                and water molecule, and producing product carbon                dioxide and HT. Producing involves irradiating                xenon lamp on surface of catalyst, catalyst                electrons transitioning from valence band to                conduction band position, forming photo-generated                electron e- on conduction band, forming                photo-generated hole h+ at valence band, inducing                to generate oxygen vacancy Ov, transferring                photo-generated electron to platinum surface,                promoting carbon monoxide activation, adsorbing                tritiated water on oxygen vacancy to be activated,                combining activated carbon monoxide and tritiated                water molecule to generate formic acid                intermediate, heating and decomposing into carbon                dioxide and HT, contacting and removing product                carbon dioxide and adsorbent by adsorption, and                flowing product HT out of reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The xenon lamp has                  wavelength of light source of 300-700 nm and                  power of 100-300 W. The temperature of reactor is                  50-150 degrees C. Preferred Components: The                  catalyst is supported catalyst. The carrier of                  catalyst is titania or cobalt(II,III) oxide                  semiconductor. The metal of load is platinum or                  palladium. The adsorbent is lithium orthosilicate                  or calcium oxide.

TI Carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst of nitrogen sulfur in-situ doped hollow porous carbon microsphere comprises e.g. amino functionalized porous nano silicon dioxide, aniline, m-amino benzene sulfonic acid and ammonium persulfate.
AU FAN M
AE FAN M (FANM-Individual)
GA 2020A9572P
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst of nitrogen sulfur in-situ doped hollow porous carbon microsphere comprises amino functionalized porous nano silicon dioxide, zinc chloride, aniline, m-amino benzene sulfonic acid, ammonium persulfate, where the mass ratio is 50-80: 100-180: 100-180: 270-385:15-60.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst of nitrogen sulfur in-situ doped hollow porous carbon microsphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The microsphere: has unique hollow and rich pore structure to improve the wettability; reduces the speed-controlled Gibbs free energy barrier during the reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst of nitrogen sulfur in-situ doped hollow porous carbon microsphere comprising (i) adding sodium hydroxide and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to a mixed solvent of distilled water and ethylene glycol in a volume ratio of 4-6:1 and heating to 70-90 degrees C, slowly adding ethyl orthosilicate by drop wise, reacting for 6-12 hours, filtering and washing, placing the precipitated product in an atmosphere resistance furnace, heating to 550-600 degrees C, calcining for 3-5 hours to prepare porous nano silicon dioxide, (ii) adding porous nano silicon dioxide to the toluene solvent carrying out uniform ultrasonic dispersion and adding the aminosilane coupling agent, heating to 100-120 degrees C and reacting for 5-10 hours, performing centrifugal separation, washing and drying to preparing amino functionalized porous nano silicon dioxide, (iii) adding amino-functionalized porous nano-dioxide and zinc chloride to the hydrochloric acid solution with the substance concentration of 0.8-1.2 mol/l and stirring for 4-8 hours after ultrasonic dispersion, placing aniline and m-aminobenzenesulfonic acid in an ice water bath, slowly adding ammonium persulfate hydrochloric acid solution by drop wise and reacting for 10-15 hours, performing suction filtration and washing, placing the solid product in an atmosphere resistance furnace with an argon atmosphere, raising the temperature to 800-900 degrees C, performing heat preservation and calcination for 2-3 hours, and prepare nitrogen and sulfur in-situ doped porous carbon microspheres coated with nano silicon dioxide, (iv) placing the nitrogen and sulfur in-situ doped porous carbon microspheres coated with nano- silicon dioxide in a mixed solution of hydrofluoric acid and ammonium fluoride, performing etching for 4-10 hours, filtering, washing and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of sodium hydroxide, hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide and ethyl orthosilicate is 3-4: 10-15: 100. The amino silane coupling agent is 3- amino propyl triethoxy silane or 3-amino propyl trimethoxy silane, where the mass ratio of porous nanometre silicon dioxide is 4-8: 10
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (i) the atmosphere resistance furnace includes a crucible rotating device and a calcining crucible rotating device is provided with a rotating shaft. The rotating shaft is fixedly connected with a connecting rod and movably connected with a gear. The gear is movably connected with a threaded ring, and the connecting rod is movably connected with a guide wheel. The guide wheel is movably connected with a runner, and the crucible is movably connected above the runner.

GA 2020413704
AB    NOVELTY - Gasification system comprises gasification furnace, where gasification furnace carbon dioxide and oxygen create an exothermic reaction, burner and high temperature air preheater. High-temperature coal gas discharged by the gasification furnace is introduced into a high-temperature air preheater, which is connected with an air pipeline for introducing oxygen-enriched gas, which flows through high-temperature air preheater and obtains the heat of the high-temperature coal gas flowing through the high-temperature air preheater, so that the temperature of the oxygen-enriched gas rises, and the high-temperature coal gas loses heat and is changed into medium-temperature coal gas. The combustor gas and the oxygen-enriched gas exhausted by the high-temperature air preheater are mixed and combusted. The combustion-generated high-temperature flue gas is exhausted into the gasification furnace.
   USE - The gasification system is used for reducing gasifying furnace hearth temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat generated by the exothermic reaction of carbon and oxygen in the gasification furnace can be offset by the endothermic reaction. The temperature in the hearth of the gasification furnace is reduced, the problems of too high manufacturing cost caused by coking in the gasification furnace and jacket increase are solved, and the occupied space of the gasification furnace is not increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gasification system comprises gasification furnace, where gasification furnace carbon dioxide and oxygen create an exothermic reaction, high temperature air preheater, and burner. High-temperature coal gas discharged by the gasification furnace is introduced into a high-temperature air preheater, which is communicated with an air pipeline for introducing oxygen-enriched gas, which flows through high-temperature air preheater and obtains the heat of the high-temperature coal gas flowing through the high-temperature air preheater, so that the temperature of the oxygen-enriched gas rises, and the high-temperature coal gas loses heat and is changed into medium-temperature coal gas. The gas in the combustor and the oxygen-enriched gas exhausted by the high-temperature air preheater are mixed and combusted. The high-temperature flue gas generated by combustion is exhausted into the gasification furnace. The heat generated by the combustion of the gas in the combustor converts the water flowing through the combustor into water vapor, which is exhausted into the gasification furnace. The carbon and carbon dioxide and the carbon and water vapor in the gasifier produce endothermic reactions.

PT P
PN CN111112212-A; CN111112212-B
TI Regenerating quartz thermal shield used for manufacturing integrated circuits comprises e.g. soaking with organic solvent, placing regenerated quartz thermal shield in organic solution treatment agent and removing metallized organic matter.
AU WANG Y
   HE X
   ZHANG Z
   JIANG L
AE SHANGHAI FULEDE INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202042167H
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating quartz thermal shield comprises e.g. soaking with organic solvent, placing the to-be-regenerated quartz thermal shield in an organic solution treatment agent, and removing or loosely attaching metallized organic matter on surface, spraying carbon dioxide particle on quartz thermal shield, placing the quartz shielding plate after carbon dioxide particle spray treatment into the etching tank, using acidic etching solution for surface etching, washing with pure water, and completing the surface repair after drying and transporting micro-etched quartz thermal shield to a class 100 clean room for ultrasonic cleaning, and washing with ultrapure water and blown drying with nitrogen, where the organic solution treatment agent comprises acid buffer, fluoride, amine organic solvent, ketone organic solvent and pure water, the acidic etching solution comprises hydrofluoric acid, acetic acid, ammonium acetate, Tween 80 (RTM: Polyoxyethylenesorbitan monooleate) and pure water.
   USE - The regenerating quartz thermal shield is useful for manufacturing of integrated circuits (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerating quartz thermal shield comprises (A) soaking with organic solvent, placing the to-be-regenerated quartz thermal shield in an organic solution treatment agent at a temperature of 30-50 degrees C, and removing or loosely attaching metallized organic matter on the surface, (B) spraying carbon dioxide particle on the quartz thermal shield, where the gas pressure used is 0.3-0.9 MPa, and the ice output is 0.1-1 kg/minutes, (C), placing the quartz shielding plate after carbon dioxide particle spray treatment into the etching tank, using acidic etching solution for surface etching, washing with pure water, and completing the surface repair after drying and (D) transporting micro-etched quartz thermal shield to a class 100 clean room for ultrasonic cleaning, and washing with ultrapure water and blown drying with nitrogen, where the organic solution treatment agent comprises 5-10 pts. wt. acid buffer, 5-10 pts. wt. fluoride, 10-20 pts. wt. amine organic solvent, 10-20 pts. wt. ketone organic solvent and 10-30 pts. wt. pure water, the acidic etching solution comprises 5-15 pts. wt. hydrofluoric acid, 10-20 pts. wt. acetic acid, 10-20 pts. wt. ammonium acetate, 0.02-2 pts. wt. Tween 80 (RTM: Polyoxyethylenesorbitan monooleate) and 30-80 pts. wt. pure water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (A), the washing buffer solution is formic acid, acetic acid, and oxalic acid; the fluoride is fluoride, ammonium fluoride, and/or ammonium bifluoride; the amine organic solvent is monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine and/or N,N-dimethylformamide; the ketone organic solvent is cyclohexanone and/or N-methylpyrrolidone; the pure water is deionized water with a resistivity greater than 6 M. In the step (A), the distance between the particle spray gun and the quartz thermal shield is set to 20-40 mm, and the angle between the direction and the clean surface is 45 degrees . Preferred Method: In the step (A), the immersion treatment time of the quartz thermal shield is 10-30 minutes, taking it out, washing with ultrapure water for 3-5 minutes and blow drying. In the method: after the carbon dioxide particle spray cleaning is completed, detecting component cleaning effect by an ultraviolet lamp with a wavelength of 254 nm, when there is no bright spot on the surface of the quartz thermal shield, the detected particles are sprayed. In the step (C), the etching temperature is 20-30 degrees C, and the etching time is 30-60 minutes after etching, washing with 18 M deionized water for 5 minutes and blow drying. In the step (D), the ultrasonic frequency used is 40-80 KHz, the ultrasonic cleaning time is 10-30 minutes, and washing with 18 M ultrapure water for 5-10 minutes, and drying with nitrogen.

PN KR2025920-B1
TI System comprises e.g. reaction space containing aqueous solution, cathode submerged in aqueous solution in reaction space, anode made of metal material partially submerged in aqueous solution in reaction space and metal recovery unit.
AU GUNTAE K
   KIM C
   KIM J W
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 201986370R
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a reaction space containing an aqueous solution, a cathode at least partially submerged in the aqueous solution in the reaction space, an anode made of a metal material at least partially submerged in the aqueous solution in the reaction space, and a metal recovery unit. The carbon dioxide introduced into the aqueous solution is captured by bicarbonate ions to produce hydrogen ions. The hydrogen ions react with electrons of the cathode to produce hydrogen. The metal recovery unit includes a supply unit for receiving an aqueous solution in which the metal ions oxidized in the anode are dissolved, a recovery space accommodating the supplied aqueous solution, a second cathode of the same material as the anode submerged in the aqueous solution contained in the recovery space, a second anode submerged in the aqueous solution accommodated in the recovery space, and a power supply unit for supplying power into the second cathode and the second anode.
   USE - Used as system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system recovers metal consumed in carbon dioxide with high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System comprises a reaction space containing an aqueous solution, a cathode (110) at least partially submerged in the aqueous solution in the reaction space, an anode (158) made of a metal material at least partially submerged in the aqueous solution in the reaction space, and a metal recovery unit. The carbon dioxide introduced into the aqueous solution is captured by bicarbonate ions to produce hydrogen ions. The hydrogen ions react with electrons of the cathode to produce hydrogen. The metal recovery unit includes a supply unit for receiving an aqueous solution in which the metal ions oxidized in the anode are dissolved, a recovery space accommodating the supplied aqueous solution, a second cathode of the same material as the anode at least partially submerged in the aqueous solution contained in the recovery space, a second anode at least partially submerged in the aqueous solution accommodated in the recovery space, and a power supply unit for supplying power into the second cathode and the second anode. The system utilizes carbon dioxide to recover oxidized metal ions from the supplied aqueous solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system.
   Cathode (110)
   First accommodation space (111)
   First inlet (112)
   First outlet (113)
   Anode (158)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anode is made of vanadium, chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, aluminum or zinc.

PN CN109906929-A
TI Method for cultivating mung bean sprout, involves            strict screening of raw beans and eliminating bad            beans, putting screened beans into hot water, and            stirring, followed by spreading beans in incubator and            completing bean sprout cultivation.
AU CHEN S
AE SHAANXI QINDOUYUAN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201957397Q
AB    NOVELTY - A mung bean sprout cultivating method involves                strict screening of raw beans, eliminating bad                beans and no germ beans, putting the screened beans                into hot water for, and stirring, soaking the                screened beans in warm water, after soaking the                beans, draining the beans and spreading the beans                in the incubator, sterilizing the incubator,                carrying the incubator into the sterile breeding                room for the sprouting and air conditioning                cultivation system, and controlling the temperature                to be sprayed, taking the volume fraction of                circulating gas under volume fraction of the                recycled gas composed of oxygen, carbon dioxide,                nitrogen and rest are rare gases, taking the volume                fraction of circulating gas under volume fraction                of the circulating gas composed of oxygen, carbon                dioxide, nitrogen and rest are rare gases and                completing bean sprout cultivation.
   USE - Method for cultivating mung bean sprout.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating mung bean                sprout with high nutrition content, low toxicity                chemical medicine residue, in accurate                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mung bean sprout cultivating method involves                strict screening of raw beans, eliminating bad                beans and no germ beans, putting the screened beans                into hot water for 1-2 minutes, and stirring,                soaking the screened beans in warm water for 90-150                minutes, after soaking the beans, draining the                beans and evenly spreading the beans in the                incubator, sterilizing the incubator by high                temperature sterilization or 100 PPM sodium                hypochlorite spray, carrying the incubator into the                sterile breeding room for the sprouting and air                conditioning cultivation system, and controlling                the temperature to be sprayed at intervals of 22-24                degrees C, taking the volume fraction of                circulating gas during the 0th to 3rd day of                cultivation under volume fraction of the recycled                gas composed of 19.6-20% oxygen, 0.030-0.032%                carbon dioxide, 76-80% nitrogen and the rest are                rare gases, taking the volume fraction of                circulating gas during the 4th to 8th day of                cultivation under volume fraction of the                circulating gas composed of 18.0-19% oxygen,                0.020-0.022% carbon dioxide, 73-77% nitrogen and                rest are rare gases and after the 9th day,                completing bean sprout cultivation. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is also included for an air-conditioning                device.

PN US2019185884-A1; US10428353-B2
TI Producing transportation or heating fuel involves e.g. treating cellulosic feedstock in processing step, conducting solids-liquid separation on process stream, removing carbon dioxide and generating renewable fuel credit.
AU FOODY P J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 201951678L
AB    NOVELTY - Producing transportation or heating fuel comprises: (i) treating a cellulosic feedstock in at least one processing step that release extractives comprising acetic acid, and/or acetate from the feedstock; (ii) conducting a solids-liquid separation on a process stream comprising the extractives and solids; (iii) feeding at least a portion of the aqueous stream; (iv) removing at least 80 wt.% of the carbon dioxide present in the crude biogas to produce a purified biogas; (v) carrying out or causing at least one parties to carry out a process; (vi) introducing the purified biogas produced in step (iv) to a pipeline and causing withdrawal of an amount of methane; (vii) providing at least one products obtained; and (viii) generating or causing generation of a renewable fuel credit with respect to the product.
   USE - The process is useful for producing transportation or heating fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The process economically captures transportation or heating fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing transportation or heating fuel comprises: (i) treating a cellulosic feedstock in at least one processing step that release extractives comprising acetic acid, and/or acetate from the feedstock; (ii) conducting a solids-liquid separation on a process stream comprising the extractives and solids, thus producing an aqueous stream comprising the extractives and a solids stream comprising insoluble components; (iii) feeding at least a portion of the aqueous stream comprising at least one of the extractive to an anaerobic digester to produce a crude biogas that comprises carbon dioxide; (iv) removing at least 80 wt.% of the carbon dioxide present in the crude biogas to produce a purified biogas; (v) carrying out or causing at least one parties to carry out a process comprising subjecting solids from at least one stream of solids stream comprising the insoluble components and a stream derived from to a thermal process to produce a fuel or fuel intermediate; (vi) introducing the purified biogas produced in step (iv) to a pipeline and causing withdrawal of an amount of methane from the pipeline corresponding to an amount of the purified biogas introduced to the pipeline; (vii) providing at least one products obtained or derived from step (v) and/or step (vi) for use as a transportation or heating fuel; and (viii) generating or causing generation of a renewable fuel credit with respect to the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The product is produced or derived from the methane withdrawn from the pipeline. The process comprises obtaining a liquid stream from the anaerobic digester and recirculating the liquid stream or its portion to the anaerobic digester or to a stage upstream of the anaerobic digester; (a) obtaining a liquid stream from the anaerobic digester and introducing the liquid stream or a portion thereof to the anaerobic digester or to a stage upstream of the anaerobic digester; and (b) conducting a second solids-liquid separation on the solids stream comprising the insoluble components or a stream derived to produce a second aqueous stream and introducing the second aqueous stream or a portion thereof to the digester or to a stage upstream of the digester. The process comprises obtaining a slurry comprising microorganisms from the anaerobic digester, conducting a solids-liquid separation on the slurry to obtain a concentrated slurry of microorganisms and introducing the concentrated slurry of microorganisms to the anaerobic digester or to a stage upstream of the anaerobic digester. The aqueous stream comprising at least one of the extractives or the portion of the aqueous stream further comprises sulfur and the process further comprises: (aq) converting the sulfur or a portion thereof to gaseous hydrogen sulfide in the digester; (bq) treating the gaseous hydrogen sulfide to produce elemental sulfur or an oxide of sulfur; (cq) recovering an acid comprising sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, and/or sulfur dioxide from the elemental sulfur or oxide of sulfur; and (dq) using the recovered acid in the process as a catalyst or for neutralizing alkali. The solids stream comprises the insoluble components comprises densified solids. The solids stream comprises lignin. The thermal process comprises gasification or pyrolysis. The fuel or fuel intermediate produced in step (v) comprises pyrolysis oil. Preferred Components: The cellulosic feedstock is straw, stover or an energy crop. The fuel or fuel intermediate produced in step (v) comprises syngas, hydrogen, methane, or pyrolysis oil. The fuel or fuel intermediate produced in step (v) comprises dimethyl ether, methanol, ethanol, or liquid hydrocarbons. The fuel or fuel intermediate produced in step (v) comprises gasoline. The fuel or fuel intermediate comprises pyrolysis oil. The fuel or fuel intermediate comprises diesel.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN108949673-A
TI Primarily isolating fetal rat cardiomyocytes by cutting fetal rat heart, digesting with trypsin, pipetting Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium, removing cell suspension, lysing, culturing, removing fibroblast, inoculating and culturing cell.
AU ZHAO C
   HUANG X
   LI N
AE WUHAN MYHALIC BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2018A27554
AB    NOVELTY - Method for primarily isolating fetal rat cardiomyocytes involves (a) cutting 19-22 days old fetal rat heart into tissue block of about 0.5-1.5 mm3, and resuspending with trypsin and type II collagenase, digesting, naturally precipitating and discarding supernatant, (b) resuspending with trypsin, type II collagenase for 8-12 seconds, allowing to stand, taking supernatant, repeating the process and separating supernatant and gently pipetting Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM) containing fetal calf serum to prepare a cell suspension, (c) removing cell suspension into undigested tissue, centrifuging, repeating, and lysing in red blood cell lysate and (d) resuspending in DMEM containing fetal bovine serum, placing in carbon dioxide incubator for 150-200 minutes, and removing fibroblasts, aspirating cell suspension, adjusting cell concentration, and inoculating single cell suspension into culture plate and culturing in carbon dioxide incubator, and changing every other day.
   USE - The method is useful for primarily isolating fetal rat cardiomyocytes.
   ADVANTAGE - The isolated fetal rat cardiomyocytes are not easily deformed, and has high purity and high survival rate and strong vigor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for primarily isolating fetal rat cardiomyocytes involves (a) cutting 19-22 days old fetal rat heart into tissue block of about 0.5-1.5 mm3, and resuspending with 0.1-0.15% trypsin and 0.08-0.12% type II collagenase, digesting at 46.5-37.5 degrees C for 4-6 minutes, naturally precipitating and discarding supernatant, (b) resuspending with 0.1-0.15% trypsin, 0.08-0.12% type II collagenase for 8-12 seconds, allowing to stand, taking supernatant, repeating the process for 3-5 times, and separating the supernatant and gently pipetting Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM) medium containing 8-12% fetal calf serum to prepare a cell suspension, (c) removing cell suspension into undigested tissue, centrifuging at 800-1200 revolutions per minute for 8-12 minutes, repeating for 2-4 times, and lysing in red blood cell lysate at 4 degrees C for 4-6 minutes and (d) resuspending in DMEM containing 8-12% fetal bovine serum, placing in carbon dioxide incubator for 150-200 minutes, and removing fibroblasts, aspirating cell suspension, adjusting the cell concentration to 4.5-5.5x 105 cells/ml, and inoculating single cell suspension into culture plate and culturing in carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, and changing every other day.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the freshly removed heart is immediately placed in pre-cooled phosphate buffered saline (PBS) and rinsed 3 times. In step (c), the collected cell suspension is passed through stainless steel mesh having a pore size of 180-220 mesh to undigested tissue. In step (d), the differential iron wall separation technique is used to remove fibroblasts.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Gas permeation membrane for laminated body and gas-separation module comprises polymerizable liquid crystal compound in which polymer is optically arranged in at least one axis in a molecular arrangement.
AU ENDO K
   SHISHIKURA M
   FUJISAWA Y
   KUWANA Y
AE DIC CORP (DNIN-C)
   DIC CORP (DNIN-C)
GA 201896755C
AB    NOVELTY - A gas permeation membrane comprises polymerizable liquid crystal compound, where polymer is optically arranged in greater than or equal to 1 axis in a molecular arrangement and has scattering profile obtained by oblique incidence X-ray-scattering measuring method, in which maximum peak in scattering-angle 2 theta is at 5-15 degrees , integration is performed with scattering angle 2 theta of 5 degrees around the same peak, integrated value is 5-175 degrees in the azimuth angle of scattering profile, and width of azimuth angle indicating half value or more of the maximum value is 30-108 degrees .
   USE - A gas permeation membrane for laminated body and gas-separation module used for separating hydrogen, helium, methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, ethane, ethylene, propane, propylene, butane, and hydrogen sulfide, or dissolved gas (all claimed). Can be used for carbon dioxide recovery from thermal power plants, steel works blast furnaces, etc.; carbon dioxide recovery from carbon dioxide sources emitted from internal combustion engine systems, such as automobiles and ships; natural gas, naphtha, liquefied natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas etc.; supply gas mixture, such as offgas derived from petrochemical industry; energy source, such as hydrogen and methane from biogas generated from sludge from sewage treatment facility, waste disposal plant, etc.; improving combustion efficiency of oxygen enrichment membrane for various air conditioning equipment and oxygen enrichment film used for products aiming to maintain and promote health by raising the concentration of oxygen in the air; oxidation prevention; corrosion prevention; explosion proof applications; and nitrogen enrichment membranes used for products intended to improve the freshness of foods.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas permeation membrane has excellent gas selectivity, maintains favorable gas permeability, and can be produced by industrially simple method. The laminated body and the gas-separation module have excellent gas separation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) laminated body comprising gas permeation membrane laminated on gas-permeable base material; and
   (2) gas-separation module comprising the gas permeation membrane formed in a hollow fiber form, which is arranged in a container, where either the inside of a hollow fiber or the hollow fiber exterior connects with mixture gas or dissolved gas supply port, and the other communicates with gas exhaust port.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The major axis direction of the molecular arrangement of greater than or equal to 1 axis of the polymer is horizontal to both surfaces of the film surface or perpendicular to both surfaces of the film. The major axis direction of the molecular arrangement of greater than or equal to 1 axis of the polymer is horizontal with respect to both surfaces of the film surface and is helical in the thickness direction of the film. The major axis direction of the molecular arrangement of greater than or equal to 1 axis of the polymer is horizontal to one film surface and perpendicular to the other film surface. Film thickness is 0.005-50 mu m. Preferred Components: The polymerizable composition comprises greater than or equal to 2 polymerizable groups, hard segment containing greater than or equal to 3 ring structures, and and soft segment; polymerization initiator; organic solvent; and if necessary, a surfactant; and the polymer is obtained by coating gas-permeable layer with polymerizable composition having average particle size of 1-100 nm, subjecting gas-permeable layer to alignment processing so that molecular arrangement is optically uniaxial or more, and polymerizing. Polymeric compound is a compound of formula (P1-Sf1)n1-HD-(Sf2-P2)n2 (1). Polymeric liquid crystal compound has structure of formula P11-(S11-X11)n11-(A11-Z11)j11-M11-(Z12-A12)j12-(X12-S12)n12-P12 (1-a-1). Polymeric liquid crystal compound has structure of formula P311-(S311X311)n31-(A311-Z311)j311-M311-(Z312-A312)j312-R311 (3-1). Gas-permeable base material is a film, porous membrane, or hollow fiber membrane selected from polyethylene glycol, polypropylene glycol, polyvinyl acetate, polydimethylsiloxane, polyimide,
