PT P
PN CN108660497-A; CN108660497-B
TI Method for photogenerated charge separation and carbon dioxide reduction performance of Pd/Mn/TNTs photoelectrode, comprises e.g. carrying out anodic oxidation of the metal titanium foil, platinum sheet and ammonium fluoride and drying.
AU FENG Y
   WU J
   LIU J
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201883603N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for photogenerated charge separation and carbon dioxide reduction performance of Pd/Mn/TNTs photoelectrode, comprises e.g. (i) carrying out anodic oxidation treatment of the metal titanium foil, pre-treated metal titanium foil as anode, platinum sheet as cathode, ammonium fluoride glycerol as electrolyte solution, washing, drying and calcining to obtain titanium dioxide nano tube array, (ii) adding acetic acid into potassium permanganate solution, magnetically stirring, then adding titanium dioxide nano tube array, then vacuum drying and calcining to obtain manganese oxide modified titanium dioxide nanotube array optical electrode, and (iii) reacting Mn/TNTs photoelectrode, platinum sheet, saturated calomel electrode to obtain palladium deposited Mn/TNTs photoelectrode, washing and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is useful for photogenerated charge separation and carbon dioxide reduction performance of Pd/Mn/TNTs photoelectrode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for photogenerated charge separation and carbon dioxide reduction performance of Pd/Mn/TNTs photoelectrode, comprises (i) taking pre-treated metal titanium foil as anode, platinum sheet as cathode, ammonium fluoride glycerol as electrolyte solution, carrying out anodic oxidation treatment of the metal titanium foil under anodic oxidation voltage of 5-20 V for 1-5 hours, taking out the metal titanium foil, washing using deionized water, drying, then adding into a muffle furnace, controlling temperature to 100-800 degrees C and calcining for 2-10 hours to obtain titanium dioxide nano tube array, (ii) adding acetic acid into potassium permanganate solution, magnetically stirring for 5-100 minutes, then adding titanium dioxide nano tube array, then dipping in vacuum drying box for 1-10 hours, taking out, then vacuum drying at 10-100 degrees C for 1-10 hours, calcining at 10-600 degrees C for 1-10 hours under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain manganese oxide (MnOx) modified titanium dioxide nanotube array optical electrode i.e. light Mn/TNTs electrode, where x is atomic number of oxygen, and (iii) taking Mn/TNTs photoelectrode as work electrode, platinum sheet as counter electrode, saturated calomel electrode as reference electrode, palladium(II) chloride of sodium sulfate solution as electrolyte, then controlling the constant potential to -0.1 to -2 V, reacting for 1-50 minutes to obtain palladium deposited Mn/TNTs photoelectrode, washing using deionized water, then vacuum drying at 10-200 degrees C for 1-20 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the metal titanium foil is pretreated with oxalic acid; The drying temperature is 50-100 degrees C and drying time is 1-10 hours; the heating rate of the muffle furnace is 1-10 degrees C/minute. Preferred Components: The concentration of the ammonium fluoride glycerol solution is 0.5 wt.% of the medium ammonium fluoride. In step (ii), the concentration of the potassium permanganate solution is 0.01 g/ml; the amount of acetic acid is 5-100 ml; the dipping temperature is 20 degrees C; the rate of temperature heating during calcination is 1-10 degrees C/minute. In step (iii), the concentration of palladium(II) chloride in the sodium sulfate solution of palladium (II) chloride is 1/asterisk10-5 mol/l.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); M11 (Electroplating; electrolytic treatment of or with metals - including electro-deposition of metals, electro-plating apparatus, electro-forming, electro-erosion, spark erosion, anodising (electrophoretically) coating metals and electrolytic cleaning and polishing (C25).); M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).)

PN CN108613454-A; CN208736003-U
TI Refrigerator for promoting sugar conversion of vegetables, comprises refrigerating compartment, freezing compartment and control system, where control system comprises main control panel, display panel, refrigerating door, and freezing fan.
AU GUO C
   LIU Z
AE WHIRLPOOL CHINA CO LTD (WHIR-C)
GA 201880348B
AB    NOVELTY - Refrigerator comprises refrigerating compartment (4), freezing compartment and control system, where the control system comprises main control panel (1), display panel (3), refrigerating door (2), and freezing fan. The vegetable sugar conversion chamber (5) is installed in the refrigerator compartment. The vegetable sugar conversion chamber includes a box body, and a conversion chamber door for opening/closing the box body. The conversion chamber door is hinged to the box body, and a control button is arranged on the box body, and LED lamp, temperature sensor, carbon dioxide generator and water mist generator are installed in the box body. The control button is connected with the main control board. An air inlet/outlet for the cold air generated by the freezing fan is opened on the side wall of the casing. An air inlet door for controlling the entry of cold air is installed at the air inlet.
   USE - Refrigerator for promoting sugar conversion of vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerator precipitates sugar produced by photosynthesis of vegetables in the early stage to improve the taste of vegetables.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The main control board is respectively connected to air inlet door, LED lamp, temperature sensor, carbon dioxide generator, and water mist generator. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling a refrigerator for promoting sugar conversion of a vegetable, which involves:
   (A) using temperature sensor that transmits the temperature of vegetable sugar conversion chamber to the main control panel in real time;
   (B) controlling opening/closing of the air inlet door and air outlet door by main control board according to the received temperature information so that the temperature of the vegetable sugar conversion room is maintained at T1 degrees C for heat preservation;
   (C) controlling the LED light to be turned on, and controlling the carbon dioxide generator and the water mist generator to perform intermittent work; and
   (D) turning off LED lamp, carbon dioxide generator and water mist generator after set time, and converting the vegetable sugar into the indoor by controlling the opening and closing of the air inlet door and the air outlet door by reducing the temperature to T2 degrees C, where T1 greater than T2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of refrigerator.
   Main control panel (1)
   Refrigerating door (2)
   Display panel (3)
   Refrigerating compartment (4)
   Vegetable sugar conversion chamber (5)

PN CN108210557-A; CN108210557-B
TI Edible biological product useful for eliminating flatulence, gastric mucosal repair and care.
AU WEI J
AE SHAANXI LIANGDI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
   SHAANXI LIANGDI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201854684L
AB    NOVELTY - Edible biological product, is claimed. The edible biological product is obtained by (i) loading the original propolis into a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction tank, adding the carbon dioxide liquid for supercritical extraction and obtaining the yellow propolis extract containing flavonoids, (ii) placing the perilla seeds into the press, pressing at room temperature and obtaining the perilla seed oil containing a-linolenic acid, (iii) soaking the Gallus gallus domesticus with 60-80?OC perilla seed oil for 1-10 hours in a mass ratio of 1:10, obtaining a Gallus gallus domesticus oil, (iv) obtaining a transparent ruby red oil containing shikonin immersion liquid, (v) obtaining a product liquid and (vi) dividing the product liquid into bottled products and preparing into soft capsule products.
   USE - The product is useful for eliminating flatulence and gastric mucosal repair (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has good curing rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Edible biological product, is claimed. The edible biological product is obtained by (i) loading the original propolis into a carbon dioxide supercritical extraction tank, adding the carbon dioxide liquid for supercritical extraction, where the extraction temperature is 15-30 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 8-10 MPa and the extraction time is 1-2 hours and obtaining the yellow propolis extract containing flavonoids, where the mass percentage of supercritical extraction is 1-2%, (ii) placing the perilla seeds into the press, pressing at room temperature and obtaining the perilla seed oil containing a-linolenic acid, (iii) soaking the Gallus gallus domesticus with 60-80?OC perilla seed oil for 1-10 hours in a mass ratio of 1:10, obtaining a Gallus gallus domesticus oil, (iv) breaking the comfrey with machinery, immersing in the perilla seed oil at 50-60 degrees C for 1-2 hours according to the ratio of 1:10 and centrifuging at low temperature to obtain a transparent ruby red oil containing shikonin immersion liquid, (v) mixing the above propolis extract, Gallus gallus domesticus solution and purple grass extract into the remaining amount of perilla seed oil, removing the precipitate by freeze centrifugation under the following process conditions, where the freezing temperature is 0-4 degrees C, the centrifugal speed is 3000-4000 revolutions/minute and the time is 10-20 minutes and separating the centrifugal liquid by a sonic membrane separator to obtain a product liquid having a molecular weight of less than 10000D and (vi) dividing the product liquid into bottled products and preparing into soft capsule products.

PT P
PN CN107790483-A
TI Stabilizing fly ash using an incineration residue stabilizing device comprises e.g. collecting waste acid during landfill composting period in the landfill, storing, separating, storing and collecting fly ash from waste incineration device.
AU CAI D
   CHI Q
   MA W
   SHI Y
   WANG Y
AE QINGDAO HAICHENG INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201823421F
AB    NOVELTY - Stabilizing fly ash using an incineration residue stabilizing device comprises (i) collecting waste acid during the landfill composting period in the landfill, storing, separating and storing; (ii) collecting fly ash from waste incineration device, adding into the spray chamber; (iii) setting the temperature of the reactor, opening the peristaltic pump and mixing the gas in the reactor; and (iv) adding the mixed gas into the reactor, dispersing with fly ash, extracting the material in the reactor via the sampling port for monitoring and stopping aerating after 90-300 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for stabilizing fly ash using an incineration residue stabilizing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate, has simple operation, does not produce secondary pollution. The device realizes the fly ash of the situ fixed at the indoor and outdoor and reuses the reaction process of acid gas e.g. carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Stabilizing fly ash using an incineration residue stabilizing device comprises (i) collecting waste acid during the landfill composting period in the landfill, storing in the waste gas storage tank, separating the collected acidic waste gas from the carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen sulfide gas, storing separately in the carbon dioxide gas cylinder and the hydrogen sulfide gas cylinder; (ii) collecting fly ash from waste incineration device, adding into the spray chamber to reduce the temperature of fly ash and stabilizes the moisture content of the fly ash to 15-35%, where the porosity is stabilized at 35-55% and then passing into the reactor through the injection port; (iii) setting the temperature of the reactor to 25-60 degrees C, opening the peristaltic pump, mixing the gas in the reactor at a speed of 1-10 l/minutes, where the carbon dioxide in mixed gas volume ratio is 60-100% and the volume ratio of hydrogen sulfide is 0-40%; and (iv) adding the mixed gas into the reactor through quartz sand pad layer and the sand cushion layer, uniformly dispersing with fly ash, extracting the material in the reactor via the sampling port for monitoring and stopping aerating after 90-300 minutes, where the vent hole maintains the pressure in the reactor to 0.1-0.6 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The incineration residue stabilizing device comprises a carbon dioxide gas cylinder, hydrogen sulfide gas bottle, gas mixing device, rotor flowmeter, peristaltic pump, air inlet, water outlet, quartz sand pad layer of the fly ash stabilization device, sand cushion layer, sample outlet, temperature probe, thermostat, vent hole, feeding port, sampling port, sample placing area, movable cover and a reactor, where the reactor is made of acid and alkali resistant poly vinyl chloride having length x width x height is 50 cm x 50 cm x 125 cm size of the cylindrical structure. The upper part of the reactor is covered with a movable cover plate and the reactor type. The upper part of the movable cover plate is provided with outer side of the bottom feeding port. The movable cover plate is uniformly provided with multiple through holes, thus adjusts the air pressure inside the reactor. The bottom of the reactor is provided with an air inlet. The upper air inlet hole of the reactor is provided with a quartz sand pad layer and the sand cushion layer, thus the gas is uniformly dispersed in the reactor, where one side of the bottom of the reactor is provided with a water outlet to permeate into the quartz sand pad layer and the sand cushion layer liquid, which is discharged. The outer side of the reactor is provided with a carbon dioxide gas cylinder and hydrogen sulfide gas steel bottle. The two bottle is filled with the reaction gas. The carbon dioxide gas bottle and a hydrogen sulfide gas steel bottle is connected with the gas mixing device through the conduit, thus the two kinds of gas are mixed completely. The gas mixing device is connected to the air inlet hole through pipe. The section of the connecting conduit is provided with rotor flowmeter and a peristaltic pump. The mixed gas enters the reactor through the air inlet rotor flowmeter to control flow rate by the peristaltic pump. The sand cushion layer is provided with a discharge port for discharging. The reactor of fly ash after stabilization of the external wall on one side is away from the bottom of the reactor position of three different heights, which is provided with three sampling ports for sampling of reaction of fly ash. The reactor is provided with a thermostat by wire and connected with temperature probe set in the reactor, thus detects and regulates the temperature inside the reactor. The discharging opening is provided with a handle and a sealing rubber ring to ensure the tightness. The distance between the bottom of the reactor having a distance of 34 cm , 72 cm and 106 cm, where the sampling opening square structure has 4.9 cm x 4.9 cm and the sampling port is sealed by a rubber plug. The thickness of the quartz sand pad layer is 3.5 cm, where the quartz sand pad layer is provided with sand cushion layer, where the thickness is 10 cm and the sand grain diameter is 1.5-2.5 cm.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

TI Preparing aromatic carboxylic acid compounds using            carbon dioxide useful in e.g. fine chemical industry            comprises dispersing bromo aromatic hydrocarbon,            palladium catalyst, organic phosphine, alkali and            silane in solvent, introducing carbon dioxide for            carboxylation reaction and acidifying.
AU XIONG W
   JIANG H
   LI D
   QI C
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 202204035B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aromatic carboxylic acid compounds                using carbon dioxide comprises dispersing bromo                aromatic hydrocarbon, palladium catalyst, organic                phosphine, alkali and silane in solvent,                introducing carbon dioxide for carboxylation                reaction and acidifying to obtain final                product.
   USE - The aromatic carboxylic acid compound is                useful in the fields of organic synthesis, fine                chemical industry and medical sanitation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction conditions, and                high yield and simple operation; is simple, and                suitable for industrial production; and utilizes                easily available raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carboxylation reaction                  is carried out at 60-100degrees Celsius for 12-36                  hours. The carboxylation reaction is carried out                  under a carbon dioxide pressure of 0.8-1.2                  atmosphere.Preferred Components: The molar ratio                  between bromo aromatic hydrocarbon, palladium                  catalyst, organic phosphine, alkali and silane is                  1:0.015-0.04:0.015-0.05:1-3:1-3. The bromo                  aromatic hydrocarbon is bromobenzene,                  1-bromo-4-methoxybenzene,                  1-bromo-4-(trifluoromethyl)benzene,                  4-bromothioanisole, 1-bromo-4-methylbenzene,                  1-bromo-4-nitrobenzene, 4-bromobenzonitrile,                  1-(4-bromophenyl)ethanone, 4-bromo-1,                  1'-biphenyl, 1-bromo-4-fluorobenzene,                  1,4-dibromobenzene, 1-bromo-4-phenoxybenzene,                  N-(4-bromophenyl)acetamide,                  1-bromo-4-n-butylbenzene,                  1-bromo-4-tert-butylbenzene,                  1-bromo-4-(trifluoromethoxy)benzene,                  1-bromo-4-ethylbenzene,                  1-bromo-3-(trifluoromethyl)benzene,                  1-bromo-3-fluorobenzene,                  1-bromo-3-methoxybenzene,                  1-(3-bromophenyl)ethanone,                  1-bromo-2-methylbenzene, 4-bromothioanisole,                  4-bromostyrene, 2-bromoanisole,                  3-bromofluorobenzene,                  1-bromo-3,5-dimethoxybenzene, 1-bromonaphthalene,                  2-bromonaphthalene, 2-bromothiophene,                  3-bromothiophene, 9-bromophenanthrene and/or                  9-bromoanthracene. The palladium catalyst is                  palladium(II) acetate,                  tris(dibenzylideneacetone)dipalladium(0),                  bis(dibenzylideneacetone)palladium(0),                  palladium(II) chloride, palladium(II) bromide,                  dichlorobis(pyridine)palladium(II),                  bis(acetonitrile)dichloropalladium(II),                  allylpalladium(II) chloride dimer, potassium                  tetrachloropalladate(II), palladium(II) iodide,                  palladium(II) trifluoroacetate and/or                  tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0). The                  organic phosphine is                  1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)benzene,                  1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)ethane,                  1,3-bis(diphenylphosphino)propane,                  1,4-bis(diphenylphosphino)butane, 2,2'-bis                  (diphenylphosphino)-1, 1'-binaphthyl,                  bis(2-diphenylphosphinophenyl)ether and/or                  4,5-bis(diphenylphosphino)-9,9-dimethylxanthene.                  The alkali is triethylamine, pyridine,                  tetramethylethylenediamine,                  1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene,                  1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane, potassium                  carbonate and/or lithium carbonate. The silane is                  phenylsilane, diphenylsilane and/or                  bis(trimethylsilyl)methane. The solvent is                  toluene, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone and/or n-heptane.

PN US2020122095-A1; KR2020045966-A; KR2339455-B1; US11484845-B2
TI Hybrid membrane used for separating gas e.g.            propylene/propane, ethylene/ethane, and carbon            dioxide/carbon monoxide, comprises polymer matrix and            nanoparticles including zeolite imidazolate framework,            dispersed in polymer matrix.
AU LEE J S
   CHO K Y
   LEE J W
   OH B K
   NA J G
   AN H S
   YONG C K
   OH B
   NA J
   AN H
   OHJINWOO
AE UNIV SOGANG RES & BUSINESS DEV FOUND (UYSO-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOGANG RES & BUSINESS DEV FOUND (UYSO-Non-standard)
GA 2020392064
AB    NOVELTY - A hybrid membrane comprises a polymer matrix                and nanoparticles including zeolite imidazolate                framework (ZIF), dispersed in the polymer                matrix.
   USE - Hybrid membrane used for separating gas e.g.                propylene/propane, ethylene/ethane, carbon                dioxide/methane, carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide/nitrogen, nitrogen/methane,                n-butane/isobutane, hydrogen/methane,                hydrogen/propane and hydrogen/propane (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hybrid membrane has excellent gas                separation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for gas                separation membrane, which comprises the hybrid                membrane for separating one or more gases from                mixed gas including two or more gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The crystal surface of                  amorphous zeolite imidazolate framework (AZIF)                  nanoparticles have distance (011) of 11.9-12.1                  degrees when metal ions are cobalt, preferably                  12.0-12.25 ANG, when metal ions are zinc. The                  nanoparticles exhibit a decrease of 3 %mass or                  less within 400 degrees C in thermogravimetric                  analysis test, an infrared (IR) peak of cobalt to                  nitrogen (Co-N) of the AZIF nanoparticles is                  increased as 1-3 cm-1 by the alkylamine-based                  organic ligand when the metal ions are cobalt,                  and an IR peak of zinc to nitrogen (Zn-N) of the                  AZIF nanoparticles is increased as 1.5-4 cm-1 by                  the alkylamine-based organic ligand when the                  metal ions are zinc. The nanoparticles have                  permeability to propylene of 200-400 Barrer,                  preferably 40-70 Barrer and a propylene/propane                  having selectivity of 270-400, preferably 33-60                  at 35 degrees C, when a central atom of the                  nanoparticles is cobalt, and the nanoparticles                  have a permeability to propylene of 300-600                  Barrer, preferably 40-80 Barrer and a                  propylene/propane having selectivity of 140-300,                  preferably 33-60 at 35 degrees C, when the                  central atom of the nanoparticles is zinc, and                  propylene/propane energy selectivity of                  1000-10000.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nanoparticles                  comprise metal ions and an organic ligand coupled                  to the metal ions. The organic ligand comprises                  imidazolate-based organic ligand and an                  alkylamine-based organic ligand. The membrane                  separates a mixed gas of gas chosen from                  propylene/propane, ethylene/ethane, carbon                  dioxide/methane, carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide,                  carbon dioxide/nitrogen, nitrogen/methane,                  n-butane/isobutane, hydrogen/methane,                  hydrogen/propane and hydrogen/propane. Preferred                  Properties: The hybrid membrane has at least one                  of a distance between crystal surfaces, and                  distribution and coupling strength of the crystal                  surfaces in the nanoparticles is adjusted based                  on ratio of the alkylamine-based organic ligand,                  and the alkylamine-based organic ligand is                  directly combined with the metal ions. The                  nanoparticles have particle size of 100 nm or                  less and pore size of 0.1-1 nm. The membrane has                  thickness of 50 nm to100 mu m and gases with                  molecular size difference of 0.1-5 degrees to be                  separated from each other. Preferred Composition:                  The ratio of the imidazolate-based organic ligand                  to the alkylamine-based organic ligand is                  99.9:0.01-80:20. The membrane comprises 15-60                  wt.%, preferably 30-60 wt.% nanoparticles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer matrix is                  formed by comprising at least one chosen from                  polyimide, polysulfone, polyethersulfone,                  cellulose acetate, polydimethylsiloxane and                  polyvinyl acetate.

PT P
PN WO2019023414-A1; US2019085472-A1
TI Producing strong alkaline solution for atmospheric            carbon dioxide capture, involves feeding alkaline            solution containing bicarbonate and carbonate ions into            electrochemical module to form hydroxide solution,            allowing hydroxide solution.
AU WILLAUER H D
   HARDY D R
   DIMASCIO F
   WILLIAMS F
AE US SEC OF NAVY (USNA-C)
   US SEC OF NAVY (USNA-C)
GA 201912230W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing strong alkaline solution involves                feeding alkaline solution containing bicarbonate                and carbonate ions into electrochemical module to                form hydroxide solution, allowing hydroxide                solution to chemically absorb carbon dioxide from                atmosphere to form re-equilibrated bicarbonate and                carbonate solution. The re-equilibrated bicarbonate                and carbonate solution back is fed into the                electrochemical module. The recovery of carbon                dioxide along with regeneration of the alkaline                solution to complete the cycle.
   USE - Method for producing strong alkaline solution                for atmospheric carbon dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce strong alkaline                solution that is highly volatile and highly                corrosive, and degrades over time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The pH of the hydroxide                  solution decreases as carbon dioxide from the                  atmosphere is absorbed. Preferred Process: The                  hydroxide solution is regenerated in the                  electrochemical module from the re-equilibrated                  bicarbonate and carbonate solution fed into the                  electrochemical module. The method involves                  adjusting surface to volume ratio of the                  hydroxide solution to maximize the absorption                  rate of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere into                  the hydroxide solution. The pH and hydroxide                  concentration increase in the hydroxide solution.                  The rate of carbon dioxide absorption from the                  atmosphere into the hydroxide solution increases.                  The alkaline solution containing bicarbonate and                  carbonate ions are fed into center compartment of                  an electrolytic cation exchange module (E-CEM),                  where E-CEM comprises an anode, an anode                  compartment adjacent to the anode. The first                  cation membrane is set between the anode                  compartment and the center compartment. The                  cathode compartment and second cation membrane                  between center compartment. The cathode is                  adjacent to cathode compartment. The water is fed                  into anode compartment and cathode compartment.                  The source of electricity is applied to anode.                  The oxygen is formed in the anode compartment,                  carbon dioxide is formed in the center                  compartment, and hydrogen and hydroxide are                  formed in the cathode compartment. The carbon                  dioxide formed in the center compartment and the                  hydrogen formed in the cathode compartment are                  removed. The effluent is collected from the                  cathode compartment. The effluent from the                  cathode compartment is allowed to chemically                  absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere to form                  re-equilibrated bicarbonate and carbonate                  solution. Preferred Components: The hydroxide                  solution comprises an alkali metal hydroxide,                  preferably sodium hydroxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN114354809-A; CN114354809-B
TI Experiment system for displacing methane by carbon            dioxide pulse displacement, has data collecting and            processing system which is provided for real-time            records and analyzing experiment data of reservoir            environment simulation system.
AU LIU J
   JIA Y
   CHENG L
   LI D
   TANG J
   PAN Z
   ZHOU J
   LU Y
   LU C
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
   CHONGQING INST GEOLOGY & MINERAL RESOURC (CNMO-Non-standard)
GA 202258140K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has gas supply system which is                provided with gas source for reservoir environment                simulation system, where gas sources includes                carbon dioxide, methane and inert gases. The pulse                control system is provided with pulses for the gas                source, and the gas passing through the pulse                control system is converted into pulse gas. The                tail gas recovery system is provided to recover and                process the tail gas of the reservoir environment                simulation system. The carbon dioxide gas output by                the gas supply system is converted into pulse gas                through the pulse control system, and the pulsed                gas is set to enter the reservoir environment                simulation system to shorten the time for replacing                methane by carbon dioxide gas. The experimental                data of the reservoir environment simulation system                is recorded, analyzed and processed in real time,                and the dynamic displacement process is reflected                in real time. The relationship between the                displacement evaluation index and the displacement                time is determined.
   USE - Experiment system for displacing methane by                carbon dioxide pulse displacement.
   ADVANTAGE - The system allows the carbon dioxide gas                output by the gas supply system to be converted                into unstable pulse gas through the pulse control                system, so that the pulse gas enters the reservoir                environment simulation system to shorten the time                for replacing methane by carbon dioxide                displacement, thus improving the efficiency of                carbon dioxide displacing replacing methane and                recovery rate of methane. The system enables                real-time recording and analyzing the experiment                data of the simulation system through the data                collecting processing system, thus accurately                obtaining the relationship of the displacement                evaluation index and the displacement time, and                hence improving the recovery efficiency and methane                recovery rate, and providing an advantageous                reference value for the actual exploitation of                non-conventional natural gas, in the technical                field of natural gas exploitation, for different                reservoir environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a test                evaluation method of carbon dioxide pulse                displacement replacing methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view                of the experiment system for displacing methane by                carbon dioxide pulse displacement.

PN WO2020059197-A1; JP2020044504-A; JP7112081-B2
TI Carbon dioxide separation recovery apparatus            comprises to-be-processed gas passed to cooler,            adsorption zone of desiccant rotor, regeneration            circulation unit, and carbon dioxide recovery            unit.
AU KOJI I
   KAZUYUKI Y
   INOUE H
   YOSHIDA K
AE SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
   NISHIBU GIKEN KK (NISH-Non-standard)
GA 2020237144
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide separation recovery apparatus                comprises to-be-processed gas passes to cooler (C1)                and sends to adsorption zone of the desiccant rotor                divided into adsorption zone and regeneration zone.                The gas which passes through the adsorption zone is                sent to a cooler (C2). One portion is returned to                adsorption zone of the rotor, and remaining one                portion to the adsorption zone of the rotor divided                into adsorption zone, purge zone, regeneration                zone, and cooling zone. One portion of gas which                passes through the cooling zone is mixed with the                gas which passes through the adsorption zone of the                rotor and sends to a cooler (C3). The gas which                passes the cooler (C3) is sent to the cooling zone.                The gas which passes through the regeneration zone                of the rotor is branched on two portions, in which                one portion is sent to the purge zone, and                remaining one portion is returned in the                regeneration zone of the rotor, regeneration                circulation is carried out, and carbon dioxide is                recovered.
   USE - Carbon dioxide separation recovery                apparatus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide separation recovery apparatus                comprises to-be-processed gas pass to a cooler (C1)                and sends to the adsorption zone of the desiccant                rotor divided into adsorption zone and regeneration                zone. The gas which passes through the adsorption                zone is sent to a cooler (C2) and it branches on                two portions. One portion is returned to the                adsorption zone of the desiccant rotor, and                remaining one portion is sent to the adsorption                zone of the carbon dioxide separation recovery                rotor divided into four zones such as adsorption                zone, purge zone, regeneration zone, and cooling                zone. One portion of gas which passes through the                cooling zone is mixed with the gas which passes                through the adsorption zone of the carbon dioxide                separation collection rotor and sends to a cooler                (C3). The gas which passes the cooler (C3) is sent                to the cooling zone. The gas which passes through                the cooling zone is branched on two portions, in                which one portion is mixed with the gas which                passes through the adsorption zone of the carbon                dioxide separation recovery rotor and remaining one                portion is sent to the regeneration zone of the                desiccant rotor. The gas which passes through the                regeneration zone of the desiccant rotor is                exhausted out of an apparatus. The gas which passes                through the regeneration zone of the rotor is                branched on two portions, in which one portion is                sent to the purge zone, and the gas which passes                through the purge zone is exhausted out of an                apparatus. The remaining one portion is returned in                the regeneration zone of the rotor, regeneration                circulation is carried out, and carbon dioxides are                recovered.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the carbon                dioxide separation and recovery apparatus.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving data from                appliances equipped with a thermal sensor (120) and                a carbon dioxide sensor (130) within a building and                configured to detect locations of elevated                temperature and / or locations of elevated levels                of ambient carbon dioxide within the building. Communication containing data                related to physical characteristics of a fire in a                building is received. Algorithms and machine                learning are applied to information to enable                construction of virtual, dynamic, real-time                computer models of a current state and real-time                probabilistic computer models of likely physical                development. Output from the real-time and                probabilistic fire models is communicated and                applied to a pre-determined policy defining actions                to be taken in response to different fire hazard                scenarios.

TI Gas adsorption material containing active metal            sites as adsorbent in hydrogen storage and carbon            dioxide capture, is prepared by weighing metal salt and            (1, 1'-biphenyl)-3 ,4 ', 5-tricarboxylic acid, and            dissolving to obtain mixed solution.
AU WANG J
   LI Y
   MA W
   YIN Z
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 2022699825
AB    NOVELTY - Gas adsorption material containing active                metal sites, has chemical formula.
   USE - Gas adsorption material containing active                metal sites used as adsorbent in hydrogen storage                and carbon dioxide capture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The two end groups of the linear[M3] cluster                core M2+ both adopt an octahedral coordination                configuration, and 1DMF and 1H2O molecule are used                as solvents to participate in the coordination,                through heat treatment, the coordinating solvent                molecules are removed to obtain stable metal active                sites, which enhances adsorption capacity of                hydrogenand carbon dioxide gas molecules.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gas adsorption material containing active                metal sites, has chemical formula of                [M3(bpt)2(DMF)2(H2O)2].1.5 DMF, where M=Ni2+, Co2+,                Fe2+, or                Mn2+;bpt=[1,1'-biphenyl]-3,4',5-tricarboxylate                anion; and DMF=N,N-dimethylformamide.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing gas adsorption material, which                involves:
   weighing metal salt and [1, 1 '-biphenyl]-3 ,4                ',5-tricarboxylic acid according to the molar ratio                of (0 .5-1 .5): 1 separately;
   dissolving [1 ,1 '-biphenyl]-3 ,4                ',5-tricarboxylic acid in 8-15 mL dimethylformamide                (N,N-dimethylformamide) solution with stirring to                obtain a solution A, and dissolving the metal salt                in 1-3 mL H2O solution with stirring to obtain                solution B, subsequently, mixing and stirring                solution A and solution B to obtain a mixed                solution;
   The above mixed solution is reacted at a                constant temperature of 120-160&#8451; for                48-96 hours, then cooled to room temperature, and                the precipitate in the reaction solution is                filtered and washed with DMF to obtain transparent                bulk crystals.
   The transparent bulk crystal is kept at                150-300&#8451; for 10-60 minutes, removed the                DMF and HO molecules coordinated on the metal ions                to obtain gas adsorption materials for active metal                sites.2
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas adsorption                  material is a crystalline microporous metal                  organic framework compound, crystallized in                  Trigonal crystal system, where space group is                  R-3, cell parameter is                  &#945;=&#946;=90&#176;,                  &#947;=120&#176; and Z=9; material                  density rho is 1.111 g cm-3.The crystallographic minimum asymmetric unit                  of the gas adsorption material comprises two M2+,                  a bpt ligand anion, one DMF and one H2 O;                  M2+forms coordinate bonds with bpt, DMF and H2O,                  two M2+are named M1and M2, M1and M2adopt                  different octahedral coordination modes: The six                  coordinating oxygen atoms around M1come from six                  different bpt ligands, respectively. The six                  coordinating oxygen atoms around M2, four oxygen                  atoms come from three different bpt ligands. The                  two oxygens come from the coordinated DMF and H2O                  molecules, where M1and M2are connected by three                  carboxylate groups to form a linear [M3 (COO)6]                  cluster core. The gas adsorption material is a                  three-dimensional porous network structure is                  formed by linear [M3(COO)6] cluster core units                  extended by bpt as a triangular bridging ligand.                  The topology type of the three-dimensional porous                  network structure is 3-6 connected topology                  network. The three-dimensional porous network                  structure contains a through-hole system with a                  porosity of 40.2%. The 3D network crystallography                  has 1D nanochannels with circular windows in the                  c-direction. Preferred Conditions: The gas                  adsorption material has a thermal stability                  temperature of up to 350 &#8451;. The gas                  adsorption material Langmuir has a specific                  surface area of 1600 m2/g, which withstand                  multiple rounds of adsorption tests and maintain                  a stable specific surface area. The adsorption                  capacity of the gas adsorption material to H2 at                  77 K and 273 K temperature exceeds 210 cm3/g and                  14 cm3/g respectively. The gas adsorption                  material has an adsorption capacity of 98 cm3/g                  for CO2 at a temperature of 273 K, shows                  selective adsorption to CH4and N2.The separation                  ratio of CO2 :CH4 is greater than 3, and the                  separation ratio of CO2 :N4 is greater than                  10.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method involves                  adjusting temperature of the semi-crystalline                  polymer solution to -5 degrees C to -45 degrees                  C. The method involves controlling crystallinity                  and orientation of the semi-crystalline polymer                  by controlling temperature of the                  semi-crystalline polymer solution within the                  above range. The method involves adjusting                  concentration of the semi-crystalline polymer                  solution to 0.5-5 wt.%. The method involves                  coating a gutter layer on a porous polymer                  support to prepare a porous support having an                  intermediate layer, and forming a                  semi-crystalline polymer selective layer by                  coating the semi-crystalline polymer solution on                  the intermediate layer, where the selective layer                  is formed to a thickness of 20-1000 nm. Preferred                  Component: The ultra-thin polymer separation                  membrane is chosen from electronic devices,                  electrodes, packaging, and sensors. The                  semi-crystalline polymer is any one chosen from                  polyamide-based, polystyrene-based,                  polyolefin-based, polyester-based and                  polyurethane-based polymers. TI Preparing composite electrocatalyst i.e. iron on            nitrogen-doped carbon, comprises e.g. carrying out            hydrothermal reaction of            iron-tris(chloroisopropyl)phosphate,            zinc-tris(chloroisopropyl)phosphate and zirconium            chloride, pyrolysing in nitrogen atmosphere, washing            with deionized water and ethanol.
AU CAO S
   PENG M
   DONG B
AE UNIV YANGZHOU (UYYZ-C)
GA 2021C4966V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite electrocatalyst i.e. iron                on nitrogen-doped carbon (I), comprises (i)                ultrasonically stirring                iron-tris(chloroisopropyl)phosphate,                zinc-tris(chloroisopropyl)phosphate, zirconium(IV)                chloride, benzoic acid, acetic acid and                dimethylformamide to perform hydrothermal reaction                until the end, cooling to room temperature, washing                with dimethylformamide and acetone, and then                centrifuging and drying to obtain precursor i.e.                zinc-iron porous coordination network-224 (II), and                (ii) placing the precursor (II) in a nitrogen                atmosphere and conducting pyrolysis at 1000degrees                Celsius for 1 hour, then washing with hydrogen                fluoride to remove zirconium dioxide, then washing                with deionized water and ethanol, centrifuging and                drying to obtain final product.
   USE - (I) is useful as working electrode for                reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has benefits of having micropores, shows                high-efficiency conversion of carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide performance and long-term                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite electrocatalyst i.e. iron                on nitrogen-doped carbon of formula (Fey-N-C) (I),                comprises (i) ultrasonically stirring                iron-tris(chloroisopropyl)phosphate (Fe-TCPP),                zinc-tris(chloroisopropyl)phosphate (Zn-TCPP),                zirconium(IV) chloride, benzoic acid, acetic acid                and dimethylformamide to perform hydrothermal                reaction until the end, cooling to room                temperature, washing with dimethylformamide and                acetone, and then centrifuging and drying to obtain                precursor i.e. zinc-iron porous coordination                network-224 of formula ((PCN-224)(ZnxFey)) (II),                where value of x is 0.2-0.8 and y is 0.2-0.8, and                (ii) placing the precursor (II) in a nitrogen                atmosphere and conducting pyrolysis at 1000degrees                Celsius for 1 hour, then washing with hydrogen                fluoride to remove zirconium dioxide, then washing                with deionized water and ethanol, centrifuging and                drying to obtain final product.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of (I), where (I) is coated and modified on the                surface of the carbon paper electrode, which is                used as a working electrode for reducing carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In use method, (I) is                  mixed with Nafion, isopropanol, and water to form                  a homogenous mixture, which is then coated on the                  surface of the carbon paper electrode and then                  dried.

PN CN112494629-A
TI Preparing cough-relieving tablet comprises crushing poppy shell into powder, extracting, crushing, mixing, collecting, concentrating, adding auxiliary material, drying, crushing, mixing, drying, spraying peppermint oil and coating.
AU XU D
   YU G
   GENG L
   ZHU Y
AE HAINAN HELEE PHARM CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 202128560S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cough-relieving tablet comprises (i) taking Aster, chrysoidine, balloon flower, radix peucedani, fructus aurantii, radix stemonae, shizandra berry, poppy shell, liquorice, peppermint oil, ammonium chloride, dried orange peel, herba schizonepetae and rhizoma zingiberis; (ii) preparing poppy shell extract; (iii) crushing Aster, liquorice, chrysoidine, balloon flower, radix peucedani, fructusaurantii, radix stemonae, shizandra berry, driedorange peel, herba schizonepetae and dry ginger eleven pure material into powder and mixing uniformly all into supercritical fluid extraction reaction kettle, and carbon dioxide as extracting agent; ethanol is cosolvent for extracting, collecting the extracting liquid, recycling ethanol, and concentrating; and (iv) adding proper auxiliary material, drying, crushing, adding ammonium chloride, and poppy shell extract, mixing uniformly, preparing into particles, drying, spraying peppermint oil, pressing into tablet and coating.
   USE - The method is useful preparing cough-relieving tablet.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cough-relieving tablet comprises (i) taking Aster, chrysoidine, balloon flower, radix peucedani, fructus aurantii, radix stemonae, shizandra berry, poppy shell, liquorice, peppermint oil, ammonium chloride, dried orange peel, herba schizonepetae and rhizoma zingiberis; (ii) crushing the poppy shell into powder, placing the crushed medicinal material powder into the supercritical fluid extraction reaction kettle using carbon dioxide as extracting agent, extracting using methanol and water as cosolvent, collecting the extracting liquid, recycling methanol, concentrating the extracting liquid into extract to obtain poppy shell extract; (iii) crushing Aster, liquorice, chrysoidine, balloon flower, radix peucedani, fructusaurantii, radix stemonae, shizandra berry, driedorange peel, herba schizonepetae and dry ginger eleven pure material into powder and mixing uniformly all into supercritical fluid extraction reaction kettle, and carbon dioxide as extracting agent; ethanol is cosolvent for extracting, collecting the extracting liquid, recycling ethanol, concentrating the extracting liquid into thick paste; and (iv) adding proper auxiliary material, drying, crushing, adding ammonium chloride, and poppy shell extract, mixing uniformly, preparing into particles, drying, spraying peppermint oil, pressing into tablet and coating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In step (ii) and step (iii), the extracting temperature is 40-60 degrees C, the extracting pressure is 20-30 Mpa, the extracting time is 1-2 hours, and the carbon dioxide flow speed is 20-30 kg/hours. In step (ii), the process of recycling methanol is pumping the extract into a vacuum concentrating tank and recovering the methanol under the condition of the vacuum degree of 0.07-0.1 Mpa. In the step (iii), the process of recycling the ethanol is to pump the extract into the vacuum concentrating tank, and the ethanol is recycled under the condition of the vacuum degree of 0.07-0.1 Mpa. In the step (ii), the poppy shell powder is sieved by 40-60 meshes. In the step (iv), the auxiliary material comprises diluent, disintegrating agent, adhesive and/or lubricant. The diluent comprises anhydrous lactose, microcrystalline cellulose and/or maltodextrin. The disintegrating agent comprises low-substituted hydroxypropyl cellulose, sodium carboxymethyl starch and/or cross-linked sodium carboxymethyl cellulose. The adhesive comprises hydroxypropyl cellulose, polyvinylpyrrolidone and/or cross-linked polyvinyl pyrrolidone. The lubricant comprise magnesium stearate, talc powder, sodium stearyl fumarate and/or micro-powder silica gel.

PN CN112276429-A; CN112276429-B
TI Welding work station comprises bearing vehicle, bearing table slidingly set on bearing vehicle, and braking mechanism set in bearing vehicle and used for braking and welding working box.
AU YAO Y
AE XIAN BEILUN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
   BAOSHAN BAOJIN LASER WELDING CO LTD (BAOS-Non-standard)
GA 202114573Y
AB    NOVELTY - Welding work station comprises bearing vehicle (1), bearing table (2) slidingly set on bearing vehicle, and braking mechanism (3) set in bearing vehicle and used for braking and welding working box (4) set on bearing table. The bearing vehicle comprises vehicle frame with an top surface, arc sliding rail detachably mounted on the top of the vehicle frame and a wheel shaft rolling set in vehicle frame. The wheel shaft is fixedly connected vehicle wheel through vehicle frame. The welding working box comprises carbon dioxide protection gas tank embedded in welding working box, electric welding machine connected with carbon dioxide protection gas tank (41) and electric welding gun. The bearing table is slidingly matched with arc slide rail through guide wheel set on bottom surface. The bearing table comprises supporting plate, and working box clamping mechanism on supporting plate. Non-polar limiting device is set on bottom surface of supporting plate and protective rod device.
   USE - The welding work station is used for welding work box.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is hung on the traction vehicle by the towing hook set on the push rod to move, through the mutual cooperation of the arc slide rail and the bearing table, even if the whole device stops under the uneven road, it can ensure the stability of the working box. The device fixes the working box through the working box clamping mechanism on the bearing table, so as to prevent the working box from falling. The stepless limiting device is used for locating the bearing table to meet the stability of the working box. The device can further prevent the working box from falling through the protective rod device. The brake mechanism of the device realizes the function of active deceleration brake, and prevents the impact caused by inertia and traction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of automatic welding device.
   Bearing vehicle (1)
   Bearing table (2)
   Braking mechanism (3)
   Working box (4)
   Carbon dioxide protection gas tank (41)

   LIAO P
   LI Y
   SHEN J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201965182G
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a target value setting unit (1), neural network inverse controller (2), proportional-integral-derivative (PID) control compensator (3), large-scale combustion coal power plant CO2 capture overall system model (4), first delay unit (5) and second delay unit (6). The target value setting unit output and large coal-fired power station CO2 capture overall system model deviation of output as PID control compensator input, solves the compensation input variable. The input variable and the output variable of the CO2 capture overall system model of a large coal-fired power station passes the first delay unit and the second delay unit respectively to obtain the delay variable. The first delay unit the second delay unit outputs the variables and the target value setting unit outputs as the neural network inverse controller input and calculates the output.
   USE - Large-scale coal-fired power plant carbon dioxide (CO2) capture system based on neural network inverse control.
   ADVANTAGE - The required control variables can be calculated according to the given value, the advance control can be realized, the large delay characteristic of the overall system can be effectively processed, and the dynamic adjustment quality of the output side can be improved. PT P
PN US2019153553-A1; US10711318-B2
TI New genetically engineered Geobacter sulfurreducens strain comprising genetically engineered modification resulting from insertion of genetic element encoding ATP-citrate lyase, useful for generating carbonaceous chemical.
AU LOVLEY D R
   UEKI T
   LOVLEY K N
AE UNIV MASSACHUSETTS (UMAC-C)
   UNIV MASSACHUSETTS (UMAC-C)
GA 201944812S
AB    NOVELTY - Genetically engineered Geobacter sulfurreducens strain comprising a genetically engineered modification resulting from the insertion of a genetic element encoding ATP-citrate lyase, is new.
   USE - The strain is useful for generating a carbonaceous chemical (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The strain: exhibits improved function as a cathode biofilm compared to the wild-type Geobacter sulfurreducens under the same conditions (claimed); and generates high value products with microbial electrosynthesis and electrofermentation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for generating a carbonaceous chemical, where carbon dioxide is a source of carbon, comprising (i) providing a reaction vessel having an anode electrode and a cathode electrode disposed in it, where the anode electrode has at least one surface and an anode electrical contact terminal, the cathode electrode has at least one surface and a cathode electrical contact terminal, the cathode electrode having a film of biologically active material comprising the genetically engineered Geobacter sulfurreducens strain adjacent the surface of the cathode electrode and in electrical communication with it, and the reaction vessel configured to contain a working fluid having mobile ions in it, (ii) providing a reaction medium in contact with the cathode electrode and the anode electrode, where the reaction medium comprises a substance configured to be oxidized, (iii) providing a source of electrical energy electrically connected to the cathode electrical contact terminal and to the anode electrical contact terminal, (iv) providing a source of carbon dioxide configured to provide carbon dioxide to the film of biologically active material adjacent the surface of the cathode electrode by way of the reaction medium, (v) operating the source of electrical power to provide electrons to the cathode and to extract electrons from the anode, and (vi) generating a carbonaceous chemical containing at least two carbon atoms in a vicinity of the cathode having the biofilm in electrical communication with it, and generating molecular oxygen at the anode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Components: The strain is capable of effectively growing on a cathode under anaerobic conditions with electrons derived solely from the cathode as electron donor source. The genetic element comprises the genes ATP citrate lyase, subunit 1 (ac1A) and ATP citrate lyase, subunit 2 (ac1B). The genetic element is inserted adjacent the gene GSU1106 of the parent Geobacter sulfurreducens. The genetically engineered modification further comprises the insertion of a gene that encodes bifunctional aldehyde/alcohol dehydrogenase. The gene encoding bifunctional aldehyde/alcohol dehydrogenase is adhE1. The genetically engineered Geobacter sulfurreducens strain is configured for producing a carbonaceous chemical containing at least two carbon atoms in a reverse tricarboxylic acid pathway. The strain is identified as strain ATP-dependent citrate lyase. The biofilm mediates the production of the chemical containing at least two carbon atoms using a reverse tricarboxylic acid pathway.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The source of carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide in an effluent from a combustion process of coal, petroleum, methane, natural gas, biomass, organic carbon, an industrial process that releases carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide from geothermal sources, atmospheric carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide from dry ice, carbon dioxide from carbonate minerals, carbon dioxide from carbonic acid, and carbon dioxide sequestered from the atmosphere.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reaction vessel has a first chamber and a second chamber, where the first chamber and the second chamber each are configured to contain a working fluid having mobile ions in it, and the first chamber and the second chamber are separated by a membrane permeable to at least a selected ionic species. The anode electrode is disposed in one of the first chamber and the second chamber and the cathode electrode disposed in the other of the first chamber and the second chamber.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2019092013-A1
TI Extracting fatty acids from triglyceride oils, comprises contacting triglyceride oil with aqueous solution, separating triglyceridol and aqueous phase, adding carbon dioxide and contacting aqueous phase with further triglyceride oil.
AU HEYMANN J
   WILLY B
   BAHLMANN M
   BRAMSIEPE C
   ZOELLNER J
AE EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 201943569U
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting fatty acids from triglyceride oils, comprises: (a) contacting a triglyceride oil comprising fatty acids with an aqueous solution comprising at least one salt including alkaline earth metal bicarbonate, alkali metal bicarbonate, forming a triglyceridol phase and an aqueous phase; (b) separating the triglyceridol phase from the aqueous phase; (c) adding carbon dioxide and optionally an organic solvent to the aqueous phase; (d) separating the aqueous phase from the fatty acid-containing organic phase obtained in the step (c); and (e) contacting at least part of the aqueous phase with further triglyceride oil comprising fatty acids to obtain a triglyceridol phase and an aqueous phase.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting fatty acids from triglyceride oils.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows recycling of the phases and ensures high quality of the extracted triglyceride oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting fatty acids from triglyceride oils, comprises: (a) contacting a triglyceride oil comprising fatty acids with an aqueous solution comprising at least one salt including alkaline earth metal bicarbonate, alkali metal bicarbonate, forming a triglyceridol phase and an aqueous phase, where triglyceride oil has a reduced content of fatty acids compared to triglyceride oil and aqueous phase has a content of fatty acids increased compared to aqueous solution; (b) separating the triglyceridol phase from the aqueous phase; (c) adding carbon dioxide and optionally an organic solvent to the aqueous phase, where an aqueous phase having a lower fatty acid content than and a fatty acid-containing organic phase are obtained; (d) separating the aqueous phase from the fatty acid-containing organic phase obtained in the step (c); (e) contacting at least part of the aqueous phase with further triglyceride oil comprising fatty acids to obtain a triglyceridol phase and an aqueous phase, where triglyceridol phase has a reduced fatty acid content compared to further triglyceride oil and aqueous phase has a fatty acid increased content relative to aqueous phase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt includes potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate and calcium hydrogen carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried out at a temperature less than 100 degrees C. The ratio of the volume of the triglyceride oil to the volume of the aqueous phase is 1:1-1:200. In step (c) an organic solvent is added to the aqueous phase. The ratio of the volume of aqueous phase to the volume of the organic solvent is 1:100-100:1. The temperature in step (c) is 0-120 degrees C. In step (b) the triglyceride oil phase is separated from the aqueous phase in a centrifuge. The concentration of all salts in the aqueous phase is in the range more than 0.4 mole/l.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The breathing device has an flexible                rebreathing air chamber (6) arranged in fluid                communication with the second end (4) of the                mouthpiece (2) thus, being in fluid connection with                the breathing channel through the second end of the                mouthpiece. The rebreathing air chamber is formed                by flexible wall section. A wall module (7) is                arranged at the second end of the mouthpiece and/or                attached to the rebreathing air chamber. The wall                module comprises through-going openings (8)                provided in the wall module and/or in the breathing                channel, allowing fluid communication between the                rebreathing air chamber and the surrounding                atmosphere.
   USE - Breathing device for increasing and/or                balancing and/or maintaining specific level of                Carbon dioxide in inhaled air.
   ADVANTAGE - The device serves to signalize to the user a                new setting for the breathing device for                optimization of the Rebreathing Ratio (RBR). The                side-inlet is arranged so as to extract a portion                of the air flowing through the breathing channel,                for forwarding the extracted air to a CO2                determining device, so as to determine the CO2                level of the user's exhaled breath. The slider                setting found during the configuration process can                be retrieved by the app and communicated to the                patient so that he/she knows where to set the                slider to be in the correct treatment window.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a nose clamp;
   (2) a kit of portions for increasing and/or                balancing and/or maintaining a specific level of                carbon dioxide in the inhaled air; and
   (3) a method for adjusting the RBR.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                breathing device composed of a rebreathing air                chamber and a mouthpiece.

   Second end (4)
   Flexible rebreathing air chamber (6)

   Through-going openings (8)

PN KR2018134578-A; KR1970120-B1
TI Combined power generation device for generating power using waste heat generated in engine, has engine cooling heat exchanger that is provided for exchanging heat between cooling line of engine and first medium line.
AU JOO K B
   HEE L C
   KI H J
   NYEON R G
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
   HYUNDAI HEAVY IND CO LTD (HHIH-C)
GA 2018A38309
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a gas compressor (211) which is provided for compressing carbon dioxide. A first medium line (221) is connected to the gas compressor. A gas turbine (213) is connected to the first medium line and driven by carbon dioxide supplied from the first medium line. A second medium line (222) is connected to gas turbine and gas compressor. A scavenging heat exchanger (231) is provided for exchanging heat between second flow line and first medium line. A re-heater (232) is coupled to the first media line and the second media line to exchange heat between the first media line and the second media line. A heater (233) is connected to third flow line and first media line to heat exchange the third flow line and the first media line. A gas cooler is installed between the re-heater and the gas compressor in the second flow line. An engine cooling heat exchanger (235) is provided for exchanging heat between the cooling line of the engine and the first medium line.
   USE - Combined power generation device for generating power using waste heat generated in engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure of the device can be simplified, cost of the device can be reduced, and device operation can be facilitated, by integrating the engine cooling heat exchanger and the waste heat recovery power generation system. Since the heated carbon dioxide drives the gas turbine, the power generation efficiency of the gas turbine can be improved and the size of the power generation device can be reduced. Since the power turbine is driven by the exhaust of the engine, the amount of generated electric power can be increased by utilizing the waste energy of the exhaust.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the combined power generation device.
   Gas compressor (211)
   Gas turbine (213)
   First medium line (221)
   Second medium line (222)
   Scavenging heat exchanger (231)
   Re-heater (232)
   Heater (233)
   Engine cooling heat exchanger (235)

PN CN108017079-A
TI Producing cubic calcium carbonate involves using lime foam calcined in lime kiln as raw material, grinding and sieving to obtain quick lime, adding quicklime and water to drum digester for digestive reaction to obtain crude slaked lime.
AU CHI J
   ZHOU J
   LI Z
   CHEN X
   WEI C
   NIE C
   YANG D
   GAO K
AE XINJIANG CORPS MODERN GREEN CHLOR-ALKALI (XJTY-C)
   XINJIANG TIANYE GROUP CO LTD (XJTY-C)
GA 201838534D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing cubic calcium carbonate involves using lime foam calcined in lime kiln as raw material, grinding and sieving to particle size of less than 30 milimeter to obtain quick lime. The quicklime and water are added to the drum digester for digestive reaction to obtain crude slaked lime. The crude slaked lime is subjected to three to five stages of slag removal, aged for 4-6 hours to obtain refined lime milk. The refined lime milk is fed into the carbonization tower, and concentration is controlled at 10-15 degrees Baume, and temperature is controlled to 55-80 degrees C.
   USE - Method for producing cubic calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce cubic calcium carbonate which produce uniform grain diameter of the product having good dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing cubic calcium carbonate by lime foam involves using lime foam calcined in lime kiln as raw material, grinding and sieving to particle size of less than 30 milimeter to obtain quick lime. The quicklime and water are added to the drum digester for digestive reaction to obtain crude slaked lime. The crude slaked lime is subjected to three to five stages of slag removal, aged for 4-6 hours to obtain refined lime milk. The refined lime milk is fed into the carbonization tower, and concentration is controlled at 10-15 degrees Baume, and temperature is controlled to 55-80 degrees C, and one of amino acid, protein and sucrose crystals is added. 0.1-0.5 wt.% Control agent is added, 25-100 wt.% carbon dioxide is added to pass carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction for 3-6 hours. The emulsion in the carbonization tower is neutral, continue to pass Carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the carbonization reaction for 2-4 hours to obtain cubic calcium carbonate emulsion. The calcium carbonate emulsion is dehydrated, dried, crushed, and packaged to obtain cubic calcium carbonate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the ratio of quick lime to water in the above digestion reaction is 1:4-8. The water content of the cake after press dewatering by the filter press is 30-35%. The drying process and secondary drying is used, and the material moisture of the material outlet of the first stage paddle dryer is less than or equal to 25% and secondary drum dryer outlet moisture is less than or equal to 0.5%. The pH of the cubic calcium carbonate product is set to 8.0 and 10.0, and whiteness is greater than or equal to 92.

AE WUXI HAOYU ENERGY CONSERVATION & ENVIRON (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 201832713C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing environmentally-friendly water-proof warp knitting fabric comprises e.g. mixing 13-15 pts. wt. ash, 2-4 pts. wt. nano toner, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide and 100-120 pts. wt. water and boiling, adding 20-35 pts. wt. ramie fiber and 10-15 pts. wt. nano carbon dioxide catalyst fiber into boiled mixture and cooking for 2-4 hours, washing cooked fiber with water, air drying naturally and blending into yarn, adding 4-6 pts. wt. chitosan, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium stearoyl lactylate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. n-butanol and 2.2-3.5 pts. wt. dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing environmentally-friendly water-proof warp knitting fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The fabric has simple preparation process, high flexibility and excellent water-proof performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing environmentally-friendly water-proof warp knitting fabric comprises (i) mixing 13-15 pts. wt. ash, 2-4 pts. wt. nano toner, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide and 100-120 pts. wt. water and boiling, (ii) adding 20-35 pts. wt. ramie fiber and 10-15 pts. wt. nano carbon dioxide catalyst fiber into boiled mixture and cooking for 2-4 hours, (iii) washing cooked fiber with water, air drying naturally and blending into yarn, (iv) adding 4-6 pts. wt. chitosan, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium stearoyl lactylate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. n-butanol, 2.2-3.5 pts. wt. dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate, 1-1.5 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and 20-35 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol into the reactor and mixing at 80-85 degrees C, at 200-400 revolutions per minute for 2-4 hours, and (v) immersing yarn of step (iii), blending into the mixture prepared in step (iv), magnetic stirring for 30-60 minutes, and (vi) naturally air-drying yarn, drying in the dryer and then weaving into cloth to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of nano-carbon powder is 20-40 nm. Preferred Method: In step (i), mixing 14 pts. wt. ash, 3 pts. wt. nano toner, 0.3 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide and 110 pts. wt. water and boiling. In step (ii), adding 30 pts. wt. ramie fiber and 12 pts. wt. nano carbon dioxide catalyst fiber into boiled mixture and cooking for 3 hours. In step (v), adding 5 pts. wt. chitosan, 1.5 pts. wt. sodium stearoyl lactylate, 1 pts. wt. n-butanol, 3 pts. wt. dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate, 1 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and 28 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol into the reactor, mixing at 82 degrees C and then reacting at 300 revolutions per minute for 3 hours. In step (v), magnetic stirring for 45 minutes.
