TI Producing ammonia, hydrogen and urea using biomass            comprises e.g. carrying out pyrolysis of biomass in a            biomass pyrolysis reactor to obtain pyrolysis gas,            pyrolysis oil and biological carbon and passing            pyrolysis gas and pyrolysis oil.
AU XIANG W
   ZHAO Z
   SUN C
   CHEN S
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
   UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201808833F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ammonia, hydrogen and urea using                biomass comprises e.g. carrying out pyrolysis of                biomass in a biomass pyrolysis reactor to obtain                pyrolysis gas, pyrolysis oil and biological carbon,                passing pyrolysis gas and pyrolysis oil through oil                and gas separator and rectifier to obtain pyrolysis                gas and liquid fuel, adding biochar into reduction                reactor, reacting with aluminum oxide, ferrous                oxide and nitrogen gas in regeneration reactor,                where product is aluminum nitride, iron or carbon                dioxide, adding aluminum nitride and iron into                steam reactor and then reacting with steam.
   USE - The method is useful for producing ammonia,                hydrogen and urea using biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing ammonia, hydrogen and urea using                biomass comprises (i) carrying out pyrolysis of                biomass in a biomass pyrolysis reactor to obtain                pyrolysis gas, pyrolysis oil and biological carbon,                passing pyrolysis gas and pyrolysis oil through oil                and gas separator and rectifier to obtain pyrolysis                gas and liquid fuel, (ii) adding biochar obtained                in step (i) into reduction reactor, reacting with                aluminum oxide, ferrous oxide and nitrogen gas in                regeneration reactor, where product is aluminum                nitride, iron or carbon dioxide, adding aluminum                nitride and iron into steam reactor, reacting with                steam to obtain ammonia, hydrogen, ferrous oxide                and aluminum oxide, heat required for the reaction                is provided by the burning of solar or fuel, adding                ferrous oxide and aluminum oxide into the                regeneration reactor, reacting with air to obtain                aluminum oxide, ferrous oxide and nitrogen gas, and                returning to the reduction reactor to obtain                hydrogen, and (iii) separating ammonia and hydrogen                obtained in step (ii), washing device with water,                separating generated ammonia using gas-liquid                separator, adding separating generated ammonia into                ammonia by a heating device and then passing                ammonia and carbon dioxide gas into reduction                reactor to obtain generate urea within the                synthesis unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: In step (i), reaction                  temperature in biomass pyrolysis reactor is                  400-800 degrees C. In step (ii), reaction                  temperature in reduction reactor is 1000-1800                  degrees C. The reaction temperature in                  regeneration reactor is 600-1200 degrees C. In                  step (iii), operating temperature of urea                  synthesis device is 120-250 degrees C. In step                  (i), (ii) and (iii), heat required for the                  reaction is provided by solar energy. In                  reduction reactor, carrier circulating between                  the steam reactor and the regeneration reactor is                  aluminum oxide and ferrous oxide, and exist in                  different forms, where aluminum oxide is aluminum                  oxide-aluminum nitride and ferrous oxide is                  ferric oxide-ferrous oxide-iron.

PT P
PN WO2021170391-A1; AU2021226686-A1; CA3169983-A1; IN202247053767-A; CN115175898-A
TI Synthesizing urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide            by performing urea synthesis with a stripping process            in a synthesis section including reactor, stripper and            condenser, and converting ammonia and carbon dioxide            into urea.
AU MARRONE L
   BERTINI P
   FUMAGALLI M
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2021A1892H
AB    NOVELTY - A urea is synthesized from ammonia and carbon                dioxide by performing urea synthesis with a                stripping process in a synthesis section including                reactor (1), a stripper (2) and a condenser (3);                converting ammonia and carbon dioxide into urea                that occurs in the reactor at a synthesis pressure                with formation of an aqueous effluent (11)                containing urea and unconverted ammonium carbamate;                performing stripping step of reactor effluent in                the stripper, where the reactor effluent is heated                and contacted with gaseous carbon dioxide (12),                with formation of a urea-containing solution (14)                and of stripper vapors (15); sending                urea-containing solution effluent from the stripper                to a low-pressure recovery section (4) for further                processing; splitting stripper vapors into a first                portion (151) which is sent to the reactor and                second portion (152) which is sent to condenser;                and withdrawing carbamate-containing condensate                stream (20) from the tube side of condenser and is                returned to the reactor.
   USE - Synthesis of urea from ammonia and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The overall efficiency of the process is                improved by transferring waste heat to the fresh                ammonia fed to the synthesis loop, to preheat the                fresh ammonia. All the heat provided to this stream                is finally recovered in the condenser as low                pressure steam usable in the recovery section. The                accumulation of inert gasses in the reactor are                kept to a minimum.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A urea is synthesized from ammonia and carbon                dioxide by performing urea synthesis with a                stripping process in a synthesis section including                reactor (1), a stripper (2) and a condenser (3);                converting ammonia and carbon dioxide into urea                that occurs in the reactor at a synthesis pressure                with formation of an aqueous effluent (11)                containing urea and unconverted ammonium carbamate;                performing stripping step of reactor effluent in                the stripper, where the reactor effluent is heated                and contacted with gaseous carbon dioxide (12)                acting as a stripping aid, with formation of a                urea-containing solution (14) and of stripper                vapors (15) comprising mainly ammonia and carbon                dioxide; sending urea-containing solution effluent                from the stripper to a low-pressure recovery                section (4) for further processing; where the                gaseous carbon dioxide fed to the stripper                represents the full input of fresh carbon dioxide                to the synthesis section; splitting stripper vapors                into a first portion (151) which is sent to the                reactor and second portion which is sent to the                condenser which is a shell-and-tube kettle                condenser with a tube side (30) and a shell side                (31) and second portion of stripper vapors (152)                which is sent to the tube side for condensation;                and withdrawing carbamate-containing condensate                stream (20) from the tube side of condenser and is                returned to the reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for carbon dioxide (CO2)-stripping urea                plant comprising a synthesis loop including                reactor, stripper and a condenser; a line which is                arranged to feed a urea-containing reactor effluent                from the reactor to the stripper, and a line                arranged to feed fresh CO2 as a stripping medium to                stripper; a line which is arranged to send a                urea-containing solution effluent from the stripper                to a low-pressure recovery section for a further                processing, the line is arranged to feed fresh CO2                to the stripper being the only CO2 feed of the                synthesis section, a line which is arranged to feed                a first portion of stripper overhead vapors to the                reactor, and is arranged to feed a second portion                of stripper vapors to the condenser, and a line                which is arranged to feed back to the reactor a                carbamate-containing condensate stream which is                withdrawn from the condenser.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                urea synthesis process and plant.
   Reactor (1)
   Stripper (2)
   Condenser (3)
   Low-pressure recovery section (4)
   Aqueous effluent (11)
   Gaseous carbon dioxide (12)
   Urea-containing solution (14)
   Stripper vapors (15)
   Carbamate-containing condensate stream                (20)
   Tube side (30)
   Shell side (31)
   First portion (151)
   Second portion of stripper vapors                (152)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbamate-containing                  condensate stream is returned to the reactor via                  a carbamate separator which separates a                  carbamate-containing liquid from the effluent of                  the condenser, and the liquid is sent to the                  reactor. The carbamate-containing liquid, which                  is obtained in the carbamate separator, is sent                  to the reactor via an ejector together with fresh                  ammonia. The fresh ammonia fed to the ejector                  represents the majority of fresh ammonia input to                  the synthesis section, preferably greater than or                  equal to 80% of the fresh ammonia input. Prior to                  introduction in the tube side of the condenser,                  the second portion of stripper vapors is mixed                  with a stream of fresh ammonia and/or with a                  carbamate-containing recycle solution coming from                  recovery section. The urea synthesis section does                  not include a high pressure scrubber and a stream                  of inert gas vented from the reactor is mixed                  with the second portion of stripper vapors prior                  to introduction of stripper vapors in the tube                  side of the condenser. The fresh ammonia input of                  the synthesis section is partially introduced                  into the reactor via the ejector and partially                  mixed with the stripper vapors before                  introduction in the tube side of the condenser.                  The shell side of the condenser is traversed by a                  cooling fluid, which is preferably boiling water,                  at a pressure of less than or equal to 6 bar,                  preferably 2-6 bar. The urea is synthesized at a                  pressure of 120-180 bar, and the low-pressure                  recovery is performed at a pressure of 2-6 bar,                  preferably 4 bar. Preferred Devices: The plant                  further comprises a carbamate separator arranged                  to separate the condensate withdrawn from the                  tube side of the condenser into a                  carbamate-containing liquid, which is recycled to                  the reactor, and a vapor or gas phase. The plant                  further includes an ejector arranged to feed the                  carbamate containing liquid from the carbamate                  separator to the urea reactor, and a line                  arranged to feed fresh ammonia as a motive stream                  to the ejector. The plant further includes                  greater than or equal to 2 lines arranged to mix                  the second portion of stripper vapors, prior to                  its introduction in the tube side of the                  condenser, with a stream of fresh ammonia and/or                  with a carbamate-containing recycle solution                  coming from the recovery section. The synthesis                  loop does not include a high-pressure scrubber                  and a line is arranged to mix a stream of inert                  gas vented from the reactor with the second                  portion of stripper vapors prior to introduction                  of the stripper vapors in the tube side of the                  condenser. The urea reactor is a vertical reactor                  having a height of less than or equal to 20                  meters, preferably 12-18 meters. The plant                  includes a structure for supporting the reactor,                  stripper and condenser, where the maximum                  elevation of the structure is less than or equal                  to 40 meters, preferably 30-38 meters.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN WO2022181939-A1; KR2022121652-A
TI New carbonic anhydrase having increased heat            resistance, basic resistance and alkali resistance,            comprising specific amino acid sequence, useful for            capturing carbon dioxide.
AU HWANG I H
   SONI A
   RAZZAKMD A
   KUMARI M
   DIET S
   ABDUL R M
AE POSTECH ACAD-IND FOUND (UYPO-C)
   BIOCOMPANION INC (BIOC-Non-standard)
GA 2022B2458F
AB    NOVELTY - A carbonic anhydrase having increased heat                resistance, basic resistance and alkali resistance,                comprising a 226 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 5)                fully defined in the specification, a 226 amino                acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 7) fully defined in the                specification, a 233 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID                NO: 11) fully defined in the specification, or a                242 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 13) fully                defined in the specification, is new.
   USE - The carbonic anhydrase is useful for capturing                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonic anhydrase has increased heat                resistance, basic resistance, alkali resistance,                and increased stability even in salt and alkaline                conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a recombinant vector comprising a gene of the                carbonic anhydrase;
   a transformant transformed with the                recombinant vector; and
   method for producing carbonic anhydrase with                increased heat resistance, base resistance and                alkali resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation (Claimed): The carbonic                  anhydrase is prepared by (i) preparing a culture                  by culturing the transformant, and (ii) obtaining                  carbonic anhydrase having increased heat                  resistance, basic resistance and alkali                  resistance from the culture. Preferred                  Biomolecule: The carbonic anhydrase is encoded                  from a 678 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 6)                  fully defined in the specification, a 678 base                  pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 8) fully defined in the                  specification, a 699 base pair sequence (SEQ ID                  NO: 12) fully defined in the specification, or a                  741 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 14) fully                  defined in the specification. The carbonic                  anhydrase is derived from Sulfurihydrogenibium                  azorenseor Persephonella marinaEX-H1 bacteria.                  The transformant is one Escherichia colichosen                  from BL21 (DE3), Rosetta (DE3), Rosetta 2 (DE3),                  ArcticExpress (DE3), STAR (DE3), C41 (DE3) and                  C43 (DE3).
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details about design of                chimeric carbonic anhydrase with enhanced thermal                stability that do not support the claims.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an expansion tobacco                thread liquid carbon dioxide power circulation                system, the design design of the utility model is                through the reverse circulation type shielding pump                with a closed structure, realizing the pump body                sealing connection between the driving source and                the pipeline, and improving the corresponding                pipeline, specifically cancelling the relatively                complex, there is a compression air device with                leakage risk and high cost, but only need to butt                the tail part of the reverse circulation type                shielding pump and the outlet pipeline to realize                reflux balance. PN CN114807263-A
TI Improving efficiency of prolonging salt Zymomonas            production according to multiple factors, comprises            culturing extended salt Zymomonas under environment            suitable, instructing oxygen uptake rate and carbon            dioxide release rate, adding sodium chloride into            culture medium and performing salt impact.
AU ZHUANG Y
   GUO M
   XIAO C
   LIU H
   ZHANG Y
   YU J
   WANG Z
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2022A2270H
AB    NOVELTY - Improving efficiency of prolonging salt                Zymomonas production according to multiple factors                comprises (i) culturing extended salt Zymomonas                under the environment suitable for growth; and (ii)                taking oxygen uptake rate and/or carbon dioxide                release rate as instruction, when is higher than                110mmol/L/h, adding sodium chloride into the                culture medium to final concentration 3-15 % (w/v),                and performing salt impact.
   USE - The method is useful for improving efficiency                of prolonging salt Zymomonas production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively has short product                production time, low corrosivity, low cost, easy                process control, effectively improves the                production efficiency of ectoine, and meets the                requirements of high efficiency, low energy                consumption and environmental friendliness in the                biological reaction process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The medium for                  culturing Zymomonas includes carbon source,                  nitrogen source and sodium chloride. The content                  of sodium chloride is lower than 2%, preferably                  lower than 1.5%. The carbon source is                  40&#177;10 g/l glucose, and the nitrogen                  source is 20&#177;6 g/l peptone and                  10&#177;3 g/l yeast extract. The content of                  sodium chloride is 1&#177;0.3% (w/v).Preferred Method: In the step (i), culturing                  at 37&#177;2&#8451;, preferably                  37&#177;0.5&#8451;; and/or a pH of                  7.0&#177;0.3, preferably 7.0&#177;0.15                  and the dissolved oxygen concentration is                  &#8814;30%. In the step (ii), adding sodium                  chloride into the culture medium until the final                  concentration is 4-9% (w/v), and the final                  concentration is between 4.5-9% (w/v) when the                  oxygen uptake rate and/or carbon dioxide release                  rate reaches 130 &#177;-20 mmol/l/hour,                  preferably 130 &#177;-10 mmol/l/hour to carry                  out salt impact for s 1-5 times, preferably 1-3                  times, where the sodium chloride is sodium                  chloride particles, where instructing by the                  oxygen uptake rate and/or carbon dioxide release                  rate, when is higher than 110 mmol/l/hour,                  performing first salt shock and performing second                  salt shock for 1.5-3.5 hours, preferably 2-3                  hours after first treatment.

PT P
PN CN114789044-A
TI Preparing modified solid amine adsorbent for            processing industrial waste gas containing carbon            dioxide, comprises e.g. dissolving porous carrier and            nanofibers in solvent, uniformly stirring, dissolving            modified porous material and polyethyleneimine in            organic solvent, evaporating and vacuum drying.
AU HAN Y
   WANG M
AE HEFEI ZHONGYA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (CHNB-C)
GA 2022A03221
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified solid amine adsorbent                comprises (i) dissolving the porous carrier and the                nanofibers in the excess first organic solvent, and                uniformly stirring to obtain a mixed solution, (ii)                placing mixed solution in the drying oven of                70-100&#176;C and drying to obtain modified                porous material, and (iii) dissolving the modified                porous material and polyethyleneimine in an excess                of the second organic solvent, stirring                continuously at 50-65&#176;C, and evaporating                and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified                solid amine adsorbent for processing industrial                waste gas containing carbon dioxide (CO2).
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes long-term continuous and                stable operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for modified                solid amine adsorbent prepared according to above                mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous carrier is                  macroporous resin or molecular sieve. The pore                  size of the porous carrier is 2-8 nm, and the                  pore volume is 0.5-3.5 cm3/g. In the step (i),                  the mass ratio of the porous carrier to the                  nanofiber is (85:15)-(99:1). In the step (ii),                  the mass ratio of the modified porous material to                  polyethyleneimine is (50:50)-(80:20). The                  polyethyleneimine is a branched-chain                  polyethyleneimine with an average molecular                  weight of 400-1800. The nanofibers are glass                  fibers, aramid fibers and/or carbon fibers. The                  first organic solvent is methanol or ethanol. The                  second organic solvent is methanol or                  ethanol.

TI Preparing strongly acidic ion exchange resin-based            carbon pellet, comprises mixing and heating strongly            acidic ion exchange resin with formaldehyde and            sulfuric acid, heating and carbonizing cross-linked ion            exchange resin under protection of inert gas, and            activating with water and carbon dioxide.
AU HUANG Y
   DUAN H
   SHEN Z
   ZHANG J
   FAN S
   XU J

AB    NOVELTY - Milling high comestible products into powder involves applying solid carbon dioxide (CO2) in the form of CO2 snow to a freeze-dried high comestible product; cooling the freeze-dried comestible product below the melting temperature; feeding the mixture of solid CO2 and cooled freeze-dried comestible product mixture into a milling apparatus, where sufficient solid CO2 is applied to the freeze-dried comestible product, such that solid CO2 exists in the milling chamber to maintain the freeze-dried comestible product below the melting temperature of the contained during a milling operation, and the milling operation produces a high comestible powder.
   USE - Method for milling high comestible products into powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables milling of high comestible products into powder, which has sufficiently high fat and/or oil content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The freeze-dried high comestible product is produced by freeze-drying a high comestible meat product that had been chopped or cubed. The freeze-dried high comestible product is layered between layers of CO2 snow in a tray or container device. The comestible product is initially cooled to less than its respective brittle point; and where the high-fat freeze-dried comestible product is maintained up to 20 degrees C below the melting point of the fat during the milling operation. The powder has particle size less than 1000 micron.

PT P
PN CN113264549-A
TI Preparing active zinc oxide, involves providing raw materials contains chlorine, high-chlorine zinc hypoxide sequentially leached through leaching process, processing leached filtrate, oxidation, and carbonizing.
AU PAN H
   WANG H
   XUE G
AE JIANGYONG XIAOXIANG CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202199256V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing active zinc oxide involves providing raw materials contains chlorine and high-chlorine zinc hypoxide are sequentially leached through leaching process; processing leached filtrate through oxidation process; removing precipitate in oxidation process and then processed through zinc powder replacement process; carbonizing replaced and purified liquid by distilling ammonia treatment, and then pressing filter, rinsing filter residue after press filter, and then dried and calcined, recycling carbon dioxide generated during drying and calcining in ammonia carbonization treatment for reuse; returning one portion of filtrate to leaching process for reuse, and other portion of filtrate is ammonium chloride, then evaporating mother liquor.
   USE - Method for preparing active zinc oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps in improving recycling of waste, water recycling, carbon dioxide recycling and ammonia recycling; reduces environmental pollution and production costs; reduces energy consumption and use of materials in production process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process for preparing active zinc oxide generating condensed water during evaporation process of ammonium chloride mother liquor, using condensed water to rinse filter residue after press filtration; adding leaching agent which includes one or more of ammonia, ammonium chloride, and ammonium bicarbonate; placing raw materials into leaching tank contains leaching solution, adding leaching agent to leaching solution, and stirring and reacting for certain period of time at room temperature; completing solid-liquid separation, then filtrate is transported to oxidation process, placing filter residue in slag washing tank, stirring for 1 hour and then filtered under pressure, preparing washing water according to composition ratio of leaching solution and then returning to leaching tank; performing oxidation process to adding filtrate in leaching process to oxidation tank and heating to 50-70 degrees C, and then slowly adding 1.5 times weight of ammonium persulfate according to total weight of divalent iron and divalent manganese in leaching solution under stirring, after 1 hour reaction, detecting manganese to less than 100 mg/L, when manganese content is higher than value, adding ammonium persulfate in appropriate amount until iron and manganese removal is qualified; removing iron and manganese for liquid-solid separation, then filtrate is transported to displacement tank, washing filter cake with clean water and then bagged for sale; performing zinc powder replacement process is to first detect content of copper and cadmium in filtrate after oxidation process, controlling liquid temperature below 40 degrees C, and stirring speed of 30-100 revolutions/minute, which is 1 to 2 times total weight of copper and cadmium then slowly add 250-300 mesh zinc powder to react for 20-40 minutes, and timely detecting cadmium content is lower than 20 mg/L; qualifying removal of copper and cadmium, adding 0.8-1.5 Kg of zinc powder and quickly filter; washing filtered copper and cadmium slag with water, then washing water returns to first step, and copper cadmium slag is sold in bags; adding purified liquid to sealed ammonia vaporization and carbonization tank heated with stirring jacket, where steam is turned on for heating and evaporation, and then ammonia is cooled and absorbed by ammonia absorption system with negative pressure device to prepare ammonia water with concentration of about 15%, where pH value drops to 7-8, stop heating, and switching steam to circulating cooling water to cool down to 30-40 degrees C; distilling ammonia, then closing ammonia gas outlet valve and carbon dioxide gas is introduced, and then waste gas of basic zinc carbonate calcined is provided for carbonization, where pH value drops to 6-6.5 and carbonization degree reaches 200, then carbonization is stopped; using membrane filter press for pressure filtration, placing filter cake in washing tank, where filtrate is further processed; rinsing process adopts four-stage countercurrent continuous rinsing, and then filter cake is obtained after rinsing; using calciner to calcined rinsed filter cake, where calciner is gas-fired calciner and calciner includes stainless steel brick kiln barrel and combustion chamber, then combustion chamber is provided with thermal insulation wall, and stainless steel brick kiln barrel passes through pipe, connecting dust bag fan and calciner exhaust fan, where inlet of calciner is equipped with screw feeder, and calcining temperature is controlled at 400-900 degrees C, then carbon dioxide gas after calciner is connected to intermediate storage tank or directly supplied carbonization tank, where calcined product is zinc oxide; performing ammonium chloride mother liquor evaporation to evaporate filtrate after pressure filtration in ammonia distillation and carbonization step, then steam is injected into device, where vacuum pump, cooling water circulation right pump, and cooling tower fan are turned on, when temperature and negative pressure of heater and vapor-liquid separation tank of each evaporator meet equipment design requirements, then enters normal operation time and vapor-liquid separation when concentration of ammonium chloride in tank reaches set concentration, then discharged in time, and when condensed water level reaches discharge height, then transported to rinsing process; placing discharged ammonium chloride concentrated hot crystal slurry in cooling crystallization tank to continue crystallization, and when temperature is close to ambient temperature, then starts to spin dry and pack.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.)

PT P
PN WO2021144042-A1
TI Treatment of water stream involves subjecting water stream to bipolar membrane electrodialysis, resulting in gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and treated water stream comprising water and acid equivalents of additional salts.
AU IN T VEEN B C
   YASSIN S
   KEYZER L P S
   CHENG Y C
   MEURS J H H
   VAN MOURIK A
   DERKING A
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 2021831086
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of a water stream comprising water, a carbonate salt and/or a hydrogen carbonate salt, and one or more additional salts, involves: (a) subjecting the water stream to bipolar membrane electrodialysis, resulting in a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and a treated water stream comprising water and acid equivalents of the additional salts; and (b) removing the acid equivalents of the additional salts from at least a portion of the treated water stream resulting from step (a).
   USE - The process is useful for treatment of a water stream comprising water, carbonate salt and/or hydrogen carbonate salts, and one or more additional salts (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides a more compact process which utilizes less utilities, including for example less solids handling, resulting in reduced capital and operational expenditures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: Step (a) is performed in a stack of repeating electrodialysis unit cells, each electrodialysis unit cell comprising two compartments formed by a bipolar membrane and either a cation exchange membrane or an anion exchange membrane, preferably a cation exchange membrane, and where the water stream is preferably fed into the compartment where protons are migrated into from the bipolar membrane. Step (a) is performed in a stack of repeating electrodialysis unit cells, each electrodialysis unit cell comprising three compartments formed by a bipolar membrane, a cation exchange membrane and an anion exchange membrane, and where the water stream is fed into the compartment formed by the cation exchange membrane and the anion exchange membrane. In step (b), one of the following methods is applied: precipitation, reverse osmosis, electrodialysis without a bipolar membrane, absorption and biotreatment. In step (b), a precipitating agent is added to portion of the treated water stream resulting from step (a), where the precipitating agent forms a precipitate with the acid equivalents of the additional salts which precipitate is then removed from the treated water stream. Preferred Components: The water stream to be treated in step (a) originates from a process for producing propylene oxide, preferably from a process for co-producing propylene oxide and styrene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additional salts are selected from phosphate, nitrate, sulfate and borate salts, preferably phosphate and nitrate salts, more preferably phosphate salts. The cation in the additional salts is a monovalent cation, preferably a monovalent metal cation. The cation in the carbonate salt and/or hydrogen carbonate salt is a monovalent cation, preferably a monovalent metal cation.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Climate warming suppressing method, involves paving reflecting device on ground surface, where sunshine is reflected, absorption of earth to solar radiation is reduced, atmospheric temperature is reduced, and climate warming is suppressed.
AU LI Q
AE LI Q (LIQQ-Individual)
GA 2021684074
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves paving a reflecting device (11) on a ground surface, where sunshine is reflected, and absorption of earth to solar radiation is reduced. Atmospheric temperature is reduced, and climate warming is suppressed. The reflecting device is paved on the ground surface, and the sunshine is reflected.
   USE - The method is useful for suppressing climate warming in a planting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively reducing the temperature of the ground surface, increasing the rainfall in the arid area, changing the desert into the green continent, making more plants absorb more atmospheric carbon dioxide and transforming the over-earth sunlight and carbon dioxide into grain, melon and fruit, vegetable or other low-temperature organic matter.

TI Preparation of natto extract rich in spermidine            comprises using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid            extraction technology to prepare the natto extract rich            in spermidine.
AU SUN H
AE TIANJIN JIANFENG NATURAL PROD R & D CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021345526
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of natto extract rich in                spermidine comprises using supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid extraction technology.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing natto                extract rich in spermidine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple processing process,                short preparation time, high extraction rate, low                extraction temperature, which can effectively avoid                the heat sensitive component heated degradation;                the obtained natto extract of spermine content is                10-15 times of the natto. The natto extract does                not contain thalli and spore, nor has no fast                ammonia smell in fresh natto or natto freeze-dried                powder; it can be accepted by more widely. The                preparation process does not generate waste                residue, waste liquid and waste gas; the extracting                slag still keeps the nattokinase and other nutrient                content contained in the fresh natto itself; it                does not affect the further use; it realizes the                best use of the material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method uses 10-70%                  ethanol aqueous solution as carrying agent.

PT P
PN US2021079293-A1; WO2021055003-A1; CA3154712-A1; EP4031597-A1
TI Production of coated proppants with polyurethane            proppant coating used in hydraulic fracturing, involves            copolymerizing epoxide and carbon dioxide monomer,            reacting with isocyanate monomer, and coating proppant            particles.
AU SAINI R K
   HAQUE M H
   ALLEN S D
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202128074J
AB    NOVELTY - Production of coated proppants with a                polyurethane proppant coating involves forming                aliphatic polycarbonate polyol from the                copolymerization of epoxide and carbon dioxide                monomers, forming the polyurethane proppant coating                (110) by reacting the aliphatic polycarbonate                polyols and diisocyanate monomer and/or isocyanate                monomer, and coating proppant particles (100) with                the polyurethane proppant coating.
   USE - Production of coated proppants with                polyurethane proppant coating used in hydraulic                fracturing applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The proppant coating protects the proppant                particles from degradation by the presence of                aqueous fluids at downhole temperatures. The                proppant coating increases the surface area of the                particles, and provides increased hardness and                tensile strength to the proppant particles. The                polyurethane proppant coating further exhibits                increased glass transition temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                proppant particles and coated proppant.
   Proppant particles (100)
   Proppant coating (110)
   Coating process (200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The epoxides and carbon                  dioxide are reacted and polymerized until a                  desired molecular weight aliphatic polycarbonate                  polyol is formed and in which at least 90% of the                  end groups in the aliphatic polycarbonate polyol                  composition are hydroxyl groups. The                  polymerization is then terminated. The proppant                  particles are heated from 10degrees Celsius to                  250degrees Celsius before coating. The proppant                  particles, the polyurethane proppant coating, and                  a surfactant are mixed to prevent clumping. The                  polyurethane proppant coating is functionalized                  with fluoroalkane alcohols, fluoroether alcohols,                  fluorosulfonamide alcohols, 6C or more                  hydrocarbons, silica groups, fluoro groups or                  perfluoro groups. Alternately, polyurethane                  proppant coating is formed by reacting at least                  one of diisocyanate monomers or isocyanate                  monomers, and polyethercarbonate polyol,                  polycaprolactone polyols, polylactide polyols,                  poly(beta-lactone) polyols, poly(s-lactone)                  polyols, polysulfide polyols, fluorinated vinyl                  ether polyols, siloxane polyols, or aminic                  polyols, and coating proppant particles with the                  polyurethane proppant coating to produce coated                  proppants with polyurethane proppant                  coating.Preferred Properties: The individual                  epoxide-carbon dioxide copolymer has number                  average molecular weight of 400-20000, and                  contains more than 90% carbonate linkages and 98%                  or more hydroxyl groups as end groups. Preferred                  Composition: The amount of at least one of                  diisocyanate monomers or isocyanate monomers is                  50-150 wt.% greater than an amount of polyols.                  The proppant particles are coated with 1-10 wt.%                  polyurethane proppant coating.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyurethane                  proppant coating further comprises an amine                  solution comprising ethylenediamine,                  propylenediamine, cyclohexadiamine,                  triethylenediamine, tetramethylethylenediamine                  and/or hexamethylenediamine. The diisocyanate                  monomers and/or isocyanate monomers comprise                  toluene-2,4-diisocyanate, 1,5-naphthalene                  diisocyanate, diphenylmethane-2,4-diisocyanate                  and/or diphenylmethane-2,2-diisocyanate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The proppants are                  coated with a lubricating agent, an additional                  resin, a coupling agent, a crosslinker, or a                  strengthening agent comprising at least one of                  glass fibers, carbon fibers, aramid fibers, mica,                  silica, alumina, carbon nanotubes, nanosilica,                  nanoalumina, nanozinc oxide, nanotubes,                  nanocalcium carbonate and nanozirconium oxide.                  The polyurethane proppant coating comprises 1-15                  wt.% strengthening agent.

PT P
PN EP3771724-A1
TI Producing polyether carbonate polyol, comprises            adding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide into            H-functional starter substance in presence of double            metal cyanide catalyst or metal complex catalyst and            adding component K into reaction mixture.
AU HOFMANN J
   LAEMMERHOLD K
   HILKEN P
   WELSCH N
   NEFZGER H
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2021127073
AB    NOVELTY - Producing polyether carbonate polyol,                comprises (a) adding alkylene oxide and carbon                dioxide into H-functional starter substance in the                presence of a double metal cyanide catalyst or a                metal complex catalyst based on the metals zinc                and/or cobalt to obtain a reaction mixture                containing the polyether carbonate polyol, and (b)                adding at least one component K into the reaction                mixture containing the polyether carbonate polyol.                A buffer suitable for buffering a pH of 3-9 is used                as component K, component K is free from phosphoric                acid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing polyether                carbonate polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces polyether carbonate polyol                having lowest possible cyclic carbonate                content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                mixture containing polyether carbonate polyol and                component K and buffer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The component K                  contains neither compounds which contain a                  phosphorus-oxygen bond nor compounds of                  phosphorus which can form at least one P-O bonds                  by reaction with OH-functional compounds. The                  buffer system is suitable for buffering a pH of                  3-7.5. The buffer is a mixture of carboxylic                  acids and their alkali metal salts, aqueous                  solutions of alkali metal salts of carboxylic                  acids, 2-(N-morpholino)ethanesulfonic acid (MES)                  and                  3-(4-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazin-1-yl)propane-1-sulfonic                  acid (HEPPS), preferably malic acid/sodium salt                  of malic acid, MES and HEPPS. Preferred Method:                  The method further comprises (c) thermally                  reducing the content of highly volatile                  constituents in the polyether carbonate polyol                  obtained from step (a) at 80-200 degrees C,                  performed before the step (b), and (d) adding                  5-2000 ppm, preferably 30-500 ppm at least one                  component K into the reaction mixture containing                  the polyether carbonate polyol obtained from the                  step (c). The step (a) comprises removing an                  H-functional starter substance or a mixture of at                  least two H-functional starter substances or a                  suspending agent and optionally water and/or                  other highly volatile compounds by increased                  temperature and/or reduced pressure (drying),                  activating the double metal cyanide (DMC)                  catalyst by adding a partial amount (based on the                  total amount of the amount of alkylene oxides                  used in the activation and copolymerization) of                  alkylene oxide into the mixture in the presence                  of carbon dioxide, waiting for the temperature                  peak (hotspot) and/or a pressure drop in the                  reactor that occurs due to the following                  exothermic chemical reaction, and adding alkylene                  oxide, carbon dioxide and optionally an                  H-functional starter substance into the mixture,                  where the catalyst H -functional starter                  substance or the mixture of at least two                  H-functional starter substances or the suspending                  agent is added before or after drying. The                  reaction mixture is removed from the reactor. The                  DMC catalyst is continuously metered into the                  reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Floating catalytic preparation method of single-walled carbon nanotubes, by preparing precursor mixed solution, catalyzer precursor and thiophene, treating, driving hydrogen and carbon dioxide carrier gas, and injecting into reaction device.
AU SHEN Y
   WAN R
AE WUXI DONGHENG NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202080727R
AB    NOVELTY - Floating catalytic preparation method of single-walled carbon nanotubes, involves (i) preparing precursor mixed solution, catalyzer precursor and thiophene, and ultrasonically treating for 15 minutes, where the mass ratio of the precursor mixed solution, catalyzer precursor and thiophene is (96.5-99.1):(0.4-2):(0.5-1.5), and (ii) driving by 2.5 SLM hydrogen and 25 sccm carbon dioxide carrier gas, injecting the ultrasonically treated mixed solution into the reaction device at 1000-1500 degrees C to collect single-walled carbon nanotubes, continuously adding the mixed solution after ultrasonic treatment to continuously collect single-walled carbon nanotubes.
   USE - Floating catalytic preparation method of single-walled carbon nanotubes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes batch preparation of single-walled carbon nanotubes, and improves the quality of the prepared single-walled carbon nanotubes.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the precursor mixed solution comprises liquid ethanol, liquid acetone and/or liquid toluene. Preferred Composition In step (i), the mass ratio of the precursor mixed solution, the catalyzer precursor and the thiophene is 98.5:1:0.5. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the ultrasonically treated mixed solution is injected into the reaction device at 1200 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN111302361-A
TI Recovery of alkali from aluminum profile mold            waste liquid by adding aluminum hydroxide into aluminum            profile mold waste liquid, stirring until pasty,            standing, and filling carbon dioxide.
AU LU C
   LI H
   PAN Y
AE GUANGDONG FENGLU ALUMINUM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   FOSHAN SANSHUI FENGLU ALUMINIUM CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 202058103G
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of alkali from aluminum profile mold                waste liquid comprises adding aluminum hydroxide                into aluminum profile mold waste liquid, stirring                until pasty, standing, and filling carbon dioxide,                so that waste liquid sodium hydroxide and aluminum                hydroxide react to generate sodium tetrahydroxy                aluminate, which reaches saturation state and                precipitation reaction occurs.
   USE - The method is for recovery of alkali from                aluminum profile mold waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, easy control,                high purity and recovery rate of alkali aluminum                hydroxide, and low processing cost, avoids waste                liquid pollution, promotes balanced forward                movement of the precipitation reaction, accelerates                the precipitation reaction, further promotes the                treatment efficiency, does not need heating,                achieves energy-saving effect, solves the problems                of multiple waste liquid processing devices and                high energy consumption, reduces the loss of                container device, and prolongs the service life of                the container device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The adding amount of                  aluminum hydroxide is not less than 240 g based                  on 1 L aluminum profile mold waste liquid. The                  method comprises adding the aluminum profile mold                  waste liquid into a stirring kettle, adding                  aluminum hydroxide, starting the stirring kettle                  to stir until pasty, stopping stirring, standing,                  infusing carbon dioxide, after standing for 2-6                  days, adding water at paste and water mass ratio                  of 1:0.4-0.6, starting the stirring kettle to                  stir for 10-20 minutes, standing for 1-3 hours,                  adding the obtained mixed liquid into filter                  press, and pressing the slag to obtain alkali                  liquid and aluminum hydroxide. The waste liquid                  is corrosive wash liquid, diluting solution of                  die casting liquid, aluminum ash liquid dissolved                  by sodium hydroxide, or oxidation pretreatment                  alkaline liquid.

The extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 30-55 degrees C, the pressure is 8-35 MPa, and the extraction time is 0.1-1 hour. In the method, further adding with an cosolvent to the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, where the cosolvent includes water, ethanol, propylene glycol and/or glycerol. The inlet temperature of spray drying is 140-250 degrees C. In the method, further adding carbon dioxide fluid in which the fragrance of sea buckthorn fruit is dissolved to the separation device, and separating the fragrance of sea buckthorn fruit, where the pressure in the separation device is 0.4-6 MPa.

   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano phytoncide used for preparing the composition (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared composition has excellent compatibility, stability and rapid deodorization effects, and is safe, environmentally-friendly and harmless to human health.

   (1) a composition comprising nano phytoncide which is prepared by the above-mentioned method; and
   (2) a method for preparing the composition, involving mixing the nano phytoncide, plant essential oil and water, and stirring for 2-4 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the crushed C.camphora leaves are passed through a sieve having a 3-6 mm aperture. The power of the ultrasound is 800-1000 W, and the ultrasonically processing time is 20-40 minutes. Preferred Components: The carrier comprises absolute ethanol, water, isopropanol, methanol, acetone and/or ethyl acetate. The surfactant comprises fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, polyoxyethylene sorbitan fatty acid ester, sucrose fatty acid ester, modified protein and/or modified starch. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of the phytoncide to diethyl ether is 1:(5-7). The composition comprises 20-35 pts. wt. nano phytoncide, 7-10 pts. wt. plant essential oil and 55-65 pts. wt. water.

TI Preparing biomass super activated carbon by mixing biomass raw material with phosphoric acid solution, drying, carrying out carbon activation in carbon dioxide atmosphere, cooling, ultrasonically washing with water and drying.
AU ZHANG Z
   LEI Y
   LI D
   LIU X
   GAO T
   ZHAO J
   WANG Y
   ZHOU G
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM HUADONG (UYPE-C)
GA 2019310553
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing biomass super activated carbon, involves: mixing a biomass raw material with a phosphoric acid solution and soaking for 20-30 minutes to obtain a mixture; drying the mixture and carrying out carbon activation in a 100 ml/minute carbon dioxide atmosphere at 800-850 degrees C to obtain a carbonized product; and cooling the carbonized product to room temperature, ultrasonically washing with water until it is neutral, and drying to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing biomass super activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: simplifies the preparation steps, shortens the preparation time, and realizes continuous production; increases the pore volume, specific surface area, yield, pore structure and adsorption performance of the activated carbon; and shortens the washing time and improves the performance of the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves carrying out carbon activation at a carbon dioxide flow rate of 50-200 ml/minute for 1-2 hours and ultrasonically washing with water at an ultrasonic frequency of 20-50 kHz and a power of 100-200 W for 2-5 minutes. Preferred Components: The biomass raw material is chosen from coconut shell and pine cone. Preferred Composition: The biomass raw material and phosphoric acid solution are used in a ratio of 1:(0.6-2.5). The amount of phosphoric acid solution is 40-50 %mass. The plant fiber content of the biomass raw material is not less than 46%.

PT P
PN CN109158107-A; CN109158107-B
TI Cobalt-based dual metal reforming catalyst useful for preparing liquid hydrocarbon.
AU QIAN Q
   HAN B
   HE Z
   CUI M
   ZHANG J
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 201906520J
AB    NOVELTY - Cobalt-based dual metal reforming catalyst represented by formula (I) is claimed.
   USE - The catalytic system comprising cobalt-based dual metal reforming catalyst is useful for preparing liquid hydrocarbon (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cobalt-based dual metal reforming catalyst represented by formula CoyMOx (I) is claimed.
   M = manganese, zinc, aluminum, and cerium; and
   y = 2-14.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing cobalt-based dual metal reforming catalyst comprising dissolving cobalt compound and second metal compound in water to obtain a mixed solution, gradually adding mixed solution dropwise to the alkaline aqueous solution under stirring condition, mixing, reacting to obtain solid powder, separating, calcining, reducing, and passivating to obtain cobalt-based dual metal reforming catalyst;
   (2) catalytic system comprising cobalt-based bimetallic catalyst and solvent;
   (3) use of the catalytic system for catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide and hydrogen to liquid hydrocarbons; and
   (4) preparing liquid hydrocarbon comprising reacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen under action of catalytic system to obtain liquid hydrocarbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cobalt compound is cobalt nitrate or hydrate, the second metal compound is manganese nitrate, zINC nitrate, aluminum nitrate, and cerium nitrate or hydrate, the mol ratio of cobalt metal to second metal compound is 2-14:1. The alkaline aqueous solution is sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate solution. The calcination temperature is 350-450 degrees C, the calcination time is 2-5 hours, the reduction temperature is 350-450 degrees C, and reduction time is 0.5-2 hours. The solvent squalane, cyclohexane, benzene, 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone and/or water, the ratio of cobalt-based bimetallic catalyst and solvent is 5-50 g:1 l before the reaction. Preferred Method: The method further comprises rinsing and drying after separation, and performing passivation using an inert gas containing a small amount of oxygen, and reducing using reducing agent i.e. hydrogen. The drying temperature is 80-140 degrees C, the drying time is 5-15 hours, the inert gas in the passivation is nitrogen and/or argon gas, the volume content of the oxygen in the inert gas is 0.5-3 vol.%. The total reaction pressure is 4-10 MPa, the partial pressure of carbon dioxide is 2-5 MPa, the partial pressure of hydrogen is 2-5 MPa, the reaction temperature is 140-220 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-20 hours.

TI Preparation of supported catalyst used for dry            reforming of methane and carbon dioxide to produce e.g.            synthesis gas by disposing active metal soluble salt            and soluble fuel into aqueous solution, mixing with            carrier, and drying.

AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 201898558D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of supported catalyst comprises                disposing active metal soluble salt and soluble                fuel into aqueous solution; mixing aqueous solution                with carrier; removing moisture at 70-100 degrees C                under vacuum condition; drying at 80-200 degrees C                for 0.5-24 hours; and calcining at 250-700 degrees                C for 1-24 hours in air.
   USE - Preparation of supported catalyst used for dry                reforming of methane and carbon dioxide to produce                synthesis gas or Fischer-Tropsch reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supported catalyst has activity that can                still reach thermodynamic equilibrium value after                long reaction at high space velocity, and produces                synthesized gas with hydrogen gas to carbon                monoxide ratio close to theoretical value of 1. It                has simple preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The aqueous solution                  comprises 0.1-50 mass% active metal soluble salt,                  auxiliary soluble salt, and soluble fuel. The                  soluble salt is nitrate, acetate, and/or                  chloride. The carrier is silica, alumina,                  magnesia, silicon carbide, titania, and/or                  zirconia. The active metal soluble salt is                  nickel, copper, gold, palladium, iron, and/or                  cobalt. The auxiliary agent-soluble salt is                  soluble salt of ruthenium, rhenium, zirconium,                  manganese, chromium, zinc, potassium, platinum,                  and/or lanthanum. Preferred Method: The aqueous                  solution is mixed with carrier by equal volume                  dipping method, vacuum evaporation dipping                  method, or spray coating method.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The soluble fuel                  comprises urea, glycine, ammonium nitrate, and/or                  citric acid. The molar ratio of soluble fuel to                  soluble salt is 0.1-10.

PN CN108813273-A
TI Fresh-preservation of semi-dry rice flour            comprises e.g. placing semi-dry rice flour in plastic            container having single-ended opening and pumping out            air in plastic container and filling composite gas            containing nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU YANG X
AE HUNAN QIPIN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019124230
AB    NOVELTY - Fresh-preservation of semi-dry rice flour                comprises (i) placing the semi-dry rice flour in a                plastic container having a single-ended opening,                where the semi-dry rice flour is present in an                amount of 15-40 wt.%; (ii) pumping out the air in                the plastic container and filling the composite gas                containing nitrogen and carbon dioxide; and (iii)                depressing the heat seal strip and sealing the open                end by hot pressing.
   USE - The method is useful for fresh-preservation of                semi-dry rice flour.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can inhibit bacterial growth and                prevents the rice flour from sticking and                breaking.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                semi-dry rice flour, subjected to                fresh-preservation as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (i) the                  plastic container is a composite packaging                  container containing biaxially-oriented                  polypropylene (BOPP) layer and a certified                  packaging professional (CPP) layer and the BOPP                  layer is an inner layer of a plastic container.                  The volume ratio of nitrogen and carbon dioxide                  in the composite gas is 3-1:1. The raw material                  of the semi-dry rice flour includes 95-99 pts.                  wt. rice and 0.5-5 pts. wt. additive. The                  additive is 0.2-4 pts. wt. modified starch, 0.1-1                  pts. wt. sodium stearoyl lactylate and 0.1-1 pts.                  wt. sucrose ester. The plastic container is                  plastic bag or plastic box and a plastic bucket.                  Preferred Method: The semi-dry rice flour is                  prepared by rice fermentation, crushing, mixing                  the material, extruding, aging, re-steaming and                  cutting. The aging condition is at 50 degrees C                  and a relative humidity of 75-85%.

PN CN108160051-A
TI Preparation of calcium alginate aerogel material used for medicines, bioremediation and sewage treatment involves preparing sodium alginate solution and calcium salt solution, solvent replacing and carbon dioxide supercritical drying.
AU CUI S
   ZHANG M
   LU Y
   JIANG R
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201849306A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of calcium alginate aerogel material comprises dissolving sodium alginate in organic solvent in mass ratio of 1:10-20, mixing and stirring to form sodium alginate solution; dissolving calcium salt in water and organic solvent to prepare concentration of 0.01-0.02 mol/L calcium salt solution; absorbing sodium alginate solution, slowly dropping into calcium salt solution to form calcium alginate gel pellet and standing; subjecting formed calcium alginate gel ball to solvent replacement with organic solvent; and subjecting solvent-replaced gel ball for carbon dioxide supercritical drying to obtain spherical aerogel material.
   USE - Method for preparing calcium alginate aerogel material used for medicines, bioremediation and sewage treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is improved compared to traditional synthesis method. The obtained material has high specific surface area and three-dimensional network structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium salt is calcium chloride or calcium bicarbonate. Preferred Method: The mixing and stirring process comprises ultrasonic dispersing, magnetic stirring and dispersing. The ultrasonic wave dispersion frequency is carried out at 200-400 kHz for 30-40 minutes. The magnetic stirring is carried out at 500-800 revolutions/minute for 40-60 minutes. The dropping speed is 20-30 times/minute. The standing time is 12-18 hours. The carbon dioxide supercritical drying is carried out at 35-45 degrees C under 7-12 MPa for 8-12 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is methanol, ethanol or glycol. The volume ratio of water and organic solvent is 1:4-9.

PT P
PN CN114164050-A
TI Industrial production of extracting DHA from Schizochytrium powder comprises e.g adopting No. 6 solvent to carry out low temperature countercurrent continuous extraction and concentrating under vacuum, adopting supercritical fluid carbon dioxide fluid to refine DHA crude oil.
AU LIU J
   CUI Y
   ZHANG H
AE QINGDAO SCITECH BIOTECH CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2022474319
AB    NOVELTY - Industrial production of extracting DHA from Schizochytrium powder comprises (i) adopting No. 6 solvent to carry out low temperature countercurrent continuous extraction and concentrating under vacuum to Schizochytrium granules to obtain DHA crude oil, and (ii) adopting supercritical fluid carbon dioxide fluid to refine DHA crude oil to obtain high-content and high-quality DHA essential oil.
   USE - The method is useful for industrial production of extracting DHA from Schizochytrium powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has large processing capacity, high extraction rate, low energy consumption, low solvent loss, and low extraction cost, is convenient for industrialized large-scale production, overcomes the shortcomings of solvent extraction solvent residues, improves the product quality, and the product has a very broad application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particles are cylindrical, with a diameter of 5-8 mm, a length of 10-15 mm, and a water content of more than 15%.Preferred Method: The method comprises adopting a low-temperature countercurrent continuous type pan-rotating extractor, and the extraction solvent is No. 6 solvent oil of food industrial auxiliary. The ratio of material to liquid in the extraction process is controlled in the range of 1:1.1-2.0, the temperature is controlled at 40-55degrees Celsius, and the rotational speed is 0.5-1 revolutions/hour. The control conditions of the extraction liquid in the centrifugal thin film evaporator: the vacuum degree is 0.070-0.090MPa, the temperature is controlled at 50-60degrees Celsius, and the rotation speed is 10-60 revolutions/minute. The vaporized No. 6 solvent oil enters the tube condenser, and the temperature is controlled at 15-25degrees Celsius. The residue after extraction enters the distillation machine to recover the residual solvent, and the temperature is controlled at 120-135degrees Celsius. The method adopts supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extraction device to refine DHA crude oil. The supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extraction device operating conditions are as follows: extraction temperature is 40-55degrees Celsius, pressure of 18MPa; the temperature of separation 1 is 35-50degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 8-10MPa; the temperature of separation 2 is 30-45degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 6-8MPa. The treatment residue in the extraction kettle adopts decompression to vacuum degree of 0.070-0.090MPa, the carbon dioxide fluid extraction cycle time is 1-2 hours, and the circulation flow is 10-30l/minute.

GA 2022373694
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-containing defect porous                carbon based on microporous organic polymer,                comprises polymerizing center and linkers to form                nitrogen-containing microporous organic polymers                through C-N coupling reaction, taking the polymer                as precursor, directly carbonizing under the action                of atmosphere template, and controlling the                carbonization temperature, so as to effectively                control the specific surface area and pore                structure of the porous carbon.
   USE - The material is useful in carbon dioxide (CO2)                adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, mild condition and                obvious effect, environmentally friendly, realizes                the preparation of the porous carbon without any                complex operation, and improves the adsorption                capacity of CO2.

The catalyst is                  bis(dibenzylideneacetone)palladium. The ligand is                  2-dicyclohexylphosphorus-2',4',6'-triisopropylbiphenyl. In                  the step (ii), the gas atmosphere template is                  nitrogen or carbon dioxide.Preferred Method: The method comprises (i)                  adding the inorganic salt to the reaction tube                  containing the center, linker, catalyst, ligand,                  alkali and solvent, and uniformly mixing,                  reacting for a certain time at a certain                  temperature, and filtering the product, washing,                  drying to obtain the nitrogen-containing                  microporous organic polymer, and (ii) carbonizing                  the nitrogen-containing microporous organic                  polymer as the precursor to obtain the porous                  carbon material containing nitrogen-containing                  defect sites of different specific surface area                  and pore structure under the action of the gas                  atmosphere template. In the step (i), the added                  molar ratio of the linker to the center is                  1:0.1-1:1. In the step (i), the reaction time is                  48-72 hours. In the step (i), the reaction                  temperature is 50-200&#8451;. In the step                  (ii), the carbonization temperature is                  800-1000&#8451;.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bismuth oxychloride/graphitic carbon nitride-2D/2D van der Waals heterojunction comprises using deionized water as a solvent, bismuth nitrate pentahydrate as a bismuth source, and tetracycline hydrochloride as a chlorine source, preparing the bismuth oxychloride complex by hydrothermal method and performing calcination in a muffle furnace to generate bismuth oxychloride, performing calcination with melamine, ultrasonically mixing graphitic carbon nitride in absolute ethanol to obtain bismuth oxychloride/graphitic carbon nitride-2D/2D van der Waals heterojunction.
   USE - The heterojunction is useful in the photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterojunction has fast photogenerated carrier migration rate and high separation efficiency. The vdW force accelerates the charges separation on the two-dimensional/two-dimensional interface, and improves the photocatalytic activity, and has good performance of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) providing 0.48-0.97 g bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate, stirring, dissolving in 10-20 ml deionized water, dissolving 0.48 g tetracycline hydrochloride in 20 ml deionized water, continuous magnetic stirring and ultrasonically dispersing for 20-30 minutes, adding the tetracycline hydrochloride solution to the bismuth nitrate pentahydrate solution, transferring the mixture into a 50 ml polytetrafluoroethylene-lined stainless steel kettle, and reacting at 140-180 degrees C for 15-24 hours, (2) taking out the sample after the hydrothermal reaction, using a centrifuge to centrifuge at 10000 revolutions/minute each time for 5-10 minutes, washing the centrifugal precipitate with distilled water and ethanol for multiple times, and finally placing in an oven at 60-80 degrees C to dry for 12-24 hours to obtain the black bismuth oxychloride complex precursor, providing 0.2-2 g dried precursor into a muffle furnace, sintering at 450-600 degrees C for 2-6 hours, where the heating rate is 1-5 degrees C/minute, naturally cooling to room temperature and collecting the sample to obtain the bulk bismuth oxychloride photocatalyst, and (3) taking 2-5 g melamine and performing calcination in a muffle furnace at 400-600?Oc at a heating rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute for 2-4 hours to generate graphitic carbon nitride, adding 100 mg bismuth oxychloride and 5-40 mg graphitic carbon nitride mixture into a beaker containing 50-100 ml ethanol, vibrating ultrasonically for 10-20 minutes to make it uniformly dispersed, magnetically stirring until the ethanol is completely volatilized naturally, drying the composite sample at 60-80 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain finally a bismuth oxychloride/graphitic carbon nitride-2D/2D van der Waals heterojunction sample, and studying the phase composition, microscopic morphology, charges separation and photocatalytic performance of the bismuth oxychloride/graphitic carbon nitride heterojunction photocatalyst by X-ray diffraction (XRD)., scanning electron microscope (SEM), DRS, and photocurrent measurement techniques. The hydrothermal temperature is 140-180 degrees C. The molar ratio of bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate to tetracycline hydrochloride is 2:1-1:1. Preferred Components: The bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, tetracycline hydrochloride, and melamine are all analytically pure. The water content of the ethanol is less than 0.01% (vol./vol.).

TI Producing renewable ethylene from biomass, involves performing multistep feedstock treatment method, culturing microorganisms in hexose fraction, subjecting succinic acid to electrolysis and utilizing unfermented feedstock.
AU GHAYUR M A
AE GHAYUR M A (GHAY-Individual)
   GHAYUR M (GHAY-Individual)
GA 202147977N
AB    NOVELTY - Process involves (a) performing multistep feedstock treatment method that separates carbohydrates into a hexose fraction comprising predominantly hydrolysed hexose carbohydrates and a pentose fraction comprising predominantly hydrolysed pentose carbohydrates, (b) culturing microorganisms in hexose fraction in one or more fermenter to produce predominantly succinic acid, succinate or their mixtures, (c) culturing microorganisms in hexose fraction in one or more fermenter to produce organic acid, (d) subjecting at least a portion of succinic acid, succinate or their mixtures to electrolysis to produce ethylene with coproduct mixture comprising carbon dioxide, where at least a portion of carbon dioxide is recycled to one or more fermenter, and (e) utilizing at least a portion of unfermented feedstock as solid biofuel in a thermal power station.
   USE - The process is useful for producing renewable ethylene from biomass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The feedstock is chosen from lignocellulose biomass, marine biomass, algal biomass, microbial biomass, crop biomass, crop waste, forestry biomass, forestry waste, organic waste and biodegradable matter. The hexose fraction is fermented by yeast or bacteria selectively capable of producing predominantly succinic acid from hexose carbohydrates. The organic acid is succinic acid or acetic acid. The succinic acid, succinate or their mixtures is further subjected to synthetic processes to form products. The ethylene is further subjected to synthetic processes to generate products. The thermal power station is cogeneration thermal power station. The thermal power station is oxy-fuel combustion thermal power station comprising (a) flue gas carbon dioxide capture unit, and (b) hydrogenating at least a portion of flue gas carbon dioxide to produce methanol.
