TI Preparing palladium-copper bimetallic nano-alloy supported cobalt tetroxide composite material useful as photocatalyst to reduce carbon dioxide comprises e.g. taking cobalt tetroxide, adding to a glass flask, and adding water.
AU LI H
   ZHONG K
   ZHU X
   YANG J
   XU H
   SHE X
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202135994G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing palladium-copper bimetallic nano-alloy supported cobalt tetroxide composite material comprises taking cobalt tetroxide, adding to a glass flask, adding water, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone, ascorbic acid and potassium bromide after ultrasonic dispersion, stirring in an oil bath at a certain temperature, adding potassium chloro palladate and copper chloride dihydrate, and finally carrying out the oil bath reaction of the mixed solution at a certain temperature in the air, washing the synthesized solid with water and ethanol many, and vacuum drying to obtain palladium-copper bimetallic nano-alloy supported cobalt tetroxide composite material.
   USE - The composite material is useful as a photocatalyst to reduce carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and environmentally friendly; has good repeatability, strong controllability; and has high application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the cobalt tetroxide, water, polyvinyl pyrrolidone, ascorbic acid, potassium bromide, potassium chloro palladate is 150 mg: 15 ml:105 mg: 180 mg: 300 mg: 0.225 - 0.270 mmol: 0.030-0.075 mmol. The mol ratio of the potassium chloro palladate to the copper chloride dihydrate is 7: 1. Preferred Method: The ultrasonic dispersing time is 20 minutes, the oil bath temperature is 80 degrees C, the stirring time is 10 minutes, reacting for 3 hours at 80 degrees C in oil bath. The method further comprises vacuum drying to obtain pink cobalt hydroxide, taking cobalt hydroxide paving in the ceramic square boat, placing into a tubular furnace, under the air atmosphere, reacting for many hours at a certain temperature, and finally obtaining black cobalt tetroxide. In the step (1), the content of cobalt nitrate hexahydrate is 0.58 g. The content of polyvinylpyrrolidone is 0.40 g. The content of water is 10 ml. The content of ethanol is 10 ml. The mixing time is 30 minutes, and the content of sodium hydroxide solution is 20 ml, and the concentration is 0.4 mol/l, and the reaction temperature is 200 degrees C, and the reaction time is 12 hours. In the step (2), the content of cobalt hydroxide is 100 mg, the reaction temperature is 400 degrees C, and the reaction time is 2 hours.

PT P
PN CN112076224-A
TI Preparing Ganoderma lucidum spore oil self-emulsifying soft capsule rich in Ganoderma lucidum acid useful for preparing e.g. anti-tumor food comprises granulating whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and making into nano-emulsion.
AU CHEN S
AE JIANGSU SHENGDIAN MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7988Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Ganoderma lucidum spore oil self-emulsifying soft capsule rich in Ganoderma lucidum acid comprises (a) taking the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder as raw material, performing one-step granulating by dry pressing, placing in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, obtaining light yellow Ganoderma lucidum spore oil rich in ganoderic acid and (b) making the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil and surfactant, auxiliary surfactant and antioxidant into whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil nano-emulsion.
   USE - The capsule is useful for preparing anti-tumor food, healthcare food or medicine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the content of ganoderic acid in the Ganoderma lucidum spore oil; has simple process flow, convenient operation and is suitable for industrial production. The product has stable quality and convenient quality control.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The whole Ganoderma lucidum is 51-60% Ganoderma lucidum fruiting body and 40-49% Ganoderma lucidum spore powder. The mass percentage of the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, surfactant, co-surfactant and antioxidant is: 45-70% Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, 20-55% surfactant, 0-20% co-surfactant, 0.1-1% antioxidant. The surfactant is at least one of soybean phospholipid, egg yolk lecithin, liquid lecithin and/or cephalin. The co-surfactant is at least one of glycerin, ethanol and/or polyethylene glycol 400. The antioxidant is at least one of vitamin E and vitamin C. Preferred Method: The raw material of the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder is made by crushing the Ganoderma lucidum fruit body to ultra-fine powder of less than or equal to 150 mesh, breaking the Ganoderma lucidum spore powder into the wall broken Ganoderma spore powder with a wall breaking rate less than or equal to 95% and mixing the two evenly to obtain the raw material of the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder. The step (a) comprises adding 0.3-1% starch or dextrin to the raw material of the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, mixing evenly, using a granulator to prepare a particle size of 0.1-0.3 by dry pressing, where the main pressure of the granulator is 5-9MPa and the side pressure is 0.5-0.9Mpa when granulating and extracting in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle. The supercritical carbon dioxide is a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction equipment equipped with an ultrasonic extraction device. The supercritical fluid is carbon dioxide, the extraction temperature is 23-45 degrees C, and the extraction pressure 10-34MPa, extraction time is 1-3 hours, extraction flow is 32-58kg/h, ultrasonic frequency is 30-60kHz, power is 100-800W. The separation process conditions are: the pressure of the first-stage resolver is 9-12MPa, and the separation temperature is 37-43 degrees C; the pressure of the secondary analyzer is 4-6Mpa, and the separation temperature is 27-33 degrees C; light yellow whole Ganoderma spore oil rich in ganoderic acid. The method comprises (a) taking the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil, adding surfactant, co-surfactant, and antioxidant at 20-60 degrees C, stirring well, filtering to obtain a transparent whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil self-emulsifying mixture, (b)placing the gelatin into the sol tank, adding water at 60-80 degrees C, sealing and heating at the same time, stirring until it melts and swell completely, adding glycerin, sealing the glue tank, turning off the stirring, turning on the vacuum pump, vacuumizing, waiting until the bubbles are drained, heat preserving at 50-70 degrees C, and (c) taking the soft capsule material liquid and the whole Ganoderma lucidum spore oil self-emulsifying mixture, placing on the soft capsule machine, adopting the compression method to prepare the soft capsule, drying, washing, then drying, separating inspection and packaging to obtain the finished product.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); ; A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B02-C03; B04-A08D2; B04-A10A; B04-A98; B04-B01B; B04-C02B2; B04-C02B4; B04-C03C; B04-N02; B05-B01P; B10-E04C; B11-C05; B12-M11C; B14-H01; B14-S18; B15-C00; B15-E00; D03-H01T2B; D03-K09; D05-A04C; A03-A00A; A03-C01; A11-C01C; A12-V01; A12-W09; A12-W14
IP A23L-031/00; A23L-033/115; A23P-010/30; A61K-031/575; A61K-036/074; A61K-009/48; A61P-035/00
PD CN112076224-A   15 Dec 2020   A61K-036/074   202108   Pages: 11   Chinese

PT P
PN AR115188-A1
TI Extraction of essential oils, aroma, flavors and dyes from plant materials involves arranging sample inside perforated extraction cartridge and placing in extraction chamber, passing carbon dioxide through extraction chamber, and separating.
AU 
AE MARTINEZ VALENZUELA P A (VALE-Individual)
   ISAACK I (ISAA-Individual)
   FRONTINI M (FRON-Individual)
   RUIZ JOHNSON I (JOHN-Individual)
   DI PAOLO F (DPAO-Individual)
GA 2020C58129
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of essential oils, aroma, flavors and dyes from plant materials comprises arranging sample inside extraction cartridge comprising perforations and placing in airtight extraction chamber; arranging solid carbon dioxide (CO2) (dry ice) inside pressurization chamber and tightly closing with lid; heating the pressurization chamber to release CO2 necessary to carry out extraction; passing CO2 through extraction chamber; contacting CO2 fluid with plant material to extract essential oils by adjusting pressure and temperature of CO2; and passing the extraction fluid through separator, reducing pressure of fluid, and releasing extracted essential oils in container.
   USE - The method is for extracting essential oils, aroma, flavors and dyes from plant materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows subcritical and supercritical extraction of essential oils, aroma, flavors and dyes from plant materials.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A05
IP C11B-009/00; C11B-009/02

PT P
PN EP3725851-A1; WO2020212204-A1; CA3128807-A1; CN113692428-A; AR118667-A1; KR2021153658-A; EP3956402-A1; IN202117044261-A; BR112021020756-A2; US2022169861-A1
TI Surface-reacted calcium carbonate production involves providing calcium carbonate-comprising material, providing inorganic acid, providing water-soluble inorganic magnesium salt, and then treating calcium carbonate-comprising material.
AU GERARD D E
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2020A2685U
AB    NOVELTY - Surface-reacted calcium carbonate production involves providing a calcium carbonate-comprising material, providing inorganic acid, providing water-soluble inorganic magnesium salt, and then treating the calcium carbonate-comprising material with the inorganic acid and carbon dioxide in an aqueous medium to form an aqueous suspension of surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the inorganic acid treatment, and is supplied by an external source, where the water-soluble inorganic magnesium salt is added before, during or after providing the providing inorganic acid.
   USE - Method for producing surface-reacted calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the surface-reacted calcium carbonate in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with modified specific Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area and porosity of the calcium carbonate in a predetermined way.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium carbonate-comprising material is a natural ground calcium carbonate or a precipitated calcium carbonate, preferably natural ground calcium carbonate is selected from marble, chalk, limestone, and mixtures. The precipitated calcium carbonate is selected from precipitated calcium carbonates having an aragonitic, vateritic or calcitic crystal form, and mixtures. The inorganic acid is selected from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, an inorganic acid salt and mixtures. The water-soluble, inorganic magnesium salt is selected from magnesium chloride, magnesium nitrate, magnesium sulfate, magnesium hydrogen sulfate, magnesium bromide, magnesium iodide, magnesium chlorate, magnesium iodate, hydrates, and mixtures, preferably the at least one water-soluble, inorganic magnesium salt is selected from the group consisting of magnesium bromide, magnesium nitrate, magnesium sulfate, hydrates, and mixtures.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).)

AE SRINIVAS G (SRIN-Individual)
GA 202094585N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for controlling the coronavirus disease (COVID-19) and other similar kinds of viruses in the air involves passing ambient air into the first chamber with the help of any type of air pump.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling the COVID-19 and other similar kinds of viruses in air and capturing the carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and particulate matter present in the ambient air and purifying air.
   ADVANTAGE - The equipment used in the method is simple, less weight with low noise and due its flexibility, can be used at various places. The complete method is performed in the closed containers and the complete equipment is sealed, hence there is no exposure of the equipment to ambient conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves performing first stage air sterilization with different UV lamps, performing second stage gas dispersion using different types of disinfectants at various concentrations, performing third stage using different chemicals to capture the carbon dioxide, and performing fourth stage for capturing particulate matter using different filter medium. Any type of material of construction is used to fabricate the equipment and for fittings. The equipment is controlled automatically, semi-automatically or manually. Preferred Component: The disinfectant is a solid or liquid or their combination. Preferred Device: The number of stages and size of the chambers varies as per the requirement.

PN CN111644148-A; CN111644148-B
TI Preparing ultrahigh-efficient sewage phosphorus            removal adsorbent, comprises e.g. burning magnesium            metal under carbon dioxide protective gas atmosphere,            collecting solid product and washing with water, and            drying.
AU YAO Y
   WANG M
   ZHAO T
   ZHANG Y
AE BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
GA 202091561G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultrahigh-efficient sewage                phosphorus removal adsorbent, comprises burning                magnesium metal at 600-800 degrees C under carbon                dioxide protective gas atmosphere, collecting solid                product after the metal magnesium is completely                burnt, washing with water having purity of not less                than that of deionized water, and drying to obtain                final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                ultrahigh-efficient sewage phosphorus removal                adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process and short                reaction process; utilizes easily available and                cheap raw material sources; and is environmentally                friendly, and does not cause secondary                pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of ultrahigh-efficient sewage phosphorus                removal adsorbent, comprising removing phosphorus                from phosphorus-containing solution using sewage                phosphorus removal adsorbent, where pH of                phosphorus-containing solution is 1-11.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The shape of magnesium                  metal is block, ribbon, strip or powder. In the                  use method: the mass ratio between adsorbent and                  phosphorus in phosphorus-containing solution is                  not less than 1. Preferred Method: The gas flow                  rate of the carbon dioxide protective gas is                  50-150 cm3/minute. The burning is carried out by                  heating to 600-800 degrees C at a rate of 5-10                  degrees C/minute and then burning. The use method                  comprises removing phosphorus from the                  phosphorus-containing solution using adsorbent,                  where the adsorption time is not less than 6                  hours, and the adsorption capacity of the                  adsorbent reaches more than 80% of the                  equilibrium adsorption capacity.

PT P
PN CN111217785-A
TI Extracting quercetin from Bidens bipinnata involves coarsely crushing fresh grass, soaking in water, immersing in liquid nitrogen, placing at room temperature, drying, crushing, mixing with solvent, extracting, and separating.
AU DONG Y
AE NANJING XIBOEN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202052065R
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting quercetin from Bidens bipinnata involves coarsely crushing fresh B. bipinnata grass, soaking in water, putting into sieve, immersing in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, placing at room temperature for 5-10 minutes, immersing in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, repeating for 2-3 times, drying, crushing, passing through 5-30 mesh sieve to obtain B. bipinnata powder, mixing B. bipinnata powder with solvent, putting into extraction kettle, passing supercritical carbon dioxide, controlling temperature to 40-50 degrees C and pressure to 30-45 MPa, passing carbon dioxide-containing extract to separation kettle, and performing reduced-pressure separation at pressure of 7-9 MPa and temperature of 25-30 degrees C.
   USE - Extracting quercetin from B. bipinnata.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides quercetin with improved quality and yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The biological enzyme is one of mu -glucosidase, pectinase, and cellulase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method preferably involves mixing B. bipinnata powder with 0.01-0.08 times biological enzyme before extraction, and leaving still for 30-60 minutes. The drying process involves stacking B. bipinnata in a vacuum drying box, controlling the degree of vacuum to 0.009-0.36 MPa, and vacuum drying at low temperature of 40-52 degrees C to a water content of less than 5%. Preferred Component: The solvent is a low boiling point solvent, preferably at least one of acetone, ethanol, ether, methanol, and methylene chloride.

MC B06-A01; B11-A02C2; B11-B03C

AB    NOVELTY - Process for providing a fiber cement product,                involves (a) providing an uncured fiber cement                product, (b) curing the uncured fiber cement                product, (c) optionally abrasive blasting of at                least part of the surface of the cured fiber cement                product, and (d) treating the cured fiber cement                product with carbon dioxide at a concentration of                0.01-100 vol.%, at a temperature of 5-90 degrees C,                at a relative humidity of 30-99 %, for a period of                1 minute to 48 hours.
   USE - The process is used for providing a fiber                cement for covering of a building construction (all                claimed) such as walls or roofs.
   ADVANTAGE - The process limits or prevents the spread of                efflorescence on fiber cement products exposed to                exterior or wet environments without detrimentally                affecting the other properties of the products,                preferably mechanical properties and product's                visual aspect.

Preferred Conditions: The step (d) takes place at                  a temperature of 20-60 degrees C and at a                  relative humidity of 40-95 %. The step (d) takes                  place for a period of 1-8 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (d), the                  concentration of carbon dioxide is 1-30 vol.%,                  preferably 5-20 vol.%.

   (3) converting carbon monoxide to methane, comprising converting carbon monoxide and hydrogen to methane and water using the catalyst at 350-400 degrees C and a pressure of 8-14 bar.

   Catalyst (1)
   Base body (2)

Preferred Method: The method comprises installing promoter based on magnesium in the base body, or directly adding promoter based on magnesium to a material mixture forming the base body. The catalyst is activated with a mixture of 8 vol.% hydrogen and 4 vol.% carbon monoxide.

TI Performing butt welding of high temperature resistant steel involves using manual tungsten argon arc welding as a primer, filling with manual carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, and using manual welding rod arc welding on the cover surface.
AU LI Y
   YU S
   ZHANG X
   SHAN L
   SHI X
AE PLA NO 63926 TROOPS (PLAS-Non-standard)
GA 202034042M
AB    NOVELTY - Performing butt welding of high temperature resistant steel involves using manual tungsten argon arc welding as a primer, filling with manual carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, and using manual welding rod arc welding on the cover surface.
   USE - Method for performing butt welding of high temperature resistant steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform butt welding of high temperature resistant steel, that can adapt to the processing of steel materials under high temperature environment, and is suitable for welding in the construction of aerospace launch missions, and welding method is simple and easy to use, and can be applied to the welding of various steel materials, and has fast welding efficiency, good heat resistance, and high welding quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The welding area is preheated before welding. The area to be welded is uniformly heated to 520-820 degrees C by gas cutting before welding. The residual height of the welded seam after welding shall be controlled within 1-3 mm. The reheating the weldment and holding it for a certain period of time includes reheating the weldment to 520-820 degrees C to ensure the constant temperature of the weldment for 2 hours. The welding current is 70-90 ampere when performing argon arc welding priming. The angle of the electrode is 45-60 degrees when performing arc welding on the cover surface of the electrode.

The aluminum-magnesium alloy comprises 47-53 wt.% aluminum, and greater than 90 wt.% aluminum+magnesium. The medium carbon ferromanganese comprises 78-83 wt.% manganese, less than 1.5 wt.% carbon, less than 0.03 wt.% sulfur, and less than 0.2 wt.% phosphorus.

PN JP6651115-B1; JP2020183559-A
TI Collecting lithium from lithium ion battery by            sintering separated active substance, reducing lithium            compound, adding metal hydroxide solution to lithium            compound, and supplying carbon dioxide gas to obtained            lithium hydroxide solution.
AU SAKUMA Y
   NAKAZAWA J
AE ASAKA RIKEN CO LTD (ASAK-Non-standard)
GA 202014438A
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium is collected from lithium ion battery                by sintering lithium ion battery in inert                atmosphere and removing organic solvent from                battery; separating active substance from battery                from which organic solvent is removed and sintering                separated active substance from battery at greater                than or equal to 600 degrees C with carbon as                reducing substance contained in the negative                electrode active material of the battery in inert                atmosphere; reducing lithium compound contained in                the active substance to obtain raw-coal acid                lithium; adding metal hydroxide solution which can                produce poorly soluble carbonate to raw coal acid                lithium to obtain lithium hydroxide solution; and                supplying carbon dioxide gas to lithium hydroxide                solution and collecting precipitated lithium                carbonate.
   USE - Collection of lithium from lithium ion                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can recover lithium with high yield                at a low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Organic solvent is                  removed from the battery by sintering at 200-300                  degrees C. Step of separating the active material                  from the battery from which organic solvent is                  removed includes crushing the battery and sieving                  to active material below the sieve. Active                  substance separated from the battery is sintered                  at 650-720 degrees C with carbon as a reducing                  substance contained in the negative electrode                  active material of the battery in inert                  atmosphere. Before supplying carbon dioxide gas                  to lithium hydroxide solution, carbon dioxide gas                  is supplied to 1st filtrate obtained by filtering                  lithium hydroxide solution, and precipitated                  lithium carbonate is collected. After supplying                  carbon dioxide gas to 1st filtrate, before                  collecting precipitated lithium carbonate, the                  1st filtrate is heated. Step of supplying carbon                  dioxide gas to 1st filtrate and collecting                  precipitated lithium carbonate is performed by                  filtering off precipitated lithium carbonate. The                  2nd filtrate after filtering off precipitated                  lithium carbonate is used for the solvent of the                  metal hydroxide which can produce the poorly                  soluble carbonate. Preferred Components: Metal                  hydroxides which can produce poorly soluble                  carbonate are selected from calcium hydroxide,                  magnesium hydroxide, and/or barium hydroxide,                  preferably calcium hydroxide. Inert atmosphere                  consists of nitrogen gas, argon gas, and/or                  carbon dioxide gas.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. TI Preparing cubic precipitated calcium carbonate by adding quick lime to warm water and stirring, filling calcium hydroxide slurry to jacketed reaction vessel equipped with dispersing paddle, cooling and filling with carbon dioxide.
AU HUANG Y
   WANG Z
   LI Z
   HE C
   YAO M
   CHEN G
   ZHENG Y
AE SHANGHAI CALCIUM CARBONATE FACTORY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5943B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing cubic precipitated calcium carbonate, involves (i) adding quick lime to warm water and stirring to make calcium hydroxide slurry, and (ii) adding calcium hydroxide slurry to the jacketed reaction vessel equipped with a dispersing paddle, opening the dispersion slurry, filling the jacket with circulating cooling water, cooling the calcium hydroxide slurry in the reaction container to less than 17 degrees C, and filling with carbon dioxide, controlling the temperature of the slurry in the reaction container at 30 degrees C until the pH value of the slurry in the reaction container is below 7, after completing of the reaction, precipitating the aqueous slurry to obtain the product.
   USE - Method for preparing cubic precipitated calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively controls the production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the warm water in step (i) is 40-60 degrees C. The stirring time in step (i) is 30-45 minutes. The linear velocity of the dispersing paddle in step (ii) when it is turned on is 5-9 m/second. Preferred Composition: The filling rate of carbon dioxide in step (ii) is 0.016 l/minute per gram of dry calcium hydroxide added.

PN CN110240978-A
TI Improving beer bitterness and storage stability comprises adding carbon dioxide and hop extract before fermentation stage of beer brewing process.
AU NIE C
   ZHANG J
   YANG G
   WANG X
   LIANG Y
   SUN S
AE UNIV QILU TECHNOLOGY (UYQQ-C)
GA 201981816C
AB    NOVELTY - Improving beer bitterness and storage stability by adding carbon dioxide and hop extract, comprises adding 0.1-1 g/l hop extract before 5-10 minutes of the fermentation stage of the beer brewing process.
   USE - The method is useful for improving beer bitterness and storage stability.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: inhibits the harmful bacteria which may appear in the beer by adding the hop extract before the fermentation stage of the beer brewing process; avoids harmful bacteria growing in beer; ensures the flavor and quality of the beer; utilizes iso- alpha decanoic acid in the hop extract, which is stable, not sensitive to light, and does not produce sunlight-odor chemicals; avoids the sunlight odor caused by the reaction of the different alpha -decanoic acid in the beer after packaging; ensures the stability of beer; reduces beer deterioration; and improves the fermentation degree of the beer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The hop extract is a carbon dioxide hop extract prepared by extracting the hop pellets by using carbon dioxide, where the content of the iso- alpha acid in the carbon dioxide hop extract is 35-40 wt.%. The carbon dioxide hop extract is prepared by (i) crushing hops, placing in an extraction column of a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction system, then sealing, separately heating the extraction system, pressurizing to extraction conditions, starting the extraction, performing constant temperature and constant pressure extraction for 2-3 hours to obtain the extracted carbon dioxide mixed fluid, where the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction process is 20-30 MPa and the extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction process is 40-55 degrees C, (ii) adding the extracted carbon dioxide mixed fluid into a separator, separating at 40-55 degrees C with a pressure of 5-6 MPa for 1 hour, recovering the carbon dioxide, reusing through the pipeline of the separator, and concentrating the extracted product at the bottom portion of the separator, and (iii) adding 5-10 wt.% extracted product, adjusting the content of the alpha -acid in the material to 35-40 wt.%, homogenizing at 40-55 degrees C, stirring at 55-60 degrees C, and sterilizing for 1 hour. After pulverization, the granule raw material has a particle size of 0.5-1 mm, and the hop raw material is a hop-free raw material having no mildew and pure aroma. The timing of the addition of the hop extract is: after cooling the wort in the beer brewing process and swirling, before the fermentation stage. Preferred Components: The amount of hop extract added is 0.1-0.5 g/l.

Preferred Method: The pomegranate seeds are                  soaked in disinfectant at a concentration of                  100-200 mg/L for 10-30 minutes. The SPEEK                  air-conditioning film has a carbon dioxide                  transmittance of 492-546 cm3/m2 for 24 hours at                  0.1 MPa and oxygen transmission of 100-120 cm3/m2                  for 24 hours at 0.1 MPa. The preparation of                  vapour-slow-release bactericidal sheet comprises                  uniformly mixing of the bacteriostatic components                  including cinnamaldehyde, eugenol and food grade                  white latex, ethanol and water to obtain a                  mixture, uniformly coated the mixture on the PP                  synthetic. The release rate of                  vapour-slow-release bactericidal sheet is 1                  wt.%/24 hours.

PN CN110052247-A; CN110052247-B
TI Graphene oxide/hectorite/chitosan aerogel type solid amine adsorbent used for adsorbing carbon dioxide, comprises graphene oxide/hectorite composite and chitosan.
AU MA R
   LIU Z
   LI Y
   LIAO P
   TANG W
AE UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES WUHAN (UYCI-C)
GA 201967021J
AB    NOVELTY - A graphene oxide/hectorite/chitosan aerogel type solid amine adsorbent comprises 0-0.3:1 mass ratio of graphene oxide/hectorite composite and chitosan. The graphene oxide/hectorite composite is prepared by mixing graphene oxide and modified hectorite according to mass ratio of 1:1.
   USE - Graphene oxide/hectorite/chitosan aerogel type solid amine adsorbent used for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of graphene oxide/hectorite/chitosan aerogel type solid amine adsorbent comprising taking graphene oxide aqueous solution and modified hectorite aqueous solution, ultrasonicating, adding acid solution to adjust pH value, standing, centrifuging to obtain precipitate, washing with deionized water to neutrality, drying, obtaining graphene oxide/hectorite composite, dissolving chitosan in acetic acid solution, adding graphene oxide/hectorite composite, stirring, irradiating with high-energy electron beam, immersing in polyethyleneimine methanol solution, carrying out surface modification, rapidly freezing by liquid nitrogen, and freeze drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The modified hectorite is prepared by dispersing hectorite in toluene according to mass volume ratio of 1 g:20-50 mL, ultrasonicating, adding 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane according to mass volume ratio of 1 g:2-5 mL, condensing and refluxing, centrifuging to obtain solid precipitate, washing successively with toluene and ethanol, and drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of graphene oxide/hectorite/chitosan aerogel type solid amine adsorbent comprising taking graphene oxide aqueous solution and modified hectorite aqueous solution, ultrasonicating, adding 5 wt.% dilute hydrochloric acid to adjust pH value, standing, centrifuging to obtain precipitate, washing with deionized water to neutrality, drying, obtaining graphene oxide/hectorite composite, dissolving chitosan in acetic acid solution according to mass volume ratio of 1 g:75-100 mL, adding graphene oxide/hectorite composite, stirring, irradiating with high-energy electron beam at irridiation dose of 10-50 kGy, immersing in polyethyleneimine methanol solution according to 1 g:10-15 mL mass volume ratio of polyethyleneimine to methanol, soaking according to mass ratio of 1-5:1, carrying out surface modification, rapidly freezing by liquid nitrogen, and freeze drying.

PT P
PN CN109745827-A; CN109745827-B
TI Adsorbent module used for adsorption and separation of methane, comprises combined adsorbent module constructed by regular structure adsorbent, where adsorbent modules are in sequence of dewatering-, carbon dioxide- and methane-adsorbent.
AU WANG S
   SUN T
   ZHAO S
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2019445631
AB    NOVELTY - An adsorbent module comprises a combined adsorbent module constructed by a regular structure adsorbent. The regular structure adsorbent module is an adsorbent material with a hydraulic equivalent diameter of larger than 10 mm, a length of larger than 30 mm and an inner diffusion distance of smaller than 0.3 mm. The regular structure adsorbent module is formed by assembling adsorbents, where the adsorbent modules are sequentially overlapped according to the airflow direction of the raw materials, and the adsorbent modules are in sequence of 15-40 vol.% dewatering adsorbent, 5-30 wt.% carbon dioxide adsorbent and 30-80 wt.% methane adsorbent.
   USE - The adsorbent module is useful for adsorption and separation of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent module has high adsorption and diffusion rate and high utilization rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The adsorbent module is used for adsorption separation of methane, and the purification of low-concentration methane gas is realized as high-concentration or high-purity methane gas based on the principle of equilibrium adsorption separation. The low-concentration methane gas is a mixed gas containing less than 90% methane. The impurities mainly contain a large amount of a mixed gas source of nitrogen and a small amount of carbon dioxide and water vapor. The low-concentration of methane gas has a methane molar concentration of 10-75%, preferably 15-50%. The shape of the regular structure adsorbent is a honeycomb, a sandwich, a fiber fabric or a foam structure. The dewatering adsorbent is one or a combination of activated alumina, silica gel or metal-organic framework materials. The carbon dioxide adsorbent is a metal-organic framework material having a carbon dioxide-methane separation factor of greater than 8. The methane adsorbent is a metal-organic framework material having a methane-nitrogen separation factor of greater than 5. Preferred Properties: The adsorbent material in the regular structure adsorbent module has a hydraulic equivalent diameter of larger than 30 mm, a length of larger than 30 mm and an inner diffusion distance of smaller than 0.15 mm. The adsorbent module has a porosity of less than 60%, preferably less than 50%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-organic framework material is a combination of one or more metal ions of copper(II), aluminum(III), magnesium(II), manganese(II), iron(III), nickel(II), cobalt(II), zinc(II), and a compound with supramolecular porous network structure formed by self-assembling with organic ligand through coordination complexing action. The organic ligand is chosen from one or more of succinic acid, fumaric acid, 1,2,3-benzenetricarboxylic acid, 1,2,4-benzenetricarboxylic acid, 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid, 1,4-phthalic acid, 2,5-dihydroxy-1,4-phthalic acid, 1,3-phthalic acid, 2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylic acid, isonicotinic acid, 3-picolinic acid, 3,4-pyridinedicarboxylic acid, 2,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid, disodium 2,6-naphthalenedisulfonate, 3-pyridinesulfonic acid, 4,5-dihydroxy-1,3-benzenedisulfonic acid, imidazole, 2-methylimidazole, 4-methylimidazole, 2-nitroimidazole, benzimidazole, 4,4'-bipyridyl, ethylenediamine, and triethylenediamine. Preferred Method: The adsorption separation process for the methane includes pressure swing adsorption, vacuum swing adsorption, vacuum pressure swing adsorption, fast cycle pressure swing adsorption, fast cycle vacuum swing adsorption, and fast cycle vacuum pressure swing adsorption.

PN CN109499599-A; CN109499599-B
TI Pyridyl nitrogen-enriched carbon nanotube catalyst useful for electrocatalytically reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, comprises carbon nanotubes and a nitrogen atom doped in the carbon nanotubes.
AU MA C
   KANG P
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
   TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 201928745Y
AB    NOVELTY - Pyridyl nitrogen-enriched carbon nanotube catalyst comprises carbon nanotubes and a nitrogen atom doped in the carbon nanotubes. The nitrogen atom is doped in an amount of 1-2.5 wt.%, and the nitrogen atom contains pyridinium nitrogen, and the pyridinium nitrogen accounts for 55-80 wt.% of the total of the nitrogen atoms.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electrocatalytically reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high pyridinium nitrogen content, high current density and high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing pyridyl nitrogen-enriched carbon nanotube catalyst, comprising dispersing carbon nanotubes into a solvent, adding a nitrogen-containing ligand, mixing uniformly, and evaporating the solvent to obtain a precursor, and grinding the precursor into a powder, and subjecting the powdery precursor to heat treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen atom further comprises pyrrole nitrogen and graphite nitrogen. The carbon nanotube is single-walled carbon nanotubes, multi-walled carbon nanotubes, and carboxylated carbon nanotubes. The solvent is a mixture of water and ethanol, where the volume ratio of water to ethanol is 3:1-1:3. The atmosphere used for the heat treatment is ammonia gas, argon gas, and nitrogen gas. The nitrogen-containing ligand is 1,10-phenanthroline, 2,2-bipyridine and 4,4-bipyridine, preferably, the mass ratio of the carbon nanotubes to the nitrogen-containing ligand is 1:1-1:5. Preferred Method: The heat treatment is carried out at room temperature for 3-5 degrees C/minute, at 300-700 degrees C for 2-4 hours and at a rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute to room temperature.

PT P
PN WO2019053541-A1
TI Producing alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid salt by reacting alkene and carbon dioxide with composition comprising carboxylation catalyst and inorganic base in protic solvent.
AU BEETSTRA D
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201926121K
AB    NOVELTY - Method of producing an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid salt involves reacting an alkene and carbon dioxide with a composition comprising a carboxylation catalyst and an inorganic base in a protic solvent under reaction conditions suitable to produce an inorganic base salt of the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides an elegant and cost-effective process for making alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid salts (e.g. acrylates), is performed in a one pot manner, avoids the need to isolate and regenerate the inorganic base and solves the problems to make a, beta -unsaturated carboxylic alkali metal or alkaline-earth metal salts which use complex steps, increasingly expensive starting materials and reagents that need regeneration or its combination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The inorganic base is at least partially soluble in the protic solvent. Preferred Components: The inorganic base comprises a carbonate or an alkaline metal carbonate and an alkaline earth metal carbonate or both. The alkaline metal carbonate is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, cesium carbonate, lithium carbonate or their mixture.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The alkene, carbon dioxide and the metal from the carboxylation catalyst form a metallolactone. The inorganic base salt of the protic solvent is formed in situ from the inorganic base, and reacts with the metallolactone to form the inorganic base salt of the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, the protic solvent and the carboxylation catalyst. The method further comprises (a) separating the inorganic base salt of an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid from the protic solvent and the carboxylation catalyst and (b) optionally providing an additional alkene, carbon dioxide and/or inorganic base to the protic solvent to produce an additional alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid salt. The reaction condition involves (a) maintaining the composition at a temperature of 120-200 degrees C, preferably 120-160 degrees C, (b) an alkene pressure of 0.1-5 MPa, preferably 1 MPa and/or (c) a carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-5 MPa, preferably 0.1-0.5 MPa. Preferred Components: The protic solvent is a 4-35C alcohol. The alcohol comprises butanol, pentanol, hexanol, 2-methyl-2-butanol, 2-pentanol or their mixture. The carboxylation catalyst comprises at least one transition metal of columns 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 or 10 of the periodic table. The carboxylation catalyst comprises at least one transition metal, preferably nickel and/or palladium. The carboxylation catalyst comprises at least one coordinating ligand, preferably a coordinating ligand comprising at least two coordinating atoms chosen from nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur and carbene that coordinate with the transition metal. The carboxylation catalyst is ((cyclohexyl)2PCH2CH2P(cyclohexyl)2)Ni(C2H4CO2) or ((cyclohexyl)2PCH2CH2CH2P(cyclohexyl)2)Ni(C2H4). The alkene is ethylene and the protic solvent is 1-butanol. The produced inorganic base salt of an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid is an alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal acrylate, preferably sodium or lithium acrylate. Preferred Property: The alcohol has a melting point of up to 120 degrees C. The composition does not contain aprotic solvent.
   EXAMPLE - In a steel autoclave equipped with a stirring bar, 18.9 mg tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(0), 9.3 mg ethylenebis(dicyclohexylphosphine) and 2.6723 g potassium carbonate were suspended in 10.0293 g 2-pentanol. The reactor was closed and charged first with 1 MPa (10 bar) of ethylene and subsequently pressurized up to 3 MPa (30 bar) with carbon dioxide. The reactor was then placed in an oil bath maintained at 120 degrees C. After stirring for 18 hours, the heating was turned off and the reactor was allowed to cool to room temperature, after which the reactor was carefully vented. 4 ml Deuterized water and 98.0 mg lithium acetate dihydrate were added to the contents of the autoclave. The contents of the reactor were vigorously stirred for 10 minutes to obtain potassium acrylate.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Preparation of aroma essential oil of Wisteria involves pulverizing dried flowers, extracting mixed powder, heating, depressurizing, separating mixture from oil layer and aqueous layer, distilling and obtaining white emulsion.
AU DING J
AE QINGDAO ZITENG ZHUANGYUAN ECOLOGICAL AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2019102660
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aroma essential oil of Wisteria involves removing 100-150 g dried Wisteria flowers, pulverizing, passing through 60 mesh sieve, obtaining mixed powder, placing obtained mixed powder into extraction tank of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, adding an ethanol immersion mixture with 60% entrainer, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide as extraction solvent at 30-50 degrees C under pressure of 15-30 MPa for 4-6 hours, heating obtained mixture, depressurizing, forming an oil-water mixture, cooling, separating the mixture from oil layer and aqueous layer, placing obtained mixture in a steam distillation unit, distilling by heating, carrying out steam distillation and obtaining white emulsion.
   USE - Preparation of aroma essential oil of Wisteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and economically provides aroma essential oil of Wisteria by simple method with high extraction rate.

PN KR2018100899-A
TI Production of acetic acid involves mixing syngas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide with hydrogen-carbon monoxide mixture, to separate carbon monoxide and produce methanol, generating acetic acid using carbon monoxide and methanol.
AU LEE J H
   LIM H M
   ROH K S
   IMRAN H
   SHAKER A H A
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2018730831
AB    NOVELTY - Production of acetic acid involves generating syngas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide using gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon, mixing the syngas with a mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon monoxide, separating into single gas of carbon monoxide, further mixing the syngas with mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon monoxide to produce methanol and generating acetic acid using the separated carbon monoxide and methanol.
   USE - Production of acetic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of acetic acid within short time and with reduced generation of byproducts. Since the usage amount of carbon dioxide is increased, global warming can be prevented efficiently.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for acetic acid-production system, which comprises a reforming unit for generating the syngas, a separation unit and reaction units.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The separation unit further comprises a removal unit for removing carbon dioxide and water from the syngas, a heat exchanger for vaporizing the carbon monoxide, a cooling unit for compressing and cooling the syngas, a condenser for condensing and separating the carbon monoxide in the cooled syngas, a discharge unit for expanding the carbon monoxide separated from the condenser, and a recovery unit for recovering unreacted gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The syngas is generated by a dry reforming reaction at 1-6 bar and 800-1000 degrees C. Unreacted carbon dioxide and byproduct water is removed from the syngas, compressed and then cooled. Some of the carbon monoxide in the cooled syngas condensed and separated, to produce acetic acid. The resultant condensed and separated is vaporized using a heat exchanger, to obtain gaseous carbon monoxide, and uncondensed residual gas is then recovered. Methanol is produced at 40-60 bar and 200-300 degrees C. Acetic acid is obtained by generating electricity through unreacted gas using a heat-exchanger turbine.

PN CN108069451-A; CN108069451-B
TI Preparation of amorphous calcium carbonate in            alcohol-water mixed system involves mixing alcohol and            magnesium salt solution into e.g. soluble amino acid            calcium salt, introducing e.g. carbon dioxide into            mixed solution and post treating.
AU YIN Y
   KONG X
   SUN R
   REN Y
   TU S
   YE L
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   BEIJING ZIGUANG CHEM ENG TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   BEIJING INSIGHT CHEM ENG TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201842327K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of amorphous calcium carbonate in                alcohol-water mixed system involves mixing alcohol                and magnesium salt solution into soluble amino acid                calcium salt or soluble calcium salt-ammonia                aqueous solution, uniformly stirring, directly                introducing carbon dioxide or purified flue gas                into mixed solution and carrying out post-treatment                after the reaction reaches the end point.
   USE - Preparation of amorphous calcium carbonate in                alcohol-water mixed system.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation of amorphous calcium carbonate                in alcohol-water mixed system is suitable for large                scale production, simple and economical. The                preparation solves problem of industrial synthesis                and stable storage of amorphous calcium carbonate,                has excellent use of industrial waste such as                calcium carbide slag, calcium chloride and flue                gas. The prepared amorphous calcium carbonate is                environmentally-friendly and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The reaction pH value                  is reduced to 7.5-9 which is the end point of the                  reaction. Preferred Process: The preparation                  involves post treatment comprising suction                  filtration, washing and drying. The filtrate                  after separating the amorphous calcium carbonate                  is directly applied or treated. The filtrate                  separated from the amorphous calcium carbonate is                  processed by using calcium glycinate solution or                  mixture of calcium chloride and aqueous ammonia                  as raw material, directly mixing the filtrate                  with calcium carbide slag, quicklime or slaked                  lime for dissolving, extracting calcium,                  alternately adjusting the alcohol-water ratio in                  order, distilling ethanol and part of solvent                  water to recover calcium carbide slag, quicklime                  or slaked lime. Preferred Component: The soluble                  amino acid calcium salt is calcium glycinate or                  calcium alanine. The soluble calcium salt is                  calcium chloride, calcium sulfate, calcium                  nitrate and/or other soluble calcium salts,                  preferably calcium chloride. The magnesium salt                  solution may be any solution that can provide                  magnesium ions such as magnesium chloride                  solution, magnesium nitrate solution and                  magnesium sulfate solution. Preferred                  Composition: The ratio of calcium ions to                  magnesium ions in the mixed solution is                  preferably 10:1-10. The introduced carbon dioxide                  is pure carbon dioxide gas, boiler-purged exhaust                  gas or other carbon-containing mixed gas carbon                  source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The alcohol is 1-10C                  alcohol, preferably methanol, ethanol, propanol,                  butanol or pentanol. Preferred Composition: The                  volume ratio of calcium solution to alcohol is                  preferably 1:0.5-10.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E11-W; E12-A02; E34-D03A

PN CN108057259-A; CN108057259-B
TI Extraction of active component from ants involves selecting ants and then washing using clean water, processing washed ants and then drying, extracting using carbon dioxide and leaving, processing and then extracting and separating.
AU LU Z
   HUANG Y
AE GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018418289
AB    NOVELTY - The extraction method of active component from ants involves selecting ants and then washing using clean water, processing the washed ants at 40-50 degrees C for 5-10 minutes and then drying using drying device at 20-25 degrees C for 1-2 hours, extracting using carbon dioxide at 25-30 degrees C for 5-10 minutes under pressure of 10-12 MPa and then leaving for 20-30 minutes, processing at 45-55 degrees C for 60-80 minutes under pressure of 35-40 MPa and then extracting at 50-55 degrees C under pressure of 38-40 MPa and separating using separator.
   USE - The method is useful for extraction of active component from ants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extraction of active component with high yield.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN107987311-A
TI Preparation of hydrophobic melamine sponge-reinforced composite polysilsesquioxane aerogel comprises preparing sol, soaking melamine sponge in sol, gelling, and supercritical carbon dioxide drying or vacuum freeze-drying.
AU YUN S
   GUO T
   ZHANG J
   LI Y
   LI H
   ZHU X
AE HUAIYIN TECHNOLOGY INST (HUIA-C)
GA 201839388E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hydrophobic melamine sponge-reinforced composite polysilsesquioxane aerogel comprises mixing alkyl-containing trialkoxysilane and distilled water, stirring to obtain a clear solution, where concentration of alkyl-containing trialkoxysilane is 100-700 g/L, and adding concentrated aqueous ammonia solution to adjust pH value of 8.5-11.5 to obtain a sol; soaking melamine sponge in sol and gelling at 0-90 degrees C for 0.1-20 hours to obtain melamine sponge-reinforced composite polysilsesquioxane wet gel; and supercritical carbon dioxide drying or vacuum freeze-drying at normal pressure.
   USE - Method for preparing hydrophobic melamine sponge-reinforced composite polysilsesquioxane aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite aerogel solves poor mechanical properties, poor brittleness, low elastic modulus and fragility and difficult to use as a load-bearing material problems.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkyl-containing trialkoxysilane is methyl trimethoxysilane, methyl triethoxysilane, ethyl trimethoxysilane, ethyl triethoxysilane, propyltrimethoxysilane, propyltriethoxysilane, vinyltris-methoxysilane, vinyltriethoxysilane, octyl trimethoxysilane, octyltriethoxysilane, hexadecyltrimethoxysilane, hexadecyltriethoxysilane, dodecyltrimethylammoniumsilane, dodecyltriethoxysilane, octadecyltrimethoxysilane, and/or octadecyltriethoxysilane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The gelling process is done at 40-70 degrees C for 10-20 hours. Preferred Components: The composite aerogel has 62.5-97.5% porosity, 1-1000 gm pore size distribution, 50-1000 m2/g Brunauer, Emmett and Teller specific surface area, 0.05-0.75 g/cm3, preferably 0.5-0.20 g/cm3 apparent density, 1-150 MPa compressive modulus, 0.1-5 MPa flexural modulus, 0.01-0.12 W/m.K, preferably 0.01-0.04 W/m.K thermal conductivity.

GA 2021722121
AB    NOVELTY - Use of electrode with residual ion in carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction, where the electrode comprises an electronic conductor and an active material, and an electrolyte attached to electrode. The electrode is provided with residual electrons or residual holes at the same time and the cation and anion introduced by the electrolyte and the remaining electrons or holes are present on the electronic conductor and/or the active material. The anion and the cation are present in the electrolyte. The absolute value of the charge number of the anion in the electrolyte and the cation in the electrolyte is not equal. The charge number of the cation attached on the electrode is greater than the total charge number of the anion. The ion net charge is positive charge. The charge number of the cation on the electrode is less than the total charge number of the anion. The ion net charge is negative charge and the electrode is applied to carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction.
   USE - The electrode with residual ion is useful in carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode with residual ion in carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction is simple and efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation method of the electrode; and
   (2) carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction by the electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The electrode is both an electronic conductor and an active material, and the electrode is an alloy composed of transition metals e.g. iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, and/or zinc. The types of anions and cations can be inorganic cations, inorganic anions, organic cations, organic anions, complex ions and/or complex ions. The inorganic cations includes hydrogen ion, organic amine ion, ammonia ion, potassium ion, sodium ion, calcium ion, magnesium ion, aluminum ion, zinc ion, iron ion, tin ion, lead ion, bismuth ion, mercury ion, silver ion, gold ion, platinum ion, palladium ion, iridium ion, indium ion, titanium ion, vanadium ion, chromium ion, manganese ion, cobalt ion, nickel ions, copper ions, niobium ions, zirconium ions and hydrated ions of the above ions, and organic complex ions of the above ions, and/or inorganic complex ions containing the above-mentioned metal ions. The inorganic anions includes fluoride ion, chloride ion, bromide ion, iodide ion, sulfide ion, silicon ion, nitrogen ion, phosphorus ion, arsenic ion, carbonate ion, nitrate ion, and the hydrated ions of the above-mentioned ions, and the organic complex ions of the above-mentioned ions, and oxyacid ions containing the above-mentioned ionic elements, and/or metal complex ions containing the above-mentioned ionic elements. The organic cations includes primary amine ion, secondary amine ion, tertiary amine ion, quaternary amine ion, organophosphorus ion, imidazole cation, pyridine cation, sulfonimide ion, sulfonamide ion, and/or above-mentioned derivative ions. The organic anions includes organic sulfonic acid ion, organic phosphate ion, organic borate ion, oxalate ion, carbonate ion, acetate ion, and/or derivative ions of the above-mentioned ions. The electrolyte is alkali metal bicarbonate and/or carbonate e.g. sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate. The preparation method of the electrode utilizes acetone, absolute ethanol, acidic aqueous solution, and electrolyte solution. The carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction by the electrode utilizes potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution.

UT DIIDW:201900445S
ER

PT P
PN US2018283779-A1; US10246660-B2
TI Apparatus for purifying natural gas from ground by separating carbon dioxide, has gas-liquid separator separating natural-gas liquid in gas, and carbon-dioxide separator maintaining remaining part of gas liquid in gas in gaseous form.
AU EDA M
   MATSUBARA W
   HONJO S
   OKINO S
   NAKATANI H
   YUSHIMA M
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND CO LTD (MITO-C)
GA 201876727P
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a pressure adjuster for adjusting pressure of natural gas from a ground. A heater heats the natural gas after a separation of a part of natural-gas liquid by a natural-gas-liquid separator. AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises a gasification furnace ring cavity, a low-temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide tail gas pipeline (7), a gasification furnace burner (6) provided with ignition rod, oxygen inner channel, fuel channel, coal slurry channel, oxygen outer channel and cooling water sequentially from inside to outside, a condensate separation tank (1), a compressor (2) and a pressure-stabilizing tank (3). The oxygen pipeline is set with a flow meter (71), a cut-off valve (72) and a regulating valve (73) in sequence. The nitrogen pipeline (17) is set with a flow meter (61), a cut-off valve (62) and a regulating valve (63) in sequence. The gasification furnace ring cavity is connected with a nitrogen pipeline (9) provided with a flow meter (91), a cut-off valve (92) and a regulating valve (93) in sequence.
   USE - Device is used for recycling carbon dioxide tail gas in low-temperature methanol washing section of coal chemical industry (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device effectively reduces the carbon dioxide emission, effectively protects the environment, at the same time, reduces the water-coal-slurry gasification furnace for oxygen and nitrogen consumption, and reduces the production cost. The low temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide tail gas replaces the nitrogen into the gasifier ring cavity to protect the water coal slurry gasifier, reducing the load of the subsequent section tail gas treatment. The carbon dioxide, hydrogen in the tail gas, and carbon monoxide are fully recycled. The method has high utilization rate, simple process, and is environment-friendly.

The nitrogen pipeline (17) is set with a flow meter (61), a cut-off valve (62) and a regulating valve (63) in sequence. The gasification furnace ring cavity is connected with a nitrogen pipeline (9) provided with a flow meter (91), a cut-off valve (92) and a regulating valve (93) in sequence. The low-temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide tail gas pipeline is connected with a torch. The outlet of low-temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide tail gas pipeline is connected to the compressor inlet through a pipeline, and the compressor outlet is connected to the pressure-stabilizing tank inlet through a pipeline. The pressure-stabilizing tank outlet is divided into two channels (C1 and C2) through pipelines. The channel (C1) is connected to the inside of the gasification furnace through the gasification furnace burner, and the other channel (C2) is connected to the gasification furnace ring cavity.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for recycling carbon dioxide tail gas in low-temperature methanol washing section of coal chemical industry, which involves subjecting low-temperature methanol washing carbon dioxide tail gas comprising 75-99% carbon dioxide, and 0.1-3% hydrogen and carbon monoxide to condensate separation, pressurizing, and passing a portion of condensate into the coal-water slurry gasification furnace through gasification furnace burner, and remaining portion into gasification furnace ring cavity to protect the coal-water slurry gasification furnace.

   HAN D

   YUE Y
   YAO T
   ZHAO J
AE ZHANGJIAGANG IND TECHNOLOGY INST CO LTD (CACP-C)
GA 202020925D
AB    NOVELTY - Low-pressure hydrogen production system comprises e.g. first splitter, first-stage first split channel, first-stage second split channel, reformer, second splitter, second-stage split channel, second secondary split channel, membrane separation and purification device, pure hydrogen output channel, and carbon dioxide mixed residual gas output channel. An outlet of the first shunt is connected to a first-stage first shunt channel and a first-stage second shunt channel. A delivery pump is arranged on the inlet pipe of the first diverter. A first heat exchanger is provided on the first-stage first splitter channel. A second heat exchanger is provided on the first-stage second splitter channel, and the working temperature of the first heat exchanger and the second heat exchanger is 20-300 degrees C for converting methanol water in the first-stage first split channel and the first-stage second split channel into methanol water vapor.
   USE - The device is useful for a methanol water hydrogenation station (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has hydrogen yield of greater than or equal to 95%, and realizes the gas circulation and purification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low-pressure hydrogen production system comprises first splitter, first-stage first split channel, first-stage second split channel, reformer, second splitter, and second-stage split channel, second secondary split channel, membrane separation and purification device, pure hydrogen output channel, and carbon dioxide mixed residual gas output channel. An outlet of the first shunt is connected to a first-stage first shunt channel and a first-stage second shunt channel. A delivery pump is arranged on the inlet pipe of the first diverter. A first heat exchanger is provided on the first-stage first splitter channel. A second heat exchanger is provided on the first-stage second splitter channel, and the working temperature of the first heat exchanger and the second heat exchanger is 20-300 degrees C for converting methanol water in the first-stage first split channel and the first-stage second split channel into methanol water vapor. The outlets of the first and second split channels of the first and second split channels are connected to a reformer and are adapted to send the methanol water vapor in the first and second split channels into the reformer and methanol water vapor is used to produce a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the reformer. The gas phase composition of the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide are 65-75% hydrogen, 20-26% carbon dioxide, 0.3-3% carbon monoxide. An outlet of the reformer is connected to a second diverter. The outlet of the second diverter and the inlet of the membrane separation and purification device are communicated through a secondary first diverter channel and a secondary second diverter channel. The membrane separation and purification device is connected to a pure hydrogen output channel and a carbon dioxide mixed residual gas output channel, and the membrane separation and purification device sends the hydrogen. The mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide separates the pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas. The working temperature of the membrane separation and purification device is 380-420 degrees C. The temperature of separating pure hydrogen is 380-420 degrees C, and the temperature of the separated carbon dioxide mixed gas is 380-420 degrees C. The gas phase components of the carbon dioxide mixed residual gas are 25-45% hydrogen, 55-75% carbon dioxide, 0.3-3% carbon monoxide, and 0-3% water. The separated pure hydrogen is transported to the hydrogen storage tank of the hydrogenation station by the pure hydrogen output channel. The pure hydrogen output channel passes through a fourth heat exchanger and a second heat exchanger in sequence. The fourth heat exchanger uses the obtained heat energy to preheat the hydrogen in the second-stage second splitter channel. The mixed gas of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and the second heat exchanger uses the heat energy exchanged to make methanol water in the first-stage second bypass channel into methanol water vapor. The separated carbon dioxide mixed gas is transported by the carbon dioxide mixed gas output channel. The carbon dioxide mixed residual gas output channel passes through the third heat exchanger and the first heat exchanger in sequence. The third heat exchanger uses the exchanged thermal energy to preheat the mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the second stage first bypass channel. The first heat exchanger uses the obtained heat energy to convert methanol water in the first-stage first bypass channel into methanol water vapor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for working of a low-pressure hydrogen production system for a methanol water hydrogenation station.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the low-pressure hydrogen production system for a methanol water hydrogenation station.

PT P
PN CN110668433-A
TI Preparing graphite/graphene composite material by            supercritical carbon dioxide fluid stripping used in            negative electrode material in lithium ion battery, by            mixing potassium permanganate with concentrated nitric            acid and heating.
AU BAI Y
   LI G
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 202006198H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing graphite/graphene                composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid stripping, by mixing and stirring potassium                permanganate and concentrated nitric acid and                heating, adding natural large scale graphite,                stirring, adding citric acid, reacting, adding                hydrogen peroxide to react to remove excess                potassium permanganate, washing, performing suction                filtration, drying in vacuum oven to obtain                expandable graphite and adding the expandable                graphite to the high-pressure reactor, cooling the                carbon dioxide to liquid state using refrigerator,                pumping the carbon dioxide into the reactor using                pressure pump, when the temperature and pressure in                the high-pressure reactor reach the preset value,                ultrasonically treating, intercalating the                supercritical carbon dioxide molecules into the                expandable graphite for a period of time, adopting                the rapid pressure relief operation to reduce the                pressure in the reactor to the atmospheric pressure                to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                graphite/graphene composite material used in                negative electrode material for lithium ion                battery(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to process, mild,                environmentally-friendly, less damaged and                economical, achieves high-yield, high-quality                graphite and graphene composite material and has                wide application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing graphite/graphene                composite material by supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid stripping, involves (i) continuously mixing                and stirring required amount of potassium                permanganate and concentrated nitric acid at 50 rpm                and heating in a water bath, when the temperature                rises to a certain condition, adding natural large                scale graphite, continuously stirring for a period                of time, adding citric acid, reacting, adding                certain amount of hydrogen peroxide to react to                remove excess potassium permanganate, and washing                until neutral, performing suction filtration,                drying in vacuum oven for 12 hours to obtain                expandable graphite and (ii) adding the expandable                graphite to the high-pressure reactor, cooling the                carbon dioxide to liquid state using refrigerator,                pumping the carbon dioxide into the reactor using                pressure pump, when the temperature and pressure in                the high-pressure reactor reach the preset value,                ultrasonically treating, intercalating the                supercritical carbon dioxide molecules into the                expandable graphite for a period of time, adopting                the rapid pressure relief operation to reduce the                pressure in the reactor to the atmospheric pressure                to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  graphite, potassium permanganate, concentrated                  nitric acid, citric acid in step (i) is                  1:(0.04-0.28):(7-15):(0.2-1.2). Preferred                  Conditions: The temperature in step (i) is 30-50                  degrees C, and the reaction time is 20-120                  minutes. The temperature in step (ii) is 35-45                  degrees C, pressure is 8-20 MPa, ultrasonic power                  is 0-2500 W, stirring speed is 500-1000 rpm, and                  the time is 6-24 hours.

PT P
PN CN107952415-A; CN107952415-B
TI Preparing chitosan-based carbon dioxide adsorbing aerogel involves adding chitosan powder to deionized water and stirring to form suspension, adding acetic acid, and stirring, and adding glycidyltrimethylammonium chloride.
AU SONG J
   CHEN Y
   CHEN X
   YAN Y
AE XIAN HUATAI BOYUAN QUALITY INSPECTION CO (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018330791
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chitosan-based carbon dioxide adsorbing aerogel involves adding chitosan powder to deionized water and stirring to form suspension, adding acetic acid and stirring for 30 minutes until the chitosan is completely dissolved, adding glycidyltrimethylammonium chloride gradually with stirring at 100 rpm with molar ratio of glycidyltrimethylammonium chloride to chitosan of 6:1. The reaction is continued in oil bath at 55 degrees C for 18 hours to obtain quaternized chitosan solution.
   USE - Method for preparing chitosan-based carbon dioxide adsorbing aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare chitosan-based carbon dioxide adsorbing aerogel in cost-effective manner, has large specific surface area, which improves carbon dioxide adsorption performance, controls humidity adsorbent regeneration with regeneration energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing chitosan-based carbon dioxide adsorbing aerogel involves adding chitosan powder to deionized water and stirring to form suspension, adding acetic acid and stirring for 30 minutes until the chitosan is completely dissolved, adding glycidyltrimethylammonium chloride gradually with stirring at 100 rpm with molar ratio of glycidyltrimethylammonium chloride to chitosan of 6:1. The reaction is continued in oil bath at 55 degrees C for 18 hours to obtain quaternized chitosan solution. The above solution is centrifuged to remove unreacted chitosan solids, added methanol to removed unreacted glycidyltrimethylammonium chloride, and added acetone until the quaternized chitosan precipitated completely. The quaternized chitosan is dissolved in a saturated sodium carbonate solution in a ratio of 1 g:100 ml, stirred for 2-4 hours, acetone is added to precipitate the precipitate completely, filtered, and dried carbonated quaternary amination chitosan. The quaternized aminated chitosan is loaded with carbonate is dissolved in water, graphene oxide is added and sonicated for 20 minutes to obtain a solution. The mass ratio of the quaternary aminated chitosan and the graphene oxide loaded with carbonate is 4:1, and mechanical stirred for 2-4 hours. The solution is placed in a glass bottle and allowed to stand at room temperature for 2 days. The glass bottle containing the solution is placed in liquid nitrogen for 20 minutes, transferred to a freeze-dryer. The temperature of the freeze-dryer is -40-55 degrees C for 24 hours, freeze-dried to obtain chitosan-based carbon dioxide adsorption aerogel which adsorbed carbon dioxide under low humidity and desorbed carbon dioxide under high humidity for regeneration.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)
MC J01-D01; J01-E02B; J04-A03; A08-S02; A10-E09; A12-W11; A12-W11D; E05-U06; E11-Q02; E12-C02; E31-N05C
