PT P
PN CN112778153-A
TI New amido bridged hexacarboxylic acid ligand used in amide group modified metal organic framework material in e.g. catalyst for conversion of carbon dioxide and cycloaddition reaction of epoxy compound to synthesize cyclic carbonate.
AU WANG C
   WANG Y
   WANG G
   ZHENG B
   CAO X
   ZENG W
   LIU Q
AE UNIV HUNAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHC-C)
GA 202155070M
AB    NOVELTY - An amido bridged hexacarboxylic acid ligand (I), is new.
   USE - New amido bridged hexacarboxylic acid ligand used in amide group modified metal organic framework material in adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide, and catalyst for conversion of carbon dioxide and cycloaddition reaction of epoxy compound to synthesize cyclic carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The amido bridged hexacarboxylic acid ligand provides amide group modified metal organic framework material with stable properties, large specific surface area, easily controllable performance, strong ability to selectively adsorb carbon dioxide, high catalytic activity, excellent catalytic conversion effect and recyclable use. The method is economical, simple, carried out under mild condition and suitable for large-scale industrial production, and uses easily obtain raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An amido bridged hexacarboxylic acid ligand of formula (I), is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I), which involves (1) performing contact reaction of thionyl chloride, dimethylformamide and 3,5-bis(methoxycarbonyl)benzoic acid, obtaining contact reaction product (p1), (2) subjecting the contact reaction product (p1) and aniline-containing compound of formula (II) to contact reaction in the presence of tetrahydrofuran and catalyst (c1), obtaining contact reaction product (p2), (3) subjecting the contact reaction product (p2) to a hydrolysis reaction in the presence of catalyst (c2) and alkaline substances, acidifying, filtering, washing with water until it is neutral, and washing with hot methanol for 2-3 times, and drying. The molar ratio of the 3,5-bis(methoxycarbonyl)benzoic acid to the compound (II) is 1:0.6-0.8;
   (2) amide group modified metal organic framework material, which comprises copper-containing compound of formula: (Cu3(C31H13NO13))n, where n is a positive integer. The amide-modified metal-organic framework material is cubic crystal system belongs to space group of P422, and unit cell parameter alpha of 90.00 degrees , beta of 90.00 degrees and gamma of 90.00 degrees ;
   (3) preparation of amide group-modified metal organic framework material, which involves performing a coordination reaction between the compound (I) and hydrated copper nitrate in the presence of a solvent at 80-100 degrees C for 70-170 hours. The solvent is chosen from one or more of dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, water, and acetonitrile, preferably dimethylformamide. The molar ratio of the compound (I) to the hydrated copper nitrate is 1:9-12;
   (4) use of the amide modified metal organic framework material in catalyst. The catalyst is a catalyst that catalyzes the conversion of carbon dioxide and the cycloaddition reaction of epoxy compound to synthesize cyclic carbonate. The epoxy compound is chosen from one or more of epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, propylene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether, 1,2-butylene oxide, styrene oxide, and cyclohexene oxide; and
   (5) method for activating the amide group-modified metal organic framework material, which involves subjecting the amide group modified metal organic framework material to soaking treatment and degassing treatment to obtain the activated amide group modified metal organic framework material. The soaking treatment is carried out in an anhydrous acetone solution for 70-90 hours. In the soaking treatment, the amount of the anhydrous acetone is 0.1-0.5 mL per 1 mg of the amide group-modified metal organic framework material. The degassing treatment is performed at 80-130 degrees C for 5-20 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: In step (1), the contact reaction is performed at 70-90 degrees C for 8-15 hours. In step (2), the contact reaction is performed at 15-30 degrees C for 8-15 hours. In step (3), the hydrolysis is performed at 15-30 degrees C for 36-96 hours. Preferred Component: The catalyst (c1) and catalyst (c2) are dimethylaminopyridine and/or tetrabutylammonium bromide.

PT P
PN CN109482139-A
TI Aluminum pillared montmorillonite loaded potassium-niobium composite adsorbent useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide, is prepared by compositely supporting potassium and niobium elements on aluminum pillared montmorillonite carrier.
AU YE Q
   WU K
   CHENG S
   KANG T
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2019272849
AB    NOVELTY - An aluminum pillared montmorillonite loaded potassium-niobium composite adsorbent is prepared by compositely supporting potassium and niobium elements on an aluminum pillared montmorillonite support, where the aluminum pillared montmorillonite is a material used for inserting a hydroxyaluminum solution into a montmorillonite layer.
   USE - The aluminum pillared montmorillonite loaded potassium-niobium composite adsorbent is useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminum pillared montmorillonite loaded potassium-niobium composite adsorbent has high specific surface area and good thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the aluminum pillared montmorillonite loaded potassium-niobium composite adsorbent, which involves (1) preparing the aluminum pillared montmorillonite carrier by stirring natural montmorillonite with 1.2-1.8 mol/l sulfuric acid solution in a water bath at 60-70 degrees C for 4-6 hours, where the volume ratio of montmorillonite to sulfuric acid solution is 1 g:10 ml, filtering the acidified montmorillonite and washing until no sulfate ion exists, testing the sample with barium chloride solution, drying the filtered sample in a dry box at 120 degrees C for 6 hours, grinding the dried sample through a 80-mesh sieve to obtain an acid modified montmorillonite, configuring 0.1-0.3 mol/l aluminum chloride aqueous solution and 0.1-0.3 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, dripping into the above solution at a flow rate of 1 ml/minute, where the molar ratio of (OH-)/(Al3+) is 2-3, maintaining the temperature at 50-70 degrees C, then stirring for 3-6 hours, aging the resulting suspension at room temperature for 12-24 hours to obtain an aluminum column solution, adding the acidified modified montmorillonite to deionized water in a certain proportion to prepare an acidified montmorillonite suspension slurry, where the mass ratio of the acidified montmorillonite and deionized water is 1:(40-80), dripping the above obtained aluminum column solution into the acidified montmorillonite suspension slurry, where the volume ratio of (Al3+) and the acidified montmorillonite is (4-10) mmol:1 g, stirring at 60-70 degrees C for 10-16 hours, aging at room temperature for 12-24 hours, drying, grinding and sieving at 100-120 degrees C, then putting the sieved mixture into a muffle furnace, heating the furnace at a heating rate of 1-2 degrees C/minute to 300-450 degrees C and calcining for 1-4 hours, and (2) preparing composite adsorbent by adding the aluminum pillared montmorillonite to a mixed solution of 0.019-0.021 mol/l potassium nitrate and 0.019-0.021 mol/l niobium oxalate, stirring at 60-75 degrees C for 1-4 hours, where the loading amount of potassium is 1-15 wt.% and the loading amount of niobium is 0.1-1 wt.%, carrying out rotary evaporation to dryness, drying at 100-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours, and roasting at 400 degrees C for 1-4 hours; and
   (2) use of the composite adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide, which involves placing the adsorbent in a thermogravimetric instrument, introducing a mixture of 5-15 vol.% carbon dioxide and 85-95 vol.% nitrogen, and adsorbing the carbon dioxide by virtue of a mass change detection adsorbent, where the reaction temperature is 50-200 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties The potassium and niobium elements supported on the aluminum pillared montmorillonite have a specific surface area of 200-300 m2/g.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN109437266-A
TI Extracting active aluminum hydroxide product for            producing polymeric aluminum, involves mixing aluminum            waste residue and sodium carbonate solution,            introducing carbon dioxide exhaust gas, performing            solid-liquid separation, washing.
AU FU K
   ZHENG B
   LI Y
   YUAN D
AE UNIV JIAOZUO (UNJZ-C)
GA 2019249166
AB    NOVELTY - The method for extracting active aluminum                hydroxide product, involves performing alkaline                treatment of waste residue, mixing aluminum waste                residue with sodium carbonate alkali solution,                drying aluminum residue mixture, heating,                calcining, purifying the carbon dioxide exhaust gas                and recovering, crushing the calcined solid                mixture, naturally cooling, obtaining alkaline                powdery mixture, washing with water, adding                aluminum residue mixture to deionized water to                obtain a suspension of sodium aluminate, performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain sodium aluminate                solution, precipitating the active aluminum                hydroxide, introducing purified carbon dioxide                exhaust gas into sodium aluminate solution,                performing solid-liquid separation to obtain solid                active aluminum hydroxide semi-finished product and                sodium carbonate alkali solution, washing to obtain                finished solid active aluminum hydroxide, returning                washed wastewater to step (S2), concentrating                sodium carbonate alkali solution.
   USE - Method for extracting active aluminum                hydroxide product for producing polymeric                aluminum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables extraction of active                aluminum hydroxide product with excellent flame                retardance, and realizes effective utilization of                waste ash such as aluminum ash.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting active aluminum                hydroxide product using aluminum ash or                aluminum-rich waste residue, involves (S1)                performing alkaline treatment of waste residue,                mixing aluminum waste residue with sodium carbonate                alkali solution with a concentration of 50-90%                under normal temperature and normal pressure state,                uniformly stirring, mixing for not less than 10                minutes and obtaining aluminum residue mixture,                drying aluminum residue mixture at a constant                temperature of 90-150 degrees C to a water content                of not more than 3%, heating at temperature of                700-1200 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, calcining in a                constant temperature environment of 700-1200                degrees C for 20-120 minutes, purifying the carbon                dioxide exhaust gas generated during the                calcination process and recovering, crushing the                calcined solid mixture by a pulverizing device,                naturally cooling to not more than 200 degrees C,                obtaining alkaline powdery mixture with particle                diameter of not more than 100 mesh, (S2) washing                with water, adding aluminum residue mixture to                dissolution tank together with deionized water in a                ratio of 1:2-6 to obtain a suspension of sodium                aluminate, passing the suspension through solid                solution, performing solid-liquid separation using                separation device, collecting separated liquid                material to obtain sodium aluminate solution, using                separated solid material as a raw material for                preparing a concrete quick-setting agent for                centralized storage, (S3) precipitating the active                aluminum hydroxide, introducing purified carbon                dioxide exhaust gas into sodium aluminate solution,                reacting to obtain solid active aluminum hydroxide,                adding sodium carbonate alkali solution until the                pH of the mixture reaches 5-9, performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain solid active                aluminum hydroxide semi-finished product and sodium                carbonate alkali solution, (S4) washing the solid                active aluminum hydroxide semi-finished product                with deionized water to obtain finished solid                active aluminum hydroxide, returning washed                wastewater to step (S2), directly adding to the                dissolution tank and mixing with the alkaline                residue mixture, heating the sodium carbonate                alkali solution obtained in (S3), concentrating                sodium carbonate alkali solution to 50-90%,                returning sodium alkali solution to step (S1) and                directly participating in the aluminum residue                mixing operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aluminum residue                  mixture is selected from aluminum ash with                  particle diameter of not more than 80 mesh and/or                  aluminum-enriched waste residue. Preferred                  Composition: The mass ratio of aluminum residue                  and sodium carbonate in sodium carbonate alkali                  solution is 1:0.5-1.0. Preferred Process: The                  step (S2) comprises mixing alkaline residue                  mixture in the dissolution tank at deionization                  temperature of 10-100 degrees C, and continuously                  stirring for 10-60 minutes under constant                  stirring. In step (S1) and step (S2), the mixture                  are stirred and mixed by mechanical stirring                  and/or ultrasonic stirring.

PT P
PN CN109111993-A; CN109111993-B
TI Preparation of jujube effective component of tobacco used in cigarette, involves taking jujube for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, distilling, extracting jujube polysaccharide and combining jujube light component and polysaccharide.
AU PANG D
   CHENG S
   CHENG Z
   CHEN S
   PENG J
AE CHINA TOBACCO HUBEI IND LLC (CNTA-C)
GA 201903679R
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of jujube effective component of tobacco, involves taking jujube for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain jujube supercritical extract, carrying out molecular weight distillation of jujube supercritical extract, collecting the light component of the molecular distillation, adding residue obtained by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of jujube to tap water according to the material ratio of (1:4)-(1:8), heating mixture and refluxing for 3-5 hours, extracting, cooling, filtering, and concentrating to relative density of 1.15, adding concentrated liquid to concentration of 95% ethanol in mass ratio of (1:2)-(1:4), filtering, dissolving filtered solid in tap water to adjust the density to 1.15, adding 50% ethanol to precipitate, concentrating filtrate to a relative density of 1.20 to obtain jujube polysaccharide, uniformly mixing jujube light component and jujube polysaccharide.
   USE - Preparation of jujube effective component of tobacco used in cigarette (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of jujube effective component with rich smoke, realizes refined development and comprehensive utilization of jujube, and effectively produces delicate, soft and elegant cigarette.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of jujube effective component of tobacco, involves taking jujube for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 56-60 degrees C and pressure of 30-40 MPa to obtain supercritical extract of jujube, carrying out molecular weight distillation of jujube supercritical extract at 40-60 degrees C, pressure of 200-400 Pa and feed rate of 1-2 ml/minute, collecting the light component of the molecular distillation, adding residue obtained by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of jujube to tap water according to the material ratio of (1:4)-(1:8), heating mixture and refluxing for 3-5 hours, extracting, cooling, filtering, and concentrating to relative density of 1.15, adding concentrated liquid to concentration of 95% ethanol in mass ratio of (1:2)-(1:4), filtering, dissolving filtered solid in tap water to adjust the density to 1.15, adding 50% ethanol to precipitate in mass ratio of (1:2)-(1:4), concentrating filtrate to a relative density of 1.20 to obtain jujube polysaccharide, uniformly mixing jujube light component and jujube polysaccharide in mass ratio of (1:10)-(1:20). The pressure of separation kettle (I) is 10-12 MPa. The temperature of separation kettle (I) is 45-50 degrees C. The pressure of separation kettle (II) is 4-6 MPa, and temperature of separation kettle (II) is 20-25 degrees C. The scraping speed is 150-300 rpm. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for usage of jujube effective component of tobacco in cigarette, which involves using jujube effective component, diluting with 70 wt.% ethanol solution and spraying onto the tobacco, adding jujube effective component at amount of 80-120 ppm, placing the sprayed tobacco into sealed bag and sealing, placing in an oven for balancing at 60 degrees C for 30 minutes, taking out and cooling, balancing mixture in a constant temperature and humidity chamber at 25 degrees C for 24 hours, and taking out to prepare cigarette.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); P15 (Tobacco (A24).)
MC D10-A05; P15-A01; P15-L05
IP A24B-003/12; C11B-009/02

TI Crystallization ammonia steam regenerating carbon capturing system comprises e.g. absorption tower, water washing tower, crystallizer, ethanol tank, blowing steam providing device, condenser and flash steaming separation device.

   ZHU D

   GE Z
   ZHANG W

AB    NOVELTY - Crystallization ammonia steam regenerating carbon capturing system comprises an absorption tower, a water washing tower, a crystallizer, an ethanol tank, a solid-liquid separator, a regenerating tower, a blowing steam providing device, a condenser and a flash steaming separation device. The absorption tower is provided with a smoke gas inlet, a smoke gas outlet and a liquid outlet. The flue gas outlet is connected with the water washing tower. The water washing tower is connected with the rich liquid outlet connected with the crystallizer. The ethanol jar connected with the crystallizer. The crystallizer, solid-liquid separator, a regenerating tower, condenser and flash separation device are orderly connected. The purging steam providing device is connected with the regenerating tower.
   USE - Used as crystallization ammonia steam regenerating carbon capturing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces ammonia slip, improves regeneration rate and reduces the regeneration energy consumption to the utmost extent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for regenerating carbon from steam capturing crystallization ammonia method , comprising (i) passing smoke through the cooling device, cooling into the bottom of the absorption tower, spraying the ammonia water absorbent into the tower from the top of tower, placing carbon dioxide absorber in the flue gas with the ammonia water in and liquid separation device to ammonia into ammonia tower top as water absorbent and (ix) separating out water washing and liquid separation device of the water into the top of the water washing tower with the supplied water absorbs water washing of the mixed gas in the tower.

PN KR1845664-B1
TI Volatile organic compound treating device for removing volatile organic compound in toxic gas, comprises influx unit in which toxic gas includes volatile organic compound, where first plasma reactive unit is connected to influx unit.
AU SUNG L
AE LEE S (LEES-Individual)

   GU C
AE SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)

GA 202208331A
AB    NOVELTY - Methanation of coke oven gas involves                subjecting the raw coke oven gas to a methanation                reaction step in a methane reaction system to                obtain a methanation reaction stream, subjecting                the methanation reaction stream to a separation                step, removing water vapour in the methanation                stream, obtaining a dehydrated methane stream,                subjecting the dehydrated methane stream to a                supplemental methanation step in a supplemental                methanation reactor to obtain a product gas, where                the methane reaction system comprises a first                methanation reaction section and a second                methanation reaction section. The raw material coke                oven gas subjected to methanation reaction in the                methane reaction system comprises mixing the raw                material coke oven gas with at least a portion of                the second methanation stream from the second                methanation reaction section.
   USE - Method for methanation of coke oven gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, it not only can reduces                the energy consumption and process cost of coke                oven gas methanation process in the existing                technology, but also can realize high efficient                utilization of coke oven gas and carbon dioxide,                providing high value of natural gas resource.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Methanation of coke oven gas involves                subjecting the raw coke oven gas to a methanation                reaction step in a methane reaction system to                obtain a methanation reaction stream, subjecting                the methanation reaction stream to a separation                step, removing water vapour in the methanation                stream, obtaining a dehydrated methane stream,                subjecting the dehydrated methane stream to a                supplemental methanation step in a supplemental                methanation reactor to obtain a product gas, where                the methane reaction system comprises a first                methanation reaction section and a second                methanation reaction section. The raw material coke                oven gas subjected to methanation reaction in the                methane reaction system comprises mixing the raw                material coke oven gas with at least a portion of                the second methanation stream from the second                methanation reaction section, subjecting the mixed                gas to a first methanation step in the first                methanation reaction section to obtain a first                methanation stream, subjecting at least a portion                of the first methanation stream and the carbon                dioxide gas stream to the second methanation step                in the second methanation reaction section,                obtaining the second methanation stream,                discharging at least a portion of the first                methanation stream and/or at least a portion of the                second methanation stream out of the methane                reaction system to obtain the methanation reaction                stream.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a coke                oven gas methanation system, which comprises first                methanation reaction section, where the first                methanation reaction section is provided with a                coke oven gas feeding pipeline, the coke oven gas                feeding pipeline is further provided with a second                methanation reaction section circulating inlet, and                the first methanation reaction section is further                provided with a first methanation reaction section                stream outlet, a second methanation reaction                section, where the second methanation reaction                section is provided with a carbon dioxide gas flow                feeding pipeline, the carbon dioxide gas flow                feeding pipeline is provided with a first                methanation reaction section circulating                inlet.

PN CN113583782-A
TI Manufacturing fine rice wine useful for injecting carbon dioxide comprises preparing beer juice, preparing clear juice, mixing beer juice and clear juice according to proportion and injecting carbon dioxide.
AU LI M
   YU X
AE HUBEI SHUANGLUSHUANG FOOD CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021C8238A
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing fine rice wine comprises (a) preparing beer juice by (i) uniformly mixing 10-30 pts. wt. malt powder and 20-60 pts. wt. water into the saccharifying boiler, adjusting the pH value to 5.2-5.5, and saccharifying, (ii) saccharifying and filtering to obtain wort, the wort is boiled under normal pressure, the boiling time is 60-70 minutes, (iii) cooling the wheat juice after involution precipitation to 8-10, pumping the cooled wort into the fermentation tank, adding beer Saccharomyces cerevisiae, the inoculation amount is 0.5% of the total weight of the wort, raising the temperature to 10degrees Celsius for main fermentation, (b) preparing clear liquor , (c) mixing the beer juice with the clear liquor according to the proportion of 1:3-5, and (d) separating the wine liquid, sterilizing the wine liquid, filling and adding carbon dioxide gas to obtain the fine rice wine.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing fine rice wine, which is useful for injecting carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has reasonable alcohol content, is refreshing and delicious, and has strong aroma, crystal clear and shiny body, which not only retains the various nutrients of rice wine, highlightstypical flavor characteristics of malt aroma.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing fine rice wine comprises (a) preparing beer juice by (i) uniformly mixing 10-30 pts. wt. malt powder and 20-60 pts. wt. water into the saccharifying boiler, adjusting the pH value to 5.2-5.5, and saccharifying, (ii) saccharifying and filtering to obtain wort, the wort is boiled under normal pressure, the boiling time is 60-70 minutes, (iii) cooling the wheat juice after involution precipitation to 8-10, pumping the cooled wort into the fermentation tank, adding beer Saccharomyces cerevisiae, the inoculation amount is 0.5 % of the total weight of the wort, raising the temperature to 10degrees Celsius for main fermentation, when the sugar degree of the fermentation liquid is reduced to 4.6-5degrees Celsius, raising the temperature to 12degrees Celsius for diacetyl reduction, when the sugar degree of the fermentation liquid is reduced to 3.5-3.9degrees Celsius, sealing the tank to 0.10-0.12Mpa beer juice, (b) preparing clear liquor by (i) taking the 80-120 pts. wt. sticky rice after cooking and cooling, then the rice and mixed yeast and stirring uniformly, putting into the fermentation tank for primary fermentation, sealing the fermentation tank, fermenting at 35degrees Celsius for 24 hours, then at 28-30degrees Celsius for 15-20 hours, where the mixed yeast comprises Angel yeast and soil yeast, the mass ratio of the two is 2: 1-2, (ii), adding water to the fermented rice wine for primary fermentation, adding activated Saccharomyces cerevisiae in the preparation liquid, fermenting for 15 -25 hours at 20-30degrees Celsius, fermenting for 20-30 hours under the condition of 10-12degrees Celsius, (iii) adding ice water to the fermented wine juice in step (b) for secondary preparing, filtering, deoxidizing and storing at low temperature to prepare the clear liquor, (c) mixing the beer juice with the clear liquor according to the proportion of 1:3-5, and (d) separating the wine liquid, sterilizing the wine liquid, filling and adding carbon dioxide gas to obtain the fine rice wine.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fine rice wine prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The saccharification method when preparing beer juice is as follows: rest the protein at 45-50degrees Celsius for 40 minutes, heat up to 65degrees Celsius for saccharification for 40 minutes, then heat up to 72degreesC for saccharification for 20 minutes, and finally heat up to 76-78degrees Celsius for 20 minutes. The added amount of the mixed koji when preparing the sake juice is 0.60-0.66% of the quality of the rice. The method of adding water in step (b) when preparing sake juice is as follows after the first fermented rice wine is squeezed out, 22g sugar is added to every 100g of wine juice, and then cool boiled water at room temperature is added, and after mixing evenly, the blended body temperature of the wine is 26-28degrees Celsius. The amount of activated yeast added in step (b) when preparing sake juice is 0.8% of the quality of the wine juice after blending. When preparing the sake juice, the temperature of the wine juice during the second blending in step (c) is -1degrees Celsius to -4degreesC. When carbon dioxide gas is added to the filling, nitrogen is added at the same time, and the volume ratio of carbon dioxide to nitrogen is 3:1.

PN CN112379105-A
TI Detecting porcine and respiratory syndrome virus            immunoperoxidase monolayer assay neutralizing antibody            comprises e.g. digesting Marc145 cells, centrifuging,            plating on well plate, placing in carbon dioxide            incubator and incubating.
AU WANG X
   LI P
   FENG C
   WANG L
   GAO X
   SUN G
   YUE F
   LI H
   ZHU Y
   ZHANG Y
   GUO D
   QI Y
   PAN P
AE UNIV XINXIANG (UNXX-C)
GA 202120317E
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting porcine and respiratory syndrome                virus (PRRSV) immunoperoxidase monolayer assay                (IPMA) neutralizing antibody comprises e.g.                digesting the Marc145 cells and centrifuging to                obtain a cell suspension, plating 100 mu l per well                on a 96-well plate, placing it in a 37 degrees C 5%                carbon dioxide incubator for 12 hours, after the                cells are fully attached, incubating an equal                volume of PRRSV virus liquid and clinical serum,                inoculating it on Marc145 cells, adding Dulbecco's                Modified Eagle's medium containing 2% fetal bovine                serum to culture, and placing it in a carbon                dioxide incubator to continue culturing, observing                the cell growth, after the cells are full, taking                out the 96-well plate and setting up control wells                for normal inoculated cells, washing the removed                96-well plate with phosphate buffered saline, and                fixing it with pre-chilled absolute ethanol,                successfully preparing cell reaction plate, washing                the cell reaction plate 2-3 times with phosphate                buffered saline.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting porcine and                respiratory syndrome virus (PRRSV) IPMA                neutralizing antibody.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has certain technical support for                the prevention, vaccine evaluation, and immune                evaluation of pigs infected with PRRSV; evaluates                the immune effect of PRRS vaccine, which is                expected to be used in the production of actual                vaccines; utilizes the interaction between the                whole virus and the antibody to infect the target                cell, which truly reflects the interaction between                the virus and the neutralizing antibody; and has                strong specificity, high sensitivity and simple                operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting porcine and respiratory syndrome                virus (PRRSV) IPMA neutralizing antibody comprises                (1) digesting the Marc145 cells and centrifuging to                obtain a cell suspension containing 1x 105                cells/ml, plating 100 mu l per well on a 96-well                plate, placing it in a 37 degrees C 5% carbon                dioxide incubator for 12 hours, after the cells are                fully attached, incubating an equal volume of PRRSV                virus liquid and clinical serum for 1 hour at 37                degrees C, inoculating it on Marc145 cells, adding                Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's medium containing 2%                fetal bovine serum to culture, and placing it in a                carbon dioxide incubator to continue culturing for                48 hours, observing the cell growth, after the                cells are full, taking out the 96-well plate and                setting up control wells for normal inoculated                cells, (2) washing the removed 96-well plate with                phosphate buffered saline for 2-3 times, and fixing                it with pre-chilled absolute ethanol at -20 degrees                C for 30 minutes, successfully preparing cell                reaction plate, (3) washing the cell reaction plate                2-3 times with phosphate buffered saline, adding                100 mu l of PRRSV standard positive serum diluted                1:400 with 0.01 M phosphate buffered saline, and                placing it at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, (4) adding                100 mu l goat anti-pig IgG-horseradish peroxidase                secondary antibody diluted 1000 times with 0.01 M                phosphate buffered saline (pH7.4), and letting it                act at 37 degrees C for 1 hours and (5)                DABdeveloping color for 5 minutes, terminating the                reaction, and observing the color result under an                optical microscope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the PRRSV virus liquid comprises washing the                  disease material tissue of PRRSV with phosphate                  buffered saline, after cutting into pieces,                  adding 1 ml phosphate buffered saline according                  to 1 g tissue sample, and freezing and thawing                  three times repeatedly to make a disease material                  grinding solution. The preparation method of the                  clinical serum comprises centrifuging the                  collected blood, and collecting serum for later                  clinical serum detection.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide.

TI Tea comprises Astragalus bud, radix astragali flower, tea and traditional Chinese medicinal component.
AU JIN X
   QIN F
   XU K
   LI A
   LI H
AE GANSU WEISHUIYUAN PHARMACY TECHNOLOGY CO (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 201959945E
AB    NOVELTY - Tea comprises 1-10 pts. wt. Astragalus bud, 1-3 pts. wt. radix astragali flower, 1-2 pts. wt. tea and 1-2 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicinal component.
   USE - As tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The tea is easy to carry, has rich nutrients, healthcare effect, utilizes the resources of Astragalus to increase social and economic benefits and utilization rate and reduces the waste of the aboveground part of Astragalus. The method is simple, cost-effective, and suitable for mass industrial production and shortens production cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of preparing tea, which involves (a) picking fresh 1-3 cm Astragalus bud to remove yellow leaves, picking and removing impurities, (b) rinsing Astragalus bud in cold water containing chlorine dioxide and ozone, where the concentration of the chlorine dioxide is 100-500 ppm, the concentration of the ozone is 1-50 ppm, and the water temperature of the cold water is 4-10 degrees C, the rinsing time is 1-10 minutes, and then rinsing with warm water at a temperature of 30-50 degrees C, (c) steaming rinsed Astragalus bud in a high temperature and high pressure steam boiler, the steam temperature is 265-270 degrees C, and the steam pressure is 100-200 KPa and steaming treatment time is 8-20 minutes, (d) placing the steamed Astragalus bud in a supercritical carbon dioxide drying device to be dried to have a water content of 5% or less and obtaining dried Astragalus bud, where the supercritical carbon dioxide drying device has a carbon dioxide flow rate of 1.0-4.0 kg/hour, and a carbon dioxide drying pressure of 10-45 MPa, the separation pressure of carbon dioxide is 6-8 MPa, the drying temperature is 30-45 degrees C, the drying time is 2-4 hours, (e) crushing dried Astragalus bud into pieces, and the diameter of the buds is 0.2-1 mm, adding water at 15-25 degrees C, where the weight ratio of the fresh Astragalus bud to the clear water is 1:2-1:5, (f) placing debris obtained in the previous step in room temperature water for 0.25-5 hour, then extracting it into an extractor, the total weight of the room temperature water is 5-20 times the weight of the fragments, the extracting speed of the extractor is 2000-8000 rpm, the extraction time is 5-30 minutes and the extraction temperature is 20-40 degrees C, (g) performing preliminary filtration on the fragments using filter fragments or filter cloth, then, the polypropylene hollow fiber membrane having a molecular weight cut-off in the range of 70,000 Da to 100,000 Da is used for filtration again, (h) concentrating filtered filtrate is concentrated with a reverse osmosis membrane at a temperature of 25-35 degrees C to a solid content of 5-15% to obtain a preliminary concentrate, concentrating preliminary concentrate under low pressure vacuum at a pressure of 0.01-0.10 MPa to a concentrate having a solid content of 30-50% and the vacuum is concentrated at a temperature of 25-40 degrees C, (i) drying content to moisture content of 0-5%, and (j) weighing and mixing Astragalus bud , radix astragali flower, tea and traditional Chinese medicinal component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The tea comprises 1-10 pts. wt. Astragalus bud , 1 pt. wt. radix astragali flower, 1-2 pts. wt. tea and 1 pt. wt. traditional Chinese medicinal component. Preferred Composition: The traditional Chinese medicinal component comprises 12 pts. wt. rose, 12 pts. wt. ginseng, 12 pts. wt. large-headed Astractylodes rhizome, 6 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 6 pts. wt. Angelica, 6 pts. wt. rhizoma ligustici wallichii, 3 pts. wt. radix paeoniae alba, 3 pts. wt. prepared Rehmannia root and 3 pts. wt. radix glycyrrhizae preparata. Preferred Method: In step (f), when extracting Astragalus bud, 0.001-0.005% pectinase, 0.01-0.05% cellulase, 0.005-0.01% chymotrypsin and 0.01-0.05% clarifier chitosan is added. In step (i), brown sugar is further added to the concentrated liquid. In step (j), the tea variety is one of oolong tea or Tieguanyin.

TI Diesel fueled chemical-looping combustion            electrical power generation method involves heating            diesel fuel combined with oxygen by heat from a heat            recovery unit, where the heated diesel fuel is            separated into a gaseous element stream.
AU PRADIPTYA I
   ADNAN M A
   HOSSAIN M M
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 202293513M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating diesel fuel                combined with oxygen by heat from a heat recovery                unit. The heated diesel fuel is separated into a                gaseous element stream including hydrogen, carbon                dioxide, hydrogen oxide, methane and coke in a                gasification chamber by a first gasification                reactor fluidly connected to a diesel fuel feed                source. A cooled exhaust stream comprising carbon                dioxide and hydrogen are outputted by the heat                recovery. The recovered heat and hydrogen are                converted to steam by a steam generator.                Electricity is generated from the steam by the                steam turbine electrical generator. The carbon                dioxide is separated from hydrogen of the exhaust                stream by a condenser of a carbon dioxide gas                purification stage.
   USE - Diesel fueled chemical-looping combustion                (CLC) electrical power generation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides energy efficient, clean                electrical power with carbon dioxide recovery. The                system allows a controller to control diesel feed                source, oxygen source, compressors, splitter                percentages and monitors electrical output to                provide an efficient system and recovery of carbon                dioxide.

TI Apparatus used for production and purification of hydrogen, comprises hydrogen-permeable separation structure, catalyst for steam-reforming reaction and water-gas shift reaction, and carbon dioxide absorber for methane reforming reaction.
AU CHOI S
   MIN Y
   CHOI S H
AE KEUM KANG CNT LTD (KEUM-Non-standard)
   KEUM KANG CNT CO LTD (KEUM-Non-standard)
GA 202060574T
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen production and purification apparatus comprises a hydrogen-permeable separation structure in which hydrogen generation reaction occurs on a side (S1) and hydrogen generated from side (S1) passes to a side (S2) to concentrate hydrogen, a catalyst, and carbon dioxide absorber for methane reforming reaction on side (S1). The methane-reforming catalyst includes a catalyst for performing steam-reforming reaction of methane by reacting methane and water to obtain carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and a catalyst for performing a water-gas shift reaction by reacting carbon monoxide and water to obtain carbon dioxide and hydrogen. Hydrogen formed by steam-reforming reaction and water-gas shift reaction from methane and water vapor-containing gas on side (S1) of the hydrogen-permeable separation structure passes to side (S2), and is removed, and carbon dioxide formed on side (S1) is captured with the solid carbon dioxide absorber.
   USE - Apparatus is used for producing and purifying hydrogen used as fuel in fuel cell, and for ammonia synthesis, refinery process, smelting process, and manufacturing polysilicon, semiconductor device and LED (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus produces high-purity hydrogen with simultaneous capture of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of hydrogen-enriched gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen-permeable separation structure comprises a hydrogen separation membrane with a metal dense membrane in which hydrogen molecules adsorb on a metal surface, dissociate into hydrogen atoms, hydrogen atoms move between metal lattices, recombine with hydrogen molecules on an opposite side of the separation membrane, and detach from the metal surface, or a hydrogen separation membrane through which hydrogen permeates, or comprises a porous structure having a pore size limiting the passage of molecules larger than hydrogen, and a palladium-based dense film layer, optionally a diffusion barrier layer, and a hydrogen-separation membrane in which a porous support is stacked. The methane-containing gas is natural gas, shale gas, or coke oven gas.

PN WO2018097260-A1; JP2018090765-A; TW201825530-A; JP2019094494-A; KR2019073453-A; CN110062771-A; US2019276682-A1; SG11201904545-A1; EP3546487-A1; EP3546487-A4; JP7026343-B2
TI New polymer used in resin composition used in antifouling-paint composition, has structure that reacts with carbon dioxide in presence of water to stabilize carbamate to be formed.
AU SUGA T
   NISHIDE H
   KATSUMATA S
   TANIGUCHI K
   NAKAMURA J
   IWASAKI T
   TAKEO S
   HIROYUKI N
   SHO K
   KANA T
   JUNICHI N
   TOMOKAZU I
AE UNIV WASEDA (UYWS-C)
   MITSUBISHI CHEM CORP (MITU-C)
   UNIV WASEDA GH (UYWS-C)
   MITSUBISHI CHEM CORP (MITU-C)
   UNIV WASEDA (UYWS-C)
   MITSUBISHI CHEM CORP (MITU-C)
   UNIV WASEDA (UYWS-C)
   MITSUBISHI CHEM CORP (MITU-C)
GA 2018428227
AB    NOVELTY - A polymer having a structure (s1) that reacts with carbon dioxide in the presence of water to stabilize the carbamate to be formed, is new. The difference between the contact angles of the polymer in water after reaction with carbon dioxide with respect to that before reaction with carbon dioxide is 10 or more.
   USE - New polymer is used in resin composition used in antifouling-paint composition (all claimed) used for forming coating film on marine structures such as boats and ships.
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer forms coating film having excellent water resistance and antifouling properties. The paint composition containing the polymer has excellent film formability and coating ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the polymer (I);
   (2) resin composition, which contains the polymer;
   (3) antifouling-paint composition, which contains the resin composition;
   (4) production of a structure (S), which involves contacting carbon dioxide with the surface of a coating film containing the polymer in the presence of water for stabilizing carbamate on the surface of the coating film; and
   (5) method for regenerating the structure (s), which involves contacting carbon dioxide with the surface of the coating film in the presence of water when the structure (s) which stabilizes carbamate on the surface of the coating film is removed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The polymer is obtained by polymerizing a monomer mixture comprising a nitrogen-containing monomer of formula (I-1), (I-2) and/or (I-3). Alternately, the polymer is obtained by reacting a polymer containing an epoxy group, and a compound capable of reacting with the epoxy group.L1 = alkylene or phenylene (both optionally substituted by E and linear or branched);E = alkyl, aryl, carboxy, alkoxycarbonyl, cyano, hydroxy, amido, halo, allyl, epoxy, alkoxy, siloxy or group exhibiting hydrophilicity or ionicity;R1 = alkylene group optionally containing a substituent or a group containing -O- or -S- between its carbon atoms;R2-R4,R11,R12 = H, optionally substituted alkyl, alkoxy or phenyl;L2 = L1;R5-R8 = H, optionally substituted alkyl, alkoxy or phenyl;R6+R8 = optionally substituted heterocyclyl;R9-R10 = alkylene optionally containing a substituent or a group containing -O- or -S- between carbon atoms of alkylene;L2+R9+R10 = optionally substituted heterocyclyl;R11+R12 = alkylene optionally containing a substituent or a group containing -O- or -S- between carbon atoms of alkylene or phenylene formed between the carbon atoms of the alkylene;andZ- = counter anion.(i) One of R2-R4 is H, optionally substituted alkyl, alkoxy or phenyl, and the other two are alkylene optionally substituted by a substituent, a group containing -O- or -N- between the carbon atoms of the alkylene, phenylene or optionally substituted heterocyclyl;and (ii) When L1 is alkylene or phenylene having no substituent, at least one of R2-R4 is optionally substituted alkyl, alkoxy or phenyl. Preferred Composition: The structure (s1) is of formula (I-1), (I-2) and/or (I-3). The polymer is a (meth)acrylic-type polymer or a novolac-type polymer. The antifouling-paint composition further contains an antifoulant chosen from cuprous oxide, pyridine triphenylborane, 4,5-dichloro-2-n-octyl-4-isothiazolin-3-one, 4-bromo-2-(4-chlorophenyl)-5-trifluoromethyl-1H-pyrrole-3-carbonitrile and medetomidine, a thermoplastic resin other than the polymer, and a silicone oil.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R2-R4 = H.
   EXAMPLE - About 3.00 g glycidyl methacrylate was dissolved in 19.90 g anisole, 21.7 mg azobisisobutyronitrile ??was added as an initiator, polymerization of the resulting mixture was carried out at 70 degrees C for 2 hours, and the resulting polymerized mixture was precipitated using methanol, purified and vacuum dried at room temperature to obtain 1.50 g white powdery polyglycidyl methacrylate. 0.200 g polyglycidyl methacrylate was dissolved in 5.50 g dimethyl sulfoxide, 2.45 g N,N-diethylethylenediamine was added and after reacting at 80 degrees C for 1 hour, and resultant was precipitated using 360 g diethyl ether and 328 g of hexane, purified and vacuum dried at room temperature to obtain an amine polymer of formula (A) as a white powder in which the epoxy group was opened. The polymer (A) has a number average molecular weight of 89300, and a polydispersity index of 2.9, and exhibits solubility in water, methanol, chloroform and toluene. The polymer (A) exhibits insolubility in acetonitrile, diethyl ether, and hexane.
   n = not defined.

PN CN114888056-A
TI Solidifying simultaneously carbon dioxide and deep            dechlorination of waste incineration fly ash involves            performing three-stage countercurrent washing on waste            incineration fly ash, during first-stage countercurrent            water washing and second-stage countercurrent water            washing process.
AU WANG X
   ZHONG W
   ZHAO Q
   YUAN R
   HUANG X
   CHEN X
   SUN Z
   JI Z
   LU S
   FAN X
   ZHANG R
   GAN M
AE BAOWU GROUP ENVIRONMENTAL RESOURCES TECH (BAOW-Non-standard)
   UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2022A7824G
AB    NOVELTY - Solidifying simultaneously carbon dioxide and                deep dechlorination of waste incineration fly ash                involves performing three-stage countercurrent                washing on waste incineration fly ash, during                first-stage countercurrent water washing and                second-stage countercurrent water washing process,                and introducing gas containing carbon dioxide into                the slurry formed by the waste incineration fly ash                and water for reaction.
   USE - Method for solidifying simultaneously carbon                dioxide and deep dechlorination of waste                incineration fly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves water consumption, and                recycles water through a three-stage countercurrent                process, and has the solid-liquid ratio of only                3:1-4:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The waste incineration                  fly ash and water are all formed into a slurry                  with specific liquid-solid ratio of 3 ml:1 g - 4                  ml:1 g. The gas containing carbon dioxide is                  introduced into the slurry at a flow rate of                  100-200 liter/minutes per kilogram waste                  incineration fly ash, during the first-stage                  countercurrent water washing process The pH of                  the slurry is controlled during the first-stage                  countercurrent water washing process within the                  range of 6.0-6.5. The gas containing carbon                  dioxide is introduced into the slurry at a flow                  rate of 60-100 liter/minutes per kilogram waste                  incineration fly ash, during the first-stage                  countercurrent water washing process. The pH of                  the slurry is controlled during the second-stage                  countercurrent water washing process within the                  range of 5.5-6.0. The volume percent content of                  carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide-containing                  gas is 5-30 vol.%. The air stone is used to                  achieve uniform bubbling in the process of                  passing the carbon dioxide-containing gas into                  the slurry. The three-stage countercurrent                  washing process, where the condition for each                  level of countercurrent washing includes the                  temperature is 40-60&#8451;, the time is                  40-60 minutes, and the stirring rate is 500-800                  revolutions per minute. The solid-liquid                  separation is realized by centrifugation after                  the three-stage countercurrent water washing is                  completed, and the solid moisture content is                  lower than 35%.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))

PN US11174201-B1
TI Process for creating geopolymeric concrete involves adding salt water to a mixture of geopolymer concrete, adding coal ash, and curing the mixture with carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN S
AE UNIV NORTH CAROLINA (UNCR-C)
GA 2021C8789J
AB    NOVELTY - A process for creating geopolymeric concrete involves adding salt water to a mixture of geopolymer concrete; adding coal ash; and curing the mixture with carbon dioxide (CO2).
   USE - A process for creating geopolymeric concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method generates materials with commercial values to offset the cost for carbon capture. The method develops highly porous, geopolymeric concrete with sea water only with CO2 curing. The material is suggested for simultaneous depletion of ash, carbon dioxide and possible furnace slag from iron manufacturing. The making of the suggested material does not require high temperature nor pressure. The product has higher strength than porous ordinary Portland cement (OPC) concrete and absorbs carbon dioxide at a faster rate.

PT P
PN CN113620808-A
TI Preparing 3,5-dinitrobenzoic acid, comprises e.g. reacting m-dinitrobenzene in dimethylformamide solvent, then adding metal-modified mesoporous material-loaded ionic liquid catalyst, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure of carbon dioxide, then stirring, and reacting obtained product.
AU CHEN R
   LIU Y
   RONG Q
   HU Y
AE UNIV CHINA THREE GORGES (UYCR-C)
GA 2021D0182Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 3,5-dinitrobenzoic acid, comprises reacting m-dinitrobenzene using dimethylformamide as solvent at 80-130degrees Celsius, then adding metal-modified mesoporous material-loaded ionic liquid catalyst, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure of carbon dioxide, keeping at the specified pressure, then stirring, and reacting for 4-20 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 3,5-dinitrobenzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The product: has excellent catalytic activity in carboxylation reaction. The method: does not generate waste acid; utilizes catalyst, which can be easily recycled and reused; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction temperature is 80-130degrees Celsius (preferably 100-120degrees Celsius); the reaction time is 4-20 hours (preferably 8-14 hours); and the carbon dioxide pressure is controlled at 0.5-5 MPa (preferably 1.5-3.5 MPa). The preparation method of metal-modified mesoporous material-loaded ionic liquid catalyst comprises (a) reacting 4,5-dimethoxy-1,3-dimethylimidazolidin-2-one and phosphorus oxychloride in toluene solvent at 50-65degrees Celsius for 22-26 hours, then adding n-hexylamine, continue reacting for 16-20 hours, adding 20% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution with mass fraction to adjust the pH to neutral, extracting the mixed solution with dichloromethane, recovering the solvent, and drying to obtain intermediate 1, and (b) reacting intermediate 1 with 3-chloropropyltriethoxysilane at 90-110degrees Celsius in toluene solvent for 22-24 hours, then recovering the solvent, and drying to obtain intermediate 2, (c) reacting intermediate 2 with chloride-containing metal salt in acetonitrile solvent at 60-70degrees Celsius for 20-25 hours, washing with acetonitrile, and drying to obtain ionic liquid 3, and (d) continue reacting ionic liquid 3 with metal-modified mesoporous material in toluene solvent at 90-110degrees Celsius for 20-30 hours, then filtering, and drying to obtain final product.Preferred Components: The amount of metal-modified mesoporous material-loaded ionic liquid catalyst is 5-50% (preferably 15-40%) the mass of m-dinitrobenzene. The metal-modified mesoporous material-loaded ionic liquid catalyst is of formula (I); the anion is ionic salt compound, preferably thulium chloride (TmCl4) or lutetium chloride (LuCl4); and the metal-modified mesoporous material is titanium-MCM-41, tin-MCM-41 or aluminum-MCM-41. The metal-modified mesoporous material-loaded ionic liquid catalyst includes titanium-MCM-41-IL thulium chloride (TmCl4), tin-MCM-41-IL thulium chloride (TmCl4), aluminum-MCM-41-IL thulium chloride (TmCl4), titanium-MCM-41-IL lutetium chloride (LuCl4), tin-MCM-41-IL lutetium chloride (LuCl4), or aluminum-MCM-41-IL lutetium chloride (LuCl4). In step (a), the molar ratio between 4,5-dimethoxy-1,3-dimethylimidazolidin-2-one, phosphorus oxychloride and n-hexylamine is 1:1-1.5:1-3 (preferably 1:1.2:2); in step (b), the molar ratio between intermediate 1 and 3-chloropropyltriethoxysilane is 1:1-1.5 (preferably 1:1); in step (c), the chlorine-containing metal salt is thulium chloride or lutetium chloride; and the molar ratio between intermediate 2 and chlorine-containing metal salt is 1:1-3 (preferably 1:1-1.5); in step (d), the metal-modified mesoporous material is titanium-MCM-41, tin-MCM-41, tin-MCM-41, or aluminum-MCM-41; and the mass ratio between intermediate 3 and metal-modified mesoporous material is 0.4-1.5:1 (preferably 0.6-1.2:1).

The inlet pressure of carbondioxide when filled with 5 megapascal. The distributing pH value after neutralization is 8-9. The spraying temperature is 25-30 degrees C. Preferred Process: The method involves subjecting carbon dioxide and water react to generate calcium carbonate under high pressure environment of 2-3 megapascal, pH value after generating carbonate is 4.8-5, obtaining A carbonate, A carbonate is firstly filled with water washing, and then cloth is completely immersed in A to A in process of neutralizing, neutralizing temperature is 30-35 degrees C, neutralization time is 30 seconds, obtaining finished cloth.

TI Preparing carbonyl compounds comprises reacting            carbon dioxide as carbonylation reagent in presence of            hydrogen sulfide and optional base.
AU ZHU N
   HE R
   CHENG S
   YU L
   WU J
   SHI G
   WANG Y

   HAN L
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA TECHNOLOGY (UYIM-C)

GA 2022848868
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbonyl compounds comprises                reacting carbon dioxide as carbonylation reagent in                the presence of hydrogen sulfide and an optional                base.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbonyl                compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has relatively high atomic economy;                reduces generation of by-products; and can be used                carbon dioxide to replace toxic and harmful                carbonylation reagent in the presence of hydrogen                sulfide and alkali.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a)                  reacting compound of formula (R1-X1) (Ia) or                  (R2-Y1) (Ib) with carbon dioxide in optional                  inert solvent in the presence of optional base                  and hydrogen sulfide to obtain ketone-based                  compound of formula (R1-VI-C(=O)-U1-R2) (I), and                  (b) (ii) reacting sulfur-based compound of                  formula (IIa) with carbon dioxide in an optional                  inert solvent in the presence of base and                  hydrogen sulfide to obtain carbonyl-based                  compound of formula (II). The method comprises                  (a1) reacting o-iodoaniline of formula (III) with                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide in the                  presence of inert solvent and base to obtain                  benzothiazolone derivatives of formula (IV), (b1)                  reacting o-nitroiodobenzene of formula (V) with                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide to                  benzothiazolone derivatives (IV), (c1) reacting                  propargylamine derivative of formula (VI) with                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide in the                  presence of optional inert solvent and optional                  base to obtain thiazolidine-2-one derivatives o                  formula (VII), (d1) reacting o-aminobenzonitrile                  of formula (VIII) with carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen sulfide in the presence of optional                  inert solvent and base to obtain thioquinazoline                  dione derivatives of formula (IX), (e1) reacting                  aromatic o-amino disulfide of formula (X) and                  carbon dioxide under the action of hydrogen                  sulfide in the presence of optional inert solvent                  and base to obtain benzothiazolone derivatives of                  formula (XI), (f1) oxazolidinone derivatives or                  thiazolidinone derivatives of formula (XII),                  diamine, alcoholamine or mercaptoamine with                  carbon dioxide under the action of hydrogen                  sulfide in the presence of optional inert solvent                  and base to obtain imidazolidinone derivatives of                  formula (XIII), and (g1) reacting amine-based                  compound of formula (R9-NH2) (XIV) and carbon                  dioxide under the action of hydrogen sulfide to                  obtain urea derivatives of formula                  (R9-NH-C(=O)-NH-R9) (XV). The carbon dioxide is                  continuously fed into the reactor, and the                  pressure of the carbon dioxide in the reactor is                  0.1-12 MPa, during the reaction process. The                  molar ratio of the reaction substrate to the                  hydrogen sulfide is 1:0.05-20. The hydrogen                  sulfide is continuously passed into the reactor,                  and the pressure of the hydrogen sulfide in the                  reactor is 0.05-1.5 Mpa, during the reaction                  process. The reaction is carried out at room                  temperature to 150 degrees C.R1 and R2 = 1-12C alkyl (i.e. optionally                  substituted 1-6C alkyl or 1-8C alkyl), 3-8C                  cycloalkyl, 2-6C alkenyl, or 2-6C alkynyl (all                  are optionally substituted by G1);R1+R2 = 1-6C alkylene, 6-10C aryl, or                  5-12-membered heteroaryl (all are optionally                  substituted by G1);ring A = 6-10C aryl or 5-12-membered                  heteroaryl (both are optionally substituted by                  G1);X1 and Y1 = halogen, CN, SH, OH, NH2, NHR', are optionally                  substituted by G1), H, SO2CH3, or phenyl                  (optionally substituted with 1-4 substituents of                  halogen, 1-6C alkyl, 1-6C haloalkyl, 1-6C The inert solvent                  includes N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone,                  dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl                  sulfoxide, 1,4-dioxane, hexamethylphosphoramide,                  dichloromethane, chloroform, carbon                  tetrachloride, toluene, ethyl acetate, and/or                  supercritical carbon dioxide. The molar ratio of                  reaction substrate to carbon dioxide is                  1:1-100.
   EXAMPLE - 1 mmol o-Iodonitrobenzene, 2 mmol alkali, 0.2                mmol cuprous iodide and 2 ml solvent were added in                the reactor successively, and tighten the reactor.                The corresponding amount of hydrogen sulfide was                introduced into the reaction kettle, and the                reaction was stirred for 30 minutes at the                corresponding temperature, and then the                corresponding amount of carbon dioxide was                introduced into the reaction kettle, and the                stirring reaction was continued at the                corresponding temperature for 24 hours. After the                reaction, the reactor was cooled to room                temperature, the gas in the reactor was slowly                exhausted, the reactor was opened, and the reaction                solution was moved to 250 ml separatory funnel. The                reaction solution was extracted with ethyl acetate,                the organic phase was dried with anhydrous                magnesium sulfate to obtain                benzo(d)thiazol-2(3H)-one, which was separated by                column chromatography.

PT P
PN WO2018094119-A1; BR112019009950-A2; EP3541541-A1; JP2019535943-W; US2020056456-A1; EP3541541-A4
TI Rehabilitating using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves utilizing varying methods to deliver cleaning energy and varying porosity of aquifers to determining varying quantities of energy over entire structure.
AU MANSUY N
   CATANIA S
   ORLANDO J
AE SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
   SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
   SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
   SUBSURFACE TECHNOLOGIES INC (SUBS-Non-standard)
GA 201841159G
AB    NOVELTY - Rehabilitating using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves utilizing varying methods to deliver cleaning energy and varying porosity of aquifers to determining varying quantities of energy over the entire well structure.
   USE - Method for rehabilitating using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The combination of energies is utilized with chemical energy, thermal energy and mechanical energy. The method for rehabilitating using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves utilizing phase changes of carbon dioxide to distribute environmentally safe chemicals throughout an entire well structure. The method for rehabilitating using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves setting suction flow control device or flow sleeve at the bottom of the pump or volume displacement pipe that is smaller diameter than a well screen into the well prior to carbon dioxide injection to allow less energy required to displace water in the well and directing the cleaning combination energies to the screen or fractures of a rock well. The method for rehabilitating using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves utilizing a suction flow control device or volume displacement pipe (VDP) in the well during the injection process resulting in less carbon dioxide, making more cost effective and shortening the following development time prior to returning water quality back to pretreatment quality. The method for rehabilitating using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves injecting carbon dioxide in a fashion as to create a vortex in the annular space between the suction flow control device and the well screen and casing allowing the combination energies to be delivered to all areas of the well. The quick release valves is placed in the lower zone of the well to release pulses of disruptive energy in repeated cycles to mobilize plugging deposits. The method for rehabiliting using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves selecting a volume of carbon dioxide energy resulting in less penetration of solubilized carbon dioxide into surrounding formations resulting in less pump development time prior to returning the well back to ambient or pre-treatment conditions. The method for rehabiliting using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves adding porosity of the aquifer, selecting the volume by evaluating the buffering capacity of the aquifer, where buffering capacity can be determined by assessment of the amount of carbonates in the aquifer and the total alkalinity of the water. The method for rehabiliting using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves directing the injection nozzle for carbon dioxide at a desired angle to achieve a vortex in the annular space between the suction pipe on the pump or volume displacement pipe (VDP) and the well screen or borehole wall, where the vortex distributes energy or combination energies more efficiently throughout the well and surrounding aquifer. The method for rehabiliting using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves injecting carbon dioxide into a sealed well to surge the well by switching between liquid injection and vapor injection cycles, where length of cycles is calculated based upon the length of the producing interval and the flow rate of carbon dioxide. The method for rehabiliting using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves shortening a vapor injection cycle and using more frequent cycles of liquid interval, creating surging of the water column, increasing the frequency of intervals between liquid and vapor and maximizing the surging effect. The method for rehabiliting using carbon dioxide with varying lengths of producing interval involves using chemical energy which is NSF approved proprietary chemicals are effective at mineral solubility, biofilm and metal dispersion. The NSF approved chemistry requires use of carbon dioxide injected into sealed well to distribute the combination energies into the surrounding filter pack and aquifer.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2018079669-A1
TI Selecting appropriate location for large-scale sequestration of atmospheric carbon dioxide involves selecting location far from sources of volcanoes, earthquakes and boundaries of tectonic plates for less availability of sulfur compounds.
AU KIM T
AE G-LAND (GLAN-Non-standard)
GA 201822343A
AB    NOVELTY - Selecting appropriate location for large-scale sequestration of atmospheric carbon dioxide involves selecting a location far from sources of volcanoes, earthquakes and boundaries of tectonic plates for less availability of sulfur compounds.
   USE - Method for selecting appropriate location for large-scale sequestration of atmospheric carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for selecting appropriate location for large-scale sequestration of atmospheric carbon dioxide further involves deploying iron-replete eco-friendly composite on ocean surface in the selected location such that iron-replete complex stays in a long period time within 100 m surface ocean with aid of iron-replete eco-friendly composite to avoid chemical conversion of iron to iron sulfide and enhance phytoplankton digestion of iron. The method for selecting appropriate location also involves on-line monitoring for successful iron fertilization by checking simultaneous concentration changes of increases in chlorophyll, oxygen, dissolved oxygen (DO) and dimethyl sulfide (DMS) and decreases in nitrate, phosphate, silicate, carbon dioxide and dissolved carbon dioxide (DCO2), deploying on the ocean surface in the selected location a water-buoyant floating enhancer and fine wood chips having a size less than 1,400 mu m and iron-reducing marine bacterium, Shewanella algae, to reduce ferric iron (Fe3+) to ferrous iron (Fe2+) for facilitated assimilation to picoplankton. Preferred Components: The iron-replete eco-friendly composite is obtained from natural desert dust, clay, volcanic ash, mucilaginous cyanobacteria and agar. Scytonema javanicum and Nostoc sp. are used as a iron-replete eco-friendly binder and as a buoyancy promoter. Preferred Conditions: The iron input for algal blooms is not bulk scale additions of direct iron or iron sulfate chemicals, but deploying natural clays or soils with content of iron in a range of 3.5-18 wt.% as observed in the continent or west Australia along with volcanic ash desulfurized by rainfall and weathering for long time of maximal 74 years. The location is selected from HNLC (high nutrient, low chlorophyll), LNLC (low-nutrient, low-chlorophyll), HNHC (high-nutrient-high-chlorophyll) and LNHC (low nutrient, high chlorophyll) regions of oceanic regions on the basis of the relative magnitude of the accumulation rates of iron from deserts and subsurface water upwelling and sulfur from volcanoes. The iron has a size of less than 2 mu m.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The sample chamber having an inlet port and an outlet port, where sample chamber is empty, concentration of carbon dioxide is varied, and introduced into a gas flow line which is connected with the inlet port of the sample chamber from first concentration to second concentration.
   USE - Method for determining rapid net assimilation rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining rapid net assimilation rate involves using compute sample internal carbon dioxide concentration response curve for photosynthesis capable sample in gas exchange analysis system (10) having enclosed sample chamber (30) defining a measurement volume for analysis of the photosynthesis capable sample. The sample chamber having an inlet port and an outlet port, where sample chamber is empty, concentration of carbon dioxide is varied, and introduced into a gas flow line which is connected with the inlet port of the sample chamber from first concentration to second concentration. The first multiple measurement times, first concentration of carbon dioxide in a gas exiting the sample chamber is measured by using first gas analyzer and simultaneously measuring each of the first multiple measurement times. The second concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas entered the sample chamber by using a second gas analyzer and each of the first multiple measurement times is measured. The empty chamber assimilation rate value is measured by subtracting the second concentration values from the first concentration values at each of the corresponding measurement times. The photosynthesis capable sample is added in chamber with the photosynthesis capable sample in the chamber, continuously varying the concentration of carbon dioxide is introduced into the gas line from the first concentration to the second concentration, and during the continuously varying, second multiple measurement times are measured, third concentration of carbon dioxide in gas exiting the sample chamber using the first gas analyzer. The second multiple measurement times is measured, fourth concentration of carbon dioxide is added in the gas entering the sample chamber by using the second gas analyzer (40). The multiple second measurement times are measured, apparent assimilation rate value is measured by subtracting the fourth concentration values from the third concentration values at each of the corresponding measurement times and net assimilation rate value of the photosynthesis capable sample by subtracting the empty chamber assimilation value from the apparent assimilation value at each of the multiple second measurement times are measured.

   Gas exchange analysis system (10)
   Console (15)

The net assimilation rate value is determined by performing a linear regression on the empty chamber assimilation rate values, where GA2 refers to the second gas analyzer, m is the slope and b is a y intercept. AU LI S
   JIN X
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 2021C0587Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate compounds, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in presence of polyetheramide ionic liquid as organic catalyst under the conditions of carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-5.0 MPa and reaction temperature of 20-130degrees Celsius to obtain cyclic carbonate compounds, adding extraction solvent, separating catalyst by liquid/liquid phase separation, and removing supernatant from the solvent to obtain cyclic carbonate compound.
   USE - The cyclic carbonate compound is useful in cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxides.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyclic carbonate compound has high efficiency, and high selectivity; utilizes ionic liquid catalyst which assembles HBD group, organic base, nucleophile and polyether chain into single molecular structure, thereby ensuring efficient integration of multiple catalytic active sites; catalytic system has metal-free, solvent-free, and co-catalyst-free, multifunctional coupling and synergistic catalysis, high catalytic activity, excellent substrate compatibility and long-term stability of the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: When the polyetheramide ionic liquid organic catalyst catalyzes the cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide, the amount of catalyst is 0.01-1 mmol.% of the epoxide.    ADVANTAGE - The method does not require electric energy, or when there is a supply of electric energy, electrolysis hardly occurs under mild conditions (e.g. -350 mV (against standard hydrogen electrode)).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing a carbon compound involves culturing a bacterium containing at least one selected from the group consisting of acetic acid-producing bacteria and methanogens in a medium containing an inorganic carbon source and solid humus, fixing carbon dioxide, and recovering the carbon compound produced through the carbon dioxide fixation;
   (2) use of electron donor comprising solid humus humin, in the method or the production method; and
   (3) use of a medium comprising solid humus humin in the method or the production method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The culture is performed under anaerobic conditions or autotrophic conditions. The culture is a non-electroculture. The method involves producing acetic acid. The chemical cleaning is alkaline cleaning or acid cleaning. Preferred Organism: The acetic acid-producing bacterium comprises at least one bacterium chosen from Firmicutes, Proteobacteria, Bacteroidetes, Actinobacteria, Chloroflexi, Acidobacteria and Spirochaetes. Preferred Components: The solid humus humin is a chemical cleaning residue of soil. The carbon compound contains at least one acetic acid, methane and formic acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
