PN WO2021009346-A1; AT522823-A1; AT522823-B1
TI Production of carbon monoxide involves using oxide freshly prepared using salt of transition metal as metal oxide, and performing stoichiometric reaction of carbon dioxide and oxide in presence of steam at elevated temperature.
AU MUELLER D
   KNOLL C
   GRAVOGL G
   WEINBERGER P
   MILETICH-PAWLICZEK R
AE UNIV WIEN TECH (UWIE-C)
GA 202108528A
AB    NOVELTY - Production of carbon monoxide involves using an oxide freshly prepared using a salt of transition metal(s) as metal oxide(s) (I), and performing stoichiometric reaction of carbon dioxide and oxide (I) in the presence of a steam at an elevated temperature and/or at an elevated carbon dioxide gas pressure to obtain carbon monoxide and a metal oxide (II).
   USE - Production of carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and large-scale production of carbon monoxide with reduced energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of carbon monoxide involves using an oxide freshly prepared using a salt of transition metal(s) as metal oxide(s) of formula: MO (I), and performing stoichiometric reaction of carbon dioxide and oxide (I) in the presence of a steam at an elevated temperature and/or at an elevated carbon dioxide gas pressure to obtain carbon monoxide and a metal oxide of formula: M3O4 (II).
   M = transition metal chosen from Mn, Co, Fe, Ni and Cu.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reaction is performed in the presence of 0.1 vol.% or more, preferably 1-2 vol.% water vapor based on the amount of carbon monoxide gas. The reaction is performed at room temperature and at a pressure of 50 bar or more, or at a temperature of 50 degrees C or more and at a pressure of 28 bar or more, or at a temperature of 100 degrees C or more and at a pressure of 10 bar or more, or at a temperature of 150 degrees C or more and at a pressure of 2 bar or more, or at a temperature of 200 degrees C or more and at a normal pressure, or at a temperature of 500 degrees C or more and at a pressure of 8 bar or more. When oxide (I) is cobalt oxide and/or ferrous oxide, the reaction is performed at a temperature of 200 degrees C or more and at a normal pressure or above. When oxide (I) is manganese oxide, nickel oxide and/or copper oxide, the reaction is performed at a temperature of 300 degrees C or more and at a normal pressure or above. The oxide (I) is regenerated and recycled.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Treating coal involves pulverizing coal into coal            dust, pelletizing coal dust into coal pellet,            pretreating coal pellet, pretreating includes            pretreating including holding coal pellet in first            supercritical carbon dioxide environment.
AU GOODRICH B L
   KIM P
   TERRELL E
   WALTER J C
AE TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
GA 2020439245
AB    NOVELTY - Treating coal involves pulverizing the coal                into a coal dust, pelletizing the coal dust into a                coal pellet, pretreating the coal pellet. The                pretreating includes pretreating including holding                the coal pellet in a first supercritical carbon                dioxide environment at a first temperature and a                first pressure for a first period of time,                generating a treated pellet product; and removing                the treated pellet product from the supercritical                carbon dioxide environment.
   USE - Method for treating coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat coal with improved                handling and storage properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for treating carbonaceous feedstock, which                involves:
   (A) pulverizing the carbonaceous feedstock                into a dust;
   (B) pelletizing the dust into a carbonaceous                feedstock pellet;
   (C) pretreating the pellet, the pretreating                including holding the carbonaceous feedstock pellet                in a first supercritical carbon dioxide environment                at a first temperature and a first pressure for a                first period of time;
   (D) generating a treated pellet product;                and
   (E) removing the treated pellet product from                the first supercritical carbon dioxide                environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves storing the treated pellet product in an                  outdoor environment, transporting the treated                  pellet product in a manner that exposes it to an                  outdoor environment, gasifying the treated pellet                  product, and burning the treated pellet product.                  The pretreating operation involves creating a                  dried coal pellet by holding the coal pellet in                  the supercritical carbon dioxide environment at a                  first temperature and a first pressure for a                  first period of time; and converting the dried                  coal pellet into the treated pellet product by                  holding the dried coal pellet in a second                  supercritical carbon dioxide environment defined                  by a second temperature and a second pressure for                  a second period of time; where the first                  temperature is from the critical temperature of                  carbon dioxide up to the boiling point of water                  at the first pressure, the first pressure is from                  the critical pressure of carbon dioxide up to 30                  mpa, and the first period of time is from 1                  minute to 48 hours, and where the second                  temperature is from the boiling point of water at                  the second pressure up to 650 degrees C, the                  second pressure is from 7.39-30 mpa, and the                  second period of time is from 1 second to 48                  hours, and the treated pellet product generated                  is a char pellet. Preferred Components: The coal                  is selected from bituminous coal, sub-bituminous                  coal, lignite, and anthracite. The first                  temperature is from the critical temperature of                  carbon dioxide up to the boiling point of water                  at the first pressure, the first pressure is from                  the critical pressure of carbon dioxide up to 30                  mpa, and the first period of time is from 1                  minute to 48 hours, and the treated pellet                  product generated by the pretreating is a dried                  coal pellet.

TI Power cycle and propulsion system for underwater            aircraft, has turbine unit connected with cooling unit            such that source generating unit after heating of            supercritical carbon dioxide to do work by turbine unit            is discharged to cooling unit.
AU CHEN J
   ZHANG S
   WEI Z
AE SHANGHAI CHAOLIN POWER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202013794Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a power cycle module connected                with a pushing module. The power cycle module                comprises a cooling unit, a compression unit, a                heat source generating unit and a turbine unit that                are connected in turn. The cooling unit cools a                supercritical carbon dioxide in the compression                unit. The heat source generating unit generates                heat such that the heat source generation of the                supercritical carbon dioxide in preset time reaches                temperature of an inlet of a turbine unit. The                turbine unit is connected with the cooling unit                such that the heat source generating unit after                heating of the supercritical carbon dioxide to do                work by the turbine unit is discharged to the                cooling unit.
   USE - Power cycle and propulsion system for an                underwater aircraft (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system sets circulating medium i.e.                supercritical carbon dioxide, and reduces                circulation power consumption and improves heat                efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a power                cycle and propulsion system.

PN DE102018007918-A1
TI Method for performing regenerative carbon dioxide circulation and energy supply through heat pump based on Clausius-Rankine process, involves connecting cooling circuit with counterclockwise cooling circuit, which evaporates gas.
AU SEUFFERHELD J E
AE SEUFFERHELD J E (SEUF-Individual)
GA 201942028C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing a counterclockwise cycle process in a cooling circuit as represented in a pressure-enthalpy diagram. The cooling circuit is connected with a counterclockwise cooling circuit, which liquefies, heats and evaporates clockwise carbon dioxide high-pressure gas to drive a turbine (21-23). Heat energy of a heat pump is utilized to drive the turbine in a Clausius-Rankine process of a power station. The turbine is driven by adiabatic change of state of the carbon dioxide. The expanded carbon dioxide is liquefied, heated and evaporated in a closed circuit.
   USE - Method for performing regenerative carbon dioxide circulation and energy supply through a heat pump in a power station based on Clausius-Rankine process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing pressures of burning fossil fuels due to climate change and environmental pollution, thus improving working efficiency of the power station.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a counterclockwise cooling circuit.

The first solution's copper source concentration is 0.05-0.1 mol/L. The molar ratio of indium source to copper source is 1. The molar ratio of zinc source to copper source is 0.5. The molar ratio of sulfur source to copper source is 2.5-5.

PT P
PN CN113772801-A; CN216711695-U
TI Carbonic acid solution dosing system comprises reaction water pipeline, and diffuser arranged in raw water or water pipeline with water flow to be treated, and is located upstream of water flow, and channel for releasing carbonic acid solution is opened on half of sidewall of diffuser.
AU LIN X
AE HAWEI SHANGHAI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (HAWE-Non-standard)
GA 2021E9555N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonic acid solution dosing system comprises a mixer and a diffuser, and further comprises carbon dioxide gas pipeline (1), and reaction water pipeline. A water pump (4) is provided on the reaction water pipeline. The outlet of the water pump is connected to the inlet of the mixer. The carbon dioxide gas pipeline is connected to the inlet of the mixer between the mixer outlet and the diffuser inlet, there are annular coils or liquid storage tanks and long-distance pipes. The diffuser is arranged in the water to be treated. The diffuser is a hollow cylindrical object that is open at one end and closed at the other end. A channel for releasing carbonic acid solution is opened on the cylindrical side wall of the diffuser. The diffuser is arranged in the raw water or water pipeline with water flow to be treated, and is located upstream of the water flow, and a channel for releasing carbonic acid solution is opened on half of the sidewall of the diffuser.
   USE - As carbonic acid solution dosing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonic acid solution dosing system accurately adjusts the water quality, greatly reduces the generation of air bubbles and greatly improves the utilization rate of carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbonic acid solution dosing system comprises a mixer and a diffuser, and further comprises carbon dioxide gas pipeline (1), and reaction water pipeline. A water pump (4) is provided on the reaction water pipeline. The outlet of the water pump is connected to the inlet of the mixer. The carbon dioxide gas pipeline is connected to the inlet of the mixer between the mixer outlet and the diffuser inlet, there are annular coils or liquid storage tanks and long-distance pipes. A diffuser is arranged in the water to be treated. The diffuser is a hollow cylindrical object that is open at one end and closed at the other end. A channel for releasing carbonic acid solution is opened on the cylindrical side wall of the diffuser. The channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution is a small hole or a narrow gap, or a combination of the two. The channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution can generate a certain back pressure and make the supersaturated carbonic acid solution spray into the water to be treated with a certain outlet pressure, ensure that there is a pressure difference of more than 2 bar between the outlet pressure of the diffuser and the pressure of the water to be treated. The diffuser is arranged in the raw water or water pipeline with water flow to be treated, and is located upstream of the water flow, and a channel for releasing carbonic acid solution is opened on half of the sidewall of the diffuser. The channels for releasing the carbonic acid solution are one or more, or one or more rows. The diffuser is inserted into the raw water or water pipeline perpendicular to the direction of the water flow, and the side of the diffuser with the channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution is facing the upstream of the water flow. The carbonic acid solution in the diffuser can be sprayed out from the channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution in the opposite direction to the water flow and can produce a vortex to strengthen the mixing effect. Alternately, the diffuser is arranged in the pool, pond or sewage tank to be treated without water flow, and on the double side walls of the diffuser or the entire cylindrical side wall, there are multiple or multiple rows of carbonic acid solution releasing pipes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a whole structure schematic view of the invention a carbonic acid solution adding system.
   Liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Electronic vaporizer (2)
   Gas heater (3)
   Water pump (4)
   Mixer (5)
   Ph probe (7)
   Signal receiver (18)

TI Plant for biogas upgrading, comprises a cooling and condensation column, liquefaction column, heat exchanger, and filtration device.
AU ROVETA G
   MUSSINATTO A
AE CRIOTEC IMPIANTI SPA (CRIO-Non-standard)
GA 201926037G
AB    NOVELTY - Plant comprises a cooling and condensation column (10) provided with a biogas feed conduit (16), a head outlet conduit (18), and a bottom outlet conduit (20); a liquefaction column (26) into which the head outlet conduit (18) flows, from the top of which departs a discharge conduit (28) for incondensable gases, and from the bottom of which departs a discharge conduit (30) for a liquid flow; a heat exchanger (40) for cooling the liquid flow in the discharge conduit (30); and a filtration device for the liquid flow in the discharge conduit (30), which filtration device retains particles of solid carbon dioxide, is located downstream of the heat exchanger (40) and upstream of the division into a recirculation branch (32) and an outlet branch (34), and comprises greater than or equal to 2 units (42a, 42b) arranged in parallel and intended to be used alternately in operating and regeneration stages.
   USE - The plant and method are used for biogas upgrading (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plant and its operating procedure allow liquefied methane to be obtained from biogas in a more efficient and economical way than those of the prior art. The plant utilizes a cryogenic technology not only to liquefy methane, but also to separate it from the other biogas components, thus allowing the plant to be simplified and its operational efficiency to be increased. The plant also allows liquid carbon dioxide to be obtained simultaneously with a level of purity suitable for use in an industrial environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for biogas upgrading by the use of a plant above by cooling the biogas with carbon dioxide condensation in the cooling and condensation column (10) maintained at a temperature preferably comprised between -40 degrees C and -55 degrees C and a pressure of 30-50 bar gage by mass and heat exchange with a countercurrent flow of liquid carbon dioxide coming from the recirculating conduit (22), so that a liquid carbon dioxide flow is produced in the bottom outlet conduit (20) and a gas flow in the head outlet conduit (18); liquefying the gas flow coming from the head outlet conduit (18) in the liquefaction column (26) maintained at a temperature of -120 degrees C to -150 degrees C and a pressure of 30-50 bar gage by heat and mass exchange with a countercurrent flow of liquid methane coming from the recirculation branch (32), so as to produce a flow of incondensable gasses in the top discharge conduit (28) and a liquid flow in the bottom discharge conduit (30); cooling by the heat exchanger (40) the liquid flow in the discharge conduit (30), so as to cause the formation of solid particles of carbon dioxide suspended in the liquid flow; separating by the filtering device the solid particles from the liquid flow; and recirculating in the liquefaction column (26) through the recirculation branch (32) a fraction of the liquid flow, while the remaining fraction flows away through the outlet branch (34) into the storage tank (36) of liquid methane, where the filtering device comprises two units (42a, 42b) arranged in parallel intended to be alternately used in the operating stage and in the regeneration stage, each unit (42a, 42b) having a chamber (46) with a filtering septum (48) and being provided with openings for the entry (52) of the liquid to be filtered and the exit (54) of the filtered liquid, which are kept open during the operating stage and closed during the regeneration stage, and with openings for the entry (56) and the exit (58) of the biogas, which are kept open during the regeneration stage and closed during the operating stage.

PT P
PN CN110684713-A
TI Constructing in vitro recombinant epidermal model of diaper-like dermatitis involves preparing epidermal cells, culturing, regulating proliferation and differentiation of epidermal cell, culturing and obtaining recombinant epidermal model.
AU WU D
   ZHANG Y
   HU C
   HE X
   LU Y
AE GUANGDONG BIOCELL BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020074028
AB    NOVELTY - Method for constructing an in vitro recombinant epidermal model of diaper-like dermatitis involves (a) preparing epidermal cells, (b) performing subcutaneous culture of epidermal cells, (c) performing epidermal cell proliferation and differentiation regulation, and (d) constructing a diaper-like dermatitis reconstituted epidermal model by transferring the weakened barrier in vitro reconstituted epidermal model to a multiwell plate, adding an appropriate amount of culture medium II in advance to each well, then adding 0.15 vol.% sodium lauryl sulfate to the surface of the epidermal model of each well, after uniform application, placing in a 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, incubating for 24 hours, washing and obtaining a recombinant epidermal model of diaper-like dermatitis.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing an in vitro recombinant epidermal model of diaper-like dermatitis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method constructs diaper-like dermatitis in vitro reconstituted epidermal model which has a structure similar to a tissue slice under normal pathological conditions and similar changes in biochemical indicators, reduces species and individual differences, and improves true validity of the model.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for constructing an in vitro recombinant epidermal model of diaper-like dermatitis involves (a) preparing epidermal cells by taking primary keratinocytes, recovering, expanding, using P3-P7 generation cells, digesting with trypsin digestion solution to obtain a single cell suspension made with MCDB153 medium, inoculating into a culture flask at a cell density of 1x 105-5x 105 cells/ml, and culturing at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, (b) performing subcutaneous culture of epidermal cells by taking epidermal cells in logarithmic growth phase, using cell culture medium I to make epidermal cell suspensions with a cell density of 1.25x 106-2.5x 106 cells/ml, taking an appropriate amount, inoculating into a cell culture chamber containing a polycarbonate filter, placing at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for 0.5-2 hours, then placing the cell culture chamber in the epidermal model dish, adding 60-80 ml cell culture medium I to each dish, continuously culturing at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for 1-3 days, and changing the fluid every day to achieve subcutaneous culture of epidermal cells, where the cell culture medium I is prepared by taking MCDB153 as a basic medium, and adding insulin, isoprenaline, hydrocortisone, epidermal growth factor, adenine, transferrin, L-carnitine and calcium chloride, (c) performing epidermal cell proliferation and differentiation regulation by discarding the culture medium in the cell culture chamber in the above step (b), replacing the culture medium in the culture plate of the epidermal model with cell culture medium II, adding 50-70 ml cell culture medium II into each culture dish until the liquid surface and the upper surface of the cells in the cell culture chamber are at the same horizontal line, performing gas-liquid surface culture at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for 8-10 days, and changing the liquid every day during the culture to obtain weakened barrier in vitro recombinant epidermal model, where the cell culture medium II is prepared by taking MCDB153 medium and then adding insulin, isoprenaline, hydrocortisone, epidermal growth factor, adenine, transferrin, L-carnitine, L-serine, palmitic acid, oleic acid, argininosuccinic acid, calcium chloride, keratinocyte growth factor and granulocyte colony-stimulating factor, and (d) constructing a diaper-like dermatitis reconstituted epidermal model by transferring the weakened barrier in vitro reconstituted epidermal model to a multiwell plate, adding an appropriate amount of culture medium II in advance to each well, then adding 0.15 vol.% sodium lauryl sulfate to the surface of the epidermal model of each well, after uniform application, placing in a 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, incubating for 24 hours, washing and obtaining a recombinant epidermal model of diaper-like dermatitis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The cell culture medium I is based on MCDB153, supplemented with 0.5-5 mu g/ml insulin, 0.1-1 mu M isoprenaline, 0.02-0.2 mu g/ml hydrocortisone, 1-10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor, 10-50 mu g/ml adenine, 5-12 mu g/ml transferrin, 0.001-0.1 mM L-carnitine and 0.03-0.2 mM calcium chloride. The cell culture medium II is based on MCDB153 medium supplemented with 0.5-5 mu g/ml insulin, 0.1-1 mu M isoprenaline, 0.02-0.2 mu g/ml hydrocortisone, 1-10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor, 10-50 mu g/ml adenine, 5-12 mu g/ml transferrin, 0.001-0.1 mM L-carnitine, 0.01-0.1 mM L-serine, 0.01-0.05 mM palmitic acid, 0.01-0.05 mM oleic acid, 0.05-0.1 mM argininosuccinic acid, 1-1.5 mM calcium chloride, 1-10 ng/ml keratinocyte growth factor and 10-20 ng/ml granulocyte colony-stimulating factor. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves in the preparation of epidermal cells, taking primary keratinocytes, after recovery and expansion, and using the trypsin digestion solution to digest P3-P7 generation cells, where the trypsin digestion solution used to digest P3-P7 generation cells is 2.5% trypsin. The step (d) involves transferring the weakened barrier in vitro recombined skin model to a multiwell plate, adding 0.9 ml cell culture medium II to each well, adding 25 mu l sodium lauryl sulfate to the surface of the epidermal model in each well, after uniform application, placing in a 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, incubating for 24 hours, and then using Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline (DPBS) buffer solution to wash the remaining liquid on the tissue surface to obtain a recombinant epidermal model of diaper-like dermatitis.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN109746022-A; CN109746022-B
TI Preparing highly dispersed copper-zinc catalyst            useful for carbon dioxide reduction, comprises e.g.            preparing organic alcohol solution of copper nitrate,            adding certain amount of water and organic acid, and            stirring at room temperature.
AU LI W
   HU X
   GUAN Q
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 201944558V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly dispersed copper-zinc                catalyst comprises (i) preparing organic alcohol                solution of 10 wt.% copper nitrate, adding certain                amount of water and organic acid, and stirring at                room temperature for at least 1 hour to obtain a                mixed solution A, where the mass ratio of the                organic alcohol solution of copper nitrate to water                and organic acid is 20-50:1:1, (ii) transferring                the mixed solution A to hydrothermal reaction                kettle, reacting at constant temperature of                150-180&#176; C for 3-12 hours, and taking out                the reaction kettle and cooling to room temperature                to obtain a sample B, (iii) prepare zinc salt                methanol solution with mass fraction of 5%, mixing                with certain amount of sample B, and stirring at                room temperature for at least 30 minutes to obtain                sample C, where the sample B accounts for 20-60                wt.% of the zinc salt methanol solution, (iv)                preparing 2-methylimidazole methanol solution with                mass fraction of 5%, mixing with sample C, stirring                at room temperature for 6-24 hours.
   USE - The highly dispersed copper-zinc catalyst is                useful for carbon dioxide reduction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has excellent catalytic                activity; and greatly promotes the catalytic                activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing highly dispersed copper-zinc                catalyst comprises (i) preparing organic alcohol                solution of 10 wt.% copper nitrate, adding certain                amount of water and organic acid, and stirring at                room temperature for at least 1 hour to obtain a                mixed solution A, where the mass ratio of the                organic alcohol solution of copper nitrate to water                and organic acid is 20-50:1:1, (ii) transferring                the mixed solution A to hydrothermal reaction                kettle, reacting at constant temperature of                150-180&#176; C for 3-12 hours, and taking out                the reaction kettle and cooling to room temperature                to obtain a sample B, (iii) prepare zinc salt                methanol solution with mass fraction of 5%, mixing                with certain amount of sample B, and stirring at                room temperature for at least 30 minutes to obtain                sample C, where the sample B accounts for 20-60                wt.% of the zinc salt methanol solution, (iv)                preparing 2-methylimidazole methanol solution with                mass fraction of 5%, mixing with sample C, stirring                at room temperature for 6-24 hours, centrifuging,                thoroughly washing using methanol, and drying to                obtain sample D, where the molar ratio of                2-methylimidazole and zinc ion is 2-10:1, and (v)                heating the sample D in a tube furnace under                flowing inert gas atmosphere at a heating rate of                1-5&#176; C/minutes to 300-400&#176; C for                at least 2 hours, raising the temperature to                500-800&#176; C at temperature increase rate of                5-10&#176; C/minutes and maintaining for at                least 2 hours, and reducing to room temperature to                obtain the desired high-dispersion copper-zinc                catalyst, where the volumetric space velocity of                the inert gas is 600-1200/hour. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS                are also included for:
   producing highly-dispersed copper-zinc                catalyst by the above mentioned method; and
   use method of highly dispersed copper-zinc                catalyst for carbon dioxide reduction, comprising                (a) after grinding the prepared high-dispersion                copper-zinc catalyst, placing 0.4 g high-dispersion                copper-zinc catalyst in a constant temperature                range of the fixed-bed reactor, and raising the                temperature to 400-500&#176; C under flowing                hydrogen atmosphere for at least 2 hours, where the                volumetric velocity of hydrogen gas is                3000-9000/hour, and (b) changing the temperature to                a reaction temperature of 300-500&#176; C, and                introducing carbon dioxide gas, where the                volumetric velocity of carbon dioxide is 3000/hour                and the volume ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen                is 1:1-5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic alcohol is                  ethanol, ethylene glycol, polyethylene glycol,                  1,2-propanediol and/or glycerin. The organic acid                  is formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid                  and/or butyric acid. The zinc salt is zinc                  nitrate, zinc acetate and/or zinc chloride.                  Preferred Method: The method further comprises                  (i) preparing organic alcohol solution of 10 wt.%                  copper nitrate, adding certain amount of water                  and organic acid, and stirring at room                  temperature for at least 1 hour to obtain a mixed                  solution A, where the mass ratio of the organic                  alcohol solution of copper nitrate to water and                  organic acid is 20-50:1:1, (ii) transferring the                  mixed solution A to hydrothermal reaction kettle,                  reacting at constant temperature of                  150-180&#176; C for 3-12 hours, and taking                  out the reaction kettle and cooling to room                  temperature to obtain a sample B, (iii) prepare                  zinc salt methanol solution with mass fraction of                  5%, rapidly mixing with certain amount of sample                  B, stirring at room temperature for at least 30                  minutes, centrifuging, thoroughly washing, and                  drying to obtain sample C, where the molar ratio                  of 2-methylimidazole and zinc ion is 2-10:1 and                  the sample B accounts for 20-60 wt.% of the zinc                  salt methanol solution, (iv) heating the sample C                  in a tube furnace under flowing inert gas                  atmosphere at a heating rate of 1-5&#176;                  C/minutes to 300-400&#176; C for at least 2                  hours, raising the temperature to                  500-800&#176; C at temperature increase rate                  of 5-10&#176; C/minutes and maintaining for                  at least 2 hours, switching to an inert gas                  again, and reducing to room temperature to obtain                  the desired high-dispersion copper-zinc catalyst,                  where the volumetric space velocity of the inert                  gas is 600-1200/hour and the volumetric space                  velocity of ammonia gas is 300-600/hour.

PN CN114836260-A
TI Extracting arsenic in Antarctic krill oil involves            drying and breaking Antarctic krill to obtain Antarctic            krill meal, taking Antarctic krill meal as raw material            to carry out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction,            through liquid carbon dioxide standstill.
AU YANG F
   GUO F
   LI H
AE SHENZHEN QISHENGWEI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A3503W
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting arsenic in Antarctic krill oil                involves drying and breaking the Antarctic krill to                obtain Antarctic krill meal, taking Antarctic krill                meal as raw material to carry out supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction, through liquid carbon                dioxide standstill, infiltration, dynamic                extraction, extraction pressure is                &#8814;40MPa, adding ethanol solution to the                shrimp meal after supercritical extraction, heating                and refluxing secondary extraction after the                secondary extraction is completed, and removing the                solvent to obtain Antarctic krill oil.
   USE - Method for extracting arsenic in Antarctic                krill oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe and practical and has                stable quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure of                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is                  40-60Mpa. The extraction time of supercritical                  carbon dioxide is 0.5-1 hour. The extraction                  temperature of supercritical carbon dioxie                  extraction is 30-60&#8451;. The mixing ratio                  of the Antarctic krill meal and the ethanol                  solution is 2g:(4-8)mL. The volume concentration                  of ethanol is 75-95%. The time of heating to                  reflux is 1.5-2.0 hours. The water content of the                  Antarctic krill meal is 7&#177;2%. The                  particle size of the Antarctic krill meal is                  &#8804; 60 meshes. The phospholipid is more                  than 45g/100g, EP, 12g/100g, DHA is 6g/100g,                  astaxanthin is more than 30mg/Kg, and total                  arsenic is not detected.

PN CN113564215-A; US2022364128-A1
TI Preparing biosurfactant using carbon dioxide            and/or lignocellulose as substrate useful in            industries, comprises e.g. synthesizing biosurfactant            fermentation broth by fermentation broth under action            of synthetic biosurfactant microorganisms, and            extracting surfactant in crude biosurfactant.
AU XIA W
AE XIA W (XIAW-Individual)
   MIKROS BIOCHEM (MIKR-Non-standard)
GA 2021C5371R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biosurfactant using carbon dioxide                and/or lignocellulose as substrate comprises using                carbon dioxide-fixed microorganisms and/or                lignocellulose degrading microorganisms as                fermentation strains, and carbon dioxide and/or                lignocellulose as basic raw materials to obtain                fermentation broth through fermentation,                synthesizing the biosurfactant fermentation broth,                which is the crude biosurfactant by the                fermentation broth under the action of the                synthetic biosurfactant microorganisms, extracting                the surfactant in the crude biosurfactant to obtain                the biosurfactant, where the surfactants are                glycolipids and/or lipopeptides.
   USE - The biosurfactant is useful in industries e.g.                petroleum, food, daily chemical, environmental                protection and medicine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The carbon                  dioxide-fixed microorganism comprises Pyrococcus,                  Pseudomonas, Metallosphaera, Rhodospirillum,                  Chloroflexus, Cyanobacteria, Chlorella,                  Dunaliella, Nannochloropsis, Scenedesmus and/or                  Botryococcus. The lignocellulose degrading                  microorganisms comprises Sordaria, Pseudomona,                  Trametes, Irpex, Lenzites, Phanerochaete,                  Klebsiella, Ochrobactrum, Dysgonomonas,                  Sphingobacterium, Parabacteroides,                  Flavobacterium, Pleomorphomonas, Arcticibacter,                  Elizabethkingia, Neisseria, Mycobacterium,                  Trichodermareesei, Stenotrophomonas,                  Paenibacllus, Nocardiopsis, Nocardia, Bacillus,                  Rhizobium, Cellulomonas, Cellvibrio, Cytophaga,                  Alistipes, Aspergillus, Ruminofilibacter and/or                  Clostridium. The synthetic biosurfactant                  microorganism comprises Pseudomonas, Bacillus,                  Candida, Acinetobacter, Pantoea, Sphingomonas,                  Streptomyces, Rhodococcus, Pseudozyma,                  Ustilaginales and/or Moesziomyces. The                  lignocellulose comprises corn stalks, straw,                  leaves and/or wood chips.Preferred Method: The method comprises                  inoculating 1-10% carbon dioxide-fixed microbial                  seeds solution into the prepared first medium                  according to the volume ratio of the first                  medium, using carbon dioxide as the carbon                  source, where aeration rate of 0.01-0.4 vvm,                  culturing under light or no light conditions,                  controlling the temperature at 20-40degrees                  Celsius, where the stirring intensity is 100-300                  revolutions per minute, and the culture is 4-10                  days to obtain fermentation broth A, where the                  first medium comprises 0.1-0.5 wt.% potassium                  nitrate, 0.1-0.5 wt.% potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate, 0.01-0.2 wt.% magnesium sulfate                  heptahydrate, 0.01-0.05 wt.% calcium chloride                  dihydrate, 0.01-0.05 wt.% ferrous sulfate                  heptahydrate, 0.001-0.01 wt.% zinc sulfate                  heptahydrate, 0.001-0.01 wt.% manganese chloride                  tetrahydrate, 0.001-0.01 wt.% copper sulfate                  pentahydrate, 0.001-0.01 wt.% disodium edetate                  and 0.1-0.3 wt.% calcium carbonate, inoculating                  1-10% lignocellulose-degrading microorganisms                  according to the volume ratio of the second                  medium to the prepared second medium, and                  culturing with lignocellulose as carbon source,                  controlling the temperature at 20-40degrees                  Celsius, where the stirring intensity is 100-300                  revolutions per minute, the initial pH of the                  medium is 7-8.5, the amount of lignocellulose                  added is 2-10%, the medium volume is 50% (volume                  ratio), and culturing for 4-10 days to obtain                  fermentation broth B, where the second medium                  comprises 0.1-0.5 wt.% ammonium sulfate,                  0.01-0.05 wt.% magnesium sulfate heptahydrate,                  0.01-0.03 wt.% copper sulfate pentahydrate,                  0.001-0.005 wt.% manganese sulfate and 0.1-0.5                  wt.% calcium carbonate, and inoculating the                  microbial seed liquid of the fermented synthetic                  biosurfactant into the prepared third medium, and                  using the mixed fermentation broth obtained by                  mixing the fermentation broth A and the                  fermentation broth B as the carbon source, where                  the carbon source fermentation temperature is                  20-40degrees Celsius, the stirring intensity is                  100-300 revolutions per minute, the aeration rate                  is 0.1-0.4 vvm, and the culture is 3-10 days to                  obtain fermentation broth C, and the third medium                  comprises 0.1-1.4 wt.% sodium nitrate,                  0.002-0.006 wt.% ferrous chloride, 0.25-1.5 wt.%                  sodium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.25-1.8 wt.%                  potassium hydrogen phosphate, 0.005-0.015 wt.%                  magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.05-0.3 wt.%                  potassium chloride, 0.05-0.3 wt.% choline                  chloride, 0.001-0.1 wt.% yeast extract powder and                  trace elements containing zinc, manganese and                  calcium, and the pH is 6-7. The preparation of                  mixed fermentation broth comprises directly                  mixing the fermentation broth A and the                  fermentation broth B, sterilizing the mixed                  fermentation broth at high temperature to obtain                  mixed fermentation broth, or concentrating the                  fermentation broth A and the fermentation broth B                  and mixing, and sterilizing the mixed                  fermentation broth at high temperature to obtain                  mixed fermentation broth. The extraction process                  for extracting surfactants in crude                  biosurfactants comprises (1) adjusting the pH                  value of fermentation broth C to 8-10,                  centrifuging at high speed to remove bacteria and                  residual oil, and obtaining clear middle layer,                  (2) adjusting the pH of the clear liquid in the                  middle layer to 2-3, and refrigerating at                  4-10degrees Celsius for 24 hours, (3) collecting                  the precipitate through the ceramic membrane at                  4-10degrees Celsius, dissolving the precipitate                  with aqueous solution of pH more than 8, and                  passing through the ceramic membrane to obtain                  the biological surface active aqueous solution,                  and (4) vacuum concentrating 10-15 times of at                  50degrees Celsius.

   SONG K D

AE KOREA ELECTRO TECHNOLOGY RES INST (KOEL-C)

   USE - Insulating gas used for electrical insulation or arc extinguishing of electrical device (claimed).

TI Preparing copper sulfide nano-dot material for            electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, by            dissolving copper chloride and polyvinylpyrrolidone in            deionized water, stirring, dissolving sodium sulfide,            reacting, centrifuging and drying.
AU QIN Q
   LI C
   LIU X
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 202072802K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing copper sulfide nano-dot                material, involves (i) dissolving copper chloride                and polyvinylpyrrolidone in deionized water and                stirring until clear, (ii) dissolving sodium                sulfide in the obtained clear solution under                stirring condition, and (iii) reacting the obtained                mixed solution, centrifuging and drying to prepare                the product.
   USE - The copper sulfide nano-dot is used as a                catalyst in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts hydrothermal method to                prepare nano-dot material, and is simple to operate                and easy to implement. The nano-dot material has                excellent carbon dioxide reduction catalytic                activity and selectivity, and high selectivity to                methane as electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (i), the                  addition ratio of copper chloride,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone and deionized water is                  (20-30) mg:(50-70) mg:(2-8) ml, preferably 21                  mg:60 mg:5 ml. In step (ii), the addition ratio                  of copper chloride, polyvinylpyrrolidone, sodium                  sulfide and deionized water is (20-30) mg:(50-70)                  mg:(20-40) mg:(2-8) ml, preferably 21 mg:60 mg:30                  mg:5 ml. Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the                  reaction of the mixed solution is carried out at                  70-120 degrees C for 10-60 minutes. In step                  (iii), the product obtained by the reaction is                  centrifuged with ethanol. In step (iii), the                  drying is carried out at 30-80 degrees C for 2-6                  hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The molecular weight                  of polyvinylpyrrolidone is 8000, 24000, 30000, or                  90000, respectively.

TI Method for continuously extracting rare metal in            tail ore by supercritical carbon dioxide, involves            crystallizing and precipitating scattered metal ions in            form of elemental substances in decompression            separation tower, in order for gas to be reused.

   YUAN Y
   LI Y
   LI J
   LI Z
   QU G
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2022B4876R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing carbon dioxide gas                through a gas compressor (1). A targeted extraction                agent is transported from a storage tank (4) to a                pressurized mixing tower. The carbon dioxide gas is                fully mixed with a targeted extractant to achieve a                supercritical state. A supercritical mixed                extractant is entered into the pressurized                extraction tower and is fully mixed with the                tailings particles in a countercurrent manner in                the pressurized extraction tower. The tailings are                contacted and extracted. An extracted leaching                residue is discharged into a leaching residue                storage tank (8) for storage. The scattered metal                ions are crystallized and precipitated in the form                of elemental substances in a decompression                separation tower, in order for the gas to be reused                after being filtered by a gas filtering device                (10). The targeted extraction agent is selected                from catechol, crown ether, citric acid, tartaric                acid, nitrilotriacetic acid and oxalic acid.
   USE - Method for continuously extracting rare metal                in tail ore by supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the continuous operation                through the equipment design, greatly improves the                extraction efficiency of the rare metal in the                tailings, and improves the existing rare metal                harsh extraction condition. The method is simple                and continuous and is suitable for industrial                production and market popularization and                application. The supercritical carbon dioxide                coupling targeted to extract agent greatly improves                the solubility of the complex in supercritical                carbon dioxide, while realizing efficient leaching                of rare metal ion, and reaching the purpose of                separating rare metal crystal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                apparatus for realizing the method for continuously                extracting rare metal in tail ore by supercritical                carbon dioxide.

   3Pressure gauge
   4Storage tank

TI New Campylobacter species being isolated from caeca and preserved in carbon dioxide incubator under carbon dioxide tension.
AU DHAYANANTH B
   KUMAR A
   UPADHYAY A
   MAANSI
   PATHAK A P
   SINGH P
   SHARMA H
   SAINI S
   BISHT P
   NAGPAL A
   ROMA
   PARVEEN S

   KUMAR R
   SAXENA M K
   BALAJIR
   ABHINAYAK
   ASWATHY P
AE UNIV GB PANT AGRIC & TECHNOLOGY (UYGB-Non-standard)
GA 2022576468
AB    NOVELTY - A Campylobacter spp. is new, where the Campylobacter spp. isolated from caeca is confirmed biochemically and molecularly, then the pure culture is isolated and preserved in carbon dioxide incubator at 40 degrees C under 5% or less carbon dioxide tension for 24 days.
   USE - As Campylobacter spp..
   ADVANTAGE - The Campylobacter spp. is excellent in growth and viability, and exhibits effective preservation rate of 100% in a simple and cost-effective manner within a short period of time while reducing unnecessary usage of glycerol stocks and avoiding exposure of thermophilic organisms towards cold stress. The culture can be streaked at any point of time within 24 days without undergoing process such as revival. The Campylobacter spp. can remain alive and not enter into VBNC mode by modifying temperature conditions and carbon dioxide pressure inside the carbon dioxide chamber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) Campylobacter-specific amplicon of 816 base pairs having a base pair sequence of atctaatggcttaaccattaaac (SEQ ID NO: Not defined), and a base pair sequence of ggacggtaactagtttagtatt (SEQ ID NO: Not defined); and (2) process for preserving Campylobacter specially under controlled environment inside carbon dioxide incubator involves extracting genomic DNA from enriched broth using whole cell heat-lysis method, extracting whole cell DNA after enrichment from all samples by heat lysis (snap chill) method, centrifuging 1.5 ml aliquots of enriched broths 13000 g for 3 minutes, re-suspending the pellets in 100 l triple distilled water, heating in a boiling water bath for 10 minutes, cooling in ice, centrifuging at 13000 g for 3 minutes, using supernatants as a template for PCR amplification, storing the bacterial lysate for further use, diluting supernatant with trisaminomethane-EDTA (TE) buffer (1:10 volume/volume) and storing at -20 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Isolation (Disclosed): The Campylobacter spp. is isolated by cutting caeca, taking a loop-full of caecal content, directly plating on modified charcoal cefoperazone deoxycholate agar (mCCDA) plate according to ISO 10272:2006 standard, incubating the plates at 40 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in a carbon dioxide incubator for 48 hours, subjecting characteristic Campylobacter colonies appearing on mCCDA plates to biochemical and molecular tests for confirmation, sub-culturing Campylobacter spp., picking suspected colonies and again sub-culturing on mCCDA plate to isolate pure colonies. Preferred Method: The pure culture of Campylobacter is stored inside carbon dioxide incubator in viable and culturable mode for 24 days. The culture is streaked by choosing any of following gap intervals of 2 days, 4 days, 6 days, 8 days, and till 24th day after which no growth will be visible representing that the organism has undergone viable but non culturable (VBNC) mode, where Campylobacter isolates are done on all the isolates since survivability rate is 100% for entire period of 24 days inside carbon dioxide incubator. The carbon dioxide incubator, carbon dioxide supply, and continuous electricity are needed for storage of Campylobacter isolates for 24 days. The isolates are preserved in glycerol stocks which is harmful for thermophilic organisms since it shows lesser recovery rates after being kept at -20 degrees C or -80 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

The controller is operable to generate the respective control signals in response to the respective gas signals.
   USE - Poultry stunning system.
   ADVANTAGE - The poultry stunning system is provided in which poultry in cages are moved through multiple stages of controlled atmosphere stunning (CAS) with increasing concentrations of carbon dioxide (CO2), or possibly another non-oxygen gas or blend of non-oxygen gases, while minimizing losses of the CO2 or other non-oxygen gases. The subsequent chambers having more elevated levels of CO2 or other breathable non-oxygen gas permit an operator to achieve an irreversible stun condition, prior to processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of stunning poultry with carbon dioxide gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the poultry stunning system.
   Poultry stunning system (10)

   Final stun chamber (16)
   Open top (18)
   Controller (32)

AU CUI C
   HUANG C
   LIU H
   WEI W
AE NINGBO AUX ELECTRIC CO LTD (AUXS-C)
GA 2020549241
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S100) indoor                temperature. The operation is started (S200) to                detect the carbon dioxide concentration, and the                indoor temperature is recorded at the time when the                indoor temperature satisfies the first preset                condition. A new fan is started (S300) and the                fresh air is introduced while regulating the indoor                temperature when the carbon dioxide concentration                exceeds the upper limit. The new fan is turned off                to regulate the indoor temperature when the carbon                dioxide concentration is not decreased to the lower                limit, and the indoor temperature is met the second                preset condition.
   USE - Indoor fresh air control method of air                conditioner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The detection of carbon dioxide concentration                is conducted while adjusting the introduction of                fresh air, when the indoor temperature meets the                comfort requirements, so as to avoid the rapid                decrease of indoor temperature caused by the                introduction of fresh air and improve indoor air                quality. The introduction of fresh air can easily                cause large changes in indoor temperature, and                improve user comfort experience.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an indoor fresh air control device;                and
   (2) a computer readable storage medium storing                program for performing the indoor fresh air                control.

   Step for obtaining indoor temperature                (S100)
   Step for starting to detect the carbon                dioxide concentration, and recording the indoor                temperature at the time when the indoor temperature                satisfies the first preset condition (S200)
   Step for starting new fan and introducing                fresh air while regulating the indoor temperature                when the carbon dioxide concentration exceeds the                upper limit (S300)

PN CN107933038-A
TI Preparing plastic wrap film with high penetration ratio of carbon dioxide/oxygen comprises e.g. adding polyvinyl alcohol into alcohol, soaking, filtering, drying, adding deionized water, heating and stirring.
AU CHEN Q
   GUAN D
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201832834J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing plastic wrap film with high penetration ratio of carbon dioxide/oxygen comprises e.g. adding polyvinyl alcohol into alcohol, soaking, filtering, drying, adding deionized water, heating and stirring to obtain polyvinyl alcohol colloid, adding propylene carbonate into polyvinyl alcohol colloid, stirring to obtain mixed colloid, coating prepared mixed colloid on the film surface of polyethylene film having micropore structure to obtain colloid/polyethylene composite film, overlapping, pressing, and bonding tightly together.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing plastic wrap film with high penetration ratio of carbon dioxide/oxygen.
   ADVANTAGE - The plastic wrap film: contains polyethylene film, having 40-60% surface porosity, 100-200 nm aperture, and 10-20 mu m hole depth; reduces concentration of carbon dioxide; can maintain environment of low oxygen; ensures long shelf life of fruits. The method is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing plastic wrap film with high penetration ratio of carbon dioxide/oxygen comprises adding polyvinyl alcohol into alcohol, soaking, filtering, drying, adding deionized water, heating and stirring to obtain polyvinyl alcohol colloid, using propylene carbonate as carbon dioxide adsorbent, adding propylene carbonate into polyvinyl alcohol colloid, forced stirring uniformly to mix uniformly to obtain mixed colloid, taking polyethylene film having micropore structure, coating prepared mixed colloid on the film surface using coating machine to obtain colloid/polyethylene composite film, overlapping obtained colloid/polyethylene composite film with polyethylene film having micro-pore structure not coated with colloid, pressing by roller, and bonding tightly together under the action of the polyvinyl alcohol colloid to obtain double-layer composite film. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plastic wrap film with high penetration ratio of carbon dioxide/oxygen, prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The mixed colloid comprises 20-30 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, 40-60 pts. wt. de-ionized water and 20-30 pts. wt. propylene carbonate. Preferred Method: In the method, the soaking time is 12-15 hours. In the method, the heating temperature is 50-60 degrees C. In the method, the coating thickness accuracy of the coating machine is 0.01-0.02 mm and the location precision is 0.02-0.03 mm. In the method, the coating thickness of the mixed glue is 0.1-0.2 mm. In the method, the roll extrusion adopts three-stage extrusion, roller gap is 0.3-0.4 mm, 0.2-0.25 mm and 0.1-0.15 mm respectively.

PN CN107552000-A; CN107552000-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent involves performing modified Hummers method by using graphite powder to prepare graphene oxide solution, adding sodium hydroxide and sodium chloride into graphene oxide solution and stirring.
AU CHEN X
   SONG J
   DENG X
   YAN Y
AE JIANGSU YOUNA YOUSHENG NEW MATERIAL CO L (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201804901P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent involves performing modified Hummers method by using graphite powder to prepare graphene oxide solution, adding sodium hydroxide and sodium chloride into the graphene oxide solution and stirring under ultrasonic for 2 hours and adding diluted hydrochloric acid to neutralize. The obtained product is centrifuged and washed by de-ionized water to obtain product solution and taken in oil bath and added ethanediamine. The obtained product is stirred vigorously, refluxed and reacted for 24 hours, added acetic acid and stirred.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon dioxide adsorbent in cost-effective manner, is suitable for large-scale production, realizes the control of carbon dioxide adsorption and desorption, and reduces the absorption material regenerating energy consumption by controlling the humidity and has high product conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent involves performing modified Hummers method by using graphite powder to prepare graphene oxide solution, adding sodium hydroxide and sodium chloride into the graphene oxide solution and stirring under ultrasonic for 2 hours and adding diluted hydrochloric acid to neutralize. The obtained product is centrifuged and washed by de-ionized water to obtain product solution and taken in oil bath and added ethanediamine. The obtained product is stirred vigorously, refluxed and reacted for 24 hours, added acetic acid and stirred. The 2,3-epoxypropyl trimethylammonium chloride is added to the obtained product and stirred in oil bath. The obtained product is washed with sodium carbonate solution, replaced the chlorine ion in the solution through ion exchange function, used deionized water to remove impurity and dried in vacuum drying cabinet to obtain carbon dioxide adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sodium carbonate solution is saturated sodium carbonate solution. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of natural graphite powder, sodium nitrate powder and potassium permanganate is 1-2:2-8:1-10. The ratio of natural graphite powder and concentrated sulfuric acid is 1g:26ml. The ratio of natural graphite powder and sulfuric acid is 1g:46ml. The particle of natural graphite powder is 2000 meshes, 4000 meshes, 6000 meshes and 8000 meshes. The ratio of sodium hydroxide, sodium chloroacetate and diluted graphene oxide solution is 50mg:50mg:1ml. The bath temperature is 75-85 degrees C. The vacuum drying box is 55-65 degrees C for 24 hours. Preferred Process: The natural graphite powder and sodium nitrate powder are mixed into the concentrated sulfuric acid, reacted for 2-4 hours under ice bath condition and added potassium permanganate at low temperature for 2 hours. The obtained product is poured under oil bath at 35 degrees C for 1 hours under ice bath condition, added de-ionized water and heated to 98 degrees C for 30 minutes. The oxidation reaction is stopped, added hydrogen peroxide and de-ionized water, stirred for 1 hours, diluted by hydrochloric acid and de-ionized water to remove impurity ions and centrifuged to obtain graphene.

TI Process for membrane permeation treatment of            methane, carbon dioxide-containing biogas stream used            for producing methane-rich gas, involves providing            plant, supplying first module with compressed biogas            stream, separating into first retentate, recycling            second, third permeate to inlet of compressor.

   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2022C1488S
AB    NOVELTY - Process for membrane permeation treatment of                methane and carbon dioxide-containing biogas stream                involves providing plant which comprises compressor                and membrane separation unit which comprises first                and second modules, each comprises at least one                membrane which is more permeable to carbon dioxide                than methane, where first module is adapted and                configured to receive compressed biogas stream and                separate into first permeate, second permeate,                third permeate, and first retentate, and second                module is adapted and configured to receive first                permeate from first module and separate into fourth                permeate and second retentate; compressing biogas                stream, compressor producing compressed biogas                stream; supplying first module with compressed                biogas stream and separating supplied compressed                biogas stream into first retentate which is                enriched in methane relative to biogas stream and                first, second, and third permeates, each are                enriched in carbon dioxide relative to biogas                stream.
   USE - Process for membrane permeation treatment of                methane and carbon dioxide-containing biogas stream                used for producing methane-rich gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables to allow production of                methane-enriched gas stream to control methane                loss; improves methane yield compared to system                containing two membrane stages; reduces proportion                of recycled CO2; improves process for membrane                separation of methane and carbon dioxide contained                in biogas which obtain high-purity methane, with                very good yield, and reduces operating cost of                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for membrane permeation treatment of                methane and carbon dioxide-containing biogas stream                involves providing plant which comprises compressor                and membrane separation unit which comprises first                and second modules, each comprises at least one                membrane which is more permeable to carbon dioxide                than methane, where first module is adapted and                configured to receive compressed biogas stream and                separate into first permeate, second permeate,                third permeate, and first retentate, and second                module is adapted and configured to receive first                permeate from first module and separate into fourth                permeate and second retentate; compressing biogas                stream, compressor producing compressed biogas                stream; supplying first module with compressed                biogas stream and separating supplied compressed                biogas stream into first retentate which is                enriched in methane relative to biogas stream and                first, second, and third permeates, each are                enriched in carbon dioxide relative to biogas                stream, first permeate being richer in methane than                second or third permeates; supplying second module                with first permeate and separating supplied first                permeate into second retentate which is enriched in                methane relative to first permeate and fourth                permeate which is enriched in carbon dioxide                relative to first permeate; and recycling second                retentate and third permeate to inlet of compressor                to produce compressed biogas stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process for membrane                  permeation treatment of methane and carbon                  dioxide-containing biogas stream involves                  prepurifying biogas stream upstream of compressor                  to dry biogas stream, reduce carbon-dioxide (CO2)                  concentration of biogas stream, or removal                  volatile organic compounds from biogas stream;                  measuring methane concentration of first                  retentate; setting pressure of first retentate as                  function of measured methane concentration, where                  biogas stream is produced by methanizer and                  measuring pressure of first retentate; comparing                  measured pressure with target pressure value;                  sending first retentate back to methanizer when                  measured pressure is lower than target pressure                  value; producing biogas stream by methanizer and                  measuring methane concentration of first                  retentate; comparing measured concentration with                  target concentration value; sending first                  retentate back to methanizer when measured                  concentration is lower than target concentration                  value, where each membrane of first and second                  modules have same carbon dioxide to methane                  selectivity, and first and second modules                  comprises membrane with first carbon dioxide to                  methane selectivity which is different from                  another membrane or membranes of first and second                  modules.

AU KOEPKE M
   LIEW F
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 2022A0889A
AB    NOVELTY - Recombinant C1-fixing bacteria comprises a                nucleic acid encoding a group of exogenous enzymes                comprising &#946;-alanine pyruvate                aminotransferase and malonic semialdehyde                reductase.
   USE - Recombinant C1-fixing bacteria for producing                3-hydroxypropionate (3-HP) from a carbon                source.
   ADVANTAGE - The recombinant C1-fixing bacteria improves                production of 3-HP and acquires ability to generate                malonic semialdehyde by transamination reaction                with pyruvate and &#946;-alanine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   a method for converting carbon monoxide and                carbon dioxide into 3-hydroxyproprionate (3-HP) by                passing a gaseous carbon monoxide-containing and/or                carbon dioxide-containing substrate to a bioreactor                containing a culture of a recombinant C1-fixing                bacteria in a culture medium such that the bacteria                convert carbon dioxide and/or carbon dioxide to                3-HP; and recovering the 3-HP from the bioreactor;                and
   a method for culturing bacteria by growing                bacteria in a medium comprising a gaseous carbon                source, where carbon source comprises carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The bacteria further                  comprises nucleic acid encoding a group of                  exogenous enzymes comprising pyruvate                  carboxylase, aspartate aminotransferase, and                  aspartate decarboxylase, where nucleic acid is                  operably linked to a promoter. The bacteria                  further comprises nucleic acid encoding a group                  of exogenous enzymes comprising alanine                  dehydrogenase and 2,3-alanine aminomutase, where                  nucleic acid is operably linked to a promoter.                  The bacteria are selected from Clostridium                  autoethanogenum, Clostridium ljungdahlii,                  Clostridium ragsdalei, Clostridium                  carboxidivorans, Clostridium drakei, Clostridium                  scatologenes, Clostridium aceticum, Clostridium                  formicoaceticum, Clostridium magnum,                  Butyribacterium methylotrophicum, Acetobacterium                  woodii, Alkalibaculum bacchii, Blautia producta,                  Eubacterium limosum, Moorella thermoacetica,                  Moorella thermautotrophica, Sporomusa ovata,                  Sporomusa silvacetica, Sporomusa sphaeroides,                  Oxobacter pfennigii, and Thermoanaerobacter                  kivui. The bacteria further comprises                  nucleic acid encoding acetyl-coenzyme A                  carboxylase, where nucleic acid is codon                  optimized for expression in the bacteria. The                  acetyl-coenzyme A carboxylase is derived from                  genus Clostridium, Metallosphaera, Sulfolobus, or                  Chloroflexus. The nucleic acid encoding a group                  of exogenous enzymes comprising                  &#946;-alanine pyruvate aminotransferase and                  malonic semialdehyde reductase is codon optimized                  for expression in the bacteria. The bacteria                  further comprises a nucleic acid encoding a group                  of exogenous enzymes comprising aldehyde                  ferrodoxin oxidoreductase and alcohol                  dehydrogenase, where nucleic acid is operably                  linked to a promoter. The nucleic acid encoding a                  group of exogenous enzymes enable production of                  1,3-propanediol. The 3-HP is converted into a                  material selected from acrylic acid,                  acrylonitrile, paint, paper, adhesives, textiles,                  specialty coatings, ink, superabsorbent polymer                  polyacrylates, 1,3-propanediol, solvents,                  adhesives, cosmetics, polytrimethylene                  terephthalate, 3-hydroxypropionaldehyde, foods,                  feed additives, and its combination. The bacteria                  is cultured by growing bacteria in a medium                  comprising an energy source. The culturing is                  strictly anaerobic and strictly anaerobic. The                  carbon source comprises an industrial waste                  product or off-gas. The energy source comprises                  an industrial waste product or off-gas.

AU YANG S
   YANG G W
AE YANG G W (YANG-Individual)
GA 202295844T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inputting supercritical                fluid carbon dioxide to a turbine through a                diffuser, and changing the phase into an ideal gas                through the diffuser. A turbine exhaust is cooled                to a low temperature below zero in an exhaust                cooler. The exhaust is compressed by a compressor,                and the exhaust is passed into a carbon dioxide                condenser. The liquid carbon dioxide is pressurized                to a supercritical liquid operating temperature,                and a refrigerant condenser is provided to cool the                refrigerant. The refrigerant having absorbed heat                is transferred to the refrigerator                compressor.
   USE - Supercritical fluid carbon dioxide gas turbine                operating utilized as turbine of coal-fired power                plant and a nuclear power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables generating heat source from                the exhaust of a general gas turbine, a coal boiler                and a nuclear nuclear reactor so as to improve                thermal efficiency of the general steam turbine or                the gas turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the flow                of the working fluid.

PN CN114573531-A
TI Synthesizing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid involves            reacting hydroxymethylfurfural as raw material with            N-heterocyclic carbene coordination metal catalyst and            carbon dioxide to obtain 1,1-difluoroethane and            dissolving diformylfuran, N-heterocyclic carbene and            alkali in solvent.
AU GUO K
   SHEN L
   LI Y
   HE W
   LIU F
   JI D
AE NANJING ADVANCED BIOMATERIAL & PROCESS (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202287986V
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid (FDCA)                involves (i) taking hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) (I)                as raw material and reacting under the                N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) coordination metal                catalyst and carbon dioxide to obtain                1,1-difluoroethane (DFE) (II), (ii) dissolving                diformylfuran (DFF), ligand N-heterocyclic carbene                (NHC) and alkali in the solvent and reacting under                the atmosphere of carbon dioxide to obtain FDCA                compound (III).
   USE - Synthesizing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical,                environmentally-friendly and efficiently produces                2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid (FDCA)                involves (i) taking hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) of                formula (I) as raw material and reacting under the                N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) coordination metal                catalyst and carbon dioxide to obtain                1,1-difluoroethane (DFE) of formula (II), (ii)                dissolving diformylfuran (DFF), ligand                N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) and alkali in the                solvent and reacting under the atmosphere of carbon                dioxide to obtain FDCA compound of formula                (III).
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  dissolving the metal catalyst, N-heterocyclic                  carbene and alkali in a solvent, heating under an                  inert atmosphere at 40-70&#8451;, stirring,                  reacting, and isolating a solid, washing the                  obtained solid with ethanol and acetone, and                  drying to obtain the N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC)                  coordinated metal catalyst.Preferred Components: The N-heterocyclic                  carbene (NHC) coordination metal catalyst is                  selected from ruthenium/N-heterocyclic carbene,                  rhodium/N-heterocyclic carbene, and                  palladium/N-heterocyclic carbene. The metal                  catalyst is one selected from ruthenium on                  carbon, rhodium on carbon or palladium on carbon.                  The N-heterocyclic carbene is at least one                  selected from                  1,3-bis(2,4,6-trimethylphenyl)imidazolium                  chloride (IMes-HCl),                  1,3-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)imidazolium                  chloride (IPr-HCl), 1,3-dimesitylimidazolinium                  chloride (SIMes-HCl), and                  N,N'-(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)dihydroimidazolium                  chloride. The alkali is one of potassium                  tert-butoxide or sodium tert-butoxide. The                  solvent is one of toluene or tetrahydrofuran                  (THF).Preferred Properties: The mass ratio of the                  metal catalyst to hydroxymethylfurfural is                  0.05-0.15:1. The mass ratio of metal catalyst,                  N-heterocyclic carbene and alkali is 1:1-3:2-6.                  The molar ratio of ligand N-heterocyclic carbene,                  alkali and 1,1-difluoroethane is                  0.1-0.3:0.15-0.6:1.Preferred Conditions: The step (i) is                  carried out at 130-150&#8451; for 4-18 hours                  at a reaction pressure of 0.1-4 MPa. The step                  (ii) is carried out at 25&#177;5&#8451;                  for 1-3 hours at normal pressure.
