TI Crystallizing oxide film for semiconductor            photoelectric integrated chip, involves preparing            amorphous oxide thin film and silica layer on            semiconductor substrate, controlling carbon dioxide            laser emission, converging beam and irradiating.
AU BI L
   CAI S
   YANG Y
   YAN W
   QIN J
   DENG L
AE UNIV CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UEST-C)
GA 202194641Q
AB    NOVELTY - Crystallizing oxide film, involves (S1)                preparing at least one layer of amorphous oxide                thin film and at least one layer of silica layer on                semiconductor substrate, where total thickness of                amorphous oxide thin film and silica layer is in                nano level, (S2) controlling carbon dioxide laser                emitted by laser to be collimated using basic                optical element through constructed carbon dioxide                laser annealing path device, expanding,                homogenizing and converging beam, irradiating                processed carbon dioxide laser to target area, (S3)                adjusting energy and scanning rate of carbon                dioxide laser emitted by laser and irradiating                amorphous oxide thin film to be crystallized in                target region.
   USE - Crystallizing oxide film for semiconductor                photoelectric integrated chip.
   ADVANTAGE - The amorphous oxide film has varying                absorptivity and thermal conductivity and low                absorption rate, and silica layer has high                absorption rate. The method achieves controllable                and uniform annealing of amorphous oxide film and                is compatible with existing complementary metal                oxide semiconductor process. The polycrystalline                oxide film has excellent photoelectric                performance.

PN CN112609092-A; CN112609092-B
TI Comprehensively recovering calcium and arsenic            from calcium arsenate/calcium arsenite precipitates            comprises decomposing carbon dioxide process by mixing            calcium arsenate and/or calcium arsenite precipitate            with water and concentrating.
AU YANG D
   FU W
   JIA Z
   LIU J
   MA Y
   ZOU W
   LI Y
   XIE T
   ZHANG H
   MU X
   DAI L
   DI Z
AE YUNNAN CHIHONG ZN & GE CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
   KUNMING METALLURGICAL RES INST (KUNM-Non-standard)
GA 202138523M
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensively recovering calcium and arsenic                from calcium arsenate/calcium arsenite precipitates                comprises (a) decomposing carbon dioxide process by                mixing calcium arsenate and/or calcium arsenite                precipitate with water for slurry adjustment,                leaching at 25-100 degrees C, introducing carbon                dioxide gas to react calcium arsenate and/or                calcium arsenite precipitate with aqueous solution                of carbon dioxide to obtain mixed slurry,                separating mixed slurry from liquid to solid to                obtain calcium carbonate solids and                arsenic-containing solutions, drying calcium                carbonate solids and dehydrating to obtain calcium                carbonate products, (b) concentrating and                crystallizing arsenic-containing solution-drying                process by concentrating arsenic-containing                solution and crystallizing, drying to obtain                arsenic pentoxide or arsenic(iii)oxide and (c)                mixing obtained arsenic pentoxide or                arsenic(iii)oxide solid into carbon for high                temperature carbothermal reduction to obtain metal                arsenic and carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensively                recovering calcium and arsenic from calcium                arsenate/calcium arsenite precipitates.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes harmless, resourceful and                environmentally friendly disposal of calcium and                arsenic precipitates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the                  molar ratio of carbon dioxide and calcium in the                  calcium arsenate and/or calcium arsenite                  precipitate is 1-10:1. The arsenic content in the                  calcium carbonate is less than 0.5%. In the step                  (c), the reducing agent is graphite, coke or coal                  and the amount of reducing agent is 0.1-5 times                  the molar ratio of C/As. Preferred Method: In the                  step (a), carbon dioxide is introduced for 30-600                  minutes, where number of stages of carbon dioxide                  conversion and decomposition is 1-10. In the step                  (b), the concentration of crystallization is                  carried out at 100-200 degrees C for 1-24 hours.                  The carbon dioxide gas obtained in step (c is                  returned to the step (a) carbon dioxide                  decomposition process. In the step (c),                  carbothermic reduction is carried out at 300-1200                  degrees C for 10-600 minutes.

PN CN110724831-A; CN210765457-U
TI Carbon recycling system for industrial recycling of zinc to produce zinc oxide, comprises primary zinc recovery and refining unit, which is mainly composed of rotary hearth furnace, heat recovery device and dust removal device.
AU GUO L
   LUO B
   LUO L
   YANG Y
   FAN H
   ZHANG H
AE CISDI THERMAL & ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 202010532E
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon recycling system comprises primary zinc recovery and refining unit, which is mainly composed of a rotary hearth furnace, a heat recovery device and a dust removal device which are used for the production of zinc oxide and metallized products and by-products from secondary zinc-containing materials with zinc content of 5%. The secondary zinc recovery and refining unit comprises desalination device, ammonia leaching reactor, purification reactor, crystallization reactor, rinsing tank, and drying and calcining device connected in this order. The carbon dioxide-containing flue gas is treated in carbon dioxide gas by carbon dioxide recovery unit. The carbon dioxide concentration is 50%, dust concentration is less than or equal to 5mg/Nm3.
   USE - Carbon recycling system for industrial recycling of zinc to produce zinc oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon recycling system reduces industrial flue gas emissions which is beneficial to environmental protection, solves source of carbon dioxide during carbonization and crystallization, and improves economy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide containing flue gas is produced by performing drying and calcining process in the primary furnace and the secondary zinc recovery refining unit of the collected zinc recovery refining unit, mixing, cooling, separating, dehydrating, and pressurizing by the carbon dioxide recovery unit. The ammonia leaching process in the secondary zinc recovery and refining unit is recycled as a crystallization agent. The method involves passing carbon dioxide gas into the ammonia extraction process in two stages, slowing down the flow of carbon dioxide gas so that the ratio of the molar concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas to the molar concentration of total ammonia in the zinc ammonia complexing solution is 1-1.5, so that the pH of the crystallization residual liquid is 8.0-8.5, speed up flow of carbon dioxide gas, so that the ratio of the molar concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas to the molar concentration of total ammonia in the zinc ammonia complex solution is 1.5-3.0, until end of the crystallization is reached, and pH of the crystallization residual liquid is 6.5-8.0. The crystallization residual liquid produced in the carbonization and crystallization process of the secondary zinc recovery and refining unit is calcined and calcium carbonate produced by the calcination treatment enters the carbon dioxide recovery unit for recycling, and calcium oxide produced is returned to calcification for recycling the reactor.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-A01; M25-B; M25-E; M25-G27; M25-J

AU WANG Y
   WANG N
   ZHAO K
   ZHANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
   HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 2018209355
AB    NOVELTY - A 2,6-bis(2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl)pyridine oxime 2-furanmethylamine zinc complex catalyst (I) is new.
   USE - 2,6-Bis(2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl)pyridine oxime 2-furanmethylamine zinc complex catalyst used for preparing cyclohexene polycarbonate by copolymerizing carbon dioxide with cyclohexene oxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high catalyzing efficiency, good reaction selectivity, mild catalytic reaction condition, and convenient operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A 2,6-bis(2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl)pyridine oxime 2-furanmethylamine zinc complex catalyst of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) application of (I) in preparing cyclohexene polycarbonate by copolymerizing carbon dioxide with cyclohexene oxide comprising placing (I) in vacuum-dried reaction vessel, purging with carbon dioxide to normal pressure, adding epoxycyclohexane to reactor, heating to 60-150 degrees C, charging carbon dioxide to pressure at 1-6 MPa, stirring mixture for 6-50 hours, cooling to room temperature to stop reaction, removing reaction solution, distilling unreacted epoxycyclohexane off, and subjecting residue to post-treatment to obtain cyclohexene carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The (I) is prepared by dissolving 2-furanmethylamine and 2,6-bis(2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl)pyridine in methanol separately, adding 2,6-bis(2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl)pyridine solution dropwise to 2-furanmethylamine solution at room temperature, heating mixture under reflux with stirring for 0.5-1 hour after addition, washing ether, drying to obtain 2,6-bis(2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl)pyridine 2-furanmethylamine ligand, placing ligand in container, evaporating under vacuum, filling with nitrogen, adding toluene to dissolve, placing solution in ice bath, adding diethyl zinc in toluene solution, stirring for 0.5-1 hour, stirring at room temperature for 1 hour, and removing solvent and unreacted diethylzinc under vacuum. Preferred Component: The molar ratio of 2-furanmethylamine and 2,6-bis(2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl)pyridine is 1:0.4-0.6. The molar ratio of 2,6-bis(2-aldehyde phenoxymethyl)pyridine 2-furanmethylamine ligand and diethyl zinc is 1:1-1.25. The mass ratio of (I) and epoxycyclohexane is 0.001-0.01:1. Preferred Method: The post-treatment is carried out by dissolving residue in dichloromethane, filtering, washing orderly with diluted acid, diluted alkali and distilled water, adding methanol dropwise, separating white solid, filtering, and vacuum-drying residue.

TI Apparatus for reducing air pollutant in ship, has branch pipe installed in exhaust gas Exhaust pollutant reduction device including carbon dioxide capture device for removing contained carbon dioxide.
AU LEE S J
   CHOI J
   HA D
AE SAMSUNG HEAVY IND CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 2022118458
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has an exhaust pipe (10) for discharging exhaust gas generated from a combustion engine (100) that generates power by burning liquefied natural gas. A methane oxidation catalyst reactor (20) is installed in the exhaust pipe and oxidizing unburned methane contained in the exhaust gas to carbon dioxide and provided with a methane oxidation catalyst. A selective catalytic reduction reactor (30) is installed in the exhaust pipe at a rear end of the methane oxidation catalytic reactor to reduce nitrogen oxides contained in the exhaust gas. A branch pipe is branched from the exhaust pipe at the rear end of the selective catalytic reduction reactor, installed in the exhaust gas Exhaust pollutant reduction device including a carbon dioxide capture device for removing the contained carbon dioxide.
   USE - Apparatus for reducing air pollutant in a ship.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus oxidizes unburned methane contained in the exhaust gas to carbon dioxide so as to minimize emission of methane, greenhouse gas, into atmosphere, thereby reducing aggravation of global warming. The apparatus is provided with the methane oxidation catalytic reactor inside the selective catalytic reduction reactor, so that the space inside the ship can be used efficiently, thereby reducing costs. The reboiler heats the absorbent using the steam generated by the economizer, there is no need to operate a separate boiler for steam generation, thereby reducing energy consumption and preventing the generation of another carbon dioxide due to boiler operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side sectional view of an apparatus for reducing air pollutant.
   Exhaust pollutant reduction apparatus (1)
   Exhaust pipe (10)
   Methane oxidation catalytic reactor (20)
   Selective catalytic reduction reactor (30)
   Combustion engine (100)

The control module represents a                personnel condition in a device room. A control                module controls a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher                (1) or a foam fire extinguisher (2) to extinguish a                fire, so that the device room does not use the                carbon dioxide flame extinguisher for extinguishing                the fire. The carbon dioxide heat extinguisher is                provided with a heat-retardant and a heat                dissipation extinguisher.
   USE - Fire-proof device of equipment room.
   ADVANTAGE - The fire-proof device enhances the protection                of equipment and personnel in the equipment room.                The control module obtains the fire information and                the personnel information to control one of the                carbon dioxide fire extinguisher or the foam                extinguisher to extinguish the fire, so that the                device does not damage the device, and the fire                extinguishing effect of the foam is good, but also                can directly extinguish fire in the device room                without people. The device is good for providing                more comprehensive protection to the device and                personnel, since the control module is capable of                selecting one of carbon dioxide and foam fire                extinguishers based on fire and personnel                information for extinguishing the fire.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fire                protection method of equipment room.

TI Radioactive carbon, hydrogen source input device in airborne nuclide environmental migration test, has source device of carbon dioxide and source device of tritiated water, where tritiated water input device has tritiated water generator, sealed experiment cabin and tritium absorber.

   LI J
   ZHANG S

GA 2021D0925W
AB    NOVELTY - The 1^4^C (carbon), 3^H (hydrogen) source input device in an airborne nuclide environmental migration test, has a source device of 1^4^ carbon dioxide and a source device of tritiated water. The 14C radioactive source liquid is transferred to the carbon dioxide reactor. The reaction produces carbon dioxide, and the produced gas is loaded into the sealed experimental chamber to carry out the ecological transfer test of the airborne nuclide ^1^4C in crops. The airborne nuclide ^1^4C pollutes the first crop in the crops. After a preset time, the sealed experiment chamber is opened, and the radioactive exhaust gas is discharged after passing through the carbon dioxide absorber. The tritiated water input device has a tritiated water generator, a sealed experimental chamber (1) and a tritium absorber. The 3H radioactive source liquid is transferred to the tritiated water generator.
   USE - 1^4^C, 3^H source input device in an airborne nuclide environmental migration test.
   ADVANTAGE - The device imulates the key nuclides tritium and 14C chemical form and emission source characteristics in the gaseous effluent of the reprocessing plant, and improves the design and improvement of the test cabin for the airborne nuclides tritium and 14C ecological environment migration. The input method and device ensure the smooth development of research experiments on the ecological transfer of tritium and 14C in a variety of crops.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for feeding 14C and 3H in an airborne nuclide environmental migration test using the above device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the 14 carbon dioxide source input device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Carbon dioxide absorber (2)
   Magnetic stirrer (3)

PN JP2021093320-A
TI Fuel cell system used to generate electric power, has carbon-dioxide removal portion which is provided to send out mixed gas after process toward fuel cell stack, and heat exchange portion which exchange heat between mixed gas after process.
AU NAKAJIMA T
   NAKAMURA K
   DOKO T
   SHIRAI M
   TAKEDA D
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
GA 2021654076
AB    NOVELTY - The fuel cell system (10A) has a fuel cell stack (16) which generate electric-power by reacting fuel gas and air. A confluence portion (M1) which merge the biogas supply path (P1) that sends out the biogas containing the fuel gas and a carbon dioxide, and the anode off gas emitted from the fuel cell stack. A carbon dioxide is removed from the mixed gas of the biogas and the anode off gas in a downstream side, and is set as after-process mixed gas, rather than the confluence portion. A carbon-dioxide removal portion (20) which sends out the mixed gas after the process toward the fuel cell stack. The heat exchange portion (18) exchange heat between the mixed gas after the process and the anode off gas is provided.
   USE - Fuel cell system used to generate electric power by reacting fuel gas and air.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the quantity of gas which flows in into the carbon-dioxide removal portion is decreased, thus, the load of the carbon-dioxide removal portion is reduced. Since water vapor is supplied to the after-process mixed gas before heat exchange with the heat exchanger, thus, the carbon precipitation under high temperature is suppressed. The gas component which contributes to the power generation reaction of a fuel cell is obtained stably, and generating efficiency is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fuel cell system operating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the fuel cell system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Fuel cell system (10A)
   Fuel cell stack (16)
   Heat exchange portion (18)
   Carbon-dioxide removal portion (20)
   Confluence portion (M1)
   Biogas supply path (P1)

PT P
PN US2020333306-A1; WO2020214814-A1
TI Sensing carbon dioxide, comprises exposing composition to gas or liquid, comprising molecular structure comprising electrically conductive backbone, heteroatoms and pores.
AU DINCA M
   STASSEN I
   DOU J
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
   MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
GA 2020A28386
AB    NOVELTY - Sensing carbon dioxide, comprises exposing a composition to a gas or liquid, where the composition comprises a molecular structure comprising an electrically conductive backbone, many heteroatoms, and many pores, the composition has a monolayer amount of water of greater than or equal to 2 wt.%, the gas or liquid comprises carbon dioxide, and the gas has a relative humidity (RH) of greater than 0% relative humidity or the liquid has a relative humidity of greater than 0% ERH.
   USE - The method is useful for sensing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes compositions having pores that are relatively hydrophilic, relatively small, and relatively high density undergoes rapid full hydration in ambient environments having range of water contents (e.g. having range of relative humidifies and/or equilibrium relative humilities), metal-organic frameworks; and utilizes compositions has properties (e.g. electrical resistivity) in form that is readily accessible by other scientific instrumentation and/or by individuals observing the sensor (e.g. visually) and physical properties (e.g. adsorb water and/or carbon dioxide in relatively benefits environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) sensor, comprising the composition, and the composition is configured to exhibit a change in electrical resistivity upon exposure to the gas; and
   (2) sensor, comprising a metal-organic framework configured to exhibit a change in electrical resistivity upon exposure to the gas and the change in electrical resistivity is directly proportional to an amount of carbon dioxide in the gas when the relative humidity of the gas is 20-80% RH or change in electrical resistivity is directly proportional to an amount of carbon dioxide in the liquid when the equilibrium relative humidity of liquid is from 20-80% ERH.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heteroatoms comprises nitrogen atom forms a component of amino group or imino group. The molecular structure comprises at least one ligand comprising at least two sets of ortho-diimine groups arranged about an organic core, and the ligand comprising at least two sets of ortho-diimine groups arranged about an organic core is a hexaiminobenzene moiety. The gas or liquid comprises carbon dioxide is 400-10000 ppm. The relative humidity of the gas is 10-90% RH. The equilibrium relative humidity of the liquid is 10-90% ERH. The composition exhibits a change in electrical resistivity upon exposure to the carbon dioxide. The change in electrical resistivity is directly proportional to an amount of carbon dioxide in the gas. The composition uptakes water to form a monolayer thereon upon exposure to a gas with a relative humidity of greater than or equal to 10% RH. The magnitude of the change in electrical resistivity upon exposure to the gas comprising the carbon dioxide is greater when the relative humidity of the gas is greater than or equal to 1% RH than when the relative humidity of the gas is less than 1% RH. The adsorption of carbon dioxide onto the composition in an ambient environment comprising less than or equal to 10000 ppm of carbon dioxide is greater than or equal to 0.5 wt.%. The composition is disposed on electrodes. The sensor is configured to determine a change in the electrical resistivity of the composition upon exposure to the gas or liquid. The composition has a monolayer amount of water is greater than or equal to 2 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   (E) transferring algal biomass generated in algae culture unit from algal biomass transfer unit to reaction tank.

PN WO2022129338-A1
TI Producing syngas using catalytic reverse water gas shift reaction, by providing feed stream comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, heating, separating cooled syngas stream, heating water-depleted syngas stream, removing syngas containing stream, and cooling.
AU SCHOONEBEEK R J
   BOS A N R
   UNRUH D J M
   VAN DER SLOOT D P
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 202281580D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing syngas using a catalytic reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction, involves (a) providing a feed stream comprising at least hydrogen and carbon dioxide, (b) heating the feed stream, (c) introducing first heated feed stream into a first RWGS reactor, (d) removing the first syngas containing stream obtained in step (c) from the first RWGS reactor, (e) cooling the first syngas containing stream removed from the first RWGS reactor, (f) separating the first cooled syngas stream, (g) heating the water-depleted syngas stream, (h) introducing the heated water-depleted syngas stream to a second RWGS reactor, (i) removing the second syngas containing stream from the second RWGS reactor, and (j) cooling the second syngas containing stream removed from the second RWGS reactor in step (i) in the second heat exchanger against the water-depleted syngas stream obtained in step (f).
   USE - Method for producing syngas using a catalytic RWGS reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method minimizes one or more of the above problems, i.e. methanation, equipment material problems and high energy input at high temperatures, low conversion to high quality syngas at low temperatures. The method produces syngas as the product (a mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide) suitable for a variety of subsequent conversion processes (e.g. methanol synthesis and Cobalt based Fischer-Tropsch synthesis), where greater than 99% of the converted carbon dioxide is converted to carbon monoxide and virtually no conversion to methane or any other hydrocarbons. The method can be performed at lower temperatures, preferably lower than 700 degrees C. The method achieves a desirable conversion of carbon dioxide of above 65% or even above 70%, and also minimizes methanation (methane formation) and coke formation. The method uses less expensive materials for the reactors in view of the lower temperatures being used. The method allows for flexibility in the carbon monoxide/hydrogen ratio of the obtained syngas product stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing syngas using a catalytic reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction, involves (a) providing a feed stream comprising at least hydrogen and carbon dioxide, (b) heating the feed stream provided in step (a) in a first heat exchanger thereby obtaining a first heated feed stream, (c) introducing first heated feed stream into a first RWGS reactor and subjecting it to a first catalytic RWGS reaction, thereby obtaining a first syngas containing stream, (d) removing the first syngas containing stream obtained in step (c) from the first RWGS reactor, (e) cooling the first syngas containing stream removed from the first RWGS reactor in step (d) in the first heat exchanger against the feed stream provided in step (a), thereby obtaining a first cooled syngas stream, (f) separating the first cooled syngas stream obtained in step (e) in a first gas/liquid separator thereby obtaining a water-enriched stream and a water-depleted syngas stream, (g) heating the water-depleted syngas stream obtained in step (f) in a second heat exchanger thereby obtaining a heated water-depleted syngas stream, (h) introducing the heated water-depleted syngas stream obtained in step (g) to a second RWGS reactor and subjecting it to a second catalytic RWGS reaction, thereby obtaining a second syngas containing stream, (i) removing the second syngas containing stream (80) obtained in step (h) from the second RWGS reactor, and (j) cooling the second syngas containing stream removed from the second RWGS reactor in step (i) in the second heat exchanger against the water-depleted syngas stream obtained in step (f), thereby obtaining a cooled syngas product stream. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for performing the method for producing syngas, comprising a first heat exchanger for heat exchanging feed stream against first syngas containing stream removed from first RWGS reactor to obtain first heated feed stream and first cooled syngas stream, a first RWGS reactor to obtain a first syngas containing stream, a first gas/liquid separator for separating the first cooled syngas stream to obtain a water-enriched stream and a water-depleted syngas stream, a second heat exchanger for heat exchanging the water-depleted syngas and second syngas containing stream removed from the second RWGS reactor, to obtain a heated water-depleted syngas stream and a cooled syngas product stream, and a second RWGS reactor to obtain second syngas containing stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: In apparatus, At least one of first and second RWGS reactors comprises two or more catalyst beds with additional intermediate heating between the two or more catalyst beds. The apparatus further comprising a second gas/liquid separator for separating the cooled syngas product stream to obtain a water-enriched stream and a water-depleted syngas product stream.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of first catalytic RWGS reaction in step (c) is maintained less than 700 degrees C, preferably less than 600 degrees C. The temperature of second catalytic RWGS reaction in step (h) is maintained less than 700 degrees C, preferably less than 600 degrees C. Preferred Components: At least one of first and second RWGS reactors comprises two or more catalyst beds with additional intermediate heating between two or more catalyst beds. At least one of first and second RWGS reactors comprises a multi-tubular reactor heated by molten salt circulating around the tubes of the multi-tubular reactor. The first syngas containing stream comprises at most 1.0 vol.%, preferably at most 0.1 vol.% methane. Preferred Method: The method further involves separating the cooled syngas product stream obtained in step (j) in second gas/liquid separator, and preparing water-enriched stream and water-depleted syngas product stream.

PN CN112773759-A
TI Preparation method of side secretion factor            wrapping body with skin cell rejuvenation effect            comprises adding umbilical cord in the storage and            transportation bottle, washing umbilical cord, testing            cultured cells and resuspending.
AU ZHU Y
AE ZHU Y (ZHUY-Individual)
GA 202154827D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of side secretion factor                wrapping body with skin cell rejuvenation effect                comprises (i) adding umbilical cord in storage and                transportation bottle containing storage and                transportation liquid, and preparing for                separation; (ii) adding umbilical cord in petri                dish, washing umbilical cord with normal saline,                cutting off each end of umbilical cord to length of                1 cm, repeatedly washing the umbilical cord tissue                until there is no obvious blood clot; (iii) testing                cultured cells, transferring the tested cells to                the cell factory at final concentration of 3-5x                104/ml, marking them, placing them in 37&#176;                C; (iv) adding collected mesenchymal stem cell                culture supernatant into a centrifuge tube; and (v)                resuspending the microcapsule inclusions in culture                medium containing fibroblasts in incubator and                inoculate them in culture flask, observing under                microscope, it can be seen that fibroblasts secrete                collagen increased, dividing actively, and                prolonging their lifespan.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation method of                side secretion factor wrapping body with skin cell                rejuvenation effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the secretion of collagen                by fibroblasts; improves the activity of division;                and prolongs the lifespan of fibroblasts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation method of side secretion factor                wrapping body with skin cell rejuvenation effect                comprising (i) adding umbilical cord in the storage                and transportation bottle containing the storage                and transportation liquid, and preparing for                separation; (ii) adding umbilical cord in a petri                dish, washing the umbilical cord with normal                saline, cutting off each end of the umbilical cord                to a length of 1cm, repeatedly washing the                umbilical cord tissue until there is no obvious                blood clot, cutting the umbilical cord into 2 cm                with length sections, washing 3 secondly, cutting                the umbilical cord longitudinally, removing the                blood vessels, tearing off the Wharton's glue,                washing the Wharton's glue 3 times with normal                saline, cutting it into tissue pieces of 1 cubic                millimeter to 3 cubic millimetres and inoculating                in a culture flask, placing it in a 37&#176; C,                5% carbon dioxide incubator for 2 hours, adding 15                ml serum-free complete medium, placing it in the                incubator, keeping the incubator absolutely still,                observing under a microscope, it can be seen that                there is growth around the tissue block fibrous                cells crawled out, where the number of cells was                large at 12 days and removing the tissue block, and                transfer the adherent cells to a new culture flask                by trypsinization for subculture; (iii) testing                cultured cells, transferring the tested cells to                the cell factory at a final concentration of 3-5x                104/ml, marking them, placing them in a                37&#176; C, adding 5% carbon dioxide incubator                for expansion increase culture and adjusting the                parameters to 37&#176; C with 5% carbon dioxide                and 5% oxide and continue culturing when the                expanded cells grow to 70-80% of cell confluence,                adjusting the parameters to 37?C, after 24 hours,                adding 5% carbon dioxide and oxygen, collecting                half of the supernatant after 48 hours,                supplementing the same amount of fresh serum-free                medium to the cell factory, adjusting the                parameters to 37&#176; C, 5% carbon dioxide,                culturing with 5 oxygen and collecting the cell                supernatant after 72 hours; (iv) adding collected                mesenchymal stem cell culture supernatant into a                centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 4000 g for 8                minutes at 4&#176; C, taking the supernatant,                removing the cell precipitate, filtering the                supernatant through a hollow fiber membrane, and                collecting the filtrate, transfering the filtrate                after de- precipitation to a 5KD ultrafiltration                concentrated centrifuge tube with a pipette, and                centrifuge at 4000g for 1.5-2 hours at 4&#176;                C, collecting the concentrated liquid into a new                centrifuge tube, store in a 4&#176;                refrigerator, transferring all the extracts after                initial concentration to an ultrafiltration tube,                and concentrate for 1.5-2 hours, discarding the                liquid under the ultrafiltration tube, add saline                and repeat ultrafiltration and centrifugation to                wash once, collecting the concentrated solution to                obtain the concentrated stock solution of paracrine                factor of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells,                which is wrapped by microcapsules to obtain                microcapsule inclusions; and (v) resuspending the                microcapsule inclusions in the culture medium                containing fibroblasts in the incubator and                inoculate them in the culture flask, observing                under the microscope, it can be seen that the                fibroblasts secrete collagen increased, dividing                actively, and prolonging their lifespan.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  storage and transportation solution is 40 ml of                  &#945; -MEM, and 100U/ml gentamicin, 50mg/ml                  penicillin, and amphotericin B5 &#956; g/ml                  are added. In the step (b), the serum-free                  complete medium is the volume concentration is 2%                  serum substitute and the volume concentration is                  1% glutalanine dipeptide, and the rest is LONZA                  serum-free medium. In the step (b), the umbilical                  cord of a healthy full-term newborn is taken, and                  the umbilical cord is 20 cm close to the end of                  the baby.

TI Molecular medium useful for culturing porcine            small intestine organoids, comprises WNR-J2 conditioned            medium and basic medium containing advanced Dulbecco's            Modified Eagle's medium (DMEM)/F12 medium, serum            substitute and gastrin I.
AU XIONG X
   ZHOU J
   SHAO Y
   WU Y
   YIN Y
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI SUBTROPICAL AGRIC INST (ASSA-C)
GA 202128275R
AB    NOVELTY - Molecular medium comprises 50-60 vol.% WNR-J2                conditioned medium and remainder basic medium                comprises 40-50% advanced Dulbecco's Modified                Eagle's medium (DMEM)/F12 medium, 10-15 mM                4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid                (HEPES) buffer, 1-1.5% B27 serum substitute, 10-15                nM gastrin I, 1-1.5 mM N-acetyl-L-cysteine, 10-15 M                nicotinamide, 1.5-2.5 nM L-glutamine, 1-1.5%                N2-MAX, 10-15 mu M ethanolamine, and 60-60 ng/ml                epidermal growth factor (EGF).
   USE - The molecular medium is useful for culturing                porcine small intestine organoids.
   ADVANTAGE - The molecular medium is suitable for the                separation and cultivation of small intestinal                crypts of suckling pigs, weaned pigs and finishing                pigs, long-term passage and maintenance of pig                small intestinal organs, and pig intestinal                nutrition and disease modeling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Molecular medium comprises 50-60 vol.% WNR-J2                conditioned medium and remainder basic medium. The                WNR-J2 conditioned medium is prepared by (a)                preparing Jaworski's (JM) medium by mixing pig                serum, Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's medium (DMEM)                high glucose medium and penicillin/streptomycin                double antibody in a volume ratio of                1:(3-4):(0.005-0.015) and storing at 4 degrees C,                (b) transferring 10 ml JM medium to a T25 culture                flask after preheating in a 37 degrees C water                bath, (c) thawing the frozen WNR-J2 cells in a 37                degrees C water bath to obtain WNR-J2 cell                solution, (d) transferring the WNR-J2 cell solution                to the T25 culture flask containing JM medium, and                incubating in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide                incubator for 2-3 days until the cells are                completely overgrown and adhere to the T25 culture                flask, (e) adding 1 ml mild enzyme-free                dissociation solution to the T25 culture flask                after removing the JM medium, making the mild                enzyme-free dissociation solution evenly cover the                surface of the cell layer on the inner wall of the                T25 culture flask, and dissociating in the 37                degrees C incubator for 5-8 minutes, (f) preheating                25 ml new JM medium at 7 degrees C, using 6 ml                preheated JM medium to reselect the cells in the                T25 culture flask to obtain a cell suspension, then                transferring the cell suspension to a T150 culture                flask, then adding the remaining 19 ml pre-heated                JM medium to the T150 culture flask, and incubating                at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator                for 24 hours, (g) after culturing, removing the JM                medium, then adding 25 ml SM selection medium to                culture for 24 hours, and removing non-WNR-J2                cells, (h) after culturing, replacing the SM                selection medium with 25 ml new JM medium, and                continuing to culture WNR-J2 cells until the WNR-J2                cells are completely overgrown and adhere to the                T150 culture flask, (i) after culturing, removing                the old JM medium, adding 120 ml new JM medium,                dispensing 25 ml obtained WNR-J2 cell suspension                into multiple T150 culture flasks, and incubating                continuously at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide                incubator for 2-3 days, until WNR-J2 cells are                completely fused and WNR-J2 cells aggregate and                fall off, (j) after culturing, removing the old JM                medium, then adding 25 ml new JM medium, and                continuously culturing at 37 degrees C in a 5%                carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours to obtain                conditioned medium, (k) collecting the conditioned                medium in each T150 culture flask to a 50 ml                centrifuge tube every 24 hours, then adding 25 ml                new JM medium to the T150 culture flask, and                continuously culturing in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon                dioxide incubator, (l) centrifuging the centrifuge                tube containing the conditioned medium at room                temperature and 2000 g for 5-10 minutes,                transferring the supernatant to a storage bottle,                and storing at 4 degrees C, (m) mixing the JM                medium and the supernatant at a volume ratio of 1:1                to prepare 50 vol.% WNR-J2 conditioned medium. The                basic medium comprises 40-50% advanced DMEM/F12                medium, 10-15 mM                4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid                (HEPES) buffer, 1-1.5% B27 serum substitute, 10-15                nM gastrin I, 1-1.5 mM N-acetyl-L-cysteine, 10-15 M                nicotinamide, 1.5-2.5 nM L-glutamine, 1-1.5%                N2-MAX, 10-15 mu M ethanolamine, and 60-60 ng/ml                epidermal growth factor (EGF).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The JM medium is prepared                  by mixing porcine serum, DMEM high glucose medium                  and penicillin/streptomycin double antibody in a                  volume ratio of 1:3.95:0.01. The molecular medium                  is also added with 10-15 mu M Rock kinase                  inhibitor, the pH of the molecular medium is                  6.8-7.2, and the molecular medium is used for                  culturing jejunum. The molecular medium is also                  added with 15-20 mu M Rock kinase inhibitor and                  450-550 nM TGF- beta type I receptor inhibitor at                  the same time, the pH value of the molecular                  medium is 6.5-7, and the molecular medium is used                  for culturing the duodenum. The molecular medium                  is also added with 15-20 mu M Rock kinase                  inhibitor, 450-550 nM transforming growth factor                  (TGF)- beta type I receptor inhibitor and 15-20                  mu M p38 mitogen-activated protein kinases (P38                  MAPK) kinase inhibitor at the same time, the pH                  value of the molecular culture medium is 7-7.6,                  and the molecular culture medium is used for                  culturing the ileum. The molecular culture medium                  comprises 15 mM HEPES buffer, 1% B27 serum                  substitute, 15 nM gastrin I, 1 mM                  N-acetyl-L-cysteine, 10 M nicotinamide, 2 nM                  L-glutamine, 1% N2-MAX, 10 mu M ethanolamine, and                  60 ng/ml EGF.

PN CN111408357-A; CN111408357-B; CA3082668-A1; US11045789-B1; CA3082668-C
TI Biomass smart fiber-based amphoteric            multifunctional adsorption material, comprises a            stepwise dual-temperature stimulus responsive biomass            smart fiber as a matrix, a crown ether modified            amphoteric hyperbranched polyamine.
AU HE H
   ZHOU H
   WANG L
   QUAN Z
   CHEN Z
   QIN C
   ZHU H
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
   UNIV GUANGXI CHINESE MEDICINE FIRST AFFI (UGCT-C)
   UNIV GUANGXI CHINESE MEDICINE FIRST AFFI (UGCT-C)
GA 202067966L
AB    NOVELTY - Biomass smart fiber-based amphoteric                multifunctional adsorption material, comprises a                stepwise dual-temperature stimulus responsive                biomass smart fiber as a matrix, a crown ether                modified amphoteric hyperbranched polyamine as a                functional reagent. The material is obtained by                directly cross-linking stepped dual-temperature                stimulus-responsive biomass smart fiber and crown                ether modified amphoteric hyperbranched polyamine                in one step. The stepped dual-temperature stimulus                response of the stepped dual-temperature                stimulus-responsive biomass smart fiber is stimulus                responsiveness with two temperature stages, and its                lower critical solution temperature is 30-37                degrees C and 40-50 degrees C, respectively.
   USE - The biomass smart fiber-based amphoteric                multifunctional adsorption material is useful in                heavy metal adsorption, carbon dioxide capture, and                biogas purification (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biomass smart fiber-based amphoteric                multifunctional adsorption material, comprises a                stepwise dual-temperature stimulus responsive                biomass smart fiber as a matrix, a crown ether                modified amphoteric hyperbranched polyamine as a                functional reagent. The material is obtained by                directly cross-linking stepped dual-temperature                stimulus-responsive biomass smart fiber and crown                ether modified amphoteric hyperbranched polyamine                in one step. The stepped dual-temperature stimulus                response of the stepped dual-temperature                stimulus-responsive biomass smart fiber is stimulus                responsiveness with two temperature stages, and its                lower critical solution temperature is 30-37                degrees C and 40-50 degrees C, respectively. It is                obtained by chemically grafting polyethyleneimine                in response to stepped dual temperature stimulation                on biomass fiber raw materials; the                polyethyleneimine in response to stepped dual                temperature stimulation is made of                N-isopropylacrylamide and dimethyl methacrylate                after the Michael addition reaction of amino ethyl                ester and polyethyleneimine, they are mixed                uniformly. The crown ether modified amphoteric                hyperbranched polyamine is obtained from                triethylenetetraamine pentaacetic acid and                4'-aminobenzo-18-crown ether-6 modified                hyperbranched polyamine. The carboxy group of                triethylenetetraamine pentaacetic acid and the                hyperbranched polyamine are respectively. The amino                group of the modified polyamine and the amino group                of 4'-aminobenzo-18-crown ether-6 undergo an                amidation reaction; the hyperbranched polyamine is                obtained by the Michael addition reaction and                self-condensation reaction of the aminating reagent                and methyl acrylate. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also                included for: (1) preparing biomass smart                fiber-based amphoteric multifunctional adsorption                material, comprising (i) preparing carboxylated                biomass fiber: using TEMPO reagent to oxidize the                hydroxy group at the C6 position of the cellulose                structural unit of the biomass fiber raw material                to a carboxyl group, and the degree of oxidation is                controlled by controlling the reaction conditions                to prepare carboxylated biomass fiber; (ii)                preparation of stepwise dual-temperature                stimulus-response polyethyleneimine: using                N-isopropylacrylamide and dimethylaminoethyl                methacrylate to react with polyethyleneimine                separately after Michael addition reaction, and                then mixed in proportion, where the prepared                modified polyethyleneimine has a stepped dual                temperature stimulus response; (iii) preparation of                stepped dual-temperature stimulus-responsive                biomass smart fiber: carrying out carboxylated                biomass fiber and stepped dual-temperature                stimulus-responsive polyethyleneimine amidation                reaction at high temperature; (iv) preparation of                amphoteric hyperbranched polyamine modified by                crown ether: adopting Michael addition reaction of                amination reagent and methyl acrylate to generate                hyperbranched polyamine precursor, and                hyperbranched polyamine precursor undergoes                self-condensation reaction to generate                hyperbranched polyamine; crown ether modified                amphoteric hyperbranched polyamines are obtained                from triethylenetetraaminepentaacetic acid and                4'-aminobenzo-18-crown ether-6 modified                hyperbranched polyamines; where the carboxy groups                of triethylenetetraaminepentaacetic acid and                hyperbranched polyamines are respectively amidated                with amino group of polyamine and the amino group                of 4'-aminobenzo-18-crown-6; (v) one-step                cross-linking using a cross-linking agent: mixing                the stepped dual-temperature stimulus-responsive                biomass smart fiber prepared and the crown ether                modified amphoteric hyperbranched polyamine                prepared uniformly in an alkaline solution, adding                the epichlorohydrin cross-linking agent under                stirring conditions, and directly cross-linking the                mixed liquid into solid particles to prepare the                biomass smart fiber-based amphoteric                multifunctional adsorption material; and (2) use                method of biomass smart fiber-based amphoteric                multifunctional adsorption material in heavy metal                adsorption, carbon dioxide capture, and biogas                purification, comprising applying the material to                heavy metal adsorption and completely remove                low-concentration anion and cationic heavy metal                ions, where the material is used in carbon dioxide                capture, the carbon dioxide adsorption capacity is                greater than 6 mmol/g, and the regeneration                temperature is lower than 60 degrees C after                saturated carbon dioxide adsorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent                  is epichlorohydrin. The biomass fiber raw                  material is bagasse pulp fiber, eucalyptus pulp                  fiber and/or bamboo pulp fiber. Preferred Method:                  stirring uniformly at 55-65 degrees C, and then                  adding TEMPO, then adding sodium hypochlorite                  solution, sodium chlorite, oxidizing for 15-17                  hours, adding ethanol to quench, washing, and                  drying to obtain carboxylated biomass fiber;                  where the mass ratio of biomass fiber raw                  material to TEMPO is 200:2.5-2.8. The step (ii)                  comprises under the condition of 55-65 degrees C                  and magnetic stirring, adding polyethyleneimine                  aqueous solution with a concentration of                  0.15-0.25 g/ml dropwise into the                  N-isopropylacrylamide aqueous solution with a                  concentration of 0.03-0.09 g/ml according to the                  volume ratio of 1:3, reacting for 22-26 hours,                  quenching with liquid nitrogen, carrying out                  dialysis for 22-26 hours, and then freeze-drying                  to obtain N-isopropylacrylamide modified                  polyethyleneimine. The step (iii) includes                  dispersing polyethyleneimine and carboxylated                  biomass fibers of equal mass in response to                  stepped dual temperature stimulation in water,                  sonicating, reacting at 100 degrees C for 8-10                  hours, and then centrifuging to precipitate until                  the supernatant is neutral, freeze-drying the                  precipitate to obtain a stepped dual-temperature                  stimulus-responsive biomass smart fiber. The step                  (iv) includes dissolving the aminating reagent in                  anhydrous methanol in a volume ratio of 9-10:5,                  and pressing the methyl acrylate to dissolve in                  anhydrous methanol with a volume ratio of 2-3:5,                  pouring nitrogen into the aminating reagent                  solution, adding methyl acrylate solution to the                  amination reagent solution with a volume ratio of                  1:1 under stirring at 0 degrees C, reacting at                  room temperature for 18-24 hours to obtain a                  hyperbranched polyamine precursor, evaporating                  hyperbranched polyamine precursor and reacting to                  prepare hyperbranched polyamine. The step (v) is                  mixing the stepped dual-temperature stimulus                  responsive biomass smart fiber and the crown                  ether modified amphoteric hyperbranched polyamine                  according in the mass ratio of 1:1-10, dispersing                  in 1-4 wt.% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution,                  stirring at 100-800 revolutions per minute,                  adding epichlorohydrin, carrying out crosslinking                  reaction for 10-60 minutes, to obtain the biomass                  smart fiber-based amphoteric multifunctional                  adsorption material.

PT P
PN CN109422641-A; CN109422641-B
TI Producing calcium formate by removing impurities such as carbon dioxide in yellow phosphorus tail gas, then reacting with calcium hydroxide in reaction tank, carrying out solid-liquid separation, drying solid, and crystallizing liquid.
AU LV J
   LI Z
   LIU F
   CAO X
   MA X
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   TIANJIN ZHONGTIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201923360Y
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous production method of calcium formate from yellow phosphorus tail gas involves: (a) connecting a raw material pulper to a quicklime feed line for preparing a calcium hydroxide raw material slurry, connecting a raw material pulper to a raw material feed port at the top of a pressurized reaction tank (A) through a pipeline, and setting a transfer pump (A) in the pipeline to control the feeding rate of the calcium hydroxide raw material slurry; and (b) connecting a yellow phosphorus tail gas pipeline to a yellow phosphorus tail gas treatment device, treating the yellow phosphorus tail gas to increase the content of carbon monoxide and reduce the amount of carbon dioxide and other impurities, and purifying after treatment, where the volume percentage of carbon monoxide in the yellow phosphorus tail gas is increased to more than 90%, and the volume fraction of carbon dioxide is reduced to less than 2%.
   USE - The method is useful for continuous production of calcium formate from yellow phosphorus tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The continuous production method has low production cost and simple technique, and is suitable for large-scale continuous automatic production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuous production method of calcium formate from yellow phosphorus tail gas involves: (a) connecting a raw material pulper to a quicklime feed line for preparing a calcium hydroxide raw material slurry, connecting a raw material pulper to a raw material feed port at the top of a pressurized reaction tank (A) through a pipeline, and setting a transfer pump (A) in the pipeline to control the feeding rate of the calcium hydroxide raw material slurry; (b) connecting a yellow phosphorus tail gas pipeline to a yellow phosphorus tail gas treatment device, treating the yellow phosphorus tail gas to increase the content of carbon monoxide and reduce the amount of carbon dioxide and other impurities, and purifying after treatment, where the volume percentage of carbon monoxide in the yellow phosphorus tail gas is increased to more than 90%, and the volume fraction of carbon dioxide is reduced to less than 2%; (c) connecting the yellow phosphorus tail gas treatment device to the raw material feed port at the bottom end of a pressurized reaction tank (B) through a pipeline, and setting a compressor in the pipeline to provide power and control the feed rate of the gas phase raw material; (d) connecting the discharge port at the top of the pressurized reaction tank (B) to the raw material feed port at the bottom end of the pressurized reaction tank (A) and introducing the gas phase raw material from the pressurized reaction tank (A) to the pressurized reaction tank (B); (e) connecting an inlet and an outlet of the upper end of the pressurized reaction tank (A) connected with an overflow tank of the pressurized reaction tank (A) to an inlet and an outlet of the upper end of the pressurized reaction tank (B) through the pipeline, introducing the raw material in the pressurized reaction tank (A) into the pressurized reaction tank (B), and arranging a flow control valve in the pipeline; (f) subjecting the gas-phase raw material and the liquid-phase raw material to countercurrent contact, and reacting in two pressurized reaction tanks; (g) connecting an inlet and an outlet of the upper end of the pressurized reaction tank connected with an overflow tank of the pressurized reaction tank (B) to a buffer tank, connecting the buffer tank with a flash tank, connecting the flash tank with a sedimentation centrifuge through the pipeline, and arranging a transfer pump (B) on the pipeline; (h) connecting the sedimentation centrifuge to a dryer (A), and sequentially connecting the sedimentation centrifuge to a mother liquor tank, an evaporation crystallizer, a centrifugal filter and a second dryer (B); (i) subjecting the product at the top end of the pressurized reaction tank (B) to flash evaporation, sedimentation and centrifugation to obtain an industrial calcium formate product; and (j) crystallizing the product at the top end of the pressurized reaction tank (B) through evaporation, carrying out centrifugal separation and drying to obtain a feed grade calcium formate product with purity of more than 99%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid content of calcium hydroxide in the calcium hydroxide raw material slurry is 300-400 g/l, preferably 320-350 g/l (mass g of calcium hydroxide/volume l of water). The volume fraction of carbon monoxide in the gas phase raw material is 90-100% ( phi ), preferably 92-100% ( phi ).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves arranging a temperature control original component and a stirrer in the raw material pulper to control the temperature and the stirring speed of the calcium hydroxide to-be-prepared, and purifying after being treated, where the volume percentage of carbon monoxide in the yellow phosphorus tail gas is increased to 90-98%, preferably 95-98%, and the volume fraction of carbon dioxide drops to the order of ppm near zero. The production method involves: providing a gas distributor on the feed line at the bottom end of the pressurized reaction tank (B) to uniformly distribute the gas-phase raw material in the pressurized reaction tank (B), or arranging a gas distributor on the feed line at the bottom end of the pressurized reaction tank (A) to achieve uniform distribution of the gas-phase raw material in the pressurized reaction tank (A); carrying out flash evaporation on the liquid phase product prepared by the pressurized reaction tank and filtering by a horizontal spiral sedimentation centrifuge; evaporating and crystallizing the filtered mother liquor by a selected mechanical vapor recompression (MVR) evaporative crystallization system or multi-effect evaporative crystallization system, where the crystallization temperature is 60-170 degrees C and performing centrifugal pumping using a piston pusher to separate the crystal product from the mother liquor after crystallization is completed; and when drying is carried out prior to packaging, selecting a fluidized bed drying or a tray drying, where the drying temperature is 120-180 degrees C, preferably 80-150 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: The reaction pressure of the pressurized reaction tank is 1.5-8 MPa, preferably 2-6 MPa, and the operating temperature is 160-230 degrees C, preferably 170-200 degrees C.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110877914-A
TI Preparation of high purity high whiteness calcium carbonate using phosphogypsum mineralization-fixing carbon dioxide by reacting phosphogypsum with organic solvent, standing and layering, separating upper and lower phases, and filtering.
AU ZHANG M
   ZHAO H
AE NORTH CHINA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NCSI-C)
GA 202022319P
AB    NOVELTY - A high purity high whiteness calcium carbonate is prepared using phosphogypsum mineralization-fixing carbon dioxide by fully removing impurities of phosphogypsum, sieving, adding into three-mouth flask containing sulfuric acid solution, adding organic solvent, reacting, oscillating, and standing and layering; separating upper organic phase and lower aqueous solution phase, filtering, washing with deionized water, and oven drying purified gypsum to obtain purified gypsum product with low impurity content; adding ammonia water with deionized water, adding with purified gypsum product with low impurity content into high pressure reaction kettle, installing high pressure reaction kettle, uniformly mixing using stirring speed, and stirring; and continuously injecting carbon dioxide gas into high pressure kettle, starting carbonation reaction, taking out slurry sample from high pressure reaction kettle sampling port, filtering material slurry, washing using deionized water, and oven drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing high purity high whiteness calcium carbonate using phosphogypsum mineralization-fixing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The product turns carbon dioxide greenhouse gasses into high-quality calcium carbonate products in a very short time, alleviates huge pressure of reducing carbon dioxide in our country, realizes green resource utilization of bulk industrial solid waste phosphogypsum, solves problem of storage and pollution of solid waste phosphogypsum, improves conversion and reaction rate of carbonation and quality of product carbonation, dramatically increases efficiency of phosphogypsum mineralization fixed carbon dioxide, and effectively promotes development of phosphogypsum mineralization and fixed carbon dioxide technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A high purity high whiteness calcium carbonate is prepared using phosphogypsum mineralization-fixing carbon dioxide by:
   (A) fully removing impurities of phosphogypsum, sieving with 40-mesh sieve, adding into three-mouth flask containing 10-45% sulfuric acid solution, adding organic solvent, putting three-mouth flask into constant-temperature oil bath, sealing, reacting for 20-60 minutes at stirring speed of 300 revolutions/minute (rpm), transferring slurry to separating funnel, oscillating for 5 minutes, standing and layering, and taking upper layer organic phase containing impurities and lower layer sulfuric acid aqueous solution phase containing purified gypsum;
   (B) separating upper organic phase and lower aqueous solution phase, filtering by vacuum, washing with deionized water to obtain separated impurity and purified gypsum, and oven drying purified gypsum at 60 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain purified gypsum product with low impurity content;
   (C) adding 25% ammonia water with deionized water to prepare dilute ammonia water, adding dilute ammonia aqueous solution and purified gypsum product with low impurity content into high pressure reaction kettle, installing high pressure reaction kettle, uniformly mixing using stirring speed, and stirring for 30 minutes; and
   (D) continuously injecting 99.9% carbon dioxide gas into high pressure kettle, starting carbonation reaction for 5-60 minutes, taking out slurry sample from high pressure reaction kettle sampling port, filtering material slurry, washing using deionized water, and oven drying to 60 degrees C to constant weight.
   The organic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, cyclohexane, sulfonated kerosene, liquid paraffin, tributyl phosphate, gasoline or benzene. The mass ratio of organic solvent to phosphogypsum is 5-0.5:1. The mass ratio of diluted ammonia water to purified gypsum with low impurity content is 4:1-1:1. The pressure of carbon dioxide gas is 0.4-1.6 Mpa.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E11-Q01A; E11-W; E34-D03A

PT P
PN CN109618833-A; CN109618833-B
TI Screening high-efficiency germplasm of spring            rapeseed comprises e.g. determining photosynthetic gas            exchange parameters of the leaves and pods, measuring            the photosynthetic area of the greenhorn peel, and            recording the data.
AU ZHAO X
   ZHANG Y
   CHEN W
   WANG Z
   HOU J
   WANG L
   ZHANG X
AE SHAANXI HYBRID RAPESEED RES CENT (SHAA-Non-standard)
   SHAANXI HYBRID RAPESEED RES CENT (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201937749X
AB    NOVELTY - Screening high-efficiency germplasm of spring                rapeseed comprises determining photosynthetic gas                exchange parameters of the leaves and pods, where                the measurement indicators include net                photosynthetic rate, stomatal conductance,                intercellular carbon dioxide concentration,                transpiration rate, stomatal limit value and water                use efficiency, and recorded data, measuring the                photosynthetic area of the greenhorn peel,                recording the data, measuring the light response                curve and the carbon dioxide response curve of the                rapeseed, and screening the data twice: (i)                screening period: seedling stage, alfalfa stage,                flowering stage and pod fruit stage, screening                objects: long-handled leaves, short-handled leaves,                sessile leaves and pods, screening index: net                photosynthetic rate; control variety: Qingza 7,                comparing the net photosynthetic rate of the                candidate material with the control variety in the                four periods of long-leaf, short-handle, sessile                and pod.
   USE - The method is useful for screening                high-efficiency germplasm of spring rapeseed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Screening high-efficiency germplasm of spring                rapeseed comprises determining photosynthetic gas                exchange parameters of the leaves and pods, where                the measurement indicators include net                photosynthetic rate, stomatal conductance,                intercellular carbon dioxide concentration,                transpiration rate, stomatal limit value and water                use efficiency, and recorded data, measuring the                photosynthetic area of the greenhorn peel,                recording the data, measuring the light response                curve and the carbon dioxide response curve of the                rapeseed, and screening the data twice: (i)                screening period: seedling stage, alfalfa stage,                flowering stage and pod fruit stage, screening                objects: long-handled leaves, short-handled leaves,                sessile leaves and pods, screening index: net                photosynthetic rate; control variety: Qingza 7,                comparing the net photosynthetic rate of the                candidate material with the control variety in the                four periods of long-leaf, short-handle, sessile                and pod, calculating the net photosynthetic rate of                the candidate material and the control variety in                each period, selecting material having a net                photosynthetic rate higher than the net                photosynthetic rate of the control species in each                period as the selected material from the materials,                calculating an average value of the total net                photosynthetic rate of the control species and the                selected materials in four periods, respectively,                and using a material having an average value                greater than 5% of the average value of the control                material as the primary high light effect material,                and (ii) screening period: seedling stage and flood                season, screening object: short handle leaf of                primary high light effect material, screening                indicators: light response curve and carbon dioxide                response curve, calculating a light saturation                point, a light compensation point, and a light                quantum efficiency by using the light response                curve, calculating a carboxylation efficiency,                carbon dioxide compensation point, and carbon                dioxide saturation point by using the carbon                dioxide response curve, screening for high light                quantum efficiency, high carboxylation efficiency,                high light saturation point, high carbon dioxide                saturation point, low light compensation point or                low carbon dioxide compensation point, and using                the germplasm having at least a high light                saturation point or a low carbon dioxide                compensation point in the primary high light effect                material as the final high light effect                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The specific steps of                  determining the photosynthetic gas exchange                  parameters of the leaves include: (i) sampling                  site: selecting the 6-8-leaf long-handle leaves                  at the seedling stage, and selecting the 1st and                  2nd-leaf short-handle leaves and the 2nd-leaf                  sessile leaves in the flood stage and flowering                  stage, (ii) determining of the number of samples:                  12 leaves per material, (iii) cutting the leaves                  from the petiole where the main stem meets,                  immediately wrapping the petiole with wet cotton,                  and quickly testing on the machine from the leaf                  to the completion of the measurement within 2                  minutes, (iv) determining of instruments and                  conditions: using Li-6400 photosynthesis                  measurement system for measurement, selecting                  6400-02B LED red and blue light source leaf                  chamber, during the measurement, the indoor                  illumination control is 1100 mu mol-m-2-s-1, and                  the temperature is 25 degrees C, at least once                  every 30 minutes, (v) measuring time is sunny, no                  wind, 9:00-12:00 am and 3:30-4:30 pm, (vi)                  carrying out the measurement from the base of the                  leaves, reading the data for 3 times for each                  measurement, calculating by the average number,                  using the method of rotation between varieties,                  and measuring two leaves per material once in 30                  minutes for a total of 6 times, (vii) measuring                  indicators include net photosynthetic rate,                  stomatal conductance, intercellular carbon                  dioxide concentration, transpiration rate,                  stomatal limit value and water use efficiency,                  and (viii) controlling the CV% of the leaves at                  10% due to the large fluctuation of                  photosynthetic data, the specific steps of                  determining the photosynthetic gas exchange                  parameters of the pods include: (i) sampling                  site: the age of selection is 25-30 days, and                  taking the main inflorescences from the 15th-20th                  cents, (i) selecting 16 pods for measurement,                  (iii) cutting the horn from the stalk near the                  main stem 1 cm, immediately wrapping the petiole                  with wet cotton, cutting the horn from the 3 cm                  from the stalk, quickly testing it on the                  machine, and controlling from sampling to                  measurement completion within 2 minutes, (iv)                  drawing two grooves are drawn according to the                  shape of the ends of the measuring cone in order                  to prevent the chamber from leaking, inside the                  lower sponge pad of the leaf chamber, during the                  measurement, placing the pods inside the two                  grooves, and sealing the pressure by the sponge                  to tight the entire chamber, (iv) measuring the                  measuring instruments and conditions by Li-6400                  portable photosynthesis measuring system, and                  using the 6400-02B LED red and blue light source                  chamber to control the indoor temperature of the                  chamber at 28 degrees C, where the light is 1300                  mu mol-m-2-s-1 light intensity, (v) measuring in                  the sunny and windless morning 9:00-12:00, (vi)                  carrying out the measurement from the base of the                  fruit body, reading the data for 3 times for each                  measurement, using the average number for                  calculation and statistics, adopting the method                  of inter-variety rotation, measuring two pods per                  material for each time, measuring once in 30                  minutes for total of 8 times, after taking all                  the measurements, transferring the measured horns                  to the laboratory to measure the area, (vii)                  measuring the length of the fruit with a vernier                  caliper, where the width and thickness of the                  horn at 1/3 and 2/3, calculating the area of the                  single horn peel according to the formula as                  given in the specification, according to N is                  1/2-S, inputting the single-corner peel area into                  the computer, performing the recalculation of the                  measured index by using the Recompute program in                  the Li-6400 Simulator 5.2.01 system of the                  photosynthetic system, and carrying the                  calculation of the green cuticle area by Clark's                  modified formula, (vii) measured and                  water use efficiency, and (ix) controlling the                  CV% of the pod to 12% because the sealing of the                  leaf chamber during photosynthetic photosynthetic        seedling stage and a flood stage using a Li-6400                  portable photosynthesis measuring system, using                  the 6400-02B LED red and blue light source                  chamber, where the indoor temperature control is                  25 degrees C, controlling the carbon dioxide                  concentration to 400 mu mol-ml-1, after 15                  minutes of adaptation at 1100 mu mol-m-2-s-1                  light intensity, carrying out the measurement                  using an automatic light curve program, and                  determining 10 strains for each material, the                  specific steps of determining the carbon dioxide                  response curve include: controlling the indoor                  temperature of the chamber to adaptation,                  using the automatic carbon dioxide curve                  measurement procedure for determination, and                  determining 10 strains for each material.
