PN CN113105131-A
TI Comprehensively utilizing calcium carbide to purify ash involves performing aerobic calcination of the purified calcium carbide ash, burning the elemental carbon into carbon dioxide to remove, and combining magnesium and silicon with oxygen.
AU SONG X
   ZHOU J
   XIONG X
   ZHOU G
   LI G
   WANG X
   WANG Y
   YUAN X
   DAN X
   LI J
   XIA R
   AI C
   CAI L
   LU X
   WANG W
   KAI F
   LI Z
   TIAN F
   JIN F
   SI J
   XIA L
AE XINJIANG ZHIZHEN CHEM ENG RES CENT CO (XINJ-Non-standard)
   XINJIANG TIANYE GROUP CO LTD (XJTY-C)
   UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 2021837629
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensively utilizing calcium carbide to purify ash involves performing aerobic calcination of the purified calcium carbide ash, and burning the elemental carbon into carbon dioxide (CO2) to remove. The metal calcium, magnesium and silicon in the purified ash combined with oxygen to form oxides, which will be used after cooling. The reducing agent is crushed and grounded so that the particle size is less than or equal to 0.15mm. The purified calcium carbide ash after the aerobic calcination is mixed with the reducing agent, grounded, sieved, and pelletized for use. The reducing agent is mixed according to 90-120 wt.% of the theoretical amount to form a mixture. The magnesium is produced by vacuum thermal reduction of the mixture material obtained in the process to obtain crude magnesium and reduced calcium-based waste residue. The crude magnesium is refined to obtain refined magnesium and magnesium ingots.
   USE - Method for comprehensively utilizing calcium carbide to purify ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is a green and high-value waste slag comprehensive utilization method, and realizes the comprehensive utilization of all components of the calcium carbide purified ash.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensively utilizing calcium carbide to purify ash involves performing aerobic calcination of the purified calcium carbide ash, and burning the elemental carbon into carbon dioxide (CO2) to remove. The metal calcium, magnesium and silicon in the purified ash combined with oxygen to form oxides, which will be used after cooling. The reducing agent is crushed and grounded so that the particle size is less than or equal to 0.15mm. The purified calcium carbide ash after the aerobic calcination is mixed with the reducing agent, grounded, sieved, and pelletized for use. The reducing agent is mixed according to 90-120 wt.% of the theoretical amount to form a mixture. The magnesium is produced by vacuum thermal reduction of the mixture material obtained in the process to obtain crude magnesium and reduced calcium-based waste residue. The crude magnesium is refined to obtain refined magnesium and magnesium ingots. The calcium-based waste slag is cooled, a certain amount of calcium carbide slag and coal slag are mixed, and the finished cement is obtained after grounding, roasting, cooling and grounding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The reducing agent includes ferrosilicon, calcium carbide and silicon powder. Preferred Conditions: The aerobic calcination is calcination in an air atmosphere at 800-900 degrees C for 2-6 hours. The vacuum degree of the magnesium produced by the vacuum thermal reduction method is 0.1-30Pa, and the vacuum degree is heated to 1100-1250 degrees C, the reduction time is 2-12 hours. The blending amount of the calcium carbide slag is 5-40 wt.% the mass of the calcium-based waste slag, and the blending amount of the coal slag is 5-20 wt.% the mass of the calcium-based waste slag. The calcination temperature is 1320-1450 degrees C.

PN CN110560075-A; CN110560075-B
TI Nano-copper-europium alloy catalyst with a core-shell structure used as raw material for preparing working electrode for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane, comprises copper as core and copper-europium alloy as shell.
AU WANG Z
   SHAN J
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 2019A6744S
AB    NOVELTY - Nano-copper-europium alloy catalyst with a core-shell structure comprises copper as a core and copper-europium alloy as a shell.
   USE - Nano-copper-europium alloy catalyst with a core-shell structure used as a raw material for preparing a working electrode for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-copper-europium alloy catalyst with a core-shell structure has high activity and selectivity for carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction to produce methane, and good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a nano-copper-europium alloy catalyst with a core-shell structure, which involves:
   (A) weighing surfactant, organic solvent containing alcohol, copper salt and europium salt, dividing surfactant into surfactant I and surfactant II, and dividing alcohol-containing organic solvent into alcohol-containing organic solvent I and alcohol-containing organic solvent II according to a volume ratio of 18:4, where volume ratio of amount of surfactant and alcohol-containing organic solvent is (0.002-2)mmol:22mL, molar ratio of copper element in copper salt and europium element in europium salt is 1-9:1, and volume ratio of sum of amount of copper element in copper salt and europium element in europium salt and alcohol-containing organic solvent II is (0.02-0.2)mmol:22mL;
   (B) preparing a surfactant alcohol solution by dissolving surfactant I in an alcohol-containing organic solvent I to obtain a surfactant alcohol solution;
   (C) preparing a precursor salt solution by dissolving surfactant II, copper salt and europium salt in an alcohol-containing organic solvent II to obtain a precursor salt solution;
   (D) heating surfactant alcohol solution under stirring condition to 220-300 degrees C, adding precursor salt solution to surfactant alcohol solution at a rate of 0.05-0.3mL/second, reacting it under stirring condition at 220-300 degrees C for 5-60 minutes to obtain reaction product, and cooling reaction product to room temperature at a cooling rate of 5-40 degrees C/minutes to obtain a cooled reaction product; and
   (E) centrifuging reaction product to obtain separated solid, washing solid sequentially with acetone, absolute ethanol and ultrapure water, washing it for 1-3 times respectively to obtain washed solid, and subjecting obtained solid to vacuum drying at room temperature for 12-24 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) K30 or hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide. The copper salt is copper(II) acetate, copper(II) chloride dihydrate or copper(II) nitrate trihydrate. The europium salt is europium(III) nitrate pentahydrate, europium(III) chloride hexahydrate or europium(III) acetate hydrate. The alcohol-containing organic solvent is ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol or triethylene glycol.

PN CN109879539-A
TI Processing method of alkaline recycling printing and dyeing wastewater by cooling discharged printing and dyeing wastewater, removing solid impurity, adsorbing and decoloring, adding flocculating agent, standing and infusing carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN X
AE SHAOXING JINDU PRINTING & DYEING CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
GA 2019549574
AB    NOVELTY - Processing method of alkaline recycling printing and dyeing wastewater involves cooling discharged printing and dyeing wastewater, filtering, removing solid impurity, adsorbing and decoloring, and aerating; adjusting pH value using composite alkali, adding flocculating agent, forming composite flocculating body, standing to precipitate, separating wastewater and sludge, collecting precipitated sludge, infusing carbon dioxide, and adjusting pH value of wastewater; oxidation reacting, removing remaining organic pollutant, adding nitrifying microorganism microbial inoculum, biological contact oxidizing, and nitrifying and converting ammonia nitrogen; and adding denitrifying inoculum, anaerobic denitrifying, filtering, chlorination disinfecting and UV sterilizing, and filtering by filter bed comprising porous particulate material.
   USE - The method is used for processing alkaline recycling printing and dyeing wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe and convenient to operate, uses physical absorbing method and redox reaction, processes all kinds of pollutants in wastewater, enhances water recovery rate, and reduces water consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing method of alkaline recycling printing and dyeing wastewater involves:
   (A) cooling discharged printing and dyeing wastewater in cooling tank, filtering by coarse grid network and fine grid network, removing solid impurity in printing and dyeing wastewater, introducing into adjusting tank by water pump, adsorbing and decoloring, simultaneously injecting air into adjusting tank, and aerating;
   (B) introducing into coagulation tank by water pump, adjusting pH value using composite alkali to 12-14, adding 200-500 mg flocculating agent in coagulation tank per 1 L wastewater, forming composite flocculating body, standing to precipitate for 6-12 hours, separating wastewater and sludge, collecting precipitated sludge, loading into neutralization tank by water pump, infusing carbon dioxide, and adjusting pH value of wastewater to 7;
   (C) introducing into oxidation reaction circulatory tank through water pump, filling oxidation reaction circulatory tank with ozone, oxidation reacting, removing remaining organic pollutant, introducing into nitration reaction tank, adding nitrifying microorganism microbial inoculum, biological contact oxidizing, and nitrifying and converting ammonia nitrogen at 25-35 degrees C; and
   (D) introducing into denitrification reaction tank, adding denitrifying inoculum, anaerobic denitrifying, filtering, sending filtered water into sterilizing tower, chlorination disinfecting and UV sterilizing, and filtering by filter bed comprising porous particulate material.
   The porous particulate material is activated carbon and/or clay. The flocculating agent is ferrous sulfate, ferric trichloride, magnesium sulfate and polyacrylamide at ratio of 60:25:30:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The absorbing and decoloring material comprises 5-10 wt.% water-soluble adhesive, 10-30 wt.% slag, 3-6 wt.% plant ash, 0-28 wt.% kaolin, 6-10 wt.% activated carbon, 10-15 wt.% decoloring agent, 5-8 wt.% active sludge, 0-30 wt.% diatomite and 0-5 wt.% chitosan. Preferred Method: Chlorination disinfection material is 10-20 g/L sodium hypochlorite or bleaching powder or chlorine disinfectant. UV sterilizing catalyst is titanium dioxide or manganese oxide.

   USE - Compact portable cooling container and keg dispenser used in dispensing beer and other beverage. Can be used for a Sixth Barrel or Home Brew or Cornelius keg of beer or other beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a portable container and dispenser specifically designed to securely hold in place a Sixth Barrel or Cornelius keg. Includes an insulated container with a housing defining an interior space, in which the exterior and interior of the insulated container are made of durable material. Includes a waterproof lid with a latch for locking the lid to the container. Includes a mechanism for holding a Sixth Barrel or Cornelius keg firmly in its center, eliminating the ability for the keg to move side to side, or up and down within the container. Creates a consistent thickness between the keg and the inside walls of the container, creating a space used for placing ice uniformly around the keg within the container. Includes a regulator and a carbon dioxide container attached on the outside of the container for easy access, and protected by a hinged door that opens and closes. Includes a drain cock directly conjoined to the side of the container, and provided to be opened or closed to allow liquid resulting from condensation or during cleaning to be removed from the container.

PN CN109742373-A
TI Preparing nitrogen-doped nano ribbon porous carbon for lithium-sulfur battery, involves heating composite of polyaniline and nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer in argon atmosphere, injecting carbon dioxide, drying and heat-preserving.
AU ZOU J
   TU W
   NIU Y
   ZENG X
   ZENG S
   LIU S
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 201944986P
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing nitrogen-doped nano ribbon porous carbon, involves compounding polyaniline and nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer, adding polyaniline monomer to 200 mL citric acid solution, stirring, dissolving, adding nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer, stirring, ultrasonically-processing, uniformly dispersing, adding ammonium persulfate solution, magnetically stirring, filtering and drying to obtain composite of polyaniline and nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer, heating composite of polyaniline and nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer in argon atmosphere, injecting carbon dioxide for 40 minutes to form nitrogen-doped nano-ribbon porous carbon, combining nitrogen-doped nano-ribbon porous carbon with elemental sulfur in a mass ratio of 2:3, uniformly adding 2 mL anhydrous ethanol, adding 1 mL carbon chloride to promote dissolution of elemental sulfur, naturally drying, placing in an oven at 155 degrees C and heat-preserving.
   USE - Method for preparing nitrogen-doped nano ribbon porous carbon for lithium-sulfur battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively promotes the chemical adsorption between the oxygen-containing functional groups on the carbon and lithium polysulfide using nitrogen-doped porous carbon and the lithium-sulfur battery fabricated by supporting the porous carbon material with sulfur exhibits excellent cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing nitrogen-doped nano ribbon porous carbon, involves (1) compounding polyaniline and nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer, adding polyaniline monomer to 200 mL citric acid solution, stirring, dissolving, adding nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer, stirring, ultrasonically-processing, uniformly dispersing, placing in 0 degrees C environment for 30 minutes, adding 40 mL ammonium persulfate solution, magnetically stirring, filtering and drying to obtain composite of polyaniline and nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer, (2) heating composite of polyaniline and nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer in argon atmosphere, injecting carbon dioxide for 40 minutes to form nitrogen-doped nano-ribbon porous carbon, (3) combining nitrogen-doped nano-ribbon porous carbon with elemental sulfur in a mass ratio of 2:3, uniformly adding 2 mL anhydrous ethanol, adding 1 mL carbon chloride to promote dissolution of elemental sulfur, naturally drying, placing in an oven at 155 degrees C and heat-preserving. The mass ratio of polyaniline to nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer is 1:2-1:5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (1) comprises magnetically stirring for 12 hours. The composite of polyaniline and nano ribbon phenolic resin-based polymer is added at 950 degrees C under an argon atmosphere. The holding time of the oven in the process of compounding the nitrogen-doped nano ribbon porous carbon with the elemental sulfur is 12 hours.

PN CN109329384-A
TI Dry rotary microwave processing of GongCheng persimmon comprises e.g. grading and washing persimmon fruit is placing in a carbon dioxide dehydration tank, carrying out deodorization, peeling and placing persimmon fruit on a drying sieve.
AU LI D
   CHEN L
AE GUILIN GONGCHENG FENGHUAYUAN FOOD CO LTD (GUIL-Non-standard)
GA 201918772W
AB    NOVELTY - Dry rotary microwave processing of GongCheng persimmon comprises (i) grading and washing persimmon fruit is placing in a carbon dioxide (CO2) dehydration tank, deodorization is carrying out under the conditions of a temperature and a CO2 concentration of 98-99.9%, (ii) peeling and placing persimmon fruit on a drying sieve and sent to a drying room, a rotating microwave drying device is arranging in center of drying room, and baking persimmon piece, sieve is placing around rotating microwave drying device, and persimmon piece is heating uniformly by microwave spin drying, (iii) removing a large amount of water vapor releasing during microwave drying by a high-temperature dehumidifier placing in a drying room, and (iv) installing many circulating fans in drying room, arranging at front and rear ends of drying room, to the upper and lower intervals on the opposite side to form a forced circulation air system from bottom to top to make the drying room temperature uniform.
   USE - The method is useful for dry rotary microwave processing of GongCheng persimmon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is saving energy and can speed up drying process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dry rotary microwave processing of GongCheng persimmon comprises (i) grading and washing persimmon fruit is placing in a carbon dioxide (CO2) dehydration tank, and deodorization is carrying out for 24-30 hours under the conditions of a temperature of 25-28 degrees C and a CO 2 concentration of 98-99.9%, (ii) obtaining persimmon fruit by peeling and peeling persimmon fruit is placing on a drying sieve and sent to a drying room, a rotating microwave drying device is arranging in center of the drying room, and baking persimmon piece, sieve is placing around rotating microwave drying device, and persimmon piece is heating uniformly by microwave spin drying, (iii) removing a large amount of water vapor releasing during microwave drying by a high-temperature dehumidifier placing in a drying room, and (iv) installing many circulating fans in drying room, circulating fans are respectively arranging at front and rear ends of the drying room, and are arranging according to the upper and lower intervals on the opposite side to form a forced circulation air system from bottom to top to make the drying room temperature uniform, and persimmon pieces are completely dry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The rotating microwave drying device comprises a 360 degrees rotating radar launching head and a microwave magnetron disposing on radar transmitting head, the radar transmitting head is placing in center of drying room, and microwave magnetron is driving by radar transmitting head to be 360 degrees of rotation. The microwave drying is performing in two stages, in the first stage, operation is continuing for 10 hours under the condition of a temperature of 45 degrees C, and the second stage is starting after stopping for 2 hours, the second stage is continuously operating at a temperature of 60 degrees C for 12 hours. The high temperature dehumidification is performing in two stages, where the first stage, high temperature dehumidification humidity of the machine is set to work continuously at 70 degrees C for 6 hours, stop working after 1 hour, humidity is set at 50 degrees C for 3 hours, and after 2 hours, the second stage is starting, the second stage, the humidity is set at 30 degrees C in first 8 hours, and dehumidifier is turning off in last 4 hours, and air is ventilating naturally from drying room.

PN CN109319812-A
TI Preparing cerium carbonate from cesium alum as raw material comprises e.g. adding cesium alum into deionized water, adding lime milk, introducing carbon dioxide, adding the barium hydroxide solution, concentrating and drying.
AU WENG H
   WANG C
   ZHANG Y
   XING S
   SUN Z
   HE Y
AE XINJIANG RES INST NON FERROUS METALS (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201917296L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cerium carbonate from cesium alum as raw material comprises e.g. (i) adding cesium alum into deionized water and dissolving and heating, cooling and crystallizing and filtering, (ii) adding in deionized water and heating and dissolving to obtain fine cesium alum solution, slowly adding lime milk, and separating the solid solution, (iii) adding the barium hydroxide solution in 2-3 times, and centrifuging, (iv) carrying out carbonization by introducing carbon dioxide into the causticizing solution until the pH of the carbonizing solution is 7-10, concentrating the carbonized supernatant, and cooling to concentrated liquid, (v) filtering the concentrated liquid by polymer filtration device, and concentrating filtrate and crystallizing and drying at low temperature, (vi) decomposing low temperature material by high temperature, (vii) dissolving in deionized water, and then filtering to obtain filtered liquid solution, and (viii) concentrating the filtered liquid and drying.
   USE - The method is preparing cerium carbonate from cesium alum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple, high product yield (92-96%), high purity (99.9-99.99%), reduced the production cost by 30-50%, reduces production cost, ensures product quality, and better meets customer's demand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cerium carbonate from cesium alum as raw material comprises (i) adding cesium alum into deionized water and dissolving and heating, cooling and crystallizing and filtering, to obtain fine cesium alum, (ii) adding fine cesium alum in deionized water and heating and dissolving to obtain fine cesium alum solution, slowly adding lime milk in fine cesium alum solution, and separating the solid solution to obtain cesium sulfate solution, (iii) adding the barium hydroxide solution into cesium sulfate solution in 2-3 times, and centrifuging to obtain barium sulfate solid and causticizing solution, (iv) carrying out carbonization by introducing carbon dioxide into the causticizing solution until the pH of the carbonizing solution is 7-10, allowing the mixture to stand, concentrating the carbonized supernatant, and cooling to concentrated liquid, (v) filtering the concentrated liquid by polymer filtration device, and concentrating the filtrate and crystallizing and drying at low temperature to obtain low temperature material, (vi) decomposing low temperature material by high temperature to obtain high temperature material, (vii) dissolving the high temperature material in deionized water, and then filtering by a polymer filtration device to obtain filtered liquid solution, and (viii) concentrating the filtered liquid and drying to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), comprises adding cesium alum into deionized water and heating at 70-100 degrees C for dissolving to obtain solution, cooling the solution to room temperature, crystallizing, filtering to obtain filter cake, adding the filter cake to deionized water and heating at 70-100 degrees C for dissolving to obtain secondary solution, cooling the secondary solution to room temperature and then crystallizing, and filtering to obtain fine cesium alum. In step (ii), the temperature of the deionized water heating to dissolve the fine cesium alum is 70-100 degrees C, the molar ratio between fine cesium alum and lime milk is 1:0.5-2, and slowly adding 5-20 wt.% lime milk dropwise into fine sputum solution. In step (iii), adding deionized water into barium sulfate solid and stirring for 1-2 hours, and separating by solid-liquid separation using centrifuge, and using the separated supernatant to dissolve barium hydroxide. In step (iv), allowing the stand time is 4-8 hours, and concentrating the carbonized supernatant to 1/3 to 1/4 of the volume of carbonized supernatant. In step (v), the concentrated crystallization temperature is 150-200 degrees C, and the concentrated crystallization time is 4-8 hours, and/or the low temperature drying temperature is 150-200 degrees C, and the low temperature drying time is from 4-8 hours. In step (vi), the pyrolysis temperature of the low temperature material is 250-600 degrees C, and the pyrolysis time of the low temperature material is 4-8 hours. In step (viii), the concentration of the concentrated liquid temperature is 150-200 degrees C, and the concentrated drying time of filtered liquid is 4-8 h, the purity of cesium carbonate is 99.9-99.99%. The polymer filter device is polymer filter element. Preferred Components: In step (3), the molar ratio between fine cesium alum and barium hydroxide is 1:1-2, and adding barium hydroxide solution into cerium sulfate solution 2-3 times.

PN CN108588425-A; CN108588425-B
TI Treatment of cobalt-nickel metallurgical wastewater slag includes adding wastewater residue to slurry, mixing, introducing carbon dioxide, filtering, washing, melting, collecting, cooling, refining, recycling, extracting and reacting.
AU FANG J
AE FANG J (FANG-Individual)
GA 201879489Y
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of cobalt-nickel metallurgical wastewater slag includes adding wastewater residue to slurry, mixing, introducing carbon dioxide, mixing, filtering, washing hot water, placing in high-temperature melting furnace, melting at 950-1150 degrees C, sending external blast to melting furnace, melting for 4-6 hours, collecting flue gas, performing spray absorption, discharging, flowing to mold, cooling, placing metal block in electrolytic cell, refining copper, recycling electrolyte, treating, adding electrolyte to calcium oxide, filtering, pretreating, performing copper extraction, extracting copper, extracting with sulfuric acid, adding cobalt-nickel-containing raffinate, introducing chlorine gas, adjusting pH, mixing, reacting, filtering to obtain second filtrate and second filter residue, washing, adding cobalt acetate, mixing, spray drying, calcining to obtain battery grade tricobalt tetroxide, performing countercurrent extraction and performing concentrated crystallization.
   USE - Method of treating cobalt-nickel metallurgical wastewater slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short process, simple process and high recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treatment of cobalt-nickel metallurgical wastewater slag comprises adding wastewater residue to slurry, mixing, introducing carbon dioxide gas to slurry, mixing at 25-45 degrees C, filtering, washing filter residue with hot water, placing washed wastewater slag, coke, silica and scrap copper material in high-temperature melting furnace, melting at 950-1150 degrees C, sending external blast to melting furnace, melting for 4-6 hours, collecting flue gas using dust collecting device, performing spray absorption, discharging molten copper water, flowing to mold, cooling into metal blocks, placing metal block in electrolytic cell, refining copper to obtain cathode copper, recycling electrolyte, treating, adding electrolyte to calcium oxide to adjust the pH to 4-4.5, filtering to obtain first filtrate and first filter residue, returning the first filter residue to mixed wastewater residue, pretreating, performing copper extraction on first filtrate, extracting copper, extracting with sulfuric acid, adding cobalt-nickel-containing raffinate to battery-grade cobalt sulfate or battery-grade nickel sulfate adjusting solution, introducing chlorine gas to sodium hydroxide solution, adjusting pH to 11-11.5, mixing for 4-5 hours, reacting at 45-60 degrees C, filtering to obtain second filtrate and second filter residue, washing second filter residue, adding washed material to pure water slurry, adding cobalt acetate, mixing, spray drying, calcining in inert atmosphere to obtain battery grade tricobalt tetroxide, performing countercurrent extraction on second filtrate to obtain nickel sulfate solution and performing concentrated crystallization on nickel sulfate solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of washed wastewater slag, coke, silica and scrap copper is 5-10:1-2:0.3-0.6:20-30. The mass ratio of dust-removing material and sodium hydroxide is 1:0.2-0.3.

PN CN107982375-A; CN107982375-B
TI Preparing cold-release bilayer tablet comprises e.g. mixing anisetree bark, divaricate and myrrh powder, extracting, decocting, filtering, concentrating, drying, sieving, spraying volatile oil, pressing and coating.
AU ZHU F
AE HEILONGJIANG SHENGE PHARM CO LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 201836720L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cold-release bilayer tablet comprises (i) adding anisetree bark, Homalomena rhizome, divaricate Saposhnikovia root, frankincense and myrrh powder into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank, and collecting the volatile oil and medicinal dreg-A, (ii) mixing radix aconite kasnezoffii and processed nux vomica, adding 10 times of ethanol, extracting, each time for 1-3 hours, combining the extracting solution, and recycling the ethanol solution and medicinal dreg-B, (iii) mixing safflower, radix aconiti feri, agaric, and medicinal dreg-A, adding water to extract for 1-3 times, decocting, combining the decoctions, filtering, concentrating, recycling the ethanol, adding medicinal dreg-B, vacuum extracting and concentrating and drying under reduced pressure to obtain dry extract, and (iv) crushing the dried extract, adding starch, mixing uniformly, sieving, spraying volatile oil, pressing, and coating sugar or film.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cold-release bilayer table.
   ADVANTAGE - preparation method is environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cold-release bilayer tablet comprises (i) adding 81 pts. wt. anisetree bark, 81 pts. wt. Homalomena rhizome, 81 pts. wt. divaricate Saposhnikovia root, 40 pts. wt. frankincense and 40 pts. wt. myrrh powder into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank at 40-55 degrees C under pressure 15-20 MPa for 1-2 hours, the carbon dioxide flow is 10 L/hour and collecting the volatile oil and medicinal dreg-A, (ii) mixing 24 pts. wt. radix aconite kasnezoffii and 8 pts. wt. processed nux vomica, adding 10 times of 70-90% ethanol, extracting for 2-4 times, each time for 1-3 hours, combining the extracting solution, and recycling the ethanol solution and medicinal dreg-B, (iii) mixing 40 pts. wt. safflower, 24 pts. wt. radix aconiti feri, 81 pts. wt. agaric, and medicinal dreg-A, adding water to extract for 1-3 times, each time adding 6-10 times of water, decocting for 1-2 hours, combining the decoctions, filtering, concentrating, recycling the ethanol, adding medicinal dreg-B, vacuum extracting and concentrating until the relative density is 1.40 (50 degrees C), and drying under reduced pressure to obtain dry extract, and (iv) crushing the dried extract, adding starch, mixing uniformly, sieving, spraying volatile oil, pressing, and coating sugar or film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The total content of benzoyl mesaconine is 0.07-0.1%, preferably 0.08-0.1%, the tannin is 1-2%, preferably 1.3-1.8%, and the hydroxy safflower yellow pigment is more than 0.1%, preferably 0.15%. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the extraction temperature is 50 degrees C, the pressure is 20 MPa, and the extracting time is 2 hours. In the step (ii), the concentration of ethanol is 70%, the extracting time is 3 times, and the extracting time is 2 hours. In the step (iii), the extracting time is 3 times, the added water is 8 times, and the decocting time is 2 hours. The preparation method comprises (i) adding anisetree bark, Homalomena rhizome, divaricate Saposhnikovia root, frankincense and myrrh powder into the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank at 50 degrees C under pressure 20 MPa for 2 hours, the carbon dioxide flow is 10 L/hour and collecting the volatile oil and medicinal dreg-A, (ii) mixing radix aconite kasnezoffii and processed nux vomica, adding 10 times of 70% ethanol, extracting for 3 times, each time for 2 hours, combining the extracting solution, and recycling the ethanol solution and medicinal dreg-B, (iii) mixing safflower, radix aconiti feri, agaric, and medicinal dreg-A, adding water to extract for 3 times, each time adding 8 times of water, decocting for 2 hours, combining the decoctions, filtering, concentrating, recycling the ethanol, adding medicinal dreg-B, vacuum extracting and concentrating until the relative density is 1.40 (50 degrees C), and drying under reduced pressure to obtain dry extract, and (iv) adding beta -cyclodextrin and 2-5 times the amount of water, adding volatile oil, grinding into a paste, and drying at low-temperature to obtain volatile oil beta -cyclodextrin, mixing 70% volatile oil beta -cyclodextrin, 30% hydroxypropylmethylcellulose K4M, microcrystalline cellulose, and 90% ethanol as viscous agent, granulating by 20 meshes sieve, and whole grain by 14 meshes sieve, and rapid drying at room temperature to obtain volatile oil slow-release layer particles, crushing 51% dried extract, adding 30% starch, and 9% calcium phosphate, granulating by 20 meshes sieve, whole grain by 14 meshes sieve, drying at 60 degrees C for 1 hour, adding 1% magnesium stearate and mixing to obtain immediate-release layer particles, pressing the slow-release layer particles and the immediate-release layer particles into a lower of hardness sheet, and laminating the two layers.

PN CN107881458-A
TI Processing stainless steel surface treatment by using food machinery involves selecting and cleaning surface of stainless steel surface of plate, polishing and drying to obtain polished plate and subjecting at carbon dioxide gas treatment.
AU WU X
AE WUHE HUANGHUAI GRAIN & OIL MACHINERY CO (WUHE-Non-standard)
GA 2018279802
AB    NOVELTY - Processing stainless steel surface treatment by using food machinery involves selecting and cleaning surface of stainless steel surface of plate, polishing and drying to obtain polished plate. The obtained polished plate is subjected under carbon dioxide gas in a high-temperature baking oven at 450-500 degrees C, treat it for 10-15 minutes, raise the temperature to 600-650 degrees C, and continue processing for 4-10 minutes to obtain treated stainless steel plate. The treated stainless steel plate is loaded into a high vacuum coating machine, vanadium ions are implanted under a vacuum of 0.00025-0.00045Pa.
   USE - Method for processing stainless steel surface treatment by using food machinery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process stainless steel surface treatment by using food machinery, which has high temperature processing and high vacuum coater coating, and heat preservation, strong corrosion resistance and antibacterial property, good stability, effectively kill multiple bacteria in contact, and widely applied to stainless steel of food machinery field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing stainless steel surface treatment by using food machinery involves selecting and cleaning surface of stainless steel surface of plate, polishing and drying to obtain polished plate. The obtained polished plate is subjected under carbon dioxide gas in a high-temperature baking oven at 450-500 degrees C, treat it for 10-15 minutes, raise the temperature to 600-650 degrees C, and continue processing for 4-10 minutes to obtain treated stainless steel plate. The treated stainless steel plate is loaded into a high vacuum coating machine, vanadium ions are implanted under a vacuum of 0.00025-0.00045Pa, and the implantation dose is 6.5-8.5x 1017 ions/cm2, resulting in a vanadium film of layer thickness 12-18nm. The degree of vacuum is adjust to 0.003-0.005Pa, implant titanium ions, injection dose is 1.2-2.2x 1017 ions/cm2, the resulting titanium film thickness is 40-60nm, where vanadium ion includes 2 wt.% monovalent vanadium ions, 36 wt.% divalent vanadium ions, 53 wt.% trivalent vanadium ions and 9 wt.% tetravalent vanadium ions. The stainless steel with steam at 120-140 degrees C for 2-3 minutes. After completion, place the sample in the muffle furnace, raise the temperature to 350-400 degrees C, incubate for 1.5-2.5 hours, and naturally cool to room temperature to obtain the desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The stainless steel is cleaned, and subjected under air-dried in hot air at 60-80 degrees C. The thickness of the high-temperature carburized layer obtained is 40-70 mu m. The energy of the monovalent vanadium ion is 40 keV, energy of the divalent vanadium ion is 80 keV, energy of the trivalent vanadium ion is 120 keV, and energy of the tetravalent vanadium ion is 160 keV.

PN US2018086683-A1; WO2018064539-A1
TI Production of methoxy compounds e.g. methyl ester from base raw material, involves converting base raw material to synthesis gas, conditioning synthesis gas to remove moisture and carbon dioxide and producing methanol from synthesis gas.
AU SAPIENZA R
AE SAPIENZA R (SAPI-Individual)
   SAPIENZA R (SAPI-Individual)
GA 201824689K
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methoxy compounds from base raw material, involves receiving the base raw material in a reformer system, converting the received base raw material to synthesis gas in the reformer system, conditioning the synthesis gas to remove moisture and carbon dioxide, producing (100) methanol from the conditioned synthesis gas at operational temperatures of below 205 degrees C, and producing methoxy compounds from the methanol.
   USE - Production of methoxy compounds e.g. methylformate, dimethyl ether, methyl ester, acid methyl esters, dimethyl ether, methyl t-butyl ether, t-amyl methyl ether, and methyl ethers form base raw material e.g. natural gas, biomass, carbonaceous base fossil material, carbonaceous renewable raw material, methane and ethane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is robust, efficiently produces methoxy compounds with high yield from base raw material, and reduces complexity. The method is economical as it does not employ cryogenic air separation. The apparatus removes high freeze point hydrocarbons at higher pressure than the existing apparatus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) apparatus for producing methoxy compounds from natural gas received (10) from a natural gas reserve, which consists of a drilling operation for recovering the natural gas, and operatively connected to the reformer system configured to convert the natural gas to the synthesis gas, a temperature swing adsorption unit for receiving the synthesis gas from the reformer system and configured to condition (40) the synthesis gas to remove moisture and carbon dioxide to produce a conditioned synthesis gas, a continuous flow stir-tank reactor configured to receive the conditioned synthesis gas from the adsorption unit and to produce methanol from the conditioned synthesis gas, and a reactive distillation tower configured to receive the methanol from the continuous flow stir-tank reactor to produce methoxy compounds from the methanol; and
   (2) process for gas drilling, which involves flaring a methane-containing gas, employing at least a portion of the methane-containing gas to produce methoxy compounds via production of methanol from synthesis gas. The method is performed in the apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an exemplary process for converting natural gas to methanol and dimethyl ether.
   Receiving process of natural gas (10)
   Pretreatment process (20)
   Conditioning of synthesis gas (40)
   Processing of syngas (50)
   Production of methanol (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: Production of methanol uses a liquid containing catalyst system operating below 205 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Conversion of natural gas to synthesis gas involves converting methane to synthesis gas using a flare gas unit. The method further involves feeding resulting gas to the continuous flow stirred-tank reactor, directing methanol vapor from the reactor to the reactive distillation tower, producing the methoxy compounds by integrating a synthesis gas reaction zone and a dehydration zone, introducing methanol into the reactive distillation tower to produce a dimethyl ether top stream and a water bottom stream, producing fatty acid methyl ester from the methanol in the presence of one of an alkali, an alkali-solid, an acidic-solid and an enzyme catalyst, and producing fatty acid methyl ester from the methanol in the absence of catalyst at atmospheric pressures. Conversion of base raw material to synthesis gas involves pretreating the natural gas to remove hydrogen sulfide, producing at least 50% methyl formate, and integrating a methyl formate production system for continuous production of methoxy-containing product obtained from methyl formate. Conversion of base raw material to synthesis gas is carried out by a method selected from oxygen transport membrane reforming, steam reforming, partial oxidation, autothermal reforming and/or dry reforming, gasification. Preferred Composition: Carbon oxides per pass conversion is higher than 65% and selectivity to methanol formation is at least 75%.

PN CN107602933-A
TI Preparing cellulose aerogel material by uniformly mixing and stirring succinic acid and potassium hydroxide, heating mixed mixture in water bath, leaving still and aging, centrifugally separating, and collecting lower-layer precipitates.
AU ZHOU Y
   JI M
   CHEN Q
AE ZHOU Y (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 2018085995
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of cellulose aerogel material involves: (1) uniformly mixing and stirring succinic acid and potassium hydroxide, heating the mixed mixture in a water bath, leaving still and aging, centrifugally separating, collecting lower-layer precipitates, freeze-drying under vacuum, and grinding to prepare matrix powder; and (2) uniformly mixing and stirring lithium chloride, microcrystalline cellulose and dimethylacetamide, heating the mixed mixture under water bath to obtain a cellulose solution, leaving still and cooling the cellulose solution to room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cellulose aerogel material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is useful for preparing cellulose aerogel material with excellent shrinkage resistance and high strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of cellulose aerogel material involves: (1) uniformly mixing and stirring succinic acid and potassium hydroxide with mass fraction of 5% in mass ratio of 1:5, heating the mixed mixture in a water bath, leaving still and aging, centrifugally separating, collecting lower-layer precipitates, freeze-drying under vacuum, and grinding to prepare matrix powder; (2) uniformly mixing and stirring lithium chloride, microcrystalline cellulose and dimethylacetamide in mass ratio of 1:5:10, heating the mixed mixture under water bath to obtain a cellulose solution, leaving still and cooling the cellulose solution to room temperature, placing in a beaker, leaving still and aging to obtain cellulose gel; (3) weighing 45-50 pts. wt. cellulose gel, 10-15 pts. wt. matrix powder and 5-8 pts. wt. (3-aminopropyl)triethoxysilane into a three-neck flask, carrying out heat preservation reaction, leaving still and heating to room temperature to obtain a mixed solution; and (4) weighing 45-50 pts. wt. mixed solution, 10-15 pts. wt. isocyanate and 3-5 pts. wt. 200-mesh bacterial cellulose, then placing weighed components in a three-necked flask, hydrolyzing and heating in a bottle, leaving still, cooling to room temperature, putting in a high-pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide into the kettle, leaving still and introducing nitrogen after the carbon dioxide is introduced, maintaining pressure and continuously introducing carbon dioxide, keeping the temperature, slowly removing the pressure to be communicated with atmospheric pressure, leaving still and cooling to room temperature after the pressure is completed to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The heat preservation reaction is carried out at a temperature of is -5 degrees C to 0 degrees C.

PN CN114118509-A; CN114118509-B
TI Method for predicting carbon dioxide (CO 2)            emissions from road traffic, involves predicting            spatial grid data of road traffic carbon dioxide            emissions in target year to realize forecast of road            traffic carbon dioxide emissions.
AU LV C
   YANG L
   XIA C
   CAI B
AE MIN ECOLOGICAL ENVIRONMENT ENVIRONMENTAL (ECOL-Non-standard)
GA 202242407B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dividing an area to be                measured into multiple reference grids in space for                the area to be tested in a future target year. A                weighted road network density spatial distribution                density data set of a future target year is                obtained. A survival probability of passenger cars                and commercial vehicles in new energy vehicles are                obtained as an age survival probability of new                energy vehicles according to a number of                registrations and ownership of new energy vehicles                in historical years. A fuel vehicle ownership of                motor vehicles in a future target year is allocated                to each benchmark grid and a spatial grid data of                road traffic carbon dioxide emissions in the target                year to realize the forecast of road traffic carbon                dioxide emissions.Based on the weighted road                network spatial distribution density data of each                benchmark grid, the number of fuel vehicles in each                benchmark grid, and the functional relationship                between the number of fuel vehicles and carbon                dioxide emissions
   USE - Method for predicting CO 2 emissions from road                traffic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully considers the traditional                fuel vehicle gradually eliminating and                popularization of new energy vehicle, can more                effectively reveal road traffic electrified                popularization the time-space difference of the                carbon dioxide emission of the road traffic. The                space model can simultaneously identify road                traffic carbon dioxide emissions on the time and                space of heterogeneity, good for the filter area of                the discharge, providing more targeted emission                reduction target. On the space grid layer,                realizing the prediction of road traffic CO 2                emissions in any grid area, so as to finish the                fast prediction of the CO 2 emission peak of                different areas. The road traffic electric                gasification rate is predicted according to vehicle                survival curve and new energy vehicles sale                permeability, according to the weighted road                network density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                prediction system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a road traffic CO 2 emission prediction method.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

PN CN111701687-A; CN111701687-B
TI Iron ore powdering device based on gas propulsion and liquid carbon dioxide unloading, has gas driving module that is connected to air inlet at high-pressure gas bin, and utilized to pass high-pressure gas into high-pressure gas bin.
AU FAN Y
   QIAO J
   FENG C
   LI S
   CHENG P
   ZHANG L
   ZHANG G
   LUO H
AE CAS QIANYAN SHENZHEN ENG SCI DEV CO LTD (CASQ-Non-standard)
GA 202096897F
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a high-pressure liquid tank (6), a liquid drive module, a high-pressure gas storage and a gas drive module. The high-pressure liquid silo is provided with a feed port (4). One side of the high-pressure liquid tank is provided with the liquid driving module. The gas driving module is connected to an air inlet provided at one end of the high-pressure gas bin, and is used to pass high-pressure gas into the high-pressure gas bin. The second rupture plate at one end of the high-pressure liquid tank ruptures, when the liquid pressure in the high-pressure liquid tank reaches a preset threshold. The first rupture plate is broken, and the high-pressure gas pushes the iron ore out of the high-pressure liquid tank. The liquid carbon dioxide that penetrates the iron ore is gasified and expanded under the action of high pressure gas. The iron ore is pulverized and discharged from the discharge port of the high-pressure liquid tank.
   USE - Iron ore powdering device based on gas propulsion and liquid carbon dioxide unloading.
   ADVANTAGE - The pulverization of iron ore can be achieved based on high-pressure gas propulsion and liquid carbon dioxide unloading. The high-pressure gas can achieve the instantaneous high pressure gradient after the rupture of the thick rupture disc. The ore is pushed to leave the high-pressure liquid carbon dioxide warehouse quickly, so that the liquid carbon dioxide stored in the iron ore gasifies and expands under the conditions of high pressure gradient to pulverize the ore.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an iron ore powdering method based on gas propulsion and liquid carbon dioxide unloading.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the iron ore powdering device based on gas propulsion and liquid carbon dioxide unloading.
   Liquid inlet (1)
   Liquid outlet (2)
   Air outlet (3)
   Feed port (4)
   High-pressure liquid tank (6)

PN KR1984711-B1
TI Container-type smart farm system for cultivating e.g. fruit crop, has moisture supply unit installed at heat exchanger and supplying moisture to culture space, and cooling part contacted in fluid and cooling adopted fluid in water chiller.
AU KAM D H
AE UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)
GA 201950602X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a container section for forming a culture space (S) as a space for cultivating crop. A carbon dioxide application part (120) separates carbon dioxide from an outer side and supplies the carbon dioxide to the culture space. A heating-cooling unit (130) controls temperature between a culture hole and the culture space. Insulation material is prepared with size according to a bottom surface of the inner wall area. A copy type air-conditioning and heating equipment is provided with a base unit that forms an internal space for receiving fluid when a movable part is prepared in the lower part at the container section. A heater part heats up the fluid, which is adapted to the internal space. A moisture supply unit is installed at a heat exchanger and supplies moisture to the culture space. A cooling part is contacted in the fluid and cools the adopted fluid in a water chiller.
   USE - Container-type smart farm system for cultivating crop e.g. fruit crop and vegetables crop.
   ADVANTAGE - The system supplies the carbon dioxide by utilizing the carbon dioxide application so as to reduce cost for supplying the carbon dioxide. The system reduces energy in the cooling-and-heating so as to reduce cost in cooling-and-heating. The system reduces the emitted thermal energy to the outer side container so as to reduce operating cost according to the smart farm operation. The carbon dioxide application part and dehumidification part generate water for the heating-cooling unit and are ventilated so as to sharply reduce generated water consumption in the smart farm operation. The system reduces temperature distribution in the culture hole by utilizing the heating-cooling unit with the copy type and reduces stress of the cultivated crop.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a container-type smart farm system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Culture space (S)
   Carbon dioxide application part (120)
   Heating-cooling unit (130)
   Circulation part (140)
   Control block (150)

The greater flexibility of plant operation is                achieved. PN CN114712984-A
TI Natural gas SMB hydrogen production amine            absorption decarburization full temperature range            pressure swing adsorption recycling carbon dioxide            replacement process, comprises e.g. shifting gas from            shift reaction in hydrogen production process by steam            reforming of natural gas, shifting gas to system.
AU ZHAN J
   WANG L
   ZHONG Y
   CHEN Y
AE SICHUAN TIANCAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202292912H
AB    NOVELTY - Natural gas SMB hydrogen production amine                absorption decarburization full temperature range                pressure swing adsorption recycling carbon dioxide                replacement process, comprises (1) shifting gas                from shift reaction in process of hydrogen                production by steam reforming of natural gas SMB,                maintaining temperature and pressure of shift gas                flowing out of reactor outlet, then cooling or heat                exchanger, and then introducing to the                medium-temperature pressure swing adsorption                concentration process, (2) introducing cooled shift                gas to medium-temperature PSA concentration system,                alternately operating multiple adsorption towers to                ensure continuous entry of raw gas, passing through                precision filter, then directly enters PSA                purification hydrogen process in SMB process,                carrying out PSA adsorption and desorption cycle                operation, and (3) subjecting carbon                dioxide-enriched gas CO2CG from medium-temperature                PSA concentration process to catalytic                hydrogenation or air oxidation.
   USE - The method is useful for natural gas SMB                hydrogen production amine absorption                decarburization full temperature range pressure                swing adsorption recycling carbon dioxide                replacement process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is alternative upgrade and                technical transformation of the organic amine                solvent or amine solution absorption                decarbonization and carbon dioxide recovery process                in the process of natural gas steam reforming SMB                hydrogen production using full temperature range                pressure swing adsorption FTrPSA recovery carbon                dioxide method; fully utilizes SMB conversion gas                as raw material gas based on pressure swing                adsorption PSA and hydrogen, small amount of carbon                monoxide and methane are the main non-adsorbed                phase gas components and main adsorbate carbon                dioxide impurity components in the temperature                range of 60-160&#8451; and the difference of                adsorption separation coefficient and physical and                chemical properties in the pressure range of                atmospheric pressure to 2.4 MPa; separating and                purifying the desired carbon dioxide from the                reformed gas with high yield, high purity, low                energy consumption and low cost and making the                carbon dioxide content in the non-adsorption phase                gas-hydrogen-rich gas produced in the medium                temperature pressure swing adsorption system less                than 1% without affecting the efficiency and load                of the PSA purification hydrogen process in the SMB                hydrogen production process and minimizing carbon                emissions from natural gas SMB hydrogen                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Natural gas SMB hydrogen production amine                absorption decarburization full temperature range                pressure swing adsorption recycling carbon dioxide                replacement process, comprises (1) shifting gas                from the shift reaction in the process of hydrogen                production by steam reforming of natural gas SMB,                maintaining the temperature of the shift gas                flowing out of the reactor outlet to                300-350&#8451;, and pressure to 1.5-2.5 MPa,                then cooling to 60-160&#8451; by a cooler or                heat exchanger, and then introducing to the                medium-temperature pressure swing adsorption (PSA)                concentration process, where the composition of the                shift gas is hydrogen with a volume percentage of                50-65%, carbon dioxide with a volume percentage of                20-25%, methane with a volume percentage of 5-10%                and carbon monoxide, light hydrocarbon compounds                and water (balance amount), (2) introducing the                cooled shift gas to the medium-temperature PSA                concentration system with an operating temperature                of 60-160&#8451; and an operating pressure of                1.5-2.5 MPa, alternately operating the multiple                adsorption towers to ensure the continuous entry of                raw gas, at least one adsorption towers in the                adsorption state, and the remaining adsorption                towers are in the regeneration state, passing                through the precision filter, then directly enters                the PSA purification hydrogen process in the SMB                process to purify hydrogen, carrying out PSA                adsorption and desorption cycle operation between                the adsorption towers, the control and adjustment                of the pressure change through the control system                composed of the regulating valve and/or the program                control valve and/or other valves arranged on the                connecting pipeline, forming closed cycle of                adsorption and desorption with the adsorption step,                flowing carbon dioxide-rich concentrated gas CO2CG                from the bottom of the adsorption tower with carbon                dioxide concentration of greater than or equal to                92% whose yield is greater than or equal to 90%,                and transferring the part to the next carbon                dioxide refining process, and returning part to the                reformer in the process of hydrogen production from                natural gas SMB as fuel gas, where the medium                temperature PSA concentration system is PSA                composed of multiple adsorption towers in series or                in parallel or in series and parallel, the                adsorption phase gas is hydrogen-rich gas, and the                carbon dioxide content is less than or equal to 1%,                the adsorption tower in the medium temperature PSA                concentration system is filled with adsorbent                combinations of activated alumina, silica gel,                activated carbon, molecular sieve and/or carbon                molecular sieve, the desorption process includes                displacement, or smooth discharge, pressure drop,                reverse discharge, vacuum, flush, vacuum + flush,                pressure increase, final charge, or a combination                of multiple steps, forming with the adsorption                step, and (3) subjecting carbon dioxide-enriched                gas CO2CG from the medium-temperature PSA                concentration process to catalytic hydrogenation or                air oxidation to remove small amount of carbon                monoxide, ethane and carbon 2 and light                hydrocarbons above carbon 2 in the CO2CG, deep                drying and one-time adsorption purification,                introducing the obtained purified CO2CG to the                carbon dioxide low-temperature rectification,                fractional condensation or flash evaporation system                to obtain carbon dioxide liquid product with a                purity greater than or equal to 99.99%, where the                non-condensable gas obtained from is used as the                deep drying regeneration gas, a part of the                generated hot regeneration waste gas is used as the                flushing gas of the medium temperature PSA                concentration system, and the other part is used as                the regeneration gas after cooling and thus the                yield of the obtained carbon dioxide liquid product                is greater than or equal to 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The shift gas is                  low-temperature shift in the process of hydrogen                  production from natural gas SMB, and the                  temperature is 120-160&#8451;, the pressure                  is 1-2 MPa, and which directly enters the                  medium-temperature PSA concentration process                  without cooling process. The desorption process                  includes replacement, and the replacement gas in                  the replacement process comes from the carbon                  dioxide concentrated gas CO2CG, or the degassing                  gas composed of the forward gas, reverse gas,                  vacuuming or flushing waste gas or from the                  stripping gas of the PSA purification hydrogen                  process in the natural gas SMB hydrogen                  production process, from the carbon dioxide                  concentrated gas CO2CG, the replacement step is                  set to be carried out after the end of the                  adsorption step, and the operating pressure of                  the replacement step is equal to the operating                  pressure of the adsorption step, returning the                  replacement waste gas produced in the replacement                  step to the shift gas feed for recycling, the                  purity of carbon dioxide concentrated gas CO2CG                  produced by the replaced medium temperature PSA                  system is greater than or equal to 96%, and the                  yield of the hydrogen product obtained by the PSA                  purification hydrogen process in the natural gas                  SMB hydrogen production process is increased by                  0.1-0.5% when the yield is greater than or equal                  to 90%. The desorption process includes flushing,                  and the flushing gas in the flushing process                  comes from the downstream gas, shift gas, or PSA                  purifying hydrogen process in the process of                  natural gas SMB hydrogen production, or the                  smooth gas/pressure equalization gas/stripping                  gas/flushing waste gas, or the non-condensable                  gas of carbon dioxide purification process or the                  regeneration gas of carbon dioxide refining                  process comes from natural gas SMB hydrogen                  production process, the downstream gas of the PSA                  purification hydrogen process is the flushing gas                  formed by the flushing gas of the PSA                  purification hydrogen process as the flushing gas                  of the medium temperature PSA concentration                  process, a part of the generated flushing exhaust                  gas is returned to the shift gas feed to further                  recover carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and a part                  is used as the fuel gas of the natural gas SMB                  hydrogen-making reformer and thus increasing the                  yield of carbon dioxide and hydrogen by 0.1-0.5%.                  The desorption includes the final charge, the                  final charge comes from shift gas, or the reverse                  degassing/degassing gas of the PSA purification                  hydrogen process in the natural gas SMB hydrogen                  production process, or the non-condensable gas of                  the carbon dioxide refining process, or the                  regeneration gas of the carbon dioxide refining                  process. The desorbed gas from the PSA                  purification hydrogen process in the natural gas                  SMB hydrogen production process is pressurized to                  2.5-3 MPa by the compressor and enters the                  hydrogen recovery system composed of the                  first-stage hydrogen permeable membrane and a                  membrane module, returning the hydrogen-rich gas                  flowing out from the permeate side to the feed of                  the PSA purification hydrogen process, and                  further recovering hydrogen and obtaining the                  yield of the hydrogen product with a purity                  greater than or equal to 99.99% from the PSA                  purification hydrogen process is greater than                  95%, does not need to pressurize the carbon                  dioxide-rich gas flowing from the non-permeate                  side, or directly returning to the medium                  temperature PSA concentration process as a shift                  gas feed or enters the replacement step of the                  medium temperature PSA concentration process as                  replacement gas, returning the replacement waste                  gas generated in the replacement step to the                  shift gas feed for recycling, where the purity of                  the carbon dioxide concentrated gas CO2CG                  produced by the replaced medium temperature PSA                  system is greater than or equal to 96%, and the                  yield is greater than or equal to 92%, obtaining                  the yield of hydrogen product by the PSA                  purification hydrogen process in the natural gas                  SMB hydrogen production process i.e increased by                  0.1-0.5% and thus the whole process of natural                  gas steam reforming SMB hydrogen production                  realizes zero emission of carbon dioxide. The                  shift gas in the process of hydrogen production                  from natural gas SMB directly enters the PSA                  purification hydrogen process, and the components                  of the desorbed gas obtained therefrom are 50%                  hydrogen, 13% methane, 34% carbon dioxide, 2% by                  volume carbon monoxide and water and carbon 2 and                  above light hydrocarbon organic matter, the                  temperature is normal temperature, the pressure                  is normal pressure, transporting by blast and                  filtering by a precision filter, then heating to                  60-90&#8451; by heat exchange with the                  preheating gas, and then enters the                  medium-temperature PSA concentration process to                  obtain hydrogen-rich gas with a carbon dioxide                  content of less than 5% from the non-adsorption                  phase, pressurizing to the adsorption pressure                  required by the PSA purification hydrogen                  process, and returning to the PSA purification                  hydrogen process and obtaining the yield of                  99.99% hydrogen product is greater than or equal                  to 95% to obtain carbon dioxide concentrated gas                  with a concentration greater than or equal to 96%                  from the adsorption phase, the yield is greater                  than or equal to 92%, a part of which enters the                  carbon dioxide refining process, and returning                  part to the reformer in the process of hydrogen                  production from natural gas SMB as fuel gas, the                  content of carbon dioxide in the hydrogen-rich                  gas is adjusted based on the load of the PSA                  purification process of hydrogen, the                  requirements of operation flexibility and the                  requirements of carbon dioxide enriched gas, and                  the zero emission of carbon dioxide in the SMB                  hydrogen production process of natural gas steam                  reforming is realized. Pc The adsorbent                  combinations of activated alumina, silica gel,                  activated carbon, molecular sieve and/or carbon                  molecular sieve are filled in the adsorption                  tower in the medium-temperature PSA concentration                  system, where the molecular sieve includes 4A, 5A                  and 13X, i.e. the molecular sieve that is mixed                  in proportion, the ratio of the combination of                  various adsorbents is distributed based on the                  concentration and pressure of carbon dioxide in                  the raw gas, the packing ratio of molecular sieve                  is 60-70% when the pressure is less than 1 MPa                  and/or the carbon dioxide concentration is less                  than 15% of the shift gas and the packing ratio                  of silica gel/activated carbon and molecular                  sieve is 40-50% and 50-60% when the pressure is                  greater than 1 MPa and/or the carbon dioxide                  concentration is greater than 15% of the shift                  gas.

PT P
PN RU2749130-C1
TI DEVICE FOR EXTRACTING OXYGEN GAS FROM MIXTURE OF CARBON DIOXIDE AND OXYGEN.
AU KUZNETSOV L G
   KUZNETSOV YU L
   BURAKOV A V
   EFREMOV A A
   ABRAMOV A I
AE KUZNETSOV L G (KUZN-Individual)
   KUZNETSOV YU L (KUZN-Individual)
   BURAKOV A V (BURA-Individual)
   EFREMOV A A (EFRE-Individual)
   ABRAMOV A I (ABRA-Individual)
GA 202164617G
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the field of equipment for the chemical and gas industry, as well as to life support systems for people in confined spaces, isolated objects, bunkers, pressure chambers, in space and underwater vehicles. The invention concerns a device for extracting oxygen gas from a mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen, which contains a case made of a non-magnetic material, equipped with inlet and outlet gas pipes, while a gas-permeable porous partition is installed in the inner cavity of the case. The gas-permeable porous partition is made of a magnetic material and is located horizontally in the inner cavity of the case. A solenoid connected to a direct electric current source is installed on the outer surface of the case, under the action of which a magnetic field is created in the pores of the partition, directed vertically along the axis of the inner cavity. The gas inlet pipe for the mixture of oxygen and carbon dioxide and the gas outlet pipe for the removal of carbon dioxide are located below the partition, and the gas outlet pipe for the removal of oxygen is located above the partition.
   USE - Chemical and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Creation of a silent small-sized device that allows for the effective separation of a mixture of oxygen and carbon dioxide by using the property of oxygen (paramagnetic) to be drawn into the area of a strong magnetic field and the property of carbon dioxide (diamagnetic) to be pushed out of the area of a strong magnetic field.
1 cl, 1 dwg

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

AE SKYTREE BV (ISYS-C)

PT P
PN WO2018048382-A1; WO2018048382-A8; US2019153488-A1
TI Converting gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide into lipid, involves culturing first organism in presence of gaseous substrate, and culturing second organism in presence of volatile fatty acids.
AU STEPHANOPOULOS G
   HU P
   LIU H
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
   MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
GA 201821651Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method of converting a gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide into a lipid, involves (a) culturing a first organism in the presence of the gaseous substrate, under conditions suitable for the first organism to reduce the carbon dioxide in the presence of one or more reducing agents, optionally hydrogen or carbon monoxide, and (b) culturing a second organism in the presence of the volatile fatty acid(s) produced in (a) under conditions suitable for the organism to convert the volatile fatty acid(s) into lipid.
   USE - The method is useful for converting a gaseous substrate into a lipid, which is used for biodiesel production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method converts gaseous substrate into a lipid in a cost-effective manner at an energetic efficiency greater than 75%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of converting a gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide into a lipid, involves (a) culturing a first organism in the presence of the gaseous substrate, under conditions suitable for the first organism to reduce the carbon dioxide in the presence of one or more reducing agents, optionally hydrogen or carbon monoxide, where the organism synthesizes one or more volatile fatty acid(s) by reduction of the carbon dioxide, and (b) culturing a second organism in the presence of the volatile fatty acid(s) produced in (a) under conditions suitable for the organism to convert the volatile fatty acid(s) into lipid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The steps (a) and (b) are integrated by a continuous processing scheme producing lipid from gas comprising carbon dioxide. The step (b) achieves a lipid titer of at least 15 grams per liter, and a lipid content of at least 30%. The carbon dioxide produced by the process in (b) is used as a feed gas for the process in (a). The non-lipid biomass generated by the process in (b) is used as a cell culture media component for use in processes (a) and (b). The energetic efficiency of acetate to lipid conversion in the integrated process is greater than 50%, measured as the conversion of energy in the hydrogen feed to lipid produced, where the lipid produced is modeled as tripalmitin. Preferred Components: The gaseous substrate comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, or their mixtures. The gaseous substrate comprises a synthesis gas (syngas). The volatile fatty acid(s) is acetic acid. The lipid comprises triacylglyceride. The triacylglyceride comprises fatty acid groups that are less than 50% saturated fatty acid groups. The triacylglyceride comprises fatty acid groups that are more than 50% oleate (C18.1), linolinate (C18.2) and palmitoleate (C16.1). Preferred Microorganism: The first organism is chosen from Moorella thermoacetica, Clostridium ljungdahlii, C.carboxidivorans P7T, C.ragsdalei, Alkalibaculum bacchi, C.autoethanogenum, C.drakei, and Butyribacterium methylotrophicum, preferably M.thermoacetica. The first organism captures carbon sourced from carbon dioxide as acetyl-coenzyme A (CoA) with a rate that is at least 1 g acetic acid/l-hour. The first organism captures carbon sourced from carbon dioxide as acetyl-CoA with an efficiency that is at least 92%. The first organism has an optimal growth temperature (T-opt) greater than 40 degrees C. The second organism is Yarrowia lipolytica. The Y.lipolytica is genetically modified to enhance lipid production, where the genetic modification comprises upregulation of one or more genes whose products (a) push carbon flux into the pathway leading to lipid synthesis, or (b) pull carbon flux through the pathway leading to lipid synthesis. The second organism comprises at least one genetic modification providing increased expression of acetyl-CoA carboxylase and/or comprises at least one genetic modification providing increased expression of diacylglycerol acyltransferase.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)
MC D05-H08A; H06-B04A; H06-P; H09-F03

AE DEYANG MEIHAO MACHINERY CO LTD (DEYA-Non-standard)
GA 2022E1986U
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a smart type carbon                dioxide protection welding machine moving trolley,                relating to the field of mobile trolley, comprising                a moving trolley: the inner part of the moving                trolley is provided with a welding machine, the                left side surface of the moving trolley is provided                with an anti-collision protection mechanism, four                corners of the lower surface of the moving trolley                are provided with a moving wheel, and the lower                surface of the moving trolley is provided with two                groups of brake mechanisms, the utility model is                provided with a brake mechanism, when the position                of the moving trolley is adjusted, starting the                driving motor to make the driving gear rotate, the                driving gear and the driven gear plate are meshing                to each other, so that the driven gear plate drives                the brake sheet to move towards the outer side, the                final brake sheet is propped against the surface of                the moving wheel, limiting the moving wheel to                rotate continuously, ensuring the stability of the                moving trolley after stopping, then ensuring the                using quality of the welding machine, reverse                driving the driving motor to make the brake block                away from the moving wheel, which can make the                moving trolley to move continuously, the structure                is simple, and it is suitable for                popularization.
