   SHAO Q
   WEI Z
   QIAN J
   CHENG L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SCI-TECH (UZST-C)
GA 2022755235
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silver nanowire doped with                laser-induced fluffy graphene-polydimethoxysiloxane                (AgNWs-LiG-PDMS) sandwich structure flexible                surface enhanced Raman spectrum (SERS) substrate                involves preparing laser-induced fluffy graphene                (LiG) on polyimide film (PI film) by using carbon                dioxide laser for induction. The LiG is transferred                to PDMS flexible substrate by polydimethoxysiloxane                (PDMS) transfer strategy to give induced fluffy                graphene-polydimethoxysiloxane (LiG-PDMS) bilayer                structure. The silver nanowires (AgNWs) are spun on                the surface of LiG, and AgNWs-LiG-PDMS sandwich                structure flexible SERS substrate is prepared after                the AgNWS is completely dried.
   USE - Method for preparing an AgNWs-LiG-PDMS                sandwich structure flexible surface enhanced Raman                spectrum substrate for use in environmental                monitoring, food safety and forensic                medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare silver nanowire                that has good anti-tensile property, good                conductivity, and excellent anti-stretching effect                and effectively surface the Raman effect. The PDMS                provides a flexible substrate, tensile property is                good, however, AgNWS is directly coated on the                surface of hydrophobic PDMS, silver nanowires are                easy to fall off, LiG layers are added, deposition                and stability of AgNWS are facilitated, and good                conductivity of LiG is beneficial to enhancing SERS                performance of the silver nanowires.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The PDMS flexible                  substrate comprises PDMS and fixing agent, where                  PDMS and fixing agent are mixed in a ratio of                  10:1 and spread in a polytetrafluoroethylene                  container to a thickness of about 1-8 mm.Preferred Composition: The AgNWs suspension                  is an ethanol or acetone solution of AgNWs with a                  concentration of 0.2-3 milligram/milliliter                  (mg/ml).Preferred Conditions: The excitation power                  of carbon dioxide laser is 6-30%, scanning                  frequency is 60-180 kilohertz (KHZ), and scanning                  speed is 10-60 millimeter/second (mm/s). The                  graphene prepared by inducing the PI film by                  ultraviolet laser is prepared into graphene                  materials with different shapes and sizes by C4D                  software. The sample after spin coating is placed                  in a vacuum drying oven at a drying temperature                  of 40-60&#8451; for 6-12 hours. The                  AgNWs-LiG-PDMS composite is prepared where bottom                  layer is flexible PDMS, middle layer is LiG, and                  top layer is AgNWs.Preferred Process: The LiG-PDMS bilayer                  structure is prepared by sticking the prepared                  LiG with the front side facing downwards in a                  polytetrafluoroethylene container paved with PDMS                  and curing agent mixed colloid, transferring the                  container into vacuum drying oven for curing, and                  stripping PI film after curing to obtain the                  flexible LiG-PDMS double-layer structure.

PN CN114378542-A
TI Method for making standard test block for non-destructive testing of crack defects, involves cooling workpiece to normal temperature, and continuously configuring carbon dioxide gas shielded welding to weld till forming complete welding seam.
AU LIU J
   LI Y
   WU C
   ZHOU H
   SHANG Z
   LIU Q
   LI Z
AE JIANGSU VOCATIONAL & TECH SHIPPING COLLE (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022604185
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves assembling a pair of steel plates with V-shaped grooves, and carbon dioxide (CO2) gas shielded welding is configured. A bottom welding is performed at the position of a butt groove of a steel plate, and the welding is continued after the bottom welding is completed. A carbon arc gouging is configured to make a notch at the predetermined position of the weld, and an aluminum alloy strip is placed in a notch. The welding is filled with CO2 gas shielded welding, some steel plates and aluminum alloy strips are melted in the notch, and the welding seam is rapidly cooled. The molten aluminum alloy bar is shrinked to form cracks during rapid cooling. A workpiece is cooled to room temperature, and welded with CO2 gas shielded welding till a complete weld is formed.
   USE - Method for making standard test block for non-destructive testing of crack defects.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes controlling the crack length, width, depth and so on in the test block with good reliability, high detection rate, high quantitative precision, and high detection efficiency, no pollution and low operation cost

   USE - Aluminum-magnesium-calcium alloy powder used                in the field of fireworks and firecrackers                (claimed), used as a photochromic agent, a reducing                agent and a loudness agent of fireworks and                firecrackers.
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminum-magnesium-calcium alloy powder                absorbs sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide in the                setting-off process of the fireworks, reduces the                emission of harmful gas sulfur dioxide and                greenhouse gas carbon dioxide after the fireworks                are set off, enhances the setting-off brightness of                the fireworks to a certain degree. The preparation                method of the aluminum-magnesium-calcium alloy                powder reduces the loss of raw materials in the                smelting process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing an aluminum-magnesium-calcium alloy                powder, which involves smelting and cooling a                calcium-aluminum alloy ingot, a magnesium ingot and                an aluminum ingot to obtain a smelted alloy ingot,                crushing and ball-milling the obtained smelted                alloy ingot to obtain the                aluminum-magnesium-calcium alloy powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The smelting                  temperature is 780-850degrees Celsius. The mass                  ratio of calcium-aluminum alloy ingot, magnesium                  ingot and aluminum ingot is 10-40:100-150:80-150.                  The content of calcium in the calcium-aluminum                  alloy ingot is 50-80 wt.%. The cooling process is                  carried out under inert gas conditions. The                  melted alloy ingot is crushed to a size that                  allows ball milling and the crushing is performed                  under an inert gas condition. The ball milling is                  performed under an inert gas condition. The                  ball-milled material is sieved. The mesh size is                  10-500 mesh. The sieving is performed under an                  inert gas condition.

TI Multi-pass welding process for joining butt of stainless steel tubes having high wall thickness, by initiating welding arc between tubes by current carrying filler wire, feeding welding arc using wire feeder from wire spool to groove between tubes, and filling groove by depositing melting wire.
AU NAINIAPPAN R
   ARUNACHALAM S
   PACHORI A
   PERIAKARUPPAN S
   ANGAMUTHU V V
AE BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
GA 202245878Q
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-pass welding process for joining butt of stainless steel tubes having high wall thickness, involves initiating welding arc between the tubes by a current carrying filler wire, feeding the welding arc using a wire feeder from a wire spool to a groove between two tubes, filling the groove by depositing a melting wire as weldment, and enclosing the groove by alternatingly supplying shielding gases from gas resource through gas alternator. The welding process includes semi-automatic gas metal arc welding process with gas alternator. The predetermined flow rate is 17 l/minute for argon and 2 l/minute for carbon dioxide, where argon flows for 0.3 seconds and carbon dioxide flows for 0.1 second through the gas alternator. The number of passes is dependent on the thickness of the tubes.
   USE - The multi-pass welding process is useful for joining butt of stainless steel tubes having high wall thickness.
   ADVANTAGE - The process reduces the production cycle time, enhances the weld joint quality from the tungsten electrode arc welding process and similar processes, and the tubes with high wall thickness can be easily welded using the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The tubes are composed of super 304H stainless steel.

TI Electrochemical method for producing calcium hydroxide comprises e.g. dissolving a calcium precursor in a first solution in contact with a first electrode to produce calcium ions, and transporting calcium ions across membranes.
AU ZENYUK I
   PEREGO A
   LI M
   KULKARNI D S
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 2022267657
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical method for producing calcium hydroxide, comprises: (a) dissolving a calcium precursor in a first solution in contact with a first electrode to produce calcium ions; (b) transporting calcium ions across a first membrane from the first solution into a second solution using a first electrochemical potential; (c) producing hydroxide ions at a second electrode; (d) transporting the hydroxide ions across a second membrane into the second solution using a second electrochemical potential; and (e) precipitating calcium hydroxide from the second solution.
   USE - The method is useful for: producing calcium hydroxide; and producing cement clinker (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has improved energy efficiency and reduced carbon dioxide emissions; and utilizes renewable electricity, lower-temperature manufacturing, and carbon dioxide capture and reuse in the electrolyzer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing cement clinker comprising providing a calcium hydroxide produced, and combining the calcium hydroxide with oxides and/or silicates to produce the cement clinker; and
   (2) an apparatus for the electrochemical production of calcium hydroxide comprising an anode, a cathode, and a flow-through channel between the anode and the cathode, where a first membrane or separator is between the anode and the flow-through channel and a second membrane or separator is between the cathode and the flow-through channel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The apparatus further comprises a flow field in contact with a first side of the anode, where the flow field comprises an anode inlet and an anode outlet configured to supply a first solution to the anode, and further comprises a channel inlet and a channel outlet, where the channel inlet is configured to supply a flow of a second solution to the channel and the channel outlet is configured to direct the flow of the second solution and precipitated calcium hydroxide away from the channel. The apparatus further comprises a flow field in contact with a first side of the cathode, where the flow field comprises a cathode inlet and a cathode outlet configured to supply a third solution to the cathode. The anode comprises a first catalyst, where first catalyst comprises iridium oxide. The cathode comprises a second catalyst, i.e. platinum nanoparticles supported on carbons. The anode and/or the cathode comprises a gas diffusion layer, where the channel has a length, measured between the first membrane or separator and the second membrane or separator, of 100 mu m-10 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first electrochemical potential is produced by water electrolysis or chlorine evolution. The second electrochemical potential is produced by water electrolysis or carbon dioxide electrolysis. The method further comprises: (f) collecting at least one by-product from the production of the first electrochemical potential or production of the second electrochemical potential. The first electrochemical potential is produced by oxidation of at least one hydrogenated fuels. The current is supplied to the anode and/or the cathode using a renewable energy source. Preferred Components: The calcium precursor comprises calcium carbonate. The first electrode is an anode, where the first solution has an acidic pH. The first membrane is a polymer electrolyte membrane. The second electrode is a cathode, where the second membrane comprises an anion exchange membrane. The second solution is a salt solution, where the second solution flows through a channel between the first membrane and the second membrane. The by-products comprise hydrogen gas, carbon monoxide gas, or chlorine gas. The hydrogenated fuels comprise methanol, ammonia, hydrazine, or sodium borohydride, where the hydrogenated fuels are in a liquid state.
   EXAMPLE - The example section provides the details of analysis and characterization of electrochemical methods that do not supports the claims.

PN CN113522320-A
TI Preparing ellipsoidal bismuth oxyhalide photocatalyst useful for e.g. degradation of organic dye, involves adding bismuth salt and halogen source to solvent, stirring, adding surface modifying agent polyvinylpyrrolidone, stirring, hydrothermally reacting, centrifuging, washing, and drying.
AU LU S
   TAO T
   LIANG B
   ZHAO X
   DAI W
   YAO Y
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 2021C4118K
AB    NOVELTY - A preparation method of ellipsoidal bismuth oxyhalide photocatalyst, involves (1) weighing bismuth salt and a halogen source, adding to solvent, and magnetically stirring uniformly to obtain solution (I), (2) adding surface modifying agent polyvinylpyrrolidone to the solution (I), continuously magnetically stirring, and leaving still until the surfactant is completely dissolved to obtain solution (II), (3) transferring the solution (II) into a hydrothermal reactor, and hydrothermally reacting at 150-180degrees Celsius to obtain solution (III) after finishing the reaction, and (4) centrifuging the solution (III), washing, and drying to obtain a final product.
   USE - Preparation method of ellipsoidal bismuth oxyhalide photocatalyst useful for degradation of organic dye, photocatalytic hydrogen production, and photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of ellipsoidal bismuth oxyhalide photocatalyst with controllable parameters and excellent degradation effect to organic dye, which is simple and convenient to operate, and effectively regulates the size, appearance and specific surface area of bismuth oxyhalide nanomaterial.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   1. an ellipsoidal bismuth oxyhalide photocatalyst; and
   2. application of the ellipsoidal bismuth oxyhalide photocatalyst in degradation of organic dye, photocatalytic hydrogen production, and photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The halogen source in step (1) is potassium chloride, cetyltrimethylammonium chloride, potassium iodide, and sodium chloride. The bismuth salt is bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate and bismuth chloride. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the bismuth salt to the halogen source in step (1) is 1:1. The concentration of the bismuth salt and halogen source dissolved in the solvent to form solution (I) is 0.5-2 mol/L. The molar ratio of the surfactant in step (2) to the bismuth salt in the step (1) is 400:1. Preferred Conditions: The rotating speed of the magnetic stirring in step (1) is 300-600 rpm, and the stirring time is 2-3 hours. The reaction time of the hydrothermal reaction in step (3) is 12-24 hours. In the step (4), the centrifugation is performed by centrifuging at a speed of 4200 rpm for 2-3 minutes, the washing is performed by washing 2-3 times with deionized water. The drying process is carried out at a temperature of 80degrees Celsius.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The solvent is deionized water or ethylene glycol.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN112940827-A; CN112940827-B
TI High-base-value thixotropic calcium sulfonate antirust agent comprises calcium sulfonate with super-high base value, dispersing medium, de-ionized water and benzene sulfonic functional graphene, fulvic acid and sodium bicarbonate.
AU WANG S
   GAO J
   PAN L
   ZHOU X
   LI Y
AE SHENYANG PARKERIZING CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021708240
AB    NOVELTY - High-base-value thixotropic calcium sulfonate antirust agent comprises 40-50 pts. wt. calcium sulfonate with super-high base value, 30-50 pts. wt. dispersing medium, 5-10 pts. wt. de-ionized water and 2-3 pts. wt. benzene sulfonic functional graphene, 2-3 pts. wt. fulvic acid and 5-10 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - Used as high-base-value thixotropic calcium sulfonate antirust agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses sodium bicarbonate as the raw material to decompose and generate carbon dioxide at high temperature, instead of the existing common technology, uses equipment to pass carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, which is economical, convenient to operate, and can replace similar imported products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the high-base- calcium sulfonate antirust agent, comprising adding super-high base value synthetic calcium sulfonate and dispersing medium into the reaction kettle, stirring and dispersing for 10-20 minutes at normal temperature, adding de-ionized water, continuously stirring for 10-20 minutes, adding fulvic acid, continuously stirring for 10-20 minutes, heating the system to 100 degrees C, adding benzene sulfonic acid functionalized graphene and sodium bicarbonate, continuously stirring and reacting for 2-3 hours, heating to 130 degrees C, vacuumizing and dehydrating, stirring for 1-2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The total alkali value of the calcium sulfonate synthesized with super-high base value is more than 500 mg KOH/g. The dispersion medium is mineral oil, synthetic oil or hydrocarbon solvent oil. The mass ratio of de-ionized water to the graphene powder is 50-100:1. The mass ratio of the amino benzene sulfonic acid to sodium nitrite to de-ionized water to concentrated hydrochloric acid is 4-5:2:1:1. The mass ratio of aromatic diazonium salt solution to the graphene aqueous dispersion solution is 3-4:10. The aminobenzene sulfonic acid is 2-amino benzene sulfonic acid and 4-amino benzenesulfonic acid. The concentration of concentrated hydrochloric acid is 37.5%. Preferred Method: The preparation method of benzene sulfonic acid functionalized graphene comprises taking graphene powder dispersed in de-ionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-2 hours to obtain graphene water dispersion, adding amino benzenesulfonic acid and sodium nitrite into de-ionized water, stirring and dispersing for 5-10 minutes, then adding concentrated hydrochloric acid under ice bath conditions, stirring and reacting for 30-50 minutes to obtain aromatic diazonium salt solution, adding aromatic diazonium salt solution and graphene water dispersion into the reaction kettle, stirring and reacting for 4-6 hours under the protection of nitrogen, centrifuging and washing the product with de-ionized water, freezing and drying for 24-48 hours.

PT P
PN CN112624068-A
TI Method for purifying high-purity argon, involves transporting purified argon through activated carbon adsorption box, filtering argon through activated carbon adsorption box, and removing hydrocarbons and oil contained in argon.
AU ZHANG Z
AE SHANGHAI LIANFENG ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021402079
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves opening the storage tank valve. The liquid argon in the cryogenic storage tank is flowed into the air-heated vaporizer, and the liquid argon is heated and vaporized. The vaporized argon is transported into the purification tank through the high-pressure pipeline, and adsorbent and deoxidizer are added. The adsorbent reacts with the oxygen agent to absorb the carbon dioxide, impurities and oxygen in the argon to generate solid compounds. The purified argon is transported through the activated carbon adsorption box, and the argon is filtered through the activated carbon adsorption box under high pressure. The carbon dioxide, nitrogen and impurities accumulated in the argon are adsorbed, and the hydrocarbons and oil contained in the argon are removed. The treated argon gas is subjected to cooling and pressure swing treatment, adsorbed to reduce the water content of the argon gas, discharged through the product gas collection port, and stored in a storage tank.
   USE - Method for purifying high-purity argon.
   ADVANTAGE - The purification of high-purity argon gas is realized by adopting an adsorbent and an activated carbon adsorption box to adsorb and filter impurities, which is convenient to operate and has high working efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the method for purifying high-purity argon. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

GA 202104824R
AB    NOVELTY - The system, comprises a soda machine adapted to carbonize water from the water supply to a predetermined Carbon Dioxide (CO2) concentration. The soda machine having a CO2 container containing CO2 at a CO2 container pressure. A soda tank (202) is connected to the water supply and to the CO2 container. A relief pipe with a controllable solenoid valve is adapted to release the pressure from the soda tank during the filling of the soda tank with water. A CO2 release mechanism is used for releasing and injecting CO2 gas from the CO2 container directly into the soda tank through a nozzle located in the soda tank. A check valve is installed in the line connecting the CO2 container and the soda tank and preventing CO2 gas from flowing back from the soda tank to the CO2 container.
   USE - Liquid supply system for providing a variety of water-based liquids, dispatched with the desired properties from a countertop faucet.

PT P
PN CN112121837-A; CN112121837-B
TI Porous graphitic carbon nitride/cesium lead bromide heterojunction material for reducing carbon dioxide, is uniform-spectrum response type perovskite and graphite-like carbon nitride composite photocatalytic material.
AU WANG Y
   YAN Y
   ZHU Z
   HUO P
   LI Z
   LI Y
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2021013629
AB    NOVELTY - Porous graphitic carbon nitride (P-CN)/cesium lead bromide (CsPbBr3) heterojunction material is uniform-spectrum response type perovskite and graphite-like carbon nitride composite photocatalytic material synthesized from graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4), cesium carbonate and lead bromide. The massive CsPbBr3 is tightly supported on porous g-C3N4 to form sheet-like porous structure.
   USE - P-CN/CsPbBr3 heterojunction material used for reducing carbon dioxide in uniform spectrum range (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The P-CN/CsPbBr3 heterojunction material is simple, cheap and easy to obtain, non-toxic and harmless, has low cost and short reaction time, reduces energy consumption and reaction cost, facilitates mass production, and in eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing P-CN/CsPbBr3 heterojunction material, which involves:
   (A) placing lead bromide, octadecene, oleylamine and oleic acid in beaker, stirring uniformly until lead bromide is dissolved, adding g-C3N4, reacting in oil bath for period of time, adding oleate solution containing cesium, cooling in ice water bath, washing with n-hexane by centrifugation, and drying to obtain B-CN/CsPbBr3; and
   (B) placing dodecanol, sodium lauryl sulfonate, gum arabic, deionized water and B-CN/CsPbBr3 in beaker, stirring in water bath, freeze-drying, washing with deionized water and ethanol multiple times, and drying at high temperature to obtain P-CN/CsPbBr3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The dosage ratio of lead bromide, octadecene, oleylamine and oleic acid is 2.1 g:10-12 mL:1 mL:1 mL. The mass ratio of g-C3N4 to lead bromide is 1-4:2.1. The dosage ratio of cesium carbonate, octadecene and oleic acid is 0.3-0.5 g:15 mL:2.5 mL. The dosage ratio of g-C3N4 to oleate solution is 1-4 g:12 mL. The temperature of oil bath is 150-200 degrees C for 30-50 minutes. The dosage ratio of dodecanol, sodium dodecyl sulfonate, gum arabic, deionized water and B-CN/CsPbBr3 is 5 mL:2 g:2 g:100-150 mL:0.5-4 g. The temperature of water bath is 50-70 degrees C for 20-30 minutes. The temperature of high-temperature drying is 100-110 degrees C for 20-24 hours. Preferred Process: The cesium carbonate, octadecene and oleic acid are mixed until cesium carbonate is dissolved.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN111849878-A
TI Improving osteogenic differentiation capability of mesenchymal stem cells by inoculating mesenchymal stem cells, culturing, using pancreatin digestion passage, culturing and adding osteogenic inducing solution and metformin aqueous solution.
AU GAO Y
   PU F
   CHENG R
AE SHAANXI BIO STEM CELLS REGENERATION MEDI (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2020A96924
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving osteogenic differentiation capability of mesenchymal stem cells involves (1) inoculating mesenchymal stem cells in alpha -minimum essential medium (MEM) culture medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator and using 0.2% pancreatin digestion passage when the fusion degree of mesenchymal stem cells is 80-85% and (2) inoculating the mesenchymal stem cells obtained in step (1) into an alpha -MEM culture medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, placing the mesenchymal stem cells in a carbon dioxide incubator for culturing and adding osteogenic inducing solution and metformin aqueous solution for induction when the density of the mesenchymal stem cells is greater than 90%, where the induction time is 14-21 days.
   USE - The method and the product are useful for improving osteogenic differentiation capability of mesenchymal stem cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has a significant inducing effect on the osteogenic differentiation of mesenchymal stem cells and can significantly improve the expression of mesenchymal stem cell osteogenic differentiation related genes SIRT1, ALP, COL1A1, RUNX2 and BGLAP, where the improvement of osteogenic differentiation capacity of the mesenchymal stem cells is of great significance for stem cell transplantation and bone biological research.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a product for improving osteogenic capability of mesenchymal stem cells comprising metformin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mesenchymal stem cells in step (1) comprise bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells, adipose mesenchymal stem cells, synovial mesenchymal stem cells or umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells. Preferred Composition: The osteogenic inducing solution is alpha -MEM medium containing 50 ug/ml ascorbic acid, 100 nM dexamethasone and 10 mM beta sodium glycerophosphate. The concentration of metformin in step (2) is 50-400 ummol/l.

Advantages: simple structure, convenient use, introducing carbon dioxide into the catalyst solution and introducing the steam, the silica gel precipitation viscosity is reduced and has good fluidity; then the silica gel particles are quickly collected and precipitated by adding the aggregation agent; the quick separation of the silica gel and the catalyst solution is realized; the separation efficiency is effectively improved; and the continuous separation can be realized; and the operation of the system is stable.

PN CN111763158-A; CN111763158-B
TI Preparation of hexazinone intermediate guanidine            compound for preparing hexazinone, by adding            N-cyano-N-methylcarbamate to dimethylamine carbonate,            reacting, then removing polar solvent, drying, adding            inert solvent, and decomposing.
AU GENG B
   DENG Y
   YANG H
   TONG L
   LUO J
AE JIANGSU LANFENG BIOCHEMICAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A2086V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hexazinone intermediate guanidine                compound involves: (S101) reacting dimethylamine                with carbon dioxide in a polar solvent at a                temperature lower than the boiling point of                dimethylamine to form dimethylamine carbonate;                (S102) adding N-cyano-N-methylcarbamate to                dimethylamine carbonate and reacting at more than 0                degrees C and less than the boiling point of the                polar solvent to produce a carbonate mixture                solution of hexazinone intermediate guanidine                compound; (S103) removing the polar solvent from                the carbonate mixture solution and drying to obtain                a carbonate of guanidine compound; and (S104)                adding an inert solvent to the carbonate of                guanidine compound under the catalysis of an                organic ammonia catalyst, and decomposing into                guanidine compound and carbon dioxide. The                hexazinone intermediate guanidine compound is                N-methyl-N-N(N',N'-dimethylamidino) ethyl                carbamate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hexazinone                intermediate guanidine compound, which is useful                for preparing hexazinone (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and safe preparation                of the hexazinone intermediate guanidine compound                with high yield in an environmentally-friendly                manner, and does not generate wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of hexazinone, which involves                subjecting the hexazinone intermediate guanidine                compound to addition reaction with cyclohexyl                isocyanate to form an adduct, and carrying out                cyclization reaction on the adduct.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step (S102)                  further involves stopping the reaction until the                  residual N-cyano-N-methylcarbamate is less than                  0.1%. Preferred Conditions: The reaction                  temperature environment in step (S101) is 0 plus                  minus 2 degrees C. The reaction temperature                  environment in step (S102) is 65-70 degrees C.                  The reaction temperature environment in step                  (S104) is 40-50 degrees C. Preferred Components:                  The polar solvent in step (S101) includes                  ethanol, N,N-dimethylformamide and/or dimethyl                  sulfoxide. The inert solvent in step (S104)                  includes toluene and/or benzene. In step (S104),                  the organic ammonia catalyst includes                  triethylamine and/or tri-n-butylamine.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high gel strength aquatic                product meat paste product comprises crushing fish                or shrimp into surimi or minced shrimp; adding 2-3%                salt in surimi or minced shrimp, vacuum chopping                for 15-30 minutes, and forming; and putting the                formed surimi or minced shrimp in high pressure                carbon dioxide (CO2) processing kettle, processing                for 5-60 minutes at 30-60 degrees C and 10-30 MPa,                and obtaining surimi or minced shrimp product with                high gel strength.
   USE - The method is for preparation of high gel                strength aquatic product meat paste product i.e.                surimi or minced shrimp product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method inactivates cathepsin L that causes                the deterioration of surimi gel through a specific                high-pressure CO2 non-thermal processing method,                improving the gel strength of the surimi or minced                shrimp products and the storage stability of the                product. It is simple, energy-saving, and                environment-friendly, produces high gel strength                surimi or minced shrimp products, and maintains the                unique flavor and nutritional components of the                meat paste products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) meat paste product;
   (2) application of the method in improving gel                strength of meat paste product; and
   (3) application of high pressure CO2 treatment                in inactivating cathepsin L.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The density of CO2 is                  0.2900-0.9480 g/ml. The vacuum chopping and                  mixing step comprises mixing for 15-30 minutes at                  3-5 degrees C, 60-100 kPa and 1500-3500                  revolutions/minute (rpm). The fish includes                  Tilapia, grass carp, silver carp, Decapterus                  maruadsi, lizard fish, Nemipterus virgatus, and                  Trachinotus ovatus. The shrimp comprises                  Macrobrachium rosenbergii, crayfish, Penaeus                  japonicus, Litopenaeus vannamei, Penaeus                  chinensis, Penaeus monodon, and antarctic krill.                  The cathepsin L is placed in high pressure CO2                  processing kettle and processed for 5-60 minutes                  at 30-60 degrees C and 10-30 MPa.

PN CN111395310-A
TI Carbonizing and mineralizing photosynthetic microorganism by pouring calcium hydroxide solution into residual soil, refilling with photosynthetic bacteria, passing carbon dioxide into soil at low pressure and circulating above steps again.
AU YU X
   YU C
   CAI X
   XU Y
AE UNIV WENZHOU (UNWZ-C)
GA 2020666617
AB    NOVELTY - Method for carbonizing and mineralizing photosynthetic microorganism involves (i) pouring the calcium hydroxide solution into the residual soil, (ii) refilling an equal volume of photosynthetic bacteria solution, (iii) passing carbon dioxide into the soil at low pressure for 6 hours, and (iv) circulating the steps (i) to (iii) until the calcium hydroxide solution and photosynthetic bacteria solution are not injected.
   USE - Method for carbonizing and mineralizing photosynthetic microorganism.
   ADVANTAGE - The method strengthens residual soil, is environmentally-friendly and economical, and makes the solidified soil possess a certain self-repairing ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a construction equipment used in the method for carbonization and mineralization of photosynthetic microorganism to reinforce residual soil comprising transport carrier, container installed on the transport carrier, lifting mechanism installed on the transport carrier and pressure plate arranged on the lifting mechanism, where the pressing plate has solution chamber, and the lower end of the pressing plate is provided with plunger having infusion channel, communicating infusion channel with the solution cavity, where the infusion tube has outlet at the end of the plunger and communicating the solution cavity with the container through a catheter, and equipping the container with a high-pressure spray device to inject the solution into the infusion channel of the pressure plate at high pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The calcium hydroxide solution comprises 10 g/l calcium hydroxide supernatant. Preferred Method: The photosynthetic bacteria solution is prepared by (i) dissolving 5 g beef extract and 3 g peptone in 1 l deionized water solution, and adjusting the pH to 9, (ii) using tin foil to secure the opening of the conical flask and placing in a sterilization pot for sterilization, sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 25 minutes, maintaining the air pressure in the sterilization pot at 0.1 MPa and cooling to normal temperature, (iii) inoculating 2% strains in a sterile operating table, after the inoculation, placing in a constant temperature shaking incubator and incubating at 30 degrees C for 36-48 hours with an OD value of 1.78, and (iv) placing the cultured bacteria liquid in a refrigerator at 4 degrees C and refrigerating. The construction equipment method involves arranging the pressure plate and lifting mechanism below the container, where the lifting mechanism is a hydraulic lifting mechanism comprising multiple hydraulic rods, fixing the base on the hydraulic rod, pressure plate on the lower side of the base, connecting the end with the solution cavity and the container in the pressure plate and forming the infusion channel in the catheter. Preferred Components: The photosynthetic bacteria comprises cyanobacteria and/or purple bacteria.

PN CN110128852-A
TI Nano calcium carbonate preparation involves formulating calcium hydroxide slurry into suspension and crystal form controlling agent, and then carrying out carbonization by introducing gas containing carbon dioxide.
AU JIANG X
   ZHONG T
AE XUANCHENG XINWEIHUA CHEM TECH CO LTD (XUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201972736T
AB    NOVELTY - Nano calcium carbonate preparation involves formulating calcium hydroxide slurry into a suspension having a mass percentage concentration of 90-120%, and a crystal form controlling agent of 0.1-3.0 wt.% of the calcium hydroxide slurry at 15-35 degrees C, and then carrying out carbonization by introducing a gas containing carbon dioxide. The surface treatment agent is formulated into an aqueous solution at 60-90 degrees C to obtain a surface treatment agent aqueous solution. The carbonized nano-calcium carbonate slurry is aged in an aging apparatus. 2.0-6.0 wt.% surface treatment agent aqueous solution is added to the aged nano-calcium carbonate slurry with stirring uniformly and coating for 0.5-3.5 hours. The surface treated nano calcium carbonate slurry is filtered to a water content of less than 60%, where the filter cake is dried at 100-150 degrees C, and then pulverized and sieved to obtain the nano calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing nano calcium carbonate for epoxy adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the nano calcium carbonate in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with good dispersibility, greatly improved physical properties such as thixotropy, temperature resistance, bonding strength, toughness, hardness and strength without agglomeration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal form controlling agent is an inorganic salt, an ammonia salt, a citric acid, a disaccharide, a polysaccharide or their combinations. The surface treatment agent is a composite of a fatty acid, a fatty acid salt and a silane-based surface treatment agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersing agent is a hydrophilic surfactant, a sulfonate surfactant, a sulfuric acid, ester salt surfactants or phosphate salt surfactants.

PT P
PN US2019208770-A1
TI Freezing chicken cartilage containing undenatured type II collagen comprises exposing the cartilage to gas at specific temperature to freeze the cartilage so that the collagen of the cartilage essentially remains in undenatured state.
AU MOORE E R
AE MOORE E R (MOOR-Individual)
GA 2019603211
AB    NOVELTY - Freezing chicken cartilage containing undenatured type II collagen comprises exposing the cartilage to a gas at -0.65 to -1 degrees C to freeze the cartilage so that the type II collagen of the cartilage essentially remains in the undenatured state.
   USE - The method is useful for freezing chicken cartilage to obtain frozen chicken cartilage (all claimed) which is used to alleviate the symptoms of arthritis in mammals. Test details are described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: freezes more than 80%, preferably 95% the collagen of, thus the cartilage remains in the undenatured state (claimed); utilizes carbon dioxide which eliminates the requirement to add up to 40-60% a salt to the cartilage; eliminates the need for the long, low temperature drying step; is safe; provides the product which can be packaged so that a consumer can easily vary a dose size to find a preferred dose, and repeat that dose; utilizes aqueous carbon dioxide which is safe for storage of type II collagen while maintaining the biological activity of this cartilage tissue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for freezing chicken cartilage containing undenatured type II collagen comprising exposing the cartilage to the gas to freeze the cartilage so that more than 80%, preferably 95% the collagen of the cartilage remains in the undenatured state.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The gas consists essentially of carbon dioxide.
   ACTIVITY - Antiarthritic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Fresh chicken sternal cartilage was washed with a dilute aqueous detergent solution, stripped free of visible flesh and then soaked in an aqueous solution containing 8 g of 5.15 wt.% sodium hypochiorite/l of water. The chicken cartilage was then rinsed with an aqueous 3 wt.% hydrogen peroxide solution. The chicken cartilage was cooled to 0 degrees C and then the chicken cartilage was exposed to carbon dioxide gas at -0.65 degrees C to -1 degrees C. The chicken was cooled to minus 0.65 degrees C. but then the temperature of the chicken cartilage remains constant at a zero rate of change before finally cooling to a temperature less than minus 0.65 degrees C. The chicken cartilage was then cooled to -15 degrees C for long term storage to obtain freeze chicken cartilage.

PN CN208968850-U
TI Mars drilling and sampling device based on supercritical carbon dioxide, has supercritical carbon drilling trolley that generates electricity, and provides electric energy to entire system together with battery provided in trolley.
AU YANG X
   ZHOU X
   ZHU H
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 201954686X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a Mars drilling and sampling device based on supercritical carbon dioxide jet flow. The device mainly includes a new supercritical CO2 Mars drilling trolley and a stepped oscillating jet flow drilling bit device. The implementation process is as follows: after the landing of the drilling trolley, the control center adjusts the self-balance of the trolley, and punches a fixing anchor stock, fixes the trolley, installs the drilling bit and the drilling rod, opens the sucking pump, extracts CO2 and transforms it into supercritical state by high temperature and high pressure treatment, CO2 enters the transportation pipeline, part of CO2 is prepared into dry ice abrasive particles by dry ice abrasive machine, the supercritical CO2 jet flow mixed with dry ice abrasives is ejected after oscillating in the self-excited oscillating chamber, the supercritical CO2 abrasive jet flow with oscillation hits the rock and breaks the rock, at the same time, the drilling pump works and drives a three-wing drilling bit to drill forward, and a rock debris collection detector collects and detects the rock debris, and feeds the test results back to the control center. Compared with the existing method, the method of the utility model has the advantages of high rock breaking efficiency, low energy consumption and fast drilling speed.

PN CN109734133-A; CN109734133-B
TI Preparation of capacitance type manganese acid lithium includes mixing manganese chloride and lanthanum chloride, pyrolyzing, collecting dust, heating, filling carbon dioxide, reacting, cooling, discharging, grinding and spray drying.
AU WANG K
   WANG H
   CAO D
   GONG L
   HUANG F
   FANG M
   GONG J
   LUO R
   WANG C
AE WANG K (WANG-Individual)
   CHINA GREATPOWER JINCHUAN ADVANCED BATTE (CHGR-Non-standard)
GA 201945188H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of capacitance type manganese acid lithium comprises mixing manganese chloride and lanthanum chloride to obtain manganese mixed solution, sending to pyrolysis furnace, pyrolyzing at 600-800 degrees C, collecting dust by cyclone, adding powder material to polyethylene glycol solution, mixing, sending to reaction kettle, heating at 120-160 degrees C, filling carbon dioxide, mixing, reacting for 3-4 hours, cooling, discharging material to obtain reaction slurry, adding lithium hydroxide in the reaction slurry, mixing at 60-75 degrees C, reacting for 2-3 hours, grinding, spray drying to obtain 5-8 mu m powder, placing powder in roller furnace, calcining in heating section, insulating section and cooling section, sieving to 200-250 mesh and packaging. The heating rate of heating section is 80-100 degrees C per hour. The temperature of insulation section is 650-700 degrees C. The holding time of insulation section is 5-6 hours. The cooling section is cooled by frozen water. The cooling rate is 90-110 degrees C per hour.
   USE - Method of preparing capacitance type manganese acid lithium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple preparation process, high production efficiency and low cost. The lithium has high heat and high temperature performance and does not produce waste water in the process of precursor preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of manganese chloride, lanthanum chloride and aluminum chloride is 1:0.01-0.02:0.02-0.04. The total molar number of manganese, lanthanum and aluminum in mixed solution of manganese is 2-2.5 mol/L. The mass ratio of powder to polyethylene glycol solution is 1:2-3. The concentration of polyethylene glycol solution is 0.1-0.2%. Preferred Method: The residence time of droplets in pyrolysis furnace is 15-30 seconds.

TI Preparing one or more types of lithium compounds            from lithium-containing mineral involves leaching            lithium-containing mineral in aqueous alkaline            carbonate leach solution for liberating lithium            compounds and phosphate from mineral.
AU TIIHONEN M
AE OUTOTEC FINLAND OY (OUTO-C)
   OUTOTEC FINLAND OY (OUTO-C)
   OUTOTEC FINLAND OY (OUTO-C)
   OUTOTEC FINLAND OY (OUTO-C)
GA 201900133Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing one or more types of lithium                compounds from lithium-containing mineral involves                leaching lithium-containing mineral in aqueous                alkaline carbonate leach solution for liberating                lithium compounds and phosphate from                lithium-containing mineral to obtain a leach slurry                containing lithium carbonate and phosphate,                carrying out carbonization step by reacting                obtained leach slurry with an alkali earth metal                compound in the presence of carbon dioxide to                obtain a carbonated slurry containing lithium                hydrogen carbonate, and for precipitating phosphate                from leach slurry as insoluble phosphate compound,                and carrying out a solid-liquid separation of                carbonated slurry, where undissolved mineral and                insoluble phosphate compound are separated as                solids that is recovered or discarded to obtain a                solution containing lithium hydrogen                carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing one or more types of                lithium compounds from lithium-containing mineral                used for preparing lithium hydrogen carbonate,                which is crystallized to form lithium carbonate                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for preparing one or more types of                lithium compounds from lithium-containing mineral,                which comprises a leaching unit having connections                for adding a lithium-containing mineral or mineral                concentrate, and for adding carbonate, a                carbonization unit having connections for adding                alkali earth metal compound and carbon dioxide, and                a separation unit having connections for separating                solids from liquids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The lithium containing                  mineral is used in form of lithium-containing                  concentrate, preferably spodumene concentrate.                  The leaching step is performed at elevated                  temperature and elevated pressure. The leaching                  is carried out at a temperature of 150 degrees C                  or more, preferably between 150-300 degrees C,                  more preferably 190-240 degrees C. The alkali                  earth metal compound is added before and/or                  during leaching step and/or before and/or during                  the carbonization step. The carbonization step is                  performed at temperature between 0-50 degrees C,                  preferably 15-40 degrees C. The solid-liquid                  separation step is performed by filtering,                  preferably by vacuum belt filtering. The method                  further involves carrying out a crystallization                  step by heating lithium hydrogen                  carbonate-containing solution to decompose                  bicarbonate and to crystallize lithium carbonate.                  The method is performed batch wise or                  continuously. The lithium compound has a                  phosphorus content of less than 300ppm. Preferred                  Components: The alkaline carbonate leach solution                  comprises sodium carbonate and/or potassium                  carbonate. The alkali earth metal compound is                  selected from a group consist of alkali earth                  metal hydroxide, alkali earth metal carbonate,                  alkali earth metal hydrogen carbonate, and alkali                  earth metal chloride, preferably alkali earth                  metal compound is magnesium hydroxide in solid                  form. The insoluble phosphate compound is alkali                  earth metal hydrogen phosphate, such as magnesium                  hydrogen phosphate or calcium hydrogen                  phosphate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC E33-G; M25-B01; M25-G04; M25-J

   USE - Carbonated beverage for reducing postprandial blood glucose response value.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonated beverage do not contain a phosphate flavoring agent and energy sweetener, and has a dietary fiber that is resistant to the action of human amylases, has refreshing odor, significant health effects, such as not causing blood glucose oscillations after drinking the beverage, ease postprandial blood glucose response, help control weight, and is suitable for diabetes, obesity and weight control people to drink.

   (A) preparing syrup by adopting thermal blending process and the dietary fiber, sodium citrate, acesulfame, sodium benzoate;
   (B) adding 85-95 degrees C hot water, incubating and dissolving for 20-25 minutes, then adding citric acid dissolved in 85-95 degrees C hot water alone; and
   (C) mixing with sterilized and cooled pure water, and cooling at 4 degrees C, carbonating mixture, filling and sealing to obtain the final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The cooling purified water is prepared by filtering water, purifying, and cooling to 4 degrees C to remove gas. PT P
PN CN111118894-A; CN111118894-B
TI Modifying carbon fiber using supercritical carbon            dioxide to treat carbon fiber surface, using nitric            acid solution to oxidize and grafting ethylene oxide            containing amino groups on surface of oxidized carbon            dioxide.
AU HE M
   ZHANG D
   QIN S
   XU G
   LIU Y
   ZHANG K
   LONG L
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2020413435
AB    NOVELTY - Method of modifying carbon fiber involves (i)                using supercritical carbon dioxide to treat the                carbon fiber (CF) surface, (ii) using nitric acid                solution to oxidize the CF after cleaning, and                (iii) grafting ethylene oxide containing amino                groups on the surface of the oxidized CF to obtain                modified carbon fibers.
   USE - Method of modifying carbon fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method cleans the CF surface residue,                damages the CF strength to lesser extent, oxidizes                the CF surface after supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid cleaning, increases the fiber surface                roughness and active functional groups, is                conducive to surface grafting, improves the                strength of the fiber monofilament through surface                grafting to repair defects, and improves interface                performance of the CF/epoxy composite                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of                  supercritical carbon dioxide on CF surface                  treatment involves placing CF in the reactor of                  the supercritical extractor, adding acetone, and                  closing the reactor, where the critical                  temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide                  fluid is 45-50 degrees C, the critical pressure                  is 14-20 MPa, and the processing time is 30-40                  minutes. The oxidation treatment of CF after                  cleaning involves fixing the three-necked flask                  equipped with stirrer, condenser and thermometer                  in an oil bath, adding nitric acid solution and                  CF after supercritical fluid cleaning to the                  flask, increasing the temperature to 60-70                  degrees C, and reacting for 3-4 hours, taking out                  the CF, cleaning the surface with acetone and                  placing in oven to dry. The method of grafting                  ethylenediamine containing amino groups on the                  surface of the oxidized CF involves fixing the                  three-necked flask equipped with stirrer,                  condenser and thermometer in an oil bath, adding                  100 ml ethylenediamine solution and placing the                  CF after the supercritical fluid cleaning to the                  flask, increasing the temperature to 60-70                  degrees C, reacting for 5-6 hours, taking out the                  CF, washing the surface with acetone and placing                  in an oven to dry. Preferred Conditions: The                  preferred supercritical carbon dioxide fluid has                  a critical temperature point of 45 degrees C, a                  critical pressure of 14 MPa, and a treatment time                  of 40 minutes. The reaction temperature is 70                  degrees C and reaction time is 4 hours. The                  reaction temperature is 70 degrees C and the                  reaction time is 5 hours.

PN CN111099590-A
TI Producing activated carbon, by mixing iron oxide and coal, heating, carbonizing coal to produce carbon, reacting carbon with iron oxide to generate carbon dioxide gas and elemental iron, and using carbon dioxide gas for activating carbon.
AU TANG E
   LI J
   ZHOU Q
   LI S
   CHEN Q
   WANG P
   FU B
AE WUHAN KESIRUIDI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2020403858
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing coal-based activated carbon, involves (i) mixing iron oxide and coal to obtain mixed material, (ii) heating the mixed material at 300-850 degrees C, and carbonizing the coal to produce carbon, and (iii) increasing the temperature to 1000-1200 degrees C, reacting portion of carbon generated in the carbonization step with the iron oxide to generate carbon dioxide gas and elemental iron, and using the carbon dioxide gas as an active agent for activating another portion of carbon to generate activated carbon.
   USE - The process is useful for producing coal-based activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves production efficiency and reduces production costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves performing cooling and magnetic separation after the activation step. The cooling is carried out by cooling the product obtained in activation step to 50-100 degrees C. The magnetic separation is carried out by passing the product after the cooling step through a magnetic separation device to separate the activated carbon and the elemental iron product. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the carbonization is carried out for 4-8 hours. In step (iii), the activation is carried out for 8-16 hours. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of the iron oxide and the coal in the mixing step is 100:(40-80). Preferred Components: The coal in mixing step comprises anthracite, bituminous coal and/or metallurgical charcoal. The coal is dry particles, powder or briquette with a particle size of 3-15 mm. The iron oxide in the mixing step is granular.

TI Polystyrene foam insulation board production process, involves preparing foaming material containing aqueous solution of ammonium carbonate, and performing foaming operation employing supercritical carbon dioxide treatment process.
AU SONG M
   WANG Z

   WANG J
   REN J
   ZHOU X
   SONG L
   MENG D
   SONG Y
   HE L
AE SONG M (SONG-Individual)
GA 201950042G
AB    NOVELTY - The polystyrene foam insulation board production process involves preparing foaming material and performing foaming operation, where the foaming material contains an aqueous solution of ammonium carbonate, and the foaming operation employs a supercritical carbon dioxide treatment process.
   USE - Polystyrene foam insulation board production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process introduces ammonium carbonate to provide a weakly alkaline activity, without increasing the impurities in the whole system, and introduces water to provide a medium in which carbon dioxide is combined with ammonium carbonate to provide a microenvironment for carbon dioxide accumulation. The ammonium carbonate is distributed more evenly so that ammonium carbonate is better distributed throughout the system as a foaming target.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The foaming material comprises 90-100 pts. wt., preferably 95-98 pts. wt. polystyrene, and 0.1-1 pt. wt., preferably 0.4-0.7 pt. wt. aqueous ammonium carbonate solution. The aqueous ammonium carbonate solution has a concentration of 10-50 g/L, preferably 17-25 g/L. Preferred Process: The foaming material is mixed by a high shear emulsifier and is subjected to a foaming operation. The supercritical carbon dioxide treatment process is carried out by stirring raw material of the foaming material treated by the high shear emulsifier after raw material of the foaming material is filled with carbon dioxide, the stirring time is not less than 0.5 hours, and foaming. Preferred Conditions: The rotation speed of the high shear emulsifier on the raw material of foaming material is not less than 3000 rpm, and the action time is not less than 5 hours. The temperature of the foaming operation is higher than 130 degrees C. The temperature of the foaming operation is 140-150 degrees C.

   AYAD S

   UNIV FLORIDA STATE (UYFL-C)

   UNIV FLORIDA STATE (UYFL-C)
GA 201878126N

   USE - Metal-organic framework is used as catalyst in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-organic framework has large specific surface area, and enables environmentally-friendly reduction of carbon dioxide with high photocatalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal-organic framework comprises titanium oxide clusters connected through an organic linker comprising an acid derivative of formula (I).
   R1,R2 = H or optionally substituted 1-14C alkyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) use of metal-organic framework as catalyst in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide; and
   (2) manufacture of metal-organic framework, which involves contacting the titanium oxide cluster with the organic linker.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The titanium oxide clusters comprise titanium oxide (Ti8O12)-based ring-shaped clusters. Preferred Process: The photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide involves contacting the metal-organic framework with carbon dioxide, forming a mixture, illuminating the mixture with light that partially overlaps with the bandgap of the metal-organic framework and reducing carbon dioxide with initial reduction rate of 5.5x 10-6 to 1.25x 10-5 mol.L-1.(hour)-1 and quantum yield of 0.3-1.8%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Structure: The metal-organic framework has structure of formula (II). Preferred Properties: The metal-organic framework has high-symmetry tetragonal crystal structure with I4/mmm-type space group. The metal-organic framework has BET specific surface area of 100-2000 m2/g, preferably 200-1000 m2/g, total pore volume of 0.05-1.5 cm3/g, preferably 0.09-0.73 cm3/g, adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide gas of 5-500 cm3, preferably 19-225 cm3, and bandgap of 2.15-2.9 eV, preferably 2.29-2.5 eV.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 = H;and
   R2 = methyl, ethyl, isopropyl, n-butyl, cyclopentyl, cyclohexyl or n-heptyl.

TI Device for gas production, has reaction unit            connected to raw material gas supply unit and including            reactor that houses reducing body, and concentration            adjustment unit provided between raw material gas            supply unit and reactor to adjust concentration of            oxygen contained in raw material gas.
AU YUKI N
   DASANAYAKE ALUTHGE R
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 202221734C
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) that produces a generated gas                containing carbon monoxide by contacting a raw                material gas containing carbon dioxide with a                reducing agent that reduces carbon dioxide, and                includes: a raw material gas supply unit that                supplies the raw material gas; a reaction unit (4)                connected to the raw material gas supply unit and                including at least one reactor (4a) that houses the                reducing body; and a concentration adjustment unit                (5) provided between the raw material gas supply                unit and the reactor to adjust the concentration of                oxygen contained in the raw material gas. The                concentration adjusting unit adjusts the                concentration of the oxygen contained in the raw                material gas to less than 1 vol.%. The                concentration adjusting unit includes an oxygen                removing device for removing the oxygen contained                in the raw material gas. The reactor includes                reactors containing a reducing agent containing a                metal oxide that reduces carbon dioxide as the                reducing agent.
   USE - The device is used for a gas production system                for producing a generated gas containing carbon                monoxide by contacting a raw material gas                containing carbon dioxide with a reducing agent                that reduces carbon dioxide (all claimed). The                generated gas is used in the applications for                synthesizing valuable substances (ethanol) by                fermentation with microorganisms (Clostridium), for                manufacturing steel using as a fuel or reducing                agent, for manufacturing electrical devices, for                producing chemicals (phosgene and acetic acid)                using carbon monoxide as a synthetic raw material,                used as a reducing agent (blast furnace), used as                fuel to generate electricity by thermal power, and                used as fuel cells used as fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The device and the system can allow a                generated gas containing carbon monoxide to be                produced efficiently from a raw material gas                containing carbon dioxide. The device utilizes                concentration adjusting unit for adjusting the                concentration of oxygen contained in the raw                material gas, so that the gas composition of the                raw material gas can be deviated from the explosion                range and the ignition of the raw material gas can                be prevented. The device improves production                efficiency of the produced gas, improves conversion                efficiency of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide,                and prevents or suppresses decrease in the                conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide into carbon                monoxide. The device can prevent or suppress the                efficiency of conversion of carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide by the reducing agent from being                extremely lowered due to the adverse effect of the                inactivating component. The device is possible to                produce a produced gas containing a high                concentration of carbon monoxide. The device is                possible to prevent or suppress a decrease in the                conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide into carbon                monoxide due to the restriction of chemical                equilibrium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a gas production system; and
   (2) a gas production method for producing a                generated gas containing carbon monoxide by                contacting a raw material gas containing carbon                dioxide with a reducing agent that reduces carbon                dioxide.

PN CN112142150-A
TI Device for simultaneously recovering nitrogen and phosphorus in biogas slurry with high efficiency and low consumption, comprises biogas slurry preheater, carbon dioxide removal reactor, and vacuum ammonia removal reactor.
AU DAI X
   YANG D
   HUA Y
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 202103073B
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a biogas slurry preheater (2), carbon dioxide removal reactor (3), and a vacuum ammonia removal reactor (6) connected by pipelines, a cooler (9), carbonization tower (11) and struvite crystallization reactor (15). The biogas slurry preheater is used for heat exchange between the initial biogas slurry and the high-temperature deammination biogas slurry to preheat initial biogas slurry. The carbon dioxide removal reactor is used to fed with initial biogas slurry preheated from the biogas slurry preheater. The vacuum reactor is provided for removing ammonia, where the water inlet is passed through the initial biogas slurry from the carbon dioxide removal reactor. The inlet of the cooler is connected with the gas outlet on the top of the carbon dioxide removal reactor and the vacuum removal ammonia reactor, where the incoming gas is cooled.
   USE - Device for simultaneously recovering nitrogen and phosphorus in biogas slurry with high efficiency and low consumption.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes recovery of struvite products, utilizes characteristics of the alkalinity of the biogas liquid, realizes synchronous recovery of nitrogen and phosphorus resources of the biogas liquid without adding alkali, and has characteristics of simple process equipment, high treatment efficiency and low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises a biogas slurry preheater (2), carbon dioxide removal reactor (3), and a vacuum ammonia removal reactor (6) connected by pipelines, a cooler (9), carbonization tower (11) and struvite crystallization reactor (15). The biogas slurry preheater is used for heat exchange between the initial biogas slurry and the high-temperature deammination biogas slurry to preheat initial biogas slurry. The carbon dioxide removal reactor is used to fed with initial biogas slurry preheated from the biogas slurry preheater. The vacuum reactor is provided for removing ammonia, where the water inlet is passed through the initial biogas slurry from the carbon dioxide removal reactor. The inlet of the cooler is connected with the gas outlet on the top of the carbon dioxide removal reactor and the vacuum removal ammonia reactor, where the incoming gas is cooled. The ammonia-containing non-condensable gas at the top is passed into the carbonization tower, where the carbonization tower reacts the introduced ammonia gas with carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for simultaneously recovering nitrogen and phosphorus in biogas slurry with high efficiency and low consumption, which involves:
   (A) passing anaerobic digestion biogas slurry into biogas slurry preheater for preheating at 50-55 degrees C;
   (B) utilizing the micro-negative pressure condition of the carbon dioxide removal reactor and the rotating brush stirring to promote the destruction of the alkalinity of the preheated biogas slurry;
   (C) pressurizing and spraying the biogas slurry to form a spray in the vacuum ammonia removal reactor to increase the gas-liquid contact area;
   (D) condensing the ammonia-containing steam by cooler and separated from gas and liquid;
   (E) utilizing the alkaline pH condition of the deaminized biogas slurry; and
   (F) adding the struvite crystallization reactor with magnesium salt to remove the remaining ammonia nitrogen and phosphate of the biogas slurry.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for simultaneously recovering nitrogen and phosphorus in biogas slurry with high efficiency and low consumption. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Biogas slurry preheater (2)
   Carbon dioxide removal reactor (3)
   Vacuum ammonia removal reactor (6)
   Cooler (9)
   Carbonization tower (11)
   Struvite crystallization reactor (15)

PN CN110994336-A
TI Tunable terahertz laser contains blazed grating, carbon dioxide gas medium, zinc selenide wafer, zinc selenide window, germanium etalon, gold-plated total reflection mirrors, stainless steel cavity, terahertz gain gas and quartz wafer.
AU LIU C
   WANG J
   LIU T
   LI L
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYCY-C)
GA 202030654F
AB    NOVELTY - A high-efficiency tunable terahertz laser consists of a blazed grating, a carbon dioxide gas medium, a zinc selenide wafer, a zinc selenide window, a germanium etalon, gold-plated total reflection mirrors (M1, M2 and M3), a stainless steel cavity, a terahertz gain gas and a quartz wafer. The blazed grating, carbon dioxide gas medium, zinc selenide wafer, zinc selenide window, germanium etalon and quartz wafer are provided coaxially in sequence. The blazed grating, carbon dioxide gas medium and zinc selenide wafer form a carbon dioxide laser, which is used as pump source of the terahertz laser. The stainless steel cavity, zinc selenide window and quartz wafer form a terahertz gas medium tank. The terahertz gas medium tank is filled with terahertz gain gas. The zinc selenide window and the quartz wafer are fixed at the left and right end surfaces of the stainless steel cavity, respectively. The gold-plated total reflection mirrors (M1, M2 and M3) form a total reflection mirror group.
   USE - High-efficiency tunable terahertz laser.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A high-efficiency tunable terahertz laser consists of a blazed grating, a carbon dioxide gas medium, a zinc selenide wafer, a zinc selenide window, a germanium etalon, gold-plated total reflection mirrors (M1, M2 and M3), a stainless steel cavity, a terahertz gain gas and a quartz wafer. The blazed grating, carbon dioxide gas medium, zinc selenide wafer, zinc selenide window, germanium etalon and quartz wafer are provided coaxially in sequence. The blazed grating, carbon dioxide gas medium and zinc selenide wafer form a carbon dioxide laser, which is used as pump source of the terahertz laser. The stainless steel cavity, zinc selenide window and quartz wafer form a terahertz gas medium tank. The terahertz gas medium tank is filled with terahertz gain gas. The zinc selenide window and the quartz wafer are fixed at the left and right end surfaces of the stainless steel cavity, respectively. The gold-plated total reflection mirrors (M1, M2 and M3) form a total reflection mirror group. The center of gold-plated total reflection mirror one (M1) and the center of germanium etalon are 40 degrees from the optical axis. The center of the gold-plated total reflection mirror (M2) and the center of the germanium etalon are perpendicular to the optical axis. The center of the gold-plated total reflection mirror (M3) and the center of the germanium etalon are 140 degrees . The total reflection mirror group, germanium etalon and quartz wafer form a resonant cavity mirror for terahertz laser. The quartz wafer is used as output coupling mirror of the terahertz laser. The carbon dioxide pump laser is oscillated between the blazed grating and the zinc selenide wafer, and the laser is introduced into the terahertz gas medium tank through the zinc selenide window and the germanium etalon, and excites terahertz gain gas, to generate terahertz stimulated radiation. The carbon dioxide pump laser transmits zinc selenide window and germanium etalon, such that the carbon dioxide pump laser is fully absorbed by the terahertz gain gas. The terahertz stimulated radiation oscillates between the mirror group, the germanium etalon and the quartz wafer, to form a laser, which is final output by the quartz wafer. When the terahertz laser is incident on the surface of the germanium etalon due to multi-beam interference, the incidence angle of the germanium etalon is adjusted to reflectivity of the terahertz laser of more than 70%, such that the terahertz laser output efficiency is increased.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The surface of the zinc selenide window is coated with an infrared antireflection coating to a thickness of 9-11 mu m, such that the transmittance of zinc selenide window to carbon dioxide laser is 98%. The surface of the germanium etalon is coated with an infrared transmission coating to a thickness of 9-11 mu m, such that the transmittance of germanium etalon to carbon dioxide laser is 95%. Preferred Property: The transmittance of quartz wafer to the terahertz laser is 78%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The terahertz gain gas is fluoromethane, heavy water, methanol or ammonia gas.

TI Intelligent monitoring type tracheal catheter used during cardiopulmonary resuscitation process, has pressure detector, gas flow meter and carbon dioxide monitor that are all respectively electrically connected to display.
AU LV S
   LIU J
AE LIU J (LIUJ-Individual)
GA 2021C71702
AB    NOVELTY - The catheter has a gas flow meter (9) that is connected to one end of a flow monitoring tube (8). The other end of the flow monitoring tube is provided with an annular catheter (12). The annular catheter is arranged around an outside of the tracheal catheter portion (1) close to an airbag (5). Multiple through holes are opened on side of the annular catheter toward the airbag. The one end of a carbon dioxide gas collection tube (10) is arranged on an inner side wall at an insertion end of the tracheal catheter portion. The other end of the carbon dioxide gas collection tube is connected with a carbon dioxide monitor (11). A pressure detector (7), the gas flow meter and the carbon dioxide monitor are respectively electrically connected to a display. The tracheal catheter portion is made of PVC.
   USE - Intelligent monitoring type tracheal catheter used in hospital during cardiopulmonary resuscitation and acute severe patient rescue process with respiratory dysfunction.
   ADVANTAGE - The tracheal catheter detects the pressure in the air bag in real time. The integrity of the airbag and the tightness of the patient airway can be ensured effectively. The airway mucosa pressing ischemia necrosis can be avoided. The smooth condition of the catheter in real-time can be detected. The working burden of repeatedly manually measuring the inner pressure of airbag is avoided. The airtightness of the tracheal catheter is effectively ensured, and the ischemic necrosis due to airway mucosal compression is avoided. The patency of the tracheal tube, the position of the tracheal tube and the ventilation effect is reflected by monitoring the end-tidal carbon dioxide gas flow value, which is helpful for clinical adjustment of the tracheal tube to ensure the patency of the tracheal catheter.
