PN WO2021105969-A1
TI Method of determining alveolar dead space of subject used in assessment of medical condition selected from group consisting of heart failure, involves measuring end tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide during sequence of deep breaths.
AU KATZNELSON R
   KHAN R A
   SOBCZYK O
   DUFFIN J
   FISHER J A
AE THORNHILL SCI INC (THOR-Non-standard)
GA 2021598367
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring (204) an end tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide during a sequence of normal breaths of the subject. A sequence of deep breaths is due by the subject. A first volume of a first gas is delivered to the subject over a first portion of each inspiration by the subject. The first volume is less than or equal to an expected alveolar volume of the subject when the subject is breathing normally. A second volume of a second gas is delivered to the subject over a second portion of each inspiration. The second gas comprises a neutral gas. An end tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide during the sequence of deep breaths is measured. The alveolar dead space using the end tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide measured during the sequence of normal breaths are computed (214) and the end tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide measured during the sequence of deep breaths.
   USE - Method of determining alveolar dead space of subject used in assessment of medical condition selected from group consisting of heart failure, congestive heart failure, adult respiratory distress syndrome, lung function after lung transplant, lung function after transplant, sepsis, trauma, and pulmonary and systemic disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The sequential gas delivery reduces or eliminates the anatomical dead space from the calculation so that looking only at the volume of gas entering the alveoli. The effect of alveolar dead space on fractional end tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide (FETCCE) is reduced or eliminated, so as to make FETCCE equal to fractional alveolar partial pressure of carbon dioxide (FACO2).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for determining alveolar dead space of a subject.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method of measuring an alveolar dead space of a subject.
   Method of determining alveolar dead space of subject (200)
   Step for normal breathing (202)
   Step for measuring end tidal partial pressure of carbon dioxide (204)
   Step for sequential gas delivery with deep breathing (208)
   Step for computing alveolar dead space (214)

The system maintains operating temperature of the fuel cell so as to overcome problem of lowering thermal efficiency as nitrogen sensible heat discharged to outer side by supplying high concentration oxygen and carbon dioxide collected at a rear stage instead of the supplied air.

GA 2019539745
AB    NOVELTY - Production of biomethane by purifying biogas (1) feed stream involves (i) introducing the feed gas stream into a pretreatment unit (5) in which the gas stream is partially separated from the carbon dioxide and oxygen and compressed to a pressure (p1) of more than 10 bar abs, (ii) subjecting the gaseous stream to be treated from the carbon dioxide-depleted stage to a cryogenic separation by introducing it into a distillation column to separate the nitrogen from the gaseous stream, and (iii) recovering 1-14C enriched stream from the cryogenic separation by pumping the vessel product from the column to a pressure (p2) of more than 25 bar absolute, preferably more than the critical pressure of the product. In step (i), the separation of carbon dioxide and oxygen from the feed gas stream is effected by a unit comprising at least two stages of separating membranes so that the gaseous stream from the step (i) depleted of carbon dioxide used in step (i) comprises 0.3-2 mol% carbon dioxide.
   USE - Production of biomethane from biogas from non-hazardous waste storage facilities used in filling stations for vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables safe production of biomethane with improved yield and quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for installation for production of biomethane, which comprises a source of biogas, a pretreatment unit for removing all or portion of the volatile organic compounds (VOCs), water, sulfur compounds from the gas stream to be treated, at least two stages of separating membranes for partially separating the carbon dioxide and iodite of the gas stream, a compressor (2) for compressing the gas flow at an absolute pressure of 25-100 bar, and a heat exchanger for cooling the depleted gaseous flow to carbon dioxide, a distillation column. The installation does not include temperature swing adsorption for removing carbon dioxide at levels less than 0.3%/mol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of production of biomethane.
   Biogas (1)
   Compression unit (2)
   Purification unit (3)
   Cryodistillation unit (4)
   Pretreatment unit (5)

The pressure (p1) is preferably more than 50 bar. The pressure (p2) is preferably more than 40 bar. In step (ii), the gaseous stream depleted of carbon dioxide obtained from step (i) undergoes a relaxation treatment at a pressure (p3) of 15-40 bar before entering the distillation column. The gaseous stream depleted of carbon dioxide obtained from step (i) is at least partially condensed in a heat exchanger.

PN WO2022184646-A1; FR3120427-A1
TI Process for separating stream of heat exchanger            containing carbon dioxide and impurity lighter than            carbon dioxide by distillation, involves cooling flow            in heat exchanger, separating liquid flow by partial            condensation stage.
AU TRAN M
   GRANADOS L
   LECLERC M
   RODRIGUES G
   TROPTARD A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2022B5957E
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves cooling flow (1) of                liquid (5) in a heat exchanger (20), and separating                the liquid in a distillation column (30) or by a                partial condensation stage in a separator pot. A                part of the liquid flow is sent to cool to form a                subcooled liquid. Parts of the sub cooled liquid                are expanded to produce a two-phase flow. The two                phase flow is separated to form gas (16) and liquid                product. The liquid is heated by indirect heat                exchange with the flow to be separated and with the                liquid to be sub cooled. Another liquid product is                produced by taking part of the bottom liquid of the                column or pot without cooling the liquid in the                heat exchanger.
   USE - Process for separating a stream of a heat                exchanger containing 95% mol of carbon dioxide and                impurity lighter than the carbon dioxide by                distillation.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables optimizing the heat                exchanger of a liquefaction process in order to be                able to subcool the product and the refrigeration                cycle liquid in the same passage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                apparatus for separating a flow containing carbon                dioxide and impurity lighter than carbon dioxide by                distillation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                Process for separating a stream of a heat                exchanger.
   1Flow of liquid
   5Liquid
   16Gas

   30Distillation column

AU ZHANG H
   WU X
   GUO X
   WANG P
   LI N
   CHEN L
   XU D
AE WANHUA CHEM NINGBO CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 202238075F
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting trace moisture in isocyanate,                comprises passing isocyanate and water through a                membrane loaded with the isocyanate-reactive                catalyst bis(3-amidomorpholinyl)diethyl ether (sic)                to obtain carbon dioxide, measuring the generated                carbon dioxide content and converting it to the                moisture content in the isocyanate, the isocyanate                active catalyst bis(3-amidomorpholinyl) diethyl                ether is                4,4'-oxybis(methylene)dimorpholine-3-carboxamide                compound (I).
   USE - The method is useful for detecting trace                moisture in isocyanate and for monitoring trace                water content, and for application of online                monitoring.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes indirect measuring                method, which reduces the detection limit of water                in isocyanate, can timely monitor whether the                production device leaks water, and avoids the                occurrence of larger accident safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting trace moisture in isocyanate,                comprises passing isocyanate and water through a                membrane loaded with the isocyanate-reactive                catalyst bis(3-amidomorpholinyl)diethyl ether (sic)                to obtain carbon dioxide, measuring the generated                carbon dioxide content and converting it to the                moisture content in the isocyanate, the isocyanate                active catalyst bis(3-amidomorpholinyl) diethyl                ether is                4,4'-oxybis(methylene)dimorpholine-3-carboxamide                compound of formula (I).
   R1= pyrocatechol ethyl;
   R2= 1-5C alkyl or phenyl, preferably methyl,                ethyl or phenyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                online monitoring of trace moisture in isocyanate                in isocyanate production.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the mass                  ratio between bis(3-carboxymorpholinyl) diethyl                  ether and thionyl chloride is 1:3-20, preferably                  1:5-8, the catalyst is amide, tertiary amine, or                  carbonate, preferably N-N-dimethylformamide, the                  mass ratio between bis(3-carboxymorpholinyl)                  diethyl ether and catalyst is 50-400:1,                  preferably 80-150:1; and/or, the temperature of                  the reaction system is 60-120&#176;C, and the                  reaction time is 2-8 hours. In step (iii), the                  dopamine solution is a mixed solution of                  dopamine, buffer salt and water, and the pH of                  the solution is 5-9, the concentration of                  dopamine in the solution is 5-20 mg/ml, the                  buffer salt is phosphate, ammonium salt, or                  organic salt, preferably potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate-dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, or                  trishydrochloride, the buffer salt concentration                  is 30-200 mM, preferably 80-150 mM, the soaking                  time is 12-24 hours, and the soaking system                  temperature is 30-60&#8451;. In step (iv),                  the solvent is amide, acetone, and/or                  cyclohexane, preferably N-N-dimethylformamide,                  the concentration of bis(acyl chloride                  morpholino) diethyl ether solution is 5-20 mg/ml,                  the soaking time is 12-24 hours, and the soaking                  temperature is 40-80&#8451;. In step (v), the                  end-capping agent is acyl chloride, preferably                  acetyl chloride, the end-capping reaction time is                  6-12 hours, and/or, the mass ratio between                  end-capping agent and catalyst is 300 -1000:1.                  The isocyanate-reactive catalyst                  bis(3-amidomorpholinyl)diethyl ether is prepared                  by preparing the precursor                  bis(3-carboxymorpholinyl) diethyl ether, and then                  through chloroacylation with thionyl chloride                  reaction, and the subsequent nitrogen acylation                  reaction, supported on the membrane, where the                  precursor bis(3-carboxymorpholinyl) diethyl ether                  is                  4,4'-oxybis(methylene)dimorpholine-3-carboxylic                  acid, and the preparation method of precursor                  bis(3-carboxymorpholinyl) diethyl ether ,                  comprises (i) dissolving 3-carboxymorpholine in a                  solvent, (ii) adding catalyzer, adding diethyl                  ether to 3-carboxymorpholine,and carrying out                  backflow reaction after required proportioning,                  and(iii) carrying out separation and purification                  to obtain final product. The preparation of                  membrane loaded with isocyanate active catalyst                  comprises (i) adding thionyl chloride and the                  catalyst required into bis(3-carboxymorpholinyl)                  diethyl ether for the reaction, then heating to                  refluxing, removing the thionyl chloride by                  rotary evaporation to obtain intermediate                  4,4'-oxybis(methylene)dimorpholine-3-carbonyl                  chloride, (ii) washing base film with solvent and                  then air-drying, (iii) soaking the basement                  membrane in the dopamine solution, and                  constructing polydopamine-mediated layer on the                  surface of the membrane, (iv) soaking the base                  film on which the mediating layer is constructed                  in step (iii) in a solvent containing the                  intermediate bis(3-acylchloridemorpholino)                  diethyl ether (sic), then carrying out nitrogen                  acylation reaction of catalyst                  bis(3-amidomorpholinyl) diethyl ether (sic), and                  supports it on the base membrane, and (v) soaking                  the catalyst-supporting                  bis(3-amidomorpholinyl)diethyl ether membrane in                  step (iv) in an end capping agent to complete end                  capping.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The isocyanate is                  diphenylmethane diisocyanate and its isomers,                  polyphenylmethane polyisocyanate, toluene                  diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate,                  dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate, hexamethylene                  diisocyanate, and/or phenyl isocyanate,                  preferably diphenylmethane diisocyanate and its                  isomers, the diphenylmethane diisocyanate and its                  isomers are 4,4-diphenylmethane diisocyanate,                  2,4-diphenylmethane diisocyanate,and/or                  2,2-diphenylmethane diisocyanate. The solvent is                  tetrahydrofuran, acetonitrile,                  N-N-dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone,                  and/or cyclohexane, preferably                  N-N-dimethylformamide, and/or, the mass ratio                  between 3-carboxymorpholine and solvent is                  1:1.5-8, preferably 1:2-4. The catalyst is alkali                  metal hydroxides, halides and/or carbonates,                  preferably potassium iodide, potassium bromide or                  sodium carbonate, and the mass ratio of                  3-carboxymorpholine and catalyst is 20-80:1,                  preferably 30-50:1.

TI Catalyst used in reaction for preparing methanol            by carbon dioxide hydrogenation, has metal copper,            metal zinc, metal zirconium, metal auxiliary agent and            carbon material, where metal auxiliary agent is cerium,            yttrium, aluminium, gallium, palladium, platinum,            magnesium, manganese and chromium.
AU WANG Y
   ZHANG Q
   LIU Z
   XIANG Y
   KANG J
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 202228842Q
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises 30-70% metal copper, 10-30%                metal zinc, 10-30% metal zirconium, 1-10% metal                auxiliary agent and carbon material (balance); the                metal auxiliary agent is at least one of cerium,                yttrium, aluminium, gallium, palladium, platinum,                magnesium, manganese, and chromium.
   USE - The catalyst is used in the reaction for                preparing methanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is suitable for carbon dioxide                hydrogenation reaction, using carbon dioxide and                hydrogen as reaction raw material, where carbon                dioxide conversion rate is more than 25 %, methanol                selectivity is higher than 80 %. The catalyst has                simple preparation method, low price and excellent                performance, and has wide industrial application                prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation method of catalyst comprising (1)                mixing uniformly metal copper salt, metal zinc salt                and metal zirconium salt solution, then adding                carbon material, and stirring to obtain mixture;                (2) dropping the sodium carbonate solution into the                mixture, controlling the pH value to be 7.5-10.5,                after finishing dropping, continuously stirring,                after filtering and washing, controlling the                conductivity of the filtrate at 10-100 s/cm to                obtain the suspension; (3) adding the metal                auxiliary agent and complexing agent into the                suspension, continuously stirring to gel shape,                then drying the gel product, calcining, reducing to                obtain carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanol                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon material is                  silicon carbide, carbon nano fiber, activated                  carbon, carbon nano tube, graphite fiber, carbon                  nitride, and carbon-based foam; the specific                  surface area of the carbon material is 300-2000                  m2/g, the pore volume is 0.2-1.5 cm/g. The metal                  copper salt, metal zinc salt, metal zirconium                  salt and metal auxiliary salt is at least one of                  nitrate, hydrochloride, and acetylacetonate salt.                  The complexing agent is at least one of glucose,                  citric acid, tartaric acid, adipic acid, alanine,                  fructose, and sucrose. In the step (1), the                  concentration of the metal copper salt, metal                  zinc salt and metal zirconium salt solution is                  0.5-2 mol/l; in the step (2), the concentration                  of sodium carbonate solution is 0.5-1.5 mol/l,                  the molar ratio of the sodium carbonate and                  copper, zinc, zirconium total metal is 0.5-3; in                  the step (3), the molar ratio of the metal                  auxiliary agent and the complexing agent is 1-3.                  Preferred Method: The stirring temperature in the                  step (1) to (3) is 40-100degrees Celsius; in the                  step (3), the calcining temperature is                  300-550degrees Celsius, the time is 2-12 hours;                  the reducing temperature is 200-400degrees                  Celsius, the time is 0.5-4 hours. In the step                  (2), the dropping is at a rate of 30-100                  ml/minute while mixing the mixture and sodium                  carbonate solution. The reaction conditions for                  preparing methanol by carbon dioxide                  hydrogenation are as follows: the carbon dioxide                  hydrogenation reaction pressure is 10-100 bar,                  the reaction temperature is 180-320degrees                  Celsius, the volume ratio of hydrogen and carbon                  dioxide is 1:9-9:1, the reaction gas airspeed is                  1000-50000 ml/hour/g.

TI Preparing silver foam catalyst based on supercritical carbon dioxide foaming technology comprises e.g. plasticizing polyvinyl alcohol by plasticizing system, heating mixed material, melting, uniformly mixing mixture and shaping.
AU BAI S
   YUAN B
   WANG M
   YANG S
   JIANG X
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2020415109
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silver foam catalyst based on supercritical carbon dioxide foaming technology comprises (1) plasticizing 100 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol by a plasticizing system containing 40-120 pts. wt. silver nitrate, heating the mixed material, melting, uniformly mixing the mixture and shaping by the molding method to obtain polyvinyl alcohol/silver nitrate composite sheet, (2) preparing the polyvinyl alcohol/silver nitrate composite board by a supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave foaming method to obtain a polyvinyl alcohol/silver nitrate composite foam material, where the process parameters of the supercritical carbon dioxide kettle pressure foaming method are the pressure during the foaming process is 5-15 MPa, the foaming temperature is 50-150 degrees C, and the pressure holding time is 10-30 minutes and (3) subjecting the polyvinyl alcohol/silver nitrate composite foam material to in-situ solid-state reaction under a temperature condition of 160-300 degrees C to obtain a foamed silver catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silver foam catalyst based on supercritical carbon dioxide foaming technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: optimizes the combination of process conditions to optimize the cell structure; has a uniform pore size distribution of the foam. The catalyst; is pure; has good catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for silver foam catalyst based on supercritical carbon dioxide foaming technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The degree of polymerization of the polyvinyl alcohol is 500-1700, and the degree of alcoholysis is 88-99. In the step (1), the thickness of the polyvinyl alcohol/silver nitrate composite plate is 1-3 mm; 80-100 pts. wt. silver nitrate. Preferred Method: In the step (1), plasticizing is to mix the plasticizing system containing silver nitrate and polyvinyl alcohol uniformly at 30-45 degrees C, and placing and plasticizing with stirring for 48-72 hours; heating and melting the mixed material obtained after plasticization, where the heating and melting temperature is 100-170 degrees C. In the step (2), the process parameters of the supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave foaming method are: the foaming pressure is 10-12 MPa, the foaming temperature is 85-95 degrees C, and the pressure holding time is 25-30 minutes. In the step (3), the in-situ solid state reaction is to incubate the polyvinyl alcohol/silver nitrate composite foam material obtained in step (2) at a temperature of 170-200 degrees C for 10-30 minutes. Preferred Composition: The plasticizing system comprises 40-80 pts. wt. water, 20-40 pts. wt. glycerol or/and 20-40 pts. wt. ethylene glycol.

PN CN110773194-A; CN110773194-B
TI Use of carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst for preparing methane, prepared by e.g. preparing Platycladus orientalis extract liquid, adding noble metal precursor, lanthanum(III) nitrate and cobalt(II) nitrate, calcining, washing and drying.
AU LI Q
   LI W
   WANG K
   HUANG X
   HUANG J
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 202015777P
AB    NOVELTY - Use of carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst for preparing methane, is claimed. The catalyst is noble metal-supported perovskite-type lanthanum cobaltite catalyst having a shell-core structure, the shell-core structure uses noble metal as core and perovskite-type lanthanum cobaltite as a shell, where the loading amount of the noble metal is 0.5-15 wt.%.
   USE - The carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst is useful for preparing methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst having excellent catalytic activity for carbon dioxide hydrogenation; utilizes plant matter as raw material; adopts ''one-pot'' synthesis method; increases utilization rate of noble metals; and is environmentally friendly, simple and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst, comprising (i) preparing 20-100 g/l Platycladus orientalis extract liquid, (ii) adding noble metal precursor, lanthanum(III) nitrate and cobalt(II) nitrate into step (i) product, drying under stirring condition, calcining at 500-900 degrees C at a heating speed of 1-5 degrees C/minute for 1-3 hours to obtain product, where the loading amount of noble metal is 0.5-15 wt.%, and mass ratio between lanthanum(III) nitrate, cobalt(II) nitrate and Platycladus orientalis extract liquid is 0.8-1.2:0.8-1.2:25-30 (mol:mol:l), and (iii) washing and drying step (ii) product to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The noble metal comprises palladium, silver and platinum, and the loading amount of noble metal is 3 wt.%. The particle diameter of the perovskite-type lanthanum cobaltite is 20-50 nm and the particle size of the internal noble metal nanoparticles is 3-8 nm. In step (ii): the noble metal precursor is silver nitrate, chloroplatinic acid, palladium nitrate or sodium chloropalladate, and the loading amount of the noble metal is 0.5-8 wt.%; and the ratio between lanthanum(III) nitrate, cobalt(II) nitrate and Platycladus orientalisextract liquid is 1:1:25 (mol:mol:l). Preferred Method: In step (i), Platycladus orientalis leaves and deionized water are mixed in a ratio of 3-15:150 (g:ml), boiled for 2-15 minutes, cooled, and suction filtered to obtain 20-100 g/l Platycladus orientalis extract liquid. In step (iii), the amount of deionized water used for washing is 80-800 ml, the drying temperature is 100-150 degrees C and the drying time is 0.5-3 hours.

PN CN109852641-A
TI Synthesizing trimethylene carbonate/lactide copolymer by immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase in supercritical carbon dioxide involves adding trimethylene carbonate, lactide, immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase and stabilizer.
AU ZHAN S
   WANG J
   SHANG X
   HOU W
AE UNIV DALIAN (UYDV-C)
GA 2019533102
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing trimethylene carbonate/lactide copolymer by immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase in supercritical carbon dioxide involves adding trimethylene carbonate, lactide, immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase and stabilizer PLLA-PDMS-PLLA to the reaction kettle, where the mass ratio of trimethylene carbonate, and lactide is 1:2, and the amount of stabilizer polylactide-poly(dimethylsiloxane)-polylactide (PLLA-PDMS-PLLA) is 10 wt.% of trimethylene carbonate and lactide. The amount of immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase is 1-10 wt.% of the total amount of trimethylene carbonate, lactide and stabilizer. The reactor is evacuated and the carbon dioxide is purged. The reaction vessel is heated to 80-100 degrees C, and carbon dioxide is introduced into the reaction vessel to a pressure of 10-14 megapascal to start the reaction. The reactor is cooled to room temperature and vented to atmospheric pressure.
   USE - Method for synthesizing trimethylene carbonate/lactide copolymer by immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase in supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize trimethylene carbonate/lactide copolymer by immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase in supercritical carbon dioxide, which is simple and efficient to prepare, and the catalyst and solvent used are all green reagents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The crude product is collected and dichloromethane is added until the crude product is completely dissolved, and precipitating in frozen methanol to obtain a white powder, which is dried to constant weight to obtain a trimethylene carbonate/lactide copolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The amount of the immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase is 5 wt.% of the total amount of the trimethylene carbonate, lactide and stabilizer. The carbon dioxide purging condition is a low pressure of 5 megapascal or less, and a low speed of 300 mL/minute or less is purged for 15 minutes. The reaction temperature is 90 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 12 megapascal, and the reaction time is 24 hours. The drying temperature is 40 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN108525637-A; CN108525637-B
TI Modifying calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent by heating sample prepared using inorganic calcium source, carbon source and inert carrier precursor under nitrogen atmosphere, followed by carbonizing, heating, calcining and cooling.
AU WANG K
   SU Z
   SUN J
   ZHAO P
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY XUZHOU (UYMT-C)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2018742593
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying a calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent by a carbon template method involves: (S1) placing an inorganic calcium source and an inert carrier precursor in a container filled with deionized water, adding a carbon source to the container, uniformly stirring at a constant temperature to obtain a mixed solution, and drying the mixed solution at a constant temperature to obtain a dried sample; (S2) heating the dried sample to a carbonization temperature of 500-700 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere and carbonizing at a constant temperature to obtain a mixture with a carbon template; and (S3) heating the mixture with the carbon template to a calcination temperature of 800 degrees C under an air atmosphere, calcining at a constant temperature for 2 hours, and then naturally cooling to room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for modifying calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent by carbon template method.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon template method uses a wide source of raw materials, is economical and simple, prevents agglomeration and sintering of particles existing in the calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, and improves adsorption capacity and stability of carbon dioxide. The modified adsorbent has high adsorption rate and adsorption capacity, weak kinetic limit, and good recycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the modified calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic calcium source is chosen from calcium nitrate and calcium carbonate. The inert carrier precursor is chosen from magnesium nitrate, aluminum nitrate, zirconium nitrate and cerium nitrate. The molar ratio of metal ions and calcium ions in the inert carrier precursor is 1:9.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon source is chosen from gluconic acid, glucose, sucrose and starch. The molar amount of the carbon source is 1/4 of the molar amount of the gluconic acid carbon source required to produce the same molar amount of calcium gluconate.

PN CN108262046-A
TI Catalyst for use in methane-carbon dioxide reforming, comprises doped hexaaluminate containing zirconium, nickel, and lanthanum or strontium, and cobalt, iron, manganese or copper.
AU LI J
   ZHAO Y
   WANG Y
   LIU C
   CHEN J
   LI Z
   PENG J
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2018575852
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises a doped hexaaluminate containing zirconium, nickel, aluminum, and lanthanum or strontium, and cobalt, iron, manganese or copper.
   USE - Catalyst is used in methane-carbon dioxide reforming (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst fully exploits the advantages of rare-earth elements, transition metals, and hexaaluminate by controlling the content of each element, has improved catalytic performance and stability in methane-carbon dioxide reforming reactions, reduces the temperature of reforming reaction, thus reducing energy consumption and equipment requirement, and can be prepared industrially by a simple and economical solution/high-temperature baking method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The doped hexaaluminate is of formula: RE0.8ZrxMaNi1-aAl11O19-d.
   RE = La or Sr;
   M = Co, Fe, Mn or Cu;
   x = 0-0.2;
   a = 0-1;and
   d = not defined.
   N.B: d is not defined. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of catalyst, which involves mixing the salt solutions of elements (RE,Zr,M,Ni,Al) with isopropanol solution of polyethylene glycol to form a mixed system, processing the mixing system into a gel in a water bath, and drying and roasting the gel; and
   (2) methane-carbon dioxide reforming, which involves reacting methane and carbon dioxide in the presence of the catalyst, at a gas hourly space velocity of 6000-10000 hour-1, a methane/carbon dioxide ratio of 1:1, and a temperature of 750-800 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The catalyst has a grain diameter of 20-60 mesh. Preferred Component: The salt solution is a nitrate solution. Preferred Conditions: The water bath temperature is 70-90 degrees C. The drying temperature is 120-160 degrees C. Roasting involves roasting at low temperature of 350-500 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and roasting at high temperature of 1150-1300 degrees C for 5-7 hours. After roasting, the product is pressed and granulated to 20-60 mesh.

TI Method for efficiently transferring carbon dioxide based separation procedure from first to second chromatographic system, involves altering cross-sectional area of column packed with media in second chromatography system.
AU SHREVE J A
   HILL J F
   MICHIENZI J D
   TARAFDER A
   FOGWILL M O
   SHREVE J
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 201823204C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves identifying an average column pressure for carbon dioxide based separation in first chromatographic system (1000). The measured average column pressure for carbon dioxide is compared based on separation in first and second chromatographic system. A cross-sectional area of column packed with media in second chromatography system is altered along length of column to closely match identified average column pressure for carbon dioxide based on separation in first chromatographic system.
   USE - Method for efficiently transferring carbon dioxide based separation procedure from first chromatographic system to second chromatographic system.
   ADVANTAGE - The cross-sectional area packed with media within the column jacket along the length of the column in the direction of flow of the mobile phase will increase the pressure drop along the length of the column. The inventors presume that the flow rate of the mobile phase is maintained along the length of the column that provides the foregoing guidance. The insert is used to enable the addition of makeup fluid by forming a channel that enables fluid to flow through the insert into the packed media.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a column for a carbon dioxide based separation procedure in a chromatography system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a chromatographic system.
   First chromatographic system (1000)
   Pump (1060)
   Detector (1070)
   Back pressure regulator (1080)
   Column (1100)

PT P
PN US2019210970-A1
TI Reducing the production of 2-hydroxyglutarate in a cell producing 2-HG involves contacting 2-hydroxyglutarate producing cell with an inhibitor of mutant isocitrate dehydrogenase-1 protein.
AU LIN J
   ERICSSON A
   CAMPBELL A
   GUSTAFSON G
   WANG Z
   DIEBOLD R B
   ASHWELL S
   LANCIA D R
   CARAVELLA J A
   LU W
AE FORMA TM2 INC (FORM-Non-standard)
GA 2019602970
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing the production of 2-hydroxyglutarate (2-HG) in a cell producing 2-HG (2-HG producing cell) involves contacting the 2-HG producing cell with an inhibitor of mutant isocitrate dehydrogenase-1 protein (mt-IDH1), where the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 has an IC50 value of 0.1 micromolar or less against HCT116 cells harboring mt-IDH1 (HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells) such as HCT116 isogenic IDH1-R132H cells and HCT116 isogenic IDH1-R132C cells, where the IC50 value of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 is determined by a cellular 2-HG assay using HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells by culturing the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells in a growth media comprising McCoy's 5A, 10% fetal bovine serum, 1 X antibiotic-antimycotic solution, and 0.3 mg/mL G418 in 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator at 37 degrees C.
   USE - Method for reducing the production of 2-hydroxyglutaratein a cell producing 2-HG.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The trypsinization and resuspension of the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells are performed in an assay media comprising McCoy's 5A with no L-glutamine, 10% fetal bovine serum, 1X antibiotic-antimycotic solution, and 0.3 mg/mL G418, obtaining an aliquot of 10,000 cells/100 mu L and transferring the aliquot to each well of a clear 96-well tissue culture plate, incubating the cells in the culture plate in 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C in an incubator overnight, obtaining an aliquot of 50 mu L of an assay media comprising the inhibitor of mt-IDH1, and adding the aliquot to each well of the clear 96-well tissue culture plate and maintaining the culture plate in 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C in an incubator for 24 hours, removing the media from each well of the clear 96-well tissue culture plate and adding 150 mu L of a methanol/water mixture (80/20 v/v) to each well, maintaining the plate at 80 degrees C freezer for 12 hours, obtaining an aliquot of 125 mu L of extracted methanol/water mixture and analyzing the aliquot by a high-throughput-mass spectrometry to determine the cellular 2-HG level and determining the IC50 value of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 for the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for reducing the level of 2-hydroxyglutarate (2-HG) in a patient diagnosed with acute myeloid leukemia (AML) having a mutation at residue 132 of IDH1 as compared to a level of 2-HG in the patient prior to administration of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1, which involves:
   (A) administering to the patient a therapeutically effective amount an inhibitor of mutant isocitrate dehydrogenase 1 protein (mt-IDH1), where the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 has an IC50 value of 0.1 micromolar or less against HCT116 cells harboring mt-IDH1 (HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells) such as HCT116 isogenic IDH1-R132H cells and HCT116 isogenic IDH1-R132C cells, where the IC50 value of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 is determined by a cellular 2-HG assay using HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells by culturing the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells in a growth media comprising McCoy's 5A, 10% fetal bovine serum, 1X antibiotic-antimycotic solution, and 0.3 mg/mL G418 in 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator at 37 degrees C;
   (B) trypsinizing and resuspending the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells in an assay media comprising McCoy's 5A with no L-glutamine, 10% fetal bovine serum, 1X antibiotic-antimycotic solution, and 0.3 mg/mL G418;
   (C) obtaining an aliquot of 10,000 cells/100 mu L and transferring the aliquot to each well of a clear 96-well tissue culture plate;
   (D) incubating the cells in the culture plate in 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C in an incubator overnight;
   (E) obtaining an aliquot of 50 mu L of an assay media comprising the inhibitor of mt-IDH1, and adding the aliquot to each well of the clear 96-well tissue culture plate and maintaining the culture plate in 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C in an incubator for 24 hours;
   (F) removing the media from each well of the clear 96-well tissue culture plate and adding 150 mu L of a methanol/water mixture (80/20 v/v) to each well;
   (G) maintaining the plate at 80 degrees C freezer for 12 hours;
   (H) obtaining an aliquot of 125 mu L of extracted methanol/water mixture and analyzing the aliquot by a high-throughput-mass spectrometry to determine the cellular 2-HG level; and
   (I) determining the IC50 value of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 for the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: 2-HG neomorphic activity of the 2-HG producing cells is reduced after contacting the 2-HG producing cells with the inhibitor of mt-IDH1. The 2-HG neomorphic activity is R-2-HG neomorphic activity. The 2-HG producing cells comprise a mutation in IDH1 at residues 97 or 100. The 2-HG producing cells comprise a mutation in IDH1 is G97D and R100Q. The 2-HG producing cells comprise a mutation in IDH1 at residue 132. The 2-HG producing cells comprise a mutation in IDH1 such as R132H and R132C. The 2-HG producing cells comprise a mutation in IDH1 such as R132S, R132G, R132L, and R132V. The 2-HG producing cells comprise a mutation in mt isocitrate dehydrogenase-2 (IDH2) at residues 140 or 172. The 2-HG producing cells comprise a mutation in IDH2 such as R140Q, R140G, R172K, R172M, R172S, R172G, and R172W. The 2-HG producing cells are obtained from a biological sample from a human patient. The 2-HG producing cells are in a patient diagnosed with acute myeloid leukemia (AML). The level of 2-HG is reduced in the patient as compared to a level of 2-HG in the patient prior to administration of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1. The level of 2-HG in the patient is reduced following administration of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 as compared to a level of 2-HG in the patient prior to administration of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1. The 2-HG producing cell comprises a mutation in IDH1 such as R132S, R132G, R132L, and R132V. Preferred Process: A method for reducing the production of 2-hydroxyglutarate (2-HG) in a cell producing 2-HG (2-HG producing cell), where the 2-HG producing cell harbors a mutant IDH1 (mt-IDH1) such as R132H, R132C, R132S, R132G, R132L, and R132V involves contacting the 2-HG producing cell with an inhibitor of mutant isocitrate dehydrogenase-1 protein (mt-IDH1), where the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 has an IC50 value of 0.1 micromolar or less against HCT116 cells harboring mt-IDH1 (HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells) such as HCT116 isogenic IDH1-R132H cells and HCT116 isogenic IDH1-R132C cells, where the IC50 value of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 is determined by a cellular 2-HG assay using HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells by culturing the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells in a growth media comprising McCoy's 5A, 10% fetal bovine serum, 1X antibiotic-antimycotic solution, and 0.3 mg/mL G418 in 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator at 37 degrees C, trypsinizing and resuspending the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells in an assay media comprising McCoy's 5A with no L-glutamine, 10% fetal bovine serum, 1X antibiotic-antimycotic solution, and 0.3 mg/mL G418, obtaining an aliquot of 10,000 cells/100 mu L and transferring the aliquot to each well of a clear 96-well tissue culture plate, incubating the cells in the culture plate in 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C in an incubator overnight, obtaining an aliquot of 50 mu L of an assay media comprising the inhibitor of mt-IDH1, and adding the aliquot to each well of the clear 96-well tissue culture plate and maintaining the culture plate in 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C in an incubator for 24 hours, removing the media from each well of the clear 96-well tissue culture plate and adding 150 mu L of a methanol/water mixture (80/20 v/v) to each well, maintaining the plate at 80 degrees C freezer for 12 hours, obtaining an aliquot of 125 mu L of extracted methanol/water mixture and analyzing the aliquot by a high-throughput-mass spectrometry to determine the cellular 2-HG level and determining the IC50 value of the inhibitor of mt-IDH1 for the HCT116 mt-IDH1 cells. The mutation at residue 132 of IDH1 is R132H and R132C. The mutation at residue 132 of IDH1 is R132S, R132G, R132L, and R132V. The patient has an IDH1 mutation such as R132H, R132C, R132S, R132G, R132L, and R132V.
   ACTIVITY - None given. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Mutant isocitrate dehydrogenase 1 protein inhibitor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2022138910-A1
TI Hydrocarbon production by using carbon dioxide contained in source material gas to produce carbon monoxide, and using hydrogen and carbon monoxide contained in source material gas, where source material gases contains carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen, which are not from fossil fuel.
AU ATSUSHI K
   AKIRA G
   TAKUYA K
AE ENEOS CORP (NIOC-C)
GA 202286464A
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbon production method comprises (a) a first production step of using carbon dioxide contained in a source material gas to produce carbon monoxide, and (b) a second production step of using hydrogen and carbon monoxide contained in a source material gas to produce a hydrocarbon, where each of the source material gases contains at least one of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen, which are not from fossil fuel origin.
   USE - Hydrocarbon production method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces fossil fuel-derived materials contained in the raw materials used for producing hydrocarbons, and reduces the use of fossil fuels such as natural gas and coal as the fuel used for the production of hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further includes a hydrocarbon gas separation step that separates a hydrocarbon gas having 4 or less carbon atoms from the hydrocarbon produced in step (b), a step of combustion for burning the hydrocarbon gas, and a temperature raising step of raising the temperature of the source material gas with the combustion exhaust gas generated in the combustion step, where the source material gas contains carbon dioxide recovered from the combustion exhaust gas. The method further includes a recycled gas separation step that separates at least one of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen contained in the gas discharged in step (b) as recycled gas, and a mixing step of mixing the separated recycled gas with the raw material gas used in step (b). In step (a), carbon monoxide is produced by a reverse shift reaction. In step (a), carbon monoxide is produced by electrolytic reduction. In step (b), hydrocarbons are produced by a reaction by the FT method (Fischer-Tropsch process). The method includes a hydrocarbon gas separation step that separates a hydrocarbon gas having 4 or less carbon atoms from the hydrocarbon produced in step (b), where at least one of the hydrocarbon gas separation step and the recycled gas separation step separates the object by at least one of a membrane separation method, a pressure fluctuation adsorption method (Pressure Swing Adsorption), and a temperature fluctuation adsorption method (thermal swing adsorption method). The step (a) or step (b) involves a reaction that produces water, where the water generated by the reaction is used as a raw material for the electrolysis. Preferred Components: The source material gas includes an unreacted gas generated in step (a) or step (b). The source material gas contains hydrogen produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy. The source material gas contains carbon dioxide recovered from the atmosphere. The source material gas contains carbon dioxide recovered from combustion exhaust gas.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN107720804-A
TI Preparing zinc carbonate comprises e.g. adding zinc oxide and water to a reaction vessel, stirring, dispersing uniformly to obtain slurry, filling the low-temperature carbon dioxide into slurry, stirring and introducing the carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN Q
AE YANGZHOU ZHENZHONG ZINC CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 201817192F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zinc carbonate comprises (i) adding zinc oxide and water in a ratio of 1:3-4 to a reaction vessel, stirring for 30-40 minutes and dispersing uniformly to obtain slurry, (ii) filling the low-temperature carbon dioxide into the slurry and stirring for 5-10 minutes, (iii) introducing the carbon dioxide continuously, pressurizing and heating the reaction kettle and reacting continuously for 1.5-2 hours and (iv) filtering the reaction material after finishing the reaction and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing zinc carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high conversion rate, is simple and environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reaction kettle comprises a rectangular main body. The main body is set on the reaction pot and set at the top part of the main body and extends into the main body of the stirring device. The bottom of the reaction pot is set with a supporting plate lifting device and the bearing plate. The bearing plate is hollow. The edge is provided with multiple connected to the hollow part of one side of the opening. The main body comprises an upper section and a lower section. The reaction pot is reduced is in the lower section, after ascending kettle wall at the upper section. The upper section is hollow. The hollow kettle wall is filled in air of 45-55 degrees C. The inner side of the bottom wall of the upper section is provided with a second opening. The second opening is provided with a sealed opening of the second baffle on the edge of the supporting plate. The one side of the opening is provided with a rake angle can be adapted in the second opening. The rake angle is provided with a hollow part and an opening a through opening so that the air in the kettle wall of the hollow can through the opening into the bearing plate. The stirring device comprises a stirring motor, a stirring shaft connected to the bottom of the stirring motor and a stirring blade connected to the lower part of the stirring shaft. The stirring motor is connected to the one end of the cylinder. The lifting device is a cylinder two. The lower end of the baffle is hinged on the hollow kettle wall and located below the second opening. The device further comprises a compression spring. The compression spring is fixed on the baffle. The other end is fixed on the kettle wall opposite to the baffle. The stirring blade is provided with multiple air inlet hole. The air inlet hole is provided with a ventilating film. The main body is provided with an air outlet valve. In the step (ii) the low-temperature carbon dioxide temperature is 0-4 degrees C. In the step (iii), the reaction kettle is heated at 40-50 degrees C and pressurized under 0.5-0.6MPa.

PN CN111559750-A
TI Preparing high-efficiency continuous electronic grade lithium fluoride, comprises e.g. adding industrial grade lithium carbonate to carbonization kettle filled with ultrapure water to prepare lithium carbonate slurry and post-processing.
AU XU M
   WANG Z
   DUAN Z
   KANG W
   HU W
   CHEN H
   LUAN Z
   ZHOU C
AE BAIYIN ZHONGTIAN CHEM CO LTD (BAIY-Non-standard)
GA 202083033T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-efficiency continuous electronic grade lithium fluoride, comprises e.g. adding industrial grade lithium carbonate to carbonization kettle filled with ultrapure water to prepare lithium carbonate slurry and passing carbon dioxide to obtain lithium bicarbonate solution and filtering to obtain pure lithium bicarbonate solution, pumping the lithium bicarbonate solution into ion exchange system, recrystallizing, transporting the high-purity lithium carbonate ointment to high-purity carbonization tank filled with ultrapure water, then adjusting slurry to obtain lithium carbonate slurry, and passing carbon dioxide for carbonization, converting lithium carbonate to lithium bicarbonate, then filtering and cooling to obtain high-purity lithium bicarbonate solution, pumping the lithium bicarbonate solution into synthesis kettle, and adding hydrofluoric acid for reaction to obtain lithium fluoride slurry and then post-processing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-efficiency continuous electronic grade lithium fluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces content of impurities in the product; ensures quality of lithium fluoride products; improves the production capacity of lithium fluoride; meets the raw material indicators and raw material supply for lithium hexafluorophosphate production; reduces labor cost of production; is circulation system, high raw material utilization rate and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-efficiency continuous electronic grade lithium fluoride, comprises (i) adding industrial grade lithium carbonate to carbonization kettle filled with ultrapure water to prepare lithium carbonate slurry, then passing carbon dioxide to react, completing the reaction to obtain lithium bicarbonate solution and filtering to obtain pure lithium bicarbonate solution, (ii) pumping the step (i) pure lithium bicarbonate solution into ion exchange system to remove calcium, magnesium and other cations in the lithium bicarbonate solution, (iii) pumping the step (ii) lithium bicarbonate solution to remove cations into the reaction kettle for recrystallization, using steam coil heating to crystallize lithium bicarbonate liquid to produce lithium carbonate solid and filtering the residue through vibrating screen and centrifuging to obtain high purity lithium carbonate ointment, and cooling the lithium carbonate mother liquor produced after centrifugation by two stages of cooling circulating water and then returning to the step (i) carbonization stage for recycling, (iv) transporting the step (iii) high-purity lithium carbonate ointment to high-purity carbonization tank filled with ultrapure water, then adjusting slurry to obtain lithium carbonate slurry, and passing carbon dioxide for carbonization, converting lithium carbonate to lithium bicarbonate, then filtering and cooling to obtain high-purity lithium bicarbonate solution, (v) pumping the step (iv) high-purity lithium bicarbonate solution into synthesis kettle, and adding hydrofluoric acid for reaction, completing the reaction to obtain lithium fluoride slurry, filtering and washing to obtain high-purity lithium fluoride ointment, and refluxing the lithium fluoride mother liquor produced during filtration for high-purity carbonization, (vi) applying the step (v) lithium fluoride ointment to vacuum dryer and using steam for heating and drying to obtain dry lithium fluoride, and (vii) cooling the step (vi) dried lithium fluoride in cooler, sieving, discharging and packaging to obtain electronic grade lithium fluoride products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i): lithium carbonate slurry ratio is 25:1-35:1; carbon dioxide inflow speed is 10-60 Nm/h; and reaction temperature is 25-35 degrees C and reaction time is 1.5-2.5 hours. In step (ii): pumping speed of lithium bicarbonate solution is 10-15 m2/hour; and plasma content of calcium and magnesium after ion exchange is 0.02-0.2 parts per million. In step (iii): pumping speed of the lithium bicarbonate solution is 10-15m2/hour; recrystallization temperature is 70-100 degrees C and recrystallization time is 1.5-2 hours; concentration of the lithium carbonate mother liquor is 9.5-13.5 g/l; and temperature of the lithium carbonate mother liquor after cooling is 20-35 degrees C. In step (iv): lithium carbonate slurry ratio is 25:1-35:1; carbon dioxide inflow speed is 10-60 Nm/h; and carbonization temperature is 25-35 degrees C and carbonization time is 2.5-3.5 hours. In step (v): mass percentage of hydrofluoric acid is 30-40 wt.%; reaction temperature is 40-50 degrees c; reaction time is 2.5-3.5 hours; addition rate of high-purity lithium bicarbonate solution is 6-10 m/hours; addition rate of hydrofluoric acid is 250-300 l/hour; and pH control of the synthesis end point is 2-6. In step (vi): drying is to dry the lithium fluoride moisture to less than or equal to 100 parts per million; and controlling drying time to within 8-12 hours.

MC E11-W; E33-G

PN EP3599218-A1
TI Continuous conversion of hydrocarbon-containing feed gas into synthesis gas involves catalytic steam reforming feed gas mixture into raw synthesis gas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide, and discharging hydrogen and carbon monoxide gas.
AU POLSTER B
   HERNANDEZ A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202009211J
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous conversion of a hydrocarbon-containing feed gas into a synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen involves providing a feed gas mixture comprising a hydrocarbon-containing gas and water vapor, splitting the feed gas mixture under reforming conditions by catalytic steam reforming in a burner-fired tube reformer into a raw synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methane, separating carbon dioxide from the raw synthesis gas, separating the raw synthesis gas into a hydrogen-rich gas, a carbon-monoxide-rich gas and a methane-rich gas, optionally feeding the hydrogen-rich gas to purification process, or discharging from the process as hydrogen product gas and using the carbon monoxide-rich gas as a carbon monoxide product gas further outside the process, optionally cleaning the hydrogen-rich gas by separating gaseous impurities and adding to the heating gas mixture and discharging the cleaned hydrogen-rich gas as hydrogen product gas.
   USE - Continuous conversion of hydrocarbon-containing feed gas into carbon monoxide- and hydrogen-containing synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically produces synthesis gas comprising hydrogen product gas having high purity of 99.9-99.999%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuous conversion of a hydrocarbon-containing feed gas into a synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen involves providing a feed gas mixture comprising a hydrocarbon-containing gas and water vapor, and a heating gas mixture comprising a fuel gas and an oxygen-rich gas, splitting the feed gas mixture under reforming conditions by catalytic steam reforming in a burner-fired tube reformer into a raw synthesis gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methane, generating heat necessary for the splitting which is indirectly transferred to the feed gas mixture, by burning the heating gas mixture in which a basic hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio is obtained during normal operation of the process, separating carbon dioxide from the raw synthesis gas, separating the raw synthesis gas into a hydrogen-rich gas, a carbon-monoxide-rich gas and a methane-rich gas, optionally feeding the hydrogen-rich gas to purification process, or discharging from the process as hydrogen product gas and using the carbon monoxide-rich gas as a carbon monoxide product gas further outside the process, optionally cleaning the hydrogen-rich gas by separating gaseous impurities and adding to the heating gas mixture and discharging the cleaned hydrogen-rich gas as hydrogen product gas, mixing at least one portion of the methane-rich gas with the feed gas mixture, and admixing at least one portion of the hydrogen product gas and/or a portion of the hydrogen-rich gas which is increased in proportion compared to the normal operation of the process, with the heating gas mixture, so that a hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio decreases or increases compared to the basic hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for plant for continuous conversion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The methane-rich gas is mixed in an amount of at least 50%, preferably at least 90% with the feed gas mixture. The hydrogen product gas or hydrogen-rich gas is added in an amount of at least 20%, preferably at least 40% to the heating gas mixture. The carbon monoxide product gas is added in an amount of at least 20%, preferably at least 40% to the heating gas mixture.

PN CN110105571-A; CN110105571-B
TI Block polyimide useful in preparing gas separation flat membrane for selective gas separation, e.g. carbon dioxide and methane, oxygen and nitrogen, hydrogen and nitrogen, comprises first repeating unit and second repeating unit.
AU REN J
   FENG Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201970675K
AB    NOVELTY - Block polyimide comprises first repeating unit (I) and second repeating unit (II).
   USE - The block polyimide is useful in preparing gas separation flat membrane for selective gas separation, e.g. carbon dioxide and methane, oxygen and nitrogen, hydrogen and nitrogen, hydrogen and methane, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, hydrogen and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The block polyimide has improved gas separation performance. The method: utilizes cheap and easily available monomer materials; avoids problem of poor reactivity of monomer in traditional two-step method cannot be synthesized high molecular weight polymer using high temperature solution method; and reduces loss of gas separation performance which is caused by phase transformation for many times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Block polyimide comprises first repeating unit of formula (I) and second repeating unit of formula (II).
   R1, R2 = (perfluoropropane-2,2-diyl)dibenzene-3,4-diyl or biphenyl-3,3',4,4'-tetrayl;
   R3 = 1,3-benzene-diyl, methylenebis(2-methylbenzene-4-yl) or methylenebis(2,6-dimethylbenzene-4-yl);
   R4 = substituted 1,3-benzene-diyl group of formula (III) or substituted 1,4-benzene-diyl group of formula (IV); and
   L1-L4 = H, F, Cl, Br, I, CF3, CCl3, CBr3, CI3, CH3, C2H5, NO2, CN, or COOH.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing block polyimide, comprising (i) preparing oligomeric polyimide block (block A) having an anhydride end group from at least one dianhydride of formula (V) and at least one diamine of formula (VI), and (ii) reacting step (i) product with at least one dianhydride of formula (VII) and at least one diamine of formula (VIII) to obtain block polyimide;
   (2) gas separation flat membrane, prepared from block polyimide; and
   (3) preparing gas separation flat membrane, comprising either (1) mixing block polyimide with solvent to obtain block polyimide solution having solid content of 150 wt.%, where the solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide and N,N-dimethyl cetamide, (2) coating the block polyimide solution on a glass plate using a scraper having gap of 400-600 mu m, and (3) drying the step (2) product in a drying box at 40-100 degrees C for 24-48 hours, peeling off the film formed from the glass plate, drying at 100-200 degrees C for 12-24 hours, and drying at 220-350 degrees C for 1-5 hours under vacuum/nitrogen atmosphere to obtain gas separation flat membrane, or (1) directly coating the obtained block polyimide solution on the glass plate using the scraper having gap of 400-600 mu m, and (2) drying the glass plate in the drying box at 40-100 degrees C for 24-48 hours, peeling off the film formed from the glass plate, drying at 100-200 degrees C for 12-24 hours, and drying at 220-350 degrees C for 1-5 hours under vacuum/nitrogen atmosphere to obtain gas separation flat membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The degree of polymerization of the block A is 1-100; and the weight ratio between block A and block B in block polyimide is 1:5-5:1. The block polyimide soluble in aprotic dipolar solvent. R1 comprises total of greater than or equal to 50 mol.% of the groups (perfluoropropane-2,2-diyl)dibenzene-3,4-diyl and/or biphenyl-3,3',4,4'-tetrayl, and any other tetravalent aromatic hydrocarbon functional groups; R2 includes total of greater than or equal to 50 mol.% of groups (perfluoropropane-2,2-diyl)dibenzene-3,4-diyl and/or biphenyl-3,3',4,4'-tetrayl, and any other tetravalent aromatic hydrocarbon functional groups; R3 includes total of greater than 50 mol.% of groups 1,3-benzene-diyl, methylenebis(2-methylbenzene-4-yl) and/or methylenebis(2,6-dimethylbenzene-4-yl), and any other divalent aromatic functional group; and R4 includes total of greater than 50 mol.% of group (III) and/or (IV), and any other divalent aromatic functional groups. R1, R2 are 0-100 mol.% (perfluoropropane-2,2-diyl)dibenzene-3,4-diyl and 0-100 mol.% biphenyl-3,3',4,4'-tetrayl respectively; R3 is 0-100 mol.% 1,3-benzene-diyl, 0-100 mol.% methylenebis(2-methylbenzene-4-yl) and 0-100 mol.% methylenebis(2,6-dimethylbenzene-4-yl); and R4 is composed of 0-100 mol.% (III) and 0-100 mol.% (IV). Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) adding molar excess of (V) and at least one (VI) into solvent, reacting by heating at 100-250 degrees C for 2-24 hours under stirring condition, stopping heating and cooling to room temperature to obtain (I), where the solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone or m-cresol, (ii) adding at least one (VII) and at least one diamine (VIII) into step (i) product, reacting by stirring at 100-250 degrees C for 2-24 hours, and adding into solvent to obtain block polyimide solution having solid content of 5-50 wt.%, and (iii) pouring step (ii) product into washing reagent, washing, where the washing reagent includes methanol, ethanol and/or water, and treating at 100-250 degrees C for 2-24 hours to obtain final product.

PN IN201741001970-A
TI Culturing e.g. neuronal cells on single tissue chip platform without microfludic method for accelerating drug discovery, comprises e.g. centrifuging whole human blood, spinning dislodged mesenchymal stem cells and seeding and dislodging.
AU BALASUNDARAM A
   KALAICHITRA P
AE ARTHI B (ARTH-Individual)
GA 201858324T
AB    NOVELTY - Robust, efficient and economical process culturing of four types of cells comprising neuronal cells, cardiomyocytes, hepatocytes and skeletal muscle cells on a single tissue chip platform without microfludic method for accelerating drug discovery, comprises: centrifuging whole human blood to separate supernatant layer and the white blood cell layer; transferring the separated supernatant layer in to a flask comprising of RPMI and incubated in 5% carbon dioxide atmosphere; spinning the dislodged mesenchymal stem cells and seeding into fresh Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) without glucose and fetal bovine serum and incubated in 5% carbon dioxide to culture mesenchymal stem cells; transferring the cultured mesenchymal stem cells into 4 cell culture flasks with varying proportion of growth factors; transferring the differentiated mesenchymal stem cells to a tissue chip with Pentagrit cell culture matrix; and dislodging mechanically the formed cells from the tissue chip.
   USE - The process is useful for culturing of four types of cells comprising neuronal cells, cardiomyocytes, hepatocytes and skeletal muscle cells on a single tissue chip platform without microfludic method for accelerating drug discovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is robust, efficient and cost-effective; and allows all the biochemical assays to be established on the single chip at any stage to study the toxicity parameters and aiding newer drug discovery, without requiring microfludic method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Robust, efficient and economical process for culturing four types of cells comprising neuronal cells, cardiomyocytes, hepatocytes and skeletal muscle cells on a single tissue chip platform without microfludic method for accelerating drug discovery, comprises: centrifuging whole human blood to separate supernatant layer and the white blood cell layer where the supernatant layer comprises of significant amount of mesenchymal stem cells; transferring the separated supernatant layer in to a flask comprising of RPMI and incubated in 5% carbon dioxide atmosphere to promote adhesion of the mesenchymal stem cells after cell growth to the bottom layer of the flask where supernatant was discarded and mesenchymal stem cells were mechanically dislodged using cell dislodger and gentle shaking; spinning the dislodged mesenchymal stem cells and seeding into fresh Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) without glucose and fetal bovine serum and incubated in 5% carbon dioxide to culture mesenchymal stem cells; transferring the cultured mesenchymal stem cells into 4 cell culture flasks with varying proportion of growth factors for differentiation and followed by incubating to form differentiated mesenchymal stem cells; transferring the differentiated mesenchymal stem cells to a tissue chip with Pentagrit cell culture matrix followed by incubation to form 3 dimensional growth of neuronal cells, cardiomyocytes, hepatocytes and skeletal muscle cells on respective matrix; dislodging mechanically the formed neuronal cells, cardiomyocytes, hepatocytes and skeletal muscle cells from the tissue chip where the tissue chip serves as a single platform for the growth of four different types of cells at the same time. The tissue chip with Pentagrit cell culture matrix contains D0422-Tryptophan, 0.024, Leucine 0.130.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The tissue chip is made of biopolymer material. The tissue chip has the dimensions with length of 1.5 cm, height of 0.3 cm with 0.1 cm as diameter of the matrix.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Carbonated aerosol useful for moisturizing skin,            comprises aerosol stock solution comprising ceramide            lipid, ionic surfactant, water-soluble polymer and            water, and propellant comprising carbon dioxide            gas.
AU MIYAZAKI T
   MATSUOKA M
   HARADA R
   SUZUKI A
   MIYAZAKI M
AE KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
GA 202248438L
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonated aerosol comprises an aerosol stock                solution comprising ceramide lipid, ionic                surfactant, water-soluble polymer and water, and a                propellant comprising carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The carbonated aerosol is useful for                moisturizing skin.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonated aerosol maintains softness and                smooth appearance of the skin, has improved                stability, carbon dioxide solubility, coatability                and applicability, and exhibits excellent                penetration into skin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The carbonated aerosol                  comprises 0.001-15 %mass ceramide lipid, 0.01-5                  %mass ionic surfactant and 0.01-2 %mass                  water-soluble polymer in the aerosol stock                  solution. The mass ratio of water-soluble                  polymer/ionic surfactant to the ionic surfactant                  is 0.01-10. The water-soluble polymer is at least                  one chosen from hydroxypropylmethylcellulose,                  hydroxypropyl methylcellulose stearoxy ether,                  carboxyvinyl polymer, alkyl modified carboxyvinyl                  polymer, acrylic acid/(meth) acrylic acid alkyl                  copolymer, and 2-methacrylic acid oxyethyl                  phosphorylcholine and butyl methacrylate                  copolymer. The mass ratio of carbon dioxide                  gas/ceramide lipid to the ceramide lipid is                  0.005-3000. The content of carbon dioxide gas is                  0.01-5 parts mass with respect to 100 parts mass                  aerosol stock solution.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological. Test details are described but                no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The carbonated aerosol is administered by                topical route (claimed). No dosage details                given.
   EXAMPLE - One %mass                N-(hexadecyloxypropyl)-N-hydroxyethyl                hexadecanamide, 0.33 %mass N-stearoyl-N-methyl                taurine sodium, 0.1 %mass acrylic acid-alkyl                acrylate (10-30C) alkyl copolymer, 15 %mass                glycerin, 0.15 %mass arginine, and purified water                (suitable amount) were mixed to prepare aerosol                stock solution. The obtained aerosol stock solution                was filled into a pressure-resistant container (100                ml aerosol glass bottle), sealed, and then the                carbon dioxide gas was pressurized to produce                carbonated aerosol.

PN WO2020010427-A1; BR102018014255-A2
TI Performing inverse respiratory process for carbon dioxide absorption, involves using composition of seven large technological systems that determine operational set, such as inhalation system or dioxide supply of carbon.
AU GARNICA L
AE GARNICA L (GARN-Individual)
GA 2020065696
AB    NOVELTY - Performing inverse respiratory process for carbon dioxide absorption, involves using composition of seven large technological systems that determine operational set, such as inhalation system or dioxide supply of carbon, transformation system, redox or energization system, drain system, secondary feeding system (substrates and micronutrients), exhaust system and integrated active system.
   USE - Method for performing inverse respiratory process for carbon dioxide absorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide inhalation or feeding system comprises arterial alveoli containing an enzyme complex composed of Carbonic Anhydrase, inserted in the transformation medium, alveoli through which carbon dioxide is inhaled, assimilated and transported, carbonates, products of carbon dioxide fixation and delivered by osmotic transfer from the alveolus to the transformation medium. The arterial alveolus is constructed of membranous tissue, permeable to carbonates, or to the transport of balance related to equation. The bed of the arterial alveolus the pH is reduced and maintained in the range of 2.5-3. The inhalation system is used in any of the productive processes of a continuous, semi-continuous or continuous nature. batch. The transformation system is the place where enzymatic or biochemical reactions occur by successive reactions, carbonates, in conjunction with primary fermentation reactions, are reduced, or transformed, to various metabolic ones. The reaction cycle is established in Glycolysis, the central route in the substrate catabolism fed by secondary feeding system, followed by pyruvate decarboxylation reactions and reduction of acetic aldehyde to ethanol. The main metabolic produced in the transformation system is ethanol. The transformation system is built in any of the enzymatic media, in a fixed bed, or in biological media with fungi of the genus Saccharomyces. The transformation system the pH is maintained in the range of 4.2-5.0. The transformation system is used in any of the productive processes of a continuous, semi-continuous or in nature batch. The energization system or Redox supplies energy to the transformation medium to stimulate carbonate reduction reactions up to the ethanol, with a natural increase in the activity of Redox-acting coenzymes, such as the NADH coenzyme. The energization system is monitored and controlled by the integrated active system, establishing functionality and balance of the reactions of transformation or redox, depending on the instant conditions of inhalation or feeding, drainage, exhaustion and instant operational levels of the temperature, pH, Brix and GL quantities in the transformation medium. The energization system operates in batch, continuous or semi-continuous modes. The drain system promotes the drainage or removal of metabolic substances in liquid form present in the transformation medium. The set of drains distributed through the transformation medium. The drains being constructed in membranous tissues, permeable to the metabolic types of ethanol, higher alcohols, acetic aldehyde, organic acids and glycerol. The drain system is effective in operation in continuous or semi-continuous mode. The secondary substrate feeding system has the function of maintaining the primary fermentation reactions. The substrate being sugar for direct fermentation, such as hexoses monosaccharides and disaccharides. The substrate feeding follows controlled flow feeding patterns. The secondary micronutrient feeding system has the function of ensuring the balance of availability and demand of enzymatic activities by inoculation, mainly of the elements phosphorus, zinc, magnesium, potassium, nitrogen. The feeding of these microorganisms follow decisions of the integrated active system. The secondary supply system operates in batch, continuous or semi-continuous modes. The exhaust system has the function of removing gases from the transformation medium with oxygen, carbon dioxide and volatile products, such as ethanol, the main gases being exhaust products. The carbon dioxide is recycled to the transformation medium. The gases are recirculated by the inhalation system. The exhaust system operates in batch, continuous or semi-continuous modes. The integrated active system has the function of supervising control and data acquisition of the set of systems involved, through sensors, processors and instantaneous actuators, capable of providing the maximization and enhancement of chemical, enzymatic and biochemical reactions, involved in the transformation environment.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

AE PRAXAIR ARGENTINA SRL (PRAX-C)
