PN KR2018037729-A; KR1955236-B1
TI Catalyst useful for reducing carbon dioxide, comprises a support, and nanoparticles including metal nanoparticles coated with polyimine-based polymer, supported on support.
AU JANG J H
   PARK H S
   JUHN K H
   JONG Y S
   YOUNG K J
   HYUNG C H
   HENKENSMEIER D
   YOUNG L S
   RYU J
   MIN G H
   HOON C Y
   KIM H J
   YOO S J
   KIM J Y
   CHUL H H
   LEE S Y
   GWAN H M
   CHUNG Y H
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 2018333359
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a support, and nanoparticles including metal nanoparticles coated with polyimine-based polymer, supported on the support.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high carbon dioxide reduction current density and reduction efficiency than regular catalysts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing catalyst, comprising adding reducing agent into a dispersion of polyethylenimine, nanoparticles precursor and support to perform reduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal nanoparticles are supported on the support in amount of 10-30 wt.% based on total weight of the support. The nanoparticles have diameter of 2-5 nm. The dispersion comprises mixed dispersion of primary dispersion containing polyimine-based polymer and support, and secondary dispersion containing nanoparticles precursor. The reducing agent is sodium borohydride. The nanoparticles precursor is chloroauric acid. The mixed dispersion is obtained by adding the second dispersion into the first dispersion.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersion, the first dispersion, and the second dispersion include ethanol as dispersion medium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyimine-based polymer is polyethylenimine. The polyethylenimine has weight average molecular weight of 1000-3000.
   EXAMPLE - 2400 mu l Polyethylenimine (molecular weight of 2000) and 0.15 g Vulcan XC72 (RTM: Multi-purpose conductive carbon black) were dispersed in 740 ml ethanol, and ultrasonically treated for 30 minutes to obtain carbon powder solution. 75 mg Chloroauric acid was added into 20 ml ethanol separately, and mechanically stirred to obtain metal precursor solution. The carbon powder solution was stirred with 20 ml metal precursor solution for 2 hours. Sodium borohydride solution was dissolved in 20 ml ethanol, and then added into 760 ml mixed solution of metal precursor and carbon powder to initiate reduction reaction of the metal precursor. The solution was mechanically stirred for 12 hours to allow metal nanoparticles to be completely reduced on the carbon powder. The synthesized metal nanoparticles were separated through a filter, red colored nanoparticles were washed sequentially with 800 ml ethanol and 800 ml water, and vacuum dried to recover catalyst of gold nanoparticles coated with polyethylenimine.

PT P
PN US2021372668-A1
TI System for storing and time-shifting excess electrical power from electrical power grid, has reservoir system containing quantity of thermal storage medium, where quantity of medium high temperature brine is maintained in storage zone.
AU UPADHYE R S
   BUSCHECK T A
AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
GA 2021D94409
AB    NOVELTY - A system (900) comprises a reservoir system (950) containing a thermal storage medium, an oxy-combustion furnace and a discharge subsystem. The thermal storage medium contains sand, rocks and/or manufactured granular material including ceramic pebble(s), or a mixture of manufactured granular material, sand and rocks. The thermal storage medium is provided to be heated using a combustible fuel source. The furnace is used for heating the thermal storage medium during a charge operation using oxygen, and natural gas, coal, coke, petroleum, petroleum coke, tires, solid waste, biomass and/or electricity supplied from a power grid (12) or power plant. The discharge subsystem contains a heat exchanger containing an input (A) provided to receive heated carbon dioxide from the reservoir system during a discharge operation of the system and to generate a quantity (X) of supercritical carbon dioxide (sCO2) at a temperature (A) and a pressure (A) at an output (A).
   USE - System for storing and time-shifting excess electrical power from electrical power grid, excess electrical power from power plant and/or heat from heat generating source in form of pressure and heat for assistance in production of electricity (claimed) in data centers, hospitals and military installations.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables increasing use of renewable and low-carbon energy, along with reducing greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions from fossil energy use. The method allows the carbon dioxide-capture system to be sized to the average electricity dispatch rate, rather than the peak dispatch rate which would maximize the return on capital investment. The time-shifting FE and/or biomass combustion can also enable the parasitic load of carbon dioxide-capture to be imposed during periods of oversupply, such as when VRE is abundant. The addition of high-/very-high-temperature TES/HTX tanks to gas-turbine and combined-cycle power plants enables combustion heat to be generated 100% of the time at a constant rate, allowing carbon dioxide capture operations to run continuously at full capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system (900) comprises a reservoir system (950) containing a thermal storage medium, an oxy-combustion furnace and a discharge subsystem. The thermal storage medium contains sand, rocks and/or manufactured granular material including ceramic pebble(s), or a mixture of manufactured granular material, sand and rocks. The thermal storage medium is provided to be heated using a combustible fuel source. The furnace is used for heating the thermal storage medium during a charge operation using oxygen, and natural gas, coal, coke, petroleum, petroleum coke, tires, solid waste, biomass and/or electricity supplied from a power grid (12) or power plant. The discharge subsystem contains a heat exchanger containing an input (A) provided to receive heated carbon dioxide from the reservoir system during a discharge operation of the system and to generate a quantity (X) of supercritical carbon dioxide (sCO2) at a temperature (A) and a pressure (A) at an output (A). A Brayton-cycle turbine (954) is provided for receiving the quantity (X) of supercritical carbon dioxide from the output (A) of the heat exchanger and generating electricity at the output (A) for transmission to a power grid. The Brayton-cycle turbine is further provided to produce a quantity (Z) of supercritical carbon dioxide having a temperature (B) and a pressure (B) at an output (B). The temperature (B) and the pressure (B) are lower than the temperature (A) and the pressure (A). A recuperator subsystem is provided for circulating the quantity (B) of supercritical carbon dioxide to reheat and re-pressurize the quantity (B) of supercritical carbon dioxide to generate a quantity (C) of supercritical carbon dioxide for reapplication to the heat exchanger. The quantity (C) of supercritical carbon dioxide is further reheated by the heat exchanger to create a quantity (D) of supercritical carbon dioxide which is output to the Brayton-cycle turbine to further assist in powering the Brayton-cycle turbine.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for storing and time-shifting at least one of excess electrical power from electrical power grid, excess electrical power from power plant, or heat from a heat generating source in the form of pressure and heat for assistance in production of electricity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of Brayton-cycle of earth battery.
   Power grid (12)
   Steam turbine (50)
   System (900)
   Reservoir system (950)
   Brayton-cycle turbine (954)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The system further comprises a drier subsystem for receiving a mixture of gaseous carbon dioxide and water vapor from the reservoir system.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); Q54 (Non-positive displacement fluid engines (i.e. driven by fluid); Miscellaneous motors and machines for producing mechanical power/thrust (F03B,D,G,H)); Q78 (Heat exchange in general (F28)); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

The third                compartment is configured to remove carbon dioxide                in the flue gas. TI Oil field three-phase separation comprises separating density difference, inputting oil field liquid or oilfield sewage into three-phase separator, introducing natural gas into three-phase separator, removing carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide and recycling.
AU YAN J
   YAN Q
AE YAN J (YANJ-Individual)
GA 2021E2828B
AB    NOVELTY - Oil field three-phase separation comprises separating the density difference, inputting the oil field liquid or oilfield sewage into the three-phase separator under the action of density difference, oil in oil field producing liquid or oil field sewage, natural gas floating, water flow downwards, (ii) floating flotation, introducing natural gas into the three-phase separator, natural gas in the rising process of micro-bubble, oil drop, mechanical impurity for flotation, and (iii) gas phase circulation, collecting the gas phase of the natural gas discharged by the three-phase separator, and removing carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide as the step two of introducing gas, and recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for oil field three-phase separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need to heat the oil well producing liquid or oily sewage in advance, can be processed at normal temperature, avoids the scale removing, and provides convenience for the subsequent water treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) Vertical oil field three-phase separation system.
   (2) Horizontal oil field three-phase separation system.

In the step (ii), the oil-water mixture in the three-phase separator is in contact with natural gas after passing through the coarse-grained material layer.

PT P
PN CN113600194-A
TI Preparing nano photocatalyst containing cobalt with different valence states used as catalyst for photo-reduction of carbon dioxide, involves calcining cobalt metalorganic framework nanospheres in inert atmosphere to obtain Co-C nano photocatalyst.
AU TAN B
   ZHANG H
   HU R
   WANG Q
   REN J
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 2021D1089K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano photocatalyst containing cobalt with different valence states involves using nitrogen-free cobalt metalorganic framework (Co-MOF) nanospheres as a precursor and dicyanodiamide as an auxiliary agent. The Co-MOF nanospheres are calcined in an inert atmosphere to obtain a Co-C nano photocatalyst or Co-MOF nanospheres are calcined in an inert atmosphere to obtain a Co-C nano-photocatalyst, and then the Co-C nano-photocatalyst is calcined in air by controlling the calcination temperature and calcination time to obtain the (CoO+Co3O4)-C nano-photocatalyst or Co3O4-C nano-photocatalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing nano photocatalyst containing cobalt with different valence states used as a catalyst for photo-reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method of the nano photocatalyst containing cobalt with different valence states widens the selection range of precursor MOF, shortens the preparation period of the cobalt-carbon based nano photocatalyst, and simultaneously ensures that cobalt species are uniformly distributed in the nano photocatalyst in a small particle form.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a nano photocatalyst containing cobalt with different valence states.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation process of the Co-MOF nanospheres without nitrogen involves adding equimolar amounts of cobalt (II) acetate tetrahydrate (Co(Ac)24H2O) and isophthalic acid into a clean reaction vessel, then pouring N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring, mixing and reacting in a constant temperature oil bath at 120degrees Celsius for 120 minutes to obtain a mixture. After the mixture is cooled to room temperature, the precipitate of the mixture is collected by centrifugation. The precipitate is washed multiple times with N,N-dimethylformamide and absolute ethanol, and then vacuum-dried to obtain Co-MOF nanospheres. The method for preparing nano photocatalyst containing cobalt with different valence states involves pulverizing the Co-MOF nanospheres, placing them on the downwind side of the quartz boat and placing the auxiliary dicyandiamine on the upwind side of the quartz boat. The pulverized Co-MOF nanospheres is calcined at 450-800degrees Celsius at a heating rate of 1-10degrees Celsius/minute for 30-240 minutes under the protection of argon atmosphere to obtain Co-C nano photocatalyst. The Co-C nano-photocatalyst is calcined in air at 190-210degrees Celsius for 10-25 minutes to obtain (CoO+Co3O4)-C nano photocatalyst. The Co-C nano photocatalyst is calcined in air at 190-210degrees Celsius for 45-150 minutes to obtain the Co3O4-C nano photocatalyst. The mass of the dicyandiamide is 0.5-2.5 times the mass of the Co-MOF nanospheres. Preferred Components: The nano photocatalysts containing cobalt in different valence states are Co-C nano photocatalysts, (CoO+Co3O4)-C nano photocatalyst or Co3O4-C nano photocatalyst.

The combustion catalyst is carbon nanotube-supported ferroferric oxide and/or cuprous oxide and/or silver oxide and/or titanium dioxide and/or metallic copper and/or metallic silver and/or two or more metallic oxides or metallic simple substances of metallic titanium, and the loading rate (mass ratio) of the carbon nanotube is 30-60%.
   USE - Carbon nanotube micro-pyrotechnic composition used in fireworks and crackers and/or liquid carbon dioxide blasting initiators (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has small using amount, stable chemical properties of raw materials, easy complete combustion, higher ignition temperature, lower explosion temperature, large gas production, less solid residues and the like, and effectively improve the combustion efficiency of pyrotechnic compositions and the safety of fireworks and crackers during production, transportation and setting off.

The mass ratio of the carbon nanotube-supported ferric oxide and copper oxide is 20:1-1:20. The mass ratio of ferroferric oxide and copper oxide loaded on the carbon nano tube is 20:1-1:20. The mass ratio of the carbon nanotube-supported ferric oxide to the silver oxide is 50:1-10:1. The mass ratio of the carbon nanotube-supported titanium dioxide to the silver oxide is 50:1-10:1. The mass ratio of the carbon nanotube-supported ferric oxide to the silver oxide is 50:1-10:1. The mass ratio of the iron oxide, the copper oxide and the titanium dioxide loaded on the carbon nanotube is 50:1:1-10:1:1.

TI Preparation of highly stable graphene oxide-ionic            liquid supported liquid membrane for separating carbon            dioxide and methane, involves mixing graphene oxide            nanosheets and deionized water, separating supernatant,            vacuum filtering with alpha-alumina substrate, and            degassing with ionic liquid.
AU LI X
   WU M
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2021B7561A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of highly stable graphene oxide                (GO)-ionic liquid supported liquid membrane                involves (1) adding graphene oxide nanosheets into                deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing to                obtain 0.01-0.05 g/L graphene oxide dispersion                liquid, centrifuging for 20-30 minutes at 3000-5000                rpm, and separating the supernatant liquid, (2)                fixing &#945;-alumina substrate in a vacuum                filtration device, vacuum filtering the supernatant                liquid, and obtaining two-dimensional GO wet film,                and (3) vacuum degassing an ionic liquid, adding                into a reaction device, fixing the GO wet film 2 cm                away from the bottom cavity, placing at                100-120&#8451; environment, at a pressure of                0.05 MPa for 1-5 hours, and vacuum drying for 10-12                hours at 40&#8451;.
   USE - Preparation of highly stable graphene                oxide-ionic liquid supported liquid membrane for                separating carbon dioxide and methane (claimed) in                marsh gas purification to obtain biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of                thin-liquid graphene membrane having high                stability, excellent gas permeability and pressure                resistance using minimum ionic liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The graphene oxide                  nanosheet has a size of 1-2 &#181;m and an                  average thickness of 1 nm.Preferred Composition: The volume of the                  supernatant is 10-20 mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The ionic liquid                  comprises 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium                  tetrafluoroborate.

TI System for manufacturing carbon monoxide gas,            consists of water electrolysis apparatus, electric            field application reverse shift reaction apparatus for            generating carbon monoxide gas using carbon dioxide and            hydrogen gas generated by water electrolysis apparatus,            and solid oxide fuel cell apparatus.
AU DAIKOH T
   JUN H
   NAOTO F
   KOHEI Y
   AKIO Y
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
GA 2021B2661A
AB    NOVELTY - A manufacturing system (100) consists of an                electric field application reverse shift reaction                apparatus (110), a water electrolysis apparatus                (120), and a solid oxide fuel cell apparatus (130).                The electric field application reverse shift                reaction apparatus uses a carbon dioxide gas and a                hydrogen gas generated by the water electrolysis                apparatus and the heat supply from a heat source of                the solid oxide fuel cell apparatus for generating                a carbon monoxide gas and water. The water                electrolysis apparatus generates the hydrogen gas                using water vapor generated from water generated by                the electric field application reverse shift                reaction apparatus.
   USE - System is used for manufacturing carbon                monoxide gas used as raw material for manufacturing                chemical products e.g. resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The manufacturing system enables efficient                manufacture of carbon monoxide gas, by economical                process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view                explaining reaction process in manufacturing                system. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
   Manufacturing system (100)
   Electric field application reverse shift                reaction apparatus (110)
   Water electrolysis apparatus (120)
   Solid oxide fuel cell apparatus (130)
   Heat (CC)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The solid oxide fuel                  cell apparatus generates water using the hydrogen                  gas and an oxygen gas and an electric energy                  generated by the production reaction. The                  manufacturing system further consists of a                  thermoelectric conversion apparatus for                  converting heat (CC) from the heat source of the                  solid oxide fuel cell apparatus into electric                  power, a power supply for supplying an electric                  field using the electric power generated by the                  thermoelectric conversion apparatus with respect                  to the electric field application reverse shift                  reaction apparatus or the water electrolysis                  apparatus, and a combustor for generating heat by                  a combustion reaction using the oxygen gas                  generated by the water electrolysis apparatus.                  The electric field application reverse shift                  reaction apparatus or the water electrolysis                  apparatus activates a catalytic reaction in the                  reaction of producing carbon monoxide gas and                  water by the electric field supplied by the power                  supply. The electric field application reverse                  shift reaction apparatus generates the carbon                  monoxide gas and water using the heat generated                  by the combustor. The thermoelectric conversion                  apparatus installs a high-temperature side in the                  heat source of the solid oxide fuel cell                  apparatus for converting the heat into electric                  power. The cooling side of the thermoelectric                  conversion apparatus is cooled using a piping                  through which the water generated by the electric                  field application reverse shift reaction                  apparatus flows. The water electrolysis apparatus                  generates the oxygen gas and the hydrogen gas                  using water generated by the solid oxide fuel                  cell apparatus, by an electrochemical reaction                  using an electric energy obtained from a marine                  wind power generation or an ocean current                  electric power generation, using the heat                  generated by the combustor, or using water vapor                  generated during cooling of evaporated water in                  the cooling side of the thermoelectric conversion                  apparatus. The electric field application reverse                  shift reaction apparatus and the water                  electrolysis apparatus are installed in a place                  where the electric field application reverse                  shift reaction apparatus receive the hydrogen gas                  generated by the water electrolysis apparatus.                  The electric field application reverse shift                  reaction apparatus and the solid oxide fuel cell                  apparatus are installed in a place where the                  electric field application reverse shift reaction                  apparatus receive the heat supply from the heat                  source of the solid oxide fuel cell apparatus.                  The electric field application reverse shift                  reaction apparatus and the water electrolysis                  apparatus are connected by a piping which conveys                  the hydrogen gas generated by the water                  electrolysis apparatus to the electric field                  application reverse shift reaction                  apparatus.

PN KR2021083579-A; KR2332465-B1
TI Recovering valuable metals from cathode material            of waste lithium ion battery by charging nickel            manganese cobalt-based powder collected from cathode            material of waste battery in heat treatment furnace,            and heating carbon dioxide.
AU CHA T
   HONG S
AE POHANG INST METAL IND ADVANCEMENT (POHA-Non-standard)
   DAWON CHEM CO LTD (DAWO-Non-standard)
GA 202179335Q
AB    NOVELTY - Valuable metals are recovered from cathode                material of a waste lithium ion battery by charging                nickel manganese cobalt (NixMnxCox) (NCM)-based                powder containing lithium (Li), nickel (Ni),                manganese (Mn), and cobalt (Co) collected from the                cathode material of waste lithium ion battery in a                heat treatment furnace, and heating carbon dioxide                (CO2) in the heat treatment furnace by performing                thermal reaction treatment at greater than or equal                to 800 degrees C while injecting lithium carbonate                (Li2CO3). Phase separation includes first step of                subjecting phase-separated NCM-based powder to                water leaching using distilled water to form Li2CO3                and second step of mixing distilled water in a                weight ratio of greater than or equal to 10 times                of the NCM-based powder.
   USE - Recovery of valuable metals from cathode                material of a waste lithium ion battery.
   ADVANTAGE - Valuable metals such as nickel and lithium                carbonate can be easily recovered. High purity                lithium is obtained through a dry recovery method                using thermal decomposition and water leaching                without using a strong acid solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: First step includes                  injecting gas at 300cc per minute and performing                  thermal reaction treatment in which the                  temperature is maintained at greater than or                  equal to 800 degrees C for a certain period at a                  temperature increase rate of 5 degrees C/minute.                  In second step, distilled water and NCM-based                  powder are mixed in a weight ratio of 30:1 and                  water leaching is performed for 5 hours. In                  second step, the mixture in which the water                  leaching is completed is filtered under reduced                  pressure through a reduced pressure filter to                  form a liquid Li2CO3, and the powder of valuable                  metal oxide in a solid state is separated. The                  method includes third step of performing a                  reduction reaction treatment to generate nickel                  oxide (NiO), and after charging the valuable                  metal oxide powder in a horizontal furnace, it is                  reacted with carbon monoxide (CO) at a first                  temperature to convert the NiO powder contained                  in the valuable metal oxide powder into gaseous                  nickel carbonate (Ni(CO)4), fourth step of                  changing Ni(CO)4 in the gaseous state flowing                  into the electric furnace by heat-reacting at a                  second temperature to decompose into nickel (Ni)                  and CO gas, and fifth step of obtaining Ni powder                  in a solid state. The horizontal furnace for                  performing the fourth step and the electric                  furnace for performing the fifth step are                  connected through a pipe, and Ni(CO)4 in the                  gaseous state generated in the fourth step is                  introduced into the electric furnace through a                  pipe, so that the fourth and fifth steps are                  continuously performed, and in the fifth step,                  the Ni powder decomposed by thermal reaction                  treatment is provided with a capacitor at the                  upper side of the electric furnace, and Ni powder                  is recovered by depositing it on a capacitor. The                  first temperature in the fourth step is set to                  less than or equal to 100 degrees C, and the                  second temperature in the fifth step is set to                  greater than or equal to 300 degrees C.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PN CN112902026-A; CN112902026-B
TI Method for detecting natural gas leakage of automobile gas supply system, involves calculating leakage rate according to formula relating to methane content in natural gas source, economic coefficient of fuel, and density of methane.
AU HU N
   XIAO W
   LIU Z
   LIU S
   XU X
AE UNIV NANJING INFORMATION SCI & TECHNOLOG (UNAI-C)
GA 202167114N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a tested vehicle in a static sealed gas chamber, and starting the vehicle. Gas in the gas chamber is pumped by an electric pump above the gas chamber. A gas analyzer is connected to monitor gas concentration in the gas chamber in real time. Time sequence data of methane and carbon dioxide concentration is obtained. Carbon dioxide discharge ratio of an air inlet of a vacuum pump is connected with a vehicle exhaust port. The collected tail gas sample with nitrogen is injected into the gas chromatograph. The methane leakage discharge coefficient is obtained, and the leakage rate is calculated according to the following formula relating to methane content in the natural gas source, economic coefficient of the fuel, and density of methane.
   USE - Method for detecting natural gas leakage of natural gas automobile gas supply system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables estimating the leakage rate of the air supply system in direct manner.

PN CN112892573-A
TI Carbon nitride coated perovskite composite material used as a catalyst for carbon dioxide reduction and water splitting to produce hydrogen, comprises perovskite body, and the perovskite body is covered by a carbon nitride layer.
AU LIU S
   BIAN H
   YAN J
   LI D
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 202167999M
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon nitride (CN) coated perovskite composite material comprises a perovskite body, and the perovskite body is covered by a CN layer.
   USE - CN coated perovskite composite material used as a catalyst for carbon dioxide (CO2) reduction and water splitting to produce hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The CN coated perovskite composite material has CN layer that has good stability and certain catalytic activity at room temperature. The composite material is coated on the outer layer of the perovskite material to improve the utilization of sunlight, and promotes the separation of carriers, improves the ability to collect electrons from the composite material, improves the stability of the perovskite composite material, and changes the stability and carrier transport properties of the perovskite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a preparation method of CN-coated perovskite composite material, which involves mixing the powdered perovskite material and the powdered coating material, and calcining to obtain a CN coated perovskite composite material, where calcination temperature is less than the melting point of the perovskite material, and the calcination temperature is greater than the melting point of the coating material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The perovskite material is ABX3, where A is gold ions (Au+), silver ion (Ag+), or cesium ion (Cs+), B is lead ions (Pb2+), manganese ions (Mn2+), germanium ions (Ge2+) or tin ions (Sn2+) and X is chloride (Cl ), bromide (Br ) or iodide (I ). The coating material is dicyandiamide, thiourea, melamine or urea. Preferred Conditions: The perovskite body is irregularly spherical, and the average diameter of the perovskite body is 5-100 nanometer. The interaction interface between the perovskite body and the CN layer has a Type-II heterojunction. The calcination temperature is 350-450 degrees C, and the calcination time is 1-4 hours. The specific process of the solid-phase reaction method involves calcining ABX3 in a vacuum environment, the calcining temperature is 500-600 degrees C, and the calcining time is 2-4 hours.

PN CN112588315-A; CN112588315-B
TI Chromium-based metal oxide-molecular sieve catalyst comprises chromium-based metal oxide and H-silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve.
AU WANG S
   FAN W
   QIN Z
   DONG M
   WANG J
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202135964F
AB    NOVELTY - Chromium-based metal oxide-molecular sieve catalyst comprises chromium-based metal oxide and H-silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve, where the chemical formula of chromium-based metal oxide is chromium metal oxide complex (CraMbOc), the a to c represent the relative amount of each element, which satisfies the total valence of chromium-based metal oxide to zero, and the M is zinc, indium, aluminum and/or zirconium.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in hydrogenating carbon dioxide for preparing ethylene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has carbon dioxide conversion rate of 12.7%, total light olefins selectivity of 95.9%, and has ethylene selectivity of 66.4%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the chromium-based metal oxide-molecular sieve catalyst comprising (a) mixing water-soluble chromium salt and water-soluble M salt with water to obtain chromium-based composite metal ion salt solution, (b) mixing chromium-based composite metal ion salt solution with complexing agent or precipitating agent, and performing complexing reaction or precipitation reaction to obtain chromium-based metal oxide precursor, (c) calcining chromium-based metal oxide precursor to obtain chromium-based metal oxide, and (d) mixing chromium-based metal oxide with H-silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve to obtain chromium-based metal oxide-molecular sieve catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio a:b of amount of chromium and metal in the chromium-based metal oxide is 1:0-0.5. The mass ratio of chromium-based metal oxide and H-silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve is 0.2-5:1. The silicon-to-aluminum ratio of H-silicoaluminophosphate-34 molecular sieve is 0.01-0.5. The particle size of chromium-based metal oxide-molecular sieve catalyst is 10-60 mesh. The ratio of total metal ions in the chromium-based composite metal ion salt solution to the amount of complexing agent or precipitation agent is 1:0.5-5. Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises performing complexation reaction and precipitation reaction independently at 60-90 degrees C for 3-8 hours. The step (c) comprises calcining at 350-700 degrees for 4-10 hours.

The height of water layer of paddy field is maintained at 1-1.5 cm, during prevention and control of weeds in paddy field.
   ACTIVITY - Herbicide.
   ADMINISTRATION - The E.adenophorum extract is applied at a rate of 0.4-6.4 g/l (claimed).

   USE - The composition is useful for producing                cooling and heating, and replacing R-410A in a                refrigeration, air conditioning, or heat pump                system (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: exhibits a global warming                potential of 300-375, preferably less than 300; is                non-flammable; exhibits a cooling capacity of plus                minus 10- plus minus 20%, preferably plus minus 15                of the cooling capacity of R-410A; and exhibits a                temperature glide of less than or equal to 7                degrees C, preferably less than or equal to 10                degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing cooling, comprising condensing                the composition and then evaporating the                composition in the vicinity of a body to be                cooled;
   (2) producing heating, comprising evaporating                the composition and then condensing the composition                in the vicinity of a body to be heated;
   (3) replacing mixture of 50% difluoromethane                and 50% pentafluoroethane (R-410A) in a                refrigeration, air conditioning, or heat pump                system, comprising providing the composition as                replacement for R-410A; and
   (4) an air conditioning system, heat pump                system, or refrigeration system comprising the                composition.

PT P
PN CN111974460-A
TI Preparing nano-iron-based compound-loaded conductive polymer comprises e.g. carrying out polymerization of conductive polymer monomers using trivalent iron ions as catalyst precursor.
AU LI K
   LIU F
   WANG K
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV JINAN (UNJN-C)
GA 2020C08008
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-iron-based compound-loaded conductive polymer comprises carrying out polymerization of conductive polymer monomers using trivalent iron ions as a catalyst precursor, and taking porous structure of the conductive polymer as a carrier of iron ions to constrain the precursor of the iron compound catalyst.
   USE - The nano-iron-based compound-loaded conductive polymer is useful as an energy conversion reaction of electrocatalytic water splitting, carbon dioxide reduction, nitrogen reduction, and oxygen reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-iron-based compound-loaded conductive polymer: improve the photocatalytic and electrocatalytic activity; promotes the separation and utilization efficiency of carrier; inhibits the aggregation of graphite phase carbon nitride; and improves its photocatalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of nano-iron-based compound-loaded conductive polymer comprising taking nano-iron-based compound-loaded conductive polymer used as a co-catalyst to be loaded on semiconductors e.g. graphitic carbon nitride, sulfides and oxides to improve its carrier transport and utilization efficiency, and can be applying to reactions e.g. photocatalytic water splitting, carbon dioxide reduction, nitrogen reduction, organic-inorganic pollutant degradation and small molecule conversion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) preparing nano-iron-based compound-loaded conductive polymer is based on the different mass ratios of polymer monomer and trivalent iron salt, dissolving a certain amount of trivalent iron salt in deionized water, and adding certain amount of pyrrole, thiophene and aniline monomers in the process of ultrasound or stirring, and (ii) fully stirring above-mentioned mixed solution at 30-100 degrees C, washing with deionized water for 0-10 times, and then drying to obtain solid powder, mixing uniformly above solid powder or with certain amount of phosphorus source, sulfur source, selenium source, heating the above solid powder at 100-1000 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours, natural cooling to obtain nano-iron-based compound-loaded conductive polymer. The temperature of high temperature treatment is from room temperature to 1000 degrees C and the processing atmosphere is air, argon, nitrogen, hydrogen and/or helium. Preferred Components: The size is small, about 1-100 nm, and the iron-based compounds include simple substances, iron oxides, carbides, nitrides, phosphide, sulfids, or selenide. The conductive polymer adopts trivalent iron ions for oxidative polymerization, including polypyrrole, polythiophene, polyaniline and other conductive polymers. The iron salt includes the precursors of ferric ions, e.g. ferric sulfate, ferric acetate, ferric citrate, potassium ferricyanide, ferric fluoride, potassium hexahydroferrite, ferric chloride, and/or ferric nitrate and their crystal hydrates. The phosphate includes sodium phosphate, sodium hydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, ammonium phosphate, ammonium hydrogen phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium phosphate, potassium hydrogen phosphate, and/or potassium dihydrogen phosphate, the sulfur source includes inorganic and organic sulfur sources e.g. sulfur powder, thiourea, thioacetamide, sulfate and/or mercaptan, and the selenium source includes selenium powder and inorganic and organic selenium sources represented by sodium selenite and/or dimethyl selenium.

PN CN111778469-A
TI Improving bonding strength of thermal spray coatings on surface of light alloy parts comprises placing the parts into ultrasonic cleaning machine and using organic solvents as cleaning agents and using high-pressure carbon dioxide airflow.
AU YU Y
   HOU W
   LIU J
   SHEN J
   LU X
   JI X
   GUO J
   ZHANG D
AE BGRIMM TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (BEJM-C)
   BGRIMM NEW MATERIALS SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO (BGRI-Non-standard)
GA 2020A3101H
AB    NOVELTY - Improving bonding strength of thermal spray coatings on surface of light alloy parts comprises (a) placing the parts into the ultrasonic cleaning machine and using organic solvents as cleaning agents to perform ultrasonic cleaning and degreasing, (b) taking out the parts, using clean compressed air to dry them and using special protective tape for sand blowing to protect the uncoated areas of parts, (c) using fine particle size, low pressure sand blowing process to roughen surface of spray area of the parts and removing sand blowing protective tape after sand blowing, (d) using high-pressure water to wash and cleaning sand blowing surface, (e) using high-pressure carbon dioxide airflow to perform secondary cleaning and drying treatment on sand blowing surface and other areas of parts and (f) transferring to spraying process.
   USE - The method is useful for improving bonding strength of thermal spray coatings on surface of light alloy parts.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved by 50-100%, higher reliability and service life; and is suitable for mass production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent in step (a) is ethanol or acetone. The mass fraction of carbon dioxide gas or dry ice is 50-80%. Preferred Method: The step (a), the ultrasonic cleaning and degreasing are performed with ultrasonic frequency of 30-40 kHz at a power density of 0.6-0.8 W/cm2 at cleaning temperature of 25-50 degrees C for 15-30 minutes. The fine particle size, low pressure sand blowing process in step (c) comprises using 50-150 mesh white corundum sand, using multi-axis manipulators or manually clamped press-in sand blowing guns to blow sand at blowing pressure of 0.1-0.4 MPa at blowing angle of 60-80 degrees with blowing distance of 140-170 mm. The step (e) comprises using high-pressure compressed air stream containing carbon dioxide gas or dry ice to quickly clean and drying surface of the part. The interface pollution of the blown sand surface after cleaning and drying is less than 2%.
DC M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).); V06 (Electromechanical Transducers and Small Machines)

PN CN111569913-A
TI Preparing rare earth ion gadolinium ion doped bismuth oxybromide photocatalytic material for sewage treatment, involves adding cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to ethylene glycol, adding bismuth nitrate pentahydrate to glycerol, centrifuging.
AU ZHANG F
   XIE Y
   WU J
   GUO Y
   ZHANG L
   ZENG G
   YU Z
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 202084880K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rare earth ion gadolinium ion (Gd3+) doped bismuth oxybromide (BiOBr) photocatalytic material involves adding cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to boiling ethylene glycol, stirring to obtain solution A, adding bismuth nitrate pentahydrate to glycerol, and adding hydrated nitric acid and stirring to obtain solution B. Solution B is added drop-wise into solution A, and ultrasonic treatment is performed to obtain a suspension. The suspension is transferred to the reaction kettle, and reacted at 140-180 degrees C for 8-16 hours. The reaction solution is centrifuged, the precipitate is separated, washed and dried to obtain the desired product.
   USE - Method for preparing rare earth ion Gd3+ doped BiOBr photocatalytic material for sewage treatment (all claimed) and air carbon dioxide removal.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic material prepared by the method has non-toxicity, easy synthesis, good stability, etc. The special electronic structure and good absorption performance of visible light are the basis of its use as a photocatalyst. The photocatalytic performance of the composite material was characterized by reducing carbon dioxide to methanol and calculating its conversion rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The time of the ultrasonic treatment is 25-30 minutes. The reaction is performed at 160 degrees C for 12 hours. The amount of nitric acid and BiOBr in the added raw materials is based on molar mass ratio of gadolinium nitrate and BiOBr of 0.02:1, 0.05:1 or 0.1:1. Preferred Components: The inner lining of the reactor is made of polytetrafluoroethylene material.

PN CN111019145-A; CN111019145-B
TI Preparation of organic covalent polymer material used in adsorption separation of carbon dioxide, involves preparing mixed solutions, adjusting pH, mixing solutions to obtain flocculant precipitate, filtering precipitate, and freeze-drying.
AU WANG X
   CHEN J
   ZHANG T
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 2020339429
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of organic covalent polymer material involves (A) mixing the aromatic amine aqueous solution with concentrated acid uniformly to obtain the mixed solution (M1), (B) mixing the mixed solution (M1) with the nitrite solution to convert the amino group into a diazonium salt to obtain a mixed solution (M2), (C) adjusting the pH of mixed solution (M2) to obtain a mixed solution (M3), (D) mixing phenol, carbonate and water evenly to obtain a mixed solution (M4), (E) mixing the mixed solution (M3) and mixed solution (M5) to obtain a flocculant precipitate, (F) filtering the flocculant precipitate, washing, and freeze-drying. The molar ratio of the mixed solution (M3) based on the amino group in the aromatic amine to the mixed solution (M4) based on the cholesterol in the phenol is 1:(0.8-1.5).
   USE - Preparation of organic covalent polymer material used in adsorption separation of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple manufacture of organic covalent polymer material and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for organic covalent polymer material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentrated acid is concentrated hydrochloric acid or concentrated sulfuric acid. Preferred Properties: The molar ratio of the nitrite solution in terms of nitrite to the mixed solution (M1) in terms of amino groups in aromatic amine is (1.02-1.1):1. The concentration of the nitrite solution is 0.1-0.15 mol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves adjusting the pH of the mixed solution (M2) by adding carbonate. Preferred Condition: The preparation method of organic covalent polymer material is carried out at a temperature of 0-30 degrees C. Preferred Properties: The molar ratio of the concentrated acid in terms of hydrogen ions to the aromatic amine in terms of amino groups is (2.5-4):1. The molar ratio of phenol to carbonate in terms of hydroxyl groups is 1:(1-1.05). The organic covalent polymer has a specific surface area of 100-2000 m2/g, pore size of 1-100 nm, and pore volume of 0.05 -1.34 cm3/g. Preferred Components: The aromatic amine is a polyaromatic amine, preferably at least one of tetra(4-aminophenyl) methane, tri(4-aminophenyl) amine , benzidine, 4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane, 2,4,6-tris(4-aminophenyl)-1,3,5-triazine and 1-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-4-(4-aminophenyl). The phenol is a polyphenol, preferably at least one of resorcinol, hydroquinone, tetrakis(4-hydroxyphenyl) methane, 3,5-tris (4-hydroxyphenyl)benzene and diphenol. Preferred Composition: The addition amount of water is 10-15 L per mole of hydroxyl groups in phenol.

PN CN110526834-A; CN110526834-B
TI Preparing high-purity perfluoroisobutyronitrile by passing non-condensable gas, moisture, acid gas, fluorine-containing olefin and fluorocarbon impurity to adsorption column, removing impurity, rectifying, monitoring purity and discharging.
AU DONG L
   QING F
   JIA X
   MA X
AE BEIJING YUJI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   QUANZHOU YUJI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (QUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2981J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-purity perfluoroisobutyronitrile, involves (i) passing perfluoroisobutyronitrile raw materials comprising non-condensable gas comprising nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, moisture, acid gas, fluorine-containing olefin and fluorocarbon compound impurities to single-stage adsorption column, removing moisture, acid gas, large portion of fluorine-containing olefin and large portion of non-condensable gas using adsorbent, and (ii) introducing raw material to stainless steel rectification tower after being adsorbed, carrying out low-temperature rectification, rectifying tower top to discharge small amount of fluorine-containing olefin, small amount of non-condensable gas and fluorocarbon front fraction impurity, monitoring the purity of the fraction using a gas chromatography-pulsed discharge helium ionization detection (GC-PDHID) gas chromatograph, and discharging product to finished gas storage tank when the purity is greater than 99.99%.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity perfluoroisobutyronitrile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables the preparation of high-purity perfluoroisobutyronitrile with a purity of more than 99.99%, removes impurities such as nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, halogenated alkanes, halogenated olefins and fluorocarbons, and is simple to operate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent is a molecular sieve comprising A-type molecular sieve, X-type molecular sieve, Y-type molecular sieve, mesoporous molecular sieve (MCM) series molecular sieve or zeolite socony mobil-5 (ZSM-5) molecular sieve. Preferred Method: The perfluoroisobutyronitrile raw material in step (i) is produced using process provided in CN108424375 A, where the purity of perfluoroisobutyronitrile is 80-99%. The product gas obtained in step (ii) is filled to a steel bottle using diaphragm compressor to certain filling coefficient and stored. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the adsorption is carried out at 0-50 degrees C under -0.1 to 0.3 MPa, and the flow rate of perfluoroisobutyronitrile raw material is maintained at 0.01-100 l/minute. In step (ii), the low-temperature rectification is carried out at -50 to 20 degrees C and at -0.1 to 0.3 MPa, and the front fraction discharge rate is maintained at 0.1-10 l/minute. Preferred Properties: The filling coefficient is 0.1-1.5. Preferred Device: The single-stage adsorption tower is a stainless steel adsorption tower.

TI Preparing cobalt/nitrogen-doped-carbon catalyst for producing cobalt/nitrogen-doped-carbon/air catalyst for synthesizing methanol, by stirring methanol solution of 2-methylimidazole and cobalt nitrate, centrifuging, washing and calcining.
AU LIU B
   LIAN Y
   FANG T
AE UNIV SOUTH CENT NATIONALITIES (USOC-C)
GA 2019841924
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing cobalt/nitrogen-doped-carbon catalyst involves separately dissolving cobalt nitrate and 2-methylimidazole in methanol, dripping 2-methylimidazole methanol solution to cobalt nitrate methanol solution, and stirring at room temperature for more than 3 hours to obtain purple solution, centrifuging, washing with methanol, and drying at room temperature to obtain zeolitic imidazolate framework-67 (ZIF-67), placing the ZIF-67 in a tubular furnace and calcining by heating under nitrogen atmosphere at 600-800 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cobalt/nitrogen-doped-carbon catalyst for producing cobalt/nitrogen-doped-carbon/air catalyst which is used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high carbon dioxide conversion rate and methanol selectivity under mild conditions and excellent stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing a cobalt/nitrogen-doped-carbon/air catalyst, which involves oxidizing the cobalt/nitrogen-doped-carbon catalyst in an air atmosphere at 200-300 degrees C for 2-6 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The heating is carried out under nitrogen atmosphere at a rate of 2.5-3.5 degrees C/minute at 600 degrees C or 800 degrees C for 6 hours. Preferred Properties: The cobalt/nitrogen-doped-carbon catalyst has a metal cobalt crystal grain size of 10-20 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of cobalt nitrate, 2-methylimidazole and methanol is (0.5-0.8) g:(2.3-2.5) g:(80-500) ml, preferably (0.6-0.7) g:2.44 g:(100-200) ml.

PN CN110229560-A
TI Preparing non-toxic dust-free shell chalk using waste shell, comprises e.g. crushing waste shell, calcining, introducing carbon dioxide gas, mixing obtained calcium carbonate powder with water-soluble binder and talc powder, and molding.
AU LIANG J
   HUANG H
   CHEN Y
   LI D
   LI Q
   XU B
AE UNIV LINGNAN NORMAL (ULNN-C)
GA 201980519J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing non-toxic dust-free shell chalk using waste shell, comprises (i) washing waste shell with water and drying at 110 degrees C, (ii) mechanically crushing the dried shell, crushing to 300 mesh, and calcining at 850 degrees C for 120 minutes to obtain calcium oxide powder, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the calcium oxide powder at certain rate, continuously calcining, and reacting to obtain high-purity calcium carbonate powder, (iv) heating diol and water in a ratio of 1:40 to obtain white solution, and stopping the heating to obtain water-soluble binder, and (v) mixing the calcium carbonate powder and the water-soluble binder in a ratio of 3:1, adding certain amount of talc powder, molding, and drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing non-toxic dust-free shell chalk using waste shell.
   ADVANTAGE - The shell chalk: reduces the chalk dust in the air; does not corrode the skin of the teacher's fingers; and ensures protection of the teachers and students health.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (v), the addition amount of talc powder is 10% of the total mass of calcium carbonate powder and water-soluble binder. Preferred Method: The step (iii) comprises introducing the carbon dioxide gas at constant rate of 5 ml/hours, and the calcination temperature is 1000 degrees C.

TI Preparing cinnamon essential oil comprises crushing cinnamon to obtain cinnamon powder, charging separately, adjusting pressure control valve, controlling pressure, adding oil-water separator, heating and stirring, filtering and separating.
AU TAN R
AE GUANGDONG YUJIN FOOD CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201953314K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cinnamon essential oil comprises (i) crushing cinnamon to obtain cinnamon powder; (ii) charging separately cinnamon powder into first extraction vessel and second extraction vessel in parallel in mass ratio of 0.9-1.2:1.2-0.9, and entering supercritical carbon dioxide from bottom of first extraction kettle and second extraction kettle, adjusting pressure control valve, controlling pressure of first extraction kettle and second extraction kettle and extracting respectively for 40-60 minutes, collecting supercritical carbon dioxide after combined extraction and separating and collecting crude cinnamon oil after analysis and separation; (iii) adding crude cinnamon oil into oil-water separator, setting separation parameter and separating and collecting oil phase, adding separated aqueous phase into reaction kettle, heating and stirring mixture, and allowing to stand and cooling, filtering and separating cinnamon oil and combining oil phase.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cinnamon essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves extraction efficiency of favorable flavor component e.g. trans-cinnamaldehyde and simultaneously reduces extraction of flavor component e.g. hexadecene or isoflavones.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, extraction conditions of first extraction kettle comprises extracting at pressure of 11-16 MPa for 20-30 minutes and extracting at pressure of 17-23 MPa for 20-30 minutes at 36-42 degrees C with flow rate of carbon dioxide of 300-450 l/hour. The extraction conditions of second extraction kettle comprises extracting at pressure of 17-23 MPa for 20-30 minutes and extracting at pressure of 11-16 MPa for 20-30 minutes at 42-48 degrees C with flow rate of carbon dioxide of 300 to 450 l/hour. The analyzing separation pressure is 6-12 MPa and temperature is 32-50 degrees C and analyzing separation comprises first analyzing separation with pressure of 9-12 MPa and temperature of 45-48 degrees C and second analytical separation with pressure of 6-9 MPa and temperature of 32-45 degrees C. The separation parameters comprises temperature of crude cinnamon oil of 30-60 degrees C and height of coarse-grained bed of 28-32 cm. The heating, stirring, allowing to stand and cooling comprises maintaining at 62-70 degrees C, stirring at 40-55 rotations/minute for 40-60 minutes, allowing to stand for 30-150 minutes, cooling to 20-45 degrees C, releasing lower layer impurities from lower end of reactor to collect upper layer and filtering. Preferred Components: The cinnamon powder has particle size of 20-50 meshes.

TI Preparing supercapacitor activated carbon by three-step physical activation comprises e.g. adding metal oxide catalyst into carbonized material, carrying out pre-activation process, and carrying out pore-forming reaction.
AU DU P
   LI G
   ZHANG Y
   WANG Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   BEIHAI SENCE CARBON MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (BEIH-Non-standard)
GA 2019350131
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supercapacitor activated carbon by three-step physical activation comprises (i) adding metal oxide catalyst into the carbonized material, carrying out pre-activation process in a high temperature furnace at 600-800 degrees C for 1-4 hours to obtain pre-activated sample, (ii) continuing reacting the pre-activated sample under a mixed atmosphere of water vapor and carbon dioxide, and controlling the presence of the right amount of oxygen, and carrying out pore-forming reaction at a high temperature of 850-950 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain preliminary pore-formed sample, and (iii) reacting the preliminary pore-formed sample at a temperature of 600-800 degrees C for 3-5 hours under carbon dioxide atmosphere, and performing adjustment and modification of the aperture to obtain activated carbon for supercapacitor electrode material having a large number of micropores and a reasonable pore size distribution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing supercapacitor activated carbon by three-step physical activation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, low cost and high yield; and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The prepared activated carbon has a pore volume of up to 0.8-1 cm/g, the volume of pores is 50-60%, the main pore size distribution peaks in the range of 1-2 nm, the pore size distribution ranges is 0-2 nm, and the proportion of micropores is 55-65%, and the specific surface area is 1600-1900 m2/g. The carbonized material in the first step is a coconut shell carbonized material. The oxide catalyst used in the pre-activation process of step (i) is oxide of metal ions in zinc, niobium, calcium, silicon, tantalum, vanadium, iron and/or copper, and the weight ratio to the carbonized material is 1-3 wt.%. In the second step of the hole making process, the volume ratio of carbon dioxide to water vapor is 2:8-4:6, and the molar ratio of oxygen to the total gas is 0.02-0.035 m3/minutesx kg. In step (iii), the total amount of carbon dioxide gas introduced during the pore diameter adjustment and modification process is 0.02-0.03 m3/minutesx kg. Preferred Method: The method is a continuous process used in industrial continuous production processes by controlling the temperature, time and atmosphere in sections in a high temperature furnace; or the method is divided into three separate processes in steps, each of which can be performed independently; and the three processes in the three-step process can be repeated as needed.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The refrigerant                  comprises 35 wt.% carbon dioxide, 32.5 wt.%                  pentafluoroethane, 32.5 wt.% difluoromethane, and                  67-62 wt.%, preferably 65 wt.% refrigerant R410A                  or R410B. The refrigerant has a                  relative carbon doixde equivalent, based on 20                  years, of less than 2500, preferably less than                  500. The refrigerant is not flammable.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The cooling circuit                  has an internal heat exchanger, where the                  internal heat exchanger is connected to a high                  pressure side of the cooling circuit in a flow                  direction before the expansion element and                  subsequently the condenser, and on a low pressure                  side of the cooling circuit in a flow direction                  upstream of the compressor and subsequently the                  heat exchanger. The heat exchanger is dimensioned                  such that the refrigerant can evaporate only                  partially in the heat exchanger. The condenser is                  designed as a cascade heat exchanger of a further                  cooling circuit of the cooling device. The                  temperature control device has a heating device                  with a heater and a heating heat exchanger in the                  test chamber. In the cooling circuit, a first                  bypass is formed with at least one controllable                  second expansion element, where the first bypass                  is connected in a flow direction in front of the                  internal heat exchanger and then the condenser to                  the cooling circuit, and the first bypass is                  designed as a controllable internal supplementary                  cooling. The refrigeration cycle is connected to                  a high pressure side of the refrigeration cycle                  in a flow direction before the expansion device                  and subsequently the condenser by an internal                  heat exchanger, and to a low pressure side of the                  refrigeration cycle in a flow direction upstream                  of the compressor and subsequently the heat                  exchanger coos the high pressure side                  refrigerant, where the Cooling of the refrigerant                  on the high pressure side is used by the internal                  heat exchanger for lowering an evaporation                  temperature at the expansion element. The                  high-pressure side refrigerant is cooled by the                  low-pressure-side refrigerant at a constant                  suction pressure on the low-pressure side by the                  internal heat exchanger.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109431995-A
TI Preparing injection comprises e.g. introducing carbon dioxide into the water for injection, adding to solvent, adding disodium edetate and vitamin C, and mixing, adding sodium hydrogencarbonate and sodium hydrogen sulfite.
AU ZOU M
   HU X
   XU M
AE JIANGXI RUNZE PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201925408Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing injection comprises (i) introducing 1.2-1.449 g/l carbon dioxide into the water for injection to prepare solvent, (ii) adding to the solvent prepared in the step (i), adding disodium edetate and vitamin C, and mixing to obtain first solution, (iii) adding sodium hydrogencarbonate and sodium hydrogen sulfite to the first solution, and adjusting the pH value to 5.9-6.2 to obtain second solution, (iv) adding activated carbon to the second solution and decarburizing to obtain third solution, (v) sequentially subjecting to coarse filtration and fine filtration to prepare a vitamin C injection, where the pore diameter of the filter membrane during the coarse filtration process is 0.4-0.6 mu m, and the pore diameter of the filtration membrane during the fine filtration process is 0.1-0.3. Mm.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing injection containing vitamin C.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the degree of oxidation of vitamin C injection, improves quality and preservation time, and avoids yellowing of liquid medicine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for injection prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The injection comprises 0.03-0.08 pts. wt. disodium edetate, 3-8 pts. wt. vitamin C, and 2-5 pts. wt. sodium hydrogencarbonate, and 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. sodium hydrogen sulfite. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the dissolving solution of disodium edetate is added to the solvent, and vitamin C is added thereto to prepare a first solution. In the step (iv), the mixing process is agitation mixing, and the stirring is carried out at 100-300 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 15-20 minutes. In the step (iv), the decarburization process is carried out under conditions of a temperature of 50-60 degrees C. In the step (iv), decarburization is performed by suction filtration to remove activated carbon. In the step (v) also includes sterilizing the finely filtered vitamin C injection. The sterilization for 15-20 minutes at a temperature of 100-110 degrees C.

PN CN109423357-A
TI Extracting Pogostemon cablin volatile oil comprises e.g. cutting Pogostemon cablin stem into small section, crushing, soaking in water, adding materials to ultra-high pressure extractor, sealing at room temperature, and raising pressure.
AU SU L
AE NANJING ZELANG PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NJZL-C)
GA 201923782T
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Pogostemon cablin volatile oil comprises (1) cutting the Pogostemon cablin stem into 1-2 cm small section, adding into the crusher, crushing, passing through 40-80 mesh sieves, soaking in water according to material to liquid ratio of 1:7-9, soaking for 24 hours; (2) adding the soaked material to ultra-high pressure extractor, sealing at room temperature, raising the pressure to 200-400 MPa, and instantaneously releasing the pressure; (3) placing the treated material in extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extractor, setting extraction pressure to 20-30 MPa, extraction temperature to 35-45 degrees C, where extracting agent carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-25 kg/hours, carrying extraction for 2-3 hours, obtaining fragrant Pogostemon cablin volatile oil from separation kettle; and (4) purifying the obtained Pogostemon cablin volatile oil with diethyl ether, and removing residual water to obtain Pogostemon cablin volatile oil.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting Pogostemon cablin volatile oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and environmentally-friendly; and can fully retains color and fragrance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the separation tank has separation temperature of 25-30 degrees C and pressure of 10-20 MPa. In the step (3), carrying the purification by extracting diethyl ether and water for 3 times according to volume ratio of 3:1, concentrating the diethyl ether under reduced pressure, and recycling.

PT P
PN CN109400673-A; CN109400673-B
TI Extracting and purifying glutathione by metal ion precipitation-cyclodextrin inclusion comprises e.g. crushing sea rice embryo, freeze-drying and removing sea rice embryo lipid substances by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction.
AU LV Y
   LUO H
   LIANG L
AE UNIV GUANGDONG MEDICAL (UYGU-Non-standard)
GA 2019226786
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and purifying glutathione by metal ion precipitation-cyclodextrin inclusion comprises (i) crushing sea rice embryo, freeze-drying at low temperature until moisture content is 5-8%, removing sea rice embryo lipid substances by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, (ii) defatting sea rice embryo, extracting by hot water, discarding supernatant and concentrating to obtain glutathione crude product, (iii) dissolving crude glutathione product in hot water, adding acetic acid, adjusting pH to 4-5, adding zinc acetate, glutathione zinc salt precipitate, maintaining at 30 degrees C, and reacting for 4 hours, (iv) high speed centrifuging, discarding supernatant, taking precipitate, adding 0.5% EDTA solution, stirring and dissolving at room temperature for 2 hours and (v) adding 10% beta -cyclodextrin aqueous solution, heating at 45 degrees C, stirring to precipitate, centrifuging, discarding precipitate, and concentrating supernatant under reduced pressure to obtain purified glutathione.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and purifying glutathione by metal ion precipitation-cyclodextrin inclusion.
   ADVANTAGE - The purifying glutathione prevents organic pollution, and prevents interference of sea rice embryo fat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) crushing sea rice embryo dinto coarse powder with size of 2-3 mm, where the extraction pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction method is 30-35 MPa, and temperature at 35-40 degrees C, adding carbon dioxide 5% ethanol as cosolvent and extracting lipid completely. In the step (ii) stirring and reacting at 100-150 revolutions/minute and forming glutathione zinc salt precipitate in muddy state. In the step (iv) centrifuging at 12000 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes, discarding supernatant, removing precipitate, placing into 0.5% EDTA solution, where the ratio of precipitation solution is 1:20. In the step (v) purifying glutathione is white crystalline solid with purity of glutathione is 96-98%.

PN CN109368610-A; CN109368610-B
TI Preparation of iron phosphorus and ferric phosphate involves suspending pyrite cinder with water, passing carbon dioxide, adding iron powder and polyethylene glycol, reacting to obtain solution, adding mixed solution, and reacting.
AU YAN Z
   LIU Z
   WAN W
   WANG Z
   WANG J
   WANG Y
   LI W
   ZHANG Z
   XIE F
AE HUNAN YACHENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201920430P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of iron phosphorus and ferric phosphate involves suspending pyrite cinder with water, passing carbon dioxide into the solution so that amount of precipitation does not increase, filtering, washing and removing the precipitate to obtain slag, reusing the alkali solution to react with the slag to remove silica to obtain an alkali leaching residue, adding an excess acid solution to the alkali leaching residue to obtain an acid immersion liquid, adding iron powder to acid immersion liquid to convert all iron ions in the solution into ferrous ions, adding a non-ionic flocculant to remove the precipitate to obtain a clarified ferrous salt solution, adding polyethylene glycol to the ferrous salt solution to obtain a reaction bottom solution, adding phosphoric acid to adjust pH of the solution to 2 or less, adding a mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide and phosphate to the reaction bottom solution, and reacting to 88-92 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation of iron phosphorus and ferric phosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cleverly designed and simple to operate, and produces high ratio of ferric phosphate and iron phosphorus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali solution is a sodium hydroxide solution or a potassium hydroxide solution. The acid solution is a sulfuric acid solution. Preferred Process: The method further involves adding barium fluoride before adding the acid solution, and leaving still after addition of hydrogen peroxide and phosphate to the reaction bottom solution until the reaction material is completely turned white, heat-preserving to obtain slurry, washing slurry until the conductivity of the slurry is below 150 mu S/cm and calcining in a furnace. The gradual addition of hydrogen peroxide and phosphate mixed solution to the reaction bottom liquid is carried out for 40-300 minutes using peristaltic pump. Preferred Composition: The added ratio of alkali solution to slag is 1:4-5. The addition ratio of the mass of the barium fluoride to the quality of the alkali leaching slag is 1:30-50.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The non-ionic flocculant comprises non-ionic polyacrylamide and/or polyethylene oxide. The polyethylene glycol has an average molecular weight of 200-20000, preferably 400-2000. The polyethylene glycol comprises polyethylene glycol-200, polyethylene glycol-400, polyethylene glycol-600, polyethylene glycol-2000, polyethylene glycol-6000 and/or polyethylene glycol-20000, preferably polyethylene glycol-600.

PN CN109354038-A
TI Salt-base combined preparation method based on ammonia-based process, involves injecting mother liquid and extracting and subjecting calcium chloride type ore to vacuum salt production and precipitating sodium chloride.
AU PENG M
   JIANG X
   RUAN J
   LI Z
AE HUBEI YUNYING MINING TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 201920677H
AB    NOVELTY - A salt-base combined preparation method based on an ammonia-based process, involves (1) adding a portion of the mirabilite type brine to the saturated mirabilite type brine, removing the nitrate and refining to obtain a refined salt brine, (2) introducing ammonia gas into the refined brine to saturation, and introducing carbon dioxide, (3) filtering out the crystals precipitated in the step (2) and calcining and obtaining slag soda ash and furnace gas carbon dioxide, (4) adding calcium carbide slag to the mother liquid in step (2) and steaming ammonia, (5) injecting the mother liquid in the step (4) into the salt well to change the sodium sulfate type ore to the calcium chloride type ore and (6) extracting and subjecting the calcium chloride type ore to vacuum salt production and precipitating sodium chloride crystals.
   USE - Salt-base combined preparation method based on ammonia-based process.
   ADVANTAGE - The waste slag waste liquid can be comprehensively utilized, which greatly reduces environmental pollution, greatly saves production costs, and brings huge economical benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The furnace gas obtained in the step (3) is recovered and used in the step (2) after being cooled and washed. The mother liquor obtained in the step (4) is recycled in the step (1). The ammonia gas recovered in step (4) is utilized in step (2). The precipitated solution in step (6) is used for preparing solid calcium chloride, which involves adding hydrochloric acid to the solution to adjust the pH, filtering to remove calcium sulfate, sequentially subjecting the solution to dust removal, preheating, cooling and hardening and slicing the concentrated high-concentration solution and drying and dehydrating the flake product and then cooling.

PT P
PN CN109289770-A; CN109289770-B
TI Preparation of yttrium modified methyl mercaptan adsorption material involves crushing amino resin glue, dissolving in ethanol solution, adding yttrium nitrate hexahydrate, solidifying, carbonizing and activating using carbon dioxide gas.
AU SONG W
   JIN Y
   SUN M
   ZHANG J
   WEI Z
AE SHANXI XINHUA CHEM CO LTD (CHNI-C)
   SHANXI XINHUA DEFENSE EQUIP RES INST CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201914778W
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of yttrium modified methyl mercaptan adsorption material involves crushing 10-30 g amino resin glue into 5-10 mm particles, and dissolving in 100-200 ml ethanol solution, adding yttrium nitrate hexahydrate to the above solution, and stirring at 60-80 degrees C for 6-10 hours, filtering above solution and transferring to an evaporating dish, leaving still for 24 hours to volatilize portion of the solvent, solidifying the concentrated liquid at 150-180 degrees C for 3-6 hours to obtain yttrium-containing resin, carbonizing resin in a high temperature tubular furnace at 600-800 degrees C for 2-4 hours, heating at 850-950 degrees C, activating for 3-5 hours by 50-100 ml/minute carbon dioxide, and naturally cooling to room temperature.
   USE - Preparation of yttrium modified methyl mercaptan adsorption material.

PN CN108928984-A; CN108928984-B
TI Method for reducing emission of reverse osmosis brine in iron and steel industry, involves decomposing organic matter in waste water into carbon dioxide and water, cooling, concentrating, filtering, evaporating and separating.
AU HU S
   CHEN P
   LONG H
   WANG F
   LIU F
   GAO J
   WANG Y
   XU W
AE ANGANG STEEL CO LTD (ANSH-C)
   ANGANG STEEL CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 201899502E
AB    NOVELTY - The method for reducing emission of reverse osmosis brine involves (1) passing concentrated brine into hood furnace, decomposing organic matter in waste water into carbon dioxide and water, cooling, continuously decreasing amount of water and gradually increasing the concentration of pollutants, where the concentration ratio is 12-14, and the outlet water temperature is controlled at 93-97 degrees C, (2) passing the high-concentration brine into coke powder adsorption filtration process, removing the impurities generated during spraying process, adsorbing organic matter in wastewater, filtering and heat-preserving at 90-94 degrees C 15-25 minutes, (3) passing wastewater into evaporation system, evaporating at 200-240 degrees C, passing the generated condensate to inlet of hood furnace for recycling, (4) subjecting the product after evaporation to solid-liquid separation, evaporating and transferring the supernatant for recycling.
   USE - Method for reducing emission of reverse osmosis brine in iron and steel industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing emission of reverse osmosis brine by simple, energy-saving and environmentally-friendly process.
