PN US2018306495-A1; US10543456-B2
TI Method for forming solid product, involves forming solid product or products from product component or components in exchangers by desublimation, and separating solid product or products from process fluid such that solid product is formed.
AU BAXTER L
   HOEGER C
   BURT S
   STITT K
   MANSFIELD E
   SAYRE A
   FRANKMAN D
   BAXTER A
   DAVIS N
AE BAXTER L (BAXT-Individual)
   HOEGER C (HOEG-Individual)
   BURT S (BURT-Individual)
   STITT K (STIT-Individual)
   MANSFIELD E (MANS-Individual)
   SAYRE A (SAYR-Individual)
   FRANKMAN D (FRAN-Individual)
   BAXTER A (BAXT-Individual)
   DAVIS N (DAVI-Individual)
   HALL LABS LLC (HALL-Non-standard)
GA 201883204Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing desublimating exchangers, where the exchangers comprise a direct-contact exchanger (DCE) (202, 206, 208, 210) comprising contact fluid (234, 238, 240). Process fluid is passed through the exchangers, where the process fluid comprises a product component or components. Solid product or products are formed from the product component or components in the exchangers by desublimation. The solid product or products are separated from the process fluid such that the solid product or products are formed, where one of the exchangers comprises an indirect-contact heat exchanger comprising a first chamber through which the process fluid passes and a second chamber through which coolant (236) passes. The contact fluid comprises solvent or mixture of solvent and compound. The solvent comprises water, hydrocarbons, liquid ammonia, liquid carbon dioxide, and cryogenic liquids.
   USE - Method for forming a solid product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separating components e.g. carbon dioxide, from carrier gas i.e. flue gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a structure for forming solid products.
   DCEs (202, 206, 208, 210)
   Separation system (212)
   Combustion flue gas (222)
   Contact fluid (234, 238, 240)
   Coolant (236)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The compound comprises ionic compounds comprising potassium carbonate, potassium formate, potassium acetate, calcium magnesium acetate, magnesium chloride, sodium chloride, lithium chloride, and calcium chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The compound comprises organic compounds comprising glycerol, ammonia, propylene glycol, ethylene glycol, ethanol, methanol, and hydrocarbons.

PN WO2018181103-A1; JP2018161109-A
TI Production of oxidized hop composition used in hop-derived food or drink, preferably acidic drinks for suppressing fat accumulation, involves contacting oxidized hop with supercritical carbon dioxide containing fluid to reduce fatty acid.
AU YOSHIMASA T
   YASUKO M
   MATSUKURA Y
   TANIGUCHI S
AE KIRIN KK (KIRI-C)
   KIRIN CO LTD (KIRI-C)
GA 201877534P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing an oxidized hop composition having a reduced fatty acid, involves contacting an oxidized hop with a supercritical carbon dioxide containing fluid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing an oxidized hop composition which is useful in hop-derived food or drink, preferably acidic drinks (all claimed). The oxidized hop composition is useful for suppressing fat accumulation or a body weight gain, producing fat (subcutaneous fat) accumulation inhibitor or a weight gain inhibitor for preventing or treating obesity, and maintaining a health enhancement function.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces oxidized hop composition by stably reducing fatty acid content while stably maintaining MHBA content, hence the oxidized hop composition has suppressed unpleasant odor (fatty acid odor), and imparts comfortable drinking and eating sensation to food or drink. The method is simple. The composition has reduced bitterness and low toxicity, and is highly safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) oxidized hop composition produced by the above method; and
   (2) hop-derived food or drink comprising the above composition or its extract as raw material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Product: The oxidized hop composition comprises 0.05 %mass or less fatty acid, 5 %mass or more matured hop bitter acids (MHBA), and 5 %mass or less alpha acid and iso alpha acid, where MHBA content is equal to or greater than the total content of the alpha acid and the iso alpha acid. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of fatty acid to MHBA in the oxidized hop composition is 0.4 weight/weight% or less. The supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) containing fluid comprises supercritical CO2 or a mixed fluid of supercritical CO2 and alcohol.
   ACTIVITY - Anorectic; Antilipemic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The oxidized hop composition is administered orally at a dose of 10-600 mg, preferably 20-200 mg or parenterally at a dose of 1-100 mg, preferably 3-30 mg.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. AB    NOVELTY - Air-adjusting package comprises a packaging container and a pigeon egg sealed in the packaging container. The packaging container is filled with a mixed gas for preserving, where the mixed gas comprises 2-10% carbon dioxide, 3-10% oxygen, and 80-95% nitrogen, based on the volume fraction of each gas in the total amount of mixed gas in the packaging container.
   USE - Air-adjusting package for preserving freshness of pigeon eggs.
   ADVANTAGE - The air-adjusting package does not allow the surface of the pigeon eggs to produce water droplets, the pigeon eggs does not deteriorate due to changes in the outside temperature in a short time, and greatly prolongs the shelf life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preserving freshness of pigeon eggs in air-adjusting package, which involves putting the pigeon eggs into the packaging container, using the mixture of carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen to form a gas mixture for the packaging of the container, and placing the air-adjusting package at 3-5 degrees C and storing under refrigeration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The volume of the mixed gas is in liters, the mass of the pigeon eggs is kilograms, and the mass ratio of the mixed gas to the pigeon eggs is 2-3:1. The air-adjusting package is that the packaging container is vacuum packed, inflated and sealed by the packaging machine to be inflated and packaged. The preservative is konjac flour. The packaging is performed at a temperature of 15-20 degrees C. The packaging is performed under vacuum conditions. The inner wall of the packaging container is coated with a composite nano-coating film. Preferred Process: The method for preserving freshness further involves the step of applying a preservative before the pigeon eggs are placed in the packaging container. The composite nano-coating film is formed by reacting and compounding polyvinyl alcohol, glutaraldehyde, acid red, nano-zinc oxide and nano-silica in an aqueous solution.

AB    NOVELTY - A methanation reaction catalyst comprises a stabilized zirconia carrier having a tetragonal system and/or a cubic system crystal structure in which a stabilizing element is solid-solved, and nickel supported on the stabilized zirconia carrier. The stabilizing element is transition element(s) chosen from manganese, iron and cobalt. The catalyst is used for methanating carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide by reacting with hydrogen.
   USE - Methanation reaction catalyst is used in production of methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methanation reaction catalyst is capable of producing methane with high yield per unit mass by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) production of methane, which involves contacting the methanation reaction catalyst with a mixed gas containing carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas at 200 degrees C or more; and
   (2) manufacture of methanation reaction catalyst, which involves mixing zirconium and/or its salts, a salt of stabilizing element, and a salt of nickel to form a mixture and heating the mixture at 550-800 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The stabilized zirconia carrier comprises 6.5-66.5 atomic% zirconium, 0.50-24.5 atomic% stabilizing element and 30.0-90.0 atomic% nickel. Nickel is supported on the stabilized zirconia carrier and is solid-solved in the stabilized zirconia carrier. The stabilizing element is preferably trivalent manganese ion. The atomic ratio of stabilizing element to total content of zirconium and stabilizing element is 0.05-0.35.

TI Corrosion resistant high-strength magnesium alloy used for preparing vascular stent, comprises neodymium, zinc, calcium, zirconium, and magnesium.
AU MA G
   MA X
AE BEIJING AMSINOMED MEDICAL DEVICE CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201803778M
AB    NOVELTY - A corrosion resistant high-strength magnesium alloy comprises 1-4.5 wt.% neodymium, 0.2-2 wt.% zinc, 0-1 wt.% calcium, 0-1 wt.% zirconium, and magnesium.
   USE - A corrosion resistant high-strength magnesium alloy used for preparing a vascular stent.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-strength magnesium alloy is non-toxic, and has good corrosion resistance and high toughness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing the corrosion resistant high-strength magnesium alloy, comprising:
   (A) melting neodymium, zinc, calcium, zirconium, and magnesium in a vacuum melting furnace at 740-760 degrees C, passing in a vacuum gas inert gas environment, and stirring for 30-45 minutes;
   (B) exposing the melt in a sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide gas mixture to be protected, heating the melt to 760-780 degrees C, insulating for 30-40 minutes, and cooling to 700-720 degrees C, and insulating for 90-120 minutes; and
   (C) carrying out semi-continuous casting using sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide mixed gas for protection, controlling temperature of the melt in the vacuum melting furnace at 700-720 degrees C, and cooling to 680-690 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The magnesium alloy comprises 1.5-3.5 wt.%, preferably 1.5-2.5 wt.% neodymium, 0.1-1 wt.%, preferably 0.4-0.8 wt.% zinc, 0.1-1 wt.%, preferably 0.4-0.6 wt.% calcium, 0.2-1 wt.%, preferably 0.4-0.8 wt.% zirconium, and magnesium.

               5 mm, b &#8804; 5 mm, and c &#8804; 5 mm,                the time for the thermal insulation and pressure                maintaining is 5-85 minutes, where a, b, c                represent the maximum size of the polybutene resin                particles in the X-axis direction, the Y-axis                direction and the Z-axis direction in the                coordinate system, where the pressure release rate                is 0.1-1 MPa/minute, when the method of the                annealing treatment is normal temperature and                normal pressure annealing treatment including                melting polybutene resin particles and cooling at                normal temperature and normal pressure, and (2) in                presence of supercritical carbon monoxide,                subjecting prefabricated product to thermal                insulation and pressure retention, and pressure                relief is obtained, where, the pressure release                rate is 5-300 MPa/s.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  polybutene resin is isotactic polybutene,                  syndiotactic polybutene and/or polybutene alloy.                  The polybutene alloy is 1-butene/propylene                  copolymer, and the molecular weight distribution                  of the 1-butene/propylene copolymer is preferably                  Mw/Mn = 1-10. The molar content of 1-butene in                  the 1-butene/propylene copolymer is 70-99%, and                  the molar content of propylene is 1-30%. The                  isotacticity of the polybutene resin is 50-99%,                  preferably 95%. The crystallinity of the                  polybutene resin is 20-60%, preferably 45%. The                  melt index of the polybutene resin is 0.1-5 g/10                  minute, preferably 0.5 g/10 minute. The                  polybutene-based resin particles have a single                  crystal form or two crystal forms. The                  polybutene-based resin particles are in the form                  of particles or sheets. In the step (1), when the                  annealing treatment method is supercritical                  carbon dioxide annealing treatment, the size of                  the polybutene-based resin particles is a                  &#8804; 50 mm, preferably a = 5-30 mm, b                  &#8804; 50 mm, preferably 5-30 mm, c                  &#8804; 30 mm, preferably 2-30 mm. The                  polybutene-based resin particles are thermally                  insulating and pressure holding time of the resin                  particles is 5-150 minutes, 20-140 minutes,                  preferably the size of the polybutene resin                  particles is a = 10-20 mm, b = 10-20 mm, c = 5-20                  mm, and the thermal insulation and pressure                  holding time of the polybutene resin particles is                  40-120 minutes. The size of the polybutene resin                  particles is a = 1-4 mm, preferably 2 mm, b = 1-4                  mm, preferably 2 mm, c = 1-4 mm, preferably 2 mm,                  and the thermal insulation and pressure holding                  time of the polybutene resin particles is 10-80                  minutes, preferably 20-60 minutes, e.g. 30                  minutes, 40 minutes or 50 minutes. In the step                  (1), the temperature of the thermal insulation                  and pressure keeping is 90-120&#8451;, e.g.                  100&#8451;, 105&#8451;, 110&#8451; or                  115&#8451;. The pressure of the thermal                  insulation and pressure maintaining is 6-18 MPa,                  e.g. 10 MPa or 15 MPa. The pressure release rate                  is 0.2-0.8 MPa/minute, preferably 0.3 MPa/minute.                  In the step (1), when the annealing treatment                  method is normal temperature and normal pressure                  annealing treatment, the cooling time is 1-168                  hours, preferably 2-72 hours, more preferably 24                  hours. In the step (2), the pressure of the                  thermal insulation and pressure maintaining is                  5-20 MPa, preferably 7-18 MPa, more preferably 10                  MPa. The time of the thermal insulation and                  pressure maintaining is 10-60 minutes, preferably                  20-50 minutes, more preferably 40 minutes. The                  temperature of the thermal insulation and                  pressure maintaining is 90-115&#8451;,                  preferably 100-110&#8451;, more preferably                  105&#8451;. The pressure release rate is                  10-200 MPa/second, preferably 100 MPa/second. The                  foaming ratio of the foaming is 6-32, preferably                  10-28, e.g. 12.1, 15.3, 20.8, 27.6 or 27.7. The                  polybutene foamed material has a bimodal cell                  structure, where the average diameter of large                  cells is 12-200 &#181;m, and the average                  diameter of small cells is 1.5-40 &#181;m,                  and relative density is 0.038-0.18 g/cm3.

The                lower layer is used to contain dihydrogen sulfide                absorption liquid. The bottom of mixed gas                separator II located in lower layer is equipped                with stirring device. The inlet nozzle of mixed gas                separator II is connected to gas preheater through                a pipe equipped with booster pump, while the gas                outlet is connected to compressor. The dihydrogen                sulfide concentration detector is used to detect                whether the concentration of dihydrogen sulfide in                the exhaust gas at the outlet meets the standard.                The dihydrogen sulfide concentration detector                comprises dihydrogen sulfide gas return pipe having                one end connected to gas outlet of mixed gas                separator II while the other end is connected to                mixed gas separator II through separation membrane                II. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a removal method                of sour gas from natural gas using the                device.

PN CN207142838-U; CN108569698-A
TI Carbon dioxide purifying device has refrigerant pipe that is provided between refrigerant inlet pipe of refrigeration unit and refrigerant return pipe, and passed through liquefaction plate.
AU SHEN J
   ZHANG J
   JIANG H
   CHEN X
AE HANGZHOU XINSHIJI MIXED GAS CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2018254958
AB    (CN207142838-U)   NOVELTY - The utility belongs to gas purifying technology field. The utility model claims a carbon dioxide purifying device, comprising a liquid storage tank, a vaporizer, an adsorption column group, liquefying the plate exchanger, a rectifying tower, a condenser, a reboiler and a freezer, liquid storage tank, a vaporizer, adsorption column group and rectifying tower are connected by air pipe, a condenser located in a rectification tower, reboiler is connected with the rectifying tower and the rectifying tower; the liquefied plate between the adsorption column group and the rectifying tower; the refrigerating unit through a refrigerant inlet pipe and a refrigerant return pipe is connected with the condenser. This utility adopts the adsorbent/catalyst has higher adsorption/catalysis efficiency, which can meet the requirement of production efficiency; the utility in the coolant can be sufficiently used, air can also be used for cooling the raw material gas when the need to realize the full use of the resources and can reduce energy consumption; treated by the purifying device can obtain 99.999% of even 99.9995% purity of the high-purity carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon dioxide purification device.
EA    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide purification device comprises liquid storage tank, vaporizer, adsorption column group, liquefaction plate exchanger, distillation column, condenser, reboiler and refrigeration unit. The liquid storage tank, vaporizer, adsorption column group and rectification column are sequentially connected by a gas pipeline. The condenser is set above rectification tower and evacuation tube of rectification tower passes through condenser. The reboiler is connected to rectification column and is set below rectification column and heater is set in the reboiler. The liquefaction plate is exchanged between adsorption column group and rectification column and gas pipe is exchanged through liquefaction plate. The refrigeration unit is connected to condenser through refrigerant inlet pipe and refrigerant return pipe. The refrigerant pipe is set between refrigerant inlet pipe and refrigerant return pipe of refrigeration unit and refrigerant pipe is exchanged through the liquefaction plate.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide purification device.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide purification device utilizes resources, reduces energy consumption and high purity carbon dioxide of 99.999% or even 99.9995% purity is obtained by treatment with purification device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The copper silver molecular sieve adsorbent comprises 100 pts. wt. zeolite molecular sieve, 13-18 pts. wt. silver nitrate solution (10 wt.%), 10-15 pts. wt. copper nitrate solution (10 wt.%), 1-3 pts. wt. citric acid and 10-30 pts. wt. water. Preferred Method: The preparation method of copper silver molecular sieve adsorbent comprises mixing silver nitrate solution and copper nitrate solution to prepare mixed solution, heating mixed solution to 80-100 degrees C, dissolving citric acid in water to prepare citric acid solution and slowly adding citric acid solution to the mixed solution, stirring mixture at a speed of 60-120 rotations/minute, adding zeolite molecular sieve and stirring for 3-5 hours to obtain wet gel, drying wet gel and crushing at 120-150 degrees C to obtain dry gel powder, sintering at 550-600 degrees C for 2-6 hours and cooling. The method comprises calcining zeolite molecular sieve in a high temperature oxygen atmosphere of 600-700 degrees C for 30-40 minutes before use, cooling to room temperature, pouring into 0.1-0.5 mol/l sodium nitrate solution for 20-30 minutes, rinsing with water for 3-4 times and drying for further use.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Composition: The device also comprises compensating plate for changing between liquefied plate and adsorption column group through which gas conduit passes and evacuation tube of the rectification column also passes through. The condenser is further provided with temperature control galvanic coupler and refrigeration machine control box is arranged on refrigeration unit and temperature control galvanizer of the freezer and control box of the freezer are electrically connected. The adsorption column group has four adsorption columns comprising desulfurization and denitrification column, drying column, dehydrocarbon column and a dealcoholization column respectively. The four adsorption columns are respectively provided with independent heating devices. The device further comprises electric control box, heating device, reboiler and refrigerating unit out of adsorption column connected electrically. The desulfurization and denitrification column is provided with an adsorbent composed of alumina, activated carbon and sodium hydroxide. The drying column is provided with desiccant comprises activated carbon and silica gel. The dehydrocarbonation column is provided with copper silver molecular sieve adsorbent and dealcoholized formaldehyde-stripping column is provided with adsorbent composed of zeolite molecular sieve and vanadium pentoxide.

AE NANJING YANCHANG REACTION TECHNOLOGY RES (NANJ-Non-standard)
   NANJING YANCHANG REACTION TECHNOLOGY RES (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B81952
AB    NOVELTY - Enhanced micro-interface preparation system of                cyclic carbonate comprises stirred tank, first                carboxylation reaction tank, and second                carboxylation reaction tank, where e.g. stirring                tank, first carboxylation reaction tank and second                carboxylation reaction tank are all filled with                ionic liquid catalyst; side of the stirring tank is                provided with a micro-interface unit,                micro-interface unit is composed of number of                external micro-interface generators, and the main                pipeline leading into micro-interface unit is also                connected with oxidant delivery pipeline, and two                branch pipelines of olefin transportation pipeline,                carbon dioxide is introduced into micro-interface                unit from gas source transportation pipeline, and                carbon dioxide, oxidant, and olefin enter                micro-interface unit for breaking carbon dioxide                gas into micron-level micro bubble; and bottom of                first carboxylation reaction kettle is provided                with a first reaction feed liquid inlet.
   USE - Used as enhanced micro-interface preparation                system of cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The system increases mass transfer area of                phase boundary between carbon dioxide and the                cyclic carbonate formed in situ during                carboxylation reaction process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhanced micro-interface preparation system of                cyclic carbonate comprises stirred tank, first                carboxylation reaction tank, and second                carboxylation reaction tank, where stirring tank,                first carboxylation reaction tank and second                carboxylation reaction tank are all filled with                ionic liquid catalyst; side of the stirring tank is                provided with a micro-interface unit,                micro-interface unit is composed of number of                external micro-interface generators, and the main                pipeline leading into the micro-interface unit is                also connected with oxidant delivery pipeline, and                two branch pipelines of the olefin transportation                pipeline, carbon dioxide is introduced into                micro-interface unit from gas source transportation                pipeline, and the carbon dioxide, oxidant, and                olefin enter micro-interface unit for breaking                carbon dioxide gas into micron-level micro bubble;                bottom of the first carboxylation reaction kettle                is provided with a first reaction feed liquid                inlet, first reaction feed liquid inlet is                communicated with the stirring tank through                overflow pipe, two built-in micro-interface                generators are provided in the first carboxylation                reaction kettle, bottom of the second carboxylation                reactor is provided with a second reaction feed                liquid inlet, second reaction feed liquid inlet is                communicated with the stirring tank through                overflow pipe, and two micro-interface generators                are also provided in the second carboxylation                reaction tank, each of the built-in micro-interface                generators is provided with carbon dioxide feed                port for dispersing and breaking carbon dioxide                into microbubbles when reaction material liquid is                used as a medium, reacting oxidative carboxylation                reaction liquid in first carboxylation reactor and                purifying second carboxylation reactor through                flash tank, dehydration tower, and rectification                tower to obtain cyclic carbonate product. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing                enhanced micro-interface preparation system using                cyclic carbonate comprising mixing and dispersing                olefin solution and carbon dioxide at                micro-interface for carboxylation reaction,                performing flash evaporation, dehydration and                rectification to obtain products for                collection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The built-in                  micro-interface generators in the first                  carboxylation reactor and the second                  carboxylation reactor are arranged sequentially                  from top to bottom. A connecting channel is                  provided between the first carboxylation reactor                  and the second carboxylation reactor for                  continuing the carboxylation reaction in the                  second carboxylation reactor. The oxidant                  delivery pipeline is connected with oxidant                  storage tank, and the oxidant storage tank stores                  tert-butyl hydrogen peroxide or hydrogen                  peroxide. The oxidant delivery pipeline is                  provided with a first delivery pump. The olefin                  delivery pipeline is connected with olefin                  storage tank for transporting the olefin solution                  in the olefin storage tank to the micro-interface                  unit through the olefin delivery pipeline. A                  second delivery pump is provided on the olefin                  delivery pipeline. Preferred Method: The                  oxidative carboxylation reaction liquid enters                  from the side wall of the flash tank, top of the                  flash tank is provided with a flash product                  outlet, and the bottom of the flash tank is                  provided with a catalyst outlet, catalyst outlet                  is connected to the side wall of the                  carboxylation reactor for recycling the ionic                  liquid catalyst for use, and flash product outlet                  is connected to the dehydration tower for                  dehydrating the flash product. The temperature of                  the carboxylation reaction is 50-80 degrees C,                  and the pressure of the carboxylation reaction is                  0.1-1 MPa.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid                  comprises acetic acid, propionic acid, n-butyric                  acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid,                  adipic acid and sebacic acid, preferably succinic                  acid, glutaric acid and adipic acid. The guanidine compound is short-chain guanidines,                  preferably tetramethylguanidine or                  polyhexamethylene guanidine. Preferred Process:                  The first component is a micromolecular organic                  amine prepared by reacting less than or equal                  to10C organic acid and N' N-dimethyl-1, 3-propane                  diamine in presence of a sodium                  fluoride-potassium bromide combined catalyst. The                  reaction is carried out under an inert                  atmosphere, the reaction temperature is                  110-160degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is                  5-10 hours, and further carried out refluxing for                  removing water in the reaction process, or                  removing water by using a molecular sieve.

PN CN111847941-A; CN111847941-B
TI Digestion of free calcium oxide in steelmaking dust ash, involves adding steelmaking dust ash in digestion container with carbon dioxide, injecting saturated steam, adjusting concentration of carbon dioxide, heating, and cooling dust ash.
AU REN Z
   YU Y
AE HEBEI LANHAI ZHITOU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO L (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020B26329
AB    NOVELTY - Digestion of free calcium oxide in steelmaking dust ash involves adding a steelmaking dust ash in a digestion container, replacing gas in the digestion container with carbon dioxide until the oxygen content in the gas is less than 1 (volume/volume)%, injecting a saturated steam into the digestion container, adjusting concentration of carbon dioxide so that the content of water molecules in the saturated steam is 50-80 mol%, adjusting pressure to 0.16-0.2 MPa, heating to 150-250 degrees C for 15-30 minutes, replacing the gas in the digestion container with carbon dioxide pre-cooled to 5-10 degrees C under a pressure of 0.16-0.2 MPa, and cooling the steelmaking dust ash in the container to 60-80 degrees C.
   USE - Digestion of free calcium oxide in steelmaking dust ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method digests free calcium oxide in the steelmaking dust with high efficiency, without the need for high reaction temperature and pressure, and without excessively oxidizing elemental iron, in a short period of time, and produces treated steelmaking dust ash having uniform particle size distribution.

PN CN111514939-A
TI Preparing ionic liquid/metal organic framework composite catalyst for catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide, involves dispersing metal organic framework material in ethanol solution of ionic liquid, centrifugal drying, and vacuum drying.
AU SHI L
   YI Q
   WU Y
   LI J
   GAO L
   SHAN Y
   QI K
   WANG X
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202078758V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ionic liquid/metal organic framework (MOF) composite catalyst involves dispersing the metal organic framework material in the ethanol solution of the bisamino functionalized ionic liquid. The obtained product is subjected to centrifugal drying, dispersed in the polar solvent of dialdehyde, and stirred at room temperature for 0.5-2 hour. The obtained product is further subjected to centrifugal drying, and vacuum drying at 60 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain ionic liquid/MOF composite catalyst material.
   USE - Method for preparing ionic liquid/metal organic framework composite catalyst for catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material preparation method is simple, the amount of ionic liquid is small, and the catalytic performance is excellent. It can efficiently catalyze the conversion of carbon dioxide to produce cyclic carbonate under normal pressure, low temperature, and without a co-catalyst, and has excellent cycle stability, which is of great significance to the realization of carbon dioxide emission reduction and resource utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for using ionic liquid/MOF composite catalyst in the catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The diamino functionalized ionic liquid is obtained by reacting imidazole or 2-methylimidazole with bromide salt in a molar ratio of 1:2 to 4. Preferred Compositions: The concentration of the ethanol solution of the ionic liquid is 5-10 wt.%. The concentration of the dialdehyde solution is 5-10 wt.%. Preferred Components: The bromo-based salt is selected from 2-bromoethylamine hydrobromide, 2-chloroethylamine hydrochloride, 3-bromopropylamine hydrobromide, or 4-bromobutylamine hydrobromide. The dialdehyde is selected from succinaldehyde, glutaraldehyde, adipaldehyde, 1,4-phthalaldehyde, and 4,4'-biphenyldialdehyde, where the molar ratio of the two is 0.5-2:0.5-2 when two are selected. The metal organic frame material is selected from chromium terephthalate metal organic framework (MIL-101 (Cr)), amine functionalized MIL-101 (NH2-MIL-101 (Cr)) and 2-Methylimidazole zinc salt (ZIF-8). The polar solvent is selected from ethanol, acetonitrile, toluene, ethyl acetate, and ether. The diamino functionalized ionic liquid is selected from formulae (I-VIII). Preferred Process: The method for catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide involves using ionic liquid/MOF composite catalyst material as catalyst, using carbon dioxide and epichlorohydrin as raw materials, synthesizing cyclic carbonate, where the reaction is performed under pressure of 0.1 megaPascal at 25-50 degrees C. When 10 mmol epichlorohydrin is involved in the reaction, the amount of ionic liquid-based supramolecular catalyst is 0.05 g..

PT P
PN DE102018106076-A1; WO2019174679-A1
TI Synthesis of methanol involves performing catalytic reforming of aqueous methanol mixture, to obtain hydrogen and carbon dioxide, reacting hydrogen and oxygen in fuel cell, and performing catalytic reaction of carbon dioxide with water.
AU SCHULZE D E
AE BAU GMBH KARL (BAUK-Non-standard)
GA 201980409D
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of methanol involves performing catalytic reforming of the a liquid mixture of methanol and water under heat, to obtain 3 moles hydrogen and carbon dioxide, separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide into two pure gas streams, supplying obtained hydrogen together with oxygen to a fuel cell, reacting hydrogen and oxygen in a fuel cell with water when emitting electrical energy, performing catalytic reaction of obtained carbon dioxide with water, to obtain mixture of methanol and water with heat release, and recycling the obtained mixture to catalytic reforming process.
   USE - Synthesis of methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient synthesis of methanol. The yield of deuterium-reduced water can be increased together with the methanol synthesis method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for arrangement for synthesis of methanol, which comprises a tank in which mixture of methanol and water is stored, a steam reforming reactor provided with a catalyst (a), a methanol synthesis reactor provided with a catalyst (b), a water supply line to the methanol synthesis reactor, a synthesis line from the methanol synthesis reactor to the tank, a hydrogen supply line, the fuel cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalysts contain metal oxides, preferably consist of 50-80 wt.% cooper oxide, 12-40 wt.% zinc oxide and 5-15 wt.% alumina.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Hydrogen, carbon dioxide and/or oxygen are compressed and temporarily stored. The methanol is separated from excess methanol-water mixture and converted into propylene and/or synthetic fuel. The synthesis of methanol is carried out together with process for production of deuterium-reduced water, which involves injecting oxyhydrogen (HHO) gas obtained from electrolysis to a combustion furnace, igniting in the presence of metal oxide as catalyst at 2800-3500 degrees C in a plasma to emerging reaction water while supplying air, cooling the plasma, condensing and collecting the reaction water. The electrical energy generated in the fuel cell is used for electrolysis of water in HHO gas. Preferred Composition: The usage amount of carbon dioxide during catalytic reaction is 1-10%, preferably 2-3%. Reaction water from the fuel cell has a reduced deuterium content of less than 140 ppm, preferably less than 80 ppm, is supplied to the deuterium-reduced water from HHO gas combustion. The exhaust air from the HHO gas combustion essentially consists of oxygen. The liquid mixture of methanol and water comprises 70-60 wt.%, preferably 68 wt.% methanol and 30-40 wt.%, preferably 32 wt.% water.

PT P
PN US2019256989-A1; US11280008-B2
TI Apparatus for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to formate, comprises anolyte compartment, gas compartment and catholyte compartment having cathode consisting of coating formed using catalyst powder containing tin-carbon particles.
AU AGARWAL A S
   RODE E J
   GAUTAM D
AE DNV GL AS (DNVG-Non-standard)
GA 2019718514
AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical reduction apparatus comprises an anolyte compartment having an anode and a membrane, a catholyte compartment having the membrane and a cathode, and a gas compartment in fluid communication with the cathode. The cathode comprises a porous substrate and a catalytic coating partially covering the substrate. The catalytic coating is formed by mixing a catalyst powder containing tin-carbon particles comprising 0.1-10 nm tin deposits on carbon support, an alcohol-based solvent, and a polymeric binder, to form a catalyst ink, applying the ink to the substrate, and drying.
   USE - Apparatus for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to formate and formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus enables effective and stable electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide with low voltage application. The physical abrasion of the electrode can be prevented by adjusting the content of binder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to formate, which involves feeding an anolyte into the anolyte compartment, feeding a catholyte into the catholyte compartment, feeding carbon dioxide into the gas compartment, and impressing a voltage between the anode and the cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The substrate is a carbon fiber paper. Preferred Composition: The catalyst loading amount is 0.1-10 mg/cm2. The binder content is 0.1-5 wt.% of the catalyst loading. Preferred Method: The ink is sprayed on the porous substrate. The ink application process and drying are repeated until a desired coating weight is achieved. Preferred Properties: The impressed voltage is sufficient to provide Vcathode of -1.5 and -2 Vsce. The Faradaic efficiency of carbon dioxide to formate is 55-95% for period of more than 100 hours. The current density is more than 100 mA/cm2.

PN CN109985487-A; CN109985487-B
TI Fluent based pressure swing adsorption carbon capture optimizing method, involves optimizing carbon dioxide adsorption process, adjusting initial conditions or boundary conditions to obtain optimal absorption condition.
AU DENG S
   ZHAO L
   LIAN Y
   LI S
   HE J
   ZHAO R
   ZHOU Y
   XU Y
   LIU B
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201962336B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing an adsorption bed model for adsorbing carbon dioxide. Basic parameters of an adsorption bed are set. A computational fluid dynamics model is established for adsorbing the carbon dioxide. Adsorbent material is filled in the adsorption bed. User defined function is compiled to solve mass conservation equation, momentum conservation equation and energy conservation equation. Initial and boundary conditions of an adsorption bed layer are set. Adsorption process is simulated by Fluent software based on discrete control equation. An adsorption result is obtained. Carbon dioxide adsorption process is optimized. Initial conditions or boundary conditions are adjusted to obtain optimal absorption condition corresponding to the adsorption bed layer.
   USE - Fluent based pressure swing adsorption carbon capture optimizing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables obtaining fluid flow distribution, component concentration and temperature distribution in an adsorption vessel in accurate and realistic manner, and optimizing energy efficiency analysis and adsorption process of a carbon dioxide adsorption system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a fluent based pressure swing adsorption carbon capture optimizing method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

TI Composition used for cooling to-be-cooled product e.g. food product, comprises mixture comprising preset amount of carbon dioxide solid particles having preset diameter, and liquid nitrogen.
AU BELOT D
   GRANDEAU R
   ZUNDEL C
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2019070331
AB    NOVELTY - A cooling composition comprises a mixture comprising 70-85 wt.% carbon dioxide solid particles having a diameter of less than or equal to 50 mu m, and a liquid nitrogen.
   USE - Composition is used for cooling to-be-cooled product (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to medical product, metal product, plastic product, organic product, food product, human tissue material, plant material, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The cooling composition quickly generates large cooling capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of cooling composition, which involves forming carbon dioxide solid particles comprising carbon dioxide in an expansion cone, and mixing carbon dioxide solid particles and liquid nitrogen; and
   (2) method for cooling to-be-cooled product, which involves mixing (i) the cooling composition at a rate of less than 1 rpm, and immersing (ii) and maintaining a to-be-cooled product in the cooling composition for preset time. In process (ii), the amount of liquid nitrogen in the cooling composition is measured, and is kept constant to less than 5% by the addition of liquid nitrogen.

PN CN110835569-A; CN110835569-B
TI Removing organic sulfur in dangerous coal using supercritical fluid hydrogenation reaction comprises e.g. crushing coal sample to specific size, excluding air and impurities, and opening inlet and outlet valves of the extraction kettle.
AU GONG L
   TIAN G
   PENG Y
   LIU J
   SONG X
   GUI X
   ZHAO G
   MA Z
AE SHANXI COKING COAL GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   JIANGSU TANQINGYUAN CLEAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY RES INSTITU (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU TANQINGYUAN CLEAN ENERGY TECHNOL (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020191158
AB    NOVELTY - Removing organic sulfur in dangerous coal using supercritical fluid hydrogenation reaction comprises e.g. (i) crushing coal sample to a specific size, (ii) excluding air and impurities from the kettle body, opening the inlet and outlet valves of the extraction kettle and connecting them to the supercritical fluid storage container, circulating the entire closed circuit system with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid circulation, carrying the ethanol to cleaning the pipeline through the auxiliary pump, closing the outlet valve of the extraction kettle to ensure the air tightness is intact, (iii) adding coal sample obtained in step (i) to the mixer, start stirring, adding carbon dioxide to a fixed temperature and pressure, opening the trimmer to make the carbon dioxide pressure constant and controlling a specific flow rate based on the determination of the solubility at different temperatures and pressures.
   USE - The method is useful for removing organic sulfur in dangerous coal using supercritical fluid hydrogenation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is safe, easy, economical and suitable for large-scale industrialization; has low toxicity and pressure; and realizes large-scale removal of 30% organic sulfur and 80% inorganic sulfur in coal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing organic sulfur in dangerous coal using supercritical fluid hydrogenation reaction comprises (i) crushing coal sample to a specific size, (ii) excluding air and impurities from the kettle body, opening the inlet and outlet valves of the extraction kettle and connecting them to the supercritical fluid storage container, circulating the entire closed circuit system with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid circulation, carrying the ethanol to cleaning the pipeline through the auxiliary pump, closing the outlet valve of the extraction kettle to ensure the air tightness is intact, (iii) adding coal sample obtained in step (i) to the mixer, start stirring, adding carbon dioxide to a fixed temperature and pressure, opening the trimmer to make the carbon dioxide pressure constant and controlling a specific flow rate based on the determination of the solubility at different temperatures and pressures, carrying out catalytic hydrogenation reaction in supercritical carbon dioxide and completely stirring in a mixer, (iv) mixing the reactants into the extraction kettle, adjusting the temperature and pressure, controlling the temperature in the extraction kettle to be the same as that in the mixer, opening the trim valve, passing the supercritical carbon dioxide mixed gas through the extraction kettle at a specific flow rate and into the separation kettle, separating carbon dioxide and hydrogen are from the product, collecting the precipitated product in the collector, pressing carbon and hydrogen into the mixer by an air compression pump to ensure carbon dioxide and hydrogen are not lost, adding a specific hydrogen pressure to keep the hydrogen pressure stable, (v) removing the processed coal samples from the collector, washing with deionized water, heating and drying, preparing samples and testing the coal quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) the particle size of the coal sample is 1-3 and/or 3-6 mm. In the step (vi) adjust the temperature and pressure range in the medium, warm and pressurize the mixed gas above the critical point, the temperature is within 35-40 degrees C, the pressure is within 7-10Mpa, and hydrogen is added to the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.

TI Converting carbon dioxide into methane through            metal hydride comprises e.g. carrying hydrogenation            reaction between magnesium powder and hydrogen gas to            obtain magnesium hydride hydrogen storage material, and            ball-milling magnesium powder and transition metals            comprising iron, nickel and/or cobalt.
AU CAI Y
   WANG J
   ZHOU Q
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2022436976
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide into methane through                metal hydride comprises (i) carrying out                hydrogenation reaction between magnesium powder and                hydrogen gas at 300-600&#8451; to obtain                magnesium hydride hydrogen storage material,                firstly ball-milling magnesium powder and                transition metals comprising iron, nickel and/or                cobalt, and then subjecting the obtained mixture to                hydrogenation reaction with hydrogen gas at                300-600&#8451; to obtain a composite                magnesium-based hydrogen storage material, (ii)                reacting the molecular sieve adsorbed with carbon                dioxide with magnesium hydride hydrogen storage                material or composite magnesium-based hydrogen                storage material under protection of inert gas at                250-600&#8451; to obtain mixed gas, and (iii)                separating the methane in mixed gas to obtain                methane, and completely the conversion                method.
   USE - The method is useful for converting carbon                dioxide into methane through metal hydride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the utilization of carbon                sequestration; promotes the conversion of carbon                dioxide; and saves the consumption of                hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), firstly                  placing magnesium powder or magnesium powder and                  transition metals of iron, nickel and/or cobalt                  in the reactor, replacing the air in the reactor                  with argon gas, where the pressure of argon gas                  is 0.1-0.3 MPa, and filling hydrogen gas to carry                  out the respective hydrogenation reactions, and                  after filling with hydrogen gas, the gas pressure                  in the reactor is 1.5-4.5 MPa, where two                  hydrogenation reactions are carried out for 3-24                  hours, and the method further comprises                  ball-milling the magnesium powder and transition                  metals of iron, nickel and/or cobalt for 3-24                  hours at a rotational speed of 300-450 rotations                  per minute (rpm). The source of hydrogen gas is                  the production of hydrogen by electrolysis of                  water by photovoltaic solar power generation or                  the production of hydrogen by electrolysis of                  water by wind power generation. In the step (ii),                  the molecular sieve is ZSM-5, and the pressure of                  zeolite (ZSM-5) in adsorbing carbon dioxide is                  1.5-3 MPa, the temperature is 50-100&#8451;,                  and the time is 1-4 hours. The pressure of the                  inert gas is 0.1-0.5 MPa, and the molecular sieve                  adsorbed with carbon dioxide and magnesium                  hydride hydrogen storage material or composite                  magnesium-based hydrogen storage material are                  methanated for 3-24 hours under the above                  conditions. The step after the reaction, the                  reacting remaining solids more than ten times in                  the order of step (i)-(ii).Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  particle sizes of magnesium powder and transition                  metals of iron, nickel and cobalt are all                  &#8804; 48 &#181;m, and the transition                  metals account for 10-50% of the total metal                  mass.

TI Generation of gaseous fuels involves gasifying            carbonaceous fuel with vitiated air in presence of            metal oxide and water.
AU KRUGER T
AE ORIGEN POWER LTD (ORIG-Non-standard)
   ORIGEN POWER LTD (ORIG-Non-standard)
GA 202195739N
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves gasifying carbonaceous                fuel with vitiated air in the presence of metal                oxide and water provide metal carbonate, gaseous                fuel and heat. The gasification of the carbonaceous                fuel produce carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and                reaction of carbon monoxide with water produce                carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The recarbonation of                the metal oxide by carbon dioxide to produce metal                carbonate.
   USE - Process for generation of gaseous fuels using                vitiated air in presence of lime and water to                provide calcium carbonate in energy                distribution.
   ADVANTAGE - The process removes the need to purify the                syngas or hydrogen produced, and removes concerns                about post-process sequestering of the carbon                dioxide by-product. The carbon dioxide is used to                recarbonate the lime (or other metal oxide) into                calcium carbonate, a solid which is easily removed                from the system. As such, there is no need for the                sorbents commonly present to remove carbon dioxide                from syneresis or hydrogen production systems where                carbonaceous fuels are the starting material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gaseous                fuel generation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of single                reactor together with separation of the gaseous                fuels.

   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple filtration and separation to obtain pure product and cheap Lewis base and PMHS (industrial silicon waste) as accelerators, avoids carbon monoxide, does not need to purify and separate intermediate isocyanates, and uses atmospheric carbon dioxide as green non-toxic carbonylation reagent and high-pressure carbon dioxide and precious metal catalysts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbonyl heterocyclic compound of formula (I) is prepared by cyclization carbonylation reacting o-substituted aniline of formula (II) with carbon dioxide gas in the co-presence of base and hydrogen silane.
   X=NH, S or O; and
   R1-R4=H, alkyl, alkoxy, halo, nitro, nitrile, ester, phenoxy, alkynyl or forms optionally substituted 3-8C cycloalkyl, optionally substituted 4-8 membered heterocyclyl, optionally substituted 5-10 membered aryl or heteroaryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Lewis base is potassium tert-butoxide, sodium tert-butoxide, potassium fluoride, cesium fluoride, cesium acetate, tetra-n-butylammonium fluoride or potassium carbonate. The molar ratio of amine to base in compound (II) is 1:1-1:5. The molar ratio of amine to hydrogen silane in compound (II) is 1:1-1:10.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen silane is alkyl hydrogen silane, phenyl silane hydrogen or polymethylhydrosiloxane (PMHS). The base alkali is Lewis base. The alkyl hydrogen silane is Et3SiH, Et2SiH2, (EtO)3SiH or (EtO)2MeSiH. The phenyl hydrogen silane is phenylsilane, Ph2MeSiH or PhMe2SiH. The average molecular weight of polymethylhydrogensiloxane is 200-10000. The cyclization carbonylation reaction is carried out in organic solvent at 30-150 degrees C for 1-36 hours. The organic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, acetonitrile, gamma -valerolactone or diglyme.

AU WANG W
   MENG F

   LI X
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 201994115Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting equivalent length of fluid in a capillary according to geometry of the capillary and current interval start time. Judgment is made to check whether the fluid is in the capillary. PN ID201810131-A
TI Producing precipitated carbonate calcium (PCC) based on carbon dioxide flue gas from industrial waste used in e.g., paint by reacting calcium oxide with aqueous ammonium chloride, and contacting carbon dioxide with ammonium hydroxide.
AU ASLYA D
   ADIAN F
   ARIEF S
   ALIF A
   ALIF M F
AE PT PERTAMINA PERSERO (PERT-Non-standard)
   UNIV ANDALAS (UYAN-Non-standard)
GA 201971197L
AB    NOVELTY - A precipitated carbonate calcium (PCC) based on carbon dioxide (CO2) flue gas from industrial waste is produced by (a) reacting calcium oxide (CaO) with aqueous ammonium chloride (NH4Cl) in agitation reactor, resulting in calcium chloride (CaCl2) and ammonium hydroxide (NH4OH), (b) contacting CO2 flue gas with NH4OH in 1st reaction reactor which is vertically higher than next reaction reactor so that calcium carbonate (CaCO3) is precipitated, (c) running CO2 and NH4OH from reaction reactor from 1st reactor to next reaction reactor which is vertically lower than 1st reactor to form CaCO3, and (d) re-flowing NH4Cl and water formed in the reaction reactor to the agitation reactor.
   USE - Production of precipitated carbonate calcium (PCC) based on carbon dioxide (CO2) flue gas from industrial waste used in paint, toothpaste, paper filler, plastic, and rubber industries.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide emissions from industrial waste are reduced. The shape of the crystal and the size and shape of the particles are fairly easy to control. The PCC has a relatively uniform particle size so that the degree of homogeneity is higher.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method includes adding precipitating reactors after 2nd reactor to further precipitate calcium carbonate carried by reaction stream, or adding filter after 2nd reactor before agitation reactor. The reaction reactor consists of greater than or equal to 2 reaction reactors arranged in series. The reaction reactor temperature is maintained at 15-80 degrees C. Preferred Components: The calcium carbonate formed has a calcite, vaterite, and aragonite crystal structure, preferably calcite crystal structure. Preferred Parameters: The precipitated calcium carbonate has maximum particle size of less than or equal to 15 mu m.

TI Method for operating solid oxide electrolysis cell            (SOEC) in carbon dioxide (CO2) electrolysis, involves            operating SOEC stack in CO2 electrolysis.
AU KUENGAS R
   HEIREDAL-CLAUSEN T
   RASS-HANSEN J
   BLENNOW B P G
   NORBY T H
   NOERBY T H
AE TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202284899K
AB    NOVELTY - A method for converting carbon dioxide into                carbon monoxide in high-temperature, dry, solid                oxide electrolysis, involves providing a fuel gas                stream (101) comprising 70-100 vol.% carbon dioxide                and 0-30 vol.% and 0-0.3 mol% carbon monoxide,                providing a flush gas stream, providing a SOEC (2)                having a fuel side and an oxy side, heating the                fuel gas stream and the flush gas stream to a gas                stream (201) inlet temperature of 600-1000 degrees                C, preferably 700-850 degrees C, supplying the fuel                gas stream to the fuel side of the SOEC at a space                velocity of 2-30 second-1, supplying the flush gas                stream to the oxy side of the SOEC at a space                velocity of 0.1-20 second-1, and applying an                electrolysis current with a current density of -0.2                to -1 A/cm2 across the solid electrolyte to                electrolytically convert a fraction of the carbon                dioxide into carbon monoxide on the fuel side of                the SOEC and to produce an oxygen enriched flush                gas on the oxy side of the SOEC.
   USE - Method for operating SOEC stack in carbon                dioxide electrolysis for producing carbon monoxide                from fossil fuels e.g. steam reforming of natural                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient operation of                solid oxide electrolysis cell in carbon dioxide                electrolysis with high selectivity towards carbon                monoxide production, low electric power                consumption, and high current density.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system                comprising the SOEC.
   Solid oxide electrolysis cell (2)
   Gas purification unit (30)
   Fuel gas stream (101)
   Product gas stream (102)
   Gas stream (201)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The product gas                  stream contains 15-95 vol.%, preferably 30-50                  vol.% carbon monoxide. The fuel gas stream                  contains 80-100 vol.%, preferably 88-98 vol.%                  carbon dioxide, 0-20 vol.%, preferably 1-12 vol.%                  carbon monoxide, 0-1 vol.% water and 0-1 vol.%                  hydrogen and the remainder being inert. Preferred                  Components: The flush gas comprises air, dry air,                  oxygen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, steam or its                  mixture.

PT P
PN WO2022133033-A1
TI Converting carbon dioxide to sugars comprises            contacting feed mixture comprising carbon dioxide and            reductant gas with reduction catalyst at reduction            temperature and reduction pressure to produce            alcohol.
AU SHEEHAN S W
   CHEN C
AE AIR HOLDINGS CO INC (AIRH-Non-standard)
GA 202281640J
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide to sugars comprises                contacting a feed mixture comprising carbon dioxide                and a reductant gas with a reduction catalyst at a                reduction temperature and a reduction pressure to                produce an alcohol.
   USE - The method is useful for converting carbon                dioxide to sugars.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the efficiency; improves                the overall conversion or economics; is scalable;                utilizes electricity to produce desired products                on-site; and is highly reliable reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a system for the conversion of carbon                dioxide to sugars comprising a reduction reactor                comprising a reduction catalyst, a dehydrogenation                reactor comprising a dehydrogenation catalyst, and                a condensation reactor comprising a condensation                catalyst;
   (2) a condensation catalyst comprising calcium                hydroxide and a chiral ligand e.g. a chiral mono-,                bi-, or tridentate ligand that coordinates through                at least one carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, phosphorus,                sulfur, or selenium atoms, e.g. a chiral amino                acid, a chiral phosphine, a chiral binaphthalene,                or a chiral oxazoline, having (chiral                ligand)x(calcium(L)y), where L is a neutral ligand                comprising water or an alcohol, x is 1-6, and y is                0-5; and
   (3) making a condensation catalyst comprising                combining a chiral ligand, e.g. a chiral mono-,                bi-, or tridentate ligand that coordinates through                at least one carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, phosphorus,                sulfur, or selenium atoms, e.g. a chiral amino                acid, a chiral phosphine, a chiral binaphthalene,                or a chiral oxazoline, and calcium hydroxide in a                solvent at a pH of 7-14.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reduction catalyst                  is: a copper-based catalyst; and a mixture of                  copper oxide, zinc oxide, and aluminum oxide. The                  dehydrogenation catalyst is: an iron-based                  catalyst; and a mixture of iron oxide and                  molybdenum oxide. The reductant gas is hydrogen.                  The feed mixture comprises less than 25% carbon                  monoxide (CO), preferably less than 1% CO. The                  feed mixture is substantially free of CO. The                  partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the feed                  mixture is 200-1000 psi, preferably 750-1000 psi.                  The ratio of carbon dioxide reductant gas in the                  feed mixture is 1:10-10:1, preferably 1:3-1:1.                  The condensation catalyst comprises the chiral                  ligand and calcium hydroxide at a ratio of                  1:100-100:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: contacting the alcohol with a                  dehydrogenation catalyst at a dehydrogenation                  temperature and a dehydrogenation pressure to                  produce an aldehyde; and contacting the aldehyde                  with a condensation catalyst at a condensation                  temperature and a condensation pressure to                  produce sugars. The reduction temperature of                  100-450 degrees C. The reduction pressure is                  500-3000 psi. The dehydrogenation temperature is                  250-400 degrees C. The dehydrogenation pressure                  is 0.09-100 psi. The condensation temperature is                  10-300 degrees C. The condensation pressure is                  0.09-1500 psi. Preferred Components: The                  reductant gas is: a hydrocarbon, e.g. methane                  (preferred), ethane, propane, and butane; and                  derived from, flare gas, waste gas, or natural                  gas. The alcohol comprises methanol, ethanol, or                  n-propanol. The aldehyde comprises formaldehyde.                  The sugar comprises glycoaldehyde,                  glyceraldehyde, arabinose, glucose, ribose,                  fructose, or sorbose. The condensation catalyst                  is a group II metal salt, optionally combined                  with a chiral ligand, e.g. a chiral mono-, bi-,                  or tridentate ligand that coordinates through at                  least one carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, phosphorus,                  sulfur, or selenium atoms, e.g. a chiral amino                  acid, a chiral phosphine, a chiral binaphthalene,                  and a chiral oxazoline. The condensation catalyst                  is calcium hydroxide, optionally combined with a                  chiral ligand e.g. a chiral mono-, bi- and                  tridentate ligand that coordinates through at                  least one carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, phosphorus,                  sulfur, or selenium atoms e.g. a chiral amino                  acid, a chiral phosphine, a chiral binaphthalene,                  or a chiral oxazoline. The condensation catalyst                  is chiral ligand of formula ((chiral                  ligand)x(Ca(L)y)) (I).L = neutral ligand comprising water or                  alcohol;x = 1-6; andy = 0-5.The chiral ligand is proline. The                  condensation catalyst comprises:                  (3aS)-1,1,1,1-tetrahydroxy-tetrahydro-3H-2,7000000                  lambda                  (4),1-pyrrolo(1,2-c)1-oxa-3-aza-2-calcacyclopentan-3-one;                  (3aS,3'aS)-1,1-dihydroxy-decahydro-3'H-spiro(cyclopenta(c)1-oxa-2-calcacyclopentane-1,1'-pyrrolo(1,2-c)1-oxa-3-aza-2-calcacyclopentane)-3,3'-dione;                  and a repeating unit cyclopentane of formula                  (I).
   Preferred Definitions:
   x = 1; and
   y = 4; or
   x = 2; and
   y = 2; and
   L = H2O.
   EXAMPLE - Reaction uses 9 l fixed bed flow reactor                equipped with carbon dioxide and electric cylinders                along with a methanol production catalyst, which                had stability equivalent to the copper-zinc-alumina                industrial methanol catalyst that had been                demonstrated for over 17500 hours of use. The                catalyst was added to the fixed-bed reactor in                pellet form, supported by a stainless steel mesh.                Feed gases were pressurized using a compressor                on-board the system then fed through mass flow                controllers and small cartridge heaters. They were                introduced to the heated fixed-bed flow reactor at                temperature of 250 degrees C and pressure of 750                psi where they were transformed to methanol with a                typically 30% of the inlet carbon dioxide converted                per pass through the reactor. The resulting gaseous                mixture was passed through a condenser chilled by a                closed-loop glycol chiller, then into a gas-liquid                separator where the unreacted gases were sent into                a recycle loop to be reintroduced to the reactor                (enabling a system-level yield of greater than 90%,                and the product liquid (25-60 wt.% methanol in                water) was collected. The product liquid had been                optimized to have ideal characteristics for                downstream conversion to formaldehyde and                ultimately sugars. Production and characterization                data for the methanol process had been gathered for                evaluation under conditions relevant for space                applications, e.g. rapid on/off cycles and varied                production rates.
DC D17 (Sugar and starch industry (C07H, C13).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

IP B01J-023/80; C01B-032/50; C07C-029/157; C07C-051/12

PN CN114517301-A
TI Electrolysis of seawater for hydrogen production combined with carbon dioxide capture used in environmentally-friendly gas treatment, comprises e. g. injecting fresh water into anode and cathode electrolytic groove medium and placing anode and cathode on power supply.
AU ZHANG T
   DUAN W
   ZHOU A
   WANG Y
   OUYANG Z
AE QINGDAO UPC ENVIRONMENT & SECURITY TECHN (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202273245R
AB    NOVELTY - Electrolysis of seawater for hydrogen production combined with carbon dioxide capture comprises (i) injecting fresh water into anode and cathode electrolytic groove medium and groove storage ion groove injecting sea water, placing anode and cathode on the power supply, starting to electrolyze, placing anode oxygen in the anode electrolyzing groove at the same time, changing the fresh water into acidic solution, placing cathode hydrogen in the cathode electrolyzing groove at the same time, changing the sea water into alkaline solution respectively collecting the generated oxygen and hydrogen, stopping electrolyzing when the pH value of the to-be cathode electrolytic groove reaches more than 10; (ii) passing the hydrogen ion generated in anode electroanalysis groove the hydrogen ion through the cation exchange membrane under the action of the osmotic pressure to enter the hydrogen storage ion, grooving the cation in the hydrogen storage ion groove surplus,
   USE - The method is useful for Electrolysis of seawater for hydrogen production combined with carbon dioxide capture used in environmentally-friendly gas treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the capture of carbon dioxide, fully utilizes the potential of solution change in the electrolysis process, generates the green energy hydrogen in the electrolysis process, and rationally utilizes the input energy in the regeneration process and avoids the problem that the existing carbon dioxide capture process regeneration process needs lot of energy to be compensated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for realizing electrolysis of seawater for hydrogen production combined with carbon dioxide capture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises generating the cathode ion in the electroanalysis groove caused by the excess anion in the cathode groove the electrolyte in the sea water in the medium groove to supplement anions to the hydrogen storage ion groove anion exchange membrane, using the cathode exchange groove for supplying cations to the cathode groove; (iii) removing dust after the flue gas, introducing into the cathode electrolyzing groove, absorbing carbon dioxide in the flue gas by the alkaline solution in the cathode electrolyzing groove until the absorption is saturated, and stopping introducing the flue gas; (iv) leading out alkaline solution in the cathode electrolyte groove, mixing the acidic solution in the hydrogen storage ion groove, generating neutralization reaction, releasing the absorbed carbon dioxide, and collecting the collected carbon dioxide; and (v) supplementing fresh water into the anode electrolyzing groove, adding sea water into cathode, medium and groove storage ion groove repeating steps (i)-(iv), and realizing continuous trapping of carbon dioxide in the flue gas.

A fraction of the compressed permeate stream is introduced into the heat exchanger at which the introduced fraction of the compressed stream is partially condensed to produce a gaseous permeate stream (15) and a liquid permeate stream. A fraction of the gaseous permeate stream is purified to obtain a purified helium stream containing certain percentage of helium. The pressurized feed stream is natural gas or a gas mixture derived from natural gas or associated gas and the pressurized feed stream also includes methane and optionally nitrogen.

PN CN114160092-A
TI Preparation of alkaline wastewater modified fly ash adsorbent by concentrating wastewater, passing through waste gas, crystallizing, precipitating potassium bicarbonate, centrifuging, and heating potassium bicarbonate crystals.
AU CAO Y
   XU Q
   TANG Z
   WEI H
   SONG F
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 202241607B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of alkaline wastewater modified fly ash adsorbent includes concentrating wastewater containing potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate and potassium chloride, passing through waste gas containing carbon dioxide until potassium carbonate absorbs carbon dioxide, converting into potassium bicarbonate, crystallizing, precipitating potassium bicarbonate, centrifuging to remove potassium bicarbonate crystals and potassium chloride solution, and heating potassium bicarbonate crystals to obtain potassium carbonate, water and carbon dioxide; weighing fly ash, soaking in sulfuric acid solution, washing with deionized water, and drying hours; and weighing fly ash, adding to potassium carbonate solution, stirring magnetically, drying, calcining, and cooling.
   USE - The method is for preparation of alkaline wastewater modified fly ash adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of alkaline wastewater modified fly ash adsorbent comprises concentrating wastewater containing potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate and potassium chloride until crystal film layer appears on the surface, passing through waste gas containing carbon dioxide until potassium carbonate absorbs carbon dioxide, converting into potassium bicarbonate, crystallizing through heat preservation, precipitating potassium bicarbonate, centrifuging to remove potassium bicarbonate crystals and potassium chloride solution, and heating potassium bicarbonate crystals to obtain potassium carbonate, water and carbon dioxide; weighing 20 g fly ash, soaking in 25 wt.% sulfuric acid solution at 90degrees Celsius for 2 hours, washing with deionized water until neutralized, and drying at 100degrees Celsius for 12 hours; weighing 2 g fly ash, adding to 20 mL potassium carbonate solution, stirring magnetically for 12 hours at room temperature, and drying to 80degrees Celsius using rotary evaporator; and calcining for 4 hours, and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of potassium carbonate solution is 10-30 wt.%.

The goods returning machine forms a user-friendly collection point for empty containers, i.e. for containers from which the carbon dioxide has been removed. The user does not have to take care of the disposal or refilling of the containers himself.
