PT P
PN KR2019044287-A; KR2108791-B1
TI Organic-inorganic complex functional catalyst useful in copolymerization reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide, comprises mesoporous zeolite and multiple ammonium halide functional group introduced on zeolite surface.
AU NA K S
   KIM D H
   NAKYUNGSU
   KIM D
AE UNIV CHONNAM NAT IND FOUND (UCHO-C)
GA 201937684J
AB    NOVELTY - Organic-inorganic complex functional catalyst comprises mesoporous zeolite and multiple ammonium halide functional group introduced on the zeolite surface.
   USE - The functional catalyst is useful in copolymerization reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The organic-inorganic complex functional catalyst has high conversion and carbonate selectivity in gas phase reaction process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing organic - inorganic complex functional catalyst, comprising (a) synthesizing a mesoporous zeolite, (b) introducing or exchanging cations on the synthesized zeolite surface, (c) separating and drying and calcining the zeolite, (d) introducing an amine functional group to the surface of the calcined zeolite, and (e) introducing an ammonium halide functional group by forming ammonium halide functional group by treating the zeolite having the amine functional group with an aqueous solution; and
   (2) producing organic carbonate, comprising either: (i) adding epoxide, organic solvent and organic-inorganic complex functional catalyst into the reactor, (ii) injecting carbon dioxide into the reactor, and performing copolymerization reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide while stirring, and (iii) separating the organic carbonate from the solution in which the organic-inorganic multi-functional catalyst is removed after cooling the reactor, or (ia) depositing organic-inorganic complex functional catalyst on the fixed bed of the gas phase reactor, (ib) maintaining nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the gas phase reactor at constant flow rate, (ic) injecting epoxides into the gas phase reactor at constant flow rate and vaporizing the gas by the nitrogen, and (id) causing the copolymerization reaction of the carbon dioxide and the epoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The synthesized mesoporous zeolite has a microporous framework structure of at least one of BEA, MFI, and MTW. The ammonium halide functional group is organic group in which quaternary ammonium and halogen anion are bound by electrostatic attraction and is introduced at density of greater than or equal to 1 mmol/g. The functional catalyst has BET specific surface area of 400-1000 m2/g, micropores volume of 0.03-0.4 ml/g, and mesopores volume of 0.1-1.5 ml/g. The composite catalyst is a catalyst which acts as a catalyst for the copolymerization reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide. The epoxide includes propylene oxide, butylene oxide and/or styrene oxide. The flow rate of the nitrogen gas and the liquid epoxide is 1:40-1:100. Preferred Method: The reaction step is carried out by adding synthesized zeolite into aqueous solution containing cation and stirring. The ammonium halide functional group is introduced into organic solvent at temperature below the boiling point of the solvent. The method further comprises compressing the ammonium halide-functionalized zeolite, and pulverizing the compact at size of 14-20 mesh. The copolymerization reaction is carried out at 80-200 degrees C and pressure of 5-15 atm.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC A02-D; A05-E06A; A08-S02; A10-D05; E07-A04; E11-A01; E11-A02; E11-F06; J04-E04; J04-E11; N04-A; N06-A; N07-D07; N07-F07; N07-F08

PT P
PN CN109453782-A
TI Catalyst useful for circulating carbon dioxide and methanation at constant temperature of calcium oxide, is loaded with metallic nickel on calcium oxide.
AU CHEN J
   WANG Z
   LIU Z
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 2019254418
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst, loaded with metallic nickel on calcium oxide, is claimed. The loading amount of metallic nickel is 0.1-10 wt.%.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for circulating carbon dioxide and methanation at a constant temperature of calcium oxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: realizes process can be carried out multiple times, and the ability of calcium oxide to capture carbon dioxide and the selectivity of methane are not significantly reduced; has stable performance, can capture and convert carbon dioxide cyclically; has high selectivity and simple preparation method; is economical and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing catalyst, comprising using nickel salt, nickel oxide or nickel hydroxide as precursors, loading precursor directly on calcium oxide by equal volume impregnation, or by impregnating the precursor with an equal volume of calcium carbonate, raising the temperature to 220-320 degrees C at heating rate of 0.5-10 degrees C/minute under hydrogen atmosphere of a concentration of 5-100 vol.%, and the nickel precursor is reduced to elemental nickel by heat preservation of 0.2-2 hours to obtain catalyst; and
   (2) use of catalyst for cyclically capturing carbon dioxide and methanating at constant temperature of calcium oxide, comprising adsorption saturating carbon dioxide with a concentration of 1-100 vol.% at 560-600 degrees C, for 0.5-2 hours, introducing mixed gas of hydrogen and nitrogen at concentration of 5-100 vol.%, and repeating the reaction for 0.5-2 hours, and the cycle is repeated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen atmosphere is composed of hydrogen and nitrogen. The 1-100 vol.% carbon dioxide comprises carbon dioxide, 50-150 mg/m3 nitrogen and 35-100 mg/m3 sulfur dioxide.

PN EP3404686-A1; WO2018210936-A1; IN201947046047-A; CN110637350-A; KR2020008593-A; US2020185171-A1; EP3404686-B1; JP2020520545-W; ES2808989-T3; US11056300-B2; JP7053676-B2
TI Circuit breaker for use in electrical device, has enclosure comprising catalytic material, which converts carbon monoxide that forms after ionization of carbon dioxide during arcing into carbon dioxide, where material comprising ceria.
AU KIEFFEL Y
   MAKSOUD L
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
GA 2018911432
AB    NOVELTY - The breaker has two arcing contacts moved relative to each other between an open position of the breaker in which the arcing contacts are separated from each other and a closed position of the breaker in which the arcing contacts are in contact with each other. A gas inlet blows an arc-control gas to interrupt an electric arc that is likely to form during movement of the arcing contacts from the closed position to the open position of the breaker, where the arc-control gas comprising 80% of carbon dioxide and fluorinated compound such as 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoro-2-(trifluoromethyl)-2-propanenitrile, 1,1,1,3,4,4,4-heptafluoro-3-(trifluoromethyl)-2-butanone, 2,3,3,3-Tetrafluoropropene, and 1,3,3,3-Tetrafluoropropene or fluoro oxirane. An enclosure comprises a catalytic material, which converts carbon monoxide that forms after ionization of the carbon dioxide during arcing into carbon dioxide, where the material comprising ceria and 0.5 to 20 wt.% of precious metal e.g. gold and platinum.
   USE - Circuit breaker for use in an electrical device of an automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The breaker provides the catalytic material to be used efficiently, so that formation of carbon monoxide does not result in excessive increase in total pressure that exists in the enclosure without result in excessive reduction of fraction of carbon dioxide that is present in a gaseous state in the enclosure.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X13 (Switchgear, Protection, Electric Drives)

TI Bionic fish-type underwater breathing apparatus            comprises respirator, carbon dioxide filtering device,            oxygen generating device, gas mixing device and air            cylinder, which are set end to end through gas delivery            line to form gas circuit.
AU SU C
   YU Y
   LI Y
   CHENG R
   YANG H
   DONG Y
   ZHAO Z
   GUO G
   ZHANG L
   YIN W
AE UNIV SHANDONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (SDST-C)
GA 201845514X
AB    NOVELTY - Bionic fish-type underwater breathing                apparatus comprises respirator (13), carbon dioxide                filtering device (14), oxygen generating device                (1), gas mixing device (5) and air cylinder (8).                The respirator, carbon dioxide filtering device,                gas mixing device and gas cylinder connected end to                end through a gas delivery line to form a gas                circuit. The oxygen preparation device is connected                to the gas mixing device and supplies oxygen to the                gas mixing device, and gas transportation between                the gas mixing device and the air cylinder air                compressor is also provided on pipeline. The carbon                dioxide filter device or the carbon dioxide filter                device and the gas mixing device oxygen                concentration sensor for detecting oxygen                concentration is disposed between the gas delivery                lines, and the breathing apparatus further includes                a control system for controlling the amount of                compression of the mixed gas by the air compressor                and transmitting oxygen according to the oxygen                concentration sensor.
   USE - Bionic fish-type underwater breathing                apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The bionic fish-type underwater breathing                apparatus has high oxygen index, high gas                utilization rate, does not produce bubbles leaking,                avoids the interference of the marine organism, and                realizes the underwater work for a long time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                bionic fish-type underwater breathing                apparatus.
   Oxygen generating device (1)
   Gas mixing device (5)
   Air cylinder (8)
   Respirator (13)
   Carbon dioxide filtering device (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composite                  polymeric film is polyvinyl alcohol-embedded                  hemoglobin polymer film, which uses polyvinyl                  alcohol as a carrier material, hemoglobin is                  fixed, and hemoglobin adsorbs oxygen from                  seawater. The carbon dioxide filtering device                  contains filter for filtering carbon                  dioxide.

TI Carbon dioxide doped conditioning function featured apparatus for controlling e.g. temperature, in indoor, has determining unit for comparing measured values with pre-set reference value, where illuminated value for weather is set up.

   PARK S

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a display unit (200) for indicating carbon dioxide measured value, temperature measuring value, hygrometry value and luminous intensity measurement value obtained from a temperature measuring part, a humidity measurement department and a luminous intensity measurement section. A measured value determining unit (400) compares the measured values with pre-set reference value through an operating panel (300), where reference illuminated value for weather is set up among clear drawing registering function and poor drawing registering function.
   USE - Carbon dioxide doped conditioning function featured apparatus for controlling environmental temperature and humidity in an indoor for cultivating a plant e.g. mushroom plant and ginseng plant, in a hospital and a kindergarten.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus controls carbon dioxide measuring amount to maintain carbon dioxide value set up and hand over amount of insufficient gas in an environment such that indoor air is formed by temperature and humidity values measured and compared with the reference value, so that plant including mushroom and ginseng can be cultivated while selecting one of the modes among plant cultivation mode and indoor air clean mode in the environment and a growing condition of the plant and administration process can be controlled under a real-time, thus increasing material times amount of the plant in an effective manner.

PN CN112642440-A
TI Sulfur-resisting carbon dioxide methanation catalyst comprises alumina as carrier, copper oxide and Rh as active components and bismuth as auxiliary agent.
AU HUANG X
   XU B
   CAI J
   WANG J
   WU X
   ZHANG J
   ZHU Y
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETRO-CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202143578Q
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfur-resisting carbon dioxide methanation catalyst comprises alumina as carrier, copper oxide and Rh as active components and bismuth as auxiliary agent.
   USE - Used as sulfur-resisting carbon dioxide methanation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has excellent sulfur-resisting methanation performance, stability and simple preparation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst comprising using deposition precipitation method to obtain catalyst by stepwise precipitation of aluminum, copper, rhodium and bismuth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises components aluminum, copper, rhodium and bismuth in a mass ratio of 10: (0.5-3): (0.01-0.3): (0.01-0.2). The alkaline precipitation agent comprises aqueous solution of sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate or potassium bicarbonate. Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by comprising (i) mixed nitrate solution containing Al3+ and Cu2+ and alkaline precipitation agent are co-currently added to the water, stirred and maintained for 1-2 hours, (ii) add rhodium chloride solution to the above solution, add alkaline precipitant, maintain the pH of the solution is 7-8, continue to stir and maintain for 1-2 hours, (iii) add bismuth chloride solution to the above solution, add alkaline precipitant, maintain the pH of the solution to 7-8, continue to stir and maintain for 1-2 hours, and (iv) carried out aging, washing, filtering, drying and roasting. In the step (iv) the aging time is 2-4 hours. The drying time is 12-24 hours. The drying temperature is 90-120 degrees C. The roasting time is 1-6 hours. The calcination temperature is 350-700 degrees C.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN DE102021106248-A1; US2022289584-A1; WO2022194423-A1
TI Production of lithium carbonate involves            evaporating aqueous salt solution comprising lithium            until concentrate is formed, evaporating concentrate,            forming lithium-rich concentrate, forming solid lithium            carbonate from concentrate by adding e.g. carbonates,            and separating lithium carbonate.
AU ROESSLER M
   SCHMIDT J
   ROE BLER M
AE SEP SALT & EVAPORATION PLANTS LTD (SEPS-Non-standard)
   SEP SALT & EVAPORATION PLANTS LTD (SEPS-Non-standard)
GA 2022B6761Y
AB    NOVELTY - Production (1) of lithium carbonate from an                aqueous salt solution involves (1) evaporating                aqueous salt solution comprising at least 0.005                wt.% lithium in an evaporation stage (3) at a                temperature of 40-160&#176; C until a                concentrate (I) is formed with 70 wt.% or less                water, (2) evaporating the concentrate (I) in an                evaporation stage (4) at a temperature of                60-180&#176; C until a concentrate (II) with 60                wt.% or less water is formed, (3) increasing the                lithium content to at least 0.14 wt.% by heating                concentrate (II) in a lithium concentration stage                (5) at a temperature of at least 60&#176; C,                thus forming a lithium-rich concentrate (III), (4)                forming solid lithium carbonate from concentrate                (III) by adding carbonates, hydrogen carbonates,                carbon dioxide and/or carbon dioxide in combination                with a lye in a precipitation stage (6), and (5)                separating the lithium carbonate precipitated or                crystallized from the concentrate (III) by                carbonate and leaving a mother liquor behind.
   USE - Production of lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of                the lithium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production (1) of lithium carbonate from an                aqueous salt solution containing lithium ions,                chloride ions and at least one other dissolved                cation species belongs to the group of alkaline                earth and/or alkali metals involves (1) evaporating                aqueous salt solution comprising at least 0.005                wt.% lithium in an evaporation stage (3) at a                temperature of 40-160&#176; C until a                concentrate (I) is formed with 70 wt.% or less                water, (2) evaporating the concentrate (I) in an                evaporation stage (4) at a temperature of                60-180&#176; C until a concentrate (II) with 60                wt.% or less water is formed, (3) increasing the                lithium content to at least 0.14 wt.% by heating                concentrate (II) in a lithium concentration stage                (5) at a temperature of at least 60&#176; C,                thus forming a lithium-rich concentrate (III), (4)                forming solid lithium carbonate from concentrate                (III) by adding carbonates, hydrogen carbonates,                carbon dioxide and/or carbon dioxide in combination                with a lye in a precipitation stage (6), and (5)                separating the lithium carbonate precipitated or                crystallized from the concentrate (III) by                carbonate and leaving a mother liquor behind.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a simplified flow diagram                illustrating recovery of lithium carbonate from an                aqueous salt solution.
   1Production of lithium carbonate
   3Evaporator stage
   4Evaporator stage
   5Lithium concentration stage
   6Precipitation stage
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The other dissolved                  cation species is sodium ions, magnesium ion,                  barium ion, strontium ion and/or calcium ion. In                  the evaporation stage (3), solid sodium chloride                  is formed as residue (I) in addition to                  concentrate (I) and separated from concentrate                  (I). In the evaporation stage (4), a solid                  residue (II) is formed in addition to the                  concentrate (II), which contains at least sodium                  chloride and separated from the concentrate (II).                  The solid residue (II) separated from the                  concentrate (II) is washed with water, slightly                  unsaturated sodium chloride solution and/or the                  mother liquor until only solid sodium chloride                  remains as a solid. The solid barium and/or                  strontium chloride is formed in the evaporator                  stage (4). The formed solid barium and/or                  strontium chloride is separated from the                  concentrate (II). After the evaporator stage (4),                  the concentrate (II) is cooled in a cooling stage                  to a temperature of 40&#176; C or less. In                  the cooling stage a solid residue (II-2) is                  formed and separated from concentrate (II). The                  solid residue (II-2) contains potassium, sodium,                  barium and/or strontium chloride, strontium                  chloride hydrate and/or their double salts. The                  residue (II-2) is washed with water at                  25&#176; C until only strontium chloride                  and/or strontium chloride hydrate remains as a                  solid. After washing the residue (II-2), the                  water used is collected as washing solution (II).                  In the lithium concentration stage (5) a residue                  (III) containing calcium chloride and/or calcium                  chloride hydrate is formed and separated off. The                  residue (III) is washed with water at                  30&#176; C until only calcium chloride                  hydrate remains as a solid, with a calcium                  chloride-depleted washing water being formed. The                  calcium chloride-depleted wash water is feed back                  to the concentrate (II). After the lithium                  concentration stage (5) in a precipitation stage                  a precipitating reagent, preferably sodium                  hydroxide solution, is added to concentrate                  (III). The lithium carbonate is formed by the                  addition of the precipitating agent, preferably                  the sodium hydroxide solution, magnesium, barium,                  strontium and/or calcium hydroxide and separated.                  The mother liquor, the wash solution (I), the                  wash solution (II) and/or the barium-rich wash                  water is returned to the aqueous salt solution                  and/or the concentrate (I). In the precipitation                  stage (6) lithium sulfate is precipitated by                  adding sodium sulfate and lithium sulfate is                  converted to lithium carbonate with sodium                  carbonate. The lithium carbonate formed is                  dissolved by supplying carbon dioxide, the                  resulting solution is filtered and lithium                  carbonate is precipitated again by adding a                  hydroxide. In the aqueous salt solution organic                  carbon compounds are present as impurities and                  these are removed before the evaporator stage (4)                  by emulsion splitting or other type of                  elimination. The aqueous salt solution originates                  from industrial plants, preferably from field of                  oil and gas industry and production, and/or                  natural sources. In the aqueous salt solution                  heavy metal residues are present and these are                  separated before the evaporator stage (3). The                  evaporator stage (3) is an evaporation plant with                  mechanical vapor compression or a multistage                  steam or otherwise heated evaporation plant. The                  evaporator stage (4) is a forced circulation                  crystallizer. Preferred Process: The method                  involves collecting the used water and the                  slightly unsaturated sodium chloride solution                  and/or the mother liquor as washing solution (I)                  after washing the residue (II). In a barium                  chloride concentration stage in scrubbing                  solution (I) and/or scrubbing solution (II), the                  proportion of barium chloride is increased to                  more than 20 wt.% with temperature-dependent                  water removal, whereby a barium chloride-rich                  wash water and, if wash solution (I) is used,                  solid sodium chloride is formed and the solid                  sodium chloride is separated. Preferred                  Conditions: In the lithium concentration stage                  (5) the lithium content is increased to lithium                  saturation by heating the concentrate (II) at a                  temperature of 60&#176; C or more.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)
MC E33-G; H01-X
IP C01D-015/08; B01D-009/00; C01D-001/30; C22B-026/12; C22B-003/22

TI Preparing a polyester material, comprises e.g.            drying nanoporous silica aerogel powder to remove            water, adding polyester resin and styrene, mixing,            adding liquid methyl ethyl ketone peroxide, stirring,            adding cobalt naphthenate and triethanolamine and            stirring, pouring into mold and foaming.
AU HE Z
   TANG K
   WANG H
AE SUZHOU HAICHEN PLASTIC CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022960207
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a polyester material, comprises (i)                drying nanoporous silica aerogel powder to remove                water, (ii) adding polyester resin and styrene,                mixing, adding liquid methyl ethyl ketone peroxide,                uniformly stirring, adding cobalt naphthenate and                triethanolamine and stirring, (iii) pouring into a                mold and foaming, placing in a drying oven and                naturally cooling to obtain a microporous polyester                material, (iv) setting the radiation voltage and                current, irradiating the polyester material with                micropores using electron beam radiation, and (v)                placing a microporous polyester material in an                injection molding machine to obtain microporous and                mesoporous polyester material, and (vi) placing the                polyester material with micropores and mesopores in                thermosetting micropore plastic forming device,                introducing carbon dioxide, sealing and filling                carbon dioxide and carrying out graded pressing to                obtain multi-stage porous polyester material with                micropores, mesopores and macropores.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a polyester                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyester material: has excellent                moisture-absorbing and quick-drying effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a polyester material, comprises (i)                drying 5-10 pts. wt. nanoporous silica aerogel                powder at 80&#8451; for 6 hours to remove                water, (ii) adding 100 pts. wt. polyester resin and                40-50 pts. wt. styrene, uniformly mixing, adding                8-10 pts. wt. liquid methyl ethyl ketone peroxide,                fully mixing and uniformly stirring, adding 1-4                pts. wt. cobalt naphthenate and 0.5-1 pts. wt.                triethanolamine after 5 minutes and stirring for 1                minutes, (iii) pouring into a mold, allowing to                stand and foaming for 30 minutes, placing in a                drying oven at 100&#8451; for 4 hours after                foaming is finished and naturally cooling to obtain                a microporous polyester material, (iv) setting the                radiation voltage and current as 130 kV and 1mA,                and the radiation quantity as 100-260kGy,                irradiating the polyester material with micropores                using electron beam radiation at room temperature,                and (v) placing a microporous polyester material in                an injection molding machine, and utilizing a gas                generating device to generate air holes in the                material in the forming process to obtain the                microporous and mesoporous polyester material, and                (vi) placing the polyester material with micropores                and mesopores in thermosetting micropore plastic                forming device, introducing carbon dioxide,                discharging clean air, sealing and filling carbon                dioxide and carrying out graded pressing to obtain                multi-stage porous polyester material with                micropores, mesopores and macropores.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for polyester                material prepared by the above mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The pore diameter of the                  nanoporous silica aerogel powder is 18-20 nm, and                  the porosity is more than 90%. The pore size of                  the pores is less than 2 nm. The process                  parameters of the gas-assisted forming technology                  are as follows; the pre-injection amount of the                  polyester material melt with micropores is 85%,                  the melt temperature is 240&#8451;, the mold                  temperature is 40&#8451;, the delay time is                  4s, the gas injection pressure is 3 &#181;Pa                  and the gas injection time is 10 s. The aperture                  of the micropores is 2-50nm. The aperture of the                  macropores is more than 50 nm. The pressure is                  maintained for 30 minutes under the pressure of 5                  &#181;Pa and the pressure is maintained for                  30 minutes under the pressure of 2                  &#181;Pa.

TI Manufacturing lightweight composite sole comprises mixing thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particles and polyurethane glue into thick foamed substrate, spreading thermoplastic polyurethane film on cavity of shoe mold, and placing in oven.

AE GIA JIU ENTERPRISE MFG CORP (GIAJ-Non-standard)
GA 202072764C
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing lightweight composite sole comprises (i) adding water dispersant and thermoplastic polyurethane particles in an autoclave in sequence, feeding carbon dioxide and nitrogen into the autoclave so that the pressure inside the autoclave is 9-11 MPa, stopping feeding carbon dioxide and nitrogen into the autoclave, stirring with a stirrer, heating the autoclave at 100-150 degrees C for 1-2 hours, stopping the agitator, (ii) mixing thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particles and polyurethane glue into a thick foamed substrate, partially covering the polyurethane glue with thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles, (iii) spreading the thermoplastic polyurethane film on the cavity of the shoe mold, and filling the foam shoe material in the mold cavity and stacking it on the thermoplastic polyurethane film to close the mold, (iv) placing the shoe mold in an oven at 100-140 degrees C for 8-13 minutes in the closed state, and (v) taking out the shoe mold from the oven.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing lightweight composite sole.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can make the sole with new shape using buffer air bag which is more lightweight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing lightweight composite sole comprises (i) adding water dispersant and thermoplastic polyurethane particles in an autoclave in sequence, feeding carbon dioxide and nitrogen into the autoclave so that the pressure inside the autoclave is 9-11 MPa, stopping feeding carbon dioxide and nitrogen into the autoclave, stirring with a stirrer, heating the autoclave at 100-150 degrees C for 1-2 hours, stopping the agitator and opening the valve of the autoclave to relieve the pressure to obtain thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particles with a specific gravity of 0.1-0.12 g/cm3, (ii) mixing thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particles and polyurethane glue into a thick foamed substrate, partially covering the polyurethane glue with thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles, where the thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles do not contact each other on their surfaces, (iii) spreading the thermoplastic polyurethane film on the cavity of the shoe mold, and filling the foam shoe material in the mold cavity and stacking it on the thermoplastic polyurethane film to close the mold, (iv) placing the shoe mold in an oven at 100-140 degrees C for 8-13 minutes in the closed state, and (v) taking out the shoe mold from the oven, opening the mold with polyurethane coating and thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particle form joined with thermoplastic polyurethane film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The foaming substrate includes 0.1-3 wt.% water, 0.8-3 wt.% catalyst and 8 wt.% anti-yellowing agent, and has mixing time of 3-5 seconds. The mixing mass ratio of the polyurethane glue and thermoplastic polyurethane foamed particles is 7:3. The viscosity of polyurethane glue is 2000-3500m Pa.second (25 degrees C). The catalyst is a polyurethane catalyst. The anti-yellowing agent is an ultraviolet absorber or an anti-UV stabilizer. The water dispersant includes water, surfactant and co-solvent in a mass ratio of 1:0.03:0.015-1:0.008:0.04. The surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfonate or cetyl trimethyl ammonium bromide. The co-solvent is cyclopentane, n-butane, ethanol or acetone. Preferred Method: The inner side of the stirring barrel is heated to 30-40 degrees C to stir and mix the foamed substrate.

GA 2019A20918
AB    NOVELTY - Installation comprises: a first zone (1) where                the effluent to be treated arrives, first zone for                example constituted by a settling tank, first zone                where a pH is preferably maintained between 10-12,                so as to favor the precipitation of calcium or                magnesium in the form of carbonate and thus                facilitate its elimination; a second zone                comprising a tank (2), zone where an injection of                gaseous carbon dioxide is carried out, means making                it possible to organize the recirculation (5) of                part of the medium located in zone 1 towards zone 2                then from there its return to zone 1; a secondary                loop (3) for taking fluid from the tank and                returning the fluid to the tank, as well as means                for injecting gaseous carbon dioxide into the fluid                flowing in the loop; and means (6) allowing the                separation and the evacuation of the solid                particles formed in zone 1.

   ADVANTAGE - The installation: exhibits better control of                the target pH to carry out the precipitation;                recovers the consumption of carbon dioxide as a                by-product of manufacturing or combustion; and                guarantees a good efficiency in the precipitation                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                treating an industrial effluent loaded with calcium                and/or magnesium with a view to eliminating all or                part of the calcium and/or magnesium of it,                comprising the implementation of the following                measures the effluent to be treated is carried out                in a first zone, first zone where a pH is                preferably maintained between 10-12, so as to                promote the precipitation of calcium or magnesium                in the form of carbonate and thus facilitate its                elimination, there is a second zone comprising a                tank, we organize the recirculation of part of the                medium located in zone 1 to zone 2 and from there                its return to zone 1, there is a secondary loop for                taking fluid from the tank and return the fluid in                the tank, as well as means for injecting gaseous                carbon dioxide into the fluid flowing in the loop                and the solid particles formed in zone 1 are                separated and removed.

   Tank (2)
   Secondary loop (3)

PN CN109772359-A
TI Efficiently removing carbon monoxide in high water vapor and high carbon dioxide environment using catalyst composed of carrier and active component, the carrier composed of nickel(II) oxide, scandium dioxide and yttrium oxide.
AU AN J
AE AN J (ANJJ-Individual)
GA 201947026D
AB    NOVELTY - Efficiently removing carbon monoxide in high water vapor and high carbon dioxide environment using catalyst composed of carrier and active component, the carrier composed of nickel(II) oxide, scandium dioxide and yttrium oxide, and the active component composed of gallium, silver, manganese and copper. The catalyst is prepared by taking nickel hydroxide, scandium hydroxide, yttrium hydroxide and sucrose, grinding and mixing to obtain solid powder, adding into gallium nitrate, mixed solution of silver nitrate and aqueous solution contains gallium, drying after being impregnated, calcining in nitrogen atmosphere and calcining in air atmosphere to obtain silver/nickel(II) oxide, scandium dioxide and yttrium oxide catalyst of gallium, adding silver/nickel(II) oxide, scandium dioxide and yttrium oxide catalyst of gallium into manganese nitrate, copper nitrate mixed aqueous solution, aqueous solution contains manganese, copper, drying after impregnation and calcining in an air atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for efficiently removing carbon monoxide in high water vapor and high carbon dioxide environment using catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high catalytic activity, good stability, which eliminates the carbon monoxide in the smoke.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Efficiently removing carbon monoxide in high water vapor and high carbon dioxide environment using catalyst composed of carrier and active component, the carrier composed of nickel(II) oxide, scandium dioxide and yttrium oxide, and the active component composed of gallium, silver, manganese and copper. The catalyst is prepared by taking nickel hydroxide, scandium hydroxide, yttrium hydroxide and sucrose in a mass ratio of 55:30:5:10, grinding and mixing to obtain a solid powder, adding 100 g of solid powder into 100 ml of gallium nitrate, mixed solution of silver nitrate and the aqueous solution contains gallium in an amount of 0.1-0.3 g, the content of silver is 0.05-0.15 g, drying at 110 degrees C after being impregnated, then calcining at 500 degrees C in a nitrogen atmosphere and calcining at 500 degrees C in an air atmosphere to obtain silver/nickel(II) oxide, scandium dioxide and yttrium oxide catalyst of gallium, adding 100 g silver/nickel(II) oxide, scandium dioxide and yttrium oxide catalyst of gallium into 100 ml of manganese nitrate, copper nitrate mixed aqueous solution, the aqueous solution contains 0.2-0.5 g manganese, 0.05 g copper, drying at 110 degrees C after impregnation and calcining at 500 degrees C in an air atmosphere.

PN CN109439619-A
TI Primary culture of canine dermal fibroblasts, by adding skin tissue to clean bag, mixing slurry, observing digestive condition, centrifuging, resuspending sample in medium, filtering and culturing medium.
AU ZHANG A
AE HEFEI HUAGAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEIF-Non-standard)
GA 2019252928
AB    NOVELTY - Primary culture method for canine dermal fibroblasts, involves obtaining sterile surgical scissors to cut skin tissue (1 cmx 1 cm) from the chest and abdomen of adult dogs, adding the skin tissue into clean bag containing 75% alcohol, cleaning the skin tissue with double-resistant phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) solution, mixing the slurry at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, observing the digestive condition, centrifuging the mixture, discarding the supernatant, resuspending the sample in the medium, filtering the suspension with cell sieve, centrifuging the filtrate, discarding the supernatant, resuspending the cell culture medium, transferring the medium to well plate, adding culture solution, incubating the medium at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 48 hours, washing the plate with medium to remove impurities, replacing the fresh medium once a day, and continuing to culture until the canine dermal fibroblast primary cells are obtained.
   USE - The primary culture method is useful for canine dermal fibroblasts (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to perform.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Primary culture method for canine dermal fibroblasts, involves obtaining sterile surgical scissors to cut skin tissue (1 cmx 1 cm) from the chest and abdomen of adult dogs, adding the skin tissue into clean bag containing 75% alcohol, cleaning the skin tissue with double-resistant phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) solution, removing the subcutaneous fat and connective tissue with sterile scissors and forceps, continuing washing process with the above double-anti-PBS solution for 2-4 times, cutting the skin tissue, transferring the skin tissue into 5 ml centrifuge tube, adding 1 ml type II collagenase solution to 5 ml centrifuge tube, cutting the skin tissue into paste, transferring the tissue slurry to 15 ml centrifuge tube, adding 5 ml type II collagenase to the tube, mixing the slurry at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, observing the digestive condition at 2 hours intervals until the tissue slurry is completely digested, centrifuging the mixture, discarding the supernatant, resuspending the sample in the medium, filtering the suspension with cell sieve, centrifuging the filtrate, discarding the supernatant, resuspending the cell culture medium, transferring the medium to well plate, adding culture solution, incubating the medium at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 48 hours, washing the plate with medium to remove impurities, replacing the fresh medium once a day, and continuing to culture until the canine dermal fibroblast primary cells are obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves pre-cooling the double anti-PBS solution at 4 degrees C. The double antibody is penicillin or streptomycin. The concentration of penicillin and streptomycin in the double-anti-PBS solution is 100 U/ml. The mass concentration of the type II collagenase solution is 0.2%. The centrifugal speed is 600-800 revolutions per minute (rpm). The centrifugation time is 4-6 minutes. The method involves filtering the mixture twice, where the first is 100 mesh sieve and second is 200 mesh cell sieve. The medium is Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) medium. The cell culture solution is 10% fetal bovine serum and 1% double antibody to DMEM cell culture medium.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108640597-A
TI Preparing carbon-permeable concrete by using waste concrete involves using crushing equipment for preliminary crushing pretreatment, milling and sieving treatment of waste concrete to obtain desired coarse aggregate and fine aggregate.
AU WANG S
   JIANG H
   LU L
   YIN H
   ZHANG Z
AE XUZHOU INST TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 201881314G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-permeable concrete by using waste concrete involves using crushing equipment for preliminary crushing pretreatment, milling and sieving treatment of the waste concrete to obtain the desired coarse aggregate and fine aggregate. The industrial waste gas is first subjected to dust removal treatment through a dust filter, mixed dedusted industrial waste gas with normal temperature air, so that the temperature of the mixed industrial waste gas is 30-40 degrees C according to the performance parameters required for the permeable concrete, determined reinforcing agent, cement, water and the ratio of the coarse aggregate and the fine aggregate obtained, and then the reinforcing agent, cement, water, and coarse bone. The material and the fine aggregate are mixed and stirred in a mixing container to form a permeable concrete mixture, and the mixing and stirred for 10-15 minutes.
   USE - The method enables to prepare carbon-permeable concrete by using waste concrete which reduces content of the carbon dioxide in theindustrial waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon-permeable concrete by using waste concrete which reduces content of the carbon dioxide in theindustrial waste gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The industrial waste gas is continuously injected into the mixing container until the end of the mixing and stirred, carbonized, the permeable concrete mixture with carbon dioxide in industrial waste gas, and collecting industrial waste gas after carbonization reaction. The permeable concrete mixture in the pre-formed mold and ensure the vibrating quality required for the permeable concrete, placed pre-formed mold into the closed permeable concrete carbon-fixing chamber, and then injected into the permeable concrete carbon-fixing chamber. The industrial waste gas, the humidity of the permeable concrete carbon fixation chamber is 60-80% through the humidifier, so that the carbon dioxide reaction between the concrete mixture and the industrial waste gas continues to carbonize, and the industrial waste gas after the carbonization reaction is collected. The curing time of concrete in the prefabricated mold is 15 days. The industrial waste gas after the carbonization reaction collected E is mixed with the normal temperature air and industrial waste gas which has been dedusted, so that the temperature of the mixed industrial waste gas is 30-40 degrees C, injected into the permeable concrete carbon fixation chamber, circulated, and reciprocated until the concrete curing time is reached, final industrial waste gas is discharged, and the prepared permeable concrete is taken out from the prefabricated mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferrred Conditions: The permeable concrete carbon fixation chamber is a transparent rubber tank, and the volume is 3-5 times the volume of the prefabricated mold. The cement is ordinary Portland cement having a compressive strength of 42.5.

TI Preparing methanol by catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by reacting nitrogen-containing monodentate ligands, NN bidentate ligands or NNN tridentate ligands with metal salts, adding sodium borohydride and organic solvent and heating.
AU WANG W
   QIU L
   WANG Q
   BAO M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201868752T
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing methanol by catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by cheap metal complex involves reacting nitrogen-containing compounds as nitrogen-containing monodentate ligands, NN bidentate ligands or NNN tridentate ligands with metal salts in a ratio of 4-1:1, stirring the mixture to form metal complexes of formulae (Ia)-(If), adding 30-600 equivalents sodium borohydride and organic solvent, heating, adding hydrochloric acid to the reaction system, and stirring to terminate the reaction to obtain methanol. The concentration of the ligand is 0.001-0.1 mol/l. The NN bidentate ligands are N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethane-1,2-diamine, N,N'-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine, 2-aminoethylamine, 2-aminophenylamine, 1H,1'H-2,2'-biimidazole, 2,2'-bipyridine, 1-pyridin-2-ylmethanamine, 2,2'-bipyrimidine, 1,10-phenanthroline, N-(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)benzamide or 4-methyl-N-(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)benzenesulfonamide. The NNN tridentate ligands are N,N-bis(2-(dimethylamino)ethyl)-N-methylamine.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing methanol by catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by cheap metal complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy, economical and convenient preparation of methanol with high yield, under mild reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for preparing methanol by catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by cheap metal complex involves reacting nitrogen-containing compounds as nitrogen-containing monodentate ligands of formula: L1M1X1n, NN bidentate ligands of formula: L2M1X1n or NNN tridentate ligands of formula: L3M1X1n with metal salts in a ratio of 4-1:1, stirring the mixture for 2-5 minutes to form metal complexes of formulae (Ia)-(If), adding 30-600 equivalents sodium borohydride and organic solvent, heating at 25-70 degrees C for 6-24 hours, adding 0.5-2 ml 4 mol/l hydrochloric acid to the reaction system, and stirring for 0.5-2 hours to terminate the reaction to obtain methanol. The concentration of the ligand is 0.001-0.1 mol/l. The NN bidentate ligands are N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethane-1,2-diamine, N,N'-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine, 2-aminoethylamine, 2-aminophenylamine, 1H,1'H-2,2'-biimidazole, 2,2'-bipyridine, 1-pyridin-2-ylmethanamine, 2,2'-bipyrimidine, 1,10-phenanthroline, N-(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)benzamide or 4-methyl-N-(pyridin-2-ylmethyl)benzenesulfonamide. The NNN tridentate ligands are N,N-bis(2-(dimethylamino)ethyl)-N-methylamine.
   L1 = 1H-pyrazolyl or 1H-imidazolyl;
   M1 = Fe2+, Co2+, Ni2+, Cu2+, Zn2+ or Cu+;
   X1 = F-, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO42- or NO3-;and
   n = 1 or 2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, dioxane or acetonitrile.

TI Use of care composition in preparing medicinal composition for treating and regulating number of secretory vesicles in nasal goblet cells and inhibiting production of leukotrienes and prostaglandins.
AU SU J
AE SU J (SUJJ-Individual)
   CHI C (CHIC-Individual)
GA 202253354K
AB    NOVELTY - Use of a care composition in preparing a medicinal composition for treating and regulating the number of secretory vesicles in nasal goblet cells. The care composition includes stem and/or leaf extracts and ionic solutions of sweet potato leaves, jicama and yam.
   USE - The care composition is useful in preparing a medicinal composition for treating and regulating the number of secretory vesicles in nasal goblet cells, modulating the amount of secretory vesicles secreted, breaking down histamine and inhibiting the production of leukotrienes and prostaglandins (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The care composition is safe and effective to regulate the nasal environment without causing burden to the body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the care composition, comprising (i) placing sweet potato leaves, jicama and/or yam leaves in an oven, drying at 70 degrees C until moisture content is 5-7% to prevent organic matter from chelating metal ions, (ii) placing the dry sweet potato leaves, jicama and/or yam leaves in a planetary ball mill and grinding into powder of 0.1-1 mu m, (iii) pressurizing the liquid carbon dioxide to above the critical pressure (more than 72.9 atm) using a high pressure pump, and flowing through the heat exchanger to make the high pressure liquid carbon dioxide into a supercritical state, and then the supercritical carbon dioxide enters the chamber containing the sweet potato leaf powderm extracting the fat-soluble components in the sweet potato leaf powder in the high-pressure tank and performing static extraction for 20 minutes and dynamic extraction for 4 minutes, (iv) electrodialyzing the ionic solution to form an isotonic ionic solution, adding into a beaker, boiling, adding sweet potato leaves, jicama and/or yam leaf powder, covering with an aluminum foil, stirring with a magnet for 1 hour at 75 degrees C, dissolving the metal ions contained in the sweet potato leaves, jicama and/or yam leaf powders to achieve magnesium ion content of more than 0.25% (wt./wt.), and magnesium ion and potassium ion content of 4-17:1 (wt./wt.), (v) cooling the solution, feeding sufficient amount of carbon dioxide gas, forming sodium bicarbonate with the sodium ion in the solution, precipitating and making the ion concentration of the sodium ion content in the filtrate is less than 0.3% (wt./wt.), and (vi) pouring the sweet potato leaf, jicama and/or yam leaf solution containing sodium bicarbonate precipitation into a filter cup (pore size of 0.1 mu m) and carrying out vacuum filtration to obtain the filtrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The number of secretory vesicles reduced by regulation does not exceed 45%. The secretory vesicles within the goblet cells were regulated to increase the amount of mucus secretion by less than 240%. The ionic solution contains 0.3-1% magnesium ion, 0.02-0.2% potassium ion and less than 0.3% sodium ion with salinity of 0.9-4%. The concentration ratio of magnesium ion and potassium ion is 4-17:1 (wt./wt.). The care composition further comprises: an immunosuppressive, immunomodulatory or antiinflammatory drug; and iron, copper, zinc, iodine, chromium and/or molybdenum ions.
   ACTIVITY - Immunosuppressive; Immunomodulator; Antiinflammatory. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Leukotriene inhibitor; Prostaglandin synthetase inhibitor.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of preparation of the care composition that do not support the claims.

TI Processing polysilicon waste water by adjusting pH of polysilicon waste water, flocculating and precipitating, carrying out materialization treatment, removing weak acid ion, concentrating and separating, crystallizing and drying.
AU ZHOU T
   XU J
AE NANTONG YOUTUO NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2022048480
AB    NOVELTY - Processing polysilicon waste water comprises adjusting pH of polysilicon waste water by pre-processing tank, and carrying out flocculation precipitation treatment; carrying out materialization treatment to mixture of silicon powder and silicon carbide; filtering; removing weak acid ion, and conveying to carbon removing device to remove carbon dioxide; conveying filtrate after removing carbon dioxide by carbon removing device to reverse osmosis tank to concentrate and separate; conveying concentrated solution after being concentrated and separated by reverse osmosis tank to secondary sedimentation tank for flocculation precipitation treatment; evaporating and crystallizing high salt waste water; conveying sludge mixture which is flocculated and precipitated by secondary sedimentation tank to concentration tank for concentration treatment; conveying slurry concentrated in concentrating tank to filter press for solid-liquid separation; and pressing and drying.
   USE - The method is used for processing polysilicon waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and reliable, and reduces production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polysilicon waste water processing method comprises adjusting pH of polysilicon waste water by pre-processing tank, and using circulating pump to pump into first stage sedimentation tank for flocculation precipitation treatment; carrying out materialization treatment to mixture of silicon powder and silicon carbide after flocculation and precipitation of first-grade sedimentation tank; conveying supernatant after flocculation and precipitation by first-level sedimentation tank to filter for filtering; conveying filtrate filtered by filter to weak acid positive bed for removing weak acid ion, and conveying to carbon removing device to remove carbon dioxide; conveying filtrate after removing carbon dioxide by carbon removing device to reverse osmosis tank to concentrate and separate; conveying concentrated solution after being concentrated and separated by reverse osmosis tank to secondary sedimentation tank for flocculation precipitation treatment; evaporating and crystallizing high salt waste water after flocculation precipitation of secondary sedimentation tank; conveying sludge mixture which is flocculated and precipitated by secondary sedimentation tank to concentration tank for concentration treatment; conveying slurry concentrated in concentrating tank to filter press for solid-liquid separation; and conveying mud block pressed and pressed by filter press drier to dry
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - Pretreatment tank (1)

   Filter press (5)
   Drier (6)

   Flushing device (12)

   Fluid separator (14)
   Degassing tower (15)
   One-stage evaporator (16)

   Two-stage evaporator (18)

PT P
PN CN112973702-A; CN112973702-B
TI Catalyst used for producing high-carbon olefins from mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen comprises ferrous oxalate, alkali metal (sodium, potassium and rubidium), other metal (manganese, zinc, copper and cobalt) and carbon.
AU FANG C
   SUN J
   GE Q
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202172027H
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises ferrous oxalate, 1-10 wt.%, preferably 4-8 wt.% alkali metal, other metal and carbon, where the mass ratio of iron to carbon is 0.7:3-10:10, preferably 1.5:2.5-10:10, the molar ratio of iron to other metals is 10:0.01-10:5, preferably 10:0.5-10:2, the alkali metal comprising sodium, potassium and rubidium, and the other metals including manganese, zinc, copper and cobalt.
   USE - The catalyst useful for producing high-carbon olefins from mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improves the activity of the catalyst, selectivity of high carbon olefin product; provides idea for carbon dioxide selective hydrogenation to prepare high added value chemical conversion process; and has good industrial application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included for:
   (1) preparing catalyst comprising (i) ultrasonically dissolving ferrous oxalate salt in 0-4 degrees C cold hydrochloric acid, slowly dissolving, adding the alkali metal salt into the iron salt solution, ultrasonically stirring and dissolving, continuously adding the other metal salt into the iron salt solution, ultrasonically stirring and dissolving until the solution is clear, (ii) evaporating the above solution in a 60-80 degrees C water bath to evaporate excess hydrochloric acid and water to obtain a solid catalyst, (iii) further drying the obtained solid catalyst at 90-100 degrees C for 5-10 hours to obtain the catalyst powder, (iv) taking the catalyst powder and the carbon powder, mechanically grinding and mixing, tableting and forming; and
   (2) use of catalyst in carbon dioxide (CO2) hydrogenation for preparing high carbon linear alpha olefin reaction comprising performing activation process by adding catalyst into a fixed bed reactor, pre-treating using hydrogen/argon (H2/Ar) mixed gas with mol ratio of 1-50% or hydrogen/nitrogen (H2/N2) mixed gas or pure hydrogen with mol ratio of 1-50%, where the pre-treatment comprising ensuring the temperature of 350-450 degrees C, at a pressure of 0-2 MPa, activating for 1-50 hours, the reaction process comprising reducing the temperature in the fixed bed reactor to 300-350 degrees C after finishing the activation process, introducing the raw material gas of H2:CO2 with mol ratio of 5:1-2:1 in the reactor, and reacting at a reaction pressure of 0.5-3.0 MPa, and feeding air speed of 1000-20000/h.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon in the catalyst is active carbon or carbon nanotube. The alkali metal salt is carbonate or acetate. The other metal salt is carbonate, acetate or nitrate. Preferred Method: The method comprises mechanically grinding and mixing ferrous oxalate and carbon, then supporting alkali metal salts and other metals on the mixture by soaking to obtain composite catalyst immediately after drying. The method comprises placing alkali metal salt and other metal on the carbon by dipping method, grinding and mixing dried powder catalyst with ferrous oxalate to obtain the composite catalyst. The method comprises performing activation process by filling the catalyst into a fixed bed reactor, pre-treating at 350-400 degrees C at a pressure of 0-5 MPa, activating for 5-10 h, reacting by reducing the temperature in the fixed bed reactor to 320 degrees C, and introducing H2: CO2 with mol ratio of 3:1 into the fixed bed reactor, at a reaction pressure of 1.0-1.5 MPa and feeding space velocity of 2000-4000 h-1.

TI Insect trap, has trapping device provided with            shell that is formed with containing cavity, and carbon            dioxide generator whose two sides are provided with            lug, where opening part of shell is formed with            avoiding groove that is matched with lug.
AU ZHENG J
   NING Y
AE NINGBO DAYANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGBO DAYANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGBO DAYANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2020C6041M
AB    (CN212116770-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims an insect trap,                comprising a trap and the upper end is provided                with an opening of the trapping box; the trapping                box is provided with a killing assembly; the                trapping box is set below the trap; the box bottom                of the trapping box is connected with the trap; a                space for the insect to pass through is left                between the trapping device and the upper edge of                the trapping box. The insect trap can trap the                insect around the movable insect in the trapping                box, which can effectively avoid the insect                climbing on the trap.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of                the three-dimensional structure of the insect                trap.
   Insect trap (1)
   Trapping box (2)
   Space (4)
EA    NOVELTY - The insect trap (1) has a trapping box (2)                which is provided with an opening in the upper end.                The trapping box is provided with a trapping                assembly. The trapping device has a shell with a                containing cavity provided with carbon dioxide                generator. The lower wall of the shell has a                through hole on the upper portion of the trap. The                generator is located above the through hole and has                an air outlet hole that is butted with one end of                the hole. The through hole has a conduit whose one                end is connected with the outlet hole and the other                end to the outside.
   USE - Insect trap for trapping insect i.e. small                night activity insect, of a bug family. Can also be                used for a tropical sting.
   ADVANTAGE - The trap can trap the insect around the                movable insect in the trapping box, which can                effectively avoid the insect climbing on the trap.                The trap is simple and compact, and can reasonably                reduce the production cost and the volume of the                product. The air outlet hole of the carbon dioxide                generator of the insect trap of the utility model                is butt jointed with one end of the through hole,                so that the structure of the conduit is saved. The                structure of trapping box is simplified, and the                structure is more compact, so as to reduce the cost                and volume of product.

PN DE102018213950-A1
TI Device for using residual carbon dioxide for            methane production, has catalysts or microbial            settlement areas which are made from organic plastics,            minerals or metallic substances with very large            specific surface area.
AU 
AE PRE POWER RECYCLING ENERGYSERVICE GMBH (PREP-Non-standard)
GA 2020146472
AB    NOVELTY - The device has reactors which comprise                anaerobic methane-forming bacteria from sludge                granules. The catalysts or microbial settlement                areas are made from organic plastics, minerals or                metallic substances with a very large specific                surface area. The reactor rooms are stacked several                times in a micro-column to form a multiple reactor.                The catalysts are various bulk materials made of                plastics, minerals or metallic substances that                enlarge the microbial settlement surface and are                layered in a steel basket. The micro-column has a                combination with the stratification of granulate                catalyst and microbial settlement reaction space.                The stratification takes place vertically one above                the other, in interchangeable filling shafts as                filling baskets with liquid and gas permeable                bottoms. The micro-columns as a slide-in system and                with sliding devices represent an equipped basic                module that is expanded to a container                vessel.
   USE - Device for using residual carbon dioxide for                methane production in biogas plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The improved method and corresponding device                for the methanation of carbon dioxide are provided.                The process takes place at both normal and elevated                temperatures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The container vessel is provided with                openable, gas and liquid-tight wall components and                is made of H2-tight weldable plastic material                composite materials. The micro-columns comprise                gas-conducting inserts, which are introduced in                layers with the layering as a spiral segment. The                gas premixing chambers are placed externally                centrally or integrated in the container below the                gas-permeable intermediate floors. The membrane                plate aerators are located below the permeable                column bottom. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                a method for using residual carbon dioxide (CO2)                for methane (CH4) production involves passing                biogas which contains CH4 and CO2 through the layer                with the sludge granulate and then through the                layer with the catalysts and settlement areas. The                pressure is increased to approximately 1.2 bar by                the vertical stratification of the basic reactor                units per container unit. The device realizes                variable methane content according to the intended                use of the final gas at the outlet of the device,                which is between the original content and a methane                content of approximately 98%. The use of excess                electricity for hydrogen production and the use of                hydrogen in the device increases the methane                concentration in biogas plants, bio-methane plants                and anaerobic fermentation plants.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                method for using the turbo methane generator.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN110777091-A
TI Developing efficient bio-energy with carbon            capture and storage system using bicarbonate as link by            selecting chemical absorbent, treating spirulina, and            performing formal training experiments by using Zarrouk            medium without carbon source.
AU SONG C
   LI M
   WEI Y
   SUN C
   WANG Y
   HUANG D
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202015691R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for developing an efficient bio-energy                with carbon capture and storage (BECCS) system                using bicarbonate as a link involves (a) selecting                chemical absorbent by selecting and generating                sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate after                completely absorbing carbon dioxide, and setting                three different concentrations, (b) performing                spirulina treatment, and (c) performing formal                training experiments using 20 ml Zarrouk medium                without carbon source as basic medium, and adding                different concentrations of sodium carbonate and                potassium carbonate (0.1 mol/l, 0.2 mol/l and 0.3                mol/l) as carbon sources, sterilizing at 121                degrees C for 30 minutes, and culturing under light                intensity of 4000 lux, temperature of 30 plus minus                1 degrees C, and full-day light conditions for 18                days.
   USE - The method is useful for developing efficient                BECCS system using bicarbonate as a link.
   ADVANTAGE - The method develops efficient BECCS system in                a less energy-consuming manner and improves carbon                utilization efficiency of microalgae during                desorption process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for developing an efficient bio-energy                with carbon capture and storage (BECCS) system                using bicarbonate as a link involves (a) selecting                chemical absorbent by selecting and generating                sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate after                completely absorbing carbon dioxide, and setting                three different concentrations of 0.1 mol/l, 0.2                mol/l and 0.3 mol/l, respectively, (b) performing                spirulina treatment by pre-culturing the spirulina                in ordinary Zarrouk medium, setting temperature at                30 degrees C, light intensity of 4000 lux,                introducing 5% carbon dioxide mixed gas (5% carbon                dioxide + 95% nitrogen) or air at a flow rate of                0.1 volume of air per volume medium (vvm),                introducing the microalgae into pre-culture phase,                aerating, growing the spirulina to the logarithmic                growth phase, terminating the pre-culture phase,                using the cultured algae species as the mother                inoculum for formal experiments, and controlling                the inoculation amount to 0.5 at optical density                (OD)560, and (c) performing formal training                experiments by using 20 ml Zarrouk medium without                carbon source as the basic medium, and adding                different concentrations of sodium carbonate and                potassium carbonate (0.1 mol/l, 0.2 mol/l and 0.3                mol/l) as carbon sources, sterilizing at 121                degrees C for 30 minutes, and culturing under light                intensity of 4000 lux, temperature of 30 plus minus                1 degrees C, and full-day light conditions for 18                days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves                  treating spirulina by nuclear radiation.

PN DE102018002374-A1
TI Three-pass blowing device useful for recycling top gas or export gas, e.g. top gas of oxygen blast furnace or export gas of Corex which are fed to smelting reduction plant in the upper hearth area through central.
AU AASLEPP H
AE AASLEPP H (AASL-Individual)
GA 201982110D
AB    NOVELTY - Three-pass blowing device for recycling top gas or export gas, is claimed. The top gas of an oxygen blast furnace or export gas of a Corex (RTM: Smelting reduction process) is fed to the smelting reduction plant in the upper hearth area through the central channel of the blowing device as reducing gas by recycling the carbon dioxide-free after carbon dioxide separation at ambient temperature, at the blast furnace in the blow molding plane. Oxygen, methane and pulverized coal separated from the recirculated reducing gas are injected through the outer channel of the device and burned. The natural gas is generated between a quasi-inert separation jet by oxidation of fuel gas with oxygen to water and carbon dioxide, for separating the core jet of recycled gas from the outer combustion jet of oxygen and a fuel gas.
   USE - The blowing device is useful for recycling top gas or export gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has environmentally friendly operation of blast furnace only with pure oxygen and top gas recycling with post-oxidation of recycled carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide by oxygen of previously unreduced iron ore; decreases carbon input, and amount of exhaust gas; does not require height adjustment; and reduces availability of lance which can be excluded by metal splashes, and other temperature-related damage due to design.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) two-pass injection device for blowing the combustion oxygen onto the foamed slag in iron bath smelting reduction plants, e.g. in Hisarna smelting reduction vessel, where a mixture of oxygen and combustible gas, e.g. natural gas is injected in stoichiometric ratio to the combustion and formation of a coherent supersonic jet through the central channel oxygen by a convergent-divergent nozzle (Laval nozzle) at supersonic speed and through the outer annular channel;
   (2) blowing device, comprising nozzle listed under item 4 stirring fiber-reinforced ceramic;
   (3) two-pass inflator for inflating combustion oxygen on foamed slag in iron bath smelting reduction plants, in a Hisarna smelting reduction vessel, through central channel oxygen by convergent-divergent nozzle at supersonic speed, and through outer annular channel injecting mixture of oxygen, and combustible gas, e.g. injecting natural gas in stoichiometric ratio to combustion and formation of coherent supersonic jet blowing device; and
   (4) coaxial injection device for reduction processes, which are used for separately einzublasende and to be burned, gases and solid-gas mixtures.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27))
