PT P
PN CN109527083-A
TI Storing rice comprises processing the rice storage bin, screening rice, sterilizing, ultra-high pressure treatment, transferring treated rice to treated storage bin, discharging the gas, refilling carbon dioxide, sealing and stabilizing.
AU YANG L
AE YINGSHANG WANRUN RICE CO LTD (YING-Non-standard)
GA 2019312258
AB    NOVELTY - Storing rice comprises firstly selecting rice storage bin, then processing the rice storage bin by cleaning and disinfecting, passing the rice to be stored to a vibrating screening machine for screening, then treating the screened rice by controlling the moisture content and sterilizing the rice, passing the processed rice to the packaging machine for vacuum packaging, and then passing the packaged rice to a ultra-high pressure equipment for ultra-high pressure treatment, transferring the ultra-high pressure treated rice to the treated storage bin, discharging the gas inside the storage bin after 7-8 days of storage period of the rice, irradiating the rice, then refilling the carbon dioxide, sealing the rice storage bin, and stabilizing the temperature and humidity of the air inside the rice storage bin by connecting the rice storage bin to the artificial climate chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for storing rice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method changes the structure of linear and branched amylopectin by high pressure treatment; inhibits the formation of surface coating of the rice grain and avoids the aging of rice; has high speed of sterilization and shortens the processing cycle of people by cooperating with microwave equipment and ultraviolet lamp; can inhibit the pests and bacteria inside the rice by treating the rice with radiation; prolong the preservation time of rice, and bring better use prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Storing rice comprises (a) firstly selecting a rice storage bin, then processing the rice storage bin, where the processing of the rice storage bin comprises cleaning and disinfecting, (b) passing the rice to be stored to a vibrating screening machine for screening, then treating the screened rice by controlling the moisture content and sterilizing the rice, (c) passing the processed rice to the packaging machine for vacuum packaging, and then passing the packaged rice to a ultra-high pressure equipment for performing ultra-high pressure treatment, (d) transferring the ultra-high pressure treated rice to the treated storage bin, then adjusting the internal temperature of the storage bin and charging the carbon dioxide, (e) discharging the gas inside the storage bin after 7-8 days of storage period of the rice, irradiating the rice, then refilling the carbon dioxide, and sealing the rice storage bin, and (f) stabilizing the temperature and humidity of the air inside the rice storage bin by connecting the rice storage bin to the artificial climate chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The processing steps of the rice storage bin in the step (a) comprises (i) firstly rinsing the surface of the rice storage bin by using a high-pressure spray gun, then opening the door of the rice storage bin and allowing to stand for 1-2 days, (b) then spraying the disinfectant solution into the rinsed rice storage bin, where the concentration ratio of disinfectant and water is 1:9, and (c) fumigating the disinfected rice storage bin, where fumigant is aluminum phosphide, and ventilating for 2-4 days after fumigating for 6-8 days. The step (b) comprises (i) firstly placing the screened rice in a container filled with pure water for soaking, and soaking for 10-15 minutes, where the water temperature during soaking is 35-45 degrees C, and (b) passing the immersed rice to a vacuum low-temperature dryer for drying, where the vacuum inside the vacuum low-temperature drying oven is -94 to -98 MPa, and drying for 20-40 minutes. The step (b) comprises coordinating the rice sterilization by the rice microwave sterilization machine and ultraviolet lamp, where the microwave sterilizer has a microwave power of 200-400 W and treating for 2-4 minutes. The step (c) comprises packaging by using vacuum packaging machine before the rice is processed by the ultra-high pressure equipment, then passing the vacuum-packed rice to the pressure vessel filled with pure water, where the ultrahigh pressure treatment is carried out at 15 degrees C under a pressure of 200-600 MPa for 8-12 minutes. The step (d) comprises firstly heating the ground inside the rice storage bin before transporting the rice to the rice storage bin, then installing the rice placement rack on the increased ground, then placing the rice on the rice placement rack, separating the rice by 3-5 cm, then paving quicklime around the heated ground, and controlling the temperature inside the rice storage bin to 10-15 degrees C. The step (e) comprises using the static irradiation process while irradiating rice with gamma -ray irradiation source, turning the sample 180 degrees at half of the absorbed dose to ensure uniform absorption of the sample, where the irradiation dose is 0.4-0.8 kGy, and the content of carbon dioxide in the air inside the rice storage bin is 15-20% after being filled with carbon dioxide. The step (f) comprises controlling the temperature inside the rice storage bin by artificial climate chamber to 20 degrees C, and controlling the relative humidity to 65-70%.

PN CN108285987-A
TI Preparing copper oxide-vanadium carbide particle reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material comprises e.g. blowing mixed powder of activated carbon, graphite and copper powder into molten magnesium alloy melt and reacting.
AU XU S
   WANG H
   YANG X
   CAO Z
   MENG Z
   LI Z
   WANG X
AE UNIV SHANDONG JIANZHU (SDSJ-C)
GA 201857023J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper oxide-vanadium carbide particle reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material comprises blowing mixed powder of activated carbon, graphite, vanadium pentoxide powder, petroleum coke and copper powder into molten magnesium alloy melt by mixed gas of carbon dioxide, methane, argon and oxygen and then reacting to obtain copper oxide-vanadium carbide reinforced particles, then adding silver powder into magnesium alloy melt, mechanically mixing, modifying, refining, casting and deforming through an equal channel angular extrusion having back pressure of mold channel at corner of 75 degrees to obtain ingot having high strength nano-copper oxide-vanadium carbide particle-reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material, where e.g. preparation steps are: (i) weighing magnesium alloy base materials: carbon, silicon, manganese, aluminum and magnesium, (ii) taking mixed gases are: methane, argon, oxygen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The alloy material is useful in human body, aerospace, automotive, computer, mobile phone, camera, high strength and high wear resistance high-end racing car.
   ADVANTAGE - The alloy material has high intensity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing copper oxide-vanadium carbide particle reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material comprises blowing mixed powder of activated carbon, graphite, vanadium pentoxide powder, petroleum coke and copper powder into molten magnesium alloy melt by mixed gas of carbon dioxide, methane, argon and oxygen and then reacting to obtain copper oxide-vanadium carbide reinforced particles, then adding silver powder into magnesium alloy melt, mechanically mixing, modifying, refining, casting and deforming through an equal channel angular extrusion having back pressure of mold channel at corner of 75 degrees to obtain ingot having high strength nano-copper oxide-vanadium carbide particle-reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material, where preparation steps are: (i) weighing magnesium alloy base materials: 0.1-0.8 wt.% carbon, 1-1.9 wt.% silicon, 0.1-0.6 wt.% manganese, 4-6 wt.% aluminum and magnesium (balance amount), (ii) taking mixed gases are: methane (8-45), argon (8-35), oxygen (8-35) and carbon dioxide (8-70), where pressure of mixed gas is 0.05-0.3 MPa, and gas flow rate is 0.02-0.15 m3/minute, (iii) calcining vanadium pentoxide powder, anhydrous ethanol and activated carbon powder in vacuum furnace at high temperature of 1550-1800 degrees C for 3 hours, cooling with furnace and then grinding to obtain ultrafine vanadium pentoxide powder and vanadium carbide mixed powder particle having particle size of 40-100 nm, and (iv) mechanically ball milling for 24 hours to obtain silver powder fine particle having particle size of 40-100 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium alloy base material melt contains silicon element having mass percentage of 1-1.9 and aluminum element having mass percentage of 4-6, and temperature of magnesium alloy melt is 700-800 degrees C. Preferred Method: The time to react to form enhanced particles is 15-60 minutes, and higher the particle content, then longer the reaction produces enhanced particles. The method comprises adding silver powder into melt of copper oxide-vanadium carbide microparticle-reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material in smelting, where mass content of silver powder is 1% in copper oxide-vanadium carbide particle-reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material, then adding silver powder and copper oxide-vanadium carbide microparticle-reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material melt in a mass ratio of 1:99, smelting, mechanially mixing, modifying, refining and casting to obtain copper oxide-vanadium carbide microparticle-reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material. The method comprises deforming at 400-500 degrees C through an equal channel angular extrusion (ECAP) having back pressure of mold channel at corner of 75 degrees to obtain ingot i.e. copper oxide-vanadium carbide particle reinforced antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material having high strength and toughness. The copper oxide-vanadium carbide particle enhances the combination of copper and silver metal in antibacterial medical magnesium alloy material having broad-spectrum antibacterial properties and improves the medical function of material.

PN CN107954818-A
TI Device for preparing ethylene and BTX, comprises preheating mixer having gas inlet and preheated mixture outlet, where upper portion of preheater is provided with multiple gas distribution pipes, and gas inlets are respectively connected.
AU LIU Q
   YAN C
   XU M
   WU D
AE BEIJING HUAFU ENG CO LTD (BJSW-C)
GA 201833026U
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises preheating mixer (1100) having gas inlet and preheated mixture outlet (1200), where upper portion of the preheater is provided with multiple gas distribution pipes, gas inlets are respectively connected to an acetylene supply device, carbon dioxide supply device (5000), hydrogen supply device (6000), methane supply device, and ethane supply device (4000). The fixed-bed reactor includes heating furnace.
   USE - Device used for preparing ethylene and BTX.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises preheating mixer (1100) having gas inlet and preheated mixture outlet (1200), where upper portion of the preheater is provided with multiple gas distribution pipes, gas inlets are respectively connected to an acetylene supply device, carbon dioxide supply device (5000), hydrogen supply device (6000), methane supply device, and ethane supply device (4000). The fixed-bed reactor includes heating furnace and a reaction tube, reaction tube is vertically penetratingly disposed in the heating furnace, and reaction tube includes ceramic reaction tube and is filled in the ceramic reaction tube. The regenerative ball and a stainless steel sleeve sheathed outside the ceramic reaction tube, where ceramic reaction tube has a preheated mixed gas inlet at its top end and a reaction product outlet at the bottom, and the preheated mixed gas inlet and preheated mixed gas outlet is connected, ceramic reaction tube defines a preheat zone, a thermal reaction zone and a low temperature zone in order from top to bottom, and the inner wall of the ceramic reaction tube has silanized coating. The condensate separation system having reaction product inlet, ethylene outlet, BTX outlet, and coke outlet, and reaction product inlet is connected to the reaction product outlet. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing ethylene and BTX, which involves:
   (A) suppying acetylene, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, methane, and ethane into reactor in the molar ratio of (1-2):(1-2):(1-3.5):(1-2.5):(0.3-0.8) to obtain a preheated mixture;
   (B) supplying a preheated mixture to the fixed bed reactor for thermal reaction at 820-960 degrees C to obtain a reaction product; and
   (C) subjecting reaction product to a condensation separation system for condensation and separation treatment to obtain final product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of ethylene and BTX preparing device.
   Preheating mixer (1100)
   Preheated mixture outlet (1200)
   Ethane supply device (4000)
   Carbon dioxide supply device (5000)
   Hydrogen supply device (6000)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The length-diameter ratio of ceramic reaction tube is (70-90):(4-15), optionally length ratio of the pre-heating area, heat reaction region and low temperature region is (25-35):(10-15):(30-45). The silanized coating has a thickness of 0.05-0.3 millimeter. The silanized coating on the inner wall of the ceramic reaction tube is prepared by cleaning inner surface of ceramic reaction tube with acetone and methanol, washing with water and dried. The inner surface of the ceramic reaction tube dried is washed with alkaline solution, acid solution and water in sequence, and then dried and calcined, brushed or dip coated inner surface of the ceramic reaction tube, baked with the silylation solution so as to form a wet film on the inner surface of the ceramic reaction tube. The ceramic reaction tube having the wet film formed is dried and calcined under inert gas protection, and repeated for 1-5 times. The silylated solution is obtained by dissolving silylating agent in an organic solven, silylating agent is methyl orthosilicate, ethyl orthosilicate or silicon tetrachloride, where organic solvent is n-hexane or cyclohexane, optionally silylated solution has a concentration of 0.5-3 mol/L, and dipping time is 10-60 minutes. Preferred Components: The surface of the heat storage sphere has a silanized coating.

PN CN113416090-A; CN113416090-B
TI Preparation of breathable mortar containing superabsorbent resin (SAP) pore system capable of carbon dioxide capture, involves placing SAP-containing mortar in test mold to form, demolding and curing in curing box, and changing SAP content and water absorption capacity to obtain SAP pore systems.
AU YANG H
   WANG W
   JIANG M
   ZHANG G
   NIU Z
   GAO Y
   SUN H
   SUN S
   YUAN W
AE UNIV SHIJIAZHUANG TIEDAO (USTD-C)
GA 2021B23719
AB    NOVELTY - A preparation of a breathable mortar, involves (1) putting the cement and drying SAP particles in a mortar mixer and dry mixing them until they are evenly mixed, (2) adding fine sand and continuing dry mixing until the raw materials are evenly mixed, and then adding the mixing water with water reducing agent, (3) after SAP absorbs water and expands and the workability of the mortar is stable, placing the SAP-containing mortar in a test mold to form, after hardening the mortar, demolding and curing in a curing box, performing reduction of internal humidity of mortar in curing process to cause SAP particles to shrink and generate SAP hole system, (4) changing the SAP content and water absorption capacity in the mortar to obtain SAP pore systems with different characteristics, and (5) putting the cured mortar test block in the environment, reducing difficulty of carbon dioxide diffusion in mortar to improve capture efficiency of carbon dioxide, in presence of SAP hole system.
   USE - Preparation of breathable mortar containing superabsorbent resin (SAP) pore system capable of carbon dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and effective, and the presence of SAP holes accelerates the diffusion of carbon dioxide in the mortar. The SAP particles in the SAP pores can store moisture when they meet with water, so as to provide a suitable humidity environment for the carbonization reaction. The breathable mortar prepared by the method has excellent carbon dioxide capture efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cement selected in step (1) is P.O 42.5 cement.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The selected SAP is granular, and its material is acrylic cross-linked acrylamide. Preferred Composition: The mixing amount of SAP is 2-5% of the cement mass, and the way of adding SAP is to mix evenly with the cement. Preferred Components: The water reducing agent used in step (2) is a high-performance polycarboxylic acid water reducing agent, the water-binder ratio of the prepared mortar is 0.5-0.8. Preferred Process: In step (3) the SAP pore system is prepared by using the expansion and contraction behavior of SAP. The characteristics of the SAP pore system in step (4) is adjusted by adjusting the water absorption capacity and dosage of SAP.

PT P
PN US2021138433-A1; US11185843-B2
TI Zirconium metal-organic framework used for capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixture, is coordination product formed between zirconium ion clusters and linker comprising benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid-containing compound.
AU HELAL A
   USMAN M
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2021502903
AB    NOVELTY - A zirconium metal-organic framework is a coordination product formed between zirconium ion clusters and a linker that links together adjacent zirconium ion clusters. The linker comprises benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid-containing compound (I).
   USE - Zirconium metal-organic framework used for capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixture (claimed) emitted from large fossil fuel or biomass electricity power plants, natural gas processing, synthetic fuel plants, and fossil fuel-based hydrogen production plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The zirconium metal-organic framework has excellent surface properties such as BET surface area and pore volume and selectively absorbs carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A zirconium metal-organic framework is a coordination product formed between zirconium ion clusters and a linker that links together adjacent zirconium ion clusters. The linker comprises benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid-containing compound of formula (I).
   R1 = H or optionally substituted alkyl;and#R2-R4 = H, or alkyl, aryl, or arylalkyl (all optionally substituted).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of the zirconium metal-organic framework, which involves mixing a zirconium(IV) salt and the linker (I) in a polar aprotic solvent to form a complexation mixture and heating the complexation mixture at 100-150degrees Celsius for 12-72 hours; and
   (2) method of capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixture, which involves contacting the gas mixture with the zirconium metal-organic framework to adsorb at least a portion of the carbon dioxide into the zirconium metal-organic framework, thereby forming a loaded zirconium metal-organic framework and a gas stream depleted in carbon dioxide compared to the gas mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) is preferably of formula (II). The molecular ratio of the zirconium ion and linker is 0.5:1-2:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zirconium ion cluster comprises zirconium oxyhydroxide of formula: (Zr6O4(OH)4)12+. The zirconium metal-organic framework is isoreticular with metal-organic framework UiO-66. The zirconium(IV) salt is zirconium chloride. The gas mixture further comprises at least one other gas chosen from hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, methane, and carbon monoxide. The gas stream depleted in carbon dioxide contains 25% or more carbon dioxide by volume compared to a volume of carbon dioxide present in the gas mixture. The gas mixture is a pre-combustion gas mixture comprising 15-50 vol.% carbon dioxide, based on a total volume of the gas mixture, or post-combustion gas mixture comprising 5-15 vol.% carbon dioxide based on a total volume of the gas mixture. Preferred Properties: The zirconium metal-organic framework has BET surface area of 300-600 m2/g, pore volume of 0.1-0.25 cm3/g, carbon dioxide uptake capacity of 75-90 cm3/g at 273 K, 40-60 cm3/g at 298 K, and 20-35 cm3/g at 313 K, each at 760 Torr, ideal selectivity of carbon dioxide/nitrogen of 55-75, and ideal selectivity of carbon dioxide/methane of 10-22. The gas mixture has temperature of -5-50degrees Celsius.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.)

PN CN111560252-A; CN111560252-B
TI Carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch type microemulsion for cleaning high-concentration naphthenic crude oil contaminated soil, comprises (anionic sulfonate-long-chain tertiary amine-n-butanol)-n-heptane-water system microemulsion.
AU LIU D
   FENG X
   DAI H
   GUO S
   ZHU W
   SHI N
   WEN F
   LI G
   LI Z
   GUO A
   WU B
   DU H
   ZHAO N
   QIU Y
   CHAI L
   WANG S
   DING Y
   CUI J
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 202083023L
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch type microemulsion comprises (anionic sulfonate-long-chain tertiary amine-n-butanol)-n-heptane-water system microemulsion which is obtained by compounding anionic sulfonate, long-chain tertiary amine, n-butanol and n-heptane. The anionic sulfonate is prepared by using400-480 degrees C naphthenic crude oil and fuming sulfuric acid as sulfonating agent. The long-chain tertiary amine is stearylamidopropyl-N,N-dimethyl tertiary amine.
   USE - Carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch type microemulsion for cleaning high-concentration naphthenic crude oil contaminated soil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch type microemulsion can realize the high-efficiency separation of the three phases of soil, water and naphthenic crude oil, and is economical and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for cleaning high-concentration naphthenic crude oil contaminated soil, which involves mixing high-concentration naphthenic crude oil contaminated soil with the carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch type microemulsion, stirring at 25 plus minus 0.1 degrees C and leaving still for 1-4 hours, filtering to separate soil and liquid where the content of naphthenic crude oil in the soil is less than 1.0 wt.%, pouring carbon dioxide into the liquid at a rate of 20 mL/minutes under the condition of 25 plus minus 0.1 degrees C, and shaking for 15 minutes to break the emulsion, leaving still for stratification, separating the naphthenic crude oil from the aqueous phase, and discharging residual aqueous phase to environmental standards after existing treatment by ion exchange and adsorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The added amount of the fuming sulfuric acid is 2-5 times the volume of the raw oil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The (anionic sulfonate-long-chain tertiary amine-n-butanol)-n-heptane-water system microemulsion is obtained by compounding 13.0 %mass anionic sulfonate, 8.0 %mass long-chain tertiary amine, 6.5 %mass n-butanol, 28.0 %mass n-heptane and 44.5 %mass water. The content of the naphthenic crude oil in the high-concentration naphthenic crude oil contaminated soil is 20 wt.% or more. The high-concentration naphthenic crude oil contaminated soil and the carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch type microemulsion are mixed in a ratio of 10 g:40 mL. Preferred Component: In the cleaning method, dichloromethane used to extract the washed oil soil. Preferred Process: The preparation of the anionic sulfonate involves taking 400-480 degrees C naphthenic crude oil as raw oil, adding 1,2-dichloroethane solvent to the raw oil in a volume ratio of 1:1, stirring until the reaction system reaches a constant temperature, using constant pressure dropping funnel to control the speed at 57 seconds/drop, adding 20 wt.% fuming sulfuric acid, and reacting at 35-65 degrees C for 1-4 hours, adding aqueous ammonia in drops to neutralize the pH to 7-8 at 25 plus minus 0.1 degrees C, using separating funnel to separate the aqueous layer and 1,2-dichloroethane layer, and performing vacuum distillation of 1,2-dichloroethane layer to remove solvent. In the cleaning method, the weight method used to calculate the oil content before and after cleaning.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H03 (Transportation and storage - only large scale systems are included.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 3H-pyran-2,6-dione compound of formula (I) by using carbon dioxide, comprises adding 2-butenone derivative of formula (II) into a reaction kettle, then adding organic alkali and reaction solvent, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting by stirring at 0-50 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours, allowing to stand at room temperature, slowly releasing unreacted gas, concentrating reaction solution under reduced pressure to obtain crude product and purifying crude product to obtain (I).
   R1 = phenyl, p-methylphenyl, p-tert-butylphenyl, 4-cyclohexylphenyl, p-methoxyphenyl, p-phenylphenyl, naphthyl, p-fluorophenyl, p-chlorophenyl, p-bromophenyl, 2-furyl, 2-thienyl, methyl, isohexenyl or cyclopropanyl;

The reaction kettle is intermittent high-pressure reaction kettle or continuous high-pressure reaction kettle. The method comprises purifying crude product by column chromatography using eluent or recrystallizing using solvent, where the column chromatography eluent or recrystallizing solvent is ethyl acetate or a mixed solvent of ethyl acetate and acetic acid in a volume ratio of 100:0-10.

The                reaction device comprises a reactor and a heating                device, and the reactor is housed in reactor. The                reaction solution is a sodium chlorite solution,                and the gas washing device contains a solid sodium                chlorite.
   USE - The device is useful for preparing high-purity                chlorine dioxide solution (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: can solve existing preparation                methods must use liquid or solid acids belonging to                dangerous goods, has high energy consumption, has                higher purity and is environmentally                friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing high-purity chlorine dioxide solution                comprising (i) passing carbon dioxide from a carbon                dioxide storage device into a sodium chlorite                solution in reactor, the carbon dioxide inlet                pressure is 0.05-2 MPa, mass fraction of the solute                of sodium chlorite solution is 3-10%, and (ii) as                carbon dioxide continues to flow into the sodium                chlorite solution, perform heating reaction to                prepare chlorine dioxide gas, where the heating                temperature is 30-50 degrees C, after washing                chlorine dioxide gas with solid sodium chlorite in                a gas washing bottle, and obtaining high-purity                chlorine dioxide gas which is absorbing by pure                water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of                the device for preparing a high-purity chlorine                dioxide solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  purity of carbon dioxide used is not less than                  99.98%. The mass ratio of iron(III)                  chloride and cobalt(II) chloride mixture in the                  sodium chlorite solution is 3-10%, and mixing                  ratio of two is an arbitrary ratio. In the step                  (ii), the heating time is 15-30 minutes. In the                  step (ii), the concentration of chlorine dioxide                  solution is 1000-1500 mg/l. In the step (ii), the                  purity of the high-purity chlorine dioxide                  solution is not less than 98%, and yield of the                  chlorine dioxide in the obtained high-purity                  chlorine dioxide solution is 25-30%.

   USE - The method and apparatus are useful for purifying acidic gas containing hydrogen sulfide (all claimed) which is used for high-value-added products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and apparatus improve gas purification efficiency and reduce load of gas purification facility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for apparatus comprising a gas generating source for generating an acidic gas containing hydrogen sulfide, a desulfurization unit connected to the gas generating source and a carbon dioxide removing unit for removing sulfur components from acidic gas containing hydrogen sulfide to obtain a clean gas and an exhaust gas, a carbon dioxide removal unit connected to the desulfurization unit and separating the carbon dioxide from the flue gas obtained in the desulfurization step into a salt form and separating it into flue gas and salt, where the flue gas obtained in the carbon dioxide removing unit is introduced into the desulfurization equipment together with the acid gas containing hydrogen sulfide discharged from the gas generating source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises removing impurities from the acidic gas containing hydrogen sulfide before the step (b), and removing impurities using an absorbent in gas obtained in the step (d). The step (c) comprises (c1) mixing the exhaust gas obtained in the step (b) with ammonia, (c2) cooling the mixed gas to remove carbon dioxide in salt form, and separating carbon dioxide into flue gas and salt, (c3) forming a salt solution by adding water to the salt and heating, and (c4) heating the salt solution to vaporize carbon dioxide, where the flue gas obtained in the step (c2) is introduced into the desulfurization step together with the acid gas prepared in the step (a). In the step (c1), the molar ratio of ammonia to carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is adjusted to 0.01-0.8. The step (c1) is carried out at 60-90 degrees C. The step (c2) is carried out by cooling the mixed gas to 10-50 degrees C. In the step (c2), chemical reaction of carbon dioxide is given in specification. The step (c) is carried out using a pyrolyzer or a distillation column. In the step (d), flow rate change rate of gas introduced into the desulfurization step by the exhaust gas obtained in the carbon dioxide removal step is more than 0 vol.% and not greater than +10 vol.%. The rate of change of the carbon dioxide concentration of the gas introduced into the desulfurization step by the exhaust gas obtained in the step of removing carbon dioxide is preferably not less than 0 vol.% and not greater than +80 vol.%. Preferred Components: The salt is ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate and/or carbamate. The concentration of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas obtained in the step of removing carbon dioxide is 0-20 vol.%. In the mixed gas, the molar ratio of ammonia to carbon dioxide is adjusted to 0.01-0.8. The carbon dioxide absorbent is a gas which indirectly ignites the gas mixture below.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The apparatus further comprises an impurity refining unit for removing impurities from the acid gas supplied from the gas generating source and the gas supplied from the carbon dioxide removal unit to the desulfurization unit and supplying the acid gas containing hydrogen sulfide to the desulfurization unit. The carbon dioxide removal unit comprises a gas mixer for mixing the exhaust gas obtained in the desulfurization step with ammonia, a carbon dioxide collector which cools the mixed gas supplied from the gas mixer to remove carbon dioxide in a salt form and separates into flue gas and salt, a reactor for producing a salt solution by adding heat and moisture to the salt obtained in the carbon dioxide collector, a heater for heating the salt solution obtained in the reactor to vaporize carbon dioxide, and a scrubbing tower for removing impurities from the gas obtained by the heater using a handmade agent and discharging carbon dioxide. The salt formed in the carbon dioxide collector comprises at least one of ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate and carbamate. The heater comprises a pyrolyzer or distillation column. The concentration of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas obtained in the carbon dioxide removal unit is 0-20 vol.%. The rate of flow rate change of the desulfurization process influent gas by the exhaust gas obtained in the carbon dioxide removal unit is 0-10 vol.%. The carbon dioxide concentration change rate of the gas introduced into the desulfurization process by the exhaust gas obtained in the carbon dioxide removal unit is 0-80 vol.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN108487883-A
TI Process for utilizing natural gas and carbon            dioxide mixed oil displacement, involves reducing            pressure of reservoir around vertical gas injection,            and shutting down production by newly drilled            horizontal well.
AU WANG B
AE TIANYI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201871227X
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves improving the well                pattern in gas injection production area. The oil                layer around the newly drilled horizontal wells is                pre-heated, so as to form thermal communication                with gas injection vertical wells (1). The device                for injecting carbon dioxide and natural gas is                installed into wellhead of gas injection vertical                well. A mixture of natural gas and carbon dioxide                is continuously injected into vertical gas                injection well, and the surrounding new wells are                disconnected from the well to produce and shut down                the production. The pressure of the reservoir                around the vertical well is increased during the                shutdown of the closed well. The newly drilled                horizontal well (2) is opened for production after                the pressure rises by 1-15 MPa. The pressure of the                reservoir around the vertical gas injection is                reduced, and the production is shut down by the                newly drilled horizontal well after the 1-15 MPa                drops, so that the well production and shut-off                production are resumed.
   USE - Process for utilizing natural gas and carbon                dioxide mixed oil displacement used in oil                production technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The process can realize safe production, and                can utilize natural gas and carbon dioxide to                improve oil recovery. The process is beneficial to                increase crude oil production, while reducing the                adverse impact of carbon dioxide on the                environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                position of a well pattern in a gas injection and                production area.
   Vertical well (1)
   Horizontal well (2)

TI System for providing climate control for            greenhouses, has air conditioning unit for air            filtering, cooling and de-humidification and heating,            and process units for desorbing Carbon dioxide on            adsorbent, and working in desorbing mode.

   JOHANSEN T
   SKJAEVELAND J
   SKJAVELAND J
   JONSSON T
   SKJEVELAND J
AE GREENCAP SOLUTIONS AS (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENCAP SOLUTIONS AS (GREE-Non-standard)

GA 2018138822
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an air conditioning unit for                air filtering, cooling and de-humidification and                heating. A heat pump and a unit capture Carbon                dioxide from ambient air. Process units (134, 172)                adsorb and desorb Carbon dioxide on an adsorbent,                and work alternately in adsorbing and desorbing                mode. Sections (138, 137, 135, 167, 171, 164) in                the processing units comprise an adsorbing                material. The adsorbing material is divided into                horizontal sections to improve the air flow. A                column filters, cools and performs                de-humidification of air from surroundings.
   USE - System for providing climate control for                closed or semi closed spaces i.e.                greenhouses.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides increased rate of Carbon                dioxide by plants and faster plant growth rates,                while avoiding plant issues associated with high or                low air relative humidity. The system integrates a                clean process to be captured from air combined with                high energy efficiency wet bulb temperature control                and dry bulb temperature control without use of                oxygen degradable chemicals with high efficiency                Carbon dioxide capture and overall net energy                savings. The system realizes hot and dry air to                create driving force for flowing water from zeolite                to the air and increase zeolite temperature, thus                reducing the zeolite ability to hold water.

PN WO2018020345-A1
TI Isothermal process for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce oxosynthesis syngas containing composition involves contacting spent chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst with reaction feed containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU MAMEDOV A
   REA C
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
   REA C (REAC-Individual)
GA 201809033Y
AB    NOVELTY - An isothermal process for hydrogenating carbon dioxide (CO2) to produce oxosynthesis syngas containing composition, is carried out by contacting spent chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst (112) with reaction feed containing hydrogen (H2) and CO2 at a H2:CO2 molar ratio of less than or equal to 1, where reaction occurs at a constant temperature of greater than or equal to 550 degrees C and a pressure of greater than or equal to 0.5 MPa to produce the oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition containing H2 and CO at a H2:CO molar ratio of less than or equal to 1.3 and greater than or equal to 1 molar% methane (CH4).
   USE - An isothermal process for hydrogenating carbon dioxide (CO2) to produce oxosynthesis syngas containing composition (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Any unreacted reactant gas can be recycled and included in the reactant feed to maximize the overall conversion of CO2 to oxo-synthesis syngas, increasing the efficiency and commercial value of the CO2 to oxo-synthesis syngas conversion process. The resulting oxo-synthesis syngas can be sold, stored, or used in other processing units.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the production method of oxo-synthesis syngas that includes methane using a combined carbon-dioxide- and hydrogen-containing reactant feed gas and the spent chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst.
   System for converting reactant gas stream of carbon dioxide and hydrogen into syngas (100)
   Combined reactant gas source (102)
   Reactor (104)
   Collection device (106)
   Spent chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: H2:CO2 molar ratio is 0.2-1, preferably 0.5-1. Reaction feed does not contain CH4. Reaction temperature is 550-650 degrees C, preferably 575-625 degrees C. Reaction pressure is 0.5-3.5 MPa, preferably 1-3 MPa. Reaction occurs at 600-630 degrees C and 2.5-3.5 MPa, where H2 gas flow rate in the reaction feed is 40-60 ml/minute, and CO2 gas flow rate in the reactant feed stream is 40-60 ml/minute; and oxy-synthesis syngas containing composition comprises 15-20 mol.% CO, 45-55 mol.% CO2, 5-10 mol.% CH4, and 20-25 mol.% H2. Reaction occurs at 570-590 degrees C and 0.5-1.5 MPa, where H2 gas flow rate in the reaction feed is 30-50 ml/minute, and CO2 gas flow rate in the reactant feed stream is 50-70 ml/minute; and oxy-synthesis syngas containing composition comprises 15-20 mol.% CO, 60-65 mol.% CO2, 5-10 mol.% CH4, and 10-20 mol.% H2. Preferred Compositions: Syngas composition contains 1-40 molar%, preferably 10-20 molar% H2, and 5-30 molar%, preferably 15-20 molar% CO. H2:CO molar ratio is 0.1-1.3, preferably 1-1.1. Syngas composition further contains 30-80 molar%, preferably 45-65 molar% CO2. Chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst contains 5-30 wt.%, preferably 13-17 wt.% chromium. The catalyst contains 0.2-30 wt.%, preferably 2-5 wt.% lithium, potassium cesium, and/or Sr. Spent chromium oxide/alumina supported catalyst contains 50-70 wt.%, preferably approximately 60 wt.% catalyst, and 30-50 wt.%, preferably approximately 40 wt.% ceramic particles. Preferred Parameter: CO2 conversion is greater than or equal to 20%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Syngas composition contains 1-15 molar%, preferably 5-10 molar% CH4. Preferred Processes: The method further includes providing CH4-containing syngas composition to an oxy-synthesis process. Oxy-synthesis syngas containing composition further includes CO2, where the process further includes removing CO2 from oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition; and providing oxo-synthesis syngas containing composition to hydroformylation of olefins reaction, carbonylation of methanol reaction, or carbonylation of olefins reaction.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN107601542-A
TI Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate, involves calcining limestone into calcium oxide, placing calcium oxide into water in slurry container to prepare calcium hydroxide solution, and atomizing calcium hydroxide solution and coagent.
AU LI W
   ZHANG C
   OU X
AE GUANGXI HUAYANG MINERAL RESOURCES CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201808631R
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of nano-calcium carbonate involves calcining limestone into calcium oxide, placing calcium oxide into water in a slurry container to prepare calcium hydroxide solution, atomizing calcium hydroxide solution and coagent separately into atomized droplets of calcium hydroxide and coalescent droplets, filling a carbonized tower with carbon dioxide, jetting calcium hydroxide droplets into a carbonizing tower for chemical reaction with carbon dioxide, heating the carbonization tower, and mixing calcium hydroxide droplets in carbonization tower with carbon dioxide.
   USE - Preparation method of nano-calcium carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of nano-calcium carbonate with uniform particle size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of nano-calcium carbonate involves (A) calcining limestone into calcium oxide using a calcining device, placing calcium oxide into water in a slurry container to prepare 3-6 wt.% calcium hydroxide solution, (B) atomizing calcium hydroxide solution and coagent separately into atomized droplets of calcium hydroxide and coalescent droplets with particle size of less than or equal to 500 nm using atomizer, (C) filling a carbonized tower with carbon dioxide, and (D) jetting calcium hydroxide droplets into a carbonizing tower for chemical reaction with carbon dioxide, heating the carbonization tower, completely mixing calcium hydroxide droplets in the carbonization tower with carbon dioxide, when the temperature of the carbonization tower reaches 120 degrees C, spraying active agent droplets, continuously the carbonization tower, heating and mixing until the temperature in the carbonization tower reaches 180 degrees C, stopping heating, continuously mixing, reacting for 3 minutes, stopping, discharging, cooling, and sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction in the carbonization tower is performed under normal pressure. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide in the carbonization tower is recovered, filtered, returned to the carbonization tower, and reused. Preferred Composition: The coagent comprises stearic acid and 1-2 wt.% calcium hydroxide.

TI Method for cleaning equipment with hollow sealed            circuit from e.g. salt deposits, involves pumping            washing solution with unsteady flow regime created by            local stops of washing solution flow at moment of            carbon dioxide gas generation.
AU STAROSTIN G S
   RODIN I Y
AE ANGARA IND LTD (ANGA-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1550B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pumping a washing solution                with an unsteady flow regime through an inner                cavity of a hollow sealed circuit. The unsteady                flow regime is created by local stops of the                solution flow at a moment of oxygen or carbon                dioxide gas generation by an intensity at which                pressure in a gas bubble during growth exceeds                pressure of the solution. The solution of hydrogen                is peroxide is added in portions of 2-100 liters,                depending on the volume of the internal flushed                cavity of equipment. The carbon dioxide is                generated due to metered adding of salt of carbonic                acid in a solid form or in the form of a solution                or suspension with a concentration from 1-20%,                followed by the addition of organic or inorganic                acids. The solution is selected from a group                consisting of water, solution or emulsion of acids,                alkalis, salts, organic solvents, complexonates and                surfactants.
   USE - Method for cleaning an equipment with a hollow                sealed circuit from deposits e.g. salt and oxide                deposits, petroleum nature deposits such as                asphalt, resin and paraffin deposits, and                biological deposits, at an industrial                facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cleaning the equipment with                hollow circuit contaminated with deposits of                organic, salt or oxide origin, thus maintaining                cleaning efficiency of the equipment.

PN CN114504937-A
TI Flue gas carbon-dioxide capture method involves acting recovered latent heat on reboiler for heating bottom of regeneration tower, cooling acid gas, condensing saturated steam into liquid phase, and separating liquid phase from carbon-dioxide to obtain.
AU LV W
   CHEN T
   WU R
   ZHENG Y
AE ANHUI FANGXIN LIHUA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTEC (ANHU-Non-standard)
   TONGXING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNO (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 202271509A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves absorbing (S1) CO2, and the feed gas is passed through the absorbent. The CO2 in the feed gas is absorbed to form a rich liquid by the absorbent. A rich liquid desorption is performed (S2), and acid gas is produced. The acid gas is mainly provided with CO2, H2O, absorbent. The acid gas is compressed (S3) to increase its pressure and temperature, and latent heat of the acid gas is recovered. The recovered latent heat is acted on the reboiler for heating the bottom of a regeneration tower. The acid gas is cooled (S4), and the saturated steam is condensed into a liquid phase. The liquid phase is separated from CO2 to obtain CO2.
   USE - Flue gas carbon-dioxide (CO2) capture method.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressure and temperature rises by directly pressurizing the acidic gas generated by the regeneration tower, the steam with increased enthalpy of steam is sent to the reboiler of the heating-boiler as steam heating compared with the existing technology. The steam is converted into electric energy, and then converted the electric energy for operation of the whole system, and the steam heat energy interest rate of the method is higher. The regeneration energy consumption is lower than the traditional technique
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the flue gas CO2 capture method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for cooling acid gas, and condensing saturated steam into liquid phase (S4)
   Step for absorbing CO2 (S1)
   Step for performing rich liquid desorption (S2)
   Step for compressing acid gas to increase its pressure and temperature (S3)

AU XUHU
AE ITA CO LTD (ITAI-Non-standard)
GA 202265590W
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a vessel (110) comprising a space for processing a liquid sample. A sample supply unit (120) supplies a liquid sample into the vessel. An ultrasonic homogenizer (150) pulverizes suspended substances in the liquid sample injected into the vessel using ultrasonic waves. A transfer unit (160) transfers the liquid sample processed in the vessel. An oxidizing unit (170) oxidizes the liquid sample processed in the vessel. A detection unit measures total organic carbon by detecting the carbon dioxide generated in the oxidation unit, where the oxidation unit burns the liquid sample transferred by the transfer unit based on a non-catalytic thermal combustion oxidation method to generate carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Apparatus for measuring TOC using ultrasonic vibration.
   ADVANTAGE - The measurement precision is improved by providing ultrasonic vibration to liquid sample to pulverize and homogenize suspended substances present in the sample. The maintenance convenience and work safety are improved by configuring the ceramic tube and ceramic ball, which are consumables to be exchanged at the lower portion of the combustion furnace. The contamination of the sample by organic debris accumulated in the flow path is prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a TOC measuring method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the configuration of a TOC measuring apparatus using ultrasonic vibration.
   Vessel (110)
   Sample supply unit (120)
   Solution supply unit (130)
   Air supply unit (140)
   Ultrasonic homogenizer (150)
   Transfer unit (160)
   Oxidizing unit (170)

TI Electrode used for electrochemical reduction of            carbon dioxide, comprises conductive substrate which is            loaded with metal bismuth and lead.

AE UNIV HENAN URBAN CONSTR (UHUC-C)
GA 202267423H
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode comprises conductive substrate                loaded with metal bismuth and lead. The conductive                substrate is foam copper.
   USE - Electrode is used for electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode is loaded with bismuth-lead                material, has bismuth metal phase and bismuth-lead                mixed phase, has abundant electrochemical reduction                CO2active site, stable work of more than 50 hours,                good conductivity, contact between the catalyst and                the resistor is small, stable under the 15.56                milliampere/centimeter-square (mA.cm-2) formic                acid, and has very high Faraday efficiency. The                electrode effectively inhibits the hydrogen                evolution reaction, solves the problem that the                existing electro-catalysis CO2reduction current                density is too low, and the stability is not good,                and effectively controls the proportion of                different metal precursor in the plating solution                and the current density in plating process. The                foam copper has a three-dimensional porous                structure, which is good for mass transfer of                reducing substance, and good conductive, the                catalytic performance shows the synergistic effect                of metal bismuth and bismuth lead alloy, generating                a large amount of interface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                method for preparation reducing CO electrode, which                involves: (a) pre-treating the conductive                substrate; (b) dissolving the bismuth salt, lead                salt, composite complexing agent, ammonium formate                according to the amount ratio of substance:                0.001-0.01, 0.001-0.01, 0.006-0.06, 0.002-0.2 in                the inorganic solvent to obtain the mixed solution,                using buffer to adjust the pH value of the mixed                solution to obtain electroplate liquid; and (c)                taking the electrode and the conductive substrate                pre-processed as the working electrode placing in                the plating solution, using pulse                electro-deposition method for electroplating to                obtain the electrode loaded with metal bismuth and                lead for electrochemical reduction of carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                electrode pulse electrodeposition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite                  complexing agent is composed of triammonium                  citrate, thiourea and disodium ethylene diamine                  tetraacetate (EDTA). The bismuth salt is                  chlorinated Bismuth, lead salt is lead chloride.                  The inorganic solvent is deionized water. The                  buffering agent is hydrochloric acid or ammonia                  water.Preferred Condition: The ratio of the                  substances of triammonium citrate, thiourea and                  disodium EDTA is                  (0.0020-0.02):(0.002-0.02):(0.002-0.02).
